chapters.title: Chapter 2548: Heavenly Punishment
chapters.body:
Chapter 2548: Heavenly Punishment

Zu An had only promised the old chieftain he'd impersonate Yi to learn about Yi’s murderer. But not only had he failed to uncover the identity of the murderer, the Chang clan had even been massacred. He hadn't thought there was a need for him to continue this act.

Little had he expected Lady Houtu to call the shots and decide that he would continue being Yi. She'd even bestowed him with a droplet of shaman blood essence so his act would be more convincing. That blood essence was none other than the aura he'd felt, and he could feel the tremendous power contained within it as it fused with his body. Fully assimilating it could bring his prowess up another level. 

Judging from Xingtian and Kuafu’s envious looks, he could tell how valuable the blood essence was, but he still had doubts about this development. He looked at the blurry figure in the sky and asked, “Lady Houtu, why do you want me to impersonate Yi?” 

Houtu’s ethereal voice rang out, “It is fate’s arrangement. You’ll know in the future.”

Zu An was mystified. Riddle after riddle.

He wanted to ask more, but Xingtian interjected, “Milady, do you know the culprit behind the Chang clan’s massacre?”

“Too much is involved in this matter. It is beyond your jurisdiction.” 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. What's involved here for her to answer in such a manner?

“As for Bao Jiang’s murder, inform the Goddess of Wu Mountain of the matter. It is most apt for someone of her lofty standing to deal with it,” Houtu said before the portrait in the sky faded.

“Yes!” Despite Houtu’s disappearance, Xingtian and Kuafu didn’t drop their etiquette. Their respect for Houtu came from the bottom of their hearts. 

Then, they bowed to Zu An and asked, “Will you accompany us to Wu Mountain to explain things to the Goddess of Wu Mountain? You are, after all, a witness to Bao Jiang’s murder.”

Zu An had been planning to make a trip back to Wu Mountain anyway, so he accepted the offer. 

Xingtian and Kuafu were overjoyed. They slit their fingers to draw runes resembling ancient characters with their blood. Soon, the surrounding spatial ki began to congregate into a unique formation.

Xingtian explained, “This is our Shaman races’ secret art. We can use our bloodline to perform long-distance teleportations to locations where we have established teleportation coordinates. Now that you have shaman blood running through your veins, you should be able to use this secret art too.” 

Zu An made sure to remember those ancient characters. All of a sudden, he asked, “Can this secret art teleport those without shaman blood too?”

“This…” Xingtian and Kuafu glanced at Yumen Beiqing. “We can definitely bring Chang’e along, but this lady…”

The future Houtu was likely the one who had transmigrated the Mermaid Queen into this world. Houtu would have granted her the shaman bloodline while she was at it, or else the Mermaid Queen would have been exposed as a fake right away.

Yumen Beiqing suddenly spoke up. “You can go on ahead. I won’t be following you.”

Her mind was in chaos. This was an opportunity for her to separate from Zu An to do what she wanted. Zu An and the Mermaid Queen’s marriage was the perfect excuse for her to do so. 

And yet, Zu An grabbed her hand and said, “How can I leave you behind?”

Yumen Beiqing didn’t protest, knowing she could give herself away if she said too much. She figured that Zu An would have to give up, since they wouldn’t slow their journey to Wu Mountain because of her. 

But Zu An turned to Xingtian and asked, “What are the teleportation coordinates of Wu Mountain?”

Even though he had been to Wu Mountain, each world had its own unique considerations when determining the coordinates. Without a deep understanding of the world, it was easy for one to get such a thing wrong. 

Stunned, Xingtian reflexively told Zu An the coordinates. He didn’t know what Zu An was going to do with them since there was no way he could bring that woman along using their Shaman races’ secret art.

Zu An closed his eyes and began overloading his mind with calculations. Being well-versed in spatial transformations, he had a bold idea in mind. He said, “Give me a moment. I’ll try it out first.” 

He morphed into a seven-colored light, forming a massive rainbow across the sky. 

Yumen Beiqing was shocked. The last time he employed this skill, Zu An’s mastery of Rainbow Morph had been severely lacking, so how had he improved so quickly? She could sense that the rainbow stretched to a very faraway place, so much so that she had no idea where the endpoint was. 

Thinking about Zu An’s earlier question, she couldn’t help but wonder. Could it be… 

The rainbow flickered out of existence, and Zu An reappeared before the crowd. “There was a small discrepancy earlier, but there should be no problem now.”

He grabbed Yumen Beiqing and the Mermaid Queen’s hands and dashed away as a rainbow. Before he disappeared, a distant voice echoed, “I’ll see you at Wu Mountain!”

Xingtian and Kuafu exchanged confused looks, but they decided to go through with it. They tapped their fingers on the floating blood character, causing the surrounding space to ripple. One after another, they and their shaman companions vanished into thin air. 

When they reappeared, they were at Wu Mountain. But much to their shock, Zu An’s group was already waiting for them with smiles. 

“How did you do it?” Kuafu was certain that Zu An had utilized the energy of the Shaman races’ secret art, but how could that secret art work on a non-shaman?

Zu An chuckled. He had used his shaman blood as the foundation and roughly deciphered those ancient characters using his understanding of spatial transformations. He'd combined those with his recently-learned Rainbow Morph and experimented several times. 

It had worked out perfectly. This was much better than arduously constructing teleportation formations between two locations, since the enemy wouldn’t give one the chance to construct teleportation formations beforehand. He could now freely use his Rainbow Morph to travel anywhere he had the teleportation coordinates for. He no longer had to worry about his enemies catching up to him. 

That being said, it was easier said than done. Many key factors had to be in place. Had he not acquired all these abilities by coincidence, Zu An wouldn’t have been able to pull this feat off either. 

Just then, those who were gathered heard the sound of jade pendants clanging. A majestic mountain peak in the distance transformed into a beautiful figure, who gracefully descended next to the group.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at Zu An and asked, “You’re looking for me?”

Somehow, she felt something inexplicable toward this man who had embraced her in their first meeting, but she couldn’t put her finger on it.

Zu An had to suppress his urge to rush forward to embrace the woman who looked identical to Pei Mianman. “Bao Jiang has been murdered…” He roughly explained the situation to her. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. “Gu dared to kill Bao Jiang!?”

Bao Jiang was one of her few friends. This terrible news introduced a tinge of rage to her usually composed state of mind. She took a deep breath and asked, “Do you have evidence?”

This was a huge matter. She couldn’t just take Zu An’s word for it. 

Zu An spent a moment in thought before taking out the Brilliant Pinflower and the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine’s seed. “Before his death, Bao Jiang handed these two things to me.”

Upon seeing the two items, the others widened their eyes, especially after seeing the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine’s seed.

With a wave of her sleeves, the Goddess of Wu Mountain cast a spell on the two items. Soon, a portrait appeared in midair. It was the sight of Bao Jiang right before his death. 

Only now did Zu An believe that the other party wasn’t Pei Mianman. The power to trace an object’s origin was indeed a formidable ability. 

By the time the Goddess of Wu Mountain was done watching the projections, her beautiful face was clouded with frost. She waved her hand, and the projected images swirled into the air as if they had become tangible. A cloud drifted over and carried the projected images into the sky. 

Zu An stared at the sky. Is the Celestial Court in the sky? But why didn’t I see it while I was flying through the sky earlier? Ah, come to think of it, there was a faint spatial pulsation right before the images vanished. Could it be concealed by a special formation?

Soon, the sky lit up with a five-colored auspicious light. A beam of light descended upon a distant mountain. An authoritative voice boomed across the lands. “Gu coveted an immortal medicine and killed Celestial Bao Jiang! His crime warrants death!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2549: Conflict
chapters.body:
Chapter 2549: Conflict

The voice contained inexplicable authority. All living beings in this world heard it and felt an overwhelming urge to kneel and kowtow. 

The weaker shamans had already caved in and fallen to their knees. Meanwhile, Kuafu was barely holding on, but his creaking bones and trembling muscles showed that he was barely stopping himself. 

As for Xingtian, he stared at the sky with an air of valiance. This hinted that he was significantly stronger than Kuafu.

Zu An initially felt compelled to kneel too, but his Seven-Colored Divine Halo suddenly shone, and the pressure he felt lightened. He quickly pulled the collapsing Mermaid Queen behind him. He was taken aback to see Yumen Beiqing standing calmly, but he still reached out to protect her with his divine halo. 

As for the Goddess of Wu Mountain, she stood completely unfazed by the pressure. 

There was no need for introductions; it was obvious to Zu An that the person who had just spoken was the legendary Celestial Emperor, though the other party’s aura felt weirdly familiar. It feels like the person who bestowed me with the Sun Slaying Bow the last time I was here. 

“That is?” Zu An glanced at the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain approached him with a surprised look. She appeared to be curious about his Seven-Colored Divine Halo. “That is Zhong Mountain, where Gu lives.”

Xingtian remarked with a frown, “Zhong Mountain is Ancestral Shaman Zhulong’s territory, and Gu is Zhulong’s son. Isn’t the Celestial Emperor going overboard here?”

Many believed Zhulong to be the strongest Ancestral Shaman. He had many legends surrounding him, such as daybreak being the moment he opened his eyes, and nightfall being the moment he closed them…

Unsurprisingly, a terrifying aura suddenly flared from Zhong Mountain, as if a formidable being had been jolted awake. Unlike the Celestial Emperor’s authoritative and dignified aura, this aura was wild and terrifying. In comparison to the Celestial Emperor's, however, the latter didn’t pale at all. 

Cheers came from the nearby mountains. They were likely shamans, standing in a united front against the Celestial Court. 

“Zhuanxu, you have overstepped your boundary,” a deep voice boomed, shaking the earth. 

The incumbent Celestial Emperor is indeed Zhuanxu. It was Zhuanxu who gave me the Sun Slaying Bow back then, Zu An thought. 

He couldn’t resist the urge to look beneath his feet with a shaky gaze. It was rumored that Zhulong was an indescribably massive snake, and thanks to ‘One Drop of Heaven’s Essence’[1] Zu An was extremely sensitive to mountainous leylines.

Zu An had felt the entire mountain range slowly moving earlier. Could the entire Spirit Mountain be Zhulong? How humongous must he be then?

A ray of golden light pierced through the clouds and scattered over the mortal world. A massive golden figure appeared in midair, emanating an air of authority, divinity, and power… 

Countless adjectives flashed through Zu An’s mind, but nothing felt apt here. He could clearly see the golden figure’s appearance, but as soon as he turned away, he forgot about the other party’s physical details. All he could remember was the other party’s overwhelming air of authority and divinity. 

This was the Celestial Emperor’s aura!

Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu said impassively, “Gu, as a celestial, violated the celestial rules by killing Bao Jiang to steal an immortal medicine. Such a crime cannot be condoned!”

A series of images showing Gu murdering Bao Jiang appeared in midair. 

Zu An was stunned. These projected images were more detailed than what the projection conjured by the Goddess of Wu Mountain had revealed. Does the Celestial Emperor have the ability to see everything that happens in this world?

A commotion broke out across the mountain range. The crowd had only spoken out in support of Zhulong because they didn’t know about Gu’s deeds.

Bao Jiang had a good reputation on Spirit Mountain for his amicable and kind-hearted personality. He had never turned down anyone who requested medicine or treatment from him, so many people were fond of him. Those who had previously received Bao Jiang’s help criticized Gu for his cruelty. 

Those images stunned Zhulong, and he fell into a prolonged silence.

Just then, a green-robed woman on Zhong Mountain said, “My son must have fallen for Qin’s deception out of folly. Celestial Emperor, I implore you to spare my immature son!”

Even though her face was concealed by her unkempt hair, her physique suggested that her appearance could be unflattering. Her very presence caused the surrounding trees to wither and the ground to crack.

Zu An could feel a powerful heat wave even from a distance away. 

The last time he was here, the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans had told him that Zhulong’s wife was the Yellow Emperor’s daughter. She'd lost her divine powers while aiding her father in his fight against Chiyou, resulting in her inability to return to the Celestial Court. Forced to stay in the mortal world, she'd turned into a Ba that was shunned and feared by everyone. Lands would be scorched wherever she appeared. 

That should be her. 

The Celestial Emperor fell silent, but he eventually shook his head and declared, “Such a grievous sin cannot be overlooked. If Gu isn’t killed, the celestial laws will be undermined!”

He slowly raised his finger. A ray of golden light fell on Zhong Mountain, and it dragged Gu, who was hiding in one of the caverns, out.

Gu panicked. He struggled with all his might, but he couldn’t break free of the golden light. He screamed, “Mother, save me!”

The green-robed woman panicked. She flew over to save her son, only for the golden light to ricochet her away. Knowing she was no match for the Celestial Emperor, she shouted, “Zhulong, hurry up and save your son!”

Zhulong didn’t answer. Zhong Mountain was silent. 

Before the green-robed woman could say anything, Gu cried out miserably. He was pinned against Zhong Mountain’s eastern cliff, his life force severed for good. His bloodshot eyes were filled with disbelief. 

Zu An was intrigued. The Celestial Emperor sure is domineering, to kill Zhulong’s son on his turf. 

The Celestial Emperor continued his declaration, “Celestial Er Fu has murdered Yayu. Considering that he was incited by Gu and Wei Yueyan, I’ll spare him from death and instead imprison him at Shushu Mountain's cliff for three thousand years.”

A woman screamed. Wei Yueyan, who had been hiding in Er Fu’s palace, was vanquished by a divine light falling from the sky, not even leaving a corpse behind. Another ray of divine light transported Er Fu to the distant Shushu Mountain. 

“What airs from the Celestial Emperor! Have you forgotten the agreement for the Fiend races to manage heaven while we, the Shaman races, manage earth? Why are you kicking up a fuss on our turf now?” a cold voice echoed.

Dozens of massive water dragons surged over from the distance. The deluge of water caused the golden light in the sky to dim a little. 

“It’s Ancestral Shaman Gonggong! Gonggong is here!” The shamans cheered. 

What happened earlier had felt like a slap to the Shaman races’ face. Many of the hardliners among the Shaman races were dissatisfied with Zhulong’s concession to the Celestial Emperor. Now that Gonggong was here, they felt as if they'd finally found their backing.

1. This is a book written in the Song dynasty about predicting the future using one’s Eight Birth Characters. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2550: Slave
chapters.body:
Chapter 2550: Slave

Zu An raised his eyebrows upon hearing the name ‘Gonggong’. He had met Gonggong the last time he was here, and the other party’s major boss vibe had left him with a deep impression. 

A figure with a human face and a snake body stood above a whirlpool, his red hair fluttering with the wind; he had an open and carefree air.

Zu An frowned. This wasn't the Gonggong he had met the last time. He belatedly recalled Gonggong’s subordinate, Xiang Liu, telling him that Gonggong was a position, so there had been many Gonggongs over the generations. 

Given how the shamans addressed the current Gonggong as an Ancestral Shaman, could he be the very first Gonggong?

But what’s with the Shaman races? Why do so many of them have human faces and snake bodies?

With Gonggong taking the lead, more figures emerged from the mountains, each of them emanating powerful auras. They were powerhouses of the Shaman races. They hadn’t dared to step forward and backed down because they were intimidated by the Celestial Emperor’s aura, but with Gonggong taking the lead, they decided to join him.

They weren’t close with Bao Jiang, and they didn’t care about the details of this matter either. What they were concerned about was the Shaman races’ reputation. They felt that the Celestial Emperor had gone overboard by intruding on Ancestral Shaman Zhulong’s territory and publicly sentencing his son to death. Zhulong, for some reason, had chosen to tolerate it, but that didn’t mean that the others would take it lying down!

Over the years, there had been too many conflicts between the Celestial Court’s Fiend races and the Shaman races to determine who had done what wrong first. The accumulated grudges had grown so great that both sides viewed each other as an eyesore. 

The Celestial Emperor coldly glanced at Gonggong. “Gonggong, what do you want?”

As he rode on a huge wave, Gonggong’s red hair fluttered. His aura was no weaker than the Celestial Emperor’s golden avatar. “Gu is a shaman. Even if he deserves to be punished, you should have let us deal with him internally. You’re undermining our agreement by executing him publicly.”

“Since Gu took on a position in the Celestial Court, he was obligated to follow celestial laws. It is within the scope of our agreement to punish him,” the Celestial Emperor calmly pointed out. 

A shaman powerhouse exclaimed, “Hah! I was wondering why the Celestial Court was conferring us with titles. Turns out it was a trap all along! Since that’s the case, there’s no reason for us to continue holding onto those positions!”

Many shaman powerhouses voiced their agreement. “Indeed! So many of our people fought with each other to get positions in the Celestial Court. Little did they know that they were just shackling themselves.”

“I don’t need a post if it means that the Celestial Court can arbitrarily sentence me to death!” 

… 

Those words riled up the crowd. 

Zu An was surprised. The Celestial Emperor must be disturbed that so many people are outright defying him. Looks like the conflict between the Fiend races and the Shaman races is creeping toward its climax. 

He turned to Xingtian and Kuafu. “You also witnessed the events. What do you make of this matter?”

Kuafu replied, “Gu deserves to be punished, but their words make sense too. The one to punish Gu should have been Lady Houtu and not the Celestial Emperor.”

Xingtian scraped his axe against his shield. “Fight, fight! My axe thirsts for blood!”

Zu An was at a loss for words. This fellow doesn’t care about the truth at all. He just wants to fight. 

He turned to the Goddess of Wu Mountain. He naturally felt goodwill toward her because she resembled Pei Mianman. Ah, our Manman is so beautiful!

“Is there something on my face? Why are you looking at me?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain calmly asked, but her eyes remained fixated on the distant Zhong Mountain.

Yumen Beiqing sneered. This man is a breeding horse. His mind is filled with nothing but filth.

The Mermaid Queen frowned as well. Big brother Zu is too lustful! But she quickly remembered how he'd initially kept his distance from her despite his nature, and that made her feel that he was a gentleman instead. 

Feeling the two ladies’ sharp gaze, Zu An’s face heated up. “I was wondering about the Goddess’ take on this matter.”

“What can you see?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain calmly asked. 

Zu An had to concede that despite their identical appearances, their personalities were vastly different. One was passionate and seductive, whereas the other was as cold as ice. The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s personality was closer to Chu Chuyan’s instead. 

Remembering Chuyan made Zu An’s heart hurt. He said solemnly, “It doesn’t look as if you care about how this matter unfolds.”

Sensing the change in his mood, the Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at him before answering, “Yes, I have nothing to do with this matter.”

She had only intervened because Bao Jiang was her friend. Now that the culprit had been punished, she couldn’t care less about what happened afterward. 

Zu An was surprised. Even though the Goddess of Wu Mountain lives on Spirit Mountain, she's more aligned with the Celestial Court than the Shaman races. Is she not worried that if she maintains a nonchalant attitude, the Celestial Court might suffer a setback here? 

In a cavern amid the mountain range, the Lord of Slaughter and Salamay stood respectfully behind a black-robed man. “Master is indeed wise! You were able to turn the fiends against the shamans with just a little ploy!”

It felt as if many things had happened since they arrived in this world.

One morning, they'd woken up to find Mojard missing. This came as a huge shock to them, as Mojard was no weakling, and he had rested alongside them that night. And yet, he'd disappeared without them noticing, leaving not a trace behind. The person behind it had to have been terrifyingly powerful for neither the Lord of Slaughter nor Salamay to have noticed a thing.

Salamay had wanted to search for the missing Mojard, but the Lord of Slaughter had dragged her elsewhere. They'd begun making their way toward the towering Kunlun Mountain, which could be seen from all parts of this world.  

Be it Kunlun Mountain’s towering peak that pierced through the clouds, or the words of the indigenous population, the Lord of Slaughter had become convinced that Kunlun Mountain was linked to the Celestial Court. He wanted to find the Celestial Court’s secret and awaken his bloodline, so he could return to his world and exact vengeance on the Monster Lord. 

Along the way, they'd encountered a mysterious man. The latter had spent a long time sizing them up, occasionally murmuring words like ‘slave’ and ‘pet’. 

The Lord of Slaughter, being the hot-tempered man he was, couldn’t tolerate such an insult and decided to kill the other party to vent his anger. 

However, what happened next had driven him to despair. He'd been defeated before he could touch the other party. It was a completely one-sided battle. 

He could still remember the other party looking down on him while saying, “I never expected to stumble upon such a rare spirit beast. I happen to be lacking underlings. Are you willing to be my mount?”

The humiliation he'd felt then permeated his entire being. To think that he, the Lord of Slaughter, was only worthy of being a mount to the other party! 

Even so, he had no choice but to submit. The other party was so strong that when going up against him, the Lord of Slaughter was completely helpless. Furthermore, the other party’s words suggested that he knew about the Lord of Slaughter's bloodline origin. That sparked the Lord of Slaughter’s hopes of becoming stronger. So, he'd decided to accept the offer. Naturally, Salamay had no choice but to follow her father too. 

As a result, both the father and the daughter had become the mysterious man’s mounts.

Over time, the Lord of Slaughter witnessed the mysterious man’s methods of wreaking havoc in the world. That reduced the indignation he felt, and he thought that he'd likely made the correct decision then. 

“The fiends and the shamans have been fighting for a long time now. I am merely giving them a push. Besides,” the black-robed man said with a chuckle, “I’m not the only one fanning this conflict.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2551: Isolation Of Heaven And Earth
chapters.body:
Chapter 2551: Isolation Of Heaven And Earth

Others are fanning this conflict too?

The Lord of Slaughter and Salamay exchanged looks. It sounds like others are playing on this chessboard as well, and master appears to be wary of them. To think there are other beings as terrifying as master! This world is a scary place!

Salamay’s eyes darted around in contemplation. She approached the black-robed man and coyly asked, “Master, master! Who are the others hiding in the shadows?”

After saying those words, she couldn’t help but shiver. This was her first time speaking like that. She had never thought that a day would come when she would have to resort to a honey trap. 

The black-robed man harrumphed as he shoved her aside. “That’s not a question you should be asking. Also, don’t even think about seducing me. Would you be interested in your pets?”

He was already being considerate by not calling them ‘livestock’.

Salamay’s face reddened in anger. She had never been humiliated in such a manner before. However, she quickly reined herself in and hid her displeasure under a beaming smile. “Yes, master is right to reprimand me. I will reflect on my mistake.”

The black-robed man turned his attention back to Zhong Mountain. Over there, more and more shaman powerhouses were abandoning their posts in the Celestial Court. The situation was spiraling out of control. 

Even the Celestial Emperor couldn’t help but frown. “Calm down; it’s meaningless to act in a fit of rage. We agreed that the fiends and the shamans would govern their own jurisdictions. If you abruptly abandon your posts, the world will be plunged into chaos.”

Gonggong sneered, “The agreement was for the Fiend races to govern heaven while the Shaman races governed the earth, but you overstepped your boundary to kill our people on our turf. Since our agreement means nothing to you, there’s no reason for us to continue wearing these shackles.” 

The other shamans cheered in agreement. Shamans had unbridled personalities, and the grudge between both races was simply too deep. At this point, they weren’t rioting just over Gu’s execution anymore. 

A shaman powerhouse roared, “A few years back, someone proposed the isolation of heaven and earth. Why is there suddenly no further word on that? We don’t want to see you celestials descending to earth as and when you like to wreak havoc!”

“That’s right! This is the only way to resolve our conflicts!”

… 

With someone taking the lead, a huge uproar broke out. It looked as if a huge battle would break out if the Celestial Emperor didn’t agree to the isolation of heaven and earth. 

Zu An clicked his tongue.

There are several troublemakers among the shaman powerhouses. Each time the matter is finally about to be put to rest, someone jumps out to further muddy the waters. It feels like someone is intentionally stoking this conflict. 

They're brave to dare scheme against the Celestial Emperor and the Ancestral Shamans.

While the Celestial Emperor’s avatar at Zhong Mountain was placating the shamans, his main body was at the Celestial Court, discussing this matter with the other celestials. 

It was chaos in the Celestial Court. The Celestial Emperor had conjured a huge screen above the hall to project the situation at Zhong Mountain. The celestials felt indignant, as they thought that the Shaman races were going too far.

To them, Gu was at fault, and it was the Celestial Emperor’s prerogative to punish him. And yet, the Shaman races made it sound as if the Celestial Court was in the wrong. It was out of goodwill that the Celestial Court had offered the Shaman races positions, yet the Shaman races made it sound like a malicious attempt to tie them down. Some of the hardliner celestials were so infuriated that they cursed the shamans.

It didn’t take long for hawkish propagandists to claim the upper hand in the discussions. 

In the first place, the conflicts between the fiends and the shamans hadn't ceased for many years now. If the Celestial Court bowed to the shamans despite having the moral high ground, they would become a joke to the whole world. No one would submit to the Celestial Court anymore! 

There were also celestials who pushed for peace, but their voices were drowned out by the hawkish faction’s saliva. After so many years of conflict, which celestial didn’t have a personal vendetta against the shamans? Under such circumstances, it was difficult for the celestials to think rationally. 

In truth, Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu had publicly executed Gu for more than just his vile deed. He'd intended to use this opportunity to punish the shamans and vent the suppressed emotions of the Fiend races’ hawkish faction as well. Otherwise, the situation could spiral out of control if feelings of discontentment continued seething. Despite being the Celestial Emperor, he couldn’t act as he pleased and neglect the interests of his subordinate celestials. 

Zhuanxu looked at Emperor Jun, who stood at the forefront of the other celestials. Most people recognized him as the next Celestial Emperor, so many celestials were gathered by his side. Emperor Jun’s harsh stance against the Shaman races was welcomed by most celestials. He hadn't said a word thus far, but his aides had already spoken up on his behalf, relaying whatever he wanted to say.

No one knew what would happen if a war truly broke out.

Just then, Fire Celestial Zhongli stepped forward and said, “War is not the only way to resolve this problem. There’s another option.”

Zhuanxu’s heart sank. Zhongli was his grandson; he could already guess what the other party was going to say. However, that was too heavy of a price to pay.

“May I know what Fire Celestial’s solution is?” a celestial asked.

“The separation of heaven and earth!” Zhongli replied with a calm but firm gaze.

The noisy hall fell silent. 

Emperor Jun, who had been silent all this while, finally spoke up. “Fire Celestial, do you know the price we’d have to pay for separating heaven and earth? Is there a need to sacrifice our Fiend races’ great celestials for mere shamans? Who is willing to put their lives on the line?”

Celestials averted their eyes wherever Emperor Jun looked. The separation of heaven and earth was simple in theory; it involved pushing up heaven and pushing down earth, thus severing the link between both places. That would allow the independent governance of heaven and earth. Once interactions between the shamans and the fiends stopped, their intense conflicts would naturally be resolved. 

But that was easier said than done. 

Even the great Pangu, to stop heaven and earth from converging back together after severing them apart, had to hold up the sky while stepping on the earth. He'd continued this grueling feat for countless years before finally taking his last breath. His body had turned into mountains, rivers, forests, and other such terrain. There were rumors that the towering Kunlun Mountain was a bone that Pangu had left behind. 

Thanks to Pangu’s sacrifice, a new equilibrium between heaven and earth had formed. Building on Pangu’s foundation, the difficulty of completely separating heaven and earth was much lower than what Pangu had done back then, but it would still require the strongest Fiend races deities to put their lives on the line to achieve the feat. 

Very few fiends could cultivate to that level, so who would be willing to sacrifice themselves to save a bunch of shaman barbarians?

“I’ll do it if no one else is willing to!” Zhongli declared.

A horrified Zhuanxu quickly stopped him. “Zhongli, you mustn’t be reckless…”

But Zhongli interjected, “Grandfather, you taught me that all cultivators must find their path. I had no idea where my path was for the longest time, but now I know it’s to prevent this world from being destroyed.”

Right after he finished speaking, his body pulsated in enlightenment. This was a sign that he had been recognized by the heavenly dao. 

Emperor Jun bowed deeply to Zhongli. “I am in awe of Zhongli’s grand wish.”

The others in the hall, regardless of factions, also bowed to Zhongli. They were also in awe of his selflessness.

Zhuanxu sighed. To a cultivator, their dao was more important than their life. He couldn’t stop Zhongli, even for the latter’s good; otherwise, Zhongli’s dao heart could unravel, causing him to descend into despair. That would make him no different from a cripple. 

“It would be best if this could resolve the war between the fiends and the shamans. It would bring tremendous karma to Zhongli.” Zhuanxu tried to console himself, but he was still disheartened. 

He had many offspring, but not many of them had achieved anything great. The same went for his descendants, too. Zhongli was the only one who had displayed outstanding talent. He'd intended to nurture Zhongli as the next Celestial Emperor, or the one after that, or even the one after that, but it looked as if his hope was going to be dashed. 

On Zhong Mountain, another golden avatar slowly manifested and declared, “To prevent the further escalation of conflict between the shamans and the fiends, Fire Celestial Zhongli shall completely separate heaven and earth under the Celestial Emperor’s order!”

The golden avatar morphed into a giant that was as tall as the sky. With a roar, he stomped his foot on the ground and propped up the sky with his hand. The surrounding ki was immediately plunged into chaos. The earth quaked and mountains collapsed. The ground undulated nonstop, as if it had been liquefied. Everyone struggled to maintain their footing. 

Zu An quickly grabbed the Mermaid Queen and Yumen Beiqing. Much to his shock, he realized that the earth was sinking downward while the sky was being propped higher and higher up.

Even powerhouses like Xingtian and Kuafu were caught off guard and fell on their butts. 

In contrast, the Goddess of Wu Mountain firmly stood her ground as if there was a stake beneath her body, keeping her in position no matter how the ground moved. 

Feeling the rampaging spatial ki around him, Zu An looked at the golden avatar in utter shock. Pangu severing the sky had always been a legend to him; he'd never thought he would see something similar in his lifetime. 

How powerful did that golden avatar have to be to pull off such a feat?

Yumen Beiqing’s hands and feet turned ice-cold. In the end, things still ended up like this!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2552: Vengeance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2552: Vengeance

Xingtian looked at the massive golden avatar with an admiring gaze. “To think there were such valiant heroes amongthe Fiend races too!”

Kuafu nodded in agreement. "Looks like not all fiends are despicable and shameless. There are noble souls among them too.”

Zu An could tell that the shamans and the fiends were extremely prejudiced toward each other. “I’m surprised that you acknowledge him.”

“The separation of heaven and earth is easier said than done. Back then, Pangu sacrificed his life to sever heaven from earth. What Zhongli is trying to achieve is nowhere as difficult as what Pangu did, but he is nowhere as strong as Pangu either. The only fate awaiting him is death by exhaustion,” Kuafu explained. 

The Shaman races were said to be created by Pangu’s blood essence, so they naturally carried great respect for Pangu. 

Upon hearing those words, Zu An looked at the golden avatar once more with a melancholic gaze. He had a greater grasp of the world now, and he could sense the golden avatar dissipating, as if fusing with the world. In due time, it would become a part of the world’s laws. 

“He is a true hero!” the other shamans in the mountains remarked as well. 

The Celestial Emperor remained silent. In the end, he let out a sigh and dissipated his golden avatar.

Even the previously infuriated Gonggong also looked conflicted. He clasped his fist at the massive golden avatar before leaving on a water wave. 

“He’s leaving like that? He acted as if was going to pick a fight with the Celestial Emperor!” Zu An was perplexed. He'd thought that Gonggong was going to make a move, but the latter hadn't gone through with it. 

“The Celestial Emperor killed Ancestral Shaman Zhulong’s son, but now the Celestial Emperor is sacrificing a grandson for this cause. The Shaman races are meticulous in settling scores. Things are even now, so there’s no reason to continue pursuing this matter,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain explained. 

“Zhongli is the Celestial Emperor’s grandson?” Zu An was surprised. It was his first time hearing about this. 

“Indeed. He is the Celestial Emperor’s most outstanding grandson, and there was a chance he could have become the next Celestial Emperor,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said. 

Zu An took this chance to gather more information. “You mentioned earlier that the Celestial Emperor’s name was Zhuanxu, and that Zhongli is in line to become the next Celestial Emperor. What’s the deal with Emperor Jun then?”

Yumen Beiqing’s face twitched at the mention of ‘Emperor Jun’.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain answered his question. “Strictly speaking, he is only the appointed successor and has yet to take the throne, so we should address him as ‘Jun’ instead of ‘Emperor Jun’. 

“Jun is likely to become the next Celestial Emperor, but Zhongli is outstanding too, not to mention that he’s Zhuanxu’s grandson, whereas Jun is only Zhuanxu’s nephew. Due to that, Zhongli has many supporters too. Even if he didn’t become the next Celestial Emperor, there was a huge chance he could become the subsequent one after Jun.”

At this point, the Goddess of Wu Mountain sighed. “But now that Zhongli has sacrificed himself to separate heaven from earth, that is all for naught.”

Zu An was at a loss for words. He hadn't thought that deities engaged in nepotism too, though it was hard to think badly of Zhongli after the latter had selflessly sacrificed himself for others. 

“Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu looks to be at the peak of his power. Why is he stepping down all of a sudden? Is he nearing the end of his lifespan?” Zu An was confused.

“Deities do have limited lifespans, but that’s not why the Celestial Emperor is stepping down. He has merely reached the end of his term. By letting go of secular responsibilities, he can go into seclusion and focus on achieving enlightenment.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s voice was cold, but she gave off an inexplicable charm. 

“Achieving enlightenment?” Zu An was surprised by the huge burden that came with being the Celestial Emperor. “Isn’t the Celestial Emperor already the strongest person in the world?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain eyed Zu An. “I see you have become a deity too, so why don’t you know the basics? Being the Celestial Emperor can accelerate one’s accumulation of faith and hasten one’s cultivation growth, but that only works to an extent before the effectiveness starts to wane. 

“On top of that, being too entrenched with the world’s karma can get someone stuck, preventing them from advancing any further. That is why the Celestial Emperors of every generation step down when they feel their time has come, purge the karma they have been afflicted with, and focus on grasping the greater dao so that they can eventually achieve Sainthood.”

“Sainthood?” Zu An recalled a phrase from a popular web novel in his previous world—’All beings beneath saints are mere ants’. I wonder if it’s the same for this world too. To verify his conjecture, he asked, “What saints are there in this world?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain rolled her eyes. “That’s not something you should concern yourself with.”

Zu An was speechless. She's scorning me, right?

He wanted to ask more when he noticed the nearby flora and fauna withering swiftly. Even the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s straight black hair had begun curling up. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. A mist enveloped her body, and her hair regained its straightness and smoothness.

A terrifying heat wave surged. Even breathing left one with a stinging sensation in the chest. 

Zu An quickly protected the Mermaid Queen and Yumen Beiqing. With their current cultivation, they wouldn’t be able to withstand this heat wave.

Just then, a hoarse female voice echoed, “Yaoji, return my son’s life to me.”

A terrifying heat wave surged toward the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

Even Xingtian and Kuafu had no choice but to retreat three hundred meters away to avoid the brunt of the attack. Zu An was also worried about the two ladies, so he took them elsewhere to avoid the heat wave. 

Much to his astonishment, the Goddess of Wu Mountain stood her ground, not bothering to dodge. Alarmed, he shouted, “Be careful!”

Soon, a green-robed woman slowly walked over from afar. Wherever she passed, lush grass and fresh flowers withered at a visible pace. She left giant footprints that looked to be crackling with flames on the ground. 

Wu Mountain was a verdant mountain shrouded in fog, but the woman’s presence dissipated the fog and withered the forests. All beings residing on Wu Mountain felt their mouths becoming dry as sweat rained from their bodies. 

Beads of sweat appeared on the usually cool Goddess of Wu Mountain’s forehead. In response, she waved her sleeves with a harrumph, and the fog reappeared around Wu Mountain. 

The green-robed woman stopped not too far away from the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Somehow, it felt as if two forces were clashing against each other, especially given the stark difference in the sceneries behind the two women. 

One had a backdrop of scorched earth, whereas the other had a verdant mountain. 

Zu An recognized the green-robed woman to be Gu’s mother. Just earlier, she'd begged the Celestial Emperor to spare her son’s life. 

Yumen Beiqing’s eyes narrowed. She came at a good time.

“Were you the one who reported this matter to the Celestial Court?” The green-robed woman’s appearance was concealed by her unkempt hair, but she was glaring at the Goddess of Wu Mountain. 

“Indeed. Gu deserves punishment for what he did,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied.

“You’re courting death!” Furious, the green-robed woman swiped her pitch-black fingernails toward the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s neck. If her attack landed, the latter’s neck would be covered in bloodied holes. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2553: The Fisherman Profiting Off The Brawling Fishes
chapters.body:
Chapter 2553: The Fisherman Profiting Off The Brawling Fishes

With a wave of her sleeves, the Goddess of Wu Mountain vanished, causing Nuba’s attack to fall empty. 

Nuba looked at the surrounding fog and sneered, “Yaoji, do you think I won’t be able to suppress you just because you morphed into fog? That might be true for others, but that doesn’t include me!”

Her robe flared outward as her unkempt hair fluttered furiously. A visible heat wave began spreading from her, causing earth to crack and spirit springs to dry up. The lush trees on the mountain swiftly withered. 

It looked as if the vibrant Wu Mountain had turned barren in an instant. Even the fog shrouding the mountain slowly dissipated, and not a single cloud was left. 

Xingtian and Kuafu couldn’t help but speak up. “Madam, this matter…”

“Shut up! This has nothing to do with you, so don’t do anything unnecessary and side with outsiders!” Nuba roared. 

Xingtian and Kuafu exchanged awkward looks. Nuba was the wife of an Ancestral Shaman, so they didn’t dare to offend her. That put them in a tough spot. 

Yumen Beiqing roared, “We were the ones who informed the Goddess of Wu Mountain about Gu’s murder of Bao Jiang. You’re wronging her!”

Zu An was taken aback. He had indeed planned to stand up for the Goddess of Wu Mountain, but he hadn’t expected Yumen Beiqing to beat him to it. Didn’t she get jealous the last time I spoke with the goddess? What an abrupt change of mind! 

“It’s you!” Nuba suddenly turned around. Her black fingernails grew longer as she swiped them at Yumen Beiqing, resembling spears. 

Yumen Beiqing reacted fast. She activated the Emperor’s Suppression Talisman and swiftly conjured a golden-armored warrior. The warrior brandished its weapon to stop the black fingernails.

“A mere celestial warrior dares to stop me?” Nuba sneered. 

Her black fingernails sunk into the warrior, afflicting it with a black aura that suppressed its energy. A second later, the golden-armored soldier disintegrated.

However, Nuba’s attack didn’t stop there. Her black fingernails continued surging toward Yumen Beiqing’s forehead.

Yumen Beiqing’s gaze turned cold, but she stood completely still as if she wouldn’t react to the attack at all. 

Just then, Zu An appeared in front of Yumen Beiqing and deflected the black fingernails with his sword. At the same time, a fog enveloped Yumen Beiqing and transported her a hundred meters away. 

The fog slowly morphed into the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Noticing Zu An’s glance, she nodded back at him in acknowledgment. 

Nuba didn’t continue her attack. She looked at Zu An in confusion and murmured, “A human? How can there be such a strong human in the world?”

This world was dominated by the fiends and the shamans. The humans were a lesser race stuck between the two. Weak and foolish, the only thing they were good for was their reproductive prowess. 

Zu An sneered, “Gu was the one who murdered Celestial Bao Jiang in cold blood. The Celestial Emperor has already resolved this issue, so why are you coming after us for an explanation?”

Nuba laughed miserably. “I am no match for Zhuanxu, and he has already lost a grandson. It wouldn’t do for me to go after him. But I haven’t settled the score with you yet.”

Zu An was speechless. This madwoman can’t be reasoned with!

The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. “Why are you acting like a crazy shrew? You’re the Yellow Emperor’s daughter.”

“Yellow Emperor… Hahaha!” Nuba looked as if she had suddenly lost her mind. “I sacrificed so much for the war back then, but what did I get in return? Nothing. Nothing at all!

“You, the Flame Emperor’s daughter, are adored by the masses despite doing nothing. What about me? Not only did I lose my divine powers and become incapable of returning to the Celestial Court, I was even reduced to this state! Others shun me wherever I go! I was lucky to have Gu as a child, but now I have nothing left. You stole everything from me!”

A thought surfaced in Zu An’s mind. He had heard about the Yellow Emperor’s life-and-death battle with Chiyou.

Chiyou, with the aid of Fengbo and Yushi, had claimed the upper hand over the Yellow Emperor’s army and even cornered them. Nuba had intervened and curbed Fengbo and Yushi, allowing the Yellow Emperor to turn the tables. 

But I didn’t expect the Goddess of Wu Mountain to be the Flame Emperor’s daughter. Hold on, Big Manman is also skilled at controlling flames. Could she be related to the Flame Emperor?

The Goddess of Wu Mountain remained calm. “After the war between the Flame Emperor and the Yellow Emperor, the Celestial Court is now dominated by the Yellow Emperor’s lineage. What you have just said is of little concern to me, and it is meaningless to argue with me about all these things.” 

Zu An and the Mermaid Queen exchanged astonished looks. They felt as if they had just stumbled upon piping hot tea. The Goddess of Wu Mountain had just revealed extremely important information.

It turned out that the Flame Emperor and the Yellow Emperor weren’t as close as they had previously thought them to be. It looked as if at one point, they were even enemies, and the Flame Emperor was the one who had fallen in defeat, resulting in the Yellow Emperor’s lineage taking power. 

“What carefree words! I bet you've been harboring a grudge against me because you were dissatisfied with the Flame Emperor’s defeat, so you used this opportunity to kill my only son!” Nuba screeched. One of her eyes appeared through her unkempt hair, and it was so widened, bloodshot, and turbid that it felt as if she had gone utterly insane. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head. “You have already gone berserk.”

“I don’t care! I’ll kill anyone responsible for my son’s death!” Nuba unleashed a screeching sonic boom. 

Yumen Beiqing nodded empathetically. She was the only one who understood how Nuba felt. 

Nuba morphed into a ray of green light that charged at the Goddess of Wu Mountain. This time, the latter didn’t transform into a fog, and instead fought with Nuba in her current form. The two figures moved extremely swiftly; all that could be seen were a green light and a white light intersecting with each other. 

Kuafu lightly bumped Xingtian’s arm and asked, “Who do you think is more likely to win?”

Xingtian analyzed the situation as he grimly replied, “Even though Nuba lost her divine powers, the Goddess of Wu Mountain doesn’t specialize in battle. Furthermore, Nuba’s power happens to suppress her. Things don’t look good for the Goddess of Wu Mountain.”

Zu An had come to the same conclusion too. He had already spotted a few close calls for the Goddess of Wu Mountain. At this rate, she’ll only last at most an hour before falling in defeat.

Just then, he heard Yumen Beiqing’s whisper, “Why don’t we take this chance to attack the madwoman together and kill her? Otherwise, she could come after us once the Goddess of Wu Mountain falls in defeat. We may not be a match for her then.”

It would be much easier for them to defeat Nuba with the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s help.

Zu An shook his head. “She’s still grieving over her son. Despite our conflict, it isn’t to the point where we should take her life.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly exclaimed. She had been sent flying by a strike on the shoulder, and Nuba made use of this opportunity to charge at her. She was intending to take the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s life!

Zu An couldn’t help but worry for the Goddess of Wu Mountain because of her extreme resemblance to Pei Mianman. Without any hesitation, he dashed in front of the Goddess of Wu Mountain and gently pushed her behind him. 

Nuba screeched in anger, “You’re courting death!”

She gathered a destructive surge of energy that emanated terrifying heat waves between her hands. 

“Be careful!” the Mermaid Queen cried. She could sense the pressure coming from the energy.

Yumen Beiqing narrowed her eyes as she secretly clenched her fists. It’d be best if they injure each other so I can make use of this chance to claim their lives. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2554: Mastermind
chapters.body:
Chapter 2554: Mastermind

Concern appeared in the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s usually cold eyes. She intended to step forward to help Zu An when a sudden flash of seven-colored light caused the furious Nuba to vanish into thin air. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s mouth opened so wide that one could fit a chicken egg into it. Thinking that Nuba had hidden herself using a special movement skill, she quickly scanned the surroundings with her ki, but Nuba was truly nowhere to be found.

Even the heat and dryness were beginning to recede. Nuba had truly vanished.

Xingtian and Kuafu exchanged relieved looks. Whew, we aren’t the only ones who fell for that.

Yumen Beiqing was shocked and intimidated. His Seven-Colored Divine Halo is capable of subjugating Nuba too! I must only make a move when I’m confident of victory, or else there won’t be a second chance.

He’s just a normal human. Other than being taller, more handsome, and more muscular than average, there are hardly any strengths to him. How did he obtain such a ridiculously overpowered ability? 

Another seven-colored light flickered around Zu An, and a disoriented Nuba fell to the ground. Zu An looked at her and said, “It was the Celestial Emperor who sentenced Gu to death based on the celestial laws. If you agree to not go after the goddess and the others, I’ll spare your life in light of the fact that you're still grieving over your son.”

Nuba coldly spat, “What if I refuse?”

“Then I’ll kill you right now,” Zu An calmly replied, as if it was something insignificant. He had weathered many storms and had been in a position of authority for a long time, so he naturally exuded an air of intimidation. 

Nuba fell silent. She sensed that it wasn’t an empty bluff. She was ill-tempered, but she was no fool. The other party’s Seven-Colored Divine Halo was incredible. If she obstinately marched down this path, only death awaited her, but she needed to stay alive to avenge her son. 

“I promise you.” She harrumphed before taking her leave.

Xingtian and Kuafu bowed to Zu An. “Thank you for showing mercy to Madam.”

As fellow shamans, they wouldn’t be able to turn a blind eye if Zu An made a move. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief too. Nuba was the Yellow Emperor’s daughter and an Ancestral Shaman’s wife. It was hard to predict what would happen if he took her life. 

“Thank you for your help,” a voice said as a faint fragrance drifted into Zu An’s nose. The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s gaze had mellowed considerably compared to before. 

“Pardon me for my unnecessary intervention, goddess.” Looking at the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s snowy skin close up, Zu An couldn’t help but think that she was carved out of the same mold as Pei Mianman. 

“You need not be so courteous. Nuba’s power happens to suppress mine. I’d have been in danger if not for your intervention.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly noticed his gaze, but Zu An's expression didn’t carry any lust; just yearning and tenderness. That shook her heart. “Are you yearning for your lover again?”

Zu An snapped out of his daze and awkwardly bowed. “My apologies.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. Do I look identical to Miss Pei? It doesn’t seem as if he’s lying, and… Her eyes fell on the Mermaid Queen. 

His two companions aren’t ordinary women. Since they backed up his words, it should be safe to assume that Miss Pei does exist. But how could there be two completely identical people in the world? Unless… 

The conclusion she arrived at alarmed her. It prompted her to assess Zu An once more. 

Meanwhile, Zu An was scanning his surroundings. He turned to the Mermaid Queen and asked, “Did you see little sister Ling’er?”

The Mermaid Queen turned to her side and was taken aback. “Hm? The Saintess was still there a moment ago. Where did she go?”

… 

Meanwhile, Yumen Beiqing was discreetly following Nuba. Under normal circumstances, she wouldn’t do something like this, as her identity could be easily revealed. But now that her plan had fallen into shambles, she no longer cared as much whether Zu An learned about her true identity or not. 

This is my final chance. I have to kill Nuba now so she won’t be scorched to death by the Golden Crows’ Ten Suns, thus preventing the series of subsequent events...

Such as Kuafu dying of exhaustion while trying to avenge Nuba, completely igniting the Shaman races’ fury. That was what later caused Hou Yi’s shooting of the suns. As long as I kill Nuba, there will be no reason for those events to happen. I might be able to change the fate of the Ten Suns!

She trailed behind Nuba, awaiting the perfect chance to make her move. Nuba was a formidable adversary, but Yumen Beiqing happened to be impervious to her power. Thus, she believed that if she utilized the element of surprise well, it wouldn’t be difficult for her to kill Nuba. 

After stalking Nuba for some time, she finally found a chance to make a move. She was about to strike when she suddenly noticed a strange movement and hurriedly hid in a nearby tree.

“Who is it?!” Nuba was alarmed, but she looked in another direction instead of where Yumen Beiqing was hiding. 

Crisp applause filled the air. A woman walked out and said, “As expected of a renowned expert. You found me so quickly.”

“It’s her?” Yumen Beiqing recognized the woman. It was Salamay.

They had parted ways with the Lord of Slaughter’s party after entering this world, and there had been no news from the latter in the meantime. She hadn’t expected to stumble upon Salamay here. 

I wonder if Mojard told her anything. Probably not, since the Time Scribe wasn’t the real Mojard.

While Yumen Beiqing was still in thought, Salamay had already arrived in front of Nuba.

Nuba frowned. “A slave?”

Salamay was speechless. Her smile immediately froze. Can’t we have a pleasant chat? I used to be the princess of a powerful world, you know! Why does everyone in this world treat us like slaves and pets?

She felt uneasy. The legends passed down through the generations are likely false, meaning we didn’t descend from a lofty bloodline. The truth would plunge the entire Monster World into despair. 

Recalling her mission, she took a deep breath and suppressed her feelings. “My master tasked me to relay a message to you.” 

“I have no interest in what your master has to say. Scram!” Nuba was in a terrible mood after having suffered a defeat. She released a powerful heat wave that pushed Salamay a hundred meters away before the latter finally regained her balance. 

Seeing that Nuba was going to leave without even sparing her a glance, Salamay anxiously shouted, “Are you not curious to know the true culprit behind your son’s death?”

Nuba instantly reappeared in front of Salamay, who was raised into the air as if an invisible pair of hands was holding her up by the neck. 

Nuba’s hair fluttered wildly as she looked at Salamay with a twisted expression. “Who is it?”

“Someone goaded Gu into stealing the immortal medicine, convincing him to see it as an opportunity to eliminate his two older brothers… Cough cough. Release me! I… can’t breathe…” Salamay struggled desperately as she instinctively clawed at her neck, but it was to no avail.

“Give me a name!” Nuba roared as she finally dropped Salamay to the ground.

Salamay gasped for air, sounding like a broken bellows. For a moment there, she had almost tasted death! This world is too dangerous!

“My master says that this clue should be enough to lead you to the mastermind,” she quickly replied, fearing that Nuba would resort to violence once more.

Nuba frowned. A while later, she turned away and left without saying a word. 

Salamay breathed a sigh of relief. She spat, “That madwoman.” She tidied up her clothes and patted her chest, muttering, “When my master refused to tell me the mastermind’s name, I thought that woman would hound me to no end. To think that she would leave just like that. Master has it all figured out!”

She glanced fearfully in the direction Nuba had departed in before heading back to report the matter to her master. 

It was only after Salamay left that Yumen Beiqing finally emerged from her hiding spot with a darkened expression. Looking at the two directions where Nuba and Salamay had gone respectively, she hesitated for a second before deciding to follow Salamay. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2555: Showing One'S Hand
chapters.body:
Chapter 2555: Showing One'S Hand

Yumen Beiqing figured that she could kill Nuba at any time. The latter was a renowned figure, and it was an easy feat to find her. So, she decided to prioritize following Salamay and figuring out who the latter's master was. 

Someone else is pulling the strings here. I might have overlooked something important. Perhaps I just have to find that important thing to change the ending. 

That greatly lifted her spirits. She carefully followed Salamay, fearing that the latter would discover her. 

Salamay was very vigilant. She took remote paths, and sometimes even took detours to confuse anyone following her. She also frequently surveyed her surroundings to ensure that no one was following her. 

But with her strength, there was no way she could find the Sun Goddess when the latter was intentionally hiding from her. 

Soon, Salamay arrived at a cliff. She knocked on the cliff wall, and a ray of light shone down. She took out a jade piece and placed it into the light; the jade glowed and reflected the light onto the cliff. 

All of a sudden, a cavern appeared on the cliff face. 

With a sweet smile, she walked in and bowed to the person seated inside. “Master, I have completed the mission you entrusted to me.”

The black-robed man impassively said, “Tell me how it went.”

Salamay recounted the details surrounding her encounter with Nuba. The black-robed man nodded in satisfaction. “Good. You didn’t do anything unnecessary.”

Seeing that the black-robed man was in a good mood, Salamay took this chance to ask, “Master, why didn’t you tell her the name directly? Why are you divulging information to her in this roundabout manner instead?”

The black-robed man laughed. “Be it shaman or fiend, mortal or deity, they share the same peculiar frame of mind. They are wary of information offered for free, but they don’t doubt the information that they personally uncover. That’s why it’s better to let her investigate the matter. She’s the Yellow Emperor’s daughter, an Ancestral Shaman’s wife. Her means and connections are not to be underestimated. It won’t be difficult for her to uncover the truth.”

Salamay was awed. “Master is truly sagacious! I am humbled by your ingenuity.”

The Lord of Slaughter secretly gave his daughter a thumbs up. He had been in a lofty position for a long time and was used to being bootlicked more than having to be the one bootlicking. It was hard for him to suddenly change his ways. Good thing my daughter is formidable. If she wins master’s favor, we can gather more intelligence. Who knows? Master might even bestow us the method to activate our ancient bloodline power… 

All of a sudden, his expression stiffened. Huh? Why am I calling him master even in my thoughts? Where has my sense of shame gone? Where has my dignity gone?!

While he was doubting himself, the black-robed man’s voice suddenly turned cold. “You trash. You brought the enemy here with you.”

The gleeful Salamay was stunned. “Master, I didn’t…” The pressure exuded by the black-robed man in a fit of rage shook her very soul. 

The black-robed man ignored Salamay and instead looked up at the entrance. “Why not come in for a chat when you’re already here?”

Yumen Beiqing slowly entered the cavern while coldly eyeing the black-robed man.

“The Fiend races’ Saintess?” How could the Lord of Slaughter not recognize the one person who had impeded the Monster World’s invasion of the Cultivation World for so many years? He sneered, “Hmph! You concealed yourself so well that I was never able to find you. To think that you would appear before me on your own accord!” 

He swiped his claw at Yumen Beiqing, hoping to take her down to quell his master’s anger lest the latter blame Salamay for this lapse. 

The black-robed man spat, “Idiot!”

The Lord of Slaughter was confused. Why does master value a mere Fiend races’ Saintess from a weak world so much? 

The truth was soon apparent. A brilliant light suddenly shone in front of him. For a moment there, he thought he was looking at the rising sun. Terrifying heat and flames gushed at him, charring his resilient skin black. For an instant, he thought he was going to die.

He hurriedly unleashed all his protective methods, knowing that this wasn’t the time for him to withhold any of his aces. As the ruler of the Monster World, he had many invaluable treasures with him. Brilliant lights pulsated from his body, helping him fend off the terrifying flames. However, judging from the swift rate at which his defensive artifacts were being depleted and destroyed, he knew that it wouldn’t take long before he was reduced to ashes. 

Salamay was horrified, but she was nowhere strong enough to intervene in this battle. She could only turn to the black-robed man with pleading eyes. “Master…”

Looking at the Lord of Slaughter’s artifacts, the black-robed man thought to himself that his slave had some value after all, and that it would be a waste for him to die here. So, he waved his cloak, and it swiftly enlarged into a humongous black cloth that enveloped the brilliant sun, causing the cavern to turn dark once more. 

Salamay finally got a look at the black-robed man’s true appearance and was shocked. Contrary to her expectations, the black-robed man wasn't an unkempt old man or a twisted mutant. Instead, he was tall and dashing. He’s not as good-looking as that Zu guy, but he’s not bad either. 

A force dragged the Lord of Slaughter backward, pulling him to safety. He quickly bowed and said, “Thank… you, master…” He was also surprised to see that the black-robed man was a young man.

Yumen Beiqing glared at the black-robed man in a mix of shock and realization. “It’s you!”

The black-robed man smiled. “I didn’t expect you to find me here. I’d expect no less from the Sun Goddess.”

Yumen Beiqing’s expression turned cold. “That explains why everything progressed in the worst direction no matter what I tried. You were pulling the strings. It’s a good thing I found the root of the problem now. It’s not too late to resolve the situation now.” 

“You’re awfully confident about defeating me, mother,” the black-robed man replied with a relaxed laugh.

Salamay was treating the Lord of Slaughter’s injuries when they heard those words and immediately looked over. They assessed the two others with slackened lower jaws that nearly fell to the ground. 

They are mother and son? How did a Fiend races’ Saintess from a weak world suddenly become master’s mother? And she looks so young that I would have thought that she was younger than master. What’s going on?

“Shut up. You have no right to call me that.” Yumen Beiqing glared at the black-robed man with wrathful eyes. 

Salamay and the Lord of Slaughter slowly grasped the situation. It doesn’t seem like they're truly mother and son… but why does master call her mother then?

The black-robed man’s expression turned livid. “You people still look down on me. Of course, you’re the lofty Sun Goddess. Someone with a bloodline as noble as yours is more than qualified to look down on others. Little do you know that your arrogance will become your downfall!”

“I shall capture and hand you over to your father. You can reason with him instead.” Yumen Beiqing attacked the black-robed man. 

“Hahaha! You’re too arrogant. Very well. I shall show you my true strength!” The black-robed man’s clothes puffed up as he transformed into a void in response to Yumen Beiqing’s attack.

… 

Meanwhile, Zu An was still searching for Yumen Beiqing.

Seeing how anxious he was, the Goddess of Wu Mountain hesitantly opened and closed her mouth several times before finally speaking up. “You don’t need to be too worried about her. She’ll be fine.”

“Goddess, what makes you say so?” Zu An looked at her with sharp eyes. “Do you know something that I’m unaware of?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2556: Search
chapters.body:
Chapter 2556: Search

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “Not really. I merely sensed a formidable power hidden inside her body. She should have no problem protecting herself.”

“A formidable power?” The Mermaid Queen was stunned. The Fiend races’ Saintess’ cultivation should be beneath mine, but the knowledge and composure she displayed… 

Zu An remained calm, as if he had already known that. 

He hadn’t doubted Yumen Beiqing at the start. The way she hadn’t hesitated to sever ties with the Fiend races for him greatly resembled Xie Daoyun. But as he spent more time with her, he'd noticed so many anomalies that it was clear something was amiss. If he was that dense, he wouldn’t have survived skirting around death for so many years. 

For instance, he had followed Yumen Beiqing’s information to locate Pei Mianman’s whereabouts, but the latter was nowhere to be found, as if she had never appeared in those places. After he thought about it, there was only one possibility: The information he'd received from that town was off. 

He'd initially suspected that Xie Daoyun was misleading him out of jealousy, but he quickly rejected that conjecture. Having known Xie Daoyun for years, he knew better than anyone else that she was an educated, sensible, and gentle young miss. She wouldn’t do something like that. 

Subsequently, more and more details popped up that further convinced him that she wasn’t the real Xie Daoyun. 

For instance, while the two of them were getting steamy, he'd tried using some secrets that only he and Xie Daoyun knew to test her. Despite Yumen Beiqing’s best attempts to impersonate Xie Daoyun, she clearly didn’t know about these secrets. That had also made him realize how even when the two of them were getting passionate, Yumen Beiqing still exuded inexplicable coldness and even hatred toward him. 

He couldn’t understand why a woman would submit to a man if she hated him. And if she truly hated him, why did the system not pick up on her rage? Sometimes, he'd wondered if he was reading too much into it. 

However, more details surfaced when they arrived in this world, and they confirmed his conjecture that Yumen Beiqing was concealing many secrets from him. He tried to test her many times, but she'd never shown any openings.

But then, he'd secretly probed her body while they were getting some action, and it was then that he detected a formidable sealed power inside her. However, he hadn’t dared to probe the sealed power too deeply out of fear that she would notice his intrusion. 

The act they'd put on together felt like a harmonious orchestra at first. It was only recently that Yumen Beiqing started looking distracted. It felt as if she was no longer interested in maintaining the act. 

Even so, Zu An had had no choice but to continue the act because he needed her to find Pei Mianman and the real Xie Daoyun. Yumen Beiqing had to have come into contact with Xie Daoyun at some point to gather so much information about them. 

He'd been planning to find a good time to have a show of hands with Yumen Beiqing when the latter suddenly went missing. There was a chance that Yumen Beiqing was leaving for good, so he had to find her, whether it was for Pei Mianman or Xie Daoyun. 

Thus, he bowed to the Goddess of Wu Mountain and said, “Thank you for your advice, but I still have to find her, or I’ll feel uneasy.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain hesitated for a second before gently replying, “I am the cause of this matter. I’ll accompany you in your search.”

Zu An looked at her in astonishment. Her offer was at odds with her cold nature, but it would indeed be helpful to have the aid of someone from this world. It was also a pleasure to travel with someone who looked so much like Pei Mianman. “Thank you, goddess.”

The Mermaid Queen shot vigilant looks at the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Is she interested in big brother Zu too?

The thought that the other party resembled the woman Zu An loved deeply left her feeling profoundly helpless. 

Xingtian and Kuafu walked over and clasped their fists at them. “We won’t be able to accompany you anymore, as we have to return to report the incident here.”

So many things had just happened at Zhong Mountain. All the powers in the world were in an uproar now. They had to return to their tribe to decide on their next move. 

“See you!” There were many questions Zu An wanted to ask them, but his priority at hand was to find Yumen Beiqing. He didn’t dare to delay this matter. 

After parting ways, Zu An began searching for Yumen Beiqing’s trail. He had been too focused on dealing with Nuba earlier, so he hadn’t noticed her sneaking away. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain pointed in a direction. “I know every blade of grass and tree on Wu Mountain. I sensed her heading in that direction. However, Wu Mountain is nothing more than a peak on Spirit Mountain. It won’t be easy to find a single person here.”

Zu An knew she was right. The Spirit Mountain of this world was tens of thousands of times larger than the mountain ranges in his previous world. Even the mountain peaks easily reached tens of thousands of meters in height. There were countless venomous creatures and ferocious beasts in the region. Powerful cultivators also established their caves here, and they constructed dangerous beguilement formations to protect their caves. Even a grandmaster from the Cultivation World would fare no better wandering the Spirit Mountain region and trying to survive in the wilderness; the odds of survival were zero. Finding a person here was harder than finding a needle in the ocean.

“It’s fine. At least we have a direction to work with now. Thank you, goddess.” Zu An began making his way in the direction pointed out by the Goddess of Wu Mountain. From time to time, he took detours, heading left at one moment and right in the next. It was as if someone was pointing out the directions for him. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain noticed his lack of hesitation in navigating and asked, “Did you plant a location talisman on that lady?”

Zu An shook his head. He had considered that, but he also knew that Yumen Beiqing wasn't as simple as she appeared. Such a move would reveal that he was suspicious of her, so he had to give up on that idea. “I can communicate with small animals. They're the ones pointing out the directions for me.”

He was relying on the jade badge’s ability to commune with living beings to find Yumen Beiqing. The latter had moved carefully, but she wouldn’t go to the extent of guarding against worms, birds, and small animals.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was awed. “You must have a pure and kind heart to be able to commune with animals.”

She had heard that only the noblest souls would inherit such powers. Even the Mermaid Queen’s eyes glowed wondrously as she looked at Zu An. I've chosen the right person!

Zu An’s face heated up. With the misunderstandings they were having, it was tough for him to break their bubble and tell them that it was an artifact’s ability.

Fortunately, he noticed a trail and soon arrived at the cliff face. He concluded, “She came here and entered that spot."

“There seems to be a concealment formation here.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain quickly noticed something amiss and waved her sleeves. A fog shrouded the cliff face, revealing the formation’s details. “It’s a complex formation. It won’t be easy to decipher it.”

“It’s not that hard.” Zu An whipped out his Tai’e Sword and plunged it into three locations. The formation on the cliff face scattered, revealing a black cavern. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain widened her eyes in shock. Absolute strength and a deep understanding of the formation’s weaknesses were required to dispel a formation in such a manner. This fellow is an enigmatic one. 

The Mermaid Queen took out a Night Illumination Pearl to gently light the cavern. Intense heat pulsated from within. There was no oxygen in it at all. If not for their high cultivation levels, the immense pressure difference would have blown their bodies to bits as soon as they stepped into the cavern. 

It was a mess inside the cavern. There had been a huge battle here.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2557: Cause Of Suspicion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2557: Cause Of Suspicion

“Such extreme heat! Could this be where Nuba dwells?” The Mermaid Queen, being from the Ocean races, was extremely sensitive to dry and hot environments. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. “That shouldn’t be the case. Nuba didn’t leave in this direction, and her dwelling is some distance away from here.”

Zu An traced his finger across the cavern wall to inspect its molten glass-like quality. “Even though they both specialize in flames, there is a vast difference between Nuba’s power and this. Nuba’s heat is purely destructive and deathly, whereas this heat also contains vibrant life force within it, despite its potential for destruction.”

Most people wouldn’t have been able to tell the difference, but Zu An’s grasp of different powers had reached a higher level than before. 

“Could the Saintess have come here to challenge the master of the cavern? This power should belong to the cavern’s master, right?” the Mermaid Queen deduced. 

Zu An neither affirmed nor denied her guess. For the battle to have become this intense, both parties had to have been on the same level. A guess was beginning to surface in his mind. 

He suddenly spotted something in his peripheral vision. He flew over and picked up a metal fragment. It looked like copper but wasn’t copper; it seemed to have broken off from a massive cauldron. Strange runes were inscribed on the metal fragment, but it was hard to decipher them, as most of them were destroyed. 

“Ah!” the Mermaid Queen suddenly exclaimed in shock.

A human face was inscribed on the metal fragment. It was carved so exquisitely that it resembled a living being, but the face was warped in extreme pain. Just the sight of it was enough to make one uncomfortable.  

“I wonder who carved something like that. With such skills, they should carve something better than this,” the Mermaid Queen grumbled.

“You’re right. This wasn’t carved onto the metal piece.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain took the metal fragment and gently touched the face on it. Her expression turned grim. “It is a living soul.”

The Mermaid Queen was stunned.

Zu An had also noticed something amiss with the human face, and he hurriedly asked what was going on. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain solemnly replied, “In this world, there are malevolent arts capable of extracting a living being’s soul and trapping them in special containers to achieve unspeakable goals. These runes serve to suppress the soul.”

Zu An grimly murmured, “Could Yumen Beiqing have come here for some reason, only to stumble upon the cavern’s master utilizing some sort of malevolent art, upon which the two of them came to blows?”

That would mean that Yumen Beiqing might be in danger. She was undoubtedly powerful, but someone who dabbled in such arts would be anything but a pushover. 

“Look here!” the Mermaid Queen exclaimed. She had been trying to distance herself from that eerie mask when she inadvertently stumbled upon another discovery

Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain looked over and saw that a corner of the cavern wall had crumbled. The extreme heat in the cavern had likely caused the walls to expand unevenly, resulting in the collapse.

Zu An gently pushed the wall. It collapsed, revealing a secret chamber. He first scanned the interior with his divine sense before leading the way in. 

The Mermaid Queen followed him. Even though she was creeped out by this place, Zu An’s figure gave her inexplicable peace of mind. 

Zu An walked straight toward the innermost stone wall. His earlier scan told him that there could be something here. He took the Night Illumination Pearl from the Mermaid Queen, and the content of the stone wall was revealed.
