There were many rough lines inscribed onto the stone wall, depicting warped humanoid figures as well as all sorts of blood-red runes. A long wooden pole towered above them, and planted on it was a pitch-black banner. The patterns on the banner felt eerie, even though they had become blurred. 

This resembles the Soul Summoning Banner I obtained from the monsters, which can induce instability in one’s soul just by waving it. But compared to the banner depicted on this mural, it’s almost like a baby standing before a giant. 

“Myriad Souls Banner!” The Goddess of Wu Mountain spat out three icy-cold words.

“Goddess, do you know this item?” The Mermaid Queen could tell that it was nothing good from the name. 

“There are malevolent beings who collect living souls and fuse them into banners. The more souls there are inside a banner, the greater its prowess. Banners with over ten thousand souls are called Myriad Souls Banners, and they wield terrifying prowess. One would have to commit many atrocities to forge something like that.” 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at the lines on the walls with a frosty gaze. “Most parts of the mural have been erased, but it likely contains the forging method of a Myriad Souls Banner. For even a simple draft to induce such eeriness; its terror far surpasses any of the Myriad Souls Banners known to the world.

“Of course, stringent conditions have to be met to forge such a terrifyingly powerful banner.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain stared at the mural for a long time before adding, “I heard from my father that a Myriad Souls Banner of this caliber requires not just countless souls, but also the aid of someone who possesses the innate Cruel Poison Constitution to fuse lethal poison into it to maximize its killing prowess.”

Zu An was taken aback.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was the Flame Emperor’s daughter, though it was unknown which generation of Flame Emperor her father was. Regardless, the Flame Emperors were from Shennong’s lineage and were medical experts. This gave great credibility to her words. 

The forging of this Myriad Souls Banner likely required someone with the Cruel Poison Constitution, and the only person Zu An knew who possessed the Cruel Poison Constitution was Ji Xiaoxi. He was extremely relieved that she hadn’t come to this world. 

But the fact that the Mermaid Queen had been transported into this world and that he had been unable to find or contact Ji Xiaoxi in the previous spacetime fragment left him with an ominous feeling. 

“Goddess, are there any rumors about a Myriad Souls Banner of this caliber appearing in the world recently?” Zu An asked. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head. “No, and that’s what makes it frightening. The other party must be hatching a massive scheme.”

“You mentioned earlier that the forging of a Myriad Souls Banner requires the collection of living souls.” The Mermaid Queen’s face suddenly paled, and her voice quivered. “Could this Myriad Souls Banner be behind the abrupt deaths of the Chang clansmen?”

Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were horrified to hear that. Now that Zu An thought about it, he hadn’t sensed their souls when he inspected their bodies. He'd thought that was because the underworld hadn’t been built in this world yet, so their souls naturally wandered around the world.

But looking back on it, it was more likely that their souls had been siphoned away by the Myriad Souls Banner. Only something as malevolent at that was capable of quietly massacring so many experts. 

Why did the Myriad Souls Banner spare me and the Mermaid Queen? And why did Houtu not mention this when Xingtian reported this matter to her?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2558: Traces
chapters.body:
Chapter 2558: Traces

Could Houtu be the mastermind behind all this… This random thought that surfaced in Zu An’s mind astounded him.

There were indeed many dubious details surrounding this matter. For instance, he had only entered this world to help the future Houtu collect the Six Paths’ power insignias, and she had also transported the Mermaid Queen into this world. Houtu had to have known that the Myriad Souls Banner was responsible for the Chang clan’s massacre, but she'd chosen not to mention a thing and even told them not to get involved. If she wasn't the culprit, what reason did she have to withhold this information?

Cold sweat began raining down from Zu An’s head. 

The Mermaid Queen noticed his pale face and asked in concern, “What’s wrong?”

“It’s nothing. I just thought of something bad,” Zu An replied with a strained smile. I hope I’m reading too much into it. Someone as powerful as Houtu has no reason to do something like this. 

He turned to the Goddess of Wu Mountain and asked, “Goddess, can you report this matter to the Celestial Court and have them investigate this issue?”

He wasn't some hero character from some big action flick. There were official departments to deal with such vile situations, so there was no need for a lone ranger like him to worry about it. 

He didn’t think that the Celestial Court was behind this. At the very least, they needed to maintain a righteous front, lest they lose the faith of the masses. It was unlikely they would do something that would shake their foundation. 

Schemers chose to scheme because they couldn’t do certain things openly. Once the powerhouses of the Celestial Court got involved, it likely wouldn’t be too difficult to resolve this matter. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded. She began writing in mid-air with her slender finger, just like what she'd done when she informed the Celestial Court of Bao Jiang’s death. 

After she was done writing the message, she tried to send it upward, but the words flickered feebly before abruptly going out.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s expression darkened. “The world laws are in disarray due to the separation of heaven and earth. We might not be able to come into contact with the Celestial Court any time soon.” 

Zu An hadn’t expected there to be such an implication. “Looks like we’ll have to investigate this matter by ourselves. I’m afraid that I’m still unfamiliar with this world. Goddess, is there anything you can deduce from these clues?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain stared at him, unsure of what he meant by ‘still unfamiliar with this world’. However, she didn’t dwell on it and replied, “I spend most of my time in isolation on Wu Mountain, so I don’t know much either.”

Zu An was stunned. Turns out she’s a hermit! This makes things troublesome. 

“But we can ask someone.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes lit up.

“Who?”

“The Kaiming Six Shamans!”

Zu An suddenly remembered how her attitude toward him had improved upon learning that he was a friend of the Kaiming Six Shamans. She was likely on good terms with them. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain brought Zu An and the Mermaid Queen back to Wu Mountain. “You can rest at my place first.”

Outsiders would have been shocked to hear that, as there had never been a precedent of her inviting others into her cave as guests.

The Mermaid Queen was confused. “Aren’t we going to meet the Kaiming Six Shamans?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled as she gestured toward the fog. “The fog will relay the news on my behalf.”

Zu An was awed. She must have a high affinity with the fog. No wonder there was a saying in his previous world—'the cloud and rain of Wu Mountain'.

The Mermaid Queen was mesmerized by the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s earlier smile. The latter had a seductive face but a cold disposition, but somehow, these two contrasting qualities came together in harmony for her. Even a woman like me is hopelessly charmed by her. Which man can possibly resist her advance?

At the same time, the Goddess of Wu Mountain was also sizing her up. It's been a while since I last saw such a beautiful woman. I heard she’s the Chang clan’s Saintess, which means that she’s a shaman. When did the Shaman races’ ladies become so beautiful? Even the renowned fairies in the sky can’t hold a candle to her. 

Before they knew it, they had arrived at the foot of the Goddess Peak. With a wave of her sleeves, the Goddess of Wu Mountain summoned a cloud before inviting the duo onto it. Zu An and the Mermaid Queen stepped onto the cloud and found that it wasn't as fragile as they thought it would be, even though it was as soft as cotton. 

The cloud carried the three of them up the mountain. 

This was Zu An’s first time admiring the Goddess Peak up close. Compared to the other mountains in the vicinity, it looked slender, almost like a fair lady’s waist. A waterfall flowed down the peak, and the splashing water turned into the fog that constantly shrouded the peak. 

The Mermaid Queen, being from the Ocean races, was extremely sensitive to water. She realized that the water flowing down the waterfall wasn't ordinary water, but condensed ki. 

Having studied ‘One Drop of Heaven’s Essence’, Zu An had a deeper understanding of the waterfall. The ki waterfall was like a dragon vein that complemented Wu Mountain, forming a unique protective energy. Intruders risked being struck by lightning. Others wouldn’t be able to enter this place without the goddess’ invitation. 

The mountain peak was filled with wondrous flowers and mysterious grasses. It was a scene as beautiful as the two women beside Zu An. The fog was much thicker on the mountain peak, forming a sea of clouds that matched the sea of flowers. It was already nearing sunset, and the golden sun rays shone through the clouds and basked the flowers in its brilliance. It was a splendid sight to behold. 

Zu An was awed. “I can see why the goddess prefers to stay here. It’d be hard to leave a place as beautiful as this.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “You can stay here longer if you like…”

Halfway through, she realized that her words were inappropriate. The golden rays shining on her face concealed her blushing cheeks. It was only when she turned around that the golden rays finally flowed down her neck.

Zu An was mesmerized. The Goddess of Wu Mountain was usually extremely cold, which contrasted sharply with Pei Mianman, but her shy expression was identical to the latter's. 

The Mermaid Queen was a little jealous. I’m still here. Can you not act like this in front of me?

Fortunately, the Kaiming Six Shamans arrived just in time to alleviate the awkwardness. 

Upon hearing the Kaiming Six Shamans’ voices, the Goddess of Wu Mountain waved her hand to form a screen in midair with her fog. It resembled a video call. 

The Kaiming SIx Shamans were surprised to see Zu An and the Mermaid Queen standing next to the Goddess of Wu Mountain. For so many years, the goddess has never allowed anyone to step foot on her peak. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain summarized what they'd discovered in the cavern. The Kaiming Six Shamans were shocked. 

“To think that there’s such a deranged madman!”

“We have to root him out!”

“Such a person should be killed a thousand times over!”

“It’s a pity that the world's laws are in disarray. We aren’t able to contact Lady Houtu at the moment.”

… 

While the Kaiming Six Shamans were outraged, a cloud brought the metal fragment they had found in the cavern before the fog screen. “Are you able to tell anything from this?”

The Kaiming Six Shamans gathered their attention to analyze the metal fragment. Soon, they reached their conclusion. “It’s no ordinary metal. It seems to be… No, it has to be from Genesis Mountain. Only that place has such unique copper!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2559: The Celestial Court’S Secrets
chapters.body:
Chapter 2559: The Celestial Court’S Secrets

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyebrows shot up. “The Yellow Emperor once mined Genesis Mountain’s copper to forge a copper cauldron at the foot of Thorn Mountain.”

Wu Peng nodded in approval. “The goddess has a good memory. The Yellow Emperor once harvested Genesis Mountain’s copper and forged a cauldron from it. The shamans residing there are known for forging artifacts. Many approach them to ask for a weapon.” 

Zu An widened his eyes. “If this metal fragment was from Genesis Mountain, the shamans there must have clues about its maker.”

The trail of clues on Yumen Beiqing had ended at the cavern. Zu An had tried using the jade badge to ask nearby little animals about Yumen Beiqing, but none of them had any other information about her. Right now, their only choice was to head to Genesis Mountain. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain pondered for a moment before turning to Zu An. “You seem unfamiliar with the world’s terrain. I’ll accompany you to Genesis Mountain.”

Zu An was overjoyed. “Thank you, goddess!”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “Since Miss Yumen went missing at my place, I also have a responsibility to uphold.”

The Kaiming Six Shamans exchanged confused looks. The Goddess of Wu Mountain rarely leaves her mountain, yet she's been taking trips more frequently than ever. But when they thought about Zu An, it all made sense.

Looking at the Kaiming Six Shaman’s knowing smiles on the screen, the Goddess of Wu Mountain felt embarrassed. She coughed softly and said, “I’ll trouble you to inform the other powers about the situation. Let’s investigate this matter together and root out the mastermind.”

The Kaiming Six Shamans’ faces turned solemn. “Of course. Other than informing the other shamans, we will also do our best to get in touch with Lady Houtu. We’ll keep you informed if there’s any update.”

“I’ll be troubling you.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded. 

Seeing her reverting to her usual cold expression, the Kaiming Six Shamans ‘tactfully’ clasped their fists and took their leave. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain turned to Zu An and the Mermaid Queen and said, “I doubt you’re in the mood to rest. Shall we begin making our way to Genesis Mountain?”

“Thank you, goddess.” Zu An was moved. Who’s the one who said the Goddess of Wu Mountain is cold-hearted? Look, isn’t she caring and meticulous?

The Mermaid Queen had the weirdest look on her face. I bet the Goddess of Wu Mountain hasn’t noticed her change in demeanor yet. 

The three of them got onto a cloud and headed off. There wasn’t much space on the cloud, so the three of them stood in close proximity to one another. Zu An could smell their unique fragrances. 

The Mermaid Queen’s fragrance felt like the ocean breeze gently caressing his skin, and it evoked thoughts about the beautiful yet enigmatic ocean. Meanwhile, he Goddess of Wu Mountain’s fragrance was like a translucent veil, light yet blurry. It was cold, yet it held irresistible allure. 

Zu An had to quickly pull himself together and divert his attention elsewhere by starting a conversation. “Goddess, you’re the Flame Emperor’s daughter, so why do you live on Wu Mountain instead of at the Celestial Court? Pardon me if this question offends you. You don’t have to answer it if that’s the case.”

He had always been curious about this world. He'd asked the Goddess of Wu Mountain a similar question before, but the latter snubbed him. He thought it was worth asking the question a second time now that they had gotten closer. He knew this question could pique her displeasure, but he needed as much information as possible to make a reasonable deduction about what was going on. It wasn’t the time to be worried about offending others anymore. 

As expected, the Goddess of Wu Mountain fell silent for so long that the Mermaid Queen thought that Zu An could have offended her. But in the end, she spoke up. “It has been many years since the Flame Emperor’s lineage lost control of the throne.”

Seeing that she was willing to speak, Zu An decided to continue probing. “Is it because of the defeat against the Yellow Emperor?”

“You can say so.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain sighed. “The first Celestial Emperor was the Green Emperor. There are many legends surrounding him, so much so that I can’t tell which ones are true.”

“The second Celestial Emperor was Shennong, who was revered by the masses as the Flame Emperor. From him, the title of ‘Flame Emperor’ was passed down for nine generations, but the Celestial Court collectively refers to these Flame Emperors as the second Celestial Emperor.”

The Mermaid Queen never thought that she would hear these secrets. I’d have never encountered all these things if not for big brother Zu. I have to say that he’s truly like an incubus; women just fall in love with him no matter which world he’s in. 

Thinking about how she'd been ensnared by him at first sight, the Mermaid Queen’s fair skin turned red like a blossoming tundra rose. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued. “The title of ‘Flame Emperor’ was passed down through the generations, but each successor was weaker than the previous. By the ninth generation, a man from the Xuanyuan clan rose through the ranks, and he later became known as the Yellow Emperor.

“The balance of power began tipping. There were multiple wars between the Flame Emperor and the Yellow Emperor, and the Yellow Emperor eventually emerged victorious. With that, the Flame Emperor’s lineage completely lost power in the Celestial Court.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain briefly paused before adding, “I am the youngest daughter of the final Flame Emperor.”

She hadn't explained it explicitly, but Zu An and the Mermaid Queen could already understand why she had taken up permanent residence in Wu Mountain. A new clan had taken over the Celestial Court. It was benevolent of the new rulers to spare her life, so how could they allow her to remain in the Celestial Court?

Zu An suddenly remembered what he had heard before and asked, “You mentioned that when they feel that their term is up, each Celestial Emperor passes down the position to their successor so that they can go into seclusion and pursue the great dao. Did the previous Flame Emperors turn a blind eye to this matter?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head. “Entities like them view the pursuit of the great dao to be their ultimate goal. Their descendants’ affairs are fleeting clouds to them. Besides, the Green Emperor was the founder of the Yellow Emperor’s lineage. The position has merely switched to the other side; the previous Celestial Emperors would not bother themselves with that.”

Zu An nodded. I wonder what the great dao that all these Celestial Emperors are pursuing is.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued, “The subsequent Celestial Emperors came from the Yellow Emperor’s lineage. After the Yellow Emperor was White Emperor Shaohao, followed by the incumbent Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu, who is also Shaohao’s nephew and is known as the Black Emperor.” 

Zu An was surprised. “Why did Shaohao pass the position to his nephew instead of his son?”

“There are many speculations.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain took a moment to construe her thoughts before continuing, “Most people believe that the White Emperor’s sons failed to achieve anything significant compared to Zhuanxu. While the Celestial Emperor’s position can be passed from father to son, the successor must also prove himself worthy of the position.”

Doubt surfaced in Zu An’s mind. “What’s the situation with Emperor Jun then?”

“Jun is White Emperor Shaohao’s offspring. Many celestials support his ascension as the next Celestial Emperor, but Black Emperor Zhuanxu had a grandson named Zhongli who was highly talented too. There was intense competition between both sides. Jun claimed the upper hand and won the position of the official successor, but many still had a high opinion of Zhongli’s future.” 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly sighed. “But who could have thought Zhongli would sacrifice himself for world peace? We all underestimated him.”

These bits of information helped Zu An to clear up his thoughts, guiding him toward an answer. 

At this point, the Goddess of Wu Mountain hesitantly spoke up. “The White Emperor’s lineage and the Black Emperor’s lineage are not on good terms. It would be best for you to steer clear of their fight.”

“All this over the Celestial Emperor’s position?” the Mermaid Queen remarked. 

“That’s not all. The White Emperor’s lineage worships the phoenix, whereas the Black Emperor’s lineage worships the dragon. Even though both are from the Green Emperor’s lineage, they come from different maternal races. The White Emperor’s maternal race is the Phoenix race, whereas the Black Emperor’s maternal race has inherited the dragon’s bloodline.

“The Dragons and the Phoenixes were the world’s powerhouses before the Fiends and the Shamans came to power, and they were mortal enemies in that era. The enmity was passed down through the generations. That’s why you mustn’t get involved in the fight between those two factions, or else the only fate awaiting you is death,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said. 

Zu An was stunned. A famous daoist work in his previous life known as ‘The Cloud Bookcase with the Seven Labels: Records of Spirit Treasures’ once mentioned—’There was a calamity named Longhan[1]. After Longhan, the world transformed and entered the era of Yankang.’

Many webnovels in his previous life had built on that setting to talk about the war between the dragons and the phoenixes. Could I have inadvertently stumbled upon the truth here?

1. Longhan refers to an era of calamity, when the world is dominated by the Ancestral Dragons, the Origin Phoenixes, and the Genesis Qilins, and they fought one another for supremacy. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2560: Aura Of Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2560: Aura Of Death

As a transmigrator, Zu An had read plenty of legends and webnovel settings in his previous life, so what the Goddess of Wu Mountain shared felt extremely familiar to him. 

“Could I be in a fantasy world…” He shook his head and dispelled those miscellaneous thoughts. 

He focused his attention on the issues before him. He'd been misled by his previous life’s impression, thinking that all the so-called emperors were humans. From the looks of it now, the emperors and even the celestials were all fiends. But that raised the question—where were the humans?

Upon hearing Zu An ask about that, the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied, “Lady Nuwa created the humans based on the traits of both the fiends and shamans, but humans turned out to be too weak. They don’t have the formidable power that shamans and fiends harness in their bloodlines. They have some prosperous tribes, but compared to the fiends and the shamans, they aren’t even worth a mention.”

She eyed Zu An with a frown. “On that note, I have never seen a human as strong as you.”

“I guess I’m just lucky,” Zu An replied in self-derision. I guess human civilization is still in its elementary phase. 

“Where’s Lady Nuwa now?” Zu An wanted to gather more information.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head. “It has been a long time since she last showed herself. Some say she was conferred sainthood for her merit in creating humans, but there are also those who claim she has already passed away. There is no way to tell which is true.”

“Sainthood…” Zu An fell into contemplation. “Sainthood is the level that the Celestial Emperors are seeking to attain, right?”

Zu An had also asked her this question back then, but she hadn’t answered. This time, she briefly hesitated before sharing, “Yes, they are pursuing the legendary sainthood.”

“What saints are there in the world?” Seeing that she was willing to answer his question, Zu An was determined to squeeze out as much information as he could. 

“Sainthood is an elusive level. The only ones said to have reached that level are the Green Emperor and Lady Nuwa. I can’t say for sure who else is a saint,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain explained. “Sainthood cannot be reached through rote cultivation. One has to find the dao suited for them and make it resonate with the great dao. Ever since ancient times, there have been many powerful entities, but many of them hit a bottleneck. They were only half a step away from achieving sainthood, yet they were unable to bridge the gap no matter what they do.”

Zu An mulled over those words. “Earlier, you mentioned that Lady Nuwa achieved sainthood from the merit of creating humans? Why is creating humans a significant feat? Aren’t the rulers of the world the Shaman races and the Fiend races?”

“Many are perplexed by that too. Humans are a weak race who are only decent in terms of reproductive prowess and learning ability.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned in confusion. “In terms of reproduction, they can’t compete with beasts. In terms of learning, no matter how they learn, there’s no way they can compete with the Shaman races and Fiend races’ powerful bloodlines.”

At this point, she turned to Zu An with an apologetic look. “I don’t mean to insult you. In fact, your appearance made me realize that human potential could be unlimited. You were able to grasp the Fiend races’ cultivation skills and learn the Shaman races’ bloodline ability.”

Zu An knew she was referring to the shaman blood essence he had received from Houtu. “Goddess, please be assured that I’m not bothered by those words. I think I have a rough idea how Lady Nuwa reached sainthood. Humans must have prospered in the future, and this huge merit went back to her.”

“What makes you so certain that humans will prosper in the future?” The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at Zu An with her beautiful eyes.

“I saw it in a dream.” Zu An couldn’t tell her that he had transmigrated here from the future. This world had deities and the Celestial Court; he was worried that the revelation that he was from the future could lead to unexpected dangers. 

The Mermaid Queen looked at the Goddess of Wu Mountain with a conflicted expression. I got ahead of myself. I felt jealous seeing her chatting along so well with big brother Zu, so I forgot that we aren’t from the same era. On that note, I’m also not from the same era as big brother Zu.

She felt disappointed, and she looked at the Goddess of Wu Mountain with empathetic eyes. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain chuckled. “Don’t put yourself down. Cultivators beyond a certain level may see revelations in their dreams. Your dream may come true in the future, and you could become an important figure for the humans.” The moonlight shone on her face, causing her fair skin to glow with mesmerizing radiance. 

Zu An was taken aback. I didn’t know I could use such an explanation too.

The Mermaid Queen’s interest was piqued. “Goddess, what are the different cultivation levels in this world? What’s the Celestial Emperor’s cultivation level?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain had refused to answer this question before, but there was no reason for her to withhold the information now. “Mortals, be it through cultivation or fortuitous encounters, can ascend to immortality and reach the level of Sky Immortal. Above Sky Immortals are True Immortals, followed by High Immortals. High Immortal is the limit for most people.

“Above High Immortals are the Golden Immortals. If there’s an insurmountable gap between mortals and Sky Immortals, the same gap lies between Golden Immortals and other immortals. Most Celestial Emperors are at that level.”

Zu An wondered what cultivation level he was at. I only ascended not long ago, but I have a feeling that I’m stronger than most Sky Immortals. I’m nowhere on par with the Celestial Emperors, so I guess I should be at the level of a True Immortal or a High Immortal.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued her explanation, “Above the Golden Immortals are the Overarching Golden Immortals. Such entities are a mere half step away from sainthood. Most retired Celestial Emperors are able to reach the level of Overarching Golden Immortals, but whether they reach sainthood or not depends on their fate. That is the level closest to sainthood that I’m aware of…”

That’s pretty similar to the novel settings I saw in my previous life. How cliche, though I guess that makes it easy to remember. Zu An wondered if this similarity was just a coincidence, or if there was something more to it. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain pointed toward the distance and said, “That’s Genesis Mountain. The shamans residing there are known for their ability to forge artifacts, and many renowned weapons are created there. It’s rumored that the sound of their hammering can be heard even from fifty kilometers away…”

The Mermaid Queen was perplexed. “Why is it so quiet?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. She could tell that something was amiss too.

Zu An’s expression darkened. “I sense the aura of death.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2561: Bizarre Prayer
chapters.body:
Chapter 2561: Bizarre Prayer

Due to being the master of the underworld, Zu An had a heightened sensitivity toward death, even though it hadn’t been established in this world yet. 

The mountain was well-lit, but it was so quiet that it felt eerie under the darkness of the night. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain grimly warned, “Be careful.”

She commanded the cloud beneath them to accelerate. However, as they approached Genesis Mountain, a wave of heat surged at them. Even the cloud they were riding on started slowly dissipating.

Zu An frowned. It felt as if nothing ever good happened whenever he encountered such intense heat. 

With a wave of her sleeves, the Goddess of Wu Mountain released water vapor, causing the cloud to become more condensed. “Genesis Mountain is a giant volcano. The shamans turned the volcano into a massive furnace that could be used to forge the strongest weapons in the world.” She gestured to the nearby mountains and continued, “The earth flame from the main peak is drawn to the surrounding 72 peaks to form smaller furnaces.”

By then, Zu An and the Mermaid Queen could already see Genesis Mountain’s main peak, which had a circular opening. A crimson light shone from the opening, dyeing the surrounding sky the color of blood. The other 72 peaks were positioned in a formation around the main peak. Zu An could sense light-blue ancient runes inscribed on the mountains. They resonated with Genesis Mountain’s main peak to form a massive formation that allowed earth flames to flow safely and smoothly. 

There was a strong, stinging smell in the air—it was the smell of metal, precious material, and sulfur. Who knew how many overpowered weapons had been born here?

As they got closer, the Goddess of Wu Mountain was no longer able to maintain her cloud, so they had no choice but to stop on a nearby mountain peak. 

Zu An looked at the formations flickering in the sky and said, “The protection formation is still functioning. It would have attacked us if we continued flying straight in.”

“That’s weird. How did something happen when the protection formation is still functioning normally?” The Mermaid Queen surveyed their surroundings. It had been some time since they arrived in Genesis Mountain’s vicinity, but they hadn’t seen a single shaman.

“It is bizarre. There should have been battle marks if it was an invasion.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain mulled it over. “The Genesis Shamans amassed a sea of wealth from forging weapons, and that drew unwanted attention upon them. However, the Genesis Shamans have powerful weapons and defensive formations that are also strong enough to deter most troublemakers. Several renowned experts invaded Genesis Mountain, only to suffer tragic deaths. Since then, no one has dared to target this place.” She couldn’t make sense of the situation either.

“Let’s head in to take a look.” Among the trio, Zu An had the best grasp of the situation. The death aura inside Genesis Mountain was extremely dense. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded. She led the way, as she had been here before.

The surrounding 72 mountain peaks were connected to the main peak via suspension bridges. Red rivers flowed beneath the bridges, but on second glance, they were made of flowing lava. Smoke lingered in the air. The suspension bridges were made out of black metal and wooden panels. Walking on them seared one’s legs; it was a wonder that the bridges hadn’t burned up yet. 

While Zu An was wondering how the Genesis Shamans ensured the suspension bridges’ safety, he noticed the Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly stopping. He asked, “What’s wrong?”

“It’s nothing.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued walking while casually fanning herself and wiping off her sweat. Her clothes were a bit more open than usual, revealing her snowy skin. 

“Big brother Zu, why are you so dense? I’m from the Ocean races, and her Wu Mountain is manifested from a fog. We don’t do well in such hot environments,” the Mermaid Queen tugged Zu An’s sleeve and secretly reminded him. 

Zu An looked at the Mermaid Queen. A hair strand was sticking to her face, and her cheeks were flushed red. Realization finally dawned on him. He secretly used the Snow Phoenix’s ability to cool down the surrounding air. Snowflakes even began drifting down. 

“How beautiful.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain instinctively reached out to touch a snowflake, only to realize right after that Zu An was caring for her. She turned to him and said, “Thank you.”

“You've helped me a lot along the way. This is nothing compared to that.” Zu An smiled. 

His smile was so warm that the Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t dare to look him in the eye. She averted her gaze as she remarked, “It’s very rare for someone to be able to control multiple kinds of elemental ki like you.”

Zu An was surprised. “Is it the norm to only be able to use one element?”

Most cultivators in the future also specialized in a single element. Could it be the same for the more powerful ancient era too?

“Not exactly. Most shamans and fiends have powerful bloodline powers, so they often specialize in a single field and develop it to its maximum potential. It’s rare for someone to simultaneously cultivate multiple elemental abilities without suffering any setbacks… Is this due to humans’ learning ability too?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain wondered.

By this point, they had already reached the main peak. 

Genesis Mountain had been modified into a nine-leveled forging platform. Nine was the number of extremity, which was why many world structures tended to involve the number nine. Each level consisted of a vast space filled with forging stands, a market, and residences, resembling a miniature city. The space became smaller the higher up one went, but there were also improvements in the scale of the forging stands and the extravagance of the residences. Going up the forging platform meant an increase in standing. 

The suspension bridge led them to the fifth level, but there wasn't a single person to be seen. The massive city felt like a ghost town.

They made their way up to the highest level before coming to a halt. The ninth level was much smaller than the levels below, but it was still a vast area. At its center was a massive copper sculpture that was over three hundred meters tall, depicting a shaman holding a hammer. 

Had Zu An and the others seen this sculpture under normal circumstances, they would have been awed by its craftsmanship. However, their attention was instead drawn to the ground. 

Countless people were kneeling before the sculpture. Their clothes suggested that they were the Genesis Shamans. It looked as if they were praying to the copper sculpture with faint smiles. And yet, this seemingly heart-warming sight felt sinister, because all of them were dead.

The Mermaid Queen leaned toward Zu An and murmured, “Big brother Zu, their plight resembles that of the Chang clan.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2562: A Friend
chapters.body:
Chapter 2562: A Friend

The many Genesis Shamans gathered around the sculpture looked calm; there were even faint smiles on their faces. It felt unnerving.

Zu An squatted down to inspect the nearest Genesis Shaman’s body. “He's been dead for 12 to 16 hours.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. “How do you know?”

Zu An replied, “His body has turned cold and stiffened. You can see marks that don’t disappear under pressure all over his body. These are signs that appear on those who have been dead for around twelve hours. Beyond that, the body will start loosening up.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was awed. “You’re well-educated, to know such information.”

“It’s all the wisdom of the predecessors.” Zu An smiled. There had been so many detective shows in his previous life; it wasn't that difficult to pick up bits and pieces of information. Besides, he had also picked up some things during his time as an Embroidered Envoy.  

The Goddess of Wu Mountain murmured contemplatively, “I have underestimated the humans’ ability to learn.”

Sensing the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s amazed gaze, the Mermaid Queen puffed her chest up in pride, but she soon felt troubled once more. It’s a good thing that my lover is outstanding, but it’s also troublesome because he’s too outstanding, so he naturally draws in other women. 

Zu An walked to the center of the crowd, where there was a Genesis Shaman who was standing instead of kneeling like the others. It looked as if he was about to bow down and pay his respects. This shaman was old, but he was more muscular than the others. His clothes were also visibly more opulent. 

“He’s the Genesis Shaman race’s chieftain, Yanshan. I have met him before,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain explained with a pale face. 

“The Genesis Shamans feel rather strong, as you mentioned.” Zu An could sense the valiance and strength of the Genesis Shamans even though they had already taken their last breaths. 

“The experts of the Genesis Shaman race are all gathered here; this is a force to be reckoned with. What power could claim all their lives in an instant?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain wondered with a frown.

Zu An inspected the surroundings before rubbing his hands together. “Their souls are nowhere to be found. They must have died to that Myriad Souls Banner.”

“The forging of the Myriad Souls Banner must have been a success.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain turned increasingly paler. She understood what this all signified. 

“Even the Myriad Souls Banner shouldn’t be able to kill so many experts without them fighting back. And you mentioned earlier that this place is guarded by a powerful protection formation!” the Mermaid Queen exclaimed. 

“Indeed. The Myriad Souls Banner might be formidable, but it doesn’t have the power to quietly siphon so many souls at once. The Genesis Shamans should have had time to activate their protection formation to guard against the invader.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain contemplated. “Unless the culprit was an insider who attacked them while they were having a mass prayer.”

“Or the culprit is someone the Genesis Shamans weren’t guarded against,” Zu An added. 

While they were speculating on the possibilities, a commotion suddenly echoed in the distance. 

“Hm? All the Genesis Shamans are gathered here?”

“Their expressions look weird.”

“They seem to be all dead!”

The voices initially sounded confused, but they were quickly overtaken by shock. 

Zu An and the others looked over. 

A group had just entered the ninth level from another direction. Their leader was draped in a scarlet cloak, and his robe was embroidered with fiery auspicious clouds. A fiery-red crimson jade was fastened to his waist. He had defined facial features and deep, penetrating eyes. His eyebrows resembled flickering flames, and he wielded a copper flame axe. Flames seemed to quietly seethe around him, making him look so imposing that it was hard to look straight at him.

Zu An narrowed his eyes. He’s strong! I can feel immense pressure from him even when we’re this far away. 

The group also noticed Zu An’s group. “There are survivors!”

“They aren’t Genesis Shamans.”

“Are they the culprits behind this massacre?”

… 

Zu An’s eyes twitched. It was such a familiar scene. Kuafu and Xingtian also had the same misunderstanding the last time they were at the Chang residence. To avoid conflict, he quickly explained, “We just arrived here too and found them dead. May I know your names?”

Though it is a huge coincidence for them to appear here at the same time. Could they be the culprits trying to frame us? 

“Empty words! We shall capture and interrogate you to get to the bottom of this!” The red-cloaked leader harrumphed. At his order, several golden-armored warriors rushed at Zu An. 

Zu An was flabbergasted.

The Mermaid Queen was irked too. She began strumming her Deep Sea Harp, prompting the golden-armored warriors to fight among themselves.

Zu An was awed. These golden-armored warriors are nowhere weaker than her in terms of cultivation, so much so that she wouldn’t have been a match for them in a direct confrontation. Her powerful control and support skills make her more suited for chaotic battlefields. 

The red-cloaked man’s eyebrows shot up, and he roared, “What a measly trick!” 

His booming voice shook the golden-armored warriors out of their daze. The realization that someone had just made a fool out of them made the golden-armored warriors so angry that they charged at the Mermaid Queen. 

“Halt!” With a wave of her sleeves, the Goddess of Wu Mountain summoned fog that stopped the golden-armored warriors. “How can you resort to violence without getting to the bottom of the matter? Are you planning to silence us because you’re the real culprits?”

“Audacious! How dare you speak to me, a celestial, like that!” The red-cloaked man was infuriated. He put down his copper axe and unleashed two waves of fire dragons toward her. 

As beautiful as the Goddess of Wu Mountain was, he saw her as little more than a skeleton wearing makeup. He'd noticed that the two ladies controlled the water element, which made him the perfect match against them.

Even before the two fire dragons could reach the Goddess of Wu Mountain, the shirts and hair of the deceased Genesis Shamans began curling up. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. His flames are formidable! Each fire dragon harnesses terrifying destructive prowess, not to mention that this environment is disadvantageous to me. I might not be able to take this attack.

Just then, Zu An appeared before her. He quickly formed a series of hand seals and unleashed a giant lotus against the two fire dragons. This terrain greatly boosts the prowess of fire ki; I can use fire to vanquish fire. 

A loud explosion ensued. The two fire dragons and the lotus disappeared at the same time. The fog hindering the golden-armored warriors’ movements was vaporized, and some nearby Genesis Shaman corpses were burnt to a crisp. 

Zu An and the other frowned. 

“White Lotus Flame and Phoenix Flame... I see, you possess the phoenix bloodline. No wonder you’re so arrogant!” the red-cloaked man sneered. He began rising into the air as the flames seething around him grew increasingly tangible. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He suddenly recalled the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s warning. Considering how hostile he is toward phoenixes, could he be a descendant of the dragons? 

Even so, this was no time to be thinking too hard. With the other party’s aura intensifying furiously, he knew he also had to go all out to survive this encounter. 

Just then, a coy voice exclaimed, “Stop! He’s a friend!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2563: Disappeared
chapters.body:
Chapter 2563: Disappeared

Zu An looked over in delight.

A white-robed woman had lifted her skirt and was frantically running over. Her black hair was casually twisted into a bun on her head. A couple of loose hair strands hung next to her ears, contrasting with her fair, slender neck. She had peach blossom eyes that would seem to be brimming with seductiveness even when looking at a dog, though the more palpable emotions that could be felt from them now were delight and excitement. 

Her face was reddened from her sprinting. Perhaps due to her robe tightening around her waist, her chest bounced more than usual due to her movements. It added a tender touch to the harsh environment of Genesis Mountain.

“Yuqing!” Zu An was overjoyed. He hadn’t expected to meet her here. For a moment, he was worried that she was just a lookalike like the Goddess of Wu Mountain, but the familiar soft touch when she pounced into his arms made him realize that he hadn’t misidentified her. 

“Big brother Zu…” Yun Yuqing buried her face in Zu An’s chest as she sobbed. She embraced Zu An with great force, as if fearing that he would disappear if she loosened her grip. 

Zu An asked, “Yuqing, what are you doing here?”

He looked around, but Ji Xiaoxi and Jiang Luofu were nowhere to be found. This unnerved him. When they first arrived at the spacetime fragment, the three of them had decided to move together, but unfortunately, he'd been unable to find them afterward. He had entered this world where the Celestial Court existed via the Fiend races’ imperial tomb, so how had Yun Yuqing gotten here?

Many doubts surfaced in Zu An’s mind. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain glanced at the Mermaid Queen. Yet another gorgeous woman has leaped into her lover’s arms, but why is she not angry in the slightest?

The Mermaid Queen was conflicted. Zu An had told her about his companions before, so she was mentally prepared for this scenario. However, she hadn’t expected them to reunite under such circumstances. 

This woman is truly beautiful, but again, a woman who catches big brother Zu’s eyes can’t possibly be ordinary. How should I introduce myself to her later? Should I say that I just got married to big brother Zu? Would that make me sound like a vixen?

“Miss Yun, who’s this guy?” The man shrouded in flames descended to their side. He glanced at Zu An with wary eyes, particularly since Yun Yuqing was embracing him. 

“He’s my husband,” Yun Yuqing was embarrassed, but she still gathered up her courage to say so.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain lifted her eyebrows. She secretly glanced at the Mermaid Queen once more. Oyoy, that woman is proclaiming her dominance now. Hm? Why does it seem as if I can’t wait for them to fight each other?

The Mermaid Queen frowned. I was hesitating about whether to first assert my dominance, but she beat me to it. 

Zu An was perplexed too. “Yuqing, who is this?”

The man took a courteous tone with Yun Yuqing, and it didn’t seem to be because he wanted to court her. Rather, his respect for her came from the bottom of his heart. This is weird, especially considering the huge difference in their strength. What’s their relationship?

“This is the Celestial Court’s Fire Celestial Lord Wuhui,” Yun Yuqing said. “He saved me when I was in danger.”

“Fire Celestial Wuhui? Celestial Court?” Zu An was taken aback. No wonder those golden-armored warriors look so familiar. They're celestial soldiers!

The Goddess of Wu Mountain sent a telepathic message to Zu An to explain, “The Celestial Court’s Fire Celestial governs the world’s flames. It is a highly respected position. Zhongli was the previous Fire Celestial, but he returned to the great dao while separating heaven from earth. The Celestial Court might have called Wuhui back to take the position.”

Zu An nodded. No wonder his flames are so formidable. So it turns out he’s the Fire Celestial. 

Wuhui assessed Zu An and remarked approvingly, “Miss Yun, your husband is formidable.”

But Zu An was more concerned with what Yun Yuqing had just mentioned. “You said you met with danger. What happened? And how did you appear… here?”

Yun Yuqing bit her lips. “After we parted ways, big sister Jiang, little sister Xiaoxi, and I investigated the whereabouts of the Brilliant Pinflower. We soon learned that it could be located in the Fiend races’ imperial tomb, so we made our way over to take a look. However, the area was already occupied by the monsters, so we could only continue our search discreetly.

“We eventually found a chance to slip into the Fiend races’ imperial tomb, but we were suddenly struck by a white light that caused the surrounding space to be plunged into disarray. Before we knew it, we were already in this world.”

The white light likely refers to the opening of the dungeon. They must have been caught off guard and were swept in as well. But this is a good thing too. It would be disastrous if they were caught by the Monster Lord or the Lord of Slaughter. 

“Where are Principal Jiang and Xiaoxi?”

Yun Yuqing’s eyes reddened. “Ever since we arrived in this world, we were treading carefully to understand what was going on, as well as looking for a way to return. But we stumbled upon a mysterious altar, and Xiaoxi’s Cruel Poison Constitution was suddenly triggered by a mysterious power…”

“What?!” Zu An was horrified. He knew how potent Ji Xiaoxi’s Cruel Poison Constitution was, and the Firecloud Cave’s master had just warned him that once the Cruel Poison Constitution flared up, the chances of survival were slim. 

The absence of Ji Xiaoxi and Jiang Luofu caused his heart to lurch. 

“We tried to suppress Ji Xiaoxi’s Cruel Poison Constitution, but to no avail. A mysterious man suddenly arrived then, and he said he was delighted that such a constitution existed in this world. He helped stabilize Ji Xiaoxi’s condition,” Yun Yuqing replied.

How strong must that person be to suppress the Cruel Poison Constitution?

“We were initially thankful to him, but it turns out that he merely coveted Xiaoxi’s unique constitution. He first kidnapped her before attempting to kill us.” Yun Yuqing’s face paled. The thought of what had happened then frightened her. “Fortunately, we were able to survive thanks to your protection artifacts, the Last Breath Talisman, and big sister Jiang’s scale artifact.”

Zu An’s expression darkened. That mysterious man was vicious enough to try to take their lives just like that.

“Where’s Principal Jiang?” he anxiously asked. 

“When we woke up, we were in despair. That mysterious man was too powerful. We wouldn’t have been a match for him no matter how many more years we cultivated.” Yun Yuqing glanced at Zu An. “If only we'd known that you were in this world too back then.”

She continued, “Big sister Jiang sees Xiaoxi as her daughter. She mumbled that she would pay any price to save Xiaoxi. She told me a bunch of things, as if entrusting her final will to me. Then one day, by the time I woke up, she'd suddenly disappeared.”

“Disappeared?” Zu An widened his eyes. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2564: Clearing The Fog
chapters.body:
Chapter 2564: Clearing The Fog

“Yes, she disappeared.” Yun Yuqing was also confused when she recalled the situation.

“Try recalling the situation. Given your cultivation, even the slightest breeze shouldn’t have escaped your notice,” Zu An urged. 

“I searched the area, but there were no traces of anyone else.” Yun Yuqing recalled the details of the situation. “If I have to point out anything peculiar, I think I heard big sister Jiang making a wish. I was too tired, and I thought she was wishing for Xiaoxi’s safety, so I didn’t pay it much heed and went to sleep. Later, I was jolted awake by a nearby flash of white light. By then, big sister Jiang was already gone. I don’t know if she had long disappeared by then, or if she disappeared along with the white light.”

“White light?” Zu An frowned. The problem probably lies with the white light.

“Can you describe the white light?” Fire Celestial Wuhui suddenly asked. 

“I don’t know much because I was still in a groggy state. I merely saw a brilliant flash.” Yun Yuqing tried her best to jog her memory as she spoke. “The white light was bright, but not glaring. I felt small and insignificant, as if I was a speck of dirt in the universe. It was an odd feeling.”

“Oh?” Wuhui spent a moment in thought. “It sounds as if a celestial might have descended. You said you overheard her making a wish, right? Sometimes, when an entity makes a strong wish, it can draw the attention of a celestial.”

Yun Yuqing was overjoyed to hear that. “You’re the Fire Celestial, right? Can you help me investigate which celestial was summoned?”

Wuhui puffed up his chest. “Leave it to me. But due to the separation of heaven and earth, I’m unable to contact the Celestial Court for the time being, so I can only help you look into it when I return to the Celestial Court in the future.”

His willingness to help convinced Zu An to drop the grudge he had against Wuhui for recklessly attacking him. “Thank you, Lord Fire Celestial.”

Wuhui eyed Zu An with a confused frown. “How weird. You’re a human. When did someone as powerful as you appear among the humans?”

“It’s a coincidence.” Zu An couldn’t explain his background. 

Yun Yuqing knew his background was a secret too, so she changed the topic. “Xiaoxi was captured, and big sister Jiang went missing, so I could only roam the world while searching for them. However, I was unable to do anything at all…”

Sensing her low spirits, Zu An gently grabbed her hand. She must have felt helpless, being transported into a foreign world filled with powerful entities while watching her companions disappear one after another. On top of that, she didn’t know I’m in this world too. She must have felt lonely. 

Feeling the warmth of Zu An’s hand, Yun Yuqing calmed down a little. “Later, I chanced upon Lord Fire Celestial. He said that I have the purest Fiend races’ bloodline and helped me a lot along the way.” 

“Fiend races?” Zu An was surprised. Isn’t she a demon? 

Hold on, the Monster World’s True Demon race often bragged about being the descendants of the Celestial Court’s Fiend races. Could it be that Yun Yuqing’s Demon race are the true descendants of the Celestial Court’s Fiend races instead?

Sensing his confusion, Wuhui explained, “Miss Yun is the Green Emperor’s descendant. Her bloodline is so pure as to be rare even among Celestial Emperors. I was curious why there are no records of her in the Celestial Court, so I was planning to bring her to the Celestial Court and have the Celestial Emperor take a look at her.”

Noticing Zu An’s wary eyes, he added with a harrumph, “Calm down, there’s no faking her bloodline. This is a fortuitous encounter for her.”

“I understand. Thank you, Lord Fire Celestial.” Zu An bowed with a smile.

Wuhui’s complexion finally looked a little better. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly echoed in Zu An’s ears, “Wuhui only gave you half the picture. It is a huge fortuitous encounter for her, but the same cannot be said for you. Based on what I know, the Celestial Court forbids romance between celestials and humans. They wouldn’t allow someone of her pure bloodline to be sullied by a human.”

Zu An glanced at the Goddess of Wu Mountain, only to see that her lips were still, and she wasn’t even looking at him. She was secretly warning him of the pitfall. He chuckled softly and replied telepathically, “I’d only be happy for her if she could have such a fortuitous encounter…”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was surprised. Does he intend to give up on this relationship? That would be a gentleman’s way of doing things, but it feels regretful. 

But Zu An quickly continued, “Even so, no one can get in the way of our relationship, not even the Celestial Court. If it comes to that, I’ll just turn everything upside down.”

“You mustn’t say such blasphemous words! If the Celestial Court learned about it… Putting all things aside, this Fire Celestial would fight you to the death!” The Goddess of Wu Mountain was horrified, and she quickly warned him. But at the same time, a faint smile formed on her lips. This man is indeed different from the others. There’s some kind of vibrancy and wildness to him that always catches others off guard. 

“What are you doing here? How did these Genesis Shamans die?” Wuhui asked. 

Yun Yuqing knew the man, and judging from their conversation, he had learned that one of the two ladies was the lofty Goddess of Wu Mountain. These made them unlikely to be the culprits. Though I heard the goddess usually holes herself up on Wu Mountain. What brings her here?

“We came here to investigate another case…” Zu An explained the situation to Wuhui. 

“Myriad Souls Banner!” Wuhui’s flames suddenly flared up. “Who dares to commit such a vile and despicable deed?! I’ll make sure his soul burns for ten thousand years if I ever find him!”

“I’ll have to trouble the Celestial Court to look into this matter.” Zu An had been planning on handing this matter to the Celestial Court in the first place. 

“We will take responsibility for this issue, but unfortunately, we are unable to reach the Celestial Court at the moment. For the time being, I’ll investigate it by myself,” Wuhui said. The separation of heaven and earth had caused great inconveniences for many issues. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. He turned to Yun Yuqing and asked, “Speaking of which, what brings you here?”

“We were investigating the mysterious man who kidnapped Xiaoxi. We stumbled upon a clue and rushed here to see if we could find him. I didn’t expect to see you here.” Yun Yuqing was heartened. It was even more moving to reunite with her lover in a foreign land. 

The Mermaid Queen suddenly spoke up. “Could the mysterious man who kidnapped Miss Ji be the same person who is forging the Myriad Souls Banner? The goddess previously mentioned that forging a powerful Myriad Souls Banner would require the concoction of special poisons.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2565: All Clues Are Before Us
chapters.body:
Chapter 2565: All Clues Are Before Us

Zu An’s expression darkened. He'd been so happy to be reunited with Yun Yuqing that he only thought about this possibility when the Mermaid Queen mentioned it. He turned to the Goddess of Wu Mountain and asked with a quivering voice, “What will happen to the person with the Cruel Poison Constitution during the forging of the Myriad Souls Banner?”

“Don’t worry. The person with the Cruel Poison Constitution will only be assimilated into the Myriad Souls Banner at the final step of the forging to maximize its prowess. The forging isn’t at its final step yet, so Miss Ji shouldn’t be in danger yet,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain consoled him, knowing what he was worried about.

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. 

Even though the Chang clan and the Genesis Shaman race had likely been wiped out by the Myriad Souls Banner, it was still considered a small-scale massacre compared to what it was truly capable of. There were legends that when a true Myriad Souls Banner appeared, the sky would darken and all lives would be assimilated. 

Wuhui also said, “I have heard some rumors about the Myriad Souls Banner. Its flagpole is usually made of thousand-year-old yin wood from Scholar Trees or coffin wood. However, this Myriad Souls Banner is a special one. Its flagpole is made from yin wood that has been soaked with malevolent energies from ancient battlefields, or corpse ki, for at least ten thousand years.

“The banner normally has to be embroidered with human skin, but neither the Chang clansmen nor the Genesis Shamans have been skinned, so the culprit must have opted for another method. The other alternative is to use cloth stripped from powerful corpses overflowing with resentment.

“Other than that, the Myriad Souls Banner requires a core—a powerful vengeful spirit dripping with resentment to control other vengeful spirits. The mysterious man has likely chosen your friend as the main vengeful spirit. Your friend might be lacking in strength, but her unique poison constitution makes up for that. If the forging succeeds, the resulting Myriad Souls Banner will be far stronger than any the world has ever seen. Wherever it appears, countless will die.”

“That means Xiaoxi is in danger!” Zu An was alarmed.

“It takes 81 days to forge the core. Miss Yun, how many days has it been since your companion was kidnapped?” Wuhui asked Yun Yuqing.

Yun Yuqing’s face turned pale. “It has been 63 days.”

“Then you still have 18 days to rescue your friend,” Wuhui said. “We can investigate based on the materials I have just mentioned.”

But Zu An wasn’t as optimistic. While it took 81 days to forge the banner core, each day that passed meant another day of suffering for Xiaoxi. Furthermore, the forging of the banner core was a malevolent process. There was no saying whether Xiaoxi would still be alive near the end of the forging process. 

“Yuqing, what abilities did the mysterious man use when he kidnapped Xiaoxi? Was there anything unusual?” Zu An knew Wuhui’s solution was unfeasible; Xiaoxi wouldn’t last that long. 

Wuhui harrumphed. “Do you think I wouldn’t have asked that? The strength disparity between Miss Yun and the mysterious man is so great that there would have been no way for her to force the other party to reveal his specialization.”

Indeed, there was nothing useful in what Yun Yuqing described afterward. It was just like a human stomping on a roadside ant; how could the ant tell how much strength the human used and his posture when stomping down?

Zu An fell into deep thought to organize the information he had gathered thus far.

The Chang clan’s massacre. Yumen Beiqing’s disappearance. The Genesis Shaman race’s bizarre death. All of these were likely tied to the forging of the Myriad Souls Banner…

Hold on, I feel as if I've neglected something important. 

He quickly racked his brains, and his eyes eventually fell on the Genesis Shamans. A thought popped into his mind. “I thought we had no more clues left, but the biggest clue is right before our eyes.” 

“What do you mean?” The others were taken aback.

Zu An slowly explained, “We came here to investigate because of that metal cauldron fragment. The Genesis Shamans forged a unique cauldron for that mysterious man. What does he need the cauldron for when he already has the Myriad Souls Banner?”

“Cauldron?” Wuhui was confused. “What cauldron?”

Zu An took out the metal fragment. When he saw the human face imprinted on it, Wuhui’s complexion darkened. “It’s a living soul!”

“Indeed. That mysterious man not only forged a Myriad Souls Banner, but also requested the Genesis Shamans to forge a cauldron for collecting living souls for him,” Zu An said. “This means his ambitions are greater than what we have imagined.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was alarmed. “Just the Myriad Souls Banner is enough to lay waste to the world. What does he need such a cauldron for?”

Wuhui’s face turned livid. “I have heard about a legendary formation that requires these two items. However, that formation requires an entire world of living beings as tribute. Who has the gall and capability to pull off something like that?

“Furthermore, the Myriad Souls Banner required for that formation needs more than just someone with the Cruel Poison Constitution; it requires at least nine other powerful vengeful spirits too. But where can you find so many powerful individuals who harbor great resentment during their moment of death? The Celestial Court would have heard about it if such a thing happened.”

He kept talking as if he had thought of something terrifying, but wanted to refute his conjecture. 

The golden-armored warriors paled upon hearing his words. Patrolling the mortal world usually gave them a sense of self-importance, as everyone else was a country bumpkin compared to them. But this case far surpassed their wildest imagination. It could even be beyond the capacity of the Fire Celestial to deal with. 

Zu An walked up to the Genesis Shaman chieftain as he said, “We overlooked an important issue earlier. Why did the Genesis Shamans agree to forge the cauldron for that mysterious man?”

According to the Goddess of Wu Mountain, the Genesis Shaman race was decently powerful. Many years ago, someone could still be able to coerce them into forging an item for them, but that should no longer have been an issue.

The Mermaid Queen’s heart skipped a beat. “Are you saying that the Genesis Shamans did it willingly?”

“Indeed. It doesn’t look as if they were coerced into it.” Zu An nodded.

“That doesn’t make sense. A cauldron that captures living souls can only be used for malicious purposes. The Genesis Shaman race, on the whole, still belongs to the lawful camp. How could they have willingly forged something like that?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed.

“There are two possibilities. One, they didn’t know the true purpose of the cauldron, which is unlikely given that they were master blacksmiths. This leaves the second possibility.” Zu An looked at the Genesis Shamans kneeling respectfully to the sculpture. “The mysterious man’s identity was significant enough to dispel the Genesis Shamans’ doubts, such that they were willing to forge a malevolent artifact for him.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2566: Leaving A Hand
chapters.body:
Chapter 2566: Leaving A Hand

Zu An walked into the center of a group of Genesis Shamans and copied their kneeling posture. “They aren’t kneeling to the sculpture to pray. No, they're kneeling to someone who is of a lofty standing.”

The others were astonished to hear that. They quickly tried kneeling alongside the Genesis Shamans and looking toward the sculpture too—their expressions slowly turned grim. Clearly, they agreed with his deduction.

“Only a handful of people can inspire such respect from the Genesis Shamans,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said. Her respect for Zu An deepened a little. 

They had been still completely clueless just a second ago, but he was able to gather so much crucial information from these minor details. She had always preferred to maintain serenity, but a little ripple of emotions spread through her heart that had remained still for many years.

“Could it be an influential celestial from the Celestial Court?” Zu An asked Wuhui, in hopes that the latter could help to brainstorm as to who fit the criteria. 

Wuhui was infuriated. “Nonsense! The Celestial Court has no such conspirators! I bet the shamans are behind this! Only another shaman could command such worship from the Genesis Shaman race.” 

You have successfully trolled Wuhui for +299… +299… +299...

The Mermaid Queen interjected, “The Shaman races have unbridled personalities and wouldn’t easily submit to others, especially their peers. Other than Lady Houtu and the other Ancestral Shamans, there is no shaman who can inspire such respect from the Genesis Shaman race. Do you really think they could be the masterminds?”

Even though she wasn’t a true shaman, she had spent a period of time in the Chang clan as their Saintess, which gave her some understanding of the Shaman races. She felt closer to the Shaman races, so she couldn’t help but speak up for them. 

Wuhui’s flames flared, but he didn’t dare to reply yes despite his anger. He was the Fire Celestial, but he, too, could land in hot water if he recklessly accused those lofty entities of a crime. 

Zu An’s mind churned into action. Could it be the Ancestral Shamans? Lady Houtu, for instance, is suspicious. She is the reason that the Mermaid Queen and I are here. Despite knowing the truth behind the Chang clan, she chose to withhold the truth. 

There’s also Gonggong, who challenged the Celestial Emperor earlier at Zhong Mountain. 

He couldn’t help but remember how he had witnessed the final generation Gonggong being sealed in the dungeon. In contrast, this generation’s Gonggong felt like a villain. 

Of course, the Celestial Court was full of suspects too.

“We have narrowed down our suspects, but we’re still lacking a clue to further our investigation. Against entities of this level, an accusation without evidence is meaningless.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at Zu An, wondering if the latter would bring them more surprises. 

It was also then that Yun Yuqing finally recovered from her rollercoaster of emotions and began paying attention to the two women beside Zu An. 

“Manman!” she exclaimed as she rushed to grab the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s hands. 

She had already lost touch with two close sisters in this foreign world. Seeing yet another familiar face here brought her great solace. The other woman is also ridiculously beautiful. Why does Manman allow him to go around flirting with other women when she’s with him?

And why does he have to get involved with such beautiful women all the time? That heaps so much pressure on me!

Unaccustomed to Yun Yuqing’s cordiality, the Goddess of Wu Mountain pulled back her hands. He wasn’t lying. He does have a woman named Manman who looks like me. Even Miss Yun, whom he had just reunited with, mistook me for her. 

“What’s wrong, Manman?” Yun Yuqing was taken aback by her distant attitude.

“She isn’t Manman. They just look alike. She is this world’s Goddess of Wu Mountain,” Zu An quickly clarified the situation. “Goddess, please don’t mind it…”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “I don’t mind it. Rather, I can tell that Miss Yun is on good terms with Miss Manman.”

She was curious why so many outstanding women were romantically involved with him, yet instead of harboring jealousy toward one another, they got along well like real sisters.

“Ah, so it’s the Goddess of Wu Mountain…” Yun Yuqing hurriedly apologized while assessing her. She sensed something amiss from how the other party looked at Zu An. Looks like I’m still underestimating big brother Zu.

“Why does this lady look so familiar? Miss Shang?” Yun Yuqing soon turned her attention to the Mermaid Queen. Even though the Mermaid Queen wore a veil, her features could still be made out when looking up close. 

“She also only resembles Liuyu; she’s the Chang clan’s Saintess Chang’e," Zu An said. It would be tough to explain the Mermaid Queen’s true identity with outsiders around. 

Wuhui and the golden-armored warriors were taken aback. This fellow is a player! How can he meet so many people who look like his lovers?

Even the Goddess of Wu Mountain couldn’t help but stare at Zu An. She would have never thought that even the Chang clan’s Saintess resembled his lover. This was starting to get a little ridiculous. 

The glares from all sides made Zu An gulp. He quickly interjected, “I thought of a clue.”

“What is it?”

That immediately diverted everyone’s attention.

Zu An pointed to the chieftain of the Genesis Shaman race. “Do you know where his room is?”

“This…” The Goddess of Wu Mountain had been to Genesis Mountain before, but she had never visited the chieftain’s room. 

Wuhui, however, replied with a harrumph, “That’s easy to figure out. The chieftain’s residence is bound to be grander than the others, and their race worships fire…”

The fire seething around him transformed into small fiery figures that scattered all over Genesis Mountain. There was no need to search the lower levels; there was no way the chieftain would live there. 

Soon, the small fire figures returned and whispered into Wuhui’s ears.

“It’s over there,” Wuhui said as he began making his way over.

The group followed him and soon arrived at a house. The gate had two ferocious beast knobs, and they were both forged out of Genesis Mountain’s copper. However, the gate was opened wide, revealing the interior. In the room were nine pillars positioned in a specific formation, and each of these pillars was wrapped in the skin of a renowned beast. They seemed to be room decor elements to highlight the chieftain’s valiance.

At the center of the room was a furnace. This furnace was linked to Genesis Mountain’s earth flame, so its fire would never go out. All sorts of smithing tools were placed neatly by the side. Even the chieftain was constantly upgrading his skills, it seemed. 

However, the room was a mess. It was as if someone had just burgled it.

Zu An walked up to the furnace and saw that it was full of ashes. There were several turtle shells inside too. The Shaman races used such things for divination. His heart sank. He'd thought that even if he chose to take the request to forge a malevolent artifact, the chieftain would have taken some precautions, so it would be possible to find clues in his room. 

However, the mysterious culprit appeared to have shared the same thoughts as him. 

Just then, a bizarre sound came from outside. The group looked up. A beast fang wind chime hanging on the roof outside was ringing despite the absence of wind. An earthquake suddenly ensued, as if something had come to life. 

“Fire Celestial, look outside…” the golden-armored warriors exclaimed in horror.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2567: The Ten Suns
chapters.body:
Chapter 2567: The Ten Suns

The group rushed out and saw that Genesis Mountain’s 72 peaks had lit up. Invisible waves rippled across the air as everything came to life. Totems carved with unique images lit up, forming a unique formation. 

“Don’t worry, that isn’t an offensive formation.” Wuhui calmed down the crowd. 

Zu An nodded. As expected of the Celestial Court’s Fire Celestial. He has a keen eye.

Suddenly, the sculptures on the 72 peaks each shot out two light beams from their eyes, and all the beams converged on the centermost volcano crater. Rumbles echoed, and the earth within a hundred-kilometer radius shook as if the volcano was about to erupt.

Wuhui looked at the crater and frowned. “The fire element ki has been triggered, but it’s not overly violent yet. It doesn’t look las if the volcano will erupt, though it won’t be a problem even if it erupts.”

Those words were spoken with a tinge of pride. 

The golden-armored warriors breathed a sigh of relief. Indeed, it should be a walk in the park for the Fire Celestial who governs over flames to curb an eruption. 

Lava suddenly spurted out from the crater. Everyone instinctively retreated, but the lava didn’t spill into the surroundings; instead, it morphed into a human silhouette.

“The Genesis Shaman chieftain!” the Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed. The forming lava silhouette was still blurry, but it wasn’t difficult to guess who he was based on his outline. 

“He isn’t dead yet?” Yun Yuqing was confused.

“He is dead. This is merely a projection he made when he was alive.” Wuhui stared at the lava silhouette warily, not knowing what the Genesis Shaman chieftain was up to.

The lava silhouette spoke up. “I should be dead by the time you see me.”

The crowd was astonished. Only Zu An was composed, having expected this.

“I am the Genesis Shamans' chieftain. An esteemed guest came not long ago and requested us to forge something,” the lava silhouette murmured with a contemplative face. “It would be the utmost honor of our Genesis Shaman race to forge something for that lord, but… we soon became hesitant when we realized that he wanted to forge a Soul Destroying Cauldron capable of amassing countless souls.

“I didn’t understand why someone as lofty and radiant as that lord would forge something so vile. I voiced my doubt, and he answered my question. He said he was planning to use it against evil scum and make a warning out of them. The explanation made sense, and I didn’t think someone of his stature would lie about a matter like this.

“But a question slowly dawned upon me as we began to forge the Soul Destroying Cauldron. Was such a huge cauldron required to deal with all the evil beings in the world? The cauldron was big enough to store all souls in the world…”

Those words alarmed everyone present. What does it mean to store all souls in the world? Just what is that enigmatic man up to?!

“Just thinking about the possibilities filled me with deep fear, but I couldn’t fathom why someone of his stature would do something like that. Could I be overthinking it? I have been making divinations using tortoise shells in recent days, but the more I divine, the worse the results become. If my deduction is true, that lord will not allow this matter to leak out. We’ll likely be silenced for the part we play in this matter.

“So, I left this recording behind. I made sure to switch off this mechanism every day to ensure that this recording wouldn’t be played. If a day came when I failed to switch off the mechanism, this recording would automatically play. From the looks of it, I must have been silenced in the end.”

The lava silhouette looked melancholic, and it left the group with heavy hearts.

“If that lord has chosen to silence me, he will likely have destroyed all possible clues too. My room must be a mess now.”

Zu An couldn’t help but smile. The chieftain sure is open-minded to crack such a joke when leaving his will behind. 

“But the things he destroyed were meaningless, as I have left the clues all over Genesis Mountain. Not even he can simply destroy all of Genesis Mountain, as that would catch the attention of the Celestial Court and the Shaman races. That’s the last thing he would want.”

Yun Yuqing panicked. “This fellow keeps prattling on, but he refuses to divulge the killer’s identity. How frustrating!”

Zu An patted her shoulder and said, “He must have his reason for doing so.”

At this point, the lava silhouette took a deep breath as if making up his mind. “I dare not reveal his name because that will draw his attention, and I don’t know if the person watching this recording has the power to deal with him. That would render all the preparations I have made meaningless. But if you’re smart enough, you should have already deciphered his identity, no?”

The lava silhouette blinked mischievously before vanishing.

Yun Yuqing was frustrated. “What’s with that? He ended up not saying anything at all!”

“No, he's already said it all,” Zu An, Wuhui, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied simultaneously with grim voices.

Wuhui and the Goddess of Wu Mountain glanced at Zu An. The two of them were of high standing, so it wasn't surprising for them to guess the culprit’s identity. 

Zu An stared at the dissipating lava silhouette.

The latter had mentioned that the culprit was of exalted standing and emanated an inviolable light. That suggested he was a powerhouse of the Celestial Court. On top of that, the chieftain had spoken so fearfully as if he was afraid he had misunderstood the other party. It was only when he could no longer turn his eyes away from the truth that he'd prepared this recording. There weren’t many people in the Celestial Court who fit these criteria. 

There was also a reason the chieftain had intentionally set this recording to play in the crater. When the lava silhouette first formed and later dissipated, it had vaguely formed the figure of a massive bird unfurling its wings. If one looked at it from a top-down angle, contrasted with the volcano’s crater, it resembled a divine bird amid the sun. 

The only one who fit all these criteria was Emperor Jun!

The Genesis Shaman race’s chieftain wasn't the only one who couldn’t make sense of the situation. Even Zu An was baffled. Emperor Jun was already chosen as the next Celestial Emperor, so why was he doing all these things?

In truth, Zu An had already guessed that Emperor Jun was the culprit based on the crystallized ground and lingering fire element ki in the area where Yi died, but he couldn’t confirm the matter. Only now was he certain that Emperor Jun was behind all this. 

Wuhui’s face darkened. “This is a huge matter. We must immediately inform the celestials.”

Since returning to the Celestial Court wasn’t an option right now, he could contact the more important celestials residing on earth. 

Zu An wondered how he could save Xiaoxi and the others when he noticed something.

Everyone present looked up at the sky. For some reason, it was many times brighter than before, and the surrounding air became searing hot, too. 

Ten suns had simultaneously appeared in the sky!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2568: Murdered
chapters.body:
Chapter 2568: Murdered
