“The Golden Crow Ten Princes!” Wuhui was both alarmed and infuriated. “How dare they! The Golden Crow Princes are supposed to come out in turn to serve as the world’s sun. There is a strict sequence to their appearances, so how can they appear together? Don’t they know that all lives will wither if they do this? This is against the celestial laws!”

Zu An was dazed. This wasn't his first time undergoing this event. Witnessing this scene once more felt unreal to him. 

He couldn’t help but ask, “I know that the separation of heaven and earth has temporarily severed connections between the Celestial Court and earth, but why doesn’t the Celestial Court react when ten suns have already risen in the sky?”

Wuhui’s complexion looked awful. “A day in heaven is a year on earth. That’s the gap between deities and mortals. Countless lives will have died by the time the Celestial Court notices the ten suns. This won’t do! I have to immediately inform the powerhouses on earth.”

Flames flared from Wuhui, and he was about to fly off when he noticed Yun Yuqing and called out, “Miss Yun, let’s go together.”

Knowing she would reject his offer, he added via telepathy, “Miss Yun, I know you can’t bear to part ways with your lover, but there’s no point staying by his side now. He has a goddess and a saintess with him now. Not only are they of a high stature, but they are also beauties who don’t pale in comparison to you. It’ll only make things awkward if you get in the middle of them now.”

Yun Yuqing was about to turn down Wuhui when she heard those words and hesitated.

Wuhui took this chance to press further. “All men have this bizarre mindset where the more noble a woman is, the more they subconsciously value them. I don’t doubt the deep bonds you share, but you’re at a disadvantage against those two women.”

Yun Yuqing lowered her gaze. Those words struck a chord in her heart.

“But you need not worry. Your bloodline is the noblest of them all; it's just that it hasn’t fully manifested. Come with us. I’ll restore your position. You shall become much nobler than them. You’ll be able to stand on equal terms with them by the time you return.” Knowing she was moved, Wuhui switched to a gentler tone and added, “We’re going to contact important celestials residing on earth right now. These connections may become invaluable in aiding your lover in the future.”

Yun Yuqing’s eyes lit up. She was finally convinced. “All right, but I’d like to have a private conversation with him first.”

Wuhui was in a good mood after convincing her. He had no reason to stop her, so he took his golden-armored warriors elsewhere to wait for her. Such a noble bloodline must not be sullied by a mere mortal. 

Seeing that they were going to talk, the Goddess of Wu Mountain and the Mermaid Queen tactfully stepped aside to give them some private space. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain hesitantly glanced at the Mermaid Queen before asking, “Don’t you feel envious seeing your man getting close to other women?”

The Mermaid Queen shook her head. “I’m already satisfied to be able to be with big brother.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. With her beauty and stature, she could have any man. I would never have expected someone this smart and rational to be so blind when it comes to love.

She didn’t understand it, but she was shaken. What sorcery does that man have to make such an outstanding woman fall head over heels for him?

The Mermaid Queen noticed her confusion, but didn’t explain it. She doesn’t understand the situation between me and big brother Zu. We’re from two different eras, so there is no future between us. All I can covet is a temporary union in this separate world.

She knew it would likely be an eternal farewell after departing from this world. That made their time together extremely precious. It was a waste to spend even a single second on jealousy.

Meanwhile, Yun Yuqing told Zu An what she had heard from Wuhui and smiled. “He underestimated our relationship. He thinks his words have moved me.”

Zu An was surprised. “But you're still choosing to go with them.”

Yun Yuqing put on a serious face and explained, “We have no comrades in this world. There are no disadvantages to forging friendly ties with the Celestial Court. We have to save Xiaoxi, so we will need all the help we can get.” 

Zu An didn’t stand on ceremony. They needed all the help they could get to rescue Xiaoxi, and this was a fortuitous encounter for Yun Yuqing, too. He took out two talismans and handed them to her. “Keep this location talisman and alarm talisman. Activate the alarm talisman if you’re in danger. As long as you have the location talisman with you, I’ll soon be by your side.”

“Ah? Are you that formidable now?” Yun Yuqing was overjoyed. She held the two talismans close to her chest.

“I recently grasped a new skill.” Zu An smiled. As long as he had the other party’s coordinates, he could quickly get there with the skill he had grasped from combining the Shaman race’s secret art and Rainbow Morph.

As for the location talisman and alarm talisman, he had developed those with Xie Daoyun. The thought of her made his heart sink. He wondered where Yumen Beiqing and the real little sister Ling’er were.

“What’s wrong, big brother Zu?” Yun Yuqing asked out of concern.

“It’s nothing.” Zu An smiled. Yun Yuqing was already agitated because of Xiaoxi; there was no need to add to her troubles. “Be careful. I can see that Wuhui harbors goodwill toward you, but the Celestial Court might not have a favorable view of our ties.”

Yu Yunqing understood what he was getting at. “Don’t worry. You’re more important to me than anything else.”

She leaped into Zu An’s arms and kissed him. Then, she ran off with a furiously blushing face. Ah, I didn’t think too much earlier, but kissing big brother Zu in front of those two ladies felt like I was exerting my dominance over him in front of them. It seems a little bitchy.

The Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t react much to the kiss, but Wuhui glared at Zu An with seething eyes. Miss Yun has fallen too deep in love. To preserve the purity of the Celestial Court’s bloodline, I mustn’t allow the two of them to meet!

You have successfully trolled Wuhui for +300… +300… +300…

He conjured a fire cloud with a wave of his hand before leaving with Yun Yuqing and his subordinates. Seeing Yun Yuqing look back at Zu An, he drove his fire cloud faster.

The fire cloud vanished astoundingly quickly. The Goddess of Wu Mountain walked to Zu An with a smile. “Looks like Wuhui is worried about Miss Yun changing her mind.”

“How can a pledge of eternal love unravel that easily?” Zu An looked at the Mermaid Queen, and the two of them exchanged smiles as they grabbed each other’s hands.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was speechless. Wow, how can there be such a seamless switch? It doesn’t feel off at all!

She quickly dispelled those miscellaneous thoughts and said, “All evidence points at that man. His high stature makes him difficult to deal with. Why don’t you follow me to Wu Mountain? I’ll request the Kaiming Six Shamans to rally other powers together against him.”

She had spent most of her life in isolation, so she didn’t have as many friends as the Kaiming Six Shamans.

“Thank you, goddess.” Zu An knew that Emperor Jun wasn't a foe he could oppose alone. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain conjured a white cloud and gestured for them to get on. 

Zu An smiled and said, “I have a faster way to return to Wu Mountain.”

They'd had to manually travel to Genesis Mountain because he didn’t know its coordinates, but he still remembered Wu Mountain’s coordinates. He grabbed the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s arm and activated Rainbow Morph.

A rainbow shot across the sky. A second later, the three of them returned to Wu Mountain. 

Despite having let go of their hands, the Goddess of Wu Mountain still felt warmth radiating from her arm. It made her blush a little. How could he grab a maiden’s hand without any warning?

It had been many years since she last had physical contact with anyone, let alone a man. However, she could tell from his clear eyes that he hadn’t intended to take advantage of her, so it seemed excessive to kick up a fuss over this. 

Just then, the nervous Kaiming Six Shamans appeared in the vicinity. The Goddess of Wu Mountain anxiously asked them what had happened.

The Kaiming Six Shamans were overjoyed to see them, but their faces quickly turned bitter. “Something huge has happened! The Golden Crow Crown Prince has burned Nuba to death!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2569: Destruction Upon The World
chapters.body:
Chapter 2569: Destruction Upon The World

“What?!” Even the usually composed Goddess of Wu Mountain was horrified. 

On the one hand, she had just met Nuba not long ago. On the other hand, Nuba was not only the Yellow Emperor’s daughter, but also Ancestral Shaman Zhulong’s wife. Her death was bound to cause massive ripples.

Zu An, however, was calm. He was aware of how Nuba had met her end in history, though he hadn’t expected it to happen so quickly. It felt bizarre to be involved in such major historical incidents.

“We found it inconceivable too. We heard that she was looking for Emperor Jun but was unable to locate him, so she began insulting him to goad him into revealing himself,” Wu Yang explained. 

“She must have gotten impatient.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. “Nuba has always had a bad temper.”

“That’s most likely the case,” Wu Peng agreed. “However, Emperor Jun was still nowhere to be seen despite her insults. It could be because he was no longer able to sense the happenings on earth after the separation of heaven and earth.”

“Emperor Jun could just be putting up with it. He is the next Celestial Emperor, and Nuba is an important figure. It wouldn’t be surprising if he decided to show some magnanimity to her taunts,” Wu Fan analyzed. 

“But the Golden Crow Ten Princes couldn’t take it lying down. They rushed out upon hearing Nuba insult their father, and a conflict broke out. That culminated in the present situation,” Wu Di said with a sigh.

“Nuba should have been strong enough to stand her ground against the Golden Crow Ten Princes, but they happen to be able to suppress her ability. They made her powers go amok before killing her,” Wu Fu added. 

… 

The Kaiming Six Shamans quickly reconstructed the situation through their discussion.

The Mermaid Queen secretly tugged Zu An’s sleeve and whispered, “Could it be that Nuba dug up something related to that person too?”

“That’s possible,” Zu An replied. Thinking about it, Emperor Jun had arrived in the middle of Yi and Gu’s fight and killed Yi before erasing his trail. This suggested that Gu and Emperor Jun had been colluding.

If they further tugged on the thread, Emperor Jun could have been the one instigating Gu to kill Bao Jiang and steal the immortal medicine, and he could have had a part to play behind Er Fu’s murder of Yayu, too. If that was the conclusion Nuba came to, she was bound to want to avenge her son. That would explain why she'd harshly insulted Emperor Jun to goad the latter to confront her.

But what could Emperor Jun’s motive be behind all these things? What was he planning with the Myriad Souls Banner and the Soul Destruction Cauldron? He was already the next Celestial Emperor, so what was the point of doing all these?

“Look! The suns are quickly moving!” The Mermaid Queen pointed to the sky. It was so hot that her face was flushed, and a thin layer of sweat had formed on her forehead. 

The group raised their heads and saw the ten suns in the sky quickly moving westward, causing the surrounding air to become cooler.

“Are the golden crows finally returning to Scalding Valley?” Wu Yang, who had opened up his robe to fan himself, asked in delight. 

“It doesn’t look like it.” Zu An shook his head. 

The ten suns’ movements were erratic; they would move quickly at one point only to suddenly stop right after, as if they were fooling around. 

Wu Xiang’s ears suddenly enlarged until they were comparable to an elephant’s ears. He focused hard on the voices around him, and soon, his expression turned livid. 

“Sixth Brother, what did you hear?” Wu Peng asked. 

“The shamans are angry at Nuba’s death. Kuafu wants to settle the score with the Golden Crow Ten Princes, but they simply flew off. That angered Kuafu, so he’s chasing after them to demand an explanation,” Wu Xiang said. 

“Kuafu chasing the suns!” Zu An exclaimed.

“What?” The others turned to look at Zu An in confusion.

Zu An had no way of explaining this historical event to them, so he said, “It’s too dangerous for Kuafu to pursue the Golden Crow Ten Princes alone.”

“Indeed. If they had the gall to kill Nuba, they wouldn’t show him mercy,” the Kaiming Six Shamans replied worriedly. Kuafu was powerful enough to stand his ground against several of the Golden Crow Ten Princes, but dealing with all ten of them at once was impossible. 

“I’ll go take a look,” Zu An said grimly. It would be a stretch to say that Kuafu was a friend, but he did have a favorable impression of the other party’s candid personality. 

Besides, Emperor Jun was likely involved in this matter. He wanted to get to the bottom of the issue and hopefully obtain news about Xiaoxi and Yumen Beiqing.

“I’ll go with you.” The Mermaid Queen tagged along, not wanting to part ways with him.

Zu An shook his head. “It’s too dangerous. You should stay here with the goddess.”

He clasped his fist toward the Goddess of Wu Mountain and said, “Goddess, I’ll be troubling you to take care of her.”

Confronting the Golden Crow Ten Princes was already dangerous, and they could even encounter Emperor Jun on top of that. Zu An could escape using Rainbow Morph, but he dared not bring the Mermaid Queen with him.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded. “Don’t worry, we’ll protect her.”

The Mermaid Queen was reluctant to part ways with Zu An, but she understood the severity of the situation. She grabbed his hand and said, “You must stay safe.”

Despite the intense heat from the surroundings, she gave off an inexplicable coolness that calmed his heart. 

Zu An was moved. A thought suddenly came to his mind, and he stuffed an item into her hand and secretly relayed some instructions to her. 

The Mermaid Queen was taken aback. “But you need this more than me!”

“Don’t worry. I dare not say the same about the others, but I'm unparalleled in the art of fleeing.” Zu An had been skirting around death ever since transmigrating back then. Over time, he had honed the art of fleeing to a masterful degree. 

Worried that the Mermaid Queen would continue turning down his gift, he bade her farewell before morphing into a rainbow to chase after the ten suns. He couldn’t move straight to where the Golden Crow Ten Princes and Kuafu were because he didn’t have their coordinates, so he could only manually chase after them.

But after crossing Spirit Mountain, his expression slowly turned cold. Spirit Mountain was an enigmatic mountain range where powerhouses and experts dwelled. While the sudden appearance of the ten suns had caused the temperature to reach unbearable levels, the powerhouses were able to protect even the flora and faunas using their formations.

But everything changed beyond that. The sky was constantly dyed a jarring dark red color. The trees beyond the Spirit Mountain swiftly withered. Their trunks cracked under the immense heat, and their sap swiftly evaporated. Lush forests were swiftly reduced to charred ground. It was hard to tell where the first spark of flame had started, but it hadn’t taken long for it to evolve into an inferno. 

Many beast fled in a fluster. They were already the stronger ones, as the weaker ones had already been scorched to death. But escaping the protection of the forest meant direct exposure to the rays of the ten suns. Many of the beasts shrieked in misery as their bodies burst into flames. In the end, they were reduced to charred carcasses. 

Zu An passed by a furiously bubbling river that was soaked in blood. Fishes and sea monsters tried leaping out from the water, but it was too late. Their flesh melted so quickly that only their bones were left before they could even leap out of the water. 

Zu An noticed a human tribe next to the river and tried to rush over to save them. It was a decently-sized tribe. Its buildings were made by piling up simple stone blocks, but it was already nearing the scale of a city. Anyone could tell that it was a vibrant civilization.

But there wasn't a shadow to be seen… 

No, it would be more accurate to say that there were shadows everywhere—the walls and the floors were covered in pitch-black carbonized silhouettes. Most people in this tribe had to have been mortals for them to not even leave corpses behind. The crude marks around were the only evidence of their existence in this world.

Zu An stopped before an underground cavern. It likely served as the tribe’s bunker in times of danger, but there was no one inside. There were only ‘wall murals’ to be seen. Some of these murals depicted a mother hugging her children tightly before their lives came to a halt. 

Zu An had heard the word ‘carnage’ many times, but it was only now that its true meaning sank in for him. These people had just died, but he couldn’t sense their souls despite being the master of the underworld. He could only sense their resentment and fear. 

Emotions gushed into his heart, leaving him suffocated. He looked at the sky. Those who have sinned must pay the price, even if they are gods!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2570: The Deafening Arrow That Shakes The Clouds
chapters.body:
Chapter 2570: The Deafening Arrow That Shakes The Clouds

Under the blazing sky, a giant reminiscent of a hill charged at the ten suns with a wooden staff. Every step he took easily traversed several mountains, yet he was still significantly slower than the ten suns. He had no idea how long he had been chasing the Golden Crow Ten Princes. It was so hot that his body was scorched red throughout, and a layer of dead white skin had formed around his lips. 

He finally saw a raging river he remembered as the human tribes’ famed Yellow River. Under the ten suns, the river bubbled furiously, but he couldn’t care less. He squatted next to the river and began drinking its contents. He almost apepared to be enjoying it even though the water was boiling. 

He drank so fast that it didn’t take long for him to gulp down the entire Yellow River. He regained his spirit and continued chasing after the ten suns with huge strides.

However, the suns seemed to be making fun of him. Instead of escaping while he was drinking water, they'd stopped to taunt him. It was only after he downed the river that they hurriedly sped off.

Under the extreme heat, Kuafu’s stamina was being quickly depleted. He soon started gasping for air, but luckily, he stumbled upon the Wei River. Like before, he drank up the entire Wei River before continuing to chase after the ten suns. 

By this point, he had already realized that he was no match for the ten suns. Even if he caught up with them, he wouldn’t be able to exact vengeance for Nuba. However, he was fueled by indignation. He refused to submit to the ten suns just like that.

The Shaman races could be defeated, but their spirit could never be quelled!

The chase continued, but Kuafu quickly felt thirsty again despite having just drunk two rivers dry. He remembered that there was a huge lake ahead. Once he drank up that lake, he would have the energy to continue the chase. 

It was a pity he didn’t last until reaching the lake. His legs felt increasingly heavy, and he soon struggled to keep his eyes open. He knew that he had reached his limit, though he couldn’t understand why. He wasn't be a match for the ten suns, but he should have been able to last longer.

He looked at the eerily smiling suns in the sky, and realization struck him.

“I see…”

The realization made him stop his chase. He used the last bit of life force he had left to toss his wooden staff. Where the staff landed, grass began to grow. Not even the ten suns’ heat could wither the vibrant life force sprouting out. 

Peach blossoms flowered into peaches. The peaches fell to the ground and turned into new sprouts. From these new sprouts, new trees rose. Soon, an entire forest of peach trees emerged from the wooden staff. 

Cries of misery suddenly echoed from the surroundings. They were wretched, yet there was a tinge of liberation in them.

“How dare you foil our plan!” a furious voice echoed.

A fireball fell from the sky and landed on Kuafu. However, Kuafu’s body was so strong that this meteorite-like attack didn’t smash his body to bits, but it charred huge swathes of his skin.

Even so, that wasn’t enough to vent the suns’ anger. They unleashed more fireballs to reduce him to ashes. 

Just then, brilliant sword ki surged in from afar and sliced the fireballs into fireworks.

The ten Golden Crows looked over and saw a rainbow headed toward them. Before they knew it, Zu An had already landed next to Kuafu.

Seeing his charred body, Zu An’s expression darkened. “I arrived too late.”

Kuafu pointed at the sky. “Be careful of the vile…”

However, he breathed his last before he could finish his words. His eyes were wide with indignation, as if he regretted not being able to finish speaking.

The ten Golden Crows circled the sky. “Who are you? How dare you meddle in our business!”

Zu An ignored them. He glanced at the nearby peach forest, and it made him realize something. He gently closed Kuafu’s eyes as he murmured, “Rest assured, I know what you want to say.”

Others would think that the Golden Crow Ten Princes had come out for vengeance because Nuba insulted their father, and then continued roaming the world out of mischief. Their deeds had caused widespread destruction, but in light of their lofty standing, their punishments wouldn’t be too severe.

But Zu An knew things weren’t that simple. Based on the information he had gathered thus far, the sudden appearance of the ten suns was no coincidence. It was planned.

The Myriad Souls Banner, the Soul Destruction Cauldron…

The appearance of the ten suns has caused widespread deaths, but the souls of the deceased are nowhere to be found. Emperor Jun must be using the ten Golden Crows to complete the final step of sacrificing all living beings in the world to his formation. 

Kuafu had to have figured that out, so he'd formed this peach forest with his staff to restrain the souls. That hindered the collection of the souls in the vicinity. No wonder the ten Golden Crows were so agitated. 

“Brat, we’re talking to you! Are you mute?!” the Golden Crow Sixth Prince roared.

Zu An finally looked up at the ten suns. His eyes were sharp enough to make out the gleeful smiles on the ten Golden Crows. They couldn’t understand the weight of what they were doing. “You must have done all these things thinking that no one in the world could stop you.”

The ten Golden Crows were initially taken aback before bursting into laughter. 

“Look at what you’re saying. A mere human like you intends to stop us?”

“Know your place, mortal ant!”

“We’re the Celestial Emperor’s sons! You don’t know how many blessings we've brought to the world over the years. So what if we have a little bit of fun now and then? Those weaklings only have themselves to blame for being unable to withstand our heat. That has nothing to do with us!”

“Why waste your breath on a mere ant? Ant, slice your detestable tongue and snap off your limbs right now; I’ll consider sparing your life.”

…

Zu An remained perfectly calm in the face of their gleeful laughter. If he's decided to send the ten suns out now, Emperor Jun’s preparations must be complete. It’s unlikely that Xiaoxi is safe.

He glanced at Kuafu’s charred body and remembered the doomsday scenarios he had seen along the way. Charred black human silhouettes filled the walls and the grounds of countless villages, as if trying to tell him something. He felt as if someone had set his blood on fire. 

Just then, Mi Li exclaimed in agitation, “Calm down! Emperor Jun has spent a long time plotting this scheme. It’s not something you can deal with by yourself. The best you can do right now is to contact the Shaman races and have Wuhui report to the Celestial Court. There are plenty of powerhouses to deal with this incident. You mustn’t get involved in this massive karma.

“Don’t let your recklessness get ahead of yourself! This is one of the biggest incidents in the flow of time. You aren’t from this time. There’s no saying what will happen if you force your way into this matter. If you’re killed by Emperor Jun in this world, you won’t even get a chance to reincarnate!”

…

Mi Li had been keeping an eye on this matter. Upon sensing Zu An’s intentios, she had no choice but to wake up and advise him. She usually cherished her words as if they were gold, yet she'd suddenly spoken so much in a single breath. That reflected just how anxious she was.

A red bow appeared in Zu An’s hands, but he looked extremely calm. “Big sister empress, I know it would be wiser to heed your advice, but if a person cultivates so hard only to dare not make a move in the face of injustice, what has he been cultivating his entire life for?”

“You’ll die!”

“All beings eventually die.”

Zu An leaned backward as he drew his bow in an arc like a full moon. The surrounding ki furiously gushed toward his body. It didn’t feel as if he was pulling the bow alone, but as if billions of living beings were standing behind him.

A white light burst forth and pierced through a Golden Crow. Its blood flowed like a river. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2571: Creeping Despair
chapters.body:
Chapter 2571: Creeping Despair

Crimson infernos unleashed by the ten suns; they crack the grounds and boil the oceans.

The Fusang branch burns to cinders, and countless souls bemoan heaven.

A bowstring is pulled in a perfect arc; its target heaven.

A thunderous snap that shakes the highest clouds!

The arrow pierces through a golden crow, causing blood to gush like a stream. 

The descending celestial flames torch the plains. 

… 

The shot Golden Crow screamed in agony. His feathers scattered all over the ground as his blood spurted across the sky before he plummeted onto a western mountain, leaving a crater in his wake. 

As time passed, a deep spring known as the Feather Spring would form there. That was also the place Zu An had seen when he first entered this place. Zu An had wondered why he didn’t see the Feather Spring when he entered this world this time; little could he have known that he was the one to inadvertently create it. 

Countless despairing living beings on earth witnessed the downfall of a sun and cheered in delight. Tears of joy flowed down their faces. Even though it was just a single sun, it brought them hope and vented the anger they felt. 

The more powerful entities thought further ahead. They wondered what would happen to the man who accomplished this feat. Emperor Jun and Xihe wouldn’t let someone who had killed their son live. 

Those on Wu Mountain had a better grasp of what was going on. The Kaiming Six Shamans exclaimed in fear, “The one who shot that arrow can’t be that little brother, right?”

The Mermaid Queen naturally knew it was Zu An. She anxiously turned to the Goddess of Wu Mountain and asked, “Goddess, now that big brother has killed one of the Golden Crows, will the Celestial Court go after him for vengeance?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked grim. “The Golden Crows’ father and their mother will likely go after him. In particular, Sun Goddess Xihe is known to dote on her sons.”

At the same time, she was also awed. Zu An had to have known all this, but he'd still chosen to make a move. He felt like a valiant hero who stood steadfast to his principles. 

“What should we do?” The Mermaid Queen panicked. She could still request help from her connections if this was her previous world, but she knew no one in this world. The inability to do anything left her feeling profoundly helpless.

“Wait for me here. Don’t go anywhere. I’ll contact those who can help.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain morphed into a fog and quickly left the area. 

The Kaiming Six Shamans were taken aback. “The goddess has always been a lofty individual. She rarely speaks to others; it’s rare for her to proactively make a move. She must be seeking her elders for help.”

“Even the goddess is making a move. We can’t just sit still here.”

“Indeed. We have to get as many old friends to help out as possible, even if it means hurling out our pride!” 

… 

The Kaiming Six Shamans headed off as well, leaving the Mermaid Queen anxiously squeezing her sleeves while she observed the situation in the distance. 

… 

In the distant battlefield, the Golden Crows’ laughter halted. They were shocked and infuriated. “How dare you kill our Ninth Brother!”

You have successfully trolled the Golden Crow Princes for +888… +888… +888…

Zu An remained impassive despite the massive influx of Rage in his system. These Golden Crows are reacting so strongly just because they lost a brother. What about the massacred tribes I've seen along the way?

Not wanting to waste his breath arguing with the Golden Crows, Zu An drew his bowstring once more. Perhaps it was an illusion, but he didn’t feel as if he had expended any of his energy. On the contrary, he felt stronger than ever. 

This didn't make sense. Perhaps he had significantly advanced his cultivation, but he didn’t have the aid of the Spirit Mountain’s Ten Shamans, Yun Jianyue, and Yan Xuehen this time. Even a single arrow should have expended his energy significantly. And yet, he felt more spirited than ever. This felt like neither the Kun Peng’s nor the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra’s ability. Rather, this energy was something he had never felt before. 

He suddenly recalled that the biggest difference between celestials and mortal cultivators was that celestials had divine personas capable of absorbing faith, wish energy, or other things along those lines. For a moment earlier, he'd felt as if he had received wish energy from billions of living beings. They strongly willed for vengeance against the ten suns and the conclusion of this apocalypse. 

His deed had happened to grant hope to all living beings in the world, so they'd offered him their earnest blessings and prayers in hopes that he could accomplish this feat. 

While he was pondering all these things, yet another white light harnessing the surrounding ki and the masses’ wish energy flew forth. 

Yet another Golden Crow plummeted from the sky with a wretched shriek. The suns in the sky became much less glaring. Resounding cheers came from the surroundings.

The remaining eight Golden Crows didn’t dare to underestimate Zu An. They quickly spread apart and began their retaliation. Meteorites and flames fell from the sky and bombarded the ground where Zu An stood. Terrifying explosions followed; massive holes were left in the surrounding radius. Even the ground was turned into slowly flowing lava. 

“That fellow must be dead now!” one of the Golden Crows hollered.

“He got off easy!”

“I think I saw a rainbow flash past.”

“You must be seeing things. How can there be a rainbow in such heat?”

… 

They quickly realized that they weren’t seeing things. Yet another white light flashed past, and yet another Golden Crow screamed in misery and plummeted from the sky. 

“What?!” The seven surviving Golden Crows were horrified. 

They began taking evasive maneuvers while bombarding Zu An with even more attacks. With their sharp golden feathers, they formed a powerful sword formation that unleashed relentless attacks upon Zu An. 

They had learned their lesson this time. The sword formation left no room for escape, and the seven Golden Crows even made sure to seal his aura off too. No matter where he dodged, the golden swords would chase him to the very end. An archer wouldn’t be able to pose a threat if he had no time to draw his bowstring.

They were determined to make him experience a fate worse than death!

Looking at the golden feather swords in the sky, Zu An suddenly felt small and insignificant. However, he didn’t panic. He knew his aura had been locked down on, and dodging would only result in him losing control over the momentum of battle. 

Thus, he didn’t bother to dodge at all!

A Seven-Colored Divine Halo shone, and all the golden swords vanished into thin air.

The eyes of the Golden Crows nearly popped out from shock.

However, there was no time for them to think before three streaks of white light flew at them. Three Golden Crows screeched in misery as their blazing bodies plummeted from the sky. 

“That is Zhuanxu’s bow, Suzeng! Why is it in his hands?”

The remaining four Golden Crows finally lost their courage. The first two deaths could still be passed off as carelessness, but despite them putting their strength together, their brothers were still falling one after another. By this point, it was clear to them that they were outclassed!

They desperately fled for their lives, morphing into four golden lights that fled in different directions. Each prayed desperately that the next victim would be one of their brothers, so they would have more time to make their getaway. 

When the Golden Crow First Prince heard two screams of agony, he knew two more of his brothers had fallen. His expression warped in anger. Wait till I return from this ordeal. I’ll gather my men and tear that scoundrel into pieces!

He wasn’t worried in the slightest. He thought that there was no way Zu An could catch up with him after killing two of his brothers. The Golden Crow Radiant Flow was a top-notch movement skill. No other movement skill, other than the Heavenly Peng Stride and Rainbow Morph, could catch up with it. 

Hold on! Rainbow Morph?

The sudden recollection of the rainbow they'd seen made the Golden Crow First Prince narrow his eyes. 

Just then, he spotted a rainbow in his peripheral vision. Before he knew it, Zu An was already standing in front of him. He finally tasted the same despair experienced by the other living beings earlier. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2572: Indignant? You Can Only Accept It
chapters.body:
Chapter 2572: Indignant? You Can Only Accept It

As Zu An coldly stared at him with a giant red bow in hand, the Golden Crow First Prince felt so frightened that his feathers stood on end. 

“Big brother, it’s a misunderstanding! It’s all a misunderstanding!” He squeezed out a smile. 

“I prefer your earlier arrogance.” Zu An didn’t hesitate to draw his bowstring and manifest a white arrow.

“I was wrong, big brother. I know my mistake now. We shouldn’t have come out together in a moment of mischief, but I have at least worked hard to bring light to the world over the years. Please spare my life this once. I’ll do more good in the future to make up for my mistake.” 

The Golden Crow First Prince had always been fawned on wherever he went; he had never been so subservient. I’ll slowly settle the score with him if I survive this ordeal. As formidable as he is, he's still nothing more than an unaffiliated cultivator. How can he compare to me, the noble son of a Celestial Emperor?

Zu An sighed. He remembered a classic line he had read in his previous life’s webnovels. “You don’t know your mistakes. You just know that you’re going to die.”

He released his bowstring, and the white light instantaneously appeared before the Golden Crow First Prince. 

Just then, Zu An’s expression suddenly turned grim. The Golden Crow First Prince’s head didn’t explode as he had anticipated—some force had stopped his arrow. He looked closer and saw that a golden bell had manifested around the Golden Crow First Prince. 

The golden bell was translucent and inscribed with obscure divine runes. It felt as if it was cloaked in a light, fluttery veil. Only those whose cultivation had reached a certain level could feel the dao flowing through it. Feeling the inviolable nature of the golden bell, Zu An suddenly felt as if his Tranquility Bell was a terrible bootleg.

A divine melody hummed as the space in front of Zu An slowly parted to reveal chaos within, before light suddenly burst forth. A figure slowly manifested amid the light. It was a man with long silver hair that cascaded like the Milky Way. The ends of his hair contained the sun’s flames, and they singed the area around them. He wore a black imperial robe with dark golden patterns that seemed to be forged out of the souls of countless primordial beasts. His very presence commanded heaven’s authority.

His eyes were gentle, but his gaze left Zu An feeling as if needles had stung him. This caused goosebumps to rise all over his body.

“How bizarre. A shaman’s aura mixed with a human’s bloodline…” The silver-haired man assessed Zu An, not understanding.

“Emperor Jun?” Zu An took a deep breath to loosen the tension in his muscles.

The silver-haired man shook his head. “Emperor Jun is my brother. People call me Taiyi.”

“Donghuang Taiyi!” Zu An’s eyes narrowed. That’s a supreme god whose standing in the legends equals Emperor Jun! The golden bell must be the legendary East Emperor Bell, renowned as the number one innate defensive artifact in the world. No wonder my arrow couldn’t pierce it!

Upon seeing Donghuang Taiyi, the Golden Crow First Prince straightened his hunched back and exclaimed, “Uncle, that scoundrel killed my other brothers! You must avenge us!” 

This fellow is dead meat! Uncle has always doted on us! Now that uncle is here, no one can touch us anymore!

“Are the other Golden Crows dead?” Donghuang Taiyi was taken aback.

Sensing a powerful aura locking onto him, Zu An calmly replied, “I was only able to take down eight of them. The world can’t be without a sun, but two suns is still one too many. One will suffice.”

Donghuang Taiyi’s expression turned cold. “Hoh. You intend to continue your slaughter?”

The Golden Crow First Prince took this chance to hide behind Donghuang Taiyi as he looked at Zu An with a gloating expression. He’s asking for it. Where does he find the guts to continue running his mouth in my uncle’s presence? Does he take my uncle to be a pushover?

Zu An remained unfazed despite the immense pressure coming from Donghuang Taiyi. “The ten suns emerged simultaneously, causing countless lives to wither and die. They deserve to die for the sins they have committed. Do you, an esteemed personage in the Celestial Court, intend to violate the celestial laws?”

Donghuang Taiyi calmly replied, “They will be judged under the celestial laws; it’s not up to you to judge their crimes and punish them. Besides, they have diligently brought light to the masses over the years. How many living beings have prospered because of them? With the karma they have accrued, they don’t deserve death for such minor mistakes.”

“On the contrary, your crime of murdering the Celestial Court’s deities is much more grave. You ought to be sliced into bits.” 

“Minor mistakes?” Zu An was first taken aback before abruptly bursting into laughter. “I thought you, with your exalted standing in the Celestial Court, would at least be capable of telling right from wrong. I didn’t expect you to have such double standards just like everyone else.”

“Right from wrong?” Donghuang Taiyi scoffed. “The world simply observes its natural flow; it doesn’t exist to be right, and neither does it unravel over wrongs. All the talk about right and wrong is just self-deceiving, mortal babble. How hilarious it is for you to preach that to me with such seriousness.” 

“Is it hilarious?” Zu An harrumphed. “You might think that humans are insignificant right now, and their values mean nothing to you. But one day, the concept of right and wrong will become mainstream in the world.”

Donghuang Taiyi’s face darkened. “How many years have you lived? How many years have I lived? Know your place! I am the stronger one here, and my standing is higher than yours. You’re just a mere unaffiliated cultivator. Even if I sentence you to death by a thousand cuts, you have no choice but to accept it, no matter how indignant you feel. This is the law of the world!”

He snapped his finger, and the air around Zu An suddenly turned turbulent. The gentle wind suddenly transformed into millions of incomparably sharp blades that covered every single inch of his skin. 

“Uncle, don’t let him die an easy death!” the Golden Crow First Prince shouted excitedly. 

The space around Zu An had been sealed off; he was unable to perform any defensive maneuvers. He couldn’t even use his instantaneous movement skills, let alone Rainbow Morph. No wonder he’s one of the top gods!

Without any hesitation, Zu An employed all his defensive methods. The Tranquility Bell manifested around him. After undergoing the heavenly tribulation, his Tranquility Bell had become many times stronger than before.

Even so, it was still instantly ripped through by the millions of air blades. 

“How can that lousy bell of yours compare to my uncle’s…”

The Golden Crow First Prince was halfway through his sentence when his eyes nearly popped out. Another bell had appeared around Zu An, and it was the East Emperor Bell! 

Living up to its reputation as the number one defensive artifact, it kept the countless terrifying air blades at bay. 

Donghuang Taiyi was taken aback. “Rune Weapon Chart? How do you have that skill?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2573: Forceful Murder
chapters.body:
Chapter 2573: Forceful Murder

Donghuang Taiyi eyed Zu An’s red bow and frowned. “The Dragon race’s bow, Suzeng, and the Dragon race’s skill. Are you a descendant of the true dragons?”

Zu An was stunned. He had heard that even though the Celestial Court’s Celestial Emperors viewed the Green Emperor as their founding ancestor, they were still divided into two factions. One was the Black Emperor’s faction, who viewed the Dragon race as their maternal race. The other was the White Emperor’s faction, who viewed the Phoenix race as their maternal race.

He had received the Rune Weapon Chart from Yu the Great back then. Looks like Yu the Great is a descendant of the Black Emperor.

He suddenly realized that this didn’t bode well for him. For various reasons, the Dragon race and the Phoenix race were on bad terms with each other, resulting in tension between the two factions. Donghuang Taiyi’s maternal race was the Phoenixes. That immediately deepened the grudge between them. 

As expected, Donghuang Taiyi’s expression turned cold. “I was wondering how such a huge mistake could have happened. Turns out you have someone backing you. Do you think that your fake East Emperor Bell can stop my attack?”

The wind blades around Zu An became even more violent. The East Emperor Bell manifested by the Rune Weapon Chart was soon covered in cracks, looking as if it would shatter any time now.

The Golden Crow First Prince smirked. “Do you regret your actions now, scoundrel? If you kneel and kowtow to me right now, I’ll consider pleading for mercy on your behalf with my uncle.”

Heh, I won’t beg my uncle even if you kowtow and beg me right now. 

He knew that most people looked tough on the surface, but once death became certain, they wouldn’t hesitate to grovel and do anything to survive, especially if they saw a ray of hope, no matter how unlikely it was. He wanted to see this detestable fellow kneel and break down in front of him. Only then would he be cleansed of the humiliation he had suffered at the other party’s hands. 

Just remembering how he had lowered his head and begged Zu An for mercy earlier left him with a stinging sensation in his cheeks. I wonder if uncle saw that. How embarrassing!

Zu An glared at the Golden Crow First Prince. “I’ll kill you.”

The Golden Crow First Prince was stunned before he bent over and guffawed. “Hahaha! You’re already a dead man yourself, and you still want to kill me? Uncle, he thinks that he can kill me in your presence! Hahaha! I have never heard a greater joke in my life!”

A tinge of contempt flashed across Donghuang Taiyi’s face. “Young man, I concede that it is outstanding for someone of your age to reach your level of cultivation, but you’re still too weak compared to me.”

He waved his hand, and the East Emperor Bell around Zu An shattered. The wind blades exploited this opening to rush at Zu An. He made sure to control the wind blades precisely to each cut off a thin slice of flesh the width of a cicada’s wing, so that Zu An wouldn’t die too quickly. 

Donghuang Taiyi smiled as if he was ready to marvel at his masterpiece. 

However, the space around Zu An suddenly distorted. As if he had turned into a black hole, all the wind blades that approached him were absorbed. 

Donghuang Taiyi's eyes widened in shock. “Taotie? The power of void?”

Zu An suddenly vanished into thin air, and in his place appeared a horrified Golden Crow First Prince. Uncle, I was waiting to admire that scoundrel’s miserable plight. How could you slip up here? 

The Golden Crow First Prince looked at the terrifying wind blades rushing at him and paled. How can I withstand my uncle’s wind blades?!

Donghuang Taiyi was startled. The Taotie’s devouring ability had distorted the space around Zu An, which loosened his grip on the latter. He must have used that opening to escape, but how did he exchange positions with the Golden Crow First Prince?

With Donghuang Taiyi’s cultivation, he naturally sensed Zu An behind him. Knowing the red bow, Suzeng, posed a threat to him too, he instinctively summoned the East Emperor Bell to protect himself. 

Even though he is much weaker than me, he possesses a lot of methods. 

At the same time, he opened his palm to divert the wind blades rushing at the Golden Crow First Prince. If he accidentally killed his own nephew here, he would become the laughingstock of the world!

A white light divided the world into two. As soon as the wind blades scattered, the white light pierced through the Golden Crow First Prince’s body. 

Pah!

The Golden Crow First Prince exploded like an inflated balloon. His feathers mixed with fresh blood scattered down, leaving a sinister, abstract painting in the sky. 

It went without saying that the arrow had been shot by Zu An. He had made use of that brief opening to swap places with the Golden Crow First Prince using the position exchange skill. 

He'd then pretended to attack Donghuang Taiyi despite knowing that the attack wouldn’t work even if he caught the latter by surprise, but it was all a feint. As he had expected, Donghuang Taiyi instinctively brought out the East Emperor Bell to protect himself. While ensuring his own safety, he also sought to rescue the Golden Crow First Prince from his wind blades. 

Zu An had then made use of this opening to release his killer shot. Due to all the actions he had taken, Donghuang Taiyi couldn’t react in time. 

Donghuang Taiyi was infuriated. “You’re courting death!”

You have trolled Donghuang Taiyi for +444… +444… +444…

How dare this ant kill my nephew before my eyes?

Not only would he be unable to face his big brother, Emperor Jun, and his sister-in-law, Xihe, but it would also sully his reputation if it became known that he, Donghuang Taiyi, couldn’t ensure the safety of a junior under his protection. How could he face others in the future?

He raised his arm and sent a palm backward. His palm encompassed an area of over twenty kilometers. Knowing that Zu An had an instantaneous movement skill, he widened the area of his attack so that Zu An would have nowhere to escape to!

This palm strike reduced the meandering mountain range behind him to bits. The ten suns had already turned the surroundings into a desert, and the palm further transformed the desert into a sea of lava. Smoke gushed into the sky. 

Donghuang Taiyi’s expression suddenly turned cold. “Rainbow Morph!”

He sensed that he had failed to kill Zu An. The latter had escaped by morphing into a rainbow. Due to the Taotie’s devouring ability distorting the surrounding space, he could no longer lock down on Zu An’s aura as he did before. 

“How many abilities does that fellow have?” Donghuang Taiyi was baffled. 

His cultivation is at most that of a True Immortal, but his fighting prowess is comparable to a High Immortal… 

No, in my presence, even a High Immortal is no more than an ant. I could easily kill a High Immortal, but that fellow’s abilities are proving to be an issue for me. 

“Trying to escape? Do you think you can slip away from me?” Donghuang Taiyi morphed into a golden surge of light and pursued the rainbow. 

There are too many secrets surrounding him. I can’t see through his background and karma. I have to capture him and get his secrets out of him.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2574: You Have Guts
chapters.body:
Chapter 2574: You Have Guts

Zu An’s Rainbow Morph was formidable, but Donghuang Taiyi was at least 30% faster than him. It didn’t take long before he was blocking Zu An’s path. 

Zu An emerged from his Rainbow Morph to confront Donghuang Taiyi.

“You might have angered me, but you have also caught my attention.” Donghuang Taiyi examined Zu An from head to toe to figure out his background.

“That’s the stereotypical domineering CEO’s declaration. What a pity I’m not the female lead of a romance drama.” Despite Zu An’s casual words, his expression remained grim. His brain was furiously churning to find a way out of this.

Donghuang Taiyi wasn't particularly large, but his presence was so overwhelming to Zu An that it felt like there was nothing else except for him. In spite of Zu An’s many near-death experiences and his recent massive breakthrough, every cell in his body was furiously screaming danger at him. 

Donghuang Taiyi frowned. He couldn’t understand Zu An’s statement about domineering CEOs and romance dramas. Experts of his caliber naturally abhorred the unknown. He harrumphed and said, “Forget it. I’ll first cripple your limbs before slowly interrogating you."

He was about to continue making a move when an anxious voice echoed, “Hold on!”

A cloud rushed over and morphed into a beautiful silhouette. It was none other than the Goddess of Wu Mountain. She was accompanied by the Kaiming Six Shamans and Xingtian.

Seeing that she was alone, Zu An was about to ask a question when she meticulously replied, “Miss Chang’e is still on Wu Mountain. You don’t have to worry about her.”

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. “What brings you here?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain assessed him with gleaming eyes. “We can’t turn a blind eye when you’re causing a huge ruckus here.”

Her words were filled with awe. Zu An had just shot down nine Golden Crows, after all. This was a feat beyond normal people, and those capable of subduing the Golden Crows would hold back because of their standing. 

Zu An had done something that no one else dared to because of his righteous heart. He was courageous enough to stand up for the countless lives who had suffered under the Golden Crows’ tyranny.

This made her harden her expression. I’ll protect him no matter what it takes.

“Goddess?” Donghuang Taiyi frowned at the sight of her. “This has nothing to do with you, so stay away!”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain stood forward and raised her hands to protect Zu An. “The Golden Crows deserve to be executed for their sins. He’s merely enacting justice on behalf of heaven. What fault does he have?”

“Enacting justice on behalf of heaven?” Donghuang Taiyi scoffed. “Heaven has its own will. How can the likes of him be worthy of representing heaven?”

“If he’s unworthy, the same can be said about you lofty gods who turn a blind eye to your juniors massacring the masses!” The Goddess of Wu Mountain raised her slender neck as she berated Donghuang Taiyi. Her firm attitude was at odds with her usual nonchalant self. 

The Kaiming Six Shamans were startled. They had never seen the goddess acting like this. Ever since Zu An arrived, they had seen many unknown sides to the goddess. 

Her words made Donghuang Taiyi’s expression darken. “Yaoji, I am showing you respect on account of the Flame Emperor. Don’t overstep your boundaries.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was unfazed. “I am merely speaking up for what’s right. You celestials are the ones who betrayed the faith of the masses.”

“Audacious!” Donghuang Taiyi roared. An ancient bell chimed out of nowhere.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain moaned and spat out blood.

The Kaiming Six Shamans had their guard up, but the East Emperor Bell’s chime rang straight from their souls, rendering their defensive methods useless. It shook up their ki and caused them to ceaselessly vomit blood.

The chime also left Zu An’s head groggy, but Hundredwarble’s automatic counterattack and the Taotie’s devouring ability minimized the prowess of the chime. Seeing the goddess plummeting from the sky after sustaining severe injuries, he quickly flew over to catch her. He pumped his ki into her body as he asked, “Are you all right?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain gradually recovered from the blow. She belatedly noticed Zu An holding her by her waist, and that made her blush furiously. However, she wasn’t averse to it. “I’m fine. Thank you.”

The Kaiming Six Shamans, who had plummeted to the ground, rolled their eyes. We fell to the ground as well, so shouldn’t you at least help us up? Tsk, hoes over bros indeed!

“Donghuang, you’re too much!” Xingtian roared. He brandished his massive axe against Donghuang Taiyi. The sky shook as if the world was being severed into two.

Zu An was stunned. Xingtian was holding back back then. His axe is strong enough to even split the Golden Crow Princes in two.

“Know your place!” Donghuang Taiyi didn’t move in the least. He stood on the spot with his hands behind his back, his golden-embroidered black robe fluttering with the wind. He looked like a lofty immortal. 

Dong!

A deep chime echoed. The East Emperor Bell had reappeared around Donghuang Taiyi. Xingtian’s powerful axe hack didn’t leave the slightest mark on the bell. 

Instead, the rebound knocked Xingtian back by a kilometer before he regained his balance. He lowered his head and saw that his hands were bleeding profusely. Even so, he didn’t display the slightest fear. He even burst into laughter as his fighting spirit heightened. “Again!”

His muscles bulged, causing his physique to grow visibly. It didn’t take long for him to grow into a three-thousand-meter giant. He raised his humongous shield and charged at Donghuang Taiyi once more. The ground shook. 

Zu An was awed. The Shamans are indeed born warriors. Their bloodline harnesses a berserk factor that makes them stronger the longer the battle drags out for. 

Pah!

Despite the protection of the East Emperor Bell, Donghuang Taiyi was still knocked a hundred meters away under the sheer force of Xingtian’s charge.

“You’re courting death!” Donghuang Taiyi was infuriated. Despite not receiving any damage due to the East Emperor Bell’s protection, being knocked back in front of so many people pricked his pride. He waved his hand, and a surge of golden light sliced toward Xingtian’s neck. 

“Be careful!” Zu An was taken aback. In the legends, Xingtian’s head was lopped off. Could this be the cause of that?

Xingtian narrowed his eyes. The East Emperor Bell was so sturdy that the rebound caused his reactions to be delayed. He tried to raise his shield to guard his neck, but he was a step too late. He wasn’t going to make it in time. 

Just as he was about to be decapitated, a water dragon suddenly surged forth and collided with the golden light. 

Boom!

The water dragon and the golden light neutralized each other. 

“Taiyi, don’t you feel ashamed bullying the juniors?” A snake-bodied, human-headed man arrived at the battlefield riding on a wave. His fluttering red hair was particularly eye-catching. 

When the waves scattered, the cracked earth regained its vibrancy. Even the desert that Donghuang Taiyi had reduced into a lava pool was extinguished and transformed into a lake. 

Donghuang Taiyi’s face darkened. “Gonggong!”

Gonggong ignored him. He turned to Zu An and shot him a thumbs up. “You did great, lad. You have guts!”

Zu An felt conflicted. The final boss of his previous trip here had been none other than Gonggong, though the Gonggong was from another generation. Nevertheless, it still felt bizarre to receive the approval of the ‘antagonist’.

Donghuang Taiyi scoffed, “Gonggong, have you forgotten the agreement between the Ancestral Shamans and the Celestial Court? That brat killed my nine nephews. Do you intend to break the promise between our races for that murderer?”

Gonggong cackled. “You Celestials have been an eyesore for a long time now. If even a junior dares to stand up against you people, why should I, an Ancestral Shaman, cower?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2575: Saving Her
chapters.body:
Chapter 2575: Saving Her

Zu An burst into laughter. He'd thought that Gonggong would claim the moral high ground and lambast Donghuang Taiyi until the latter could no longer raise his head. Who could have thought that Gonggong wasn’t interested in reason at all?

He'd outright declared his determination to mess with them. How could anyone rebut that? 

Donghuang Taiyi’s eyelids twitched. “I can give you a fight if you want one, but I have other matters to settle today. I’ll fight you another day.”

Gonggong scoffed, “Do you take me for a fool? Who wants to fight you another day? I’ll fight you right now.”

The waves beneath Gonggong diverged into countless streams to simultaneously attack Donghuang Taiyi from multiple directions.

Donghuang Taiyi scoffed. A massive giant bell that harmonized with the dao manifested around him and blocked all the incoming water arrows. “How do you intend to fight me when you can’t even breach my defense?”

“We’ll have to fight to find out.” Gonggong laughed. A staff appeared in his hand, and he charged at Donghuang Taiyi.

The staff shone with a golden light. Zu An couldn’t help but stare at it. Why does it look so familiar? Could that be the future Sun Wukong’s Golden-hooped Staff?

Gonggong and the golden staff’s afterimages appeared all around Donghuang Taiyi, attacking the latter from different directions at an astounding speed. A deafening chime would sound with each strike, shaking up the spectators’ blood. Only by retreating fifty kilometers away did the impact of their clashes become more bearable.

“I am feeling better now…” the Goddess of Wu Mountain meekly told Zu An with a reddened face.

Zu An belatedly realized that he was still holding her in his arms. Alarmed, he quickly put her down and said, “My apologies. I was too focused on the battle…”

The sensation of her body lingered on his palm. Her body is so soft.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s heartbeat quickened as well, and she didn’t dare to look at him. She could only try to change the topic. “Who do you think will win?”

Zu An studied the two entities clashing in the sky. “Gonggong has been testing the East Emperor Bell’s defense from different angles for an opening, but he hasn’t found a breakthrough yet. The East Emperor Bell lives up to its reputation as the number one defensive artifact. It’s unlikely that Gonggong can defeat Donghuang Taiyi. At best, he can only push for a draw.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain said, “Gonggong is an Ancestral Shaman. His abilities can’t be underestimated. There’s a chance he can turn things around.”

Meanwhile, the battle was moving on to the next stage.

Even though Donghuang Taiyi remained unharmed despite Gonggong’s barrage of attacks, those who didn’t know better could think he had been overwhelmed and couldn’t counterattack. How could he, the great East Emperor, leave behind such a dark spot on his record?

Flames blazed from Donghuang Taiyi. He transformed into a massive phoenix and rushed toward Gonggong, burning the sky wherever he crossed. Gonggong’s afterimages were instantly devoured by the phoenix, but Donghuang Taiyi didn’t look satisfied with his counterattack. Instead, he looked at the ground with a grim expression.

A massive snake boasting a length of over thirty thousand meters suddenly rose from the ground. Despite its realistic appearance, it wasn't made out of blood and flesh, but water ki. This was Gonggong’s transformation.

The snake lurched at the phoenix. In response, the phoenix screeched and fought back. It was an intense fight where all sorts of laws and daos clashed, causing the world to shake. 

But Gonggong soon found himself at a disadvantage. The East Emperor Bell continued to protect the phoenix, allowing it to claim the upper hand each time they clashed. 

“Lord Gonggong, I’ll help you!” Xingtian noticed this subtle detail and charged into the fray with his massive axe and shield. 

“Scram! This is my business with him!” Gonggong roared in anger. 

He was already annoyed at failing to claim the upper hand, and this tactless junior still wanted to join the battle. If I fight him two on one, it won’t look good on me even if I win the battle!

That remark left Xingtian in an awkward position. 

Zu An flew to Xingtian’s side and said, “Don’t mind it. He needs to uphold his pride too. Leave it to me instead.”

Zu An drew his Sun Slaying Bow and aimed it at the phoenix in the sky. 

Xingtian anxiously exclaimed, “The Ancestral Shaman will reprimand you for this too!”

“I’m not a shaman; I don’t obey your Ancestral Shaman,” Zu An coldly spat. This was a rare opportunity to take down Donghuang Taiyi for good. He would be in trouble if Donghuang Taiyi got away, as there wouldn't necessarily be a Gonggong and a Xingtian to help him the next time.

“Brat, what are you doing?” Gonggong noticed Zu An’s actions and hissed.

“I’m just stretching my body. Don’t think too much of it.” Zu An fully drew his bowstring but didn’t fire the arrow.

Gonggong didn’t continue harping on it, since Zu An didn’t release his arrow. Besides, he had to focus on dealing with Donghuang Taiyi’s barrage of attacks. 

Zu An’s gesture, however, made Donghuang Taiyi extremely uncomfortable. Under normal circumstances, he wouldn’t fear Zu An’s arrow since he had the protection of his East Emperor Bell. But Zu An’s refusal to shoot meant that he had to constantly keep an eye out for Zu An and guard against the other party’s attack. Zu An’s red bow, Suzeng, was a deity-grade artifact. If he let his guard down, the tables could be turned quickly.

But being distracted in a fight against Gonggong put him at a huge disadvantage. In the first place, Gonggong’s strength rivaled his. His biggest advantage over Gonggong was the East Emperor Bell. But now that his East Emperor Bell needed to be ready to block the Sun Slaying Bow’s attack at any moment, he had to think twice before deploying it for Gonggong. 

This caused the tides of the battle to change. Donghuang Taiyi even had a few close calls, leaving him angry and frustrated. He wanted to charge over and kill Zu An first, but Gonggong wouldn’t give him the chance to do so. 

You have trolled Donghuang Taiyi for +888… +888… +888…

The Goddess of Wu Mountain chuckled. She looked at Zu An with gleaming eyes. Indeed, there’s nothing more threatening than an unshot arrow. To think that he would come up with such a solution. 

Perhaps Zu An’s actions looked lackadaisical, but in truth, he was focused on finding Donghuang Taiyi’s opening to unleash a fatal blow on him.

Suddenly, his hair stood on end. There were two exclamations from the side. “Be careful!”

A golden light flashed across the sky. He had never seen such a fast attack before. He'd have been able to dodge the attack under normal circumstances, but he was so focused on dealing with Donghuang Taiyi that he couldn’t react in time.

Fortunately, Xingtian was right beside him. With a furious roar, the mountain-like Xingtian stood in front of Zu An and raised his shield, but much to everyone’s horror, the golden light sliced through the shield and continued surging forth.

Blood splattered as Xingtian’s massive head was lopped off and flew into the air.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain transformed into a fog and enveloped Zu An to pull him to safety, but she, too, was scraped by the golden light. A moan escaped from her lips as she reverted to her human form. 

Sensing that she was about to fall, Zu An quickly grabbed her. Her face was ghastly pale. Blood seeped from the corners of her lips. The golden light had inflicted severe injuries on her even though it was just a scrape. Zu An quickly turned toward the culprit.

The golden light slowly morphed into a middle-aged man, who wore a crown with twelve dangling jade pieces. His body was wrapped in the sun’s flames. A celestial chart revolved behind him. A golden fire flower bloomed with each step he took in the air. He had a dashing appearance, but his cold eyes and sharp eagle nose made him look cold. 

“What a pity. You escaped from that attack,” a cold voice echoed. He eyed Zu An from the sky as if the latter was no more than an ant, though his cold eyes were filled with hatred. 

Zu An’s heart sank. The middle-aged man’s imperial gown, and the solar flames that resembled Donghuang Taiyi’s and the Golden Crow Ten Princes’ made his identity apparent. 

He was the next Celestial Emperor, Emperor Jun!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2576: His Life Is Mine
chapters.body:
Chapter 2576: His Life Is Mine

Zu An had no spare energy to reply to Emperor Jun. He rushed to Xingtian’s side with the Goddess of Wu Mountain in his arms to see if he could save Xingtian.

To his surprise, Xingtian’s headless body suddenly brandished his axe and shield as he roared, “I have not been defeated yet! Let’s continue the battle!” Shockwaves rippled from his weapons. Having his head lopped off made him so furious that his attacks were filled with murderous intent. It was impossible to approach him like that.

Zu An had no choice but to back away along with the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

“How can he still speak?” The Goddess of Wu Mountain gasped in astonishment.

Zu An pointed to Xingtian’s belly button. “His voice seems to be coming from there.”

He had naturally heard the story about the headless Xingtian brandishing his axe and shield, but he could never have imagined that Xingtian’s head would be lopped off while saving him. His blood raced at that thought.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain took a closer look. Xingtian’s belly button was indeed opening and closing like a mouth. “Let’s find his head. He might be able to put himself back together.” 

Stitching one’s head back onto their body wasn't difficult for cultivators of their caliber. 

Zu An nodded in agreement. He flashed toward Xingtian’s falling head. As he was about to grab the head, however, a ray of flames shot through it and reduced it to ashes. 

Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were shocked and infuriated. They looked back at the culprit.

Emperor Jun looked back at them with cold eyes. “Am I invisible to you?”

Xingtian howled chillingly. He clutched the severed stump of his neck as if his head were still there. Despite his head already being separated from his body, having it reduced to ashes still inflicted unbearable pain upon him. 

Two blood streams flowed down from his nipples. All of a sudden, they opened to reveal his new eyes. He glared at Emperor Jun with bloody tears in his eyes. 

“Die!” Xingtian roared from his belly button as he brandished his axe at Emperor Jun. A huge gorge was left on the ground wherever his axe crossed. 

Emperor Jun morphed into a golden light to dodge the attacks. 

However, light could be seen distorting in the wake of Xingtian’s attacks, indicating that they were strong enough to distort even space. That made Emperor Jun’s expression darken. He hadn’t expected Xingtian to become even stronger after having his head destroyed. 

Hmph, the Shaman races’ berserk bloodline is abominable!

The headless Xingtian leaped over to Emperor Jun’s side and unleashed dozens of cuts. 

Emperor Jun parried the attacks with his hand, but the attacks were so relentless that he began to lose his patience. “You’re courting death!”

Flames suddenly surged around Emperor Jun and rushed at Xingtian. 

Just then, however, a powerful wave gushed over and stopped the flames.

Emperor Jun was startled. He raised his head to look at Gonggong. Isn’t he fighting Donghuang Taiyi? How can he have the energy to interfere with my fight? Did something happen to Taiyi?

But Gonggong and Donghuang Taiyi were still clashing intensely with each other.

Gonggong noticed the situation as well and was surprised. “That lad can control water?”

Indeed, Zu An had used the Hundredwarble and Crown of the Ocean Deity’s abilities to borrow Gonggong’s water and extinguish Emperor Jun’s flames.

Donghuang Taiyi shouted out a warning.

Emperor Jun’s eyes fell on Zu An once more. Hatred flared in his eyes. “It’s you!”

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +444… +444… +444… 

Two grand wings unfurled behind Emperor Jun. Each feather on his wings was comparable to a deity-grade artifact, and they shrouded Zu An with their sharp sword ki. 

Having fought the Fiend Emperor and the Golden Crows in the Cultivation World, Zu An was familiar with this move. But it was Emperor Jun executing it this time, so it was more than a thousand times stronger. After sensing its terrifying destructive prowess, Zu An didn’t dare let his guard down. He quickly sent the Goddess of Wu Mountain to safety with a gentle force before drawing his Tai’e Sword to confront the sword ki. 

In a matter of moments, Zu An was already inflicted with many sword wounds. Fresh blood oozed from his injuries. Even his manifested East Emperor Bell was instantly destroyed by the sword ki. He had only survived because of his Heaven-Devouring Sutra, but even so, he was still unable to neutralize most of the ki.

Emperor Jun was shocked by Zu An as he hadn’t held back in his attacks. The latter had killed his nine sons and ruined his plans. He hated Zu An so much that he wanted nothing more than to grind the other party’s bones into ashes.

His first attack had been blocked by Xingtian’s head, but he hadn’t expected Zu An to take his second attack head-on and survive!

How can someone of his cultivation withstand my attack?

Emperor Jun gritted his teeth. He was about to charge at Zu An when the headless Xingtian roared and charged at him once more. His expression turned cold as he directed his sword ki toward Xingtian.

Xingtian raised his shield to protect himself while brandishing his axe to cut down the golden sword feathers. It didn’t take long before he was bleeding profusely, but that further invoked his bloodline power, causing his attacks to become more ferocious, like a war god.

Worried that Xingtian could slip up, Zu An quickly attacked Emperor Jun to share Xingtian’s burden.

“You despicable rats!” Emperor Jun spat through gritted teeth. 

He could have easily defeated either of them individually. Xingtian was strong, but he specialized in physical confrontations. Any skills hurled at him would have put him in a fluster. But Zu An, despite his lower cultivation, happened to have all kinds of random methods. The sheer number of abilities he had shocked even Emperor Jun. 

Their coordination covered each other’s flaws, making it difficult for Emperor Jun to take them down quickly. Of course, that didn’t mean he couldn’t defeat them; it would just take him longer than anticipated. 

But what he lacked the most now was time. The ten suns in the sky had caused such a huge commotion that it was bound to catch the attention of all kinds of powerhouses. Gonggong was merely the first to arrive. Things could get messy if the other Ancestral Shamans or Zhuanxu came too.

He suddenly noticed the worried Goddess of Wu Mountain in his peripheral vision, and a thought surfaced in his mind. He feigned an attack before thrusting his palm at the distant Goddess of Wu Mountain.

A golden palmprint instantly appeared before the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Space compressed wherever the palmprint crossed, as if gravity had been amplified ten thousand times. If this attack landed, the goddess would immediately go splat. 

The Kaiming Six Shamans wanted to save her, but they were too far away to make it in time. 

Zu An was horrified. He performed an instantaneous movement to get to the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s side, but there was no time for him to escape with her. He could only embrace her and take on the attack with his back. 

Pu!

Despite Zu An’s countless defense skills and the protection of the Heaven-Devouring Sutra’s devouring power, he was still unable to neutralize the palm strike and spat large mouthfuls of blood. Even pieces of his innards were visible amid the blood. 

“Are you all right?!” the usually cold Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed in a fluster. She hurriedly cast her recovery spells to treat his injuries. 

Zu An didn’t answer. With his current regenerative prowess, he could make a full recovery even if someone blasted half his body into bits, but it went without saying that Emperor Jun wouldn’t give him the time to recover.

Xingtian tried to stall Emperor Jun, but the latter had already anticipated this situation. While dealing with Xingtian’s attacks, Emperor Jun thrust his palm once more. If this palm strike landed, both Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain would be reduced to a bloody mist. 

Gonggong wanted to help, but Donghuang Taiyi wouldn’t give him the chance.

Just then, another sun suddenly appeared and blocked that devastating palm strike. 

“What are you doing?!” Emperor Jun was shocked and infuriated.

The sun slowly morphed into a beautiful woman. “His life is mine. I shall end him personally!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2577: Pain
chapters.body:
Chapter 2577: Pain

A searing crack appeared in the sky. A ravishing, mature lady emerged from billowing golden clouds. She was dressed in a fluttering, long skirt embroidered with 360 days of constellation patterns. Her hair resembled a golden waterfall, and her body was cloaked in a faint purple mist. It was as if sunset and twilight were in harmony on her impeccable face. She had a divine and lofty air.

She stared at Zu An coldly, looking as if she would tear him into pieces.

Emperor Jun nodded. “All right, I’ll hand him to you.” He then charged at Xingtian, hoping to use this opportunity to end the latter sooner.

Alarmed, the Goddess of Wu Mountain telepathically urged the Kaiming Six Shamans to aid Xingtian. 

Two intense battles raged on the battlefield, but somehow, Zu An’s side had quieted down instead.

Looking at the woman, Zu An bitterly remarked, “Sun Goddess Xihe!”

The last time he was here, he'd followed Yi’s heroic path and shot down the nine suns, prompting the Sun Goddess Xihe to emerge and exact vengeance on him. Back then, he'd been too weak to survive Xihe’s rampage, so he had no choice but to use the Golden Phoenix’s ability to make her fall in love with him. Thanks to that, he escaped with his life. There was no way he would forget her face. 

However, he had gone farther back in time now. He was likely thousands or even tens of thousands of years behind in terms of the timeline compared to the last time he was here. She might not be affected by the Golden Phoenix yet.

“We meet again. Last time, I told you that I’d kill you.” Xihe glared at him with hatred.

Zu An’s mind jolted. I’m not surprised that she knows me, but how does she remember the conversation we had the last time we were here? I’m now tens of thousands of years back in time; she shouldn’t know something that hasn’t happened yet.

For a moment, he even doubted time itself.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shouted, “Sun Goddess, you should know about the deaths caused by the emergence of the ten suns. They would have been sentenced to death either way. He was merely exacting punishment on behalf of heaven!”

“Shut your mouth!” The Sun Goddess coldly waved her hand, and her jade bracelets clanged resoundingly against one another. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain felt as if the stars were shifting around her. She was sent flying and spat a mouthful of blood. 

The Sun Goddess coldly eyed the Goddess of Wu Mountain. “They are my sons.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain understood where the Sun Goddess was coming from. The Golden Crows might have committed grievous sins, but they are still her children. There’s no way she can spare the man who murdered her children. 

Zu An rushed to the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s side and inspected her body. The latter had already been severely injured before, and this blow pushed her further into death’s embrace. He took out a bunch of spirit medicine and fed it to her while addressing the Sun Goddess. “I’m the one you’re after. Don’t implicate the innocent.”

“Oh? Does your heart bleed to see her injured?” the Sun Goddess sneered. “I remember seeing more beautiful women by your side the last time we met, but she wasn’t one of them.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was baffled. Why does her tone sound so off? It’s almost as if… she’s envious. Hold on, I also sensed something unusual from that woman before. Could it be…

Just then, Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. “It hasn’t been long since we last met. It’s not as if you haven’t met Yaoji.”

Upon hearing him call her by her name, the Goddess of Wu Mountain blushed. 

The Sun Goddess narrowed her eyes. “I don’t know what you’re talking about.”

“You don’t?” Zu An settled the Goddess of Wu Mountain down on a nearby peak before facing the Sun Goddess. “Should I call you the Sun Goddess… or Yumen Beiqing?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain widened her eyes. Just as I thought!

The Sun Goddess was taken aback. She was silent for a long time before asking, “When did you find out?”

Zu An felt stifled by her admittance. He explained, “When you provided me with a clue to Manman’s whereabouts, but no one saw her except for those in the first town we visited. It struck me that there could be something wrong with the intel. However, I didn’t think you had a reason to deceive me then. Little sister Ling’er wouldn’t do that out of envy.”

“When did you realize I wasn’t your little sister Ling’er?” 

“I didn’t doubt you initially. The Fiend races were hostile toward me, but you unconditionally sided with me and even…” 

At this point, Zu An and the Sun Goddess’ faces reddened as their hearts skipped a beat. They unceremoniously recalled what had happened in the shadows of the night… 

The Sun Goddess took a deep breath. “Stop beating around the bush and get to the point.”

Zu An reined himself in and continued, “You led me to the imperial tomb using the Brilliant Pinflower as bait, but it wasn’t there. Later, I realized that you were trying to lure me to this world.

“When we arrived in this world, there was a mysterious power outburst near our cavern’s vicinity. I thought something had happened to you and rushed out, only to see you returning. Your reason made sense, but it was still too coincidental. Especially because I sensed the Sun Goddess’ power there.”

The Sun Goddess scoffed, “It’s all the Time Scribe’s fault. You wouldn’t have noticed it so quickly if not for him.”

“So you were the one who killed the Time Scribe.” Zu An nodded in realization. He had been feeling uneasy, as the Time Scribe had disappeared. “Indeed, you wouldn’t have been able to kill him without using your true powers.
