“There were many lapses in your disguise afterward. I wouldn’t have doubted you based on those, but with my suspicion already piqued, these giveaways were conspicuous.” Zu An was perplexed. “The only thing I don’t get is why you've been doing all these things.”

“Someone as smart as you should be able to fathom a guess,” the Sun Goddess sneered. 

Zu An thought about the time they had spent together. “You were trying to change history, or more specifically, the fate of the Golden Crows.”

The Sun Goddess lowered her gaze in despair. “I tried everything. I tried to save Yayu. I tried to protect Bao Jiang… But nothing worked. I even tried to kill Yi in advance, but fate pulled a prank on me—you became the new Yi. I realized that the hands of fate are correcting my actions. I can’t change anything no matter how I struggle.”

Zu An fell silent. The Golden Crows deserved death, but she’s a noble mother. She did all these things to save her children, but she was unable to outmaneuver fate.

“Where are Manman and Xie Daoyun?” As much as he sympathized with her, there were some things that he needed to get to the bottom of.

“I killed them,” the Sun Goddess sneered.

“What?!” Zu An was shocked and infuriated. He was so shaken that he struggled to maintain his balance. 

His reaction brought glee to the Sun Goddess’ face. “Do you finally understand the pain of losing a loved one now? Don’t worry, you can join them in death!”

With that, she morphed into thousands of rays of golden light that charged toward Zu An. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2578: Shocking Gossip
chapters.body:
Chapter 2578: Shocking Gossip

“Be careful!” the Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed.

Zu An was still feeling downtrodden, but the Sun Goddess had already begun her onslaught. Unfortunately, the Goddess of Wu Mountain was too severely injured to help him even if she wanted to.  

Her voice was nowhere as fast as the Sun Goddess, who was already before Zu An. As if vanquished, everything else vanished in the presence of her light. 

Emperor Jun nodded in approval. He'd been dissatisfied when he saw how Xihe was having a pleasant chat with Zu An instead of killing the latter. Just as he was about to rush her, she'd finally made a move and gone all out. That put his mind at ease.

“Be careful! Despite that fellow’s low cultivation, he possesses many bizarre skills. He isn’t that easy of an enemy to deal with!” Emperor Jun warned when he sensed how Zu An was still alive despite Xihe’s attack. 

Xihe coldly looked toward the ground. Zu An was standing there, gasping for air. 

“You’re really after my life!” Zu An sweated profusely when he thought about the earlier situation. If not for his position swap ability, that attack would have taken his life. He had felt that aura before. It was similar to the attack that the mysterious Time Scribe had died to. Looks like Xihe really wants me dead. 

Xihe scoffed, “Did you think I would swear loyalty to you just because I went to bed with you?”

She darted toward Zu An while unleashing rays of golden light. Her light vanquished everything in its path, even severing mountains apart. Zu An didn’t dare to let his guard down. He mustered his attention to deal with her onslaught. 

Meanwhile, the Goddess of Wu Mountain widened her eyes in shock. Even though the two of them had previously behaved like a couple, considering Xihe’s true identity, she hadn't thought they would be involved in a physical relationship. Learning that the two of them had reached that level of intimacy was a shock.

Even the warring Donghuang Taiyi and Gonggong paused out of sheer astonishment.

Donghuang Taiyi looked at Zu An and Xihe in utmost shock. Xihe, as one of the loftiest goddesses of the Celestial Court, had plenty of suitors, and Donghuang Taiyi had been one of them too. But fate had other plans, and she'd eventually become his sister-in-law. 

Nevertheless, he'd still harbored special feelings for his beautiful sister-in-law, which was why he doted on her ten sons a lot. Others had even jokingly remarked that the Golden Crows were his sons. He would sternly warn them not to run their mouths, but he still felt warm inside. 

That was partly why he'd been so angry when he learned that Zu An had killed the Golden Crows.

However, because of his brotherly ties with Emperor Jun, he had never overstepped the line. He also respected Xihe for her upright character as well, so he'd never harbored inappropriate thoughts toward her. He'd thought that it was sufficient bliss to admire her and wish her happiness. 

And yet, the goddess of his heart had rolled in the sheets with another man? And that man was a human, at that!

No, she must be saying that to deceive me! 

He tried convincing himself of that, but deep down, he knew the truth. Many images flashed through his mind—the lofty goddess whom he didn’t dare to touch had been thoroughly sullied by a far lesser man of lowly bloodline! This made his anger flare. He was on the verge of exploding.

You have successfully trolled Donghuang Taiyi for +999… +999… +999…

In contrast to the infuriated Donghuang Taiyi, Gonggong shot a thumbs up at Zu An and burst into laughter. “You’re a completely different breed!”

He was already in awe of Zu An for daring to kill the Golden Crows—that was a feat that even the powerhouses dared not do—but to think that there was something more explosive than that!

He had been long displeased with the lofty celestials, but he had to admit that they were getting increasingly formidable. The world was slowly becoming accustomed to the rule of the Celestial Court. And yet, the next Celestial Emperor’s wife had been bedded by a mortal! This would become a stain on Emperor Jun's and the Celestial Court’s reputation!

I've never seen such a huge drama before! It was worth making the trip here!

He had already decided to spread this news even if everyone else kept it a secret. He would even dispatch his subordinates to plaster posters and advertise this matter. Thinking about the mess it would cause made him grin widely.

The Kaiming Six Shamans, already injured from the fighting, silently retreated in the face of Emperor Jun’s terrifying outburst of energy. It was clear to see that Emperor Jun was on the verge of going on a rampage. Only someone tired of living would trifle with him now. 

They couldn’t help but sneak glances at Zu An. When they first saw the latter, they hadn’t thought there was anything special about him other than his looks, his height, and his ability to charm women. They would never have expected him to pull off something this insane.

Why is even the legendary Sun Goddess interested in him?

The Kaiming Six Shamans couldn’t make sense of the situation.

Emperor Jun had been confident that everything was in his grasp when he suddenly heard this explosive news. Xihe had mentioned something along this line the last time they met, but he'd thought that she was uttering nonsense in a fit of anger and shrugged it off. He was the lofty Celestial Emperor, whereas Zu An was an insignificant mortal. Any woman with eyes would know who to side with, right?

But Xihe’s words and that brat’s reaction hinted that it was real. The thought of his wife being under another man’s body made him so angry that he could pop a vein!

Is Xihe doing this to exact vengeance on me? Why? Why?!

He roared, “You scoundrel! I’ll tear your tendons and crush your bones! I’ll burn your soul with the sun’s true flames for a hundred thousand years!”

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +1024… +1024… +1024…

He was about to charge at Zu An when a figure blocked his path. 

It was Xingtian, who still had blood tears flowing down his nipples. “Your opponent is me!”

“Scram!” Emperor Jun roared in anger, but he couldn’t shake off Xingtian no matter how he tried. Xingtian was so sturdy that he firmly stood his ground no matter how battered his body was. Instead, his bloodline’s war god prowess made him more valiant the more he fought.

Seeing this, the Kaiming Six Shamans had no choice but to muster their courage to help Xingtian. Damn it! He’s having fun while the rest of us are suffering.

Zu An sneezed. Looking at the insane influx of Rage points in his system, he couldn’t help but wonder why Xihe had chosen to reveal this matter in public. Despite being enemies now, he had never thought of blackmailing her with it. 

So why did she choose to reveal it on her own accord? She has nothing to gain out of this!

While he was perplexed, he suddenly received a telepathic message. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2579: Gamble With My Life
chapters.body:
Chapter 2579: Gamble With My Life

Zu An was taken aback to hear the content of the message, though he didn’t let anything show on his face. 

Xihe’s attack was becoming fiercer. As the Sun Goddess, she commanded the sun’s brilliance. The sun could be gentle and warm, but it could also be wild and destructive. Mountains were crushed wherever they passed. 

Zu An faced several close shaves. He would have long been torn into shreds if not for his rich combat experience and arsenal of mystical abilities.

Watching as Zu An was continuously pushed in his direction, Emperor Jun snarled. He finally found a chance to knock Xingtian back and charge out of the Kaiming Six Shamans’ encirclement to hurl a palm strike at Zu An’s back. 

It was beneath his standing to attack a human, let alone such an assault. However, not a single person frowned on his action. If anything, they thought that he was too late to make a move.

Xihe radiated a brilliant golden light to seal off the surrounding space, giving Zu An no chance to slip away. 

Emperor Jun nodded in glee. She’s my wife, after all. Despite being estranged in recent years, our coordination remains impeccable. 

“Be careful!” 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain anxiously unleashed the skill she had been channeling. A massive fog suddenly enveloped Emperor Jun in an attempt to stop his attack. 

However, the flames around Emperor Jun flared, and the fog was instantly vaporized. The Goddess of Wu Mountain coughed from the backlash and nearly fainted. She was unable to help Zu An despite having depleted the final sliver of her energy. 

But the split second she'd bought for Zu An turned out to be invaluable. Zu An was able to pull himself together to flash twenty meters away and dodge Emperor Jun’s palm strike.

But Emperor Jun wasn't that easy to deal with. Like a maggot suckling on a bone, he relentlessly pursued Zu An and unleashed yet another strike. This fellow is slippery like a loach. It’s hard for me to kill him alone, but now that Xihe is cooperating with me, it’ll be much easier.

Suddenly, Emperor Jun’s eyes widened. He abandoned his pursuit of Zu An and instead morphed into a golden light to dodge sideward. However, in his fluster, he forgot that Xihe had already sealed off the surrounding space with her golden light, rendering any teleportation abilities useless. 

Excruciating pain assaulted his lower back. Without any hesitation, he thrust his palm to knock away Xihe, who had just assaulted him. 

At the same moment, however, Zu An suddenly appeared beside him to plunge a pitch-black dagger at his ribcage. The ominous runes on the dagger alarmed Emperor Jun. Despite his strength and standing, he still felt threatened by the weapon. Golden light flared from his body as he quickly activated all his defensive methods.

But Zu An and Xihe’s cooperation was so impeccable that Emperor Jun couldn’t completely avoid the attack despite unleashing his full strength. He managed to avoid being struck in his vitals, but the pitch-black dagger still cut into his arm. 

Emperor Jun used his artifacts to block further attacks from Zu An and Xihe so that he could retreat and catch a breather. He stared at his arm in horror, looking nowhere as relaxed as before. A black aura containing bizarre runes was slowly creeping up his arm. 

In retaliation, the divine marks on his arm flickered, and a golden light shone to cleanse away the black runes, but for some reason, it wasn’t working. Instead, the black aura was gradually forcing back his golden light. 

Emperor Jun was one of the supreme gods of the Celestial Court. His body was in harmony with the dao, granting him formidable recovery and cleansing abilities. Malevolent energies shouldn’t have been able to breach his defenses! 

“What’s wrong with that dagger?!” Horrified, Emperor Jun unhesitatingly sliced off his injured arm. 

The black aura devoured his arm as soon as it was severed from his body. It withered at a visible pace and turned into white bones. 

Emperor Jun’s eyelids twitched. With his cultivation, his corpse wasn't supposed to wither for a million years after his death. His divine bones should also have been as pristine as the finest jade, emanating a faint divine light and pressure. And yet, his severed arm was already decomposing.

Meanwhile, Zu An and Xihe broke free from Emperor Jun’s defensive artifacts to flank him. What a pity! That was such a good chance to kill Emperor Jun! As expected of the Celestial Emperor. He survived despite being struck by the Poisonous Prick. 

Earlier, Xihe had sent him a telepathic message suggesting that the two of them put on a show to deal with Emperor Jun. Since there was no reason for her to turn against Emperor Jun, Zu An couldn’t tell if Xihe was trying to entrap him. If this was her ploy, Zu An could find himself cornered by the celestial couple. 

But when he looked into her eyes and remembered the time they had spent together, he'd still decided to trust her once more. It wasn't entirely a reckless decision, but rather, reflecting on the time they had spent together had left him with a guess. 

Besides, there was no need for Xihe to resort to such trickery. The combined prowess of the celestial couple was more than enough to corner him. There was no need to do something this superfluous.

So, he'd decided to gamble with his life. 

Xihe was surprised at first when Zu An agreed to go along with her, but in the end, they'd succeeded in injuring Emperor Jun. 

A divine light shone from Emperor Jun’s severed right arm, and it didn’t take long for a new arm to sprout out. As a Golden Immortal, even if his body and Primordial Spirit were destroyed, he could make a full recovery as long as he had a single droplet of blood essence or soul fragment left; healing an arm was nothing in comparison. 

Of course, that came at a price. If he were to sustain such grievous damage, it was unlikely that he would ever reach Sainthood.  

This minor injury wouldn’t hinder him from reaching Sainthood, but the effects of that bizarre dagger left him at least 10% weaker than when he was at his peak. His complexion darkened. How could I, the Celestial Emperor, be pushed so far by a mere ant?

Of course, what angered him the most was still his wife’s betrayal. How dare that slut collude with that scoundrel to assassinate me? Turns out I was the laughingstock all along!

The knowledge that Zu An had not only conquered his wife’s body, but also her heart, made Emperor Jun seethe with anger. 

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +1024… +1024… +1024…

“Why?!” Emperor Jun glared at his wife. He couldn’t understand why she would do this.

They had been estranged for many years now, but they were still a couple on the surface. On top of that, Zu An had just killed nine of her sons. He couldn’t understand why she would side with Zu An to assassinate him. 

Are Zu An’s techniques so great that she can overlook his crimes of killing her sons? 

The images that popped into Emperor Jun’s mind made his face darken further. 

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +999… +999… +999…

Xihe glared back at Emperor Jun. “You know what you have done better than anyone else!”

“What did I do?” Emperor Jun was so furious that he began laughing manically. “That fellow killed our nine sons! I am trying to avenge our sons now! Instead of helping me, you collude with him to deal with me. You are unworthy of being a mother!”

“Shut your mouth!” Xihe’s mouth trembled. “You know who killed our sons better than anyone!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2580: The Truth
chapters.body:
Chapter 2580: The Truth

Even Zu An was dumbstruck, let alone the others. Huh? Wasn’t I the one who shot down the nine Golden Crows?

The Goddess of Wu Mountain stared at Xihe weirdly. 

Could she have fallen in love with Zu An, so she’s subconsciously trying to find an excuse to absolve him of blame? 

Emperor Jun was probably the one who instigated the simultaneous appearance of the ten suns to activate his vile formations, so it wouldn’t be a stretch to say that he’s played a part in the ten suns’ deaths. 

Still, should I say that Xihe is blinded by love, or that Zu An is too charming? To think that she would overlook even her sons’ murderer…

Emperor Jun was infuriated. While glaring at Xihe, he pointed at Zu An and roared, “Xihe, are you out of your mind? Anyone with eyes can tell that he killed our sons! Are you so infatuated with him that you would go this far to defend him?”

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +811… +811… +811…

Those words pricked Emperor Jun’s heart as well. Even though he was a philanderer, and the women around him often expressed dissatisfaction about it, he would console them by saying that he was just fulfilling his physical needs, and that his heart was still with them.

He hadn't realized it would hurt this much when he was on the receiving side.

Donghuang Taiyi interjected, “Indeed, sister-in-law. Your misunderstandings with my big brother can be resolved in private. It isn’t appropriate to thrash them out in front of outsiders.”

He couldn’t understand why Xihe was siding with that man. Did he thoroughly conquer her?

That thought made his complexion turn awful.

You have successfully trolled Donghuang Taiyi for +911… +911… +911…

Gonggong looked at Zu An with eyes of admiration, wondering how the latter pulled it off.  Moments ago, he had still been viewing Zu An as a junior, but now, he was thinking that he should call him ‘senior’ instead. 

Xihe glared at Emperor Jun with eyes brimming with hatred and tears. “I’m defending him? Isn’t it because you have long since killed my sons?”

“What?!” Everyone was stunned to hear that.

Most people thought Xihe had gone crazy, but some fell into contemplation.

Emperor Jun frowned. “You woman! How can you slander me with such nonsense to defend your lover?”

Xihe glared at him. “You thought you had fooled everyone, but your Ten Suns Apocalypse Formation requires the ten Golden Crows’ powerful souls as formation cores. To keep others from realizing what you have done, you severed fragments of your soul and possessed their bodies. You had them travel around to create a false impression that they were still alive.” 

“Ten Suns Apocalypse Formation!” Gonggong was startled.

Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain exchanged looks and saw the shock in each other’s eyes. This was aligned with the intel they had gathered thus far. 

Emperor Jun’s face darkened. “I don’t know where you heard these rumors from, but they are utterly ridiculous! I am the Celestial Emperor; all living beings are my subjects. What reason do I have to do this?”

The Kaiming Six Shamans nodded. Despite being enemies, they couldn’t understand Emperor Jun’s motive for doing this.

“You’re seeking to eradicate the Shaman races so that the Fiend races will be the sole ruler of the world. You hope that this accomplishment can prop you up to Sainthood,” Xihe replied coldly, her words echoing loudly in the world.

Gonggong cursed in anger, “I’ve always thought this fellow was a dirtbag, but he’s worse than I thought!”

Emperor Jun’s cheeks twitched. He could already sense several unique divine senses paying attention to the situation here. He took a deep breath and replied, “What a load of nonsense! I am the Celestial Emperor, and I am a mere step away from Sainthood. I have no need to take such a risk to do something this depraved.

“Besides, the Shaman races are no weaker than our Fiend races. I would have to be out of my mind to oppose them single-handedly! Not to mention that I would lose my place in the Celestial Court too.” 

His explanation had many powerhouses nodding in agreement. The logic behind Xihe’s accusations didn’t check out. Is she saying this because she fell in love with another man?

Zu An spoke up as well. “It goes without saying that you couldn’t deal with the Shaman races alone, so you had to incite conflict between the races to spark a war.”

Emperor Jun’s eyes widened. “I haven’t settled the score with you yet. How dare you make such slanderous remarks about me?”

He was about to make a move when he was interrupted by a water arrow from the side. It was Gonggong. “Let him speak his piece.”

Donghuang Taiyi’s eyebrows shot up. The drama had been so exciting that their fight stalled to a halt. That gave Gonggong the opportunity to interfere with the situation on that side.

Zu An took this opportunity to speak up. “You exploited Gu’s swelling ambitions and had him goad Er Fu into killing Yayu. The Celestial Court was bound to punish Er Fu after he committed such a grievous crime. All you had to do then was to stoke public opinion. If the Celestial Court sentenced Er Fu to death, Zhulong and the Shaman races would resent the incumbent Celestial Emperor. With that, the seeds of hatred would be planted.

“Given Zhulong’s standing, there was a good chance he could obtain an immortal medicine to revive Yayu. That would create a chance for Gu to obtain the immortal medicine. But as long as Gu killed Celestial Bao Jiang, whether out of his greed or your incitement, the Celestial Court would have to sentence him to death as an example to the others.

“No matter how things went, Ancestral Shaman Zhulong would lose at least two of his sons. That's not a grudge that could be overlooked that easily. Even though Zhulong chose to stay put, Gu’s mother, Nuba, challenged the Celestial Court’s authority. There were also several individuals stirring the Shaman races’ emotions to rile them up. I bet you planted those people on Zhong Mountain beforehand to control the narrative.”

Emperor Jun coldly harrumphed. “A load of nonsense!”

Zu An didn’t bother arguing with him and continued, “But you didn’t expect the incumbent Celestial Emperor to resolve this conflict by severing heaven from earth. The sacrifice of his most talented grandson appeased the Shaman races.

“Of course, you wouldn’t give up just like that. You intentionally leaked the news that you were responsible for her son’s death to Nuba, prompting her to go around cursing you out. It wasn’t convenient for you to confront her, so you had the Golden Crow Princes deal with her instead. Even if the Golden Crow Princes went overboard, the Celestial Court would show them leniency on the grounds that they were doing it out of filial piety. Some in the Celestial Court might be disgruntled, but they wouldn’t be able to say anything about it.

“But the Shaman races wouldn’t take it lying down. Renowned shamans like Kuafu and Xingtian confronted the Golden Crow Princes for an explanation, but the former was burned to death, whereas the latter had his head lopped off by you.” Zu An briefly paused as he wondered whether to mention that Emperor Jun had killed Yi too, but he held back since he had now become Yi. 

“I just exacted vengeance for Kuafu by killing nine of the Golden Crows. That gave you the perfect excuse to step forward and kill me.”

“The Shaman races are hot-tempered. Given the conflicts between the races over the years, and the recent deaths of so many important shamans, the Shaman races were bound to come after you for vengeance. Gonggong is an example of that.”

Gonggong puffed up his chest. That was indeed something he would do. 

Zu An sharply eyed Emperor Jun. “The Celestial Court wouldn’t know that you were behind this, so they wouldn’t sit still as the Shaman races sought to kill their next Celestial Emperor. An all-out war would ensue, and both sides would fight to the bitter end. When the fight heated up, you could bring out the Apocalypse Formation to massacre the Shaman races. Not only would the Celestial Court not blame you for it, they would even be thankful to you for your ample preparations. With that, you would have secured yourself a huge accomplishment. Am I right, future Celestial Emperor?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2581: Show Of Hands
chapters.body:
Chapter 2581: Show Of Hands

Zu An had previously harbored many doubts about the situation, but the clues swiftly connected when Xihe told him that Emperor Jun had already possessed the ten Golden Crows and stolen their souls to fuel the Apocalypse Formation.

Gonggong was horrified and enraged. “What a vicious man you are, Emperor Jun! I underestimated you!”

Emperor Jun’s face darkened. “Hah! How naive must you be to trust that brat’s far-fetched conjecture?”

“Am I to trust a deceitful scumbag like you over him?” Gonggong scoffed.

Emperor Jun was rendered speechless.

“What did you do to Xiaoxi?” Zu An asked aloud. He was feeling increasingly uneasy. Someone who could stoop to murdering his sons wouldn’t hold back against Xiaoxi.

“So it was for her.” Emperor Jun was at the peak of his anger. “Why don’t I send you to her side if you miss her so much?”

As he fully unleashed his aura as the next Celestial Emperor, two massive wings sprouted from Emperor Jun’s back. He jolted Xingtian backward with a powerful shockwave before morphing into a golden light that moved faster than the eye could follow.

“Careful!” Xihe anxiously shoved Zu An aside before transforming into a sun to confront Emperor Jun.

However, the golden light was so formidable that the sun was immediately split in two.

Horrified, Zu An hurriedly charged forward. The sun’s avatar faded, revealing Xihe’s plummeting figure.

Zu An swiftly caught her, only to see a deep laceration that exposed her bones across her chest. Her beautiful face had turned ghastly pale. He was relieved that Xihe was strong enough to withstand the attack, or else it would have split her in two. 

Seeing how naturally Xihe snuggled into Zu An’s arms, Emperor Jun’s eyelids twitched. “Very well. You adulterous couple dare to flaunt your love in front of me! I’ll make you suffer a fate worse than death!”

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +999… +999… +999…

He unfurled his wings. As he morphed into a golden light and charged at the duo, his feathers transformed into sharp weapons. 

He was truly enraged. This was the third time the golden light had appeared on this battlefield. The first time, it had severed Xingtian’s shield and head. The second time, it had severely wounded Xihe. This time, he was planning to kill Zu An and Xihe together. 

Gonggong tried to rush over to rescue Zu An, only to be blocked by Donghuang Taiyi. “Your opponent is me.”

Unable to bypass Donghuang Taiyi, Gonggong could only watch as everything unfolded. Deep down, he knew that Zu An and Xihe were no match for the enraged Emperor Jun, especially now that Xihe had been incapacitated.

A seven-colored light suddenly shone. The golden light that was going to slice Zu An and Xihe apart vanished. 

Gonggong was speechless, while Donghuang Taiyi was shocked. They couldn’t even sense Emperor Jun’s aura anymore, as if he had been erased from the world.

The Kaiming Six Shamans were dumbfounded. Only the Goddess of Wu Mountain realized what had happened. He’s finally used that ability, but is it enough to trap a Celestial Emperor?

Xihe stared at the man holding her in his arms. She had hated him so much that she wanted nothing more than to tear him apart, but perhaps due to the time they had spent together and the effects of the other party’s Golden Phoenix ability, she realized that she had unknowingly fallen in love with him.

She'd had many chances to kill him, but still hesitated and eventually held herself back. Even so, at the same time, she couldn’t forgive him for killing her sons. This contradiction had tortured her. 

Later, when she learned that the culprit behind her sons’ deaths wasn't him but Emperor Jun, she'd felt as if a weight had been lifted from her heart. She was relieved. 

There were so many words she wanted to tell him, but it all condensed into a single sentence. “Be careful. He isn’t that easy to deal with.”

Zu An knew that too. 

In the Seven-Colored Divine Halo’s world, Emperor Jun transformed into a thirty-thousand-meter-wide Golden Crow. His wings were so vast that they covered the sky. Countless artifacts floated in his vicinity, emanating ancient auras. Golden lights pulsated from Emperor Jun’s body, causing the Seven-Colored Divine Halo’s world to shake intensely as if it would shatter at any moment. A piercing, majestic bird call echoed.

Emperor Jun finally broke free from the Seven-Colored Divine Halo’s world and circled the sky as he gazed down on Zu An in astonishment. A mere mortal can actually imprison me! Wouldn’t I have been in danger if he were any stronger?

His eyes turned cold. I can’t let someone like that roam free even if I have no grudge against him. Otherwise, there is a chance that he might become a threat to me. 

He flapped his wings, and the world trembled. 

Zu An embraced Xihe tightly and activated his position swap skill. But to his horror, he appeared right in front of Emperor Jun instead. He must have changed the world laws here to make me teleport in front of him!

“An ant like you can’t begin to comprehend what beings like us are capable of.” 

Emperor Jun didn’t even make a move—he simply looked at Zu An—and Zu An’s body began disintegrating as if eroding to the wind. No matter how he channeled his cultivation techniques, he could only slow the rate of disintegration. 

“It’s futile. He has the power to modify world laws. I never thought I’d end up dying with you,” Xihe said with a conflicted expression. Even she had no idea if she was sad or heartbroken.

But Zu An refused to submit to fate. His only option was to use the power of Keyboard Come, but if he used it in his current state, he would likely die. Even so, he was determined to kill Emperor Jun even if it spelled his death!

He was about to speak when ancient runes suddenly enveloped him. They shone with peculiar lights that protected him and Xihe. 

“The Shaman races’ ancient runes!” Zu An had seen similar characters when he used the Shaman races’ secret art. 

His body’s disintegration halted, and he even began to recover. He took this opportunity to channel the Primordial Origin Sutra to heal his own and Xihe’s wounds. The ki around him pulsated, and before he knew, they were already some distance away from Emperor Jun. 

Emperor Jun’s massive avatar didn’t pursue them. Instead, he looked warily in their direction.

Sensing something, Zu An turned around to take a look. What he saw caused him to freeze up. Behind him was a snake of inestimable size. Even the largest dragon he had seen was nothing but a lizard compared to it. The snake had brilliant eyes—one was reminiscent of the sun while the other resembled the cold moonlight. 

Xihe immediately recognized the snake. “Ancestral Shaman Zhulong!”

Zhulong’s standing and strength was top-notch even among the Ancestral Shamans. Past generations of Celestial Emperors had treated him with utmost respect. It had been ten thousand years since he last made an appearance, which led to rumors that he had passed away and returned to the dao. 

Zu An was shocked. He had encountered many huge things that triggered his fear, but those paled in comparison to the humongous entity right before him. The pressure emanated by Zhulong was overwhelming. His only source of relief was that Zhulong wasn’t an enemy. In fact, it was thanks to Zhulong’s intervention that he and Xihe had survived Emperor Jun’s attack.

Gonggong burst into laughter. “You’re finally here!”

Zhulong ignored him. He turned his humongous head toward Emperor Jun and sneered, “Emperor Jun, you must think that I’m dead to pull so many stunts.”

Emperor Jun quickly reined in his earlier arrogance and said, “Zhulong, you mustn’t listen to that brat’s nonsense. I have nothing to do with what happened to Nuba and your sons.”

“Do you think I’d believe your words?” Zhulong glared at Emperor Jun with wrathful eyes. 

He had been in seclusion for a long time, but this time, he awoke to find his wife and two sons dead. On top of that, his only remaining son had committed a grievous mistake and would soon be punished under the celestial laws. How could he not be angry?

Emperor Jun’s expression darkened. “What do you want?”

“A blood debt must be repaid with blood!” A humongous snake body was vaguely visible amid the clouds, encircling the surroundings. 

Emperor Jun suddenly let out a frenzied laugh. “Hah! Do you think I fear you people?”

With a wave of his hand, a massive cauldron appeared on the ground. An ominous wind blew, and the clear sky suddenly darkened. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2582: Hellscape
chapters.body:
Chapter 2582: Hellscape

A blood-colored light enveloped the sky. Some of it reflected off the clouds and cast a sinister glow on the crowd beneath. Jarring noises resembling crunching bone echoed from the ground. Nearby mountains began to collapse.

Alarmed, Zu An quickly flitted to the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s side and brought her to safety. 

Shortly after they departed, the mountain they had previously been standing on collapsed. What came surging out of it wasn't searing lava, but an army of deceased spirits that emanated dim green light. Their faces were warped with resentment, as if they would pull any living beings they could get their hands on down to hell with them, so that they would suffer the same pain. 

Zu An was startled. These deceased spirits resembled the vengeful spirits he had seen in the Oblivion River. He hadn’t expected to see something similar here. He anxiously warned the people present, “Don’t let those vengeful spirits touch you. They can corrode your soul.”

Those present here understood the severity of the situation. They either avoided the vengeful spirits or exorcised them. 

However, the other living beings further away weren’t as lucky. Many of them found themselves grabbed by the vengeful spirits. They tried to fight back, but they quickly lost their strength and were dragged into the vengeful spirit horde. They began turning into vengeful spirits themselves before joining the others in hunting down more victims.

Noticing Zu An’s worry, the Goddess of Wu Mountain consoled him. “Don’t worry, the Flame Emperor personally refined Wu Mountain. Chang’e will be safe there.”

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. No wonder Wu Mountain felt so mystical—it was constructed by the Flame Emperor. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain glanced at Xihe, only to find herself meeting the latter’s eyes. Both of them felt awkward. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was only friends with Zu An, yet here she was in his arms. Xihe and Zu An had an intimate relationship, but she was Emperor Jun’s wife. 

While the two women were feeling awkward, a loud buzzing sound echoed. Gigantic pillars slowly rose from the ground and towered over everyone else. Every pillar was made out of squirming, blood-red flesh, and it looked as if some kind of enigmatic, ancient being was about to come back to life.

Zu An did a quick count—there were 108 pillars in total. Their positions also aligned with the Great Constellation Formation.

Zhulong roared in shock and anger, “Emperor Jun, you massacred so many living beings to forge such vile pillars of flesh and blood!”

Emperor Jun remained calm. “It’s their fate. I’m merely fulfilling their destiny.”

“How can you, the Celestial Emperor, commit such a vile deed and proclaim it as fate? How shameless can you get?!” Gonggong berated him while clashing with Donghuang Taiyi. “Taiyi, have you lost your mind as well to side with him?”

Donghuang Taiyi didn’t answer. Instead, his attacks only became fiercer.

Millions of flesh tendrils that resembled blood serpents suddenly extended from the pillars. They were monsters manifested from grudges. The tendrils grabbed every living being in sight and furiously devoured them. As far as Zu An’s divine sense could reach, the world had been plunged into a hellscape.

Brilliant lights shone from Zhulong’s eyes. Wherever the lights reached, the flesh tendrils were reduced to ashes. 

Gonggong unleashed water dragons to rip apart the flesh tendrils. The others also began their counterattack. However, there were too many tendrils, and ten more sprouted out in the time it took them to destroy one. There was no way to destroy all the tendrils.

Just then, many small cracks appeared across the ground. Shrill laughter that induced goosebumps echoed. Dense congregations of specters emerged from the cracks and began ripping off the flesh of the living with their sharp teeth. 

The Kaiming Six Shamans’ Wu Yang was so severely injured in his battle with Emperor Jun that he failed to dodge the specters’ onslaught. In the blink of an eye, he was sucked dry until only his skin was left. The other five shamans were in agony, but they were unable to save him. 

With even Wu Yang falling prey to these specters, there was no way mere mortals would stand a chance against them. It didn’t take long for them to be sucked dry into thin pieces of skin. These skins fluttered into the air and morphed into specters as well. They screamed “Just like me, just like me” as they searched for new victims. 

At the same time, fireballs fell from the sky. Upon closer examination, these fireballs were the skulls of those who had died to the ten suns.

The water in the distant rivers defied gravity and flowed into the sky. The beings residing in the rivers were quickly reduced to bones. 

Massacres were happening no matter where one looked.

The blood clouds in the sky began revolving to form ten vortices. These vortices slowly transformed into a sun each, recreating the phenomenon of the ten suns. However, these suns actually resembled eyes. 

The screams and wails of countless beings filled the world. 

“We need to destroy those eyes! Otherwise, all living beings will die once the Ten Suns Apocalypse Formation is completed!” Xihe exclaimed.

Zhulong and Gonggong had already gotten to action, charging straight at the eyes in the sky. However, a massive bell blocked their attacks. 

Zhulong roared in anger, “Taiyi, are you still siding with Emperor Jun despite the terrible sins he has committed?”

Donghuang Taiyi smiled. “My big brother’s methods might be extreme, but since he’s doing it for our Fiend races, it’s understandable.” He was blatantly siding with Emperor Jun. 

Gonggong cursed out of anger, but Zhulong understood the underlying meaning behind those words. “You’re also seeking to destroy our Shaman races.”

“The world may be big, but there’s only enough room for our Fiend races,” As he gazed down on the living beings below, Donghuang Taiyi floated in midair. 

Zhulong burst into laughter. “Do you think that you two can destroy our Shaman races?”

Right after those words were spoken, translucent figures began appearing out of thin air. Wherever those figures appeared, the surrounding vengeful spirits wailed in agony before disintegrating into dust. 

“What powerful auras!” Zu An was shocked.

“They are Ancestral Shamans,” Xihe explained. “They have only projected their avatars here, but their strength is still not to be underestimated.”

“With this, Emperor Jun and Taiyi should be dead meat, right?” Zu An’s heart was put at ease. This is the power of a strong backing!

Emperor Jun and Taiyi were powerful, but their strength was only comparable to a single Ancestral Shaman each. Now that the other Ancestral Shamans were here, it was time for the tides to be turned on them. 

Zu An and Xihe were interacting naturally when it suddenly struck them that they were no longer a couple. The atmosphere between them turned awkward.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain sensed the change in the atmosphere and interjected to alleviate the awkwardness, “That’s not necessarily the case. Emperor Jun looks confident.”

That was indeed the case, as Emperor Jun didn’t look surprised in the slightest. He opened his arms wide and said, “Do you think we’re the only ones who wish to eradicate the Shamans? This is the collective wish of most fiends in the Celestial Court!”

The celestial gate opened, and countless celestial soldiers appeared behind him. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2583: The Path To Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 2583: The Path To Heaven

Looking at the sky full of celestial guards and feeling the pressure they exerted, Zu An finally understood how Sun Wukong in the myth must have felt when he stood against the Celestial Court.

Gonggong bellowed in fury, “Do you think you’re the only one with troops?”

He took out a seashell-shaped artifact and blew a melancholic note. Countless shaman warriors charged in from all directions, and there was no lack of renowned shamans among them. In terms of aura, they didn’t pale against the celestial guards.

Zhulong, on the other hand, wasn’t as reckless as Gonggong. He looked at the celestials in the sky and sneered, “Does Zhuanxu know about this?”

Emperor Jun calmly replied, “There wouldn’t be so many of us if he didn't know about it.”

The Shamans were infuriated. Some of them even cursed loudly. How could they not be angry when the Celestial Court’s Fiend races had been scheming to eradicate them for such a long time? Even the Ancestral Shamans were enraged.

It was unknown who made the first move, but a full-blown battle soon broke out. Terrifying lights filled the battlefield. Powerful shamans and celestials began dying right from the get-go. 

Zu An wanted to join the battle, but Xihe stopped him. “Let’s leave this place first.”

Zu An frowned. “I have to settle the score with Emperor Jun and force him to divulge Xiaoxi’s whereabouts.” 

“I have a rough idea of where she is,” Xihe replied.

“You do?” Zu An was surprised.

“Let’s leave this place first.” Xihe clutched her chest as she spoke. Her face was pale from the severe injuries she had sustained. “If we continue dawdling here, we won’t be able to get away.”

Zu An knew she was right. There were many experts stronger than him here. While he could temporarily stand his ground with his Sun Slaying Bow and his arsenal of skills, it was only a matter of time before he was worn down and killed. To make things worse, both Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were severely injured. It was impractical of him to take care of them while dealing with the chaotic battlefield.

On top of that, Zu An sensed that the surrounding ki was flowing peculiarly—a formation was being constructed. Not daring to hesitate anymore, he held the Goddess of Wu Mountain and Xihe in each of his hands, morphed into a rainbow, and fled the battlefield. 

Emperor Jun noticed his movement and attempted to claw at the rainbow. His hatred for Zu An had reached a breaking point; there was no way he would let the latter escape under his watch. 

However, a humongous head suddenly appeared in front of him. It was Zhulong, who said, “Emperor Jun, it’s time to settle our score.”

Zhulong was grateful to Zu An for uncovering the truth behind his wife and sons’ deaths, so he'd decided to step in and ensure the latter’s safe departure. 

Wary of Zhulong’s strength, Emperor Jun could only watch as Zu An left.

Zu An traveled for a long time before finally releasing his Rainbow Morph. Even when he was so far from the battlefield, he could still sense terrifying shockwaves rippling through the air. The fight between the Shamans and the Fiend was getting heated up.

“This is weird. If Zhuanxu supports the crusade against the Shamans, why would he sacrifice his cherished grandson to sever heaven from earth...” the Goddess of Wu Mountain murmured in confusion. 

If Zhuanxu had known that a war would break out nevertheless, he wouldn’t have sacrificed his grandson to appease the Shamans in the first place.

“Zhuanxu and Emperor Jun have never been on good terms. There’s no way Zhuanxu would support him,” Xihe said. “I’m guessing that Zhuanxu might be incarcerated somewhere.”

Zu An was taken aback. “He’s the Celestial Emperor! Who could incarcerate him?”

“You lack understanding of the Celestial Court. Many in the Celestial Court view themselves as superior beings and are reluctant to share the world with the Shaman races. It also doesn’t help that the Shamans are bad-tempered, which gave rise to many conflicts between both sides over the years. The situation has reached a boiling point,” Xihe explained. “You saw the celestial guards Emperor Jun summoned. I don’t think they are reluctant participants in this war. Chances are that they have been wanting to deal with the Shaman races for a long time now.”

“Aren't the Shaman races in danger then?” Zu An was worried. The world was big, but if the Shaman races fell in defeat here, it was only a matter of time before Emperor Jun found him. 

“Not necessarily. The Ten Ancestral Shamans are born from Pangu’s blood essence. They are incredibly powerful entities, each having unique abilities. The Shamans are also born warriors. It’s too early to tell who will emerge victorious,” Xihe consoled him. “These are beyond our influence. The outcome will not change with or without us.”

Zu An fell silent. He had only gotten a glimpse of the battlefield between the Fiends and the Shamans, but he had already witnessed the deaths of many experts whose strength didn’t pale in comparison to his. It was indeed beyond him to influence the outcome of the battle.

“Where do you think Xiaoxi is?” he asked.

“The Celestial Court," Xihe answered.

Zu An was taken aback.

“Jun’s plan should have been flawless, but your appearance…” Xihe paused briefly before continuing, “Your appearance messed up his plan. The battle between the Shamans and the Fiends broke out prematurely. He didn’t use the Myriad Souls Banner earlier, likely because it hasn’t been fully refined yet.

“The earth is under the Shaman races’ jurisdiction. It’s unlikely he would risk placing something as important as the Myriad Souls Banner here. It’s more likely for him to have placed it in the Celestial Court, especially since he has the support of most celestials. There is no place safer than that.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain harrumphed. “How many people have died for those bastards’ greed? How dare they call themselves celestials?” The realization that most celestials were aware of the events that were transpiring on earth made her angry despite her usual coldness.

“Survival of the fittest. If they win this battle, history will be theirs to write.” Xihe remarked impassively. Being in a lofty position, she had already seen through all these things. “Let’s quickly make our way to the Celestial Court.”

“Heaven has been severed from earth. How can we get to the Celestial Court?” Zu An was worried.

“There’s another road to heaven on Kunlun Mountain.” Xihe looked westward.

Zu An nodded. He grabbed the two women and rainbow morphed toward Kunlun Mountain. Halfway into the journey, he belatedly remembered that the Goddess of Wu Mountain was severely injured and felt apologetic for dragging her along.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled at him. “Don’t worry. I have recovered after taking your pills. I am, after all, the Flame Emperor’s daughter. I won’t die that easily.” For some reason, she just wanted to spend more time with him.

It was too late for Zu An to escort her back to Wu Mountain, so it would be safer to bring her along with him. Silence loomed among the three of them for the remaining part of the journey. 

“I’m sorry,” Zu An suddenly said. He had shot down nine of the Golden Crows despite not knowing that they were already possessed by Emperor Jun.

Xihe understood what he was getting at. There was a moment of silence before she suddenly remarked, “I had planned to kill you.”

“But you didn’t.”

“I didn’t know why either… Someone I thought was my enemy turned out to not be an enemy, and the true enemy turned out to be the person I was the closest to.” Xihe thought about how history had remained the same despite her efforts, but she'd ended up uncovering such a truth instead. She could only be awed by how powerful the forces dictating fate were. 

Zu An tightly grabbed her hand and said, “I’ll exact vengeance for you!” There was no chance of reconciliation between him and Emperor Jun anymore.

Xihe wanted to withdraw her hand, but he was so strong that after several tries, she gave up and let him be. There was no lust in her, only bottomless sorrow. 

Following the two women’s guidance, Zu An finally arrived at Kunlun’s peak and stopped before a massive tree. This tree had no branches; it rose straight toward the sky. Its trunk emanated a purple light as if it were shrouded in a veil. At its top were nine sprouts with green leaves and black flowers. 

“Is this Jianmu, the sacred tree connecting heaven and earth?” Zu An murmured. This tree was huge, but it wasn’t so big as to cover the sky. 

Xihe nodded. She walked up to the tree and placed her hand on it. 

A golden light shrouded them. Zu An felt himself being transported inside Jianmu, and he swiftly moved upward, as if riding on an elevator.

Suddenly, their surroundings shook intensely. It felt as if the tree was going to collapse.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2584: Meeting Xiaoxi Once More
chapters.body:
Chapter 2584: Meeting Xiaoxi Once More

“What’s going on?” Zu An looked downward and saw giants swinging their axes against the sacred tree. They appeared to be shaman warriors, and they were likely intending to cut down the sacred tree to stop the Celestial Court from dispatching further reinforcements. 

“Don’t worry. Jianmu was born alongside heaven and earth; it can’t be cut down that easily. You should be worried about them instead,” Xihe sneered.

Profound ancient runes lit up around the sacred tree. These weren’t divine runes, but something superior. These characters were born alongside heaven and earth, and they harnessed the dao of the world. The giants didn’t even have any time to scream before they were reduced to ashes.

Zu An was taken aback. Those runes are incredible. What a pity I can’t remember and copy them. It must be because my cultivation isn’t high enough. 

A light suddenly appeared in front of Zu An. Before they knew it, they had already emerged from the other end of the sacred tree. The first thing he noticed was that they were standing on top of clouds. He was initially worried about falling through the clouds, but he soon realized that it was no different from walking on the ground, except for it being softer. It must be a huge formation to sustain this effect throughout the Celestial Court. I wouldn’t be able to do thiswith my current abilities. 

He raised his head and saw a towering celestial gate with curved eaves and interlocking wooden brackets. It looked narrow, but he knew that was just a visual effect. Even the largest being in this world could pass through it easily. Behind the celestial gate was a bunch of palaces built amid foggy mountains. Immortal birds flew freely in the clouds, making the place look ethereal. 

“Is this the Celestial Court?” 

This was Zu An’s first time visiting the legendary Celestial Court, but he wasn't in the mood to enjoy the scenery. He'd thought that given the huge number of celestial guards that descended earlier to clash with the Shaman races, there wouldn’t be many celestials left in the Celestial Court. And yet, there was still a platoon of celestial guards guarding the celestial gate.

Their leader was a giant built like a metal tower. He held a massive axe while vigilantly eyeing the surroundings. He would have noticed Zu An and the others if they hadn’t concealed themselves beforehand. 

“That is General Zhao Lingguan of the South Celestial Gate. He’s known as the number one slaughterer of the South Celestial Gate. I didn’t expect him to not participate in the battle and instead remain here. This complicates things,” Xihe said worriedly.

“Is he powerful?” Zu An asked.

“Very. I’m not sure I could defeat him even if I weren’t injured,” Xihe replied grimly. “But if we wish to enter the Celestial Court, there is no way to circumvent this gate.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain suggested, “I’ll distract him so you can slip through in the meantime. I am the Crimson Emperor’s daughter, after all. He won’t make things difficult for me even if I’m captured.” Now that she couldn’t fight anymore, this was all she could do for Zu An.

Zu An chuckled. “There’s no need for that. Is Zhao Lingguan Emperor Jun’s subordinate?”

“Yes, he is Jun’s close aide. Jun wouldn’t have placed him here otherwise.” Xihe nodded. 

“That makes things easy,” Zu An said. Zhuanxu is a failure of a Celestial Emperor. How could he let his successor usurp his power to such an extent when he hasn’t even stepped down yet?

Zu An suddenly transformed into Emperor Jun. Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain instinctively darted aside, as if scalded by hot water. 

“Don’t worry, it’s me,” Zu An said in his original voice.

The two goddesses remained in disbelief. Zu An could only tell them about the Lord of Myriad Transformations, and the two of them finally breathed a sigh of relief. 

“This ability of yours can’t be used for anything good,” Xihe remarked.

Before Zu An could explain anything, Zhao Lingguan sensed their presence and shouted, “Who’s there?” The metal tower-like figure instantly appeared before them. 

Zu An naturally held Xihe in his arms. Xihe’s face reddened, but she didn’t struggle because she knew that he was doing this to convince Zhao Lingguan of his disguise. 

Zhao Lingguan was taken aback to see Zu An. “Emperor Jun, what are you doing here?”

“Xihe is injured. I’m sending her back so that she can be healed,” Zu An calmly replied.

Zhao Lingguan was perplexed, knowing that Emperor Jun and Xihe had been estranged for many years now. Have they patched things up?

Zu An wasn’t about to give him time to think. “How are things on Zhuanxu’s side?”

That drew Zhao Lingguan’s attention elsewhere. “Rest assured, Emperor Jun. Zhuanxu has been suppressed by the Great Constellation Formation. He won’t be able to do anything.”

Zu An was shocked, but he didn’t let it show. “Good. Continue guarding this place well; don’t allow anyone to enter or leave the Celestial Court. The future of our Fiend races depends on this.”

Zhao Lingguan was about to ask Emperor Jun if his departure from the battlefield would affect their plans when he heard those words. He solemnly replied, “Rest assured, Emperor Jun. No one will cross the South Celestial Gate as long as I’m here!”

Zu An nodded in approval.

Zhao Lingguan could hardly suppress his grin. This contribution should at least raise my position by three ranks, right?

Just like that, Zu An strutted into the Celestial Court with Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain glanced at Zhao Lingguan, who was waving goodbye with a smile. They couldn’t believe that a hurdle they had thought to be impossible would be overcome so easily.

This man is unbelievable.

Zu An looked at a brilliant congregation of stars and planets in the distance. That must be where Zhuanxu is imprisoned.

The most effective way to change the tide of the battlefield would be to rescue Zhuanxu, but Zu An didn’t hesitate to make his way to Emperor Jun’s residence. To him, the outcome of the war between the Shamans and the Fiends was nowhere as important as saving Xiaoxi.

Under Xihe’s guidance, the three of them soon arrived at Emperor Jun’s residence. The place was protected by countless defensive formations, but Xihe breached them easily. 

As soon as they entered his main hall, they felt like they had been transported into another world. The world outside looked ethereal, and a faint fragrance lingered in the air. However, the world inside the main hall was eerie and sinister. 

A pitch-black Myriad Souls Banner floated in the middle of the hall. Tortured screams and miserable cries emanated from the banner. Just listening to those voices could shake one’s psyche. Countless warped specters flew forth from the banner, but no matter how far they flew, they were unable to break free of the Myriad Souls Banner’s restraint. 

A fragile woman lay atop an altar in the middle of the hall. She was unconscious, and her skin was so pale that it looked transparent. It felt as if she could vanish at any moment. 

“Xiaoxi!” Zu An was horrified. Emperor Jun was indeed using her to forge the Myriad Souls Banner!

Xiaoxi’s eyelids fluttered, but she didn’t regain consciousness. A tear flowed down the corner of her eye. As soon as the tear fell on the ground, it transformed into a black aura and was absorbed by the Myriad Souls Banner.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2585: Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2585: Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation

The Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed, “Her soul is being siphoned away! She might only be left with the final sliver of her soul, which is why she looks nearly transparent.” As the Flame Emperor’s daughter, she was highly knowledgeable. 

Zu An panicked. “Is there a way to save her?”

“We have to destroy the Myriad Souls Banner and find a way to bring her soul back to her body…” 

Before the Goddess of Wu Mountain could finish speaking, Zu An charged forward with his Tai’e Sword and unleashed a radiant burst of sword ki. 

But the black aura surrounding the Myriad Souls Banner was almost tangible. As it approached the Myriad Souls Banner, the sword ki swiftly lost its radiance. In the end, it weakly struck the banner before vanishing into thin air. 

Zu An was stunned. The black aura surrounding the Myriad Souls Banner was so corrosive that it could even affect his sword ki!

Unwilling to give up, he drew the Sun Slaying Bow and shot at the Myriad Souls Banner. A white light reminiscent of a comet violently slammed into the banner.

This attack wielded the power to bring down a sun, yet it merely shook the banner. 

Zu An was dumbstruck.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain explained, “Your arrow absorbs surrounding ki to amplify its destructive prowess, but all the natural ki in here has been eroded by the Myriad Souls Banner’s demonic ki. That’s why your arrow was unable to destroy it.”

Xihe surveyed the hall’s layout. “There’s a convoluted formation in this hall that resonates with the Myriad Souls Banner. In essence, you're not just attacking the banner, but the entire palace along with it. Not even the Celestial Emperor can destroy the banner like that.”

This palace had been around for a long time. It had been reinforced by countless celestials, such that one could call it a top-notch defensive artifact. Zu An’s attack was bound to fall short against it. 

Zu An frowned. He tried stowing away the Myriad Souls Baner with his Seven-Colored Divine Halo, but it refused to budge. The Myriad Souls Banner was one with the palace, and Zu An didn’t have the means to stow away the entire palace.

Xihe looked at the woman on the altar and said, “If we can’t destroy the Myriad Souls Banner, we’ll have to kill her. If he forges the Myriad Souls Banner, no one will be able to stop Jun. The whole world will be plunged into destruction.”

“Absolutely not!” Zu An knew this was the rational decision to make, but how could he abandon Xiaoxi?

Xihe looked at him before falling silent. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain opened her mouth, but she decided against saying anything. 

Zu An surveyed the surroundings while raking up everything he knew about formations in his head. A rough idea popped in his mind. “Emperor Jun is formidable to have linked the Myriad Souls Banner with his palace, but this method comes with a downside. The Myriad Souls Banner is a malevolent yin artifact, whereas this palace represents light. Those are two opposing forces.

“Emperor Jun must have found a way to temporarily suppress the conflict. All we have to do is to undo that, and the palace will help us to suppress the Myriad Souls Banner.” 

Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes lit up. They thought that it was a feasible plan. “How should we go about it?”

“I know a Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation that can achieve that effect. However, it requires cleansing ki to be simultaneously channeled from five directions, but even with my instantaneous movement, I can only cover three directions.” Zu An racked his brain for a solution.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain said, “Have you forgotten that Xihe and I are here too? Even though I’m weak against fire, I’m still the Flame Emperor’s descendant. I can channel the fire element in my bloodline to cleanse the surrounding demonic ki. Xihe, as the Sun Goddess, is the nemesis of all malevolent forces. She should have no trouble taking a direction too.”

Xihe nodded. “Indeed, but are you sure you’re up to the task? You were severely injured earlier.”

“Don’t worry, I am a goddess too,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied with a confident smile.

Knowing Xiaoxi didn’t have much time to spare, Zu An quickly explained the crux of the formation to the two women. Then, he took out a formation compass from his Brilliant Glass Bead and planted formation flags all around the hall. The Goddess of Wu Mountain and Xihe made their way to the southeast and southwest directions as planned. 

Xihe took her position and sat down. Her body radiated a searing light reminiscent of the sun. In the face of her cleansing ki, the surrounding black aura swiftly disintegrated. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain nicked her palm with her fingernail before forming a hand seal to invoke the Flame Emperor’s bloodline. Flames suddenly blazed around her. Shockingly, it was on par with Xihe’s power. 

Zu An was finally able to put his heart at ease. Without any hesitation, he began flitting between three directions, channeling the Primordial Origin Sutra in the north direction, the Phoenix Flame in the northwest, and the White Lotus Flame in the northeast. He alternated among the three directions so fast that outsiders would have thought that there were three Zu Ans.

Needless to say, this was a highly exhausting feat, and he swiftly felt his limit approaching despite his cultivation and resilient body. However, he gritted his teeth and held on for Xiaoxi’s sake. 

The five streams of cleansing ki suffused the room like five stars lighting up the dark sky. As the formation flags lit up one after another, the Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation eventually fully activated. 

The black aura in the hall swiftly thinned. Sensing danger, the Myriad Souls Banner unleashed a thick black aura that swiftly enveloped the five sources of brilliance.

“This is bad!”

The trio found themselves plunged into darkness. The lights they released gradually dimmed like candle flames before a wind, on the verge of being extinguished. 

Zu An began to feel flustered. He continued to move between the three locations based on his memory while searching for a solution out of this quandary.

He could still hold on because his cultivation skill had the power to purge evil beings, and as the Netherworld’s master, demonic aura born from souls could hardly hurt him. However, Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were already injured. It would be terrible if they were to be inflicted with the demonic aura. 

All of a sudden, his vision blurred. The demonic aura around him suddenly morphed into the universe, and several eyes slowly opened. Each of these eyes was on the scale of a planet, and they had lights and runes shimmering around them. 

Zu An could hardly describe the appearance of the eyes with words, but there was a feeling that was particularly strong in him. He felt a sense of divinity. It was higher than the deities of the Celestial Court, as if it represented the celestial beings at the very core of the universe. 

Coming into contact with these eyes felt bizarre and conflicting. It felt as if he was going to fall into an eye, be killed by another eye, and fuse into yet another eye.

… 

Meanwhile, unlike the bizarre situation Zu An had found himself in, the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s face had gone completely red. She found herself placed in the position of a bride in a marriage ceremony, and the groom was none other than Zu An!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2586: Dream Butterfly
chapters.body:
Chapter 2586: Dream Butterfly

The Goddess of Wu Mountain fell into a daze. Hm, what was I doing earlier?

Something felt amiss, but her mind refused to process what was going on. It was as if the logical part of her brain had shut down, rendering her incapable of recognizing the bizarre situation she was in. 

Her attention was quickly drawn to the scene before her. She was in a huge room, seemingly a palace. It looked simplistic and humble, a far stretch from the glorious celestial palaces and her cavern on Wu Mountain. She vaguely heard the maids outside addressing someone as king.

Judging from the surrounding ki, this seems to be a human country’s palace?

She thought that it was a decent palace by human standards, especially since her impression of humanity was still one of primitive tribes. 

She was sitting on a huge bed. It was so soft that it made her heartbeat hasten. The bed was covered in celebratory red drapes, and the word ‘囍 (Bliss in Marriage)’ was plastered everywhere. All this left her wondering: When did I get married?

If her groom wasn’t Zu An, she would have immediately grown vigilant, but somehow, everything seemed to make sense when she looked at his familiar face and faint smile. I actually married him! And it looks like we’re on the consummation stage of our marriage.

“How beautiful.” Under the illumination of the flickering candlelight, Zu An examined her up close. Wonderment was reflected in his eyes. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain shyly averted her gaze, not daring to meet his eyes. Despite the lack of a mirror in the room, she knew that her face had to be bright red. Even though she could sense the admiration in his eyes all along, it was her first time being directly complimented by him. He has a way with words… Is he not worried about angering the other ladies around him?

She suddenly fell into another daze. What are the names of the ladies around him? Why can’t I remember them? She even became uncertain whether there were other ladies around him or not.

In her daze, she felt hot, soft lips pressing against her cheeks. Zu An had kissed her. What’s he doing? Why is he suddenly so proactive? 

Shocked, the demure Goddess of Wu Mountain instinctively tried to push him away. However, his hands held her body in place like metal bars.

“You…” She was about to say something when Zu An’s face suddenly became magnified in front of her, and before she knew it, her lips were sealed. 

Her eyes widened as her mind completely went blank. He kissed me? Has he always been this wild…

What further flustered her was that she should have gotten angry, but she felt no anger at all. 

She had heard fairies chatting about love before, but the concept of love had always eluded her. It was only at this moment that she realized that their descriptions were apt—she could hear her heart beating loudly.

Zu An’s movements were so skillful that she swooned right away and instinctively closed her eyes. Is my response too promiscuous? Should I push him away lest he think I am a casual woman? But he’s so good at it… 

Sensing Zu An going further and further, the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes suddenly shot open. She finally pushed him away and said, “No, we can’t do this anymore.”

Zu An was perplexed. “Why are you so shy when we’re already an old couple? Ah, I get it now. How could I be so dense? What a frisky one you are.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was stunned.

Come again, who’s frisky? And what do you mean by old couple?

While she was confused, Zu An wrapped his arm around her waist once more and pushed her down. This familiar yet foreign sensation prompted the Goddess of Wu Mountain to instinctively embrace him. 

“AH!”

“It’s getting more and more realistic. Manman, it’s a waste you didn’t pursue an acting career.”

… 

Tears brimmed in the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes as she lay in a daze. Why was I so proactive earlier? How could I have given my body to him just like that? But our movements were so smooth that it really felt like we were an old couple.

… 

Many doubts surfaced in her mind, but it was quickly interrupted by another dominant thought. I didn’t know this could feel so… No no no, I shouldn’t think about it now!

… 

Before her mind shut down, she suddenly became cognizant of a crucial detail. Wait, why did he call me Manman?

She looked at the copper mirror by the side. It was indeed her, but her reddened face and moist eyes looked feminine and seductive. It was unlike her usual cold demeanor. 

She belatedly remembered how Zu An had excitedly called her Manman on their first meeting and embraced her. He has a lover who looks exactly like me. This must be his marriage night with that Miss Manman, and they are the king and queen of some human country. 

She shyly pushed Zu An away. “I’m not your Manman!”

Looking at Zu An’s shocked eyes, the scenery before her shattered to reveal a pitch-black fog suffused with eerie demonic ki. She finally remembered what she'd been doing earlier. The many souls inside the Myriad Souls Banner must have shaken my mind and stirred up hidden desires that even I was unaware about. 

But why did I see such an embarrassing illusion? 

Thinking back to the moment their bodies and souls became one, the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s face turned scarlet. It had felt so realistic that it made her wonder if it was truly just an illusion. 

Just then, Zu An suddenly reappeared beside her. She shouted in a mix of frustration and embarrassment, “I won’t let you bully me anymore!”

She warned herself that it was only an illusion, and she was even ready to shatter this embarrassing illusion once more.

Zu An awkwardly dodged her attack as he asked, “What’s wrong, goddess?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was startled. Ah, it’s the real Zu An. Her face reddened. “It’s nothing. I was in an illusion earlier, but I have woken up now.”

Zu An was perplexed. What does she mean by bullying?

“Did you and Miss Manman reside in a palace, where you're the king and she’s the queen?” Something prompted the Goddess of Wu Mountain to ask this question which, even she found hilarious. How could he know what happened in my illusion?

Zu An was taken aback. “How did you know?”

He and Manman had spent decades as a couple in Yinxu. The time they'd spent together was so beautiful that he nearly couldn’t extricate himself from it. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s face reddened. Hold on, was that not my hallucination?

But Zu An wasn’t in any state of mind to probe deeper. He flashed away after confirming her safety, saying, “Take care! I’ll check on Xihe.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2587: Revealing Himself
chapters.body:
Chapter 2587: Revealing Himself

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s face turned hot. Even now, she still remembered Zu An’s scent and burning passion. Based on his earlier words, what I just experienced might be what he and Miss Manman went through. But why would I have such an illusion when I am completely unaware of it?

She suddenly remembered Zu An mentioning that she looked identical to Miss Manman, and she had seen her reflection in the illusion too—they were identical. They only diverged in their disposition and expressions. With her vast knowledge, she suddenly wondered: Could Miss Manman be my reincarnation?

When this thought emerged, her heart suddenly raced. Hold on, this is unbelievable. Could I still be in an illusion? Given that I was plunged into an illusion, the same should have happened to Zu An too. Is he able to escape that quickly?

She looked in the direction where Zu An disappeared with uncertain eyes.

Zu An had indeed been in an illusion earlier. He'd gone through an endless cycle of being devoured and restored by the eyes, undergoing countless destructions and rebirths, before he finally woke up from it.

Years of treading around death had tempered his resilience. It would take more to bring him down. It was also a good thing that not all the eyes had wanted to destroy him. The balance between destruction and rebirth had given him the chance to regain consciousness. 

He had no idea what those eyes were, and he couldn’t help but notice how Mi Li hadn’t intervened in the entire process. Even now that he was out of the illusion, he still retained a vivid memory of those eyes. 

Are these the universal deities that Mi Li and the Time Scribe previously mentioned?

Just then, he arrived at Xihe’s position. Xihe’s light was flickering, and she was smiling bizarrely. It was an eerie sight. 

“Xihe! Xihe!” Zu An hurriedly cleansed the black fog around her and infused a surge of warm primordial ki into her body. 

In the first place, Xihe's position as the Sun Goddess granted her strong immunity against demons. With Zu An’s help, it didn’t take long for her to regain consciousness. The first thing she did upon opening her eyes and seeing Zu An was to embrace him.

Zu An was stunned, saying, "I still have to maintain the other three directions.”

While the softness pressing against him was tempting, he could only leave his posts to check on them using his flames and ki temporarily. He needed to be ready to return to the other three directions at any moment to replenish the energies, so the formation wouldn’t collapse. 

Xihe belatedly realized this wasn’t a dream and anxiously retracted her hand.

There were a thousand things Zu An wanted to ask her, but he had no choice but to return to his position and maintain the formation. 

Xihe’s cheeks reddened. Was it all an illusion?

In the illusion, she'd lived a blissful life with Zu An. The ten Golden Crows were still alive, and she'd made them call Zu An 'father'… Recalling the scene made Xihe’s toes curl up so hard that they nearly dug into the ground.

However, when she remembered that the ten Golden Crows were dead, her expression quickly sank. Her previously reddened face became pale once more. 

Given her capability, she had already subconsciously realized that it was all a lie in the illusion, but she'd wanted the happiness to last for just a moment longer. She'd even considered never waking up again. It was only when Zu An woke her up that she could no longer find reasons to stay in there.

With the three of them regaining consciousness, the Five Directions Demon Suppressing Formation finally whirred back to life. Under the influence of the formation, the palace glowed as other demon suppression formations began to activate. 

The pitch-black demonic ki inside the hall gradually thinned as many souls were freed from the Myriad Souls Banner’s control. Transparent faces that were previously marred with anger and ferocity regained their peace, and their souls dissipated into starlight. 

The remaining demonic ki gathered within a three meter radius of the Myriad Souls Banner. The banner buzzed eerily as it tried to make a final stand against the celestial palace’s cleansing powers. 

But these formations had been constructed and refined by countless Celestial Emperors and deities; it would be an understatement to say that they were powerful. On top of that, they were aided by Zu An’s Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation. 

The Myriad Souls Banner was fighting a losing battle. The radius of the demonic ki grew smaller and smaller. 

Just then, a dashing youth dressed in a black robe with golden embroidery suddenly appeared in the palace. Xihe shuddered at the sight of the youth.

The youth turned to Xihe. He pointed a finger at Zu An and said, “Mother, that fellow killed my nine older brothers. Are you really going to work with the enemy?”

He was the only Golden Crow who had escaped.

“No, that’s not how it is…” Xihe’s voice quivered.

“That is how it is! He shot us down with his bow, Suzeng. I saw my nine older brothers die before my eyes. He would have killed me too, if not for uncle arriving in time. Mother, you have to avenge my big brothers!”

Xihe’s face turned pale. “You have misunderstood. The culprit is Jun. He…”

The Golden Crow interjected before she could finish her piece, “Mother, there’s no way our father would hurt us! Who fooled you? Is it that gigolo? You mustn’t be fooled by him!”

“It’s not him…” Xihe thought about the mysterious man she'd met and hesitated.

Now that I think about it, it was a one-sided claim on his part, saying that Emperor Jun killed the Ten Suns and was planning to construct the Apocalypse Formation. Why did I believe him so easily? Could I have been subconsciously hoping for that to be the case because I don’t want Zu An to be the enemy?

“Mother, I don’t blame you for being fooled by that gigolo. If you aren’t going to avenge my big brothers, I’ll do it alone!” the youth roared angrily. He spread his wings and unleashed a barrage of sword ki against Zu An.

Zu An was already struggling to maintain three directions. The Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed in astonishment, “Be careful!”

However, she was so severely injured that just maintaining the formation sapped all her strength. She couldn’t help him.

Zu An had to admit that this last Golden Crow had chosen a good time to attack them. His hands were indeed full at the moment… but sometimes, hands weren't required to suppress someone.

A Seven-Colored Divine Halo shone, and the Golden Crow charging toward him vanished. 

“Spare him!” Xihe exclaimed.

Zu An replied, “Don’t worry, my divine halo only imprisons people. He'll be safe. Besides, I doubt I can hold him in.”

Xihe was taken aback. Why won’t you be able to hold him in? My tenth son is the youngest and weakest among his brothers. There’s no way he can struggle free of your divine halo. 

A golden light shone through the Seven-Colored Divine Halo, and the Golden Crow youth broke free from his incarceration. However, his eyes now looked much older and more vicious. 

“Emperor Jun, you can’t keep hiding it,” Zu An remarked with a sigh. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2588: Focusing On The Weakest Link
chapters.body:
Chapter 2588: Focusing On The Weakest Link

The familiar coldness coming from her tenth son made Xihe’s heart sink. Her rational self had known that the youth before her was likely to be a fake, but she still harbored a sliver of hope. There was no need to go very far to deceive someone who had lost everything like her—all one had to do was to show her a glimmer of hope, and she would fool herself. 

The youth glared at Zu An coldly. “I spent countless years meticulously setting up my grand plan, but it was all nearly ruined because of you. You should take pride in that.”

“Grand plan my ass! It’s just your greed. In order to construct this vile formation, you even killed your own sons, yet you have the cheek to appear self-righteous,” Zu An spat in disdain.
