“A mere ant like you would never understand!” Emperor Jun sneered. “For the sake of eliminating the vile Shamans, sacrifices are inevitable. Once the Shamans have been vanquished, we will have a new world. They will exist eternally in a new form in that new world. Isn’t that much better?”

“You lunatic! You're out of your mind!” Xihe cursed.

“Xihe, you are a disappointment. You are the Sun Goddess, the wife of the next Celestial Emperor. How could you hook up with a mere mortal human? Your lack of shame is appalling!” Emperor Jun coldly glared at her. “However, I’ll magnanimously grant you another chance. Return to my side, and I won’t pursue this matter.”

Xihe seethed with anger. “Do you think it’s possible for anything to remain between us after you killed my sons?”

“They are only dead now,” Emperor Jun calmly replied. “Once I achieve Sainthood, it’ll be a walk in the park to revive them. There’s no need to fuss over the temporary.”

“You can revive them?” Xihe’s eyes lit up. She had schemed for many years, even going as far as to return to the past, all to rescue her sons from their miserable fate. If Emperor Jun could revive the Golden Crows after achieving Sainthood, his proposal would be worth considering.

“Of course. Do you think a saint wouldn’t be capable of that?” Emperor Jun replied proudly.

“I don’t think you’d be capable of that,” Zu An suddenly interjected.

Emperor Jun narrowed his eyes. “A summer worm will never know of winter. What does an ant like you know about the capabilities of a saint?”

“There is a good example from not long ago. When Zhulong’s son, Yayu, died, Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu immediately bestowed him with an immortal medicine to revive him. However, upon revival, Yayu turned into a witless monster. Does that count as bringing someone back to life?” Zu An had figured things out, allowing him to speak in a composed but confident manner. 

“Hmph! Zhuanxu has yet to reach Sainthood. Just because he can’t do it doesn’t mean I can’t do it after reaching Sainthood,” Emperor Jun impatiently replied. 

“Immortal medicines are naturally-formed resources harnessing the universe’s origin dao. Zhuanxu and Zhulong were unable to revive Yayu even when using such a medicine; I doubt you’d be able to do it as a saint,” Zu An sneered. “The crux to reviving the Golden Crows doesn’t lie in Sainthood, but something else.” 

“What is it?” Xihe was both taken aback and elated. Zu An seemed to be suggesting that he had a way to save her sons. 

“How can you trust that gigolo’s words?” Emperor Jun scoffed. “This is a feat that even Zhuanxu failed at. Even I’d have to reach Sainthood to perform this miracle, but that ant claims he can do it?”

It was as if a bucket of cold water was poured over Xihe. He’s right. Zu An isn't weak by any means, but there are stronger experts in the Celestial Court. If even the Celestial Court’s deities could only rescue a walking corpse, what can he possibly do?

“The immortal medicine failed to properly revive Yayu because this world doesn’t have the netherworld and the Six Paths of Reincarnation. The souls of the deceased are prone to dissipating into the void. Zhuanxu and Zhulong only managed to rescue Yayu’s body and not his soul.” Zu An turned to Emperor Jun and continued, “Even if you reach Sainthood, the most you can do is to reforge the Golden Crows’ bodies. You won’t be able to do a thing about their souls.”

“Netherworld, Six Paths of Reincarnation…” Emperor Jun frowned. “What nonsense is that?”

Xihe, however, had heard about the Six Paths of Reincarnation in the future. Looking at Zu An, she solemnly asked, “Will you be able to save my sons?”

“I can give it a try, but I can’t guarantee it.” Zu An didn’t want to deceive her.

But his hesitant answer brought a relieved smile to Xihe’s face. “I trust you. I’m grateful to you for helping me. I have mentally prepared for failure.”

Emperor Jun was infuriated. “Xihe, you used to be a smart woman. How can you fall for that gigolo’s nonsense?! Have you truly fallen in love with that human?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s gaze swiftly alternated among Emperor Jun, Zu An, and Xihe; she was making full use of her front row seat to relish in this drama. 

Xihe’s ears flushed in anger. “He might only be a mortal human, but he’s a hundred times better than a beast who murders his sons!”

Zu An interjected, “Xihe, don’t let him sway your feelings. He’s only saying so much to buy time.” He turned to Emperor Jun and continued, “I bet your main body is busy fighting the Shamans. This body only contains a sliver of your soul, so you aren’t confident you can deal with us. That’s why you’re saying so much to secure Xihe’s aid.” 

Emperor Jun’s eyes narrowed. “I should have killed you a long time ago, bastard!”

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +444… +444… +444…

“It’s high time we settle our score!” Zu An stood up. Now that the Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation had activated the celestial palace’s cleansing power, there was no need for them to continue maintaining it.

“You’re courting death!” Now that his ploy was exposed, Emperor Jun couldn’t hold himself back anymore. He unfurled his wings and unleashed his feathers as a barrage of golden arrows at Xihe, Zu An, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

Zu An opened his arms wide and manifested a massive black hole to swallow the barrage of golden arrows. Despite stopping the attack, he narrowed his eyes as he realized that he had lost track of Emperor Jun.

The next moment, he sensed Emperor Jun standing right next to the Goddess of Wu Mountain, brandishing a whip of golden light to sever her neck. Alarmed, Zu An quickly used his position swap to take her position.

Emperor Jun smirked. He had anticipated Zu An’s reaction. Golden light gushed forth from his hands, instantly transforming into a cage to imprison Zu An. At the same time, he shot two beams of golden light from his eyes at Zu An.

Emperor Jun had long since realized that the Goddess of Wu Mountain was the weakest link of the trio. Whenever he attacked her, Zu An would have no choice but to rush to her rescue. This created chances for him to lay traps for Zu An. This was the same move that would have taken Zu An’s life earlier if not for Zhulong’s timely intervention. But Zhulong wasn't here to save him this time. 

Just then, a figure appeared in front of him to take Emperor Jun’s two beams of golden light.

Xihe spurted a mouthful of fresh blood. It was fortunate that she, as the Sun Goddess, had an exceptionally resilient constitution and the Celestial Court’s divine jurisdiction. It also helped that Emperor Jun hesitated at the sight of her and held back a little. Otherwise, she could have been reduced to ashes. Still, suffering another blow in her already weakened state pushed her to the verge of death. 

Emperor Jun was infuriated. How could his wife sacrifice herself for another man?!

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +999… +999… +999…

The silver lining to Xihe’s incapacitation was that she could no longer aid Zu An. There was no need for him to hold back anymore. As long as he exploited these two women well, it would be a walk in the park for him to kill Zu An. 

As he once again charged at the Goddess of Wu Mountain, viciousness flashed through his eyes.

Zu An understood what Emperor Jun was up to. Such a battle was too disadvantageous to him, so he decided to use his ‘Fragrant Barf’ ability to draw Emperor Jun’s aggro. The latter immediately halted his attack and glared at him with raging eyes. 

“Let’s have a proper duel as men instead. It’s pathetic seeing a future Celestial Emperor desperately attacking women over and over again,” Zu An sneered.

“I’ll kill you!” Emperor Jun knew that Zu An was taunting him, but for some reason, he couldn’t control his emotions at all. The only thought he had in mind was to kill Zu An right away. 

Fearing that the shockwaves from their clashes would injure the two ladies, Zu An morphed into a rainbow and departed from the celestial palace. Emperor Jun followed him as a surge of golden light. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2589: Counterattack
chapters.body:
Chapter 2589: Counterattack

The rainbow and golden light intertwined now and then, only for the chase to resume shortly after. It didn’t take long for Zu An to incur injuries.

Emperor Jun was the future Celestial Emperor, after all. Even with just a sliver of his soul in a Golden Crow Prince’s body, he was still not an opponent Zu An could deal with. His only concern earlier had been that Xihe and Zu An would join forces against him, but in a 1-on-1, Zu An had no chance of turning the tables on him at all.

“Don’t worry, I won’t let you die so easily. I’ll rip off your flesh bit by bit, and your soul shall eternally suffer in the Myriad Souls Banner!” Emperor Jun spat threats through clenched jaws while chasing after Zu An. He wanted to see Zu An suffer a mental breakdown.

But Zu An didn’t show the slightest fear. Instead, he calmly replied, “You said such words earlier too, but I’m still prancing around here. As a Celestial Emperor, you need to do more than running your mouth.” 

Emperor Jun’s cheeks twitched. He'd wanted to erode Zu An’s willpower, but the latter had turned the tables on him.

You have successfully trolled Emperor Jun for +600… +600… +600…

He had no idea why he easily lost control of his emotions whenever he was dealing with Zu An. Someone of his level of cultivation was supposed to have far greater control over their emotions. 

However, a smile soon formed on his face. Zu An was fleeing toward the South Celestial Gate. Despite his calm exterior, he must be panicking inside to flee to the South Celestial Gate of all places. Did Xihe not tell him that Zhao Lingguan and the celestial soldiers stationed there are all my people?

He couldn’t wait to see the look on Zu An’s face.

However, when he saw Zu An’s appearance changing into that of his original body, his smile suddenly stiffened up. “What is that bastard up to? Who does he think he's fooling with such cheap tricks?”

In the meantime, the two of them reached the South Celestial Gate. Zhao Lingguan and the celestial soldiers had long noticed the commotion and rushed over. 

Emperor Jun was just about to order Zhao Lingguan to take down Zu An when the latter beat him to it. “Zhao Lingguan, take down that unfilial son of mine right now!”

Emperor Jun’s heart skipped a beat. He finally understood where the problem lay. He was currently taking the identity of the Golden Crow’s Tenth Prince. Very few people in the Celestial Court knew about him possessing the Golden Crow Ten Princes, and Zhao Lingguan clearly wasn’t one of them. 

“Your Majesty, what happened?” Zhao Lingguan and the other celestial guards were baffled. The Golden Crow Tenth Prince is trying to kill Emperor Jun? Did the sun rise from the wrong direction today?

Putting aside how the father and son came to blows in the first place, shouldn’t Emperor Jun be one-sidedly teaching his son a lesson? How did Emperor Jun end up on the losing side against his son?

Having guessed their thoughts, Zu An quickly replied, “He has gone berserk under someone’s influence. He assaulted me and Xihe in an attempt to refine us.”

He tried to explain how ‘Emperor Jun’ was being put in a disadvantageous position against his son with a generic cover story. He didn’t dare to go into detail, as he didn’t know whether Zhao Lingguan knew about the Myriad Souls Banner or not.  

Emperor Jun was stunned. How can this bastard be so shameless? “Don’t listen to him, Zhao Lingguan! I am the real Emperor Jun!”

Zhao Lingguan stared at the arguing duo in bewilderment. He couldn’t believe such a ridiculous thing could happen in the Celestial Court, especially with Emperor Jun. This Emperor Jun looks and feels like the real Emperor Jun, but for some reason, the Golden Crow Tenth Prince’s manner of speech feels inexplicably familiar. 

“Quickly send some men to my chamber to rescue Xihe. After being attacked by that unfilial son, she’s on the verge of death.” Zu An said.

Through this ‘unwitting’ reminder, Zhao Lingguan suddenly remembered that Emperor Jun had carried Xihe into the Celestial Court via the South Celestial Gate earlier. That proved this Emperor Jun was the real one. This is my chance to prove myself.

With a heart quivering with glee, he turned to the real Emperor Jun and roared, “Tenth Prince, how can you commit such an atrocious deed? You should surrender right away!”

I must hold back against the Tenth Prince later. They are still father and son, after all. If they mend their relationship afterward, I’ll become the sinner instead. What I should do is capture the Tenth Prince alive and hand him over to Emperor Jun to mete out punishment.

Emperor Jun was rendered speechless. “Zhao Lingguan, how can you be this dumb?”

Zhao Lingguan was infuriated. “I shall apprehend you on His Majesty’s behalf!”

He raised his axe, which instantaneously expanded to three thousand meters in length, and hacked it down on Emperor Jun. Even the Celestial Court’s sea of clouds was split into two before the overwhelming might of his axe. 

But this attack landed on empty air. Emperor Jun surged over to Zhao Lingguan’s side as a surge of golden light and spat, “Scram!” He sent Zhao Lingguan flying with a kick.

Zhao Lingguan was speechless. I thought this would be an easy battle. When did the Tenth Prince become so powerful?

However, he wasn’t about to be humiliated in front of his subordinates. With a furious roar, he charged at Emperor Jun with his massive axe. “Where do you think you’re going?!”

Emperor Jun was rendered speechless. Zu An had already been in his grasp, only for this fellow to appear out of nowhere and tie him down. What made things even more frustrating was that he couldn’t tell Zhao Lingguan the truth! It was shocking news for a Celestial Emperor to possess his son’s body. Revealing the truth could solve the problem at hand, but there could be severe repercussions in the future. 

Left with no choice, Emperor Jun had to devote his attention to shaking off Zhao Lingguan. By the time he succeeded, Zu An was nowhere to be seen.

“Where did he go? Did he return to the celestial palace?” Emperor Jun was about to head back when he suddenly felt a rush of anger. His gut feeling was telling him that Zu An was in another direction. 

So, he followed his gut feeling and eventually saw Zu An rushing down the sacred tree Jianmu. He burst into laughter. “I should have let Xihe witness your cowardice. Who could have thought you would abandon them and escape by yourself?”

After everything that had happened, he didn’t want to waste any more time. He just wanted to kill Zu An and get this over with. Golden light beams erupted from his eyes. No matter how fast Zu An was, he couldn’t be faster than light.

Much to Emperor Jun’s surprise, Zu An made no attempt to dodge the attack. Instead, he took out a formation plate and created a spatial rift in front of him. The golden light shot through the spatial rift and was diverted onto the sacred tree Jianmu. 

Emperor Jun was horrified. It finally dawned on him that Zu An wasn’t banking on Zhao Lingguan to defeat him. Zu An had merely been buying time to construct a teleportation formation here in order to use Jianmu’s divine runes against him! He would have nothing to fear if he was in his peak condition, but he only had a sliver of his soul here. In his current state, Jianmu’s divine runes were more than enough to pose a threat to him. 

Still, he wasn’t the Celestial Emperor for no reason. With a wave of his sleeves, he swiftly formed another spatial rift around Jianmu to divert his beams of golden light elsewhere. 

Zu An sighed. He hadn’t expected Emperor Jun to react so quickly.

Emperor Jun secretly wiped away cold sweat before glaring at Zu An with cold eyes. “You are a scheming one. I nearly fell for your trick. There won’t be a second time.” 

The same trick wouldn’t work twice.

Zu An was aware of that, but he chuckled nevertheless. “I know that you won’t attack Jianmu on your own accord anymore… but I can!”

He emulated the Golden Crow’s sword ki using the Rune Weapon Chart and attacked Jianmu. At the same time, he used his position swap to exchange places with Emperor Jun.

Emperor Jun sneered, “What’s the use of swapping positions? The divine runes don’t recognize the aggressor by their position, but…”

His expression suddenly warped. He noticed that Zu An had reverted to his original form, which made him realize that Zu An had struck Jianmu in his appearance using his methods. 

He had no idea how Zu An’s disguise worked, but even Zhao Lingguan had been fooled by it. In other words, there was a chance that the divine runes could perceive him as the aggressor!

“NO!”

He was about to dodge when the divine runes lit up and devoured him with their terrifying light.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2590: Rescue
chapters.body:
Chapter 2590: Rescue

In his normal condition, Emperor Jun wouldn’t fear these divine runes. He had plenty of ways to deal with such situations. But this was only a sliver of his soul, and it was possessing the Golden Crow Prince’s body. This was considered an evil, unorthodox method. 

Furthermore, Jianmu’s divine runes had the power to dispel evil!

Zu An had attacked Jianmu using Emperor Jun's face and aura to trigger the divine runes, and to make things worse, he'd even swapped their positions! The divine runes didn’t have intelligence and were incapable of telling people apart, so they could only identify the aggressor through their aura. Meanwhile, Zu An’s earlier attack had harnessed the auras of the Golden Crow Prince and Emperor Jun’s soul sliver. 

Emperor Jun was instantly devoured by the divine runes’ light.

Cold sweat dripped down Zu An’s forehead. He had been taking on a huge risk there. If the divine runes had somehow identified him as the aggressor, he would have been the one suffering that fate. Fortunately, he had played plenty of games in the past and was skilled at finding loopholes to exploit. 

His bet had paid off!

With a scream of agony, Emperor Jun plummeted from the sky like a sandbag.

The divine runes reined in their light and reverted to normal.

Zu An carefully caught the Golden Crow Tenth Prince’s body and confirmed that there was no longer any soul pulsing in it. That meant Emperor Jun’s soul sliver had been vanquished! He had no idea why the divine runes had only obliterated Emperor Jun’s soul sliver, but hadn’t harmed the Tenth Prince’s body; but this wasn’t the time to be thinking about that. He quickly brought the prince’s body back to the celestial palace. 

On the battlefield between the Fiends and the Shamans, Emperor Jun, who had been imposingly massacring his enemies, suddenly clutched his head and let out a miserable cry. The nearby Ancestral Shamans took this chance to counterattack. If not for Donghuang Taiyi protecting him with the East Emperor Bell, he could have lost his life there and then.  

“What happened?” Donghuang Taiyi asked, concerned.

Emperor Jun didn’t answer, but he stared intently in the celestial palace’s direction. The soul sliver he had left there as a precaution had been killed! The backlash of that dealt a heavy blow to him. To make things worse, this hinted that something had gone awry in the celestial palace, and the Myriad Souls Banner wouldn't necessarily be safe anymore…

Knowing that he couldn’t leave things be, he said, “I’ll leave things here to you. I need to make a quick trip back to the Celestial Court.” 

He was about to leave when his surroundings were suddenly sealed off. It was Zhulong’s doing. 

“I’m your opponent, Emperor Jun. You won’t get away that easily,” Zhulong said as he angrily charged at Emperor Jun.

Emperor Jun had no choice. These Ancestral Shamans were so strong that he could only barely fend against them with his cultivation. There was no way he could escape when they were doing all they could to tie him down. 

… 

At the Celestial Court, Zhao Lingguan was delighted to see Zu An. “You’re back, Your Majesty!”

He saw the Golden Crow Tenth Prince in Zu An’s hand and was awed. As expected of His Majesty. He still subdued the Tenth Prince in the end. He probably struggled earlier because he didn't want to go too far dealing with his own son.

Hm, why does the Tenth Prince look dead?

“Mm. Continue guarding the South Celestial Gate well. I’ll bring him to apologize to his mother,” Zu An said as he entered the Celestial Court with the Tenth Prince. 

Zhao Lingguan nodded. Disobedient brats ought to be beaten up by their parents.

The shame he felt from being outmaneuvered by the Tenth Prince disappeared. He puffed up his chest as he regained his usual liveliness. 

Zu An soon returned to the celestial palace, where he found Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain pacing around the hall anxiously. When they saw him, their eyes lit up in delight. They were about to rush forward to welcome him back when they each remembered that there were other women present and held themselves back.  

Xihe’s gaze soon fell on the Golden Crow Tenth Prince. “He…”

“Emperor Jun’s soul sliver died. I brought the Tenth Prince’s body back,” Zu An replied. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was astonished. To think that he could kill Emperor Jun’s soul sliver? That’s practically a miracle!

Unable to hold herself back anymore, Xihe rushed forward to grab her son’s corpse and began sobbing pitifully. 

Zu An gently patted her shoulder. He knew that at times like this, no words could help. 

He then turned his gaze to the Myriad Souls Banner, which no longer looked as imposing as before. Under the suppression of the celestial palace and the Five Directions Demon Suppression Formation, its demonic ki had completely receded into it. 

Thus, he drew the Tai’e Sword and unleashed a brilliant sword ki strike at it. 

The Myriad Souls Banner could no longer absorb such powerful ki. With a loud crack, it shattered into pieces. Countless souls gushed out from the banner and fled in all directions, delighted that they had finally regained their freedom. Even though they would soon dissipate into thin air, they would rather disappear than suffer eternally in the banner. 

However, some vile souls noticed Ji Xiaoxi’s body lying on the altar and charged at her, hoping to occupy her body and come back to life. 

“Scram!” Zu An roared. The combined prowess of his primordial ki and his authority as the Netherworld’s master easily crush those vile souls. No other soul dared approach Ji Xiaoxi’s body, fearing they would suffer the same fate.

As the netherworld’s master, Zu An had exceptional sensitivity when it came to souls. It took a while, but he eventually found a faint silhouette amid the countless souls. This silhouette looked timid, but no other souls dared to approach her as if she were an incarnation of horror. 

As expected of the Cruel Poison Constitution. Emperor Jun used her as the Myriad Souls Banner’s core for a reason. Even her soul is laced with lethal poison that intimidates other souls. 

Ji Xiaoxi’s soul had been split into many fragments to dampen the effects of the Cruel Poison Constitution, lest she poison the other souls inside the Myriad Souls Banner to death. It was thanks to that she'd lasted long enough for him to rescue her.

Still, it hurt Zu An to see how fragmented her soul had become. She must have suffered a lot. 

Not daring to hesitate, he used a secret art inside the Netherworld Book of Life and Death to summon a couple of specters. These figures were initially frightened and tried to escape, but the sight of Zu An inspired inexplicable trust in them, prompting them to subconsciously fly toward him. 

“Don’t be afraid, Xiaoxi. I’m here to save you,” Zu An spoke gently for fear that he would scare her. 

It was hard to tell whether the specters understood his words or not, but they smiled and allowed him to do as he pleased. In the end, they returned into Ji Xiaoxi’s body. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain passed a jade bottle over to Zu An. “This is Wu Mountain’s Cloud Fog Essence. It helps to heal the soul.”

Zu An looked at her with grateful eyes. That was indeed what Xiaoxi needed right now. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. She waved a hand, and a droplet of viscous liquid resembling honey flew from the bottle into Ji Xiaoxi’s mouth. 

Zu An could feel the rich energy and life force contained within the droplet. This is much more potent than Milk of Purple Frost. 

Ji Xiaoxi moaned as her complexion became visibly redder. However, her injuries were so severe that even with the Netherworld’s secret arts and the Cloud Fog Essence healing her soul, she wouldn’t regain consciousness so quickly.

So, Zu An entrusted the Goddess of Wu Mountain to take care of her before scanning the room. One by one, he found ten unique souls resembling little birds. He used his secret art to channel the souls toward the Golden Crow Tenth Prince’s body.

These little Golden Crows became more lively the closer they got to Xihe. Their wings fluttered as if they were cheering.

Xihe was both stunned and delighted to see this. With a quivering voice, she asked, “Is it still possible… to save them?”

She almost wanted to cover her ears, fearing that the answer would shatter her heart. 

“Of course. This world must have a sun.” Whether for the greater good or his ties with Xihe, Zu An was inclined to help the Golden Crows. “However, they have been dead for too long, and on top of that, Emperor Jun used their souls to control the Myriad Souls Banner. That has resulted in the severe erosion of their souls.” 

Xihe’s heart sank. Her hatred for Emperor Jun hit a boiling point. 

“I’m incapable of saving them individually. What I can do now is to fuse their souls to form a complete soul. This soul will retain their individual memories and personalities. We can only nurture their souls in a body and try to find ways to heal them in the future.”

Xihe had thought that it was hopeless; she hadn’t expected there to be such a solution. She was so overjoyed that she cried tears of joy. “This is good enough! It’s good enough!”

Over the years, she had tried countless ways to change the trajectory of history, but she was unable to defy fate no matter what she did. She had already resigned herself to despair, so how could she not be excited upon learning that there was a chance to rescue her ten sons?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2591: The Celestial Emperor’S Repayment
chapters.body:
Chapter 2591: The Celestial Emperor’S Repayment

The Golden Crows were much stronger than Ji Xiaoxi in the first place. Shortly after their fragmented souls entered the Golden Crow Tenth Prince’s body, the Tenth Prince’s body slowly opened his eyes.

“Mother…” Seeing Xihe, the Tenth Prince burst into tears. He was about to get up when he suddenly clutched his head and exclaimed, “Ugh, it hurts!”

Xihe was overjoyed to hear the voice of her son, but she was horrified to see him in such pain. She anxiously turned to Zu An.

Zu An explained, “Their souls are incomplete. It’s only normal for them to suffer a headache.”

Soon, different voices echoed from the Tenth Prince’s mouth.

“Huh? What am I doing here?”

“Who are you?”

“Isn’t this tenth brother’s body?”

“Mother, what are you doing here?”

“I vaguely recall father killing me.”

… 

The commotion caused by the different voices caused the Tenth Prince’s headache to worsen. Just then, the Goddess of Wu Mountain threw a jade bottle over and said, “This should make them feel better.”

Xihe directed a grateful nod at her before feeding a droplet of Cloud Fog Essence to the Tenth Prince. That eased the Tenth Prince’s pain, and he gradually calmed down. That gave Xihe the chance to explain the situation to them.

Zu An returned to Ji Xiaoxi’s side and gently held her hand, infusing ki into her body to aid her recovery. When he felt just how cold her hands were, his heart stung. 

“Don’t worry, Miss Xiaoxi will be fine,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain consoled him.

But Zu An wasn’t that optimistic. Xiaoxi had been the ‘main ingredient’ in the Myriad Souls Banner, so her soul was bound to have suffered a lot of damage. Furthermore, it was unknown whether this incident would trigger her Cruel Poison Constitution. 

Just then, Ji Xiaoxi’s eyelids fluttered. Overjoyed, Zu An called out for her. 

A while later, Ji Xiaoxi finally opened her eyes. Delight and hesitation flickered in her eyes when she saw Zu An. “Big brother Zu, am I dreaming?”

Unable to hold himself back anymore, Zu An pulled her into his embrace. “No, it’s not a dream. It’s all right now. Big brother Zu will protect you.”

His warm embrace convinced Ji Xiaoxi that this was all real. She finally revealed a smile that came from the bottom of her heart as she said, “I was in a scary place earlier. My body felt light, and it was on the verge of being devoured by darkness. I was so afraid, until I heard big brother Zu’s voice in the distance. It was as if I finally found my pillar of support. It’s because of you that I didn’t lose myself to the darkness…”

Her words made Zu An feel even more sorry for her. “It’s all over now. I’ll be by your side from now onward.”

It was hard to tell what was in Ji Xiaoxi’s head, but upon hearing his words, her face turned red. She then happened to notice the Goddess of Wu Mountain, and that sent her into a fluster. She tried to push Zu An away but was too weak to succeed. In the end, she shyly smiled at the latter and said, “Big sister Pei is here too.”

“I’m not Miss Pei,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied. I must truly resemble Miss Pei for her to say that too. That made her even more curious about Zu An’s history with Pei Mianman.

“Ah?” Ji Xiaoxi blinked in confusion. Is big sister Pei angry at me?

Zu An quickly explained the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s identity to her. Ji Xiaoxi was fascinated. She hadn't thought there could be two people who resemble each other so much in the world. 

Meanwhile, Xihe brought the Golden Crow Tenth Prince over to thank Zu An when a huge explosion suddenly echoed in the distance. The entire palace shook due to the explosion. 

The group was startled. “What happened?”

“Something happened to the formation suppressing Zhuanxu.” Zu An recalled the Great Constellation Formation being in the direction of the explosion.

The group rushed to the window to ascertain the situation. There, they saw the stars in the sky plummeting one after another, a sign that the formation was unraveling. 

Zu An grimly said, “Let’s quickly leave this place.”

There was no telling how Zhuanxu would react to them. Given their relationship with Emperor Jun, Zhuanxu’s ire could be drawn to Xihe and the Golden Crow Tenth Prince. At the same time, Zu An carried the shamans’ aura right now, and the shamans had bad blood with the fiends. 

Besides, Zu An had a terrible impression of Zhuanxu. The latter had little control over the Celestial Court despite being the Celestial Emperor, and had simply watched as Emperor Jun caused so much trouble. 

The others agreed with his suggestion.

Zu An picked Xiaoxi up. 

The group was about to leave when a divine light suddenly surged from the distant formation. Zhuanxu had to have broken free of his restraint. 

Alarmed, Zhao Lingguan rushed over with his subordinates. “Where do you think you're going?”

He charged at Zhuanxu with his massive axe. 

The figure amid the divine light casually pressed downward, and a giant palm descended from the sky and smacked Zhao Lingguan and his subordinates down from the Celestial Court. The falling stars’ debris then fell on them, turning into mountains that buried them within. 

Zu An was stunned. Has Zhao Lingguan always been this courageous?

Of course, Zhao Lingguan wasn’t so foolish as to think he could subdue a Celestial Emperor. He'd thought that Emperor Jun was nearby and all he had to do was buy some time for Emperor Jun to appear. He could have accrued merit that way. Little did he know the Emperor Jun he had just seen was Zu An. 

The person in the divine light glanced in the direction of where Zu An and the others were before taking a step forward. Before Zu An and the others could react, he was already inside the celestial palace. 

Zu An and the others froze in place. Damn it, we were still a step too late.

Zu An anxiously urged Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain to be careful, only to realize that they weren’t responding, as if someone had frozen them in place.

“What did you do to them?” Zu An furiously glared at the man shrouded in divine light.

The man wore a resplendent coronet and a five-colored silk robe embroidered with gleaming jewels. His presence was so powerful that it made one overlook his appearance; all one would remember was his inviolable authority. 

“Don’t worry, they are fine. I just want to talk to you,” Zhuanxu said.

Zu An fell silent. He must have used some kind of spatial domain-type skill. As expected of a Celestial Emperor. His methods are impressive. 

But the thought of how Zhuanxu had done nothing all this while despite his incredible prowess, sitting idly as Emperor Jun messed around, made him even angrier. 

“You seem to be blaming me.” Zhuanxu saw through Zu An’s thoughts.

“I wouldn’t dare. I’m merely perplexed as to why you haven’t done anything despite your formidable abilities. You were even imprisoned by your own people. I find it baffling,” Zu An replied sarcastically.

“It’s more complicated than you think. I’m not just up against Emperor Jun and Taiyi here…” Zhuanxu suddenly paused here, as he was reluctant to share more details with Zu An. “And how do you know I haven’t done anything at all?”

“Oh? Enlighten me, please,” Zu An replied. All you've done is to sever heaven and earth by sacrificing your most talented grandson.

“How could you have shot down the Golden Crows and foiled Emperor Jun’s plan if I hadn’t bestowed Suzeng to you?” Zhuanxu replied with a faint smile.

Zu An frowned. “You might not have been the one who gifted Suzeng to me. Besides, how could a future event resolve a past incident?”

Zhuanxu chuckled. “The past, the present, and the future; those are concepts thought up by mortals. To beings like us, there’s no difference among them. We can influence the timeline with a simple intervention at any temporal point. I might have gifted Suzeng to you in the future, but it came in handy here, no? It would be unnecessary to bestow it upon you a second time.”

Zu An’s head shook, as he felt as if he had heard the chime of truth. He vaguely felt something, but the moment passed, and he failed to fully grasp what exactly it was. 

Zhuanxu wasn’t interested in speaking further on the matter. “You have done me a huge favor, so I’ll allow you to make a request. Is there anything you want?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2592: Defying Spirit Tears
chapters.body:
Chapter 2592: Defying Spirit Tears

Zu An’s eyes lit up. “Will any request do?”

Zhuanxu saw the gleam in Zu An’s eyes and chuckled. “It has to be within my means. I won’t be able to help you if you request to become a saint.” 

Zu An replied, “I don’t need that much. I just want you to save my lovers, such as Chu Chuyan, Qiu Honglei, Ji Xiaoxi… I want them to recover from their afflictions.”

Zhuanxu’s smile froze up. “You’re a sly one. You’re making several requests of me at once.”

He did some calculations on his finger as if divining their conditions. A while later, he slowly replied, “Saving Miss Chu is your fate; I can’t intervene in that. As for Miss Qiu, you have saved her, and she should recover in due time. As for Miss Ji…”

Zhuanxu’s gaze fell on the lady in Zu An’s arms. A divine light shone on her. “Emperor Jun has triggered her Cruel Poison Constitution through the Myriad Souls Banner. I can only temporarily suppress it. You need to treat her in time, and I believe you already know the way to do so.”

Zu An was stunned. I still have to find those medicines in the end. But at least Xiaoxi’s Cruel Poison Constitution has stabilized for the time being. 

“So there’s nothing you can help me with. Is this all you’re capable of as the Celestial Emperor?”

Zhuanxu’s eyelids twitched. “Fine, I’ll take that as a gift from me. You can make another request, but I suggest you think things through. You won’t be granted another chance.”

Zu An pondered hard, but in truth, he already knew what he needed. “You’ll fulfill my request as long as it’s within your means.”

“Of course. I, the Celestial Emperor, will not renege on my promise,” Zhuanxu replied in displeasure. 

“I want the power insignias of the Celestial World and the Human World.” Zu An quickly added, “You said you wouldn’t renege on your promise.”

Zhuanxu was speechless.

It was a long time before he finally replied, “You... I might have obliterated you on the spot for your insolent request had I not known about the karmic effects beforehand.”

Zu An was stunned. He knows what I’m doing?

A golden light suddenly manifested in Zhuanxu’s hands. He gently flicked it into Zu An’s hands. “This is a sliver severed from the Celestial World’s power insignia. It should be enough for you.”

Zu An couldn’t help but ask, “Do you already know that I’m using the power insignias to forge the Six…”

Zhuanxu interrupted him before he could finish that statement. “Some things mustn’t be said out loud… The Human World’s power insignia isn’t with me, but you need not worry. It’s fated to fall into your hands very soon.”

Zu An was shocked. It felt as if Zhuanxu knew everything. For a moment there, he wondered if he was nothing but a piece on a chessboard. 

He wanted to ask more questions, but Zhuanxu interjected, “Alright, I have to settle the war between the Shamans and the Fiends now. Farewell.”

His figure vanished right after. 

Everything soon reverted to normal. Xihe and the Goddess of Wu Mountain anxiously said, “Let’s hurry up and leave. We’ll be in danger once Zhuanxu comes…”

Zu An replied, “He was already here.”

“He was here?” The two women were confused. They were unaware of the conversation that had just taken place.

Zu An was also awed by Zhuanxu’s abilities. He had no idea how the other party had achieved such a formidable feat. 

It was then that Xihe suggested, “It’s too dangerous to get implicated in the war between the Shamans and the Fiends. Why don’t you come to Scalding Valley with me? It should serve as a safe refuge for the time being.”

Zu An hesitantly replied, “I might have to take a trip back to Wu Mountain. I feel uneasy for some reason, as if I've overlooked something.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain and Ji Xiaoxi had no objections to that. 

Xihe said, “I have to bring the Golden Crows back to Scalding Valley as soon as possible to nourish their impaired body and souls. I won’t be able to accompany you for the time being.”

Saving the Golden Crows had always been her goal, so Zu An didn’t stop her. Silence loomed between the two of them. They vaguely realized that this could be farewell for them. 

“I’m sorry for the things I've done in this time. Don’t worry, the lady named Xie Daoyun is fine. I’m only temporarily occupying her body. As for Miss Pei, she came to look for me, but I was worried that she would see through my impersonation, so I captured and imprisoned her in a formation. You just have to do this upon returning to rescue her…”

Xihe quickly shared the imprisonment location and the formation details. She then turned to the Goddess of Wu Mountain and remarked, “Miss Pei truly resembles Yaoji. If I’m not mistaken, Yaoji should be her former incarnation.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain had already guessed as much. Given their striking resemblance and inexplicable connection, there had to be some kind of link between them. Still, when she thought about the intimate relationship she would have with Zu An in the future, she couldn’t help but blush furiously. 

“Xihe, I…” Zu An’s voice was hoarse. He wasn’t sure what to say in this situation. 

He thought about their time together, including their intimate moments. They had been no different from a real couple. But at the same time, he had also shot down nine of the Golden Crows and used the Golden Phoenix ability against her. He had done her a great injustice.

Xihe placed her finger on his lips. There was no point in thinking so much about it now. The outcome was better than she'd expected, and the time she spent with him was enjoyable. She had never experienced such love before. Her marriage to Emperor Jun had always been a political one; there was no true love between them. 

“Can I hug you one last time before we part ways?” Xihe looked at him carefully, worried that he would turn her down.

Zu An pulled her in for an embrace. The two of them hugged each other tightly. Words failed them at this moment.

The Golden Crow Tenth Prince stared dazedly at this scene as the ten souls in him broke into an uproar. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain tactfully walked away to give them some privacy. 

Ji Xiaoxi wanted to walk away too, but she was in Zu An’s arms and was currently sandwiched tightly between the duo. Her face was flushed. I shouldn’t be here. No one can see me. Oof, big sister Xihe is too big. Crap, I’m running out of breath…

… 

Xihe eventually left.

Yumen Beiqing’s body fell limply, and Zu An rushed forward to support her. He first checked her vitals and confirmed that there was nothing wrong. Xihe had warned him that she would require some time to recuperate before regaining consciousness.

Ji Xiaoxi suddenly exclaimed, “What’s that?”

Zu An looked in the direction she was pointing and noticed a droplet of golden fluid on his shoulder. It appeared to be a teardrop Xihe had left behind when embracing him earlier. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain walked over and took out a jade bottle to store the golden fluid. “You mentioned that you were looking for an ingredient called Defying Spirit Tears. I pride myself on being knowledgeable, but I have never heard of that before. However, I think I get it now.”

“Is this it?” Zu An was astounded. He would never have thought that what he was looking for had been by his side all along. And yet, he felt bitterness instead of joy. 

“This is incomplete. It’s still lacking something,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain remarked with a glint in her eyes. Before Zu An could ask anything, she added, “It isn’t safe here. Let’s return to Wu Mountain first.”

Zu An nodded. He morphed into a rainbow and transported them out of the Celestial Court. 

As they landed on Wu Mountain, the Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly remarked with a frown. “Someone barged into my cavern.”

Zu An was shocked. He anxiously flashed into the cavern, but the Mermaid Queen was nowhere to be found. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2593: Forlorn Heart
chapters.body:
Chapter 2593: Forlorn Heart

In the meantime, Xie Daoyun gradually regained consciousness. She was temporarily dazed as she surveyed her foreign surroundings. Memories were surfacing in her mind, filling her in about what had happened while she was possessed. Despite stealing control over her body, Xihe hadn’t sealed off her senses, so Xie Daoyun recalled most things that had happened. In particular, when she thought about what Xihe had done with big brother Zu…

While she was berating Xihe for being shameless in her mind, she noticed that Zu An was looking for something. She asked, “What’s wrong?”

“Big sister Xie, you’re awake! The Mermaid Queen has gone missing, and big brother Zu is searching for her.” Ji Xiaoxi quickly explained the situation to her. The two were resting beneath a tree, looking after each other.

“The Mermaid Queen…” Xie Daoyun recalled the Ocean races’ renowned beauty and the intimate moments she'd shared with Zu An. Big brother Zu is a playboy! But the Mermaid Queen truly resembles Miss Shang… 

Meanwhile, Zu An grabbed the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s arms and asked, “Didn’t you say she would be here? What happened?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain winced in pain. Zu An snapped out of his daze and quickly released her. “My apologies. I got too anxious.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t mind something this minor. She consoled him, saying, “Calm down. Let me see what happened first.”

Zu An was surprised to hear that. Is she able to peer into the past?

Sensing his confusion, the Goddess of Wu Mountain flung her sleeves as she explained, “All of the fog shrouding Wu Mountain is my eye.”

The surrounding fog converged into a screen that projected everything that had happened on Wu Mountain while they were away.

… 

The Mermaid Queen nervously paced to and fro across Wu Mountain’s peak. The changing colors in the sky and the occasional deafening explosions made her so worried that every second was a torture to her. She was worried sick about Zu An. She wanted to help, but she had no idea where he was. With her current cultivation, a mere shockwave could cost her life if she got embroiled in the war between the Shamans and Fiends. If so, not only would she be of no use to Zu An, but she would also never meet him again. 

She remembered the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s warnings before she left. Despite her anxiety, her rationality still prevailed. She wasn't on the main battlefield, but the shockwaves rippling from the distance were destructive enough to shatter mountains and crack earth. And yet, when they reached Wu Mountain, a mysterious light always neutralized the energy. 

Just as she was feeling awed by Wu Mountain’s extraordinary qualities, she suddenly sensed something and turned around. A figure was flashing up the mountain. When she noticed that the figure was carrying a bow, she was initially happy —she thought that Zu An had returned. She was about to rush forward to welcome him back when she noticed that the figure was different and halted her footsteps.

That figure soon reached the mountain peak. It was indeed not her lover, but a man whom she had met once before, Feng Meng. He was the one who had previously led a platoon to capture her.

Feng Meng looked at her with admiring eyes. “As expected of the Chang clan’s renowned Saintess. You are gorgeous!” 

Sensing he was up to something, the Mermaid Queen put up her guard. “What do you want?”

Feng Meng grabbed the bow he was carrying and gently stroked it. “The world sees Yi as the world’s number one archer. No matter how hard I work, I am still nothing more than second to the world. I refuse to accept that. Now, there is a way for me to prove my superiority over him. His fiancée is going to be my woman!”

The Mermaid Queen’s face darkened. She quickly formed a hand seal to unleash water arrows on the other party.

Feng Meng easily shot down her water arrows one after another. “You dare to use arrows against me? Arrogant!” 

The Mermaid Queen didn’t answer. She continued unleashing skills on Feng Meng.

However, Feng Meng nocked and shot arrows so fast that it left afterimages in his wake. He dissipated the Mermaid Queen’s skills easily with an arrow each, leaving the latter with a lurching heart. She had crossed blows with Feng Meng when the latter pursued her, and she knew that his strength was far superior to hers.

While leisurely shooting down her water skills, Feng Meng chuckled, “I’ll spare your life if you hand the immortal medicine over to me.”

The Mermaid Queen was startled. “How do you know it’s with me?”

Zu An had secretly slipped her the immortal medicine as they parted ways. Outsiders shouldn’t have known about that. 

“Heh! Your movements are all within my master’s…” Feng Meng noticed something halfway through and hurriedly stopped talking. “Hmph! Cut the crap and hand the immortal medicine over. I might consider sparing your life if you do so. Otherwise, I don’t mind taking both your body and the immortal medicine.”

Looking at his vile smile, the Mermaid Queen knew better than to trust his words. There was no way he would let her off the hook just because she handed over the immortal medicine. He was merely trying to grind down her desire to resist. As the Ocean races’ number one beauty, she had come into contact with countless people who harbored malicious intentions toward her. Her instincts about this were unmatched by others. 

Thus, she'd chosen to engage Feng Meng while fighting him off to stall for time, hoping that the others would rush back in the meantime. Unfortunately, no one returned. 

Feng Meng wasn’t a fool either. It didn’t take him long to figure out her intention.

The Mermaid Queen realized her time of reckoning had come. In this critical moment, she stuffed the immortal medicine into her mouth and jumped down Wu Mountain’s peak. Feng Meng wanted to grab her, only for her to shove him aside with a palm strike. 

An expert of her caliber wouldn’t die even when jumping off a cliff, but it would be a different story if she dissipated her ki and unraveled her protection. Gazing into the distance, the Mermaid Queen left a tear droplet behind as she murmured, “Farewell, my lover…”

Looking at this sight, Zu An felt as if his blood had frozen in place. He had never felt such piercing pain in his chest before. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain grabbed him out of worry. “Don’t do anything silly. Watch to the end first; there might be more to the situation.”

Zu An mustered his courage to look at the screen in the sky. A white glow suddenly enveloped the Mermaid Queen, and her fall suddenly stopped. Instead, she began floating upward. Feng Meng was overjoyed. He flew over to capture her, only for the white light to jolt him away. An ethereal melody played from the sky as flower petals and divine light fell on the Mermaid Queen. That added an air of divinity to her already gorgeous self. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain exclaimed, “She’s ascending to immortality! And it looks like a high-tier ascension.”

“Ascending to immortality?” Zu An’s eyes reddened as he finally saw a sliver of hope. 

“Indeed. There are other ways to achieve immortality aside from overcoming a heavenly tribulation, such as consuming potent world herbs. That immortal medicine was innately born, and it is without doubt one of the most potent herbs in the world. It helped her ascend straight to immortality.”

Zu An stared at the screen.

The Mermaid Queen continued ascending. Feng Meng, hindered by the laws of heaven, could only watch in frustration as she ascended. A moon suddenly appeared in the sky and welcomed the Mermaid Queen into it. 

Zu An’s expression turned weird. He suddenly recalled the poem ‘Chang’e should regret stealing the spirit medicine, with her forlorn heart consoled only by blue sky and emerald sea.’ 

He hadn’t expected the Mermaid Queen to become the real Chang’e, and he wouldn’t have expected there to be such a background story to Chang’e stealing the spirit medicine and ascending to the moon. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2594: Exchanging Hearts
chapters.body:
Chapter 2594: Exchanging Hearts

Zu An continued staring at the screen in the sky. 

After the Mermaid Queen ascended, Feng Meng spent a while venting his anger before leaving in frustration. Wu Mountain reverted to normal, and nothing else happened afterward. 

Zu An was infuriated. That Feng Meng deserves to die!

But now wasn’t the time to settle the score yet. He had to first look for the Mermaid Queen on the moon.

“Where’s the moon?” Zu An asked the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Unlike on Earth, the moon in this world wasn't a planetary body floating in the universe. Similar to how Xihe, the mother of the ten suns, lived in the Scalding Valley, the moon existed in a physical location too. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain spent a moment in thought before replying, “I remember there being a place called the Frost Palace at a remote location in the Celestial Court…”

Zu An nodded in comprehension. The Frost Palace was likely where the moon was, so he began to set off. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain advised him, “A war is raging right now, and there is currently unrest in the Celestial Court. It’ll be extremely risky to head over right now.”

Zu An replied, “I have to make the trip no matter how risky it is. I can’t let her stay there alone.”

He had already shot down the suns. What was there to fear from the moon?

Seeing that he had made up his mind, the Goddess of Wu Mountain said, “Give me a moment.” 

She took out a jade bottle and pointed it at the sky. Shortly after, a droplet of moon-white fluid descended from the fog. Zu An widened his eyes at the familiar fluid. “That’s the teardrop from the Mermaid Queen!”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded as she stowed the droplet into her jade bottle. This was the teardrop shed by the Mermaid Queen out of extreme sorrow when she thought that she would never meet Zu An ever again. 

“So this is how the Defying Spirit Tear is formed.” Ji Xiaoxi, as a medical expert, swiftly noticed the extraordinariness of the teardrop. “But it’s lacking something.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain had a worried frown. “We’ll go with you.”

Zu An hesitated but didn’t turn her down. He was worried that if he left them here, another Feng Meng could appear to harm the ladies. So, he quickly made his way to the sacred tree Jianmu with the ladies.

After Zhuanxu severed heaven from earth, this was the only remaining road to the Celestial Court. If one were to simply fly upward, one would only reach outer space and not the Celestial Court. 

It didn’t take long for them to return to Jianmu, only to find the passageway sealed. It turned out that after regaining control of the Celestial Court, Zhuanxu had decided to temporarily seal the tree's passage to safeguard against retaliation from Dijun’s subordinates.

Zu An’s heart sank. He hadn’t expected such a thing to happen. I shouldn’t have left the Celestial Court so quickly! But then again, he wouldn’t have known about the Mermaid Queen’s plight if he hadn’t returned to Wu Mountain.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain consoled Zu An, “You don’t need to be too worried. The Frost Palace should be far from the war between the Shamans and the Fiends, so Miss Chang’e is at least safe for the time being.”

Zu An knew that too; it was just that he felt terrible not knowing when the two of them could reunite. He said, “Since I can’t meet her for the time being, I’ll first settle my scores.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was worried. “There appears to have been someone behind Feng Meng. Feng Meng is already formidable as he is; his master is bound to be even more so.”

“I already have a rough idea who it is.” Zu An recalled the words Xihe had secretly told him while they were embracing. The doubts he had were slowly unraveling. 

“Big brother Zu, I’ll go with you,” Xie Daoyun said. Now that she had made huge advancements in her cultivation, she was confident that she would be able to help.

“I’ll go too.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain was worried about leaving him be. 

“I want to go too!” Ji Xiaoxi raised her hand.

Zu An burst into laughter. “You're severely injured, and your soul is impaired. How can you come with me when you’re already struggling as it is?”

Ji Xiaoxi stuck out her tongue and murmured wretchedly, “I’m so useless that I can’t help big brother Zu at all.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain argued, “It’s too dangerous for you to go alone.”

Zu An pondered briefly before nodding. “You’re right. Let’s return to Wu Mountain first. We need to make some preparations.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was hugely relieved to see that he was no longer planning to move recklessly. “All right.”

Xie Daoyun frowned at the sight. Zu An’s proposal to return to Wu Mountain was so smooth that it almost sounded as if that was his home. She had been watching the situation all along, so she was aware of how the two of them had become acquainted. 

She still remembered how cold the Goddess of Wu Mountain was when they first met. When did they become so close?

… 

The group made their way back to Wu Mountain. Due to her weak soul, Ji Xiaoxi soon fell asleep.

Xie Daoyun told Zu An about what happened after she was transported away by the Nature Jade Plate and unwittingly became the Fiend races’ Saintess Yumen Beiqing. Similarly, she also learned about what had happened recently from Zu An. 

She wanted to continue chatting with Zu An, but her soul was also shaken from being possessed by Xihe. Before she knew it, she had also fallen asleep in her lover’s arms.

Zu An carried her to a room and placed her down. He considered lying down too, but he couldn’t sleep at all. Too many things had happened recently. He went out to the cliff where the Mermaid Queen had jumped down from. He looked at the bright moon in the sky. Gong Suyin must be feeling lonely there.

He felt bitter regret at how he had initially avoided her despite her determined advances. 

“Are you thinking about Miss Chang’e?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain asked gently from behind.

Zu An turned around. Under the cold moonlight, the Goddess of Wu Mountain looked ravishing. “Goddess, you haven’t gone to sleep yet?”

“Don’t call me 'goddess'. Call me Yaoji instead.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain blinked. “We are friends, aren’t we?”

Zu An understood what she was getting at and replied, “My real name is Zu An.”

“I know.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain chuckled. She tidied up her skirt before sitting down beside him.

“You know?”

“The ladies around you have been calling you big brother Zu. I’m not a fool.”

Zu An felt apologetic. “I’m sorry. I didn’t mean to deceive you.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain wasn’t bothered by it. She looked at the moon in the sky and asked, “Miss Chang’e probably isn’t Chang’e either, right? I recall you all calling her the Mermaid Queen?”

“Indeed…” Zu An thought about how they had been through so many life-and-death situations, realizing that it would be heartless for him to continue putting distance between them. Thus, he decided to tell her the truth about their backgrounds. 

“I see. You aren’t from this period in spacetime…” The Goddess of Wu Mountain lowered her head contemplatively. The anomalies she had noticed finally made sense, and it made her realize why a droplet was missing from the Defying Spirit Tear…

Zu An wanted to apologize again when the Goddess of Wu Mountain raised her head and looked at him with her beautiful, starry eyes. “Big brother Zu, can you tell me more about you and Miss Pei?”

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected her to make such a request. I guess she must be curious, given how much they resemble each other. 

Thus, he told her how he first met Pei Mianman and everything that had happened afterward. 

When she heard about the decades they had spent as a couple in the Yinxu dungeon, the Goddess of Wu Mountain blushed. It reminded her of what she had seen not long ago in an illusion.

Was that really an illusion… 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2595: I’M The Kind To Exact Vengeance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2595: I’M The Kind To Exact Vengeance

Zu An was perplexed by the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s particular interest in the Yinxu dungeon. He tried asking about it, but she refused to answer. She simply looked away with flushed cheeks and evasive eyes. Despite being confused, he still told her everything he knew. Of course, he wasn’t so tactless as to carelessly reveal insider details about the time he spent with Manman.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was extremely curious about that, but how was she supposed to ask about that? She could only ask details about the Yinxu dungeon to corroborate the illusion she'd seen back then.

A long time later, she remarked, “Xihe said that I could be Miss Pei’s previous incarnation. Do you believe that?”

Zu An chuckled. “Yes and no.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was confused. “Ah?”

“I believe it because the two of you are identical. That’s why I mistook you for her the first time we met. It would be too much of a coincidence to say that there's no connection between the two of you. However, I also don’t believe it because your personalities are drastically different. You’re cool and graceful, while Manman is as passionate as fire.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes drooped. “You must find me too cold.”

“That’s not it. I have met women whose personalities resemble yours, such as Chu…” Zu An’s smile suddenly stiffened up. It didn’t seem like a wise idea to talk about other women in front of a woman. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain teased him. “You’re indeed a player.”

Zu An felt awkward. “It’s not what you think…” But he had no idea how he could explain it.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t harp on this topic. “Do you prefer ladies who are cooler or more passionate?”

Zu An was stunned. For a moment, he couldn’t tell whether she was asking him to compare between Chuyan and Manman or her and Manman… However, he reacted quickly. “Only children do multiple choice questions. Adults take it all.”

It wasn't a direct answer, but it still said it all.

“You’re so sly.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain regretted the question as soon as she blurted it. She had no idea what prompted her to ask such a question. Is it because he’s fated to leave this world?

His answer was just nice. It didn’t make her feel awkward or uncomfortable. 

Just like that, they sat by the cliff shoulder beside shoulder and chatted for a long time… 

After several days of rest and recuperation, the ladies made huge strides in their recovery. Even Ji Xiaoxi was much more spirited than before.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain asked, “Where do you intend to head next?”

“Taoqiu!”

“The human race’s capital?” The Goddess of Wu Mountain was taken aback. Zu An had been gathering information about the human race in recent days, but she didn’t expect that to be his next destination. 

“I need the Human World’s power insignia, so I have to pay a visit to the human race.” Zu An didn’t explain any further. He had planned to leave the three ladies here, but now that they had recovered, they insisted on tagging along. 

Zu An didn’t turn them away. He grabbed them and began making his way to Taoqiu. He had already figured out the directions there over the last few days. This world was massive, but with his Rainbow Morph, it didn’t take him long to reach Taoqiu. Unlike the humble tribes he had seen before, Taoqiu was already taking the shape of a city. It even had city walls.

In this era, most humans wore straw clothes and grass skirts. Some of them even just went around nude, except for some leaves covering their genitals. In contrast, Taoqiu’s populace was clothed, albeit in rough and crude clothes. Nevertheless, hints of the future prosperity of the human civilization could already be seen here. 

“So this was how human civilization came to be.” Ji Xiaoxi was awed. As a human, she felt proud to see how this city was slowly becoming more prosperous.

Xie Daoyun also observed the city in excitement. This was a rare glimpse into history. 

“What a pity we’re here as enemies,” Zu An remarked with a sigh.

Ji Xiaoxi and Xie Daoyun were stunned.

Even the Goddess of Wu Mountain was baffled. You’re a human too! Did you come here to massacre your ancestors?!

Zu An was not here to deal with the ordinary humans. Instead, he took a deep breath and roared, “Feng Meng, get out here!”

His voice boomed thunderously across the city.

The humans were horrified. Some warriors rushed out with their weapons.

“Who dares to cause trouble…” Some warriors rushed out with their weapons and glared at the group in animosity… although that lessened when they saw how ravishing Xie Daoyun, Ji Xiaoxi, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were. “What do you want? Feng Meng is the hero of our human race!”

Zu An frowned. He roared once more, “Feng Meng, are you going to hole yourself up like a tortoise?”

The warriors were infuriated. They angrily criticized Zu An for his disrespect. 

Zu An received a huge influx of Rage points, but his calm gaze remained fixed on a specific direction in the city.

There was a burst of white light, and an arrow appeared before him the next instant. It was so fast that the three women beside him didn’t even have time to warn him to be careful. 

However, the arrow stopped in front of Zu An, unable to advance any further, as he'd caught it with two fingers.

The human warriors gasped. They hadn’t expected the intruder to catch Feng Meng’s divine arrow so easily. What’s his background?

Feng Meng appeared on the city wall. He was taken aback to see Zu An. “It’s you.”

Without any hesitation, Zu An drew the Sun Slaying Bow and shot an arrow at him. 

Feng Meng was startled, but he quickly nocked his bow and retaliated with an arrow of his own.

His archery was indeed formidable; his arrow accurately struck the one Zu An shot. This brought a hint of glee to his lips. However, his smile soon froze in place when Zu An’s arrow split his arrow into halves before whizzing toward him. The arrow was moving so fast that he had no time to dodge.

Just then, a saber suddenly appeared out of nowhere and hacked down on the arrow, changing its trajectory. 

Feng Meng snapped out of his daze and hurriedly dodged to the side, but the arrow still scraped his arm. There was a burst of bloody mist as the arrow’s sheer momentum ripped off his arm. 

A woman beside him quickly fed him a pill. “Eat it!”

Feng Meng quickly popped down the pill. The stump of his ripped arm began wriggling as another arm swiftly sprouted out. However, his complexion remained awful. I didn’t expect a mere scrape to result in such a severe injury.

The brawny, saber-wielding man looked at Zu An in astonishment, too. “It hasn’t been long since we parted ways. How did you become so strong?”

“It has been a while, Lord of Slaughter, and…” Zu An’s gaze fell on Salamay.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2596: Courting Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2596: Courting Death

Salamay chuckled. “It has been a while, young master. You’re still as dashing as ever.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain, Ji Xiaoxi, and Xie Daoyun frowned upon hearing her laughter. Her way of speaking is so detestable.

Zu An looked around and noticed someone missing. “Where’s Princess Suolun?”

“Tsk tsk tsk. The princess will be moved to know that you’re so concerned about her.” Salamay shook her head. “But shouldn’t you show concern to me too, young master? How heartless!”

Zu An harrumphed. “It hasn’t been that long, but you have changed.”

“Changed? Am I more beautiful than before?” Salamay asked.

“You’ve become cheap.”

“…” Salamay’s smile froze on the spot.

You have successfully trolled Salamay for +444… +444… +444…

The smiles didn’t disappear. In this case, they merely moved from Salamay’s face to Ji Xiaoxi and the others. 

Seeing his daughter getting humiliated, the Lord of Slaughter harrumphed in anger. “Brat, you’re courting death!”

He stomped his feet, causing the ground to shake. He leaped into the air and unleashed terrifying saber ki on Zu An. 

The humans on the city wall, despite being a far distance away, couldn’t withstand the terrifying pressure and fell paralyzed on their butts. Their weapons scattered all over the ground.

Zu An drew the Sun Slaying Bow and shot an arrow. It pierced right through the Lord of Slaughter’s saber ki and accurately struck his saber. 

Ding!

A piercing sound rang out, and the Lord of Slaughter was sent flying. His arm was numb, and he stared at his saber in shock. It was cracked.

This put him in a terrible mood. He had already returned to his peak, and several fortuitous encounters had further raised his fighting prowess. On top of that, Salamay had shared with him how Zu An defeated the Monster Lord back then, which gave him a good grasp of Zu An’s skills. Given that Zu An’s cultivation was beneath his, as long as he leveraged on his own strengths, he should have been able to pull off an easy victory against the latter. He hadn’t expected to suffer such a huge setback right from the get-go.

“Be careful of his bow. That’s Suzeng, a divine weapon that can even shoot down the suns in the sky,” Feng Meng warned.

The Lord of Slaughter was appalled. He had seen Zu An use this bow before, but never had it displayed might comparable to what shot the suns down earlier. He was glad Zu An had opted for rapid fire instead of going for a full power shot, or else that earlier arrow could have claimed his life. 

“Don’t you pride yourself on being the number one archer? Why don’t we compete on those terms?” Zu An sneered as he shot another arrow at Feng Meng.

Feng Meng took in the earlier lesson and didn’t dare to let his guard down. He rapidly fired three arrows, each one accurately striking the heads of Zu An’s arrows to neutralize their force. 

In the meantime, the Lord of Slaughter used the chance to charge at Zu An like a cannonball. 

However, the Goddess of Wu Mountain gently moved her fingers, and layers of rippling water suddenly blocked his path. This caused the Lord of Slaughter’s movements to slow as if he was trapped in a bog. 

In the meantime, Xie Daoyun channeled her talismans. Two massive golden-armored warriors descended from the sky. One held the Lord of Slaughter down, and the other swung his massive axe down on the latter’s head. 

The Lord of Slaughter roared. He transformed into his true body, enlarging into a humongous dinosaur monster that towered over the others. He knocked down a golden-armored warrior with a kick, before brandishing his saber to slice the other one in half.

The golden-armored warrior who was knocked down tried to return to his feet, only for the Lord of Slaughter to raise his massive foot and stamp down on the former's head. 

Pu!

The golden-armored warrior’s head burst like a watermelon, and his figure dissipated.

These kills drew out the Lord of Slaughter’s bloodthirst. He raised his neck upward and unleashed a terrifying roar.

The humans spectating the battle were so intimidated by the roar that some even pissed their pants. This was natural fear they felt toward their nemesis.

“Monster! He’s a monster!”

“He isn’t a human!”

“What kind of monstrosity is he?!”

… 

A few human warriors had still been planning to aid the Lord of Slaughter, but the sight of his true form caused their feet to halt in place. Some even retreated out of fear. 

The Lord of Slaughter’s eyes reddened. These lowly, reprehensible humans! Just wait till I subdue Zu An; I’ll surely capture some to have a taste! Fine, in light of that lord, I’ll just gobble up a thousand of you. He shouldn’t punish me if it’s only to this extent.

Many more hands suddenly sprouted out of Feng Meng’s body, and his bow seemed to clone itself as well. He began firing arrows at Zu An like a gatling gun, not giving the latter any time to rest while buying time for the Lord of Slaughter to approach him. 

The Lord of Slaughter understood the intent and charged toward Zu An. The next moment, however, the sky abruptly darkened, and a purple sea of lightning instantly enveloped him.

It was the High Firmament Talisman!

Despite the Lord of Slaughter’s resilient body, the ferocious purple lightning still had him screaming in pain. As this world was rich in natural ki, the prowess of heavenly lightning was far stronger compared to other worlds.

“You lowly slut! How dare you!” Salamay roared as she charged at Xie Daoyun with a dagger. 

Xie Daoyun was startled. She was too busy channeling the High Firmament Talisman to dodge.

“I won’t allow you to hurt big sister Xie!” Ji Xiaoxi suddenly cried out as she sprayed a black fog at the incoming Salamay.

Salamay’s plan was to endure the poison fog and first get rid of Xie Daoyun. Since Ji Xiaoxi's cultivation was only on the level of Taoqiu’s ordinary warriors, Salamay didn't think she was a threat. Furthermore, she always carried a couple of defensive artifacts with her. 

It was only upon coming into contact with the black fog that she realized how grievous of a mistake she had made. Her protective artifacts enveloped her with protective light barriers, but all of them shattered as soon as they came into contact with the fog. She felt an excruciating pain assaulting her, and that prompted her to quickly retreat.

“GAHHH! MY FACE!” Salamay retreated while clawing at her face. It didn’t take long before her beautiful features were disfigured. To make things worse, the black fog was still seeping deeper into her body. It melted her flesh and blood, revealing her white bones beneath. 

This made the Lord of Slaughter roar in anger. 

The spectators were horrified as well. They couldn’t believe that the sweet-looking, frail lady would employ such a terrifying poison. 

Ji Xiaoxi was stunned too. She quickly tossed a porcelain bottle over. “Here, hurry up and take the antidote.”

As a renowned physician, she had previously concocted medicine to suppress her body’s poison. At the same time, she also controlled the black fog to leave Salamay’s body. 

Without any hesitation, Salamay grabbed the porcelain bottle and downed its contents. Her frenzy gradually receded as the medicine took effect. With her formidable recovery prowess, she swiftly regrew her flesh and skin. However, perhaps due to the poison seeping in too deeply, all kinds of warts and scars were left on her beautiful face, making her look hideous. 

“You bitch! I’ll murder you!” Salamay glared at Ji Xiaoxi with venomous eyes. She had never expected to suffer such a setback in the latter’s hands. 

“S-Sorry…” Ji Xiaoxi profusely apologized, not having expected Salamay to end up like that. 

Zu An was speechless How can anyone be this kind? If the situation was reversed, there’s no way Salamay would spare her. 

He turned toward the city and shouted, “You’re still holding out. Are you planning to watch as they march to their deaths here?”

After saying his piece, Zu An shot a gleaming arrow that easily dispersed Feng Meng’s exaggerated arrow rain. The arrow whizzed onward with unabated momentum to shatter Feng Meng’s bow before pinning him against the city wall.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2597: Motive
chapters.body:
Chapter 2597: Motive

Feng Meng was flabbergasted. He had neutralized Zu An’s arrow with three arrows earlier, so he thought he could catch Zu An off guard by firing many arrows at once. He hadn't thought Zu An could unravel his arrow rain with just a single arrow!

Was he toying with me earlier?

The arrow forcefully pinned him to the city wall, and the excruciating pain nearly caused him to pass out. He tried raising his hand to pluck out the arrow, but he couldn’t muster the strength. He finally understood how the Golden Crows felt when being shot down. 

It was a terrifying feat, considering how Zu An hadn’t taken his time to build up energy for this arrow, and the earlier arrow rain had weakened the arrow’s might by at least half. If not for that, it would have torn Feng Meng apart.

Salamay could no longer be counted on in this situation. She had just clawed her face apart and was now glaring at Ji Xiaoxi with venomous eyes.

As for the Lord of Slaughter, he had finally struggled free of the purple lightning storm, but he was in terrible shape. His body was charred black, and his red flesh could be seen through his cracked skin. Smoke was rising from his body. He smelled so delicious that a few people gulped.

That further angered the Lord of Slaughter. How dare these ants think of eating me?!

But he didn’t dare to charge in recklessly anymore. With Feng Meng incapacitated, it would be suicidal for him to continue the fight.

Zu An paid no heed to the Lord of Slaughter; his eyes were focused on the city. Seeing no one was coming out, he shot another arrow at Feng Meng. This is the culprit who caused the Mermaid Queen to ascend to the Frost Palace. I’ll exact vengeance for her.

Feeling the devastating energy harnessed in that arrow, Feng Meng’s every cell frenziedly screamed at him to dodge the attack. However, he was pinned to the city wall and was unable to move at all. He could only watch as death encroached on him. He cried, “Save me, master!”

A divine light descended and knocked away the arrow at the last moment. 

Feng Meng felt his crotch turning wet, but he felt no shame at all; only relief. I’m glad that master intervened. I should be able to survive this ordeal now.

A figure emerged from the city’s skyline and leisurely approached Zu An a step at a time. 

“Emperor Yao!” The humans in the city kneeled and kowtowed to the figure.

Zu An assessed the humans; there was no fear on their faces. They hadn't been coerced into worshipping him. Their respect for him came from the bottom of their hearts.

The figure landed on the city wall as he asked, “Isn’t it too much for you to come to Taoqiu to kill my people?” 

He had a squarish face, thick eyebrows, and deep eyes. He naturally commanded authority when he spoke. 

“Is he one of your people too?” Zu An glanced at the monstrous-looking Lord of Slaughter.

Emperor Yao’s expression stiffened. It took a while before he recovered and asked, “Why have you come to Taoqiu to cause trouble?” 

“You’re asking why?” Zu An sneered. He pointed at Feng Meng and scoffed, “Not too long ago, that fellow went after my friend. You were the one who ordered him to do so, Emperor Yao, so why are you feigning ignorance?” 

“I don’t know what you’re talking about.” Emperor Yao didn’t seem surprised that Zu An knew his identity. He gestured for the human warriors to seek refuge in the city. 

The human warriors insisted on fighting alongside him, to which Emperor Yao replied with a chuckle, “They are very powerful. You’ll only lose your life in vain if you remain here. Besides, there might not necessarily be a fight between us.”

Upon hearing those words, the human warriors finally retreated into the city. There were no longer any humans in the city gates’ vicinity.

Zu An frowned. “Are you roleplaying a good ruler in front of me?”

“You’re overthinking it. Since they worship me as the Human Emperor, I am obliged to protect them well. It has nothing to do with you,” Emperor Yao replied calmly. 

“You put on a righteous face in front of them, but do they know about your despicable deeds?” Zu An sneered.

“Despicable deeds? What do you mean?” Emperor Yao chuckled, unfazed by Zu An’s accusations. 

“You orchestrated the war between the Fiends and the Shamans, didn’t you?” Zu An glared at Emperor Yao. 

“Nonsense. Who doesn’t know that Emperor Jun is the mastermind behind the war between the Shamans and the Fiends? What does this have to do with me?” Emperor Yao shrugged. The faint smile on his lips hinted at his interest in this conversation. 

“Yes, Emperor Jun’s ambitions played a huge part. He secretly forged the Myriad Souls Banner, collected souls from all over the world, and even killed and possessed the ten Golden Crows. However, you have also been actively pushing things along,” Zu An sneered.

“Take the events at Zhong Mountain, for instance; Zhuanxu sacrificed his grandson to sever heaven from earth. That should have suppressed the conflict between the Shamans and the Fiends, but shortly after, Nuba began publicly cursing Emperor Jun, demanding he reveal himself.

“Emperor Jun had carried out his plans covertly. Nuba wouldn’t have figured out the truth so quickly if someone hadn’t tipped her off. Due to Nuba’s relentless actions, Emperor Jun had no choice but to accelerate his plans and bring the ten suns out to devastate the world.”

Emperor Yao calmly replied, “It’s only normal for Nuba to seek vengeance for her son in a fit of anger. Besides, if not for my reminder, Emperor Jun wouldn’t have been forced to put his plans into motion in a rush.
