More people would have died if he had more time to prepare.” He turned to Ji Xiaoxi and added, “This lady would have also become a part of the Myriad Souls Banner if not for my reminder. You should be thanking me for that.”

Zu An was rendered speechless. Emperor Yao made so much sense that he couldn’t retort against that. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain asked, “Yao, isn’t Emperor Jun your father? I don’t understand why you did all this.”

“Emperor Jun is a womanizer. I am but one of his many sons. My mother was a rag to him—to be used and thrown away—and she died lamenting her fate. That made him pay even less attention to me.” Emperor Yao chuckled self-deprecatingly. “But I am thankful for that, or else I could have ended up in the same plight as the ten Golden Crows.” 

“Did you foil his plan out of hatred?” The Goddess of Wu Mountain nodded in realization.

To her surprise, Emperor Yao harrumphed in disdain. “Don’t take me to be that shallow. Ultimately, his deeds were reprehensible. Anyone with a conscience would do everything they could to stop him.”

He spoke with an air of righteousness, which made him look bigger than before. Even the Goddess of Wu Mountain had a better impression of him. 

But Zu An coldly pointed out, “You could have publicly exposed him if you wished to stop him, but you quietly supported his ploy instead. I was perplexed about your aim all along, but it finally made sense when I saw how much the human race respected you.

“Emperor Jun sought to obliterate the shamans so the fiends could monopolize the world. He attempted to attain Sainthood through accomplishing this unprecedented deed.

“And yet, your gaze was set further ahead. You wished to use the war between the shamans and the fiends to stage the downfall of the two races, so the human race could rise through the ranks and prosper. Your goal was to attain Sainthood through your merit as the Human Emperor.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2598: Frenzy
chapters.body:
Chapter 2598: Frenzy

“Attain Sainthood?” 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain and the others widened their eyes in realization. Zu An’s words cleared up all their doubts.  

Even the Lord of Slaughter and Feng Meng nodded in agreement. They had known all along that their master was plotting something, but they had no idea what his ultimate goal was.

Emperor Yao looked at Zu An with approving eyes. “You’re indeed smart to have guessed my motive. It’s no surprise you were able to foil Emperor Jun's scheme and even win his wife over in bed.”

He seemed to respect Zu An more because of the latter than the former. Perhaps due to his mother’s plight, Zu An and Xihe’s scandal made him feel vindicated. 

The other ladies looked at Zu An with bizarre gazes.

Zu An said, “…Please don’t talk nonsense. Xihe and I have a clear conscience. Our relationship is nowhere as dirty as you put it out to be.”

Emperor Yao burst into laughter, but he couldn’t be bothered to argue. “Yes, my goal was to use the rise of the human race to attain Sainthood. Everyone has their way of attaining Sainthood; no path is superior to the other. If anything, you should be thankful to me.”

“I should be thankful to you?” Zu An snorted in contempt.

“Of course!” Emperor Yao replied proudly. “Look at the human race. They are weak, foolish, and backward. But you and your friends… You are charming and strong, despite being human too. This proves my path is right. The human race will prosper in the future. As someone who has benefited from my efforts, shouldn’t you be thankful to me?”

Zu An frowned. Emperor Yao’s words made sense. If not for Emperor Yao, the human race wouldn't have prospered, and Zu An and his friends could have never come into existence either. Still, he said, “That doesn’t change the fact that you killed so many beings to fulfill your greed.”

Emperor Yao shook his finger. “You have two things wrong. One, it was not I, but Emperor Jun who killed those beings. Two, my actions were not to fulfill my greed but for the greater good. More beings would have died in Emperor Jun's hands if not for my intervention.”

Zu An and the others exchanged looks. They knew that Emperor Yao was twisting the narrative, but there was some sense in his words, making it hard to refute his points. 

“That’s why we aren’t enemies.” Emperor Yao chuckled upon seeing their faces. “I’m not afraid of you, but there is no reason for us to fight. Even if you defeat and kill me, that will affect the future. The basis of your existence might cease to be.”

Zu An took a deep breath. Considering all the records about Emperor Yao in human history, he knew it was unwise to make a move against the other party here. 

Besides, given that this was a turning point in history, it was unlikely Zu An could touch Emperor Yao even if he gave it everything he had, similar to how Xihe couldn’t stop the simultaneous appearance of the ten suns and the subsequent downfall of the nine suns no matter how she tried. 

So, Zu An pointed to Feng Meng and asked, “Why did you send him to harm Chang’e, if we aren’t enemies as you said?”

Emperor Yao frowned. “I only ordered him to retrieve the immortal medicine. I didn’t expect him to act on his own accord out of lust for Chang’e.”

Feng Meng panicked. “Master, I have served you with devotion over the years. You have to save me!”

Emperor Yao’s expression turned cold. “You should have known such a day would come when you used my name to commit such outrageous deeds. I have already saved you several times now. Who was the one who healed your soul when you were on the verge of death?”

Feng Meng knew he couldn’t count on Emperor Yao anymore, so he turned into countless black little pigs and escaped in all directions. This was his escape method; it was the tactic he'd used to escape last time. He would suffer a grievous loss that would take a long time to recover, but at least he would be alive. 

The Lord of Slaughter was taken aback. Even Emperor Yao was surprised. He hadn't expected Feng Meng to be so decisive. 

But Zu An reacted right away. His aura had always been locked onto the culprit who caused him and the Mermaid Queen to be ‘eternally separated’. He shot an arrow via the Sun Slaying Bow, and the arrow exploded mid-flight into a massive rain of golden arrows that covered everything within a radius of five kilometers.

Boom!

Countless black pigs squealed as they were killed by the arrow rain. A massive pit over fifty meters deep was left in the wake of the arrows. The nearby city wall also collapsed as a result. It was fortunate Emperor Yao had ordered his men to retreat into the city, or else they could have been implicated in this. 

Sensing that Feng Meng’s soul had been thoroughly destroyed by this assault, the Lord of Slaughter gulped and reflexively retreated. The Sun Slaying Bow was too scary; he wasn’t confident he could deal with it. Sensing Zu An’s gaze falling on him, he hurriedly retreated to Emperor Yao’s side. “Master!”

It had previously filled him with shame and anger to call Emperor Yao ‘master’, but he was now saying it on his own volition. He knew that was the only way he could preserve his life. 

Emperor Yao looked conflicted. He debated whether to protect the Lord of Slaughter or not.

Just then, Salamay shouted, “Hmph! Let us go right now, or else it’ll be the end of this lass!” She had slipped back into the city during the chaos and returned with Suolun Shi as a hostage.

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief when he saw that Suolun Shi was still alive. “It’s a relief that you’re fine.”

“Big brother Zu…” Suolun Shi was ashamed. Zu An had entrusted her to guard the prisoners, but she'd foolishly fallen for Salamay’s tricks and ended up becoming the prisoner herself. 

“It’s up to you to decide whether she’ll escape unscathed or not.” Salamay pressed her knife tightly against Suolun Shi’s neck. “Do you know why she released us in the first place?”

“Why?” Zu An and the others were curious to know how Salamay and the others escaped. 

“Don’t say it!” Suolun Shi exclaimed with a reddened face. She even attempted to charge forward to let the knife slit her neck. 

But Salamay had been on guard against that. She stopped Suolun Shi by grabbing her vitals before continuing with a smirk, “It’s because she wants your love, but she knows she’s nothing compared to your lovers. With such an inner demon, it wasn't too difficult for us to convince her.”

Despair filled Suolun Shi’s eyes. She would rather die than confront this moment. She lowered her head, not daring to face Zu An.

Zu An gently spoke up. “Princess Suolun is beautiful, kind, and has a powerful background. Which man wouldn’t like her? You merely took advantage of her lack of worldly experience.”

Light returned to Suolun Shi’s eyes. She could tell that Zu An didn’t truly like her, but his willingness to console and preserve her dignity was more than enough to move her. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain stared at Zu An. No wonder he has so many women by his side. With a mouth as sweet as his, which woman wouldn’t fall for him?

But those words greatly triggered Salamay. “Fine, fine. She lacks worldly experience, but I’m sly and scheming. Since that’s the case, you can regret it for the rest of your life!”

Her face distorted in frenzy. She knew that their only way out of this was to continue holding Suolun Shi as a hostage, but she couldn’t control her emotions at all. Anger and killing intent poured out from her heart. Let everything go to ruin then!

She unhesitatingly slit Suolun Shi’s neck. 

“NO!” The Lord of Slaughter panicked, knowing they would be doomed if they lost the hostage. My daughter is usually smarter than this. What happened?

Zu An also hadn’t expected her to go berserk. It was already too late for him to stop her. 

Just then, Salamay’s body stiffened. She gurgled, and fresh blood spurted out of her mouth. 

She looked down and saw a spear conjured from flames piercing through her chest. She turned around with great difficulty and saw a man covered in flames.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2599: The Final Power Insignia
chapters.body:
Chapter 2599: The Final Power Insignia

“Wuhui?” Salamay had been in this world for some time now. While serving Emperor Yao, she'd consciously tried to gather information about the powerhouses in this world. She needed to know who could and couldn't be trifled with. She'd even considered exploiting the conflicts between the powerhouses to free herself from Emperor Yao’s slavery. 

Fire Celestial Wuhui was a renowned expert in this world, and he fell into the category that she couldn’t mess with. She couldn’t comprehend why Wuhui would suddenly attack her when there was no grudge between them.

Everything only clicked when she noticed Yun Yuqing standing behind Wuhui. Her eyes brimmed with indignation. Why can others always find such a strong and loyal backing? The Incestuous King was devoted to me but he was weak, whereas Emperor Yao is strong but isn’t willing to stand up for me. 

Why?! Why do others have such a good life when mine is so tough?!

She wanted to vent the seething anger from life’s injustices with a roar, but Wuhui’s fire spear had already claimed her life. Her body collapsed to the floor. Her eyes remained wide, but the light in them had been extinguished. 

“My daughter!” The infuriated Lord of Slaughter charged over.

A glint flashed through Wuhui’s eyes. With a flick of his wrist, his fire spear transformed into a massive fire saber. He brought it down upon the Lord of Slaughter with unstoppable momentum. 

The fire saber’s terrifying aura and killing intent shocked the Lord of Slaughter, but it was already too late to back down. He mustered his courage and swung his saber with all his might. 

The physical saber and the flame saber clashed. However, the former caved in and exploded into countless fragments. The flame saber continued rushing forth with unabated momentum to slice through the Lord of Slaughter’s chest. 

Pu!

A bloody mist splattered. The Lord of Slaughter’s massive body collapsed.  

“Hm? He’s still alive even after taking a strike from me?” Wuhui was surprised too. He brandished his flame saber once more to kill the Lord of Slaughter for good. 

Yun Yuqing took this chance to transport the terrified Suolun Shi to Zu An’s side. 

Zu An was overjoyed to see Yun Yuqing. “What brings you here?”

“We happened to pass by. Wuhui is in the midst of contacting the experts of this world.” The sight of her lover made Yun Yuqing’s eyes curve into beautiful crescents.

Suolun Shi further lowered her head miserably. Big brother Zu’s lovers are so beautiful and competent, whereas I only cause trouble for him. 

Just then, there was another twist on the battlefield. Emperor Yao flashed over to the Lord of Slaughter’s side. He made no particular movement, but a spherical light barrier that manifested around him blocked Wuhui’s flame saber. 

“Are you going to stand in my way, Yao?” Wuhui coldly asked. He had a fiery temper to match his position as the Fire Celestial. 

Emperor Yao smiled. “There isn’t a grudge between you two, and he isn’t fated to meet his end here.” The latter half of the sentence was directed at Zu An.

Zu An was stunned. He knows a fair bit. “Indeed, he’s fated to meet his end in the future.”

In the future, the Lord of Slaughter would break free of his seal and go on a massacre. Qiu Honglei would be forced to perform her mysterious ritual dance to summon an enigmatic entity to subdue him. Since Qiu Honglei was already out of danger, it would be unwise to tamper with the cause and effect of the world, or else the future could be thrown into disarray. 

Seeing that, Wuhui retracted his flames. “Fine, I’ll spare him then.”

The Lord of Slaughter was shocked. Wuhui surprisingly respects Zu An’s opinion. Are the two of them acquainted?

But what shocked him more was their discussion. Salamay had told him what would happen in the future. In the first place, he had come to this world in hopes of obtaining an ancient legacy to rewrite his fate. He'd thought his encounter with Emperor Yao was a blessing, as the latter could help him awaken his ancient bloodline. Little did he know that the other party had thought of him and his daughter as disposable tools all along. Despite his struggles, his fate remained unchanged.

His daughter’s death and the realization that he was helpless to change fate lifted his reservations, and he roared at Emperor Yao, “Yao, how can you just throw us aside after we did so many dirty deeds for you?! I’ll haunt you even as a ghost!”

Emperor Yao laughed. “You won’t have a chance to become a ghost.”

He tapped the Lord of Slaughter’s forehead, sending a ray of golden light into the latter’s head. The Lord of Slaughter’s angry eyes slowly lost their light before gradually closing. 

Zu An was startled. “What are you doing?”

“Calm down, I didn’t kill him. I’m merely erasing his memories. I don’t think he recalls anything that happened here when you encounter him again in the future, right?” Emperor Yao smiled.

Zu An looked at Emperor Yao wryly. I bet you’re just silencing him lest he reveal the dirty things he's done for you. 

But he had to concede that Emperor Yao was right. He was also worried that if the Lord of Slaughter retained his memories about this world, the latter could assassinate him as soon as he broke free of his seal. 

Zu An suddenly empathized with Xihe. Fate was so powerful that it felt as if they could only be helplessly swept away by it. Somehow, fate had a way of correcting its trajectory in unexpected ways, ensuring that history aligned with the future. 

“What are you doing here, Wuhui?” Emperor Yao asked.

“I was rallying the experts on earth together to investigate Dijun’s forging of the Myriad Souls Banner, but that has little meaning now that the war between the shamans and the fiends has already commenced. So, I came here to propose an alliance with the human race to deal with the shamans,” Wuhui replied. 

Emperor Yao chuckled. “When did the Celestial Court start thinking so highly of the human race as to extend a hand to them?”

Wuhui harrumphed. “The human race might be insignificant, but it’s only right for them to raise their arms and fight in this tumultuous period.”

Emperor Yao reined in his smile. “That may be true, but why does the human race have to fight for your people?”

Wuhui was appalled and enraged. “The Celestial Court protected the human race over the years, and the human race worships the deities of the Celestial Court too. You yourself are from the Celestial Court, and a descendant of the Celestial Emperor no less. How can you choose to fight for the enemy?”

“But the shamans have also protected the human race, and the humans worship some shamans as deities too. It doesn’t seem right to choose a side.” Emperor Yao paused as he glanced at the city. “I might be from the Celestial Court, but the human race now worships me as the Human Emperor too. I cannot let down their faith in me.”

Wuhui’s expression darkened. “Are you planning to help the shamans?!”

Emperor Yao shook his head. “The human race is too weak to be caught in the war between the shamans and the fiends. We shall help neither side.”

Wuhui exhaled in relief. “Fine. Just remember your own place and fulfill your promise!”

The worst-case scenario was for the human race to side with the shamans. Compared to that, the human race taking a neutral stance was an acceptable outcome.

Wuhui still needed to recruit the other powers and bring them under the Celestial Court, so he didn’t want to waste time here. He turned to Yun Yuqing.

Yun Yuqing shuffled to Zu An’s side and said, “I want to stay with him.”

Wuhui frowned. Were it in the past, he would have never allowed someone with such pure Celestial Emperor bloodline to stay with a mortal, lest their pure bloodline be sullied. However, with the war between the shamans and the fiends intensifying, Wuhui would soon have to join the battlefield too. It would be dangerous for him to continue taking Yun Yuqing with him. So, he made sure to warn Zu An before leaving on his fire cloud. 

Emperor Yao turned to Zu An and smiled. “We’re the only ones left now. Would you like to fight or not?”

Zu An was speechless. This fellow is a master of psychological warfare. My hostility toward him has mostly faded after everything he said. 

More importantly, Emperor Yao was key to the human race’s future prosperity. Unpredictable things could occur if something were to happen to Emperor Yao now. Perhaps Zu An and his lovers would even cease to exist in the future. 

“You’re right. There’s no reason for us to fight.”

“It’s a pleasure to communicate with a smart man. Here, I have a gift for you. You’ll probably need it.” Emperor Yao casually tossed an item over. 

Zu An caught the item and inspected it. It was a staff harnessing the Human Emperor’s aura. It was the Human World’s power insignia!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2600: The Frost Palace’S Curse
chapters.body:
Chapter 2600: The Frost Palace’S Curse

Zu An remembered that the Lord of Slaughter was the one who had brought this staff to this world. He hadn’t expected it to fall into Emperor Yao’s hands.

He found it even more surprising how easily Emperor Yao was handing it to him, especially since it harnessed the power of the Human Emperor. Becoming the Human Emperor was no easy feat; one needed to be acknowledged by the vast majority of the human race. Only Emperor Yao had the means to pull it off in this era. 

“Do you know what I’m going to do?” Zu An was intrigued.

“I have a rough idea. Didn’t Zhuanxu share a sliver of his Celestial Court’s power insignia with you as well? This is the best decision we can make for the future.” Emperor Yao looked toward the battlefield where the shamans and fiends clashed with a deep look.

Zu An suddenly felt a surge of respect for Emperor Yao. The latter wasn't just the shallow schemer he'd previously thought. Emperor Yao harbored great ambitions; it was just that their paths diverged.

“I’m apologetic about what happened to Chang’e. On second thought, I’ll give you another present as a token of my sincerity.” Emperor Yao waved his hand through the air, opening a spatial rift that quickly expanded into a warp portal. Starlight shimmered across the pitch-black portal.

“This…” Zu An was surprised to sense the Celestial Court’s aura inside the gate. 

“It’s a trump card I prepared back then. This portal allows me to travel between earth and heaven without passing through the South Celestial Gate. However, the severance of heaven and earth has shaken this portal too. I can only maintain it for a day.” Emperor Yao smiled. “You should need it right now.”

Zu An had to concede that Emperor Yao understood human emotions well. “Thank you.”

Emperor Yao glanced at the Lord of Slaughter, saying, “I’ll leave this to you.”

With that, he turned around and returned to the city, where he was met with the human populace’s resounding cheers. 

Zu An was awed by Emperor Yao’s popularity. He glanced at Salamay’s corpse and felt wistful. Who could have known that she would meet such an end?

Considering that they were at least acquainted, he dug a simple grave for her on a distant mountain. He considered erecting a tombstone for her, but he didn’t know what to inscribe on it. Looking at the mound before him, he thought about everything she had done and sighed. “You didn’t have to put yourself through all this.”

Yun Yuqing murmured, “Big brother Zu laments whenever a beauty dies.”

Ji Xiaoxi shook her head. “Big brother Zu has always been kind-hearted.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes flickered, but it was hard to tell what she was thinking. 

After burying Salamay, Zu An stowed the Lord of Slaughter in his Seven-Colored Divine Halo. Following that, he turned to Xie Daoyun and said, “You’ll have to seal him away when we return to Yumen Beiqing’s spacetime fragment to close this loop.”

Xie Daoyun nodded. She had already deciphered the sealing formation; it was just that she hadn’t found a way to subdue the Lord of Slaughter thus far. 

He then turned to the group and said, “I’ll send you back to Wu Mountain first before making a trip to the Celestial Court.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain frowned. She looked at the portal with worried eyes. “Be careful. It might be a trap.”

“I can sense the Celestial Court’s aura coming from the other side of the portal, so there should be nothing wrong with the destination. I also have Wu Mountain’s coordinates, so I can return if things go awry.” Zu An was confident in his odds of survival given his current strength and grasp of space. 

That eased the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s worries. 

Yun Yuqing was perplexed. She had asked Wuhui about the Goddess of Wu Mountain after their previous encounter, and Wuhui had told him that the Goddess of Wu Mountain was a cold and reclusive person. And yet, the Goddess of Wu Mountain allowed Zu An to freely enter and leave Wu Mountain as if it were his home. 

How far have the two of them progressed?!

Zu An gently lifted the ladies with a wave of his hand before flashing back to the Wu Mountain via Rainbow Morph.

“Be careful,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain warned him. It wouldn’t be convenient for her to tag along, since he was there to look for Chang’e. The other ladies thought the same too.

However, Zu An turned to Yun Yuqing and said, “Wuhui mentioned you have the purest Celestial Court’s Fiend Bloodline. You shouldn’t miss this fortuitous encounter. I’ll bring you with me.”

Yun Yuqing was taken aback, but she happily agreed to it. Zu An’s lovers were so outstanding that it heaped a lot of pressure on her. There was no way she would miss a chance to become stronger. This would allow her to better share some of Zu An’s burdens in the future. 

But most important of all, this allowed her to have some private time with Zu An. 

Zu An brought Yun Yuqing back to Taoqiu’s outskirts using Rainbow Morph. 

Yun Yuqing was amazed. “Big brother Zu, you’re formidable! To think that you can cover such a huge distance in the blink of an eye.” What she enjoyed more was monopolizing big brother Zu’s hands, however.

“I was only able to return so quickly because I recorded the coordinates to this place,” Zu An explained with a smile. He confirmed the safety of Emperor Yao’s portal once more before stepping in with Yun Yuqing.

The space around them distorted. As soon as Zu An stepped out of the portal, he was faced with the celestial palace. He breathed a sigh of relief, knowing that the portal had worked. 

A divine light suddenly surged out of the celestial palace, and Zhuanxu’s silhouette appeared behind the two of them. The latter was surprised to see Zu An.

Zu An was surprised too. “I only just arrived, but you've already found me?”

“I closed all the passageways leading to the Celestial Court so that the battle wouldn’t reach here. Your portal would have been hard to detect under normal circumstances, but it is exceptionally conspicuous now.” Zhuanxu was perplexed too. “How did you get here?”

Zu An made no attempt to conceal the truth for Emperor Yao. The latter was too sly. It wouldn’t be that bad for the Celestial Emperor to keep an eye on him.

“No wonder I kept sensing the traces of another enigmatic entity. So he’s the one.” Zhuanxu chuckled. “But he has helped me a fair bit this time.” 

Zu An was taken aback. Did Emperor Yao open this portal for me as a message to Zhuanxu? Now that I think about it, they have a common enemy—Emperor Jun—and an enemy’s enemy is a friend. 

It feels as if Emperor Yao had everything calculated, but I bet he didn’t expect to be sealed away by Shun in the future, while Shun is none other than Zhuanxu’s descendant. 

But that was a fight between the Celestial Emperor and the Human Emperor; Zu An couldn’t be bothered to get involved. He had more important things at hand, so he told Zhuanxu about what had happened to the Mermaid Queen. 

Zhuanxu did a quick calculation with his fingers. “I see… I’m afraid you will be disappointed. You won’t be able to take her away with you.”

“Why?!” Zu An was stunned. “You are the Celestial Emperor. Can’t you at least do this much?”

Zhuanxu frowned. “The Celestial Emperor has to obey the rules of nature too. Chang’e’s ascension is inscribed in fate. The Frost Palace carries an ancient curse: anyone who moves into the Frost Palace is destined to be lonely. Now that Chang’e has become one with the Frost Palace, separating her from it would only bring about her destruction. But this isn’t necessarily a bad thing. She’ll cultivate faster than anyone else in the Frost Palace…”

Zu An interjected, “What’s the point of having unparalleled cultivation when the tradeoff is a life of loneliness? Is there no solution?”

Zhuanxu briefly paused before replying, “It might be possible to do something about the Frost Palace’s curse ten thousand years from now…”

“Ten thousand years…” The thought that the Mermaid Queen had to suffer ten thousand years of loneliness made his heart lurch. 

“It might not be a short time, but it isn’t a long time for a deity either,” Zhuanxu consoled him. 

“Is the ten thousand years counted by the Celestial Court’s time or mortal time?” Zu An asked.

“Of course, it’s calculated by the Celestial Court’s time.”

Zu An’s heart sank. A day in heaven was equivalent to a year on earth. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2601: Fulfilling Her Wish
chapters.body:
Chapter 2601: Fulfilling Her Wish

Zhuanxu smiled. “You don’t have to be so pessimistic. She might not be able to leave the Frost Palace, but you can drop by often to visit her. Once the war settles down and order is re-stablished, it won’t be as difficult for you to enter the Celestial Court.” 

Zu An fell silent. He knew that the war between the Shamans and the Fiends would cause both races to retreat into the shadows. On top of that, Zhuanxu wouldn't necessarily be the one calling the shots in the Celestial Court by then. 

Besides that, he would eventually have to leave this world. There was another world waiting to be rescued, and he also needed to complete the Six Paths of Reincarnation.

Once he left this world, the Mermaid Queen would have to spend a long time alone. Who knew whether she could endure it until their reunion?

Just then, Zhuanxu turned to Yun Yuqing and remarked, “Ah, you must be the lady Wuhui mentioned. Indeed, you have the purest Celestial Emperor bloodline. It’s a pity that you have yet to awaken your bloodline power. Child, follow me. I’ll help you awaken your bloodline.”

Yun Yuqing glanced at Zu An.

Noticing their reservation, Zhuanxu smiled. “You can say that she’s my descendant in a way. I have no reason to harm her. Awakening her bloodline power requires the invocation of ancient rituals…” 

Zu An finally nodded. “Yuqing, I’ll look for you later.”

At the same time, he shot her a glance. Yun Yuqing nodded. He had given her a teleportation talisman; she could use it if she felt that she was in danger, and he would rush over. 

Zhuanxu lifted Yun Yuqing onto a cloud, and the two of them flew off into the distance. 

Zu An quickly made his way toward the Frost Palace. The further he flew, the colder the resplendent immortal palaces became. Even the air was no longer as sweet; it was tinged with cold as well.

A forlorn palace floated in the distance, as if signaling the world’s edge. The words on the plaque, ‘Frost Palace’, made Zu An frown. Just looking at these characters made him feel empty and cold. 

He gently pushed the door open. The courtyard was barren except for a withered, ancient tree, a stark contrast to the other vibrant immortal palaces that cultivated flowers and herbs. 

Zu An was perplexed. Didn’t the Frost Palace in the legends have an osmanthus tree? Could this be the osmanthus tree? But it's already withered!

Creak!

The door in front of him suddenly opened. A beautiful figure stood in the doorway, staring at him in disbelief. 

“Big brother Zu?”

“Suyin!”

Even though it hadn’t been that long since they parted ways, the Mermaid Queen looked much frailer. Her eyes were red and puffy. She had to have cried a lot over the last few days. 

Zu An's familiar voice made her eyes light up with joy. Unable to hold herself back anymore, she leaped into Zu An’s arms and embraced him tightly. “It really is you. It really is you…”

Her arms were wrapped around him so tightly that it hurt a little, but that only made Zu An’s heart hurt more. She'd no doubt suffered much turmoil. Zu An said, “Don’t worry, I'm here now…”

Pearls fell to the ground with crisp clacks. Zu An felt the Mermaid Queen's tears drenching his chest. He gently patted her shoulder as he said, “I know what happened. Before coming here, I killed Feng Meng to avenge you.”

“I don’t want vengeance. I just want to be with you,” the Mermaid Queen whimpered.

Zu An had never seen her so frail. She was a strong-willed woman who had mesmerized an entire world’s experts. She was mature and graceful. And yet, she was now acting like a child. This incident had undoubtedly frightened her. He recalled what Zhuanxu said earlier, and that made him feel even more awful. 

The Mermaid Queen cried for a long time before gradually drifting off to sleep. Looking at her sleeping visage, Zu An gently wiped her tears and tidied up her unkempt hair. She probably hasn’t slept a wink since coming here. 

He carefully carried her into the nearby room, which only contained the bare necessities—a table, a chair, and a bed. He noticed that the bed was made out of ice and frowned. He couldn’t bear to lay her on it, so he sat on the bed himself and laid her down in his embrace. 

This movement, albeit gentle, caused the Mermaid Queen to jolt awake. She anxiously clutched onto Zu An’s sleeves as she exclaimed, “Don’t leave me!”

Zu An’s heart ached. He patted her head and said, “I’m here, I’m here.”

The Mermaid Queen exhaled in relief. She took a few seconds to compose herself before replying, “I’m sorry. I made a fool of myself.” Even though she had only rested briefly, she was in a much better condition than before.

“I doubt I would have fared any better than you if I were placed in the same position,” Zu An replied with a bitter smile. 

“Nonsense. You’re the most incredible person I know. You always overcome the hurdles standing in your way, no matter how tall they may be. I love how you remain composed no matter how hopeless the situation looks.” The Mermaid Queen hooked her hands around Zu An's neck. There were no more tears in her eyes; only tenderness remained. 

“I'm not that incredible…” Zu An replied. Just like how I have no way of rescuing you from the Frost Palace now. Unwilling to resign himself to fate, he stood up and said, “I’ll take you out of here!”

He carried the Mermaid Queen up and flew her to the South Celestial Gate. He kept a close eye on her condition and was delighted to see her safe and sound. Who in the world said she can’t leave the Frost Palace?

The Mermaid Queen guessed his thoughts and bitterly explained, “It’s futile. I’ve tried it. I can leave the Frost Palace temporarily, but I’ll find myself suddenly reappearing there shortly after.”

She vanished right after saying that. Startled, Zu An rushed back to the Frost Palace and saw her standing by the doorway, waiting for him. When she saw him, her worry faded to joy. 

“There’s something amiss about this Frost Palace.” Zu An drew his Tai’e Sword and slashed the palace door. With his current cultivation and the Tai’e Sword’s prowess, he could even slice a mountain, let alone a mere palace door. 

Much to his astonishment, his slash failed to slice anything off the door. Not only that; divine runes also started lighting up around the Frost Palace and a terrifying aura began to build up.

Zu An was stunned. These divine runes resembled those he had seen from the sacred tree Jianmu.

The Mermaid Queen immediately stood in front of him and protected him with both hands. “Stand down! He’s my man!”

The divine runes slowly extinguished as if they understood her words. 

Zu An blinked in confusion.

The Mermaid Queen explained with a bitter smile, “After it acknowledged me as its master, I have become one with the Frost Palace. I am now the Frost Palace, and the Frost Palace is me.” 

Zu An had been wondering whether to draw his Sun Slaying Bow to give it another try, but after hearing her words, he had no choice but to give up on that plan. 

“Big brother Zu, you don’t need to worry about me. I understood everything from the moment the Frost Palace acknowledged me as its master. There is no way for me to leave this place.” The Mermaid Queen grabbed his hand and consoled him. 

Zu An fell silent. If she'd truly come to terms with her plight, she wouldn’t have cried earlier.

“Big brother Zu, I have an unfulfilled wish. Can you help me with that?” The Mermaid Queen looked at Zu An with glistening eyes reminiscent of the evening sky’s afterglow.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2602: A Day In Heaven, A Year On Earth
chapters.body:
Chapter 2602: A Day In Heaven, A Year On Earth

“What’s your wish?” Since he couldn’t save her right away, Zu An solemnly swore to fulfill her wish no matter what it was. 

The Mermaid Queen blushed, but she determinedly raised her head and looked at him with her beautiful eyes. “We have gotten married in the Chang clan, but we didn’t finish the final step.”

Zu An’s heart quivered. They had gone through all the marriage formalities that day. The only step they had stopped short of was the consummation of their marriage. He was still wondering what kind of unfulfilled wish she had, but who could have thought this would be it?

The Mermaid Queen squirmed in embarrassment. “Big brother Zu, am I shameless for asking…”

Zu An sealed her lips before she could finish her words. Her body initially stiffened up, but it soon fell limp in her lover’s embrace. 

It was a long time later before their lips parted. By then, her fair complexion had already become completely red. 

With a wave of her hand, ‘Blissful Marriage (囍)’ papers were plastered on the walls and windows. Red lanterns hung on the doors of the Frost Palace. The icy-cold bed was covered in a thick couple quilt. Red drapes hung over the previously monotonous bed. The once-cold room suddenly looked merry.

Zu An was amazed. After becoming an immortal, he had learned how to conjure illusions like turning stones into gold, but he could sense that the changes around him weren’t just an illusion. These additions might have appeared out of nowhere, but they were real. 

The Mermaid Queen shyly said, “I can now control everything inside the Frost Palace. Do you like the decorations I have put up?”

“I love it!” Zu An embraced her tightly. All these things showed how important this was to her. It was, after all, a woman’s most important day. How could it not be important to her?

He then took out all the jeweled accessories he had stashed away in his Brilliant Glass Bead and put them on her. 

“Aiyo, that’s enough! I can hardly lift my head now,” the Mermaid Queen coyly exclaimed. Zu An’s haphazard actions had resulted in her head being covered in accessories. 

Looking at the mess on her head, Zu An couldn’t help but laugh. “You’re already beautiful as it is. It feels as if I've done something extraneous.”

He removed the accessories he'd placed on the Mermaid Queen before taking out a red cloth and gently placing it over her head in their place.

The Mermaid Queen’s heart quivered. She felt inexplicably nervous. Having her eyes once again covered in red reminded her of the night of her marriage at the Chang clan. She had also nervously waited for him to arrive like that, only to be met with an unfortunate outcome. 

Will the same thing happen this time too?

The sight before her suddenly lit up. The red cloth had been lifted.

Zu An was dazzled by the beauty before his eyes. The Mermaid Queen was already gorgeous normally, and the redness reflected on her snowy skin only further accentuated her charms. Zu An exclaimed, “Suyin, you’re truly beautiful!”

“Are you still calling me Suyin now…” The Mermaid Queen pouted.

“My wife!”

“Hubby~”

Her exclamation of ‘hubby’ was so sweet and tender that Zu An nearly melted. He quickly grabbed two wine cups and filled them to the brim. Noticing the Mermaid Queen’s confused gaze, he explained, “This is a human tradition—cross-cupped wine. Doing it on the night of consummation signifies two individuals coming together as one.”

“We have to do it then!” The Mermaid Queen’s eyes lit up. She grabbed a wine cup and crossed her hand with Zu An’s, her gaze expectant.

Her overflowing excitement made Zu An’s heart ache. He quickly did the same too. 

After downing their cross-cupped wine, the Mermaid Queen asked with a reddened face, “What do we do next?”

Zu An chuckled. “It’s now time to get down to business.”

The sudden approach of Zu An’s face caused a mini explosion in the Mermaid Queen’s head. She felt as if she was heating up from head to toe.

… 

Ji Xiaoxi marveled at the rare flowers and herbs growing on Wu Mountain’s peak and remarked, “Goddess, it’s so beautiful here!”

The usually cold Goddess of Wu Mountain revealed a faint smile at her adorable gestures. “Don’t call me goddess. Call me Yaoji instead.”

“All right, big sister Yaoji.” Because of her striking resemblance to Pei Mianman, Ji Xiaoxi instinctively felt close to the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

Just then, Xie Daoyun walked over with a vibrant flower. “Xiaoxi, this is the medicine you wanted from the cliff.”

“Thank you, big sister Xie.” Ji Xiaoxi took the flower with glowing eyes. 

Xie Daoyun and the Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled.

“Miss Yaoji, it's already been a month. Why isn’t big brother Zu back yet? Is he in danger?” Xie Daoyun asked worriedly. “Didn’t that man say that the teleportation formation would only last for a day?” 

“With his abilities, he should be able to deal with any danger that comes his way.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked at the sky. “Emperor Yao said the teleportation formation only lasts a day, but a day in heaven equals a year on earth. It’s only normal for him not to be back yet.”

“Ah?” Ji Xiaoxi and Xie Daoyun were astounded. While big brother Zu spends a day in heaven, we have to wait a year for him down here?

“It’ll be a year in no time. Years would pass in a flash when I admired the fog from the mountain peak,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said, though she was surprised to find that time seemed to pass slower now, even though she should have already grown accustomed to it. 

Ji Xiaoxi and Xie Daoyun exchanged looks and stuck out their tongues. Immortals have a scary sense of time. 

As the Goddess of Wu Mountain fell into a melancholic mood, the surroundings seemed to become a little heavy. Xie Daoyun decided to break the mood, asking, “Xiaoxi, why were you looking for this flower?” 

“This flower is called Beauty Blood. I've only seen it in books; I didn’t expect to find it on big sister Yaoji’s Wu Mountain,” Ji Xiaoxi explained as she tore out the flower’s bright-red petals one by one, revealing a pristine jade-like fruit within. 

“How beautiful!” Xie Daoyun widened her eyes. 

The fruit inside was at least three shades fairer than her, and it had a form reminiscent of a beauty. 

“Is it called Beauty Blood because of the flower petals’ blood-red color?”

“Both its petals and fruit can be used as medicine, but its most valuable part is still this.” Ji Xiaoxi gently pressed her fingernail against the white fruit, and a blood-red fluid flowed out from within. 

“How mysterious!” Xie Daoyun remarked, but right after, she suddenly thought about something that caused her face to redden. Tsk, this flower is so inappropriate!

When she looked at the innocent Ji Xiaoxi and unfazed Goddess of Wu Mountain, however, she suddenly realized that she was the only dirty-minded one. But again, I’m the only one who is experienced among the three of us. That brought a smile to her lips, and she felt a slight sense of superiority. 

Just then, the Goddess of Wu Mountain said, “You can maximize Beauty Blood’s medicinal effect if you grind the petals and fruit together.”

“Big sister, thank you for your pointer.” Ji Xiaoxi brought out her mortar and pestle to grind the petals and fruit. However, the petals and fruits remained distinct even as her forehead became slick with sweat.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain flicked her finger. A fog condensed into a droplet of water, which fell into the mortar. The petals and fruit softened up, allowing them to be ground into a bright red paste. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2603: The Solution To Saving Her
chapters.body:
Chapter 2603: The Solution To Saving Her

Xie Daoyun wiped away Ji Xiaoxi’s sweat before looking at the celestial palace. “I wonder how big brother Zu is faring.”

“He and Miss Chang’e must be cherishing their final moments together.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain let out a melancholy sigh as she thought about Chang’e’s plight, and by extension, her relationship with Zu An as well.

In a way, I'm in the same position as her. 

Xie Daoyun sighed as well. “I hope their last moments together are happy.”

Just a moment ago, she'd been feeling impatient about having to wait a year, but the realization that Zu An and Mermaid Queen only had a day with each other made her feel sorry for them. 

“I wonder what they're doing.” Ji Xiaoxi continued grinding the medicine while looking at the sky with curious eyes.

… 

The Mermaid Queen felt as if she was being battered by a heavy storm.

As a member of the Ocean races, she was no stranger to storms. She'd even enjoyed diving into storms and challenging rapid tides in her younger years. No matter how intense the storm was, she could always conquer it with ease. 

It was only now that she realized she had gotten ahead of herself. It turned out that there were storms in the world that were beyond her means to handle. She felt like a lone leaf floating amid choppy waters, at risk of drowning at any moment. 

Thinking back on how she'd maneuvered through rapid currents back then, she tried her best to relax her body as much as possible and move along with the storm instead of opposing it.

Fortunately, heaven seemed to have detected her fear, and the wild storm died down. The sun emerged through the clouds, and a refreshing drizzle ensued. Basking under the warm sunlight felt exceptionally comfortable. 

Unable to curb her emotions anymore, she tightly embraced her lover and bit his ear. “I want a child…”

Those words were as good as a war horn on the battlefield, especially when coming from a top-tier beauty. Which man could resist such a passionate confession from a woman like her?

The swaying red lanterns outside the Frost Palace seemed to say it all. The withered tree in the courtyard suddenly sprouted to life. Green leaves grew from its barren branches, and beautiful flowers blossomed. The sweet fragrance of osmanthus flowers drifted through the Frost Palace, as if reflecting the cloying sentiments between the newlyweds. 

… 

Some time passed. The Mermaid Queen lay on Zu An’s chest with bright-red cheeks. She reflexively twirled her hair against his chest as she said, “Hubby, you seem unhappy.”

“Ah? Do I?” Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected her to be so sharp as to sense that.

“Yes, something seems to be on your mind. Are you worried about me?” the Mermaid Queen asked as she adjusted into a more comfortable position in his arms. 

Zu An didn’t know how to answer that question.

The Mermaid Queen smiled. “You don’t need to feel so conflicted. I know I can’t leave this place. After being able to spend this night with you, I have no other regrets. Who knows? I might even have your child now. If so, I'll have someone to accompany me here.”

She suddenly stiffened up and hurriedly corrected herself. “No, that would be too selfish. It would be unfair to keep our child here with me.”

Zu An held her even more tightly. “My wife, I’ll rescue you from here.”

The Mermaid Queen chuckled. “Hubby, you don’t need to feel pressured. I’ll be here anyway. You can visit me whenever you miss me.”

Zu An felt a sharp pain in his chest. They had to be at least more than ten thousand years away from the era he lived in. She would be all alone in the meantime.

“I’ll stay here with you,” he suddenly said. It would be too cruel to leave her here alone. 

The Mermaid Queen was taken aback before bursting into laughter. She was laughing out of joy, though; she was so happy that tears flowed uncontrollably from her eyes. She pressed her cheek against Zu An's chest and said, “Hubby, I’m happy to hear those words. It makes me happy to know that you've thought about that. But I can't be that selfish. You're shouldering too many responsibilities, and there are many things you need to do. I can’t keep you here with me.”

Zu An opened his mouth. He usually had a glib tongue, yet his throat suddenly felt so dry. 

“Don’t worry. I’ll wait. I’ll wait till the day you come for me, no matter how long it takes.” The Mermaid Queen suddenly straightened her posture. A lock of her hair fell on his cheek. “Hubby, let’s not discuss such unhappy things. Let’s talk about something more light-hearted. Why don’t you tell me about your experiences before our meeting? I want to learn more about you.”

Zu An wrapped his arms around her tightly and shared his history with her. 

A day could sometimes be so long, yet it could also be so short. 

The Mermaid Queen nudged Zu An. “Hubby, it’s almost time.”

She'd learned about the warp portal from what Zu An shared with her. She tried her best to maintain a smile, but her eyes couldn’t help but tear up. The thought of separating from Zu An filled her with misery. 

Zu An suddenly thought about something and summoned Mo Xi. The Mermaid Queen was startled by the sudden appearance of a curvaceous woman dressed in skimpy clothes. 

“She’s Mo Xi…” Zu An roughly shared Mo Xi’s background. “Her ability, ‘Farewell, Nanchao’, allows her to sever any contract in the world. Your relationship with the Frost Palace can be considered a contract, but the problem is that she can only sever contracts that are of her level.”

The Mermaid Queen was disappointed. The lady before her could only be considered a slightly stronger human; it was unlikely that Mo Xi could grow to her level anytime soon.

Zu An had also figured as much. The gap between the two ladies was so huge that it was unlikely that Mo Xi could catch up with the ascended Mermaid Queen even if she was given years to cultivate. 

All of a sudden, however, an idea popped into his head. He asked Mo Xi if she could stay and cultivate with the Mermaid Queen. This would give her plenty of time to grow. This was an era rich with natural ki, and the countless powerful celestials and shamans meant that there could be fortuitous encounters at every corner. Staying here could greatly accelerate Mo Xi’s growth. 

Mo Xi looked away in response, but that lifted Zu An’s mood. Judging from her response, he could tell that it was possible; it was just that she was reluctant to do so. 

“What do I have to do to convince you? Do you want more Ki Fruits?” Zu An had accumulated 3,039,680 Rage points thus far, so he could redeem a lot of Ki Fruits. 

Mo Xi shook her head. Zu An communed with her soul and sensed her intention, but that only made him frown in bewilderment. What she wanted wasn’t Ki Fruits, but something else, and it was an item that he could have never imagined. 

She wanted a costume from his Rage System’s shop, one that provided no benefits whatsoever. It was the costume that had startled Zu An back then—the bikini!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2604: Mutation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2604: Mutation

The Keyboard System’s shop did sell beautiful costumes, but they provided no combat benefits or special effects, and on top of that, the prices were exorbitant. Zu An remembered complaining that only someone out of their mind would spend so many Rage points to buy a bunch of rags. 

He'd been moved by the bikini though. He'd thought that the price could be worth it if he could get a valkyrie to wear it. But that was merely a fleeting thought. The series of events that happened afterward resulted in him forgetting all about it. 

He'd never thought that all of a sudden, Mo Xi would make this request!

He eyed the bikini’s price—2,999,999 Rage points. 

Those damned capitalists are the same everywhere. It’s three million, but they still want to play mind games to make you think you're only going to spend two million-ish. 

“Are you certain that’s what you want?” Zu An sought confirmation, as the situation felt nothing short of ridiculous.

Mo Xi nodded. A rare hint of desire shimmered in her eyes.

“You must work hard to raise your cultivation and sever her contract afterward,” Zu An added worriedly.

Mo Xi nodded impatiently in response. Not daring to dawdle any longer, Zu An quickly bought the bikini.

When she saw the little strips of cloth Zu An held in his hand, the Mermaid Queen blushed. The Ocean races were open when it came to clothing, with many opting for revealing swimming clothes. Even so, she had never seen such skimpy clothing before. 

Overjoyed, Mo Xi snatched the bikini.

For a moment, Zu An was worried that Mo Xi would start changing her clothes on the spot. While that thought sparked his anticipation, the mood didn’t seem right for that. 

In the end, however, Mo Xi merely turned on the spot, and her super short skirt was immediately swapped with the bikini.

Zu An’s nose almost spurted blood on the spot. This woman doesn’t even try to hide it at all! But he had to admit that her figure was out of this world. Her healthy brown skin tone gave her an air of primal wildness and vibrance. 

Even the Mermaid Queen couldn’t help but remark, “Little sister Mo Xi has an incredible figure.”

Mo Xi turned to Zu An with expectant eyes.

Zu An understood her intention and complimented her. “It looks great, but you shouldn’t wear it outside. It’d feel as if other men were taking advantage of you if they saw you in it.”

His worried expression brought amusement to Mo Xi’s eyes, but she still accepted his suggestion. Her expression seemed to say, “Don’t worry, I’ll wear it for you alone.”

Zu An asked her to use ‘Farewell, Nanchao’ to sever her contract with him so she could stay with the Mermaid Queen, but she turned down his suggestion, saying that she could stay here without severing the contract. 

Zu An was surprised. These valkyries are incredible, to be able to exist independently in a distant spacetime far away from their hosts. Have I underestimated their potential? Was I too focused on my growth, so I neglected them?

“Thank you, hubby.” The Mermaid Queen’s eyes regained their usual spirit. 

Hope was always the most important thing in this world. Mo Xi was currently still weak, but her existence brought hope to the Mermaid Queen. Even if she couldn’t sever the contract in the end, her company would still make the days in the Frost Palace more bearable.

Zu An grabbed her hand and said, “I’ll be back.”

“Mm~” The Mermaid Queen leaped into his arms and embraced him tightly.

The two of them hugged for a while, cherishing their final moments together. 

“Big brother Zu, it’s time to go,” Yun Yuqing’s voice suddenly echoed. She had flown over from the distant celestial palace. Her disposition felt sharper than before, as if she had gained greater confidence. 

She quickly noticed the bikini-wearing Mo Xi and was taken aback. Hold on! What’s with this sexy woman wearing such bold clothes?

Zu An also sensed the flickering of the distant warp portal and understood that it was on the verge of disappearing, but he stared yearningly at the Mermaid Queen. 

The Mermaid Queen walked them out to the Frost Palace’s entrance. Her hands were so tightly locked with Zu An’s that their palms turned pale. It was as if they were inscribing each other into their bodies. 

Separation was usually signified by the setting sun, but why did the warm rising sun suddenly feel so sorrowful too? The Frost Palace was shrouded in a white fog that seemed to represent indissipable melancholy.

“Big brother Zu, we have to leave now, or else we won’t make it in time.” Yun Yuqing didn’t want to be the villain in this situation, but she had no choice but to remind him. The warp portal was flickering with increasing intensity, and its light was dimming. It looked as if it would vanish at any moment now. 

The Mermaid Queen leaned in and passionately kissed Zu An. Unable to suppress her emotions anymore, her tears fell to the ground, making crisp sounds resembling pearls striking a jade plate. These sounds echoed endlessly in Zu An’s heart. 

Yun Yuqing lifted her eyebrows. She felt as if she should be angry at this situation, but she could sympathize with the persistent melancholy lingering in the air. 

Zu An opened his mouth, but the countless words in his mind were jammed in his throat. In the end, they manifested as a string of meaningless, quivering noises. 

“Go on. I’ll keep waiting for you.” Suddenly, the Mermaid Queen gently but determinedly pushed Zu An toward the warp portal. 

“I’ll be back for you!” Zu An was finally able to release some of the stifled feeling in his chest. 

“Mm!” The Mermaid Queen tearfully waved Zu An goodbye. The wind tugging at her robe made her appear even more frail.

As her voice slowly grew more distant, Zu An finally came to understand what it meant for the ‘wind to sting his eyes’.

Yun Yuqing dragged him into the warp portal, and the two of them returned to Taoqiu’s outer perimeter. The portal vanished practically as soon as they stepped out of it. Had they been a second slower, they would have been trapped in the Celestial World for good. 

The Fiends and the Shamans were the ruling races of heaven and earth. A clash between entities of their might would take a long time to conclude. This battle could easily last for tens of thousands of years. A war marking the end of an era was bound to be anything but simple.

“Big brother Zu…” Yun Yuqing didn’t know how to console Zu An. Words fell flat here. 

Zu An rubbed his cheeks. By the time he stood up once more, he had already reverted to normal. “Don’t worry about me. At least there’s a sliver of hope. I’ll find her one day. On another note, have you awakened your bloodline?”

Yun Yuqing felt conflicted. She would have been less worried if he'd simply burst into tears and vented his emotions. If a day when he could no longer maintain his strong front ever came, he would only end up in greater pain. 

But she couldn’t open his heart, so she could only reply, “Indeed. I seem to be Zhuanxu’s descendant. Our bloodline should have grown thinner with each generation, but it suddenly mutated when it came to me and became purer. My bloodline is closer to the founding Green Emperor than Zhuanxu’s.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2605: Perplexed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2605: Perplexed

“That's wonderful news!” Zu An was happy for her. “But why isn’t there any exceptionally huge advancement in your cultivation?”

He had examined Yun Yuqing using his aura and confirmed that her cultivation had advanced from Grandmaster’s Ascension Mortal Form to Earth Immortal’s Heaven Soul. The feat of raising her cultivation by two stages in an instant would have caused a huge stir in their previous Cultivation World.

But this was the Celestial World. Yun Yuqing had the purest Celestial Emperor Bloodline, and she'd even had Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu personally helping her to awaken her bloodline. Considering these factors, her growth felt rather lackluster. 

Yun Yuqing tidied up Zu An’s unkempt clothes as she answered, “Zhuanxu told me that my cultivation is too low, and if my cultivation suddenly advanced too much within a short time, my body wouldn’t be able to take it. So, he only awakened my bloodline. He told me to naturally raise my cultivation through training so I would have a stronger foundation.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Zhuanxu’s explanation made sense. He asked, “What power does the Celestial Emperor bloodline hold?”

Yun Yuqing replied uncertainly, “I’m not sure either. Zhuanxu told me that once my cultivation reaches a certain level, the secrets of the world will naturally unravel. Right now, my bloodline allows me to cultivate a hundred times more efficiently compared to before.”

Zu An was stunned. Putting aside the Celestial Emperor bloodline’s other abilities, just the boost in Yun Yuqing’s cultivation speed was already out of this world. He asked, “How did you awaken your bloodline?” 

That was what the True Demon race had craved back then, but their efforts ended in futility. 

Zu An had learned from Emperor Yao that when the Lord of Slaughter and Salamay escaped, the elders of the True Demon race had escaped with them. The Lord of Slaughter had tried to recruit them, but the True Demon race’s elders valued the awakening of their bloodlines more. They'd agreed to serve the Lord of Slaughter, only to find an excuse to flee later so they could find a way to awaken their bloodlines.

By sheer coincidence, they'd then bumped into Emperor Jun. Emperor Jun could have helped them awaken their bloodlines, but back then, he needed living souls for the forging of his Myriad Souls Banner. A tragic fate had thus befallen the True Demon race’s elders as a result. 

“Zhuanxu brought me to a garden in the Celestial Court. That appears to be a place where generations of Celestial Emperors are worshiped…” Yun Yuqing shared what she'd seen along the way. Even Zu An was awed. 

Chatting like that, they arrived at Wu Mountain before they knew it. On the mountain peak, Ji Xiaoxi was grinding her medicine, Suolun Shi was practising her swordsmanship, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain was standing in a daze by the mountain cliff. Meanwhile, Xie Daoyun quietly drew the scene by the windowsill. 

There were several people on the mountain peak, but only the mountain wind could be heard. Everything was stiflingly quiet. 

Zu An was amazed. Other than the Goddess of Wu Mountain, the other ladies looked different from usual, both in their choice of clothes and make-up. On second thought, however, it wasn’t surprising for them to change their clothes and make-up every day.

Ji Xiaoxi spotted Zu An and shook her head. “Aiyo, I’m seeing things again.” It was as if she had seen this sight far too many times before.

“What do you mean, seeing things?” Zu An was perplexed.

Tok!

Ji Xiaoxi dropped her mortar, which shattered into several pieces. She looked up in disbelief. “Big brother Zu?”

The other women sensed the commotion and looked over. When they saw Zu An, they immediately rushed over.

Even the Goddess of Wu Mountain ran over, though she quickly remembered her usual demure self and hurriedly slowed her footsteps. Suolun Shi also slowed down, worried about her current appearance. 

Xie Daoyun didn’t harbor that many reservations, however. She ran the fastest despite being the furthest away.

But Ji Xiaoxi still beat her to the punch. She leaped into Zu An’s arms and began sobbing. 

Feeling her trembling body and seeing the tears brimming in the other ladies’ eyes, Zu An was taken aback. “It's only been a day. Why are you all so worked up?”

“What do you mean, a day? We've already spent a year waiting for you!” Ji Xiaoxi sobbed.

Realization suddenly dawned on Zu An. Ah, a day in heaven equals a year on earth!

He had heard that saying many times before, but his previous trips to the Celestial Court had been short ones, so the difference in time hadn’t registered strongly to him. This time, he'd only stayed in the Celestial Court for an entire day because he didn’t want to part ways with the Mermaid Queen. Only now did he realize that an entire year had passed on earth!

This left him feeling conscience-stricken. He turned to the ladies and said, “I’m sorry. I kept you waiting for so long.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “It’s fine. A year isn’t a long time to me, though the others might not be used to the time difference.”

She often spent her time standing in a daze on Wu Mountain. Even a decade or a century would feel nothing more than a snap of a finger to her. 

Xie Daoyun reined in her feelings and said, “Big brother Zu, we were worried about Emperor Yao tampering with the warp portal. We might have confronted him by now if not for big sister goddess telling us that a day in heaven equals a year on earth.”

As the other ladies walked over, Ji Xiaoxi hurriedly stepped aside, feeling too embarrassed to continue staying in Zu An’s arms. However, a joyful smile had appeared on her tearful face. 

“I failed to consider that and warn you beforehand.” Zu An couldn’t resist the urge to look at the sky. For there to be such an exaggerated time difference between heaven and earth, could the Celestial World be located in the vicinity of a black hole… 

“You have only been to the Celestial Court twice. It’s normal for you to overlook that,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain consoled him. If the Kaiming Six Shamans were here, they would surely wonder when the cold goddess had become so understanding. 

“Did you manage to see big sister Suyin?” Xie Daoyun looked around. “Why isn’t she with…”

Her words trailed off as she realized something, and she regretted blurting those words out. 

“I met her.” Zu An’s expression darkened at the mention of the Mermaid Queen.

“What happened?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain asked.

“She has become the Frost Palace’s master and can no longer leave its premises,” Zu An replied grimly.

“AH?!” The other ladies were shocked.

Suolun Shi asked, “Is it impossible to save her, even with your abilities?”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain explained, “I have heard legends about the Frost Palace. Its existence predates even the Celestial Court. No one knows about its background, but it is a known fact that the Frost Palace has a terrifying curse…”

Upon hearing her explanation, the other ladies turned pale. They couldn’t help but wonder how much despair they would have been in if they were in the Mermaid Queen’s position. This made them extremely sympathetic to her plight.

“The divine marks on the Frost Palace are a testimony to its extraordinary nature.” Zu An told them about how he had tried to destroy the Frost Palace, only to be nearly killed by its backlash. His story stunned the other ladies.

“Little sister Chang’e is too pitiful. I’ll visit her frequently at the Frost Palace once the path between heaven and earth is reestablished," the Goddess of Wu Mountain said. Even she, despite being the Flame Emperor’s daughter, couldn't do anything about the enigmatic Frost Palace.

The other ladies exchanged looks as they realized that among them, only the Goddess of Wu Mountain could do that. The rest of them weren’t from this world and couldn’t stay here in the long term. Even if they remained behind, it was unlikely that they could survive the long years. Only the Goddess of Wu Mountain was impervious to the erosion of time. 

“Thank you, goddess!” Zu An was overjoyed. With her company, the Mermaid Queen wouldn’t be so lonely. “There’s someone else accompanying Suyin in the Frost Palace right now…” 

He told the ladies about Mo Xi. 

The ladies were relieved to hear that. Just thinking about the Mermaid Queen’s plight made them feel suffocated with despair. They were glad to hear that there was a chance she could be freed from her plight. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was perplexed. “Farewell, Nanchao? Is there such a formidable ability in the world? Why have I never heard about it?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2606: The Final Tear
chapters.body:
Chapter 2606: The Final Tear

Zu An was stunned. He'd never considered that issue. Given its mysterious background, he'd always assumed that it was normal for the Keyboard System to have skills that others had never heard about. However, now that the Goddess of Wu Mountain had pointed out this issue, he couldn’t help but think deeper about it.

It wouldn’t be a stretch to say that this was the golden era of cultivation, with powerful shamans and celestials all over the world. Besides that, the Goddess of Wu Mountain was the Flame Emperor’s daughter. She wasn't as strong as the powerhouses of this era, but she had vast knowledge of the world. 

For someone as knowledgeable as her to have never heard of this ability, there could only be two possibilities: One, the ability didn’t exist in this world. Two, the ability hadn’t been created in this era. 

If it were the former, it would suggest that there were stronger worlds out there. Zu An was mentally prepared for that, but nevertheless, this was the legendary Celestial Court! The people he'd met thus far were mythological figures he had grown up hearing about. He couldn’t imagine what even more powerful entities would be like.

If it were the latter, it would mean that ‘Farewell, Nanchao’ would be created someday in the future. This experience of transmigrating into the past gave Zu An a better grasp of spacetime and historical events. Thus, he knew this ability could have been created through a similar process. 

Was I the one who created this skill in history?

He thought about it, and felt that he was the only one who would be motivated to create such an ability. That was a grim thought. To be honest, Zu An didn’t like the feeling of traversing into the past. The slightest misstep could spell disaster. If something similar to the Frost Palace scenario were to happen several more times, he didn’t know if his heart could take it.

“What’s wrong?” the Goddess of Wu Mountain asked worriedly. 

“I had a few bad conjectures.” Zu An snapped out of his thoughts. “Yaoji, thank you for taking care of them.”

“You don’t need to thank me for this. I’m used to being alone. Their company soothed some of my loneliness.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. Under the rays of the morning sun, she looked so beautiful that even the other ladies were mesmerized.

Xie Daoyun thought it was unfair. Pei Mianman is already gorgeous as she is. Not only does the Goddess of Wu Mountain look identical to her, she even has Chu Chuyan’s ethereal disposition. How can anyone compete with that?

Zu An was about to say something when he suddenly sensed something. He quickly took out a box. It was the Nature Jade Case that had brought them to this world. The box was shaking nonstop, and its top was emanating a white light. The surrounding spatial ki was whirring to life. 

Xie Daoyun’s eyes lit up. “We can return now!”

There was no place like home!

The other ladies were excited too. After being away for so long, they couldn’t wait to return home. Only the Goddess of Wu Mountain was stunned. However, she was so used to concealing her emotions that the others didn’t notice her unusual mood.

“Oh no!” Ji Xiaoxi suddenly exclaimed. “We haven’t found my little aunt yet!”

Zu An replied, “I asked Celestial Emperor Zhuanxu. He isn’t sure who took big sister Jiang away, but that person appears to be of a high stature. She should be safe for the time being.”

He suddenly remembered the mysterious entity Qiu Honglei had summoned back then. I wonder if the person who took Jiang Luofu away is stronger than that. 

The answer eased some of Ji Xiaoxi’s worry, but she remained in low spirits, as there was no way of knowing whether her little aunt was truly safe or not. It also dampened the other ladies’ mood. They had come here together, only to still have several people missing even now that they were about to return. On top of that, the Mermaid Queen was still trapped in the Frost Palace…

Zu An felt distressed. He didn’t want to return so quickly, as there were still many secrets in this world waiting to be uncovered. In particular, he wanted to pay a visit to the Houtu of this era, as there were many questions he wanted to ask her. 

But the Nature Jade was already reacting. If he missed this chance, there was no telling when it would next react, or whether it would even react at all. He didn’t dare to make that gamble, especially not when he had his companions with him.

“But we haven’t completed Xiaoxi’s Defying Spirit Tears,” Xie Daoyun suddenly pointed out.

They had been helping Ji Xiaoxi collect all kinds of herbs in the past year, so they understood that the Defying Spirit Tears were still lacking something. Even Ji Xiaoxi, despite her medical prowess, had no idea how to complete them. 

Zu An’s expression darkened. The ingredients to treat Ji Xiaoxi were extremely rare, and most of them could only be found in this world. If they left now, perhaps they could never gather all the ingredients. 

They had everything else they needed; the only thing they were lacking was the Defying Spirit Tears. If they missed this chance, the regret would be unbearable.

“Don’t worry, I have found the missing part,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said.

“Really?” Zu An was overjoyed.

The other ladies were skeptical. We spent a year together. Why has she never mentioned this to us before?

“Of course. I wouldn’t lie about this,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain replied with a smile. “May I talk to you in private?”

The other ladies tactfully excused themselves to give them some space. 

Yun Yuqing whispered to the others, “Do you think they'll have a farewell kiss?”

“Farewell kiss?” Xie Daoyun was taken aback. “Surely not, right?”

She had come to understand the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s personality in the time they'd spent together. The latter was unlikely to do something that bold.

“Huh? Is big sister goddess in love with big brother Zu too?” Ji Xiaoxi asked in astonishment.

Yun Yuqing rolled her eyes. She couldn’t be bothered to answer the foolish lass’ unnecessary question. She turned to the others and asked, “Shall we make a bet?”

That piqued Xie Daoyun and Suolun Shi’s interest. They began gossiping among themselves.

However, Zu An and the Goddess of Wu Mountain were strong enough to overhear their words. Zu An awkwardly said, “Goddess, please pardon them. They don’t mean any ill will.”

“It’s fine. I have come to know them after spending a year with them,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain smiled. “And they aren’t wrong. I do fancy you.”

“Ah?” Zu An was shocked. The other party had used such a calm tone to relay such shocking news that his mind stalled to a halt for a brief moment. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain walked to the mountain cliff as she spoke with her usual cold voice, though it carried a slightly unusual tinge. “Before I got to know you, I used to spend my days standing here, feeling the mountain’s breeze, watching sunrise, sunset, and the billowing clouds. I was content with those days. But after coming to know you, everything changed.

“I came to know about joy and sorrow.” At this point, the Goddess of Wu Mountain turned around to reveal the tears flowing down her usual stoic face. “From the moment I saw Xihe’s and Chang’e’s tears, I knew that the final tear lay with me, but I couldn’t imagine what would lead up to that.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2607: Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 2607: Return

A glistening teardrop flowed down the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s cheeks. This one floated, unlike the others that had fallen to the ground. She raised her dainty hands and gently guided the teardrop into a jade bottle. 

She passed the jade bottle to Zu An. “With this, you should be able to complete the Defying Spirit Tears and treat Xiaoxi.”

Seeing how she was still thinking on his behalf despite being in tears, Zu An couldn’t hold it back anymore and pulled her into his arms. 

The previous him, like many other men, had thought that it would be best if all beautiful women in the world could be his. But now that he had many lovers, he realized that love could be hurtful at times.

He had been through far too many separations. After his separation from the Mermaid Queen at the Frost Palace, he'd thought that nothing else could hurt him anymore.

But now, he realized that he was extremely wrong. A heart could be hurt twice in succession, even if one time was more painful than the other. 

The other ladies in the distance noticed them hugging and frowned, as if discussing their earlier bets. But when they noticed the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s tears and Zu An’s pained look, their excitement waned. Even from a distance away, they could feel the sorrowful air on Zu An’s side.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain trembled in Zu An’s arms. It was rare for her to even speak to others, let alone be embraced by a man. Being held by his sturdy arms left her shy, but she also couldn’t help but wonder how much longer she could enjoy this embrace. 

Zu An racked his brain for a solution, not wanting a repeat of what happened with the Mermaid Queen. All of a sudden, his eyes lit up, and he reached out to wipe her tears as he asked, “Yaoji, why don’t you come with us if you don’t mind?”

The Nature Jade would likely have no problem taking another person with them. Unlike Chang’e, who was bound to the Frost Palace, the Goddess of Wu Mountain wasn’t tied down by anything. 

To his surprise, the Goddess of Wu Mountain shook her head and replied, “I can’t go.”

Seeing the confusion in Zu An’s eyes, the Goddess of Wu Mountain slowly explained, “I have been on this mountain ever since I was conferred as the Goddess of Wu Mountain. I don’t know how long has passed, but I often unknowingly morph into Wu Mountain’s Goddess Peak. In a way, I have already become one with Wu Mountain. I’m in a similar situation to little sister Chang’e. She is cursed by the Frost Palace, whereas I’m tied down by Wu Mountain.”

Zu An felt as if his heart had been plunged into a glacier. He could take a person, but he couldn’t take an entire mountain away with him. He felt helpless. When he first transmigrated over, he'd been much weaker, but he had never felt as helpless as this. Despite having grown stronger, it felt like there were more things that he couldn’t resolve now.

“Don’t be upset about it. This is my fate. I’m glad I met you. Meeting you has opened my eyes, and I was able to experience many different emotions I never knew about before.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain turned around and consoled Zu An instead.

That only pained Zu An more, however.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain grabbed his hand and led him to the cliff. “Big brother Zu, you don’t have to be sad. Whenever you see this cloud and fog, it will be the same as me being by your side. If Wu Mountain still exists in the future, you can look for me there too. It’s just that I might have completely morphed into the Goddess Peak by then.”

Zu An’s eyes reddened, and his voice was deeper than usual. “I’m worried about you. I have other friends with me, but you’ll be staying here alone.” 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain laughed. “I’m not alone. The wind, the clouds, and the rain are my friends. Besides, little sister Chang’e is still around. I can visit her now and then for a chat.”

Zu An often heard that his mouth was as sweet as if it were smeared with honey, but why did everything just feel so bitter right now? He struggled to find the words to console her. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain wouldn’t have cried so hard if she were truly fine. Her composure was merely a front to lessen his worry, but her consideration only made him feel worse. 

“I’d love to meet Miss Manman,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly remarked.

Her judgment aligned with Xihe’s—she was likely Pei Mianman’s previous incarnation. It was good news that the future Pei Mianman got together with Zu An, but her very existence meant that the Goddess of Wu Mountain had eventually passed away. She had no idea how she died and why she had chosen to reincarnate, but there must have been a compelling reason.

My love is fulfilled in the future, but this also means that I’m unlikely to meet him ever again in this life. That thought made her emotions go into turmoil again, and tears glistened in her eyes. 

A drizzle began falling upon Wu Mountain, enveloping it in fog. 

Zu An was about to say something when the Nature Jade suddenly emanated a brilliant light that enveloped him and the other ladies. The next second, they all vanished into thin air.

The Goddess of Wu Mountain tried to grab him, but it was too late. 

For a long time, she was reluctant to lower her hand that had frozen in mid-air. The vibrant Wu Mountain suddenly felt unbearably empty. She initially hadn't been able to get used to the guests residing in Wu Mountain, finding them too noisy. However, she found herself reminiscing about their joyous laughter. 

She walked to the cliff and looked at the distant sea of clouds. Her figure slowly blurred until she morphed into the Goddess Peak. 

That day, Wu Mountain rained heavier than ever. 

… 

Before they knew it, Zu An and the others appeared inside a lake. He quickly pulled the others up to shore before falling silent.

“Where are we?” Ji Xiaoxi surveyed their surroundings, but it was so dark that if they weren’t cultivators, they couldn’t have seen a thing at all. 

“It’s the Fiend race’s Imperial Tomb,” Xie Daoyun, who retained the memories of the time she'd been possessed by Xihe, said. 

“Ah, we returned to that spacetime fragment!” Ji Xiaoxi was delighted. That meant they could soon return to their original era. However, she soon recalled something and apologetically pulled on Zu An’s arm. “Big brother Zu, are you still feeling awful?”
