Before Zu An could answer, a gleeful laugh echoed in the distance. “Milord was right! You would eventually return here!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2608: Vanished
chapters.body:
Chapter 2608: Vanished

Monster soldiers swarmed in from the surroundings and encircled them. Several powerful monsters emerged from their ranks. Xie Daoyun, remembering them from her memories, recognized them to be the renowned Monster Generals!

They were none other than the Shadow Monster King, the War Priest, the Shadowhorror Devil, and the Doomsday Spirit!

The other Monster Generals had been either killed or severely injured by Zu An and the Lord of Slaughter in their previous showdown. It looks like the injured ones haven’t recovered yet. 

Faced with so many ferocious monsters, Ji Xiaoxi fearfully leaned closer to Zu An. Xie Daoyun, Yun Yuqing, and Suolun Shi secretly prepared their hand, ready to attack at any moment. 

Had it been before, they would have spiraled into a panic when faced with so many monsters. However, they'd met far too many powerful entities in the Celestial World, so much so that the enemies before them, despite being far stronger, didn’t look as intimidating anymore.

The ladies looked to Zu An for instructions, only to be shocked to see a mask on his face. 

Zu An had to wear a mask to avoid affecting the future. Since he would come into contact with some of these Monster Generals in the future, there was no telling what would happen if they saw his true face here and recognized him when they met again in the future. That was a risk he couldn’t take. 

The War Priest was perplexed by the absence of notable figures in the party. “Where are the Lord of Slaughter and the others?”

Zu An had been a difficult opponent for them back then, but after reviewing their battles, they realized that his strength was only on par with that of an average Monster General. It was just that he had all kinds of tricks that made him a troublesome foe. But now that they had a better grasp of those tricks and knew how to guard against them, it would be much easier to deal with him now. Not to mention they had come up with new skills and tricks for this rematch.

“Scram! I’m not in the mood to deal with you now.” Zu An was already in a terrible mood, and these sidekicks still wanted to annoy him at this juncture!

What further frustrated him was that he couldn’t kill them right now. He needed them alive so he could deal with them in the future. What a hassle!

The Shadow Monster King and the others were enraged. “You scum! How dare you blatantly disrespect us? I’ll rip off your skin and remove your tendons! You shall watch in despair as the ladies beside you suffer fates worse than death!”

You have successfully trolled the Shadow Monster King for +444… +444… +444

You have successfully trolled the War Priest…

… 

The influx of Rage points didn’t lift Zu An’s mood; it only fanned his anger. 

All of a sudden, there was a flash of seven-colored light. It swept away the Monster Generals before they could understand what was going on. They found themselves twirling all around a seven-colored world as if they were laundry inside a washing machine, causing their heads to spin. 

Then, they were hurled out of the light and landed heavily on the ground. 

The Monster Generals tried to leap to their feet to counterattack. How could they admit defeat when they hadn’t even used their artifacts and skills? But no matter how they flailed around with their hands and feet, they couldn’t get to their feet, as if the earlier fall had broken them for good. They felt so giddy that they could vomit. 

The monster soldiers were dumbstruck. What’s going on? We came here to destroy these humans, right? How did the tables turn on us? How in the world were the almighty Monster Generals defeated in the blink of an eye?

The monster soldiers lost their fighting spirit. If not for their fear of the Monster Lord’s punishment, they would have thrown their weapons away and surrendered by now. 

Zu An couldn’t be bothered to waste his time on the monster soldiers. He grabbed Ji Xiaoxi’s hand and led the other ladies out of the Imperial Tomb. 

The monster soldiers instinctively opened a path for them to cross. They lowered their heads, not daring to meet Zu An’s eyes. However, despite the monsters’ attempt to steer clear of Zu An’s party, there were simply too many of them here, and the space in the Imperial Tomb was limited.

Ji Xiaoxi and the others could still smell the monsters’ repulsive aura. However, none of the monsters dared to step out of line. This peculiar experience left the ladies excited and awed. They would never have thought that the savage monsters could be this obedient. 

They soon left the Imperial Tomb. The sight of the sun evoked a wistful feeling. It felt as if so many things had changed since. 

A powerful aura came surging in from the distance. It was the Monster Lord and his aides. They had rushed over upon receiving news about Zu An’s return. 

When he saw Zu An’s party, the Monster Lord frowned. “Where’s the Lord of Slaughter?”

There was a flash of seven-colored light, and the Lord of Slaughter’s massive body was hurled onto the ground. A huge commotion broke out. 

The Lord of Slaughter had dominated the Monster World for so long that many monsters still viewed him as the strongest despite the Monster Lord’s usurpation of power. They thought that the Monster Lord had only come to power because of his schemes.

And yet, they were witnessing the strongest monster, the Lord of Slaughter, being hurled on the ground like a sandbag with their own eyes.

The Monster Lord’s eyes quivered. He had only usurped the throne from the Lord of Slaughter by developing a comprehensive plan and planting many chess pieces beforehand, and even so, it was a narrow victory. 

To make things worse, the Lord of Slaughter had even grasped the secret behind the Fiend race’s Imperial Tomb and entered the legendary Celestial World!

All this while, the Monster Lord had been worried that the Lord of Slaughter would stumble upon a fortuitous encounter in the Celestial World and awaken his bloodline power. As a countermeasure, he'd prepared many slaughter formations and stationed the Monster Generals and their armies in the Imperial Tomb. He'd even developed new moves to catch the Lord of Slaughter by surprise.

To think that his strongest enemy would be defeated just like that!

The Monster Lord quickly snapped out of his daze and laughed. “So you have dealt with the Lord of Slaughter! The enemy of one's enemy is a friend. I will handsomely reward you for your merit!”

At the same time, he wondered what was going on with the Shadow Monster King, the War Priest, and the others. Why aren’t they making a move?

Those two Monster Generals were extremely powerful, and they were aided by the Shadowhorror Devil, the Doomsday Spirit, and the monster armies. Even the Monster Lord would struggle to deal with such a lineup. 

He had rushed here as soon as he received the signal. Surely they can’t be dead yet?

“Who’s your friend? This is the human world. Scram from this world with your subordinates, and I’ll consider sparing your lives,” Zu An sneered coldly. 

The Monster Lord’s smile stiffened. He first glanced at the unconscious Lord of Slaughter, followed by the eerily silent Imperial Mausoleum. There’s something amiss about this fellow. However, he soon burst into laughter and said, “You’re merely putting on a strong front. If you were truly that powerful, you wouldn’t have to demand our departure.”

However, a blade of sword ki suddenly appeared before his eyes. It was so fast that he didn’t have time to react. By the time he glanced at his right arm, it had already vanished from sight!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2609: Looking For Chang’E
chapters.body:
Chapter 2609: Looking For Chang’E

Even Xie Daoyun and the others were shocked.

Even though Zu An had attained immortality, the Monster Lord’s strength was at least on par with many deities. It would be one thing if he was caught off guard by Zu An’s seven-colored light, but how could the Monster Lord’s arm be lopped off so easily in a confrontation?

Only Yun Yuqing could fathom a guess as to what happened. Big brother Zu has fought too many powerful figures in the Celestial World, including Celestial Emperor-tier opponents. His will is far too strong for the Monster Lord to oppose. 

The concept of one’s willpower influencing one’s fighting prowess was no secret. In a fight between two people of similar strength, the one with stronger willpower was likely to claim the upper hand. Yun Yuqing had seen plenty of such examples in her many years of cultivation. 

But tempering one’s willpower was easier said than done. It had to be slowly built up by accruing victories against powerful opponents. Zu An had only fought the Celestial Emperors to a draw, but considering his far weaker cultivation, that was a formidable feat. If that couldn’t temper an invincible will, nothing would.

The Monster Lord, not knowing the true story, couldn’t understand how Zu An could have become this powerful when he had only left for a short time. In the end, he attributed it to a fortuitous encounter in the legendary Celestial Court. He glanced at the unconscious Lord of Slaughter, and he suddenly felt extreme fear.

This isn’t an opponent I can defeat! The thought of that made him lose all fighting spirit. Smoke swiftly enveloped him as he unleashed all his escape methods. By the time the smoke dissipated, he was already nowhere to be seen.

Zu An sensed a spatial disturbance, and judging from that, he deduced that the Monster Lord had used a teleportation artifact. However, he didn’t stop the Monster Lord, as the latter wasn’t meant to die in this era. 

Instead, he cast a sweeping gaze across the monster army and bellowed, “I’ll give you ten days to scram from this world. I’ll purge every monster who’s still here after ten days.”

The invading monster army was so huge that Zu An couldn’t hope to defeat them single-handedly. Even if he could, the monster army’s desperate retaliation would only inflict greater damage on this world. 

Ten days would be enough for most monsters to leave via large-scale teleportation formations, but they wouldn’t have enough time to bring their weapons and resources back with them. Those would serve as wartime reparations. Besides that, it was also unlikely that all the monsters could evacuate within ten days. The remaining ones would serve as fodder to quell the anger of this world’s races.

The monsters were flabbergasted. Cowardice had already been forming in their hearts when they saw the Lord of Slaughter being hurled out like a dead dog. The sight of the Monster Lord losing his arm and escaping further crushed their morale. Their formations collapsed, and the monsters fled frantically in all directions. 

Xie Daoyun said worriedly, “Big brother Zu, the monsters have no principles. You might have intimidated them for the time being, but they might change their minds once they snap out of it.”

Her experience of dealing with the monsters as the Fiend races’ Saintess Yumen Beiqing made her opinion credible. After some thought, Zu An took out his Sun Slaying Bow. Now that the Monster Lord had escaped, he wasn't worried about the latter seeing it.

He nocked an arrow and shot it into the sky. It soon plummeted from the sky like a comet, falling directly on the monster army’s camp. 

Boom!

That was, after all, an arrow that could shoot down a sun. 

Zu An had to hold back because he wasn’t sure if this world could withstand his full-powered arrow, but even so, it smashed through the monster camp and annihilated it. A huge pit was left in the heart of the camp, and the ground turned into lava. Smoke filled the surrounding air. The monsters who were slow to escape were blown to bits; the survivors, scared out of their wits, fled with everything they had in desperation.

Meanwhile, a translucent bell around Zu An protected the ladies around him, keeping the terrifying shockwaves at bay. 

Yun Yuqing, Suolun Shi, and the others wanted to pursue the escaping monsters; they held a deep grudge against the monsters, and wouldn’t miss the chance to massacre them. 

Xie Daoyun stopped them. “There’s no point chasing them. We also need them to spread the news about what happened here for the other monsters to give up and return to their world.”

Yun Yuqing nodded in agreement. “As expected of the Fiend races’ Saintess! Little sister Xie is truly meticulous!”

Xie Daoyun blushed in embarrassment. 

The sight of the burning monster camp left the ladies with many thoughts, especially Xie Daoyun. She had led the Fiend races in this world on a counterattack against the monsters. Even now, she could still remember how suffocated she felt whenever she thought about the monsters’ fearsome strength. 

Who would have thought that Zu An could resolve this situation with such ease?

“Big brother Zu, let’s return to the Fiend races first and inform them about the monsters’ movements. We should also discuss the sealing of the Lord of Slaughter,” Xie Daoyun said. 

Zu An replied, “You return to the Fiend races first. I’ll leave you to prepare for the Lord of Slaughter’s sealing. There’s another place I need to go to first.”

Xie Daoyun was talented in formations, and she knew how the Lord of Slaughter was sealed in the future. This task was within her means. 

Sensing the sorrow in his eyes, Xie Daoyun’s heart quivered. She could guess where he was heading. The other ladies also exchanged looks, knowing better than to touch his wound. 

After bidding the ladies farewell, Zu An stowed away the Lord of Slaughter with his Seven-Colored Divine Halo and flew into the sky. 

Looking at his departing silhouette, Ji Xiaoxi asked, “Big sister, is big brother Zu heading to the Frost Palace to visit big sister Chang’e?”

“It seems so.”

“But it’s still day. The moon hasn’t come out yet.”

“Big brother Zu is very knowledgeable. He should have a way to find the moon… right?”

… 

Zu An could naturally find the moon. He flew above the clouds and dashed out of the stratosphere, eventually entering the vastness of space. After attaining immortality, his body was tough enough to withstand the extreme conditions of space, though he wasn’t sure how far he could travel in the void. 

He looked at the world behind him and saw a familiar blue planet. It is indeed Earth! It looks much larger than the planet I’m familiar with though. Could it be because it's rich in ki?

Zu An tossed aside his miscellaneous thoughts and soon found the moon. He quickly descended on it. Circular pits were visible on its dirt-cloaked surface. He couldn’t detect the slightest life on it.

His heart sank. He couldn’t find the Frost Palace, which meant Chang’e wasn’t here. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2610: Everything Has Changed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2610: Everything Has Changed

He had already expected as much when he emerged from the stratosphere, however. Flying upward should have led him into the Celestial Court, but it was nowhere to be found. All he encountered was boundless space. 

Zu An fell silent. Did the Celestial Court disappear altogether? Or does it not exist in the sky, unlike what everyone thought, and that there is some kind of teleportation formation in the sky that transports you to the Celestial Court? What kind of place is the Celestial Court?

He suddenly recalled the Celestial Court’s peculiar flow of time. A day in heaven equaled a year on earth. 

It’s probably located near a black hole. Is there no way to access the Celestial Court in this era? 

Maybe Zhuanxu’s severing of heaven and earth and the felling of the sacred tree Jianmu resulted in the beings on Earth ceasing to worship the Celestial Court. It’s probably tough for those legendary gods to appear in our world now too. 

Zu An felt frustrated. His inability to access the Celestial Court meant that he couldn’t reunite with the Mermaid Queen. He had marked some of the Celestial Court’s coordinates back then, but he couldn’t sense them anymore. It could be because he was too far away, or that a formation had blocked them. 

In the end, he descended from the moon and returned to Earth.

This Earth was much larger than the one he was on before his transmigration. Did the later drying up of ki cause the planet to contract? He had several conjectures, but there was no way to confirm them. 

While descending from the sky, Zu An suddenly turned to a mountain range. I might be unable to reach the Celestial Court now, but what about the surface? The Goddess of Wu Mountain is also waiting for me!

Countless years had passed since the era of the Celestial Court, so the world was bound to have changed in the meantime. 

Zu An made his way to Wu Mountain based on the coordinates he remembered, but there was no mountain there when he arrived—in its place was a lake. He thoroughly searched the lake, but there wasn't the slightest trace of Wu Mountain. 

“The coordinates must have changed due to the terraforming of the world,” he said to himself. He'd even constructed a teleportation formation on Wu Mountain, but so much time had passed that he could no longer sense it.

He searched based on his memories and grasp of space, and he also asked the locals about their regional myths and legends. Days later, he finally found Spirit Mountain.

This world’s Spirit Mountain was richer in ki than other places, but it was severely lacking compared to the Celestial World’s Spirit Mountain. No wonder people seemed to get weaker with each passing generation in the cultivation stories I read in my previous world. Turns out it’s because of the change in environment.

He enveloped Spirit Mountain with his divine sense—its topography roughly lined up with the Celestial World’s Spirit Mountain. 

Soon, he arrived before a beautiful mountain peak. Everything was different from how he remembered it to be, but as he made his way over, he felt an inexplicable sense of intimacy. He flew up to the peak. The ki waterfall and flower fields were nowhere to be found; the place was covered in weeds instead. Even so, he could still see through the weeds to recognize the original mountain peak.

He made his way to the cliff. He tried opening the cave using the method he remembered, though he didn’t bear much hope. Too much time had passed, so he felt the cave would have eroded over time as well.

Much to his delight, however, the cliff opened to reveal a familiar cave!

Zu An trembled. Could Yaoji still be inside?

Yaoji, as the Goddess of Wu Mountain, was one with Wu Mountain. If Wu Mountain was still around, chances were that she was also around too. 

“Yaoji?” Zu An gently called out. Despite his nervousness, his footsteps were slow. He was afraid that he would receive a disappointing answer. 

The dark cave suddenly lit up, and a pleasant melody played. Flower petals fell from the sky. A beautiful figure slowly walked out, just as when they first met. 

“Yaoji!” Overjoyed, Zu An rushed straight over to embrace her.

Little did he expect to pass right through her figure. His smile immediately froze in place. It turned out that was merely an illusion of the Goddess of Wu Mountain. 

It should have been apparent to him, but he was so shaken up that he'd overlooked that. 

“Big brother Zu, I have always been curious how you would react when you see me once more. Let me guess. You must have been very excited, only to be disappointed when you realized that I was an illusion.” The beautiful figure’s cold voice was tinged with a bit of mischief. 

Her words slightly consoled Zu An’s heavy heart. He could tell this wasn’t a soul fragment but merely a recording, so conversation wasn’t possible between them. 

“Actually, I have another guess—you might have tried to embrace me too.” She suddenly let out a sigh. “Maybe it’s one-sided, but I still remember our final embrace. It was so warm and comfortable, yet so sorrowful too.”

“Yaoji, I did try to embrace you earlier, and I'm still embracing you now.” Zu An walked up to the illusion and gently ‘held’ her despite knowing it was an illusion.

The illusion spoke yet again. “I don’t know whether the first thing you did was to embrace me or not, but I bet you are embracing me now after hearing my words.”

Zu An quietly looked at her face. She was identical to the Yaoji he remembered. He hadn’t expected a cold beauty like her to be so meticulous.

“I want to embrace you too, but I can’t.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain also took an embracing posture, as if lying against his chest. 

“You are the only one other than me who knows how to open this cave. If someone tries to open it by force, everything in this cave will be destroyed,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain continued. “I promised to wait for you. I waited for a thousand years, ten thousand years… I don’t know how many tens of thousands of years I have waited. You know me. I don’t have a sense of time.”

Her gentle words shook Zu An's heart. To him, it had only been half a day since they parted ways, but to her, it had already been tens of thousands of years. He couldn’t imagine how sorrowful it must have been for her to wait for him day after day with an expectant heart, only to be disappointed again and again.

“Yaoji…” Zu An’s voice became hoarse. Words fell short in the face of the harshness of time. 

Worry brimmed in the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s eyes. “I know you wouldn’t forget me, big brother Zu. You promised to look for me, but you didn’t come after so long. I was worried that something had happened to you, so I went to look for little sister Chang’e…”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2611: Reincarnation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2611: Reincarnation

Zu An’s heart quivered. He had just gone to the moon to search for Chang’e, only to learn that the Celestial Court had vanished from the world. He'd been grieving over possibly never meeting the Mermaid Queen again when a clue suddenly popped up here. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued, “Little sister Chang’e knows you better. She feels it’s unlikely for any mishap to have befallen you given your wits and strength. There must be a compelling reason why you haven’t looked for us yet. She has been pondering that question, and we concluded that the most likely reason is time.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain lowered her head in sorrow. “If a day in heaven translates to a year on earth, there could be other temporal disparities in the world too. A spacetime traveler like you might have a different flow of time from us. Our ten thousand years might have been just a few years or even a few days for you.”

The Goddess of Wu Mountain couldn’t hold back her tears anymore.

Zu An felt his heart clench. He wanted to wipe off her tears, but his hands passed right through her projection. That made him feel worse. 

“Let’s not talk about sad matters anymore. You must be concerned about little sister Chang’e. She’s famous in the Celestial Court, with many male deities seeing her as their dream lover.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain chuckled. “But little sister Chang’e never paid them any heed. She told them that her heart already belongs to someone else. The male deities were so angry that some declared they would teach that person a lesson. There were several extremely powerful individuals among them. Little did they know that their love rival wasn’t in this world.”

Her teasing words brought a smile to Zu An’s face, though it pained him to be unable to protect the Mermaid Queen from other people’s harassment. 

As if sensing his worry, the Goddess of Wu Mountain quickly added, “But there’s nothing to worry. Little sister Chang’e is one with the Frost Palace. No one can hurt her in the Frost Palace, not even the Celestial Emperor.”

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. At the same time, he became increasingly curious about the Frost Palace’s origin.

“I was worried that little sister Chang’e would feel lonely while I wasn't around, so I offered to bring her a pet to stave off her loneliness. I thought she would choose a cat or a dog, but she is surprisingly fond of rabbits. So, I caught a mutated jade rabbit in the wilderness for her. She was very fond of it,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said gleefully. She was satisfied with her gift. 

Zu An hadn’t expected the Goddess of Wu Mountain to be the one who gave Chang’e her Jade Rabbit. The Jade Rabbit was capable of cultivating, and she would later transform into a pretty woman and slip into the mortal world. Although, that was a thing for the future.

“Little sister Chang’e also introduced me to Miss Mo Xi. We have been looking for ways to hasten her cultivation. Miss Mo Xi isn’t fond of speaking, but she’s very talented and cultivates fast. However, she’s still unable to catch up with little sister Chang’e. We always seem to be slightly off the mark from meeting the requirements for ‘Farewell, Nanchao’.”

Zu An frowned. Mo Xi should have received an abundance of resources from the Goddess of Wu Mountain and the Mermaid Queen, yet she was still unable to surpass Chang’e. This made him even more curious about the Frost Palace’s origin.  

Hold on, the Goddess of Wu Mountain said that Mo Xi isn’t fond of speaking. Did Mo Xi speak in front of them?

Zu An had many questions, but all he had now was a recording left behind by the Goddess of Wu Mountain. Conversation wasn’t possible, so he could only listen to her narration. 

“I thought the Frost Palace could use some greenery and tried transplanting some plants and flowers there, but I haven’t succeeded yet. But I’ll continue trying to find a plant that can thrive in the Frost Palace.”

Zu An was moved. The Goddess of Wu Mountain was a reclusive individual who enjoyed her peace. It was completely unlike her to proactively help the Mermaid Queen. In other words, she had done all that for his sake. 

“You must be curious about the outcome of the war between the Shamans and Fiends.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain wryly shook her head right after saying those words. “I nearly forgot. You’re from the future. You must already know the conclusion.”

“The war continued for a long time, and countless powerful Shamans and Celestials met their end. The final showdown even resulted in the deaths of most Ancestral Shamans, with only Houtu and a few other Ancestral Shamans surviving the ordeal. Emperor Jun and Donghuang Taiyi died in that battle too.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain looked a little shaken up when she spoke about that grand battle. It had to have been truly terrible. 

“There was no victor in the war between the Shamans and the Fiends. Both sides receded into the shadows, opening room for the rise of humankind. Humans are hardworking and brave, and they are adept at learning the skills of both races. I’d expect no less from big brother Zu’s race,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said, talking about the human race with a gentle gaze.

She didn’t continue elaborating on that, knowing Zu An had to be familiar with that history. Instead, she moved the topic on to something else, “Xihe often drops by the Frost Palace to chat with us. She also visits me at Wu Mountain from time to time. A lot of Miss Mo Xi’s cultivation resources came from her.”

Zu An harbored conflicting feelings about Xihe. Even though the latter had approached him with the intention of exacting vengeance for her sons, they had indeed spent a long time together as an intimate couple. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain was awed. “To think that you could make the renowned Sun Goddess, the ex-Celestial Empress, fall in love with you in such a short time. You’re impressive indeed…”

Faced with her cold eyes, Zu An’s face reddened despite knowing she was an illusion. 

“I don’t like her presence at Wu Mountain. She’s too searing. Her presence dissipates the mountain fog and withers the plants.” The Goddess of Wu Mountain pouted. “But little sister Chang’e is fond of her. The Frost Palace feels much warmer whenever she’s around. As they often appear together in the Celestial Court, a legend has begun spreading among the humans, saying that the Sun Goddess and the Moon Goddess are close sisters.”

… 

Hearing the events that had transpired over the years filled Zu An’s heart with warmth. If only I could have gone through these events with them.

“Xihe heard from little sister Chang’e that there is a Miss Shang in the future who greatly resembles her. She was envious that we had future incarnations, and she said that she wanted to be Chang’e’s big sister if she ever reincarnated,” the Goddess of Wu Mountain said with a smile. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Could Shang Hongyu be… 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain’s voice suddenly sped up. “When I first heard Miss Gong and Xihe’s talk about reincarnation, I initially didn’t get it. I know myself. I’d rather wait for you as myself than to undergo reincarnation. However, I’m starting to see why as of late.”

Zu An was startled. Her tone… Did something huge happen?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2612: Reunion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2612: Reunion

However, the Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t mention what happened. She hesitantly said, “There are many things I can’t tell you. Xihe and little sister Chang’e have told me that the past has both fixed and uncertain variables. Things may become fixed if I say them now, and that may lead to unexpected outcomes.” 

Zu An could roughly understand her concerns.

Mi Li had also shared her understanding of spacetime with him. It would be more accurate to refer to recorded history as Schrodinger’s history. Major events were fixed in place, but the unrecorded details granted room for peculiar maneuvers. 

Zu An’s journey to the ancient era of the Celestial Court was an example of that. He had done many things there, and while his actions conformed to what had happened in recorded history, the nuances were completely different. 

The Goddess of Wu Mountain sighed. “I wonder if big brother Zu will ever see this recording.”

Zu An’s heart quivered at her sigh. The Goddess of Wu Mountain continued to share events that had happened over the years, as well as her deep yearning for him. He quietly took in her stories and sentiments. 

Some time later, the Goddess of Wu Mountain suddenly stood up to look into the distance. Her face reflected both trepidation and determination. “It’s about time. May reincarnation truly exist in this world, so our bond can continue in the future.”

A teardrop flowed down her beautiful cheek. She flew to Wu Mountain’s peak and stood there, gazing in the direction where Zu An had disappeared back then. Her body slowly transformed into the elegant Goddess Peak, fixing itself to the mountain.

Zu An’s heart became heavy. He knew that she often transformed into a mountain peak while gazing at the scenery, but he could tell that something was different this time. She couldn’t transform back anymore. 

He dashed out of the cave and headed to Wu Mountain’s peak. Amid the fog, an elegant peak stood out like a graceful, dignified lady. A gentle breeze blew, tugging on the red maple leaves on the mountain. For a moment, he thought he saw the Goddess of Wu Mountain standing there, smiling at him. 

“Yaoji…” Zu An subconsciously walked forward, only for her silhouette to vanish. It was only him and the mountain peak.

He turned into a giant as he walked up to the mountain peak and gently embraced the Goddess Peak. He couldn’t stop his tears anymore. “Yaoji, I’m sorry for coming late.”

He didn’t dare to exert any force, fearing that it would hurt the mountain. His tears fell on the Goddess Peak. 

As if sensing his sorrow, it began to drizzle on Wu Mountain. A wind blew, and the clouds billowed. It was as if the world was manifesting his remembrance of the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

Zu An spent ten days on Wu Mountain just like that. He even constructed several defensive formations around the Goddess Peak, lest it be damaged by external forces. There were many formidable cultivators in this world capable of destroying mountains, after all. 

Even though the Goddess Peak was located in the middle of a remote mountain range, he didn’t dare to take any risks. Worried that the formations would run out of energy over time, he designed a mechanism based on his previous world’s solar panels to tap into the sun and the moon’s essences.

Ten days later, however, Zu An had no choice but to leave. Be it the monsters, the Fiend races, or the Ocean races, there was a huge list of things that he needed to deal with. He kissed the Goddess Peak before departing via Rainbow Morph. 

Wu Mountain’s maple leaves swayed as if bidding him goodbye. 

… 

Zu An descended upon the Fiend races’ new Imperial Court.

The Fiend races tensed up as if a terrifying monster had sprung out of nowhere. It was only when their Saintess leaped into his arms in delight that they remembered his identity.

Many in the Fiend races had previously harbored hostility toward Zu An because their Saintess had threatened to break ties with them to be with him. However, they recently learned that Zu An had single-handedly driven off the monsters from this world. Given his massive accomplishment and his astounding strength, who would dare continue to harbor dissatisfaction toward him?

All the race leaders expressed goodwill toward him, hoping to get into his good books. It was just that Zu An was too tired to deal with all that.

Fortunately, Xie Daoyun took care of it on his behalf. She scattered the crowd and brought him back to her residence. “Big brother Zu, did you find the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain?”

Zu An shook his head before explaining what had happened.

The ladies could tell that things hadn’t gone well from his expression, but his story still left them feeling melancholic. Even the most sincere promise couldn’t overcome the relentless passage of time. 

“Big brother Zu, there is someone you'll be happy to meet.” Xie Daoyun revealed a rare mischievous smile. 

Zu An widened his eyes. He already had a guess who it was.

The ladies escorted him to the entrance of a residence before urging him to enter alone. 

Zu An took a deep breath before pushing the door with trembling hands. A woman wearing a red top and a black skirt was applying makeup by the window. Perhaps to conserve her energy, she rested her breasts on the table. 

“Ling’er, you have to help me stall him…” The woman reflexively glanced over when she heard the door opening, only to freeze up when she saw Zu An. Her face swiftly reddened. She felt embarrassed to have him see her earnestly doing her makeup.

“Manman…” Zu An was dazed for an instant. It was only moments since he'd returned from Wu Mountain. He thus briefly thought the woman before him was the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

However, they had spent so much time together that he could tell the difference right away. Her air of seductiveness was at odds with the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s lofty disposition. 

Pei Mianman had paid Yumen Beiqing a visit back when Xihe was still occupying her body. Afraid that her disguise would be exposed, Xihe had subdued her, but for some reason, she'd opted to imprison Pei Mianman in a formation instead of killing her. 

Xihe had already told Zu An the location and the method to unravel the formation. Knowing that she was safe, Zu An had entrusted the task of rescuing her to Xie Daoyun instead. 

“They're making fun of me.” Pei Mianman put down her brush as she stood up with a pout. 

Unable to repress his yearning anymore, Zu An pulled Pei Mianman into his arms. The strength in his arms evoked the latter’s smile, and she tightly embraced him back. They had been apart for so long that Pei Mianman had even wondered if they would be separated eternally. 

Some time later, Pei Mianman suddenly looked at him with an ambiguous smile. “Who are you thinking about right now… me or that Goddess of Wu Mountain?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2613: Suppression
chapters.body:
Chapter 2613: Suppression

“Both,” Zu An admitted frankly as he hugged Pei Mianman’s body tighter.

“Scum!” Pei Mianman feigned anger and struggled. But Zu An continued holding her tightly, not letting her go. 

Sensing his low spirits, Pei Mianman stopped struggling and gently said, “I heard you went off to look for Chang’e and the Goddess of Wu Mountain. You didn’t find them?”

“I didn’t…” Zu An finally released her. He grabbed her hand and led her to the table, where the two of them sat down. He told her everything that had happened in the last few days.

“To think tens of thousands of years would pass just like that.” Pei Mianman sat in a daze for a brief moment before abruptly asking, “Does the Goddess of Wu Mountain really look like me?”

Zu An assessed her beautiful face and replied, “You’re identical, but opposite.”

“What nonsense is that? You don’t need to tease me.” Pei Mianman harrumphed.

“I mean it.” Zu An stroked her hand. “The Goddess of Wu Mountain is opposite of you. You’re seductive and passionate, whereas she’s cold and lofty. If not for your identical appearances, I wouldn’t have believed she was your past incarnation.”

“Cold and lofty?” Pei Mianman felt stifled. “I knew it. You like Chuyan’s type more.”

Zu An burst into laughter. “Are you getting jealous of yourself?”

“What do you mean by myself? She is herself, and I am myself. We aren’t the same.” Pei Mianman puffed up her cheeks. 

She had been imprisoned for a long time. When she was finally rescued, she abruptly learned that her lover had gotten together with another woman who was identical to her. To make things worse, the other party was likely to be her past incarnation, so she couldn’t even lash out. It felt as if something was seething inside her, but she couldn’t let it out. 

“You are indeed different. I have never thought of her as you, and I would never think of you as her either.” Zu An thought it was interesting how two identical people could have such different personalities. 

That made Pei Mianman feel better, but when she thought about how she was throwing a tantrum when he was already in a bad mood, she felt apologetic. “I heard the others chatting about the Goddess of Wu Mountain. She sounds like a very impressive woman.” 

Zu An looked at her weirdly. “It sounds as if you’re complimenting yourself.”

“I am a beautiful woman. If she looks like me, she deserves to be complimented too.” Pei Mianman harrumphed with mirthful eyes. She hugged his arm and lay her head on his shoulder. “Tell me about your story with her.”

Feeling the pressure on his arm, Zu An was once again reminded about how Pei Mianman was different from Yaoji. “Didn’t the others already tell you?”

“They wouldn’t know as much as you.” Pei Mianman cast a sideward glance at him, her beautiful eyes nearly siphoning his soul away. 

Zu An finally recounted his story with the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

At the very end, Pei Mianman was taken aback. “You didn’t bed her?”

Black streaks appeared on Zu An’s face. “Was that the point?”

“Of course it is! That’s completely unlike you.” Pei Mianman looked truly astonished. “I didn’t think you would be capable of a purely platonic relationship!”

Zu An looked away, not wanting to respond to her.

“Fine, fine. I won’t tease you anymore, so don’t be angry, all right?” Pei Mianman coyly pulled his arm. “You want to meet that goddess again, right? That’s not that hard.”

Zu An was stunned. “You have a way?”

He had spent a long time racking his brain for a way he could be together with Yaoji, but it was to no avail. Could Pei Mianman have stumbled upon a fortuitous encounter or something?

Pei Mianman straightened her posture and looked at Zu An with an air of loftiness. “Big brother Zu, it has been a while, but we have finally reunited.”

Zu An was stunned.

He knew it was Pei Mianman’s cosplay, but he had to admit that her demeanor was indeed identical to the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s. He fell into a daze as he asked, “How did you learn to imitate her?”

“What do you mean, big brother Zu? This is how I have always been. It wasn’t easy for us to be reunited. Shall we head to Wu Mountain to reminisce our old days?” Pei Mianman continued speaking in a lofty manner.

Zu An burst into laughter. 

That made Pei Mianman break out of character. “What’s with your expression?”

Zu An explained to her how in his previous world, ‘head to Wu Mountain’ referred to the union of a couple.

That made Pei Mianman’s face redden in embarrassment. “Why does your homeland have so many weird sayings? How indecent!” Thinking about the bold words she had said made her so ashamed that she wanted to bury herself in the ground. 

Zu An hugged her. “Your acting is on point. Have you seen her before, or inherited any of her memories?”

Pei Mianman rolled her eyes. “Have you forgotten I’m close friends with Chuyan? Not to mention her master is the same type too. I’m more familiar with the cold beauty sort than anyone else. Copying her is a walk in the park for me.”

The mention of Chuyan and her master brought a tinge of tenderness to Zu An’s heart. 

Just then, Xie Daoyun knocked on the door and said, “I don’t want to disturb your reunion, but there’s something I need to discuss with big brother Zu.”

Pei Mianman pushed Zu An away and tidied her clothes. She double-checked that everything was in place before replying, “You don’t have to be so polite. Come in!”

Xie Daoyun entered the room wearing shaman robes. She first glanced at the two of them before finally turning to Zu An. “Big brother Zu, we're almost done preparing for the Lord of Slaughter’s sealing. I thought about it, and I think it would be better for you to participate in the sealing ceremony to intimidate the monsters.” 

“All right.” Zu An was aware that not all monsters were willing to retreat to the Monster World. There were still some who harbored hopes that he was bluffing. 

Thus, he made his way to the sealing ground with Xie Daoyun. Many fiends were already on standby for the ritual.

To maximize the intimidation factor, Zu An let the Lord of Slaughter out and removed the seal placed on the latter. 

The freed Lord of Slaughter angrily roared, “How did I get here? You’re courting death!”

Seeing those he deemed as ants encircling him, the Lord of Slaughter had an inexplicable feeling that he had forgotten something extremely important, but no matter how he racked his brain, he couldn’t recall it. 

Zu An was impressed by Diyao’s abilities. The latter had only erased the Lord of Slaughter’s memories to prevent his dirty deeds from leaking out, but this worked out in Zu An’s favor too. 

The Lord of Slaughter’s roar intimidated even the bravest Fiend race warriors. The immense pressure he emanated made the fiends gathered here tremble in fear. Even so, he couldn’t shake off the uncanny feeling he had. Thus, he suppressed his bloodlust and instead leaped into the sky with his humongous physique, opting to escape from here first before deciding on his next move. 

However, a golden palm print suddenly appeared in midair and slapped him down to earth. The Lord of Slaughter struggled in anger, but he couldn’t break free of the palm. 

Boom!

He was crushed into the ground, and a massive pit formed around him. He tried to rise back up, but several mountains were already being piled up on top of him.

Knowing he couldn’t hold back anymore, he unleashed his ultimate skills, causing the mountains on top of him to ripple. It looked as if he would break free at any moment. 

Xie Daoyun was prepared for this, however. She flew into the air and began channeling the formation she had prepared beforehand. Formation runes glowed on the mountains, and the quakes shaking the mountains gradually calmed down. By the time the final formation rune was completed, the mountains had already fallen completely still.

The Lord of Slaughter had been completely suppressed.

Deafening cheers broke out from the fiends as they sang praises about Yumen Beiqing. To them, it looked as if their Saintess Yumen Beiqing had sealed the terrifying Lord of Slaughter.

However, the monster experts secretly observing this scene fell into despair. They could tell the truly terrifying one wasn’t the woman, but the man behind her. The Lord of Slaughter had displayed strength comparable to his peak earlier, but he was still suppressed with ease. What hope did they have against an entity like that?

The remaining hawkish monsters unhesitatingly turned dovish. They hurriedly opened their teleportation formations to escape from this terrifying world as fast as they could. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2614: Going Into The Ocean
chapters.body:
Chapter 2614: Going Into The Ocean

Upon sensing the Monster Lord’s and other powerful entities’ auras leaving this world, Zu An flew into the air, took out the scepter he had obtained from the Monster Lord, and channeled a secret art to activate this world’s barrier. Due to his experience controlling the World Law Beacon in his previous life, he was able to do it smoothly. 

Golden streaks of light appeared in the sky. All living beings in this world watched with bated breath as they interwove into a golden net. Despite no one explaining what was going on, they felt an inexplicable sense of calm. They looked at the humongous figure in the sky and speculated who it was.

Those with insider knowledge whispered that he was Saintess Yumen Beiqing’s lover. By this point, none of the fiends were jealous of him anymore. All they had were sincere blessings for the two of them. 

The Saintess had truly descended from heaven to save the world. Not only had she turned the tides against the monsters, she'd even managed to seal off the powerful Lord of Slaughter. Even her man was powerful enough to unleash such a massive skill.  

As he activated the barrier, Zu An couldn’t help but wonder why this world barrier was weaker compared to the future, despite the richer ki here. This world barrier was nowhere strong enough to seal off the entire world. On top of that, it would gradually weaken over time, eventually reaching a point where it could no longer ward off invaders.

But these things were all inscribed in fate. Since the future him would clean up the mess anyway, he wasn’t too worried about it.

Once the world barrier was reactivated, Zu An made his way to the Ocean races. There were many things he needed to settle, be it the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons or the Netherworld. 

Pei Mianman accompanied him. They had been separated for so long that she didn’t want to part ways with him just yet. Zu An missed her dearly too. Each time took a look at her, he couldn’t help but think about the Goddess of Wu Mountain. 

The other ladies were envious, but they had other matters to attend to.

Yumen Beiqing, as the Saintess, needed to lead the Fiend races to eradicate the remaining monsters in the world. Yun Yuqing and Suolun Shi, as members of the Fiend races, decided to stand with the people of this world and aid the Saintess. Even though the strongest ones were no longer around, the remaining monsters still posed danger. It would be more reassuring if the three of them could cover one another’s backs. 

Ji Xiaoxi remained in the imperial court to concoct the medicine to curb her constitution. The ingredients were all in place now; all that was left was for her to work her expertise. 

Instead of using Rainbow Morph, Zu An and Pei Mianman rode on a cloud to the Ocean races. After reaching immortality, he'd naturally learned how to maneuver clouds. 

On the cloud, Zu An rested on Pei Mianman’s thighs while the latter massaged his temples. His exhaustion and sorrow were apparent to Pei Mianman, and it left her with a heavy heart. From their conversations, she learned about his interactions with the Goddess of Wu Mountain, but somehow, she was no longer jealous. Instead, she became curious about the other party. 

If she was truly my past incarnation, why would she have such a personality? How baffling.

Meanwhile, Zu An was feeling a little better thanks to Pei Mianman’s service. It felt as if he had been hurrying around nonstop and was finally able to catch a breather. Ah, such days are the best! 

“Ah Zu, why do you think my personality changed so much?” Pei Mianman suddenly asked.

Zu An knew she was asking about the Goddess of Wu Mountain, so he replied, “I don’t know. Could she have subconsciously channeled her personality toward another direction because I told her about you?”

Pei Mianman shook her head. “It’s possible for a person’s character to change, but not to that extent. I do have a thought in mind though…”

“What is it?” Zu An’s curiosity was piqued. 

“Forget it. You’ll laugh at me,” Pei Mianman replied after some thought. 

“So what is it?” Her refusal to speak only further stoked Zu An’s desire to know. 

“When you first talked about the Goddess of Wu Mountain, I thought she resembled Chuyan more than me,” Pei Mianman spoke in a much softer voice than before. “Is there a chance that she reincarnated not as me, but as… Chuyan?”

Zu An was startled. Her conjecture sounded credible. Putting their appearances aside, Yaoji’s personality is identical to Chuyan’s.

He was reminded of the suffering Chuyan had suffered under the Bridge of Helplessness, and it made his heart clench up. He couldn’t bring himself to continue lying on Pei Mianman’s thigh anymore. 

Pei Mianman noticed his expression and immediately regretted mentioning Chuyan, so she tried changing the topic. “I don’t think the possibility is big though, considering how our appearances are identical. It’s more likely that something huge happened to her, resulting in the change to her personality.” 

Zu An thought about the projection left by the Goddess of Wu Mountain, but her character seemed the same then. 

While discussing this matter, they arrived above the ocean before they knew it. Zu An dove into the ocean with Pei Mianman in his arms. The seawater naturally parted for him, and a flight of stairs even formed to welcome him. 

Pei Mianman was awed. Which woman can withstand such a romantic scene? No wonder Ah Zu has so many beautiful women chasing after him no matter where he goes!

She thought about how she had even gotten jealous of herself, and that evoked her laughter. 

The commotion on the ocean surface soon caught the Dragon Palace’s attention. The Four Dragon Kings led their men out to investigate the situation. They were initially tense, but upon seeing Zu An, they revealed relieved smiles. 

“Lord Necropolis, have you accomplished your mission?” the Dragon Kings asked as they bowed to Zu An, though they couldn’t help but notice that Zu An was accompanied by another beautiful woman on par with the Mermaid Queen. 

Pei Mianman was surprised by how the Ocean races addressed Zu An, but she didn’t expose him. 

“I’m done with most of it. I’ll be making a trip to the Mermaid race,” Zu An said grimly. 

The Four Dragon Kings exchanged looks, and they could see the worry in one another’s eyes. Could something bad have happened to the Mermaid Queen for him to return without her?

The Mermaid Queen was the Ocean races’ dream lover. Even the Four Dragon Kings harbored tremendous goodwill for her. How could they not grieve if something happened to her?

Soon, they arrived at the Mermaid race’s territory. The elders of the Mermaid race rushed out upon hearing the news. 

“Lord Necropolis, where is my queen? Why didn’t she return with you?” A maid rushed out from the crowd and grabbed Zu An’s sleeves. Her eyes reflected her trepidation. She was afraid that she would receive bad news.

She was the Mermaid Queen’s maid, Peach. She had been awaiting both their return, only for one to return alone. How could she not be horrified?

Zu An grimly replied, “I’m here to talk about that. Something has happened to the Mermaid Queen…”

A huge uproar broke out before he could finish speaking his piece.

“Is our queen dead?”

“How can that be? How can she be dead?”

… 

Most present were loyal fans of the Mermaid Queen. There were also women who were jealous of her, and they rejoiced at the news despite feigning sorrow. 

“Suyin isn’t dead yet, but she’s currently stuck in a palace and won’t be able to return for the time being,” Zu An explained. 

“What do you mean by that?” Peach anxiously asked. 

Zu An roughly explained the Mermaid Queen’s plight to the crowd, which prompted the crowd to raise their heads to look at the moon. The Mermaid Queen is on the moon?

Some thought to themselves that if she couldn’t return here ever again, the Mermaid Queen was as good as dead to them. 

Only Peach rejoiced at the news. There’s hope as long as my queen is alive!

Zu An continued, “It won’t do for the Mermaid race to be without a ruler. The Mermaid Queen has tasked me to relay her intention to pass down her position to the Shang clan.”

The Mermaid race didn’t question his words, especially since they already had the tradition of alternating the ruler position among their five major clans of Gong, Shang, Jiao, Zhi, and Yu. Gong Suyin had no offspring, and there was no one suitable in the Gong clan to take her position. It made sense for her to pass the throne on to the Shang clan.

Her edict made the succession more legitimate. 

Zu An felt dazed. 

They could have placed another member of the Gong clan on the throne, but Gong Suyin had still decided to pass the position on to the Shang clan while they were chatting in the Frost Palace. She appeared to have made that decision after learning that Shang Hongyu and Shang Liuyu were descendants of the future Mermaid Queen. 

Did the past determine the future, or did the future determine the past?

After settling the Mermaid race’s affairs, Zu An proceeded to share what had happened in the dungeon with the Four Dragon Kings. He also told them that most of the monsters had left the world, and thus, the world was finally at peace now. 

The Ocean races cheered at the news. They even declared that they would build a divine temple in the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons to commemorate his accomplishment. 

Zu An tried to dissuade them, but the Ocean races were adamant. They even said that they would work with the Asuras and the Shamans to build the most majestic palace ever. 

That left Zu An in a daze. Hold on, that palace I saw in the future was built for me?

After dealing with the Ocean races, Zu An made his way to the Netherworld. There were many questions in his mind that he needed someone to answer.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2615: Confrontation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2615: Confrontation

Pei Mianman requested to accompany him.

Zu An wanted to turn her down, since the death energy was extremely dangerous to the living, but on second thought, he decided to bring her along. He brought her to the Eye of the Ocean, where they were faced with the bleak abyss. Even Pei Mianman shuddered at the sight. 

“Don’t worry. I’m here.” Zu An wrapped his arm around her slender waist before leaping into the abyss. 

His words calmed Pei Mianman’s anxious heart. Indeed, as long as he’s by my side, I wouldn’t fear a mountain of blades or a sea of fire.

Grayish-white energy lingered in their surroundings as they descended the abyss. Zu An wrapped Pei Mianman with a brilliant light that shielded her from the energy. His cultivation was now strong enough to thoroughly protect his companion from harm. 

As soon as they entered the Netherworld, a massive army mounted on elephants rushed over to confront them. The men were hideous, but the women were gorgeous. 

“Who dares…” As soon as they recognized Zu An, the soldiers’ hostile faces lit up in delight. 

The beautiful woman at the army’s forefront leaped off her elephant and landed before Zu An. “Big brother Necropolis, you’re finally here! Hm? Who is she?”

She was the Asura race’s Princess Ni Huang. She had been anticipating Zu An’s return for quite some time now, only for the other party to return with another woman. She instinctively glanced at Pei Mianman’s chest, and her breath caught in her throat for several seconds. 

What a pair of lewd boobs! Hmph! She’s no fighter. I bet she’s baggage in battle!

While Princess Ni Huang was sizing up Pei Mianman, the latter did the same too. Princess Ni Huang was truly beautiful, especially when contrasted against the hideous Asura men standing around her. Her copper skin tone made her look vibrant, as if she was radiating energy itself. 

“Ah Zu, is it just me, or does she resemble Qinghe…” Pei Mianman discreetly asked Zu An via telepathy. Their facial features are identical, though compared to the youthful Murong Qinghe, this woman feels more mature. She also resembles Mo Xi a little…

She could tell that the latter fancied Zu An from Princess Ni Huang’s hostile gaze.

Ah Zu is too much! Why is he drawing in bees everywhere? First Chang’e, then the Goddess of Wu Mountain, and now some Princess Ni Huang too?

Zu An stood in a daze. His encounter with the Goddess of Wu Mountain had convinced him about the concept of reincarnation in this world. Murong Qinghe might be Princess Ni Huang’s future incarnation, and Mo Xi might be Princess Ni Huang’s past incarnation.

He had left Mo Xi in the Frost Palace, hoping that one day, she could grow strong enough to sever Gong Suyin’s contract with the Frost Palace. If Mo Xi has reincarnated, could it mean that something bad happened at the Frost Palace that resulted in her death? If so, something might have happened to Gong Suyin too.

Princess Ni Huang was initially angry, but her anger dissipated when she noticed Zu An staring at her in a daze. Sweetness tinged her heart. I knew he liked me more! Ahh, but there’s so many people looking at us. How embarrassing!

There was a cough, and the Asura King descended to their side. “Brother Necropolis, did everything go well?” I know my little sister is beautiful, but isn’t it too much for you to gawk at her when you already have a gorgeous babe with you? You’re putting me in a tight spot here!

Zu An finally snapped out of his daze. “Ah, it went fine.”

Loud rumbling echoed in the distance. Ox Head and Horse Face had rushed over upon hearing the commotion. 

When they saw Zu An, they quickly shrank their gigantic physiques and gave him a tight hug. “My brother, you’re incredible!”

They had already heard about the monsters’ eviction from this world, and they were awed by his prowess. 

Zu An smiled in response. “I hope you've been fine.”

“Peeps like us remain the same for centuries. It’s all fine,” Ox Head said with a laugh. He suddenly noticed Pei Mianman and exclaimed in bewilderment, “Hm? Why did the lady with you suddenly change? What happened to the Mermaid Queen you were previously with?”

Horse Face quietly kicked Ox Head. Can’t you tell that our Necropolis bro is a player? You shouldn’t be talking about his other lovers right now!

Ox Head quickly realized that and scratched his head awkwardly. He struggled to find the words to alleviate the atmosphere. 

Zu An held Pei Mianman’s hand and gently said, “She is Pei Mianman, my wife…”

Pei Mianman hadn’t expected him to introduce her as such in public. The word ‘wife’ filled her with indescribable bliss. 

She noticed Princess Ni Huang’s face turning pale and her eyes becoming glazed. Sighing softly, she put on a smile before grabbing the latter’s hand, saying, “You must be Princess Ni Huang. He often mentions you. You resemble a little sister of mine a lot.”

“Little sister?” A rush of curiosity dampened Princess Ni Huang’s sorrow. “I heard him mention something like that before. Who is it?”

Pei Mianman patiently told her about Murong Qinghe and Mo Xi, though she did reserve some information too. 

To others, it looked as if Pei Mianman accepted her husband’s philandering ways and even magnanimously extended her goodwill to Princess Ni Huang. This made them extremely awed by Zu An. Not only does he have a lot of lovers, but they're all so caring too. 

Zu An proceeded to tell them about what had happened to the Mermaid Queen too. The others were at a loss for words. 

Naturally, he didn’t forget about his goal here either. After a quick explanation, he went off to look for Lady Houtu. 

Soon, he arrived at the Reincarnation Platform. Ox Head and Horse Face kneeled and kowtowed. “Milady, Brother Necropolis has accomplished his mission and returned.”

A female silhouette soon appeared on the Reincarnation Platform, and a powerful aura rippled outward. 

Startled, Pei Mianman looked at Zu An with worried eyes. Why is there such a powerful expert here?

Zu An patted her hand to calm her down.

“Have you found the six races' power insignias?” The female silhouette nodded at Zu An.

“You could say that.” Zu An suddenly changed his tone. “But there are a few things I’d like to ask you.”

The female silhouette gestured to the crowd. “I need to talk to him in private. You may leave first.”

Ox Head and Horse Face respectfully took their leave.

The Asura King and Princess Ni Huang frowned. “Houtu, we aren’t your subordinates.”

The female silhouette wasn’t angered by their refusal to obey. “Asura King, you should know how dangerous some secrets are. There’s no benefit in knowing too much.”

A thought came to the Asura King’s mind, and he narrowed his eyes. With a harrumph, he grabbed his little sister’s hand and left the place. Before he walked out, he turned to Zu An and said, “Necropolis, just call for us if you need our help. We’ll rush in right away.”

“Thanks!” Zu An was thankful for the sentiment. The Asuras were fierce, but they were loyal.

After the others were gone, the female silhouette finally turned to Zu An and asked, “What do you want to know?”

“Were you the one who sent the Mermaid Queen and the others to that Celestial Court world?” Zu An asked coldly. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2616: Negotiation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2616: Negotiation

“It sounds as if you’re interrogating me,” Houtu replied with a voice so impassive that it no longer sounded human.

“Should I not be angry? She wouldn’t have been trapped in that world if you hadn’t sent her there,” Zu An replied in a severe tone. The Mermaid Queen had been faring perfectly well here, but Houtu’s intervention had caused her to be trapped in the Frost Palace as Chang’e.

“I was indeed the one who sent her back in time. It wasn't just her, but your other lovers too,” Houtu replied unhurriedly.

“Why did you do that?” Zu An was both perplexed and infuriated.

“You can think of it as their fate.” Houtu’s voice carried a mystical air. “These events are all inscribed in their fate.”

“But all this wouldn’t have happened if not for your intervention!” Zu An retorted. Even Jiang Luofu had gone missing in the Celestial Court world as well. 

“How do you know it’s not their fate?” Houtu returned the question.

Zu An was taken aback. Many thoughts flashed through his mind. A long time later, he slowly asked, “Can you see fate?”

Houtu slowly replied, “As the governor of reincarnation, I can vaguely see the trajectory of fate, but I’m not all-knowing. Often, it comes as a brief impulse, guiding me as to how I can achieve a certain goal.”

“I heard something similar from Zhuanxu. He said that time is an inconsequential concept to beings like him. He gave the ‘future’ me the Sun Slaying Bow so that the ‘past’ me could use it to shoot down the suns,” Zu An said as he tried to make sense of the situation. “I also met a Houtu in the Celestial Court world. She bestowed unto me a droplet of the Shaman races’ blood essence. Would it be right to say that you two are the same entity? That Houtu knows about me and the mission you entrusted me with.”

“Your understanding is too simple and riddled with errors, but given your current conceptualization of the world, you can indeed think of it that way.” Houtu was surprised that he could figure out this much. 

“Emperor Jun sought to attain Sainthood by vanquishing the Shaman races. Emperor Yao sought to attain Sainthood through the prosperity of the human race. All powerhouses aim to reach Sainthood in their own way. Now that I think about it, it’s probably the same for you.” Zu An looked at the powerful silhouette before him. “The only difference is that they failed, whereas you succeeded.” 

Pei Mianman blinked. Ever since her child days, she had always been complimented for her wits, so why was it that she couldn’t follow the conversation before her? I feel like a dimwit. 

“You’re half right. I did this to attain Sainthood, but I have yet to reach it,” Houtu candidly replied. 

Zu An looked unfazed. “You still need the things in my hand to forge the Six Paths of Reincarnation in order to attain Sainthood.”

Houtu’s way of attaining Sainthood was through the Six Paths of Reincarnation. This system allowed wandering ghosts to reincarnate, and it also encouraged individuals to do more good and commit less evil so that they could reincarnate into better lives. It went without saying that this was a vast contribution to the world. 

“You’re right. My Sainthood is founded on the Six Paths of Reincarnation.” Houtu eyed Zu An. “Are you thinking of blackmailing me through this?”

Zu An chuckled. “That is indeed what I have in mind. You need the Six Worlds’ power insignias to attain Sainthood. You can try taking them from me by force, but since you haven’t attained Sainthood yet, I have the power to resist.”

Pei Mianman tensed up. They sounded like friends earlier. Why does it sound as if they're going to come to blows? 

She was determined to stand with Zu An, so she secretly channeled her cultivation, ready to make a move at any moment. But this time, our enemy is an enigmatic silhouette. I wonder how I can hurt something like that.

“You forgot. You said it yourself. Time is inconsequential to beings such as us. The past me, the present me, and the future me are one entity. The future me has attained Sainthood. You don’t have the means to deal with the present me,” Houtu replied impassively, as if she was talking about something unrelated to her.

Zu An’s heart sank. He knew she was right, but he couldn’t give up. “You can’t forge the Six Paths of Reincarnation without the power insignias I've collected. That will hinder your path to Sainthood.”

“It looks as if you still have yet to understand the mechanisms of time and history. My attainment of Sainthood in the future is a fact, which means that it will happen with or without you. Someone may aid me in this task in the future. The trajectory of fate is beyond an individual’s means to alter,” Houtu replied.

Zu An thought about how Xihe had done everything she could to change the conclusion of the Ten Suns’ downfall, only for her attempts to end in vain. 

Mi Li had also once told him that important points in history, once determined, could no longer be changed. Houtu’s forging of the Six Paths of Reincarnation and attainment of Sainthood was one such important event that couldn’t change. 

But Zu An didn’t give up because of that. “Your Sainthood is inevitable, but you still aren’t a Saint yet. Someone else might help you collect the six power insignias if I refuse to cooperate with you here, but you risk losing a lot of time. Who knows how many more years it will take you to get there?”

Houtu shook her head. “You don’t understand beings such as us. What appears to be a long time to you humans is nothing more than a nap to us.”

“Perhaps I didn’t phrase it well enough. Let me put it in another way. If I don’t help you now, you may face many more variables in your path to Sainthood. Existences like you fear nothing more than variables, no?” Zu An asked calmly. “A Houtu will attain Sainthood in the future, but are you certain you will be that Houtu?”

He was using the knowledge he'd learned from the Ten Suns’ incident. Important points in history couldn’t be changed, but a slight change in the process could make a world of difference in the nuances, such as him becoming Yi in the past. 

Houtu showed a rare emotional fluctuation. “I have to say that you’re an intelligent human. You have convinced me. Speak, what do you want?” Human politics had little meaning to beings like her. She knew what Zu An was after.

“Help me save the Mermaid Queen and Jiang Luofu,” Zu An said. He could have simply requested her help, but he'd been worried that Houtu wouldn't give it her all since it didn’t concern her.

Houtu frowned. “Ask for something else. What you’re asking for is beyond my means.”

Zu An grabbed Pei Mianman and turned around to leave. “You can find someone else to collect your six power insignias then.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2617: Predetermined Fate
chapters.body:
Chapter 2617: Predetermined Fate

“Wait!” Unsurprisingly, Houtu quickly called for him. “You would be helping yourself by helping me too.”

“What do you mean by that?” Zu An asked.

“Too much time has passed since that era, not to mention the Mermaid Queen is imprisoned in the Frost Palace. I couldn’t help you right now even if I wanted to,” Houtu patiently explained.

“What about Jiang Luofu? You were the one who sent her into the past. Don’t tell me you can’t do anything about that either.” Zu An suppressed his anger as he negotiated. 

“Jiang Luofu’s strong emotions caught the attention of an exalted entity. That’s her fate, as well as her fortuitous encounter. You don’t need to worry about her.” Houtu’s voice quivered when she mentioned the words ‘exalted entity’.

Zu An recalled hearing similar words from Zhuanxu. Looks like she isn’t in any danger for now. Still, he asked, “Who is that entity?”

“You should already be aware that secrets are dangerous. You aren’t strong enough yet. Learning about that entity right now will only result in your destruction.”

Zu An fell silent. He knew about that rule, and he wasn’t a reckless brute either, so he didn’t continue harping on this matter. Instead, he changed the topic and asked, “Is that entity stronger than you and the Celestial Emperors?”

Houtu nodded. “His strength not only surpasses ours, but Saints too. This is our first time learning about beings stronger than Saints.”

Zu An was speechless. Sainthood was usually the peak for most myths and webnovels. He hadn’t expected there to be an even higher level than that. 

He suddenly thought about the Time Scribe and the information he'd received from Mi Li, and a thought popped into his mind. 

There must be countless worlds in the universe.

Entities like Houtu, even after attaining Sainthood, only command influence within the Six Worlds. Other worlds don’t necessarily recognize her, or there could be other deities in the same position as her.

The stronger entities could be some kind of conceptual god that exists across all worlds in the universe. 

This thought sparked the fighting spirit in Zu An. Now that he had a goal in mind, his thoughts suddenly felt clearer than ever. He then asked, “Why bother stopping me when you can’t do anything to help me?”

“To help me is to help yourself.” Houtu got straight to the point. “If you help me in the forging of the Six Paths of Reincarnation, I can use its power to help the Mermaid Queen…”

She paused a second and glanced at Pei Mianman. “...As well as the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s incarnation.

“Reincarnation has to be impartial; it is bound to the laws of the world. What I can do here undermines the system. My main body will suffer a severe backlash because of it, but considering how much you have helped me, I am willing to pay this price.”

Pei Mianman’s heart skipped a beat. She had been wondering if the Goddess of Wu Mountain was truly her previous incarnation, and whether it could have been a coincidence that they resembled each other. Their personalities were vastly different, after all. 

But Houtu’s glance appeared to suggest otherwise. 

Zu An said grimly, “But they're different people to me. What you’re offering means nothing to the Goddess of Wu Mountain of that era.”

“You need to widen your perspective. Once you’re on a higher level, you’ll come to realize that a person’s appearance, personality, and experiences may change when reincarnated, but their core remains the same. They are still the same person.”

Zu An frowned. He didn’t agree with Houtu’s perception, which conflated two different people into a single entity. 

Sensing his indignation, Houtu added, “You need to think it through. What will become of your current lovers if you refuse to let them reincarnate? Do you wish for them to cease to exist?”

“What?!” Zu An was taken aback to hear that. He instinctively glanced at Pei Mianman, only to see her usual reddened face looking a little pale. 

“You don’t have to feel so conflicted. A few things happened in that era afterward. Their only option was to reincarnate,” Houtu added. 

“What happened?” Judging from the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s projection, Zu An had realized that a major incident had to have happened in that era, but the latter refused to elaborate on it. Houtu would no doubt know more about the matter. 

Houtu shook his head. “Very coincidentally, I lost a portion of my memories and don’t remember what happened there. I only vaguely recall there being a major disaster.”

Zu An was startled. Even an entity like Houtu can suffer from memory loss? Did she suffer an injury in that disaster?

He thought about how the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain had waited for countless years in vain for him, and eventually faced such a terrifying disaster by themselves. That made his heart ache. He asked, “Was it caused by the war between the Shamans and the Fiends?”

“I don’t think so. The war between the Shamans and the Fiends was a huge calamity, but it ended in mutual destruction for both sides, and they retreated from the front lines together. The human race began to prosper afterward. The two events should be unrelated,” Houtu deduced.

Zu An also remembered the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s projection mentioning that she had waited over tens of thousands of years, during which there hadn’t seemed to be any major disaster.

“I have offered you a solution, and this is the most I can do as well. Now, it’s just a question of whether you want to accept the deal or not. In the worst case, I’ll just take another nap and wait for the next person who is willing to help me. You should know that I succeeded in forging the Six Paths of Reincarnation in history,” Houtu calmly pointed out.

Time indeed had little meaning to an entity like her.

Zu An pondered hard, and he was forced to concede that her words made sense.  He had no idea what disaster had happened in that era, but the only thing he could do for the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain was to prepare a way out for them. Thus, he took out the power insignias.

Houtu’s expression turned serious. She moved her hands around, creating a swirling vortex. This vortex was much smaller than the Eye of the Ocean, but it looked far darker and deeper. Intense death energy could be felt from it, but it carried hints of overflowing life energy too. 

Houtu flicked her sleeves, and the six power insignias floated toward six different directions. They extended a ray of light toward the vortex, filling it with six different colors.

Zu An and Pei Mianman saw flickers of countless worlds in each of the colors. This was a sign that the Six Paths of Reincarnation was establishing its connection with the countless worlds in the Six Paths. 

Houtu said, “I am about to be assimilated into the Six Paths of Reincarnation. You have accrued tremendous karma in aiding me in the construction of the Six Paths of Reincarnation. The will of the world will remember this. After the Six Paths of Reincarnation are formed, the Netherworld will be required for their functioning. You are fated to be the master of the Netherworld. Are you willing to take on this role, Necropolis Emperor?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2618: Vow
chapters.body:
Chapter 2618: Vow

The mention of the words ‘Necropolis Emperor’ left Zu An in a daze. For a moment there, he felt as if he was fulfilling his destiny.

In the future, he'd stepped into the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons, entered the Netherworld, and obtained the Necropolis Emperor’s legacy. But now, he had returned to the past. In order to not influence the timeline, he'd decided on a whim to use ‘Necropolis’ as his alias. Little had he expected to become the Necropolis Emperor in history.

In other words, I'm my own successor? 

Even the most dimwitted person could feel the inexplicable pull of fate here.

“I never expected myself to become the Necropolis Emperor. I wonder what happens to the Necropolis Emperor in the future,” Zu An remarked. By the time he inherited the other party’s legacy, the Necropolis Emperor had already gone missing. That mystery made him hesitant to take on this position.

But rejection wasn't an option. The world needed the Netherworld and the Necropolis Emperor to ensure the functioning of the Six Paths of Reincarnation. There was no better candidate than him right now. 

Sensing his thoughts, Houtu said, “Don’t worry about being bound to the identity of the Necropolis Emperor. You are the Necropolis Emperor, but the Necropolis Emperor doesn’t have to be you.”

Zu An narrowed his eyes. “What do you mean?”

“I can’t reveal the secrets of the future.” Houtu didn’t elaborate. Her silhouette began to thin as specks of light flew out of her and into the Six Paths of Reincarnation. The Six Paths radiated a sacred glow—that was the amalgamation of life and death. It slowly began to spin. Despite its smaller size, it felt vast and enigmatic like the stars in the sky. 

A peculiar resonance rippled outward, and the Netherworld began to change. The tangible, grayish-white death energy greatly thinned, revealing the previously concealed Oblivion River and Yin Mountain. The terrain also began to terraform, collapsing and rebuilding itself. Everything soon began to resemble the Netherworld Zu An remembered in his memories. 

Upon noticing the commotion, Ox Head, Horse Face, and the Asuras rushed in. With terrified faces, they asked, “What happened?”

“Lady Houtu has become one with the cycle of reincarnation.” Zu An summarized what had happened earlier, and that caused the Shaman race to burst into tears. 

Since their defeat in the war between the Shamans and the Fiends, they had been hiding in this dark place. Ancestral Shaman Houtu was their final pillar of support. Now that even Houtu was gone, how could they not suffer a mental breakdown?

Zu An consoled them, “You need not grieve. Lady Houtu isn’t dead yet. She’s merely living in another form. She has already attained Sainthood and became one with the world. You should be happy for her.”

Despite his words, he didn’t know whether it was an evolution or a death for a living being to turn into the unfeeling Six Paths of Reincarnation. He dispelled those thoughts from his mind and continued, “Lady Houtu has planned a way out for you. I’ll be establishing the Netherworld here to govern ghosts from different worlds and facilitate the Six Paths of Reincarnation. You'll become deities of the Netherworld and enjoy the worship of the mortal world.”

The knowledge that they could become deities assuaged the Shamans’ sorrow. Ox Head and Horse Face bowed to Zu An. “Lord Necropolis, Lady Houtu has also told us about this matter. We’re under your command now!”
