“You’re being too polite.” Zu An supported them upright before turning to the Asuras. “Asura King, Princess Ni Huang, once the Netherworld is established, I’ll be able to use the Six Paths of Reincarnation to open a path back to the Asura World for you.”

The Asura King voiced his doubts. “Won’t we be reincarnated if we travel through the Six Paths of Reincarnation?”

Zu An explained, “I don’t mean for you to reincarnate through the Six Paths of Reincarnation. Rather, I plan to use the Six Paths of Reincarnation’s power to open a corresponding world. Given how you have the complete Asura World’s power insignia, it shouldn’t be too hard.”

A thought suddenly popped into his mind. Can’t I use this power to open the Celestial Court world and look for the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain?

He quickly gave up on that thought, however.

The Asura King had the complete Asura World’s power insignia. It remained mostly intact even after Houtu used it in the forging of the Six Paths of Reincarnation. That was why Zu An could use the Six Paths of Reincarnation’s power, with the Asura World’s power insignia as a medium, to open up a passageway to the Asura World.

In contrast, he had only received a fragment of the Celestial World’s power insignia from Zhuanxu, and it had been completely consumed for the forging of the Six Paths of Reincarnation. Without the Celestial World’s power insignia, he wouldn’t be able to open a passageway to the Celestial World.

That filled him with regret. I should have stolen the complete power insignia from Zhuanxu!

Of course, that could only remain a thought. Zhuanxu, as a Celestial Emperor, could easily crush him just by the sheer disparity in their cultivation. Attempting to steal the Celestial World’s power insignia from Zhuanxu would have been tantamount to suicide. 

The Asuras cheered in delight. Only Princess Ni Huang appeared to be brooding. 

For the next few days, Zu An drafted up the Netherworld’s blueprints based on his memories. Fortunately, he didn’t need to draft it out comprehensively on paper the way architects had in his previous life. All he needed to do was to create a projection to show the buildings. 

The Shaman races enthusiastically commenced construction. Their superior strength allowed them to build with exceptional efficiency.

The Asura race also lent a helping hand for the construction. They worked with the Shaman races to erect a massive sculpture at the entrance of the Netherworld. 

Even the Ocean races cooperated with them to construct a complementary palace for the sculpture in the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons. 

Zu An was embarrassed, but the Asura King, Ox Head, and Horse Face insisted he needed a sculpture to emphasize his exalted standing. Besides, the sculpture had the pragmatic effect of encouraging faith and intimidating troublemakers. Knowing that the sculpture existed in history, he reluctantly accepted it. 

Later, however, it suddenly struck him that he'd forgotten to instruct them on the sculpture’s design. By the time he checked on the sculpture, he realized that it didn’t have distinctive facial features, the same way he remembered it in history. 

He asked the Asura King about it, and the latter replied with a chuckle, “It was my little sister’s idea. She said a deity’s appearance shouldn’t be casually revealed, and we thought her explanation made sense. She was the one who designed this sculpture.”

After explaining the sculpture, the Asura King took his leave to give Zu An some private time with Princess Ni Huang,

“Thank you, princess.” Zu An could tell that much thought had been put into the majestic sculpture. 

“You’re still being so polite with me,” Princess Ni Huang remarked with conflicted eyes. She sighed softly before continuing on, “Big brother Necropolis, I wanted to stay here with you, but I’m a member of the Asura royal family. I enjoyed a privileged life, growing up with luxuries and the best cultivation resources. I can’t abandon my clan when its fate is uncertain. I have to aid my royal brother in settling the new world. Do you blame me for that?”

“No, I only respect you more. A woman who doesn’t lose herself to emotions will only charm more men,” Zu An replied.

Princess Ni Huang was delighted to hear that. She patted his shoulder and said, “As expected of the man I've set my eyes on. Once things in the new world settle down, I’ll return to look for you.”

Zu An briefly hesitated before revealing his true background. “Actually, I'm not from this world.”

“I guessed as much.” Princess Ni Huang wasn’t disappointed to hear that. “Can you tell me your true identity?”

Looking into her sincere eyes, Zu An chose to divulge his identity to her. 

Princess Ni Huang murmured his name to etch it into her mind. All of a sudden, she leaned in and kissed his lips before fleeing. “Big brother Zu, I’ll definitely find you in the future!”

Shortly after she left, Pei Mianman popped out of nowhere and remarked, “You can keep her with you if you can’t bear to part ways with her.”

Zu An shook his head as he looked at Princess Ni Huang’s departing figure. “She has her own mission to fulfill.”

Pei Mianman sighed. “I used to think that you’re a horrible philanderer who went around seducing women, but your life seems pretty tough too.”

“Manman, I’m glad you finally understand my struggles!”

“Don’t push your luck!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2619: Asura’S Tear
chapters.body:
Chapter 2619: Asura’S Tear

Strong people weren't lacking among the Shamans, the Asuras, and the Ocean races. They built structures even more quickly than the modern world's technology could manage. What they struggled with was formation designs.

Fortunately, the three races had a strong foundation, and Zu An was adept in formations. It took time, but they gradually perfected each of the formations.

As their work approached completion, however, Princess Ni Huang seemed to smile less.

Soon, it was time for them to part ways. Zu An used a secret art to channel the Six Paths of Reincarnation’s power to open a passageway to the Asura World, allowing all the Asuras to return home.

Princess Ni Huang was the last to leave. The Asura King sighed from the passageway and urged her, “Ni Huang, we should get going.”

Princess Ni Huang lowered her head and replied with a “Mm”. All of a sudden, she rushed toward Zu An and gave him a tight hug. Feeling a wet sensation on his shoulder, Zu An’s body stiffened up a little. In the end, he raised his hands to gently embrace her back. 

Pei Mianman displayed no jealousy at that sight. If anything, she only felt wistful. After what had happened with the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain, she realized that there was no point getting jealous of lovers who were separated across spacetime. 

“Ni Huang, the passageway is about to close,” the Asura King anxiously urged. Opening a passageway between worlds was bound to require a tremendous amount of resources. It had taken a lot of time for the other Asuras to cross the passageway; the chances were that Zu An was already reaching his limit. 

“Don’t forget me!” Princess Ni Huang said as she wiped off her tears and ran after her brother. Shortly after she entered the passageway, its entrance closed.

Ox Head and Horse Face walked up to Zu An’s side and said, “The Asuras pride themselves on being a warrior race. They see courage and strength as a virtue. Tears are a symbol of weakness for the Asuras, which is why they never cry except in one scenario.”

“What scenario?” Zu An asked worriedly as he recalled Princess Ni Huang’s earlier tears. 

“An Asura woman crying for her lover. They only cry if their feelings for their lover have reached an extreme, to the point of causing them great anguish.” Ox Head and Horse Face were awed. Zu An and Ni Huang had only known each other for a short time, but he had already charmed the Asura race’s princess to this degree.

Zu An’s heart clenched. He fell silent for a long time before Pei Mianman walked to his side and gently grasped his hand. Her consolation brought some warmth back to his icy-cold body. 

Following that, Zu An selected the Ten Yamas[1] and established all kinds of roles for the Shamans to take on. He couldn’t stay here for long, so he needed to make sure everything was in place to ensure that the Netherworld could run on its own. 

At the same time, he wondered why all the ghost guards were missing when the future him arrived at the Netherworld. What happened to them, and where did they go? The more he learned, the more insignificant he felt. There were simply too many things that he was utterly helpless about.

Nevertheless, he still had to try his best. He looked for Ox Head and Horse Face and subtly reminded them to leave a message in the vicinity of the Six Paths of Reincarnation if something huge happened to the Netherworld in the future. In the Netherworld, other than the Oblivion River and Yin Mountain, the Six Paths of Reincarnation were likely the most resistant to the erosion of time. 

Ox Head and Horse Face were confused, but they still thumped their chests and gave their word. Zu An made a mental note to check the Six Paths of Reincarnation for information once he returned to his timeline.

Last but not least, he slipped the new World Law Beacon into the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons' palace. He looked at the Mermaid Queen sculpture that was based off Gong Suyin and solemnly bade her farewell. 

Everything was finally in place. He turned down the Shamans' and Ocean races' offer to stay and began making his way back to the Fiend races’ imperial court. 

While riding on a cloud, Zu An asked the accompanying beauty, “Manman, you seem much quieter in recent days. Is there something on your mind?”

“Too much has happened recently. I’m feeling wistful.” Pei mianman leaned on Zu An’s shoulder while hugging his arm. “After seeing what happened to the others, I can’t help but wonder if we’ll face such a separation in the future too.”

“Let’s cherish every day.” The past Zu An would have promised to protect her well, but after everything he had been through, he'd learned that there were too many things in the world that were beyond his ability to change. 

Pei Mianman nodded. Without any warning, she leaned in and kissed him.

There had been too many eyes on them in the past few days, be it in the Netherworld or the Dragon Palace, and it didn’t help that they'd had their hands full with work too. The two of them had hardly enjoyed any private time together. The two of them had too many pent-up emotions that they urgently needed to vent. 

There was no need for words; they were too familiar with each other. It didn’t take long for them to be glued together. 

“It’s broad daylight. Will anyone see us?”

“Don’t worry. We’re in the sky.”

“What if some powerful cultivators pass by or look toward the sky?”

“No wonder you’re more sensitive than usual. So that’s what you’re worried about!”

“Hey! Don’t talk nonsense!”

… 

A cloud in the sky shook intensely for a while. Pei Mianman exclaimed worriedly, “Will this cloud break apart? You need to be gentler!”

“Don’t worry, it won’t break apart.”

… 

Looking at the red spider lily tattoo that had appeared on Pei Mianman's lower back due to a rush of blood, Zu An asked, “You often went into a daze whenever we traveled through the Yellow Spring. Were you looking at the red spider lilies?”

Zu An had been busy putting the Netherworld into order, and Pei Mianman had been serving as his secretary. However, in her free time, she had sat by the Yellow Spring and stare dazedly at the red spider lilies. 

“Why are you saying this now… I just found the red spider lilies familiar, and it sparked some inspiration.”

“Oh? What kind of inspiration?”

“It’s hard to put it in words. The Paramita Butterfly I acquired seems intricately linked to the Netherworld’s red spider lilies, but I still don’t know what that black butterfly is…” 

Pei Mianman suddenly let out an exclamation—Zu An had interrupted her reply. It quickly dawned on her that Zu An wasn’t sincere about those questions either. She felt so frustrated that she clawed him like a little kitten. 

… 

Xie Daoyun and the others had already gotten anxious from waiting by the time they returned to the Fiend races’ imperial court. Upon seeing the two of them, they breathed a sigh of relief. 

“We received news that you were returning, but you were nowhere to be seen even after waiting a while. We thought you encountered danger along the way.”

“I told you. There’s nothing in this world that can threaten big brother Zu now.”

“Hm? Are you sick, Manman? Why is your face so red? Xiaoxi, you should take a look at her.”

… 

Pei Mianman was horrified. She would die of shame if Ji Xiaoxi saw through her. 

At the most crucial moment, however, Zu An stepped forward and rescued her. “Now that our business here is settled, it’s about time for us to return to our world.”

1. The ten great kings of hell. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2620: Reunion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2620: Reunion

The other ladies reined in their teasing smiles and solemnly replied, “All right.”

They had first arrived in this ancient spacetime fragment before being transported into the even older Celestial Court world. So many things had happened that it felt as if they had already lived a lifetime. 

Only Ji Xiaoxi was hesitant. “What about my little aunt?”

Zu An patted her head and consoled her, “Don’t worry, I checked with Houtu. Big sister principal isn’t in danger. This is a fortuitous encounter for her.”

That lessened Ji Xiaoxi’s worry. 

The others asked Zu An about how his trip to the ocean had gone, so Zu An shared the story with them. In the meantime, the other ladies dragged Pei Mianman aside and asked her if Zu An had encountered any other beauties in the ocean. 

Pei Mianman immediately thought of Princess Ni Huang, but she decided against revealing that. “No, his heart is too hurt to romance anyone else.”

She figured it wasn’t a lie. For instance, the Peacock Princess had visited the Dragon Palace several times in search of Zu An, but the latter had avoided her by seeking refuge in the Netherworld. He probably doesn’t want her to become fixated on him.

The Peacock Princess had figured out his intentions after missing him several times and spent days crying in the Dragon Palace. She'd looked so pitiful that some from the Ocean races secretly tried to speak up on her behalf. However, Zu An had still refused to meet her. In the end, the Peacock Princess could only leave with a broken heart. 

Pei Mianman had tried persuading Zu An to meet the Peacock Princess, but he was too hurt to consider the Peacock Princess, and the last thing he'd wanted to do was to hinder her happiness. 

… 

Zu An asked Xie Daoyun, “Are you done settling things on the Fiend races’ side?”

Xie Daoyun nodded. “We cleared out the remaining monsters. The Fiend races have reclaimed their previous territories.”

She even meticulously left a projection in the Sealed Land for the future Zu An to find so as to close the time loop.

“Will there be any problem if you, the Saintess, suddenly disappear?” Zu An was worried.

“Don’t worry, the Fiend races have their imperial family. Previously, we had no choice but to band together to deal with the monsters, but now that the external threat has been eliminated, my prestige as Yumen Beiqing would only stoke the imperial family’s wariness. That could lead to a series of complications.” Xie Daoyun, as the wise Saintess of this era, could tell as much.

Yun Yuqing harrumphed. “What use do we have for the imperial family? The Fiends only survived this crisis thanks to yours and Ah Zu’s efforts. I’d say you’re far more qualified to become their empress! Most people in the Fiend races would support you. You can take big brother Zu as your consort afterward…”

The furiously blushing Xie Daoyun clasped Yun Yuqing’s mouth and said, “Shush! That wouldn’t do. I would be changing history if I did that!”

Yun Yuqing’s eyes rolled mischievously. “Oh~ So you’re only worried about changing history. That means you do harbor such thoughts in mind too. Ahh, stop tickling me!”

The pleasant sight of two beauties bantering brought a smile to Zu An’s face. His gloomy mood lifted a little.  

While Xie Daoyun made her last preparations, Zu An took the chance to visit the Firecloud Cave. He'd recently remembered the Firecloud Cave, and he had this uncanny feeling that the mysterious person residing there had been luring him to the ancient Celestial Court world. He wanted to get to the bottom of the matter, as well as figure out the other party’s relationship with the Human Emperor. 

But when he reached the Purple Ganoderma Cliff, he didn’t see anyone. The Firecloud Cave had also disappeared into thin air. 

As he wondered who he had met back then, Zu An allowed the furious winds to batter him. Somehow, it felt as if an invisible hand was controlling him, as if he was a piece on a chessboard.

He tried tapping into the influence of the Fiend races, the Ocean races, and the Netherworld, but he was unable to gather any intelligence about the Firecloud Cave. It was also then that the Nature Jade Plate began to pulsate. Their time here was finally coming to an end. 

The Nature Jade Plate should have lost its divine power and turned into a normal stone when they entered this spacetime fragment. However, perhaps due to the special ki in the ancient Celestial Court world, it had amazingly recovered its energy. There had been sufficient energy to not only transport them back, but even to activate again afterward. For a moment, Zu An An even wondered if the Nature Jade Plate was planning to send them to the Celestial Court world once again. 

The Nature Jade Plate shone, and Zu An’s group was transported away from the spacetime fragment into another world. Feeling the thinner ki in the surroundings and the connection with the World Law Beacon, Zu An immediately realized that they had returned to their original Cultivation World. 

The light in the Nature Jade receded, and it turned back into an ordinary stone container. 

Zu An couldn’t help but wonder when the Nature Jade Plate had recharged in the Celestial Court world. If I can find a way to recharge the Nature Jade Plate, can I return to the Celestial Court world again?

“We’re finally back!” Yun Yuqing could tell that they were in the vicinity of the Demon race’s altar.

The other ladies were also overjoyed to see the familiar scenery. This was, after all, their home world. 

Zu An could feel everything reverting to normal. He had already re-established his connection with this world’s power insignia. The artifacts he had given away, such as the Oblivion River Water and the Treasure Sinking Coin, returned to his hands. His Human Emperor Seal also regained its powers as the Human Path's power insignia. 

He was awed by the effects of a closed time loop. If he could get to the bottom of this phenomenon, wouldn’t he be able to control time? After what had happened to the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain, that was the power he desired the most now. 

Someone had been guarding the Demon race’s altar, so it didn’t take long for the different clans of the Demon race to rush over. “Welcome back, regent!”

Zu An wasn’t in the mood to deal with this, so he left Yun Yuqing and the others to answer the Demon race’s questions and headed elsewhere himself. 

Pei Mianman had an idea of his destination. “You go ahead. We’ll return by ourselves.” 

After Zu An left, the other ladies curiously asked Pei Mianman, “Where is he heading to?”

“The Dragon Palace, of course,” Pei Mianman replied with a sigh.

The other ladies nodded in realization. 

On a small rocky island, a red and blue mermaid played the zither while fooling around. Their slender fish tails gently flicked the water, producing ripples reminiscent of pearls. 

“Little sister, what’s with that glum face? Are you still worried about that fellow?”

“He is strong and cunning. I’m sure he can resolve any crisis that comes his way.”

“Why do you look so glum then?”

“I don’t know either. I have been dreaming a lot recently, but when I wake up, I can never remember what happened in the dream. All I remember is a feeling of coldness and loneliness.”

The red mermaid was startled. She quickly swam to the blue mermaid’s side and placed her hand on the latter’s forehead. “Are you ill? Or did someone hex you? That shouldn’t be. The Dragon Palace’s defense formations are thorough.”

“Don’t worry, I haven’t been hexed. It’s just a dream.” Shang Liuyu clutched her chest. Even though she couldn’t remember the dream anymore, the thought of it left her with an inexplicable heartache. 

Shang Hongyu exhaled in relief. “Your worries in the day must have manifested in your dreams. Haa, I wonder when that heartless guy is coming back. But again, even if he returns, he has so many lovers that I doubt he’d look for us right away.”

Shang Liuyu turned around. The familiar figure before her eyes made her stiffen up.

Looking at Shang Liuyu’s familiar face, Zu An could no longer suppress the raging emotions within him. He rushed forward and embraced her tightly. “It’s so good to meet you again!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2621: What To Do When Your Lover Is Sad
chapters.body:
Chapter 2621: What To Do When Your Lover Is Sad

Shang Liuyu initially tensed up in response to Zu An's tight embrace, but his familiar scent caused her to relax quickly. She had been feeling inexplicably melancholic and lonely in recent days, but for some reason, when he embraced her, those emotions dissipated into thin air. She raised her arms and returned the embrace.

A long time later, sloshing sounds suddenly echoed from the side. Shang Hongyu was slapping the waves out of boredom.

“Oi, are you done hugging yet?” Shang Hongyu had initially been overjoyed to see Zu An’s return, but the latter rushed to hug her little sister right away. 

She'd initially consoled herself by inwardly saying that those two knew each other first, and she would be next once they were done hugging. To her frustration, those two had refused to let go of each other. Zu An showed no inclination to hug her at all, stoking her jealousy. We’re supposed to be the closer ones!

Startled, Shang Liuyu pushed Zu An away. Her face was so red that it resembled the evening afterglow. Even though she had feelings for Zu An, they had yet to reach that point yet, not to mention there was someone else around. She felt embarrassed. 

Zu An looked at Shang Hongyu and recalled that she was likely to be Xihe’s reincarnation. Unable to hold back his feelings, he rushed over and hugged her too. 

“Hey hey hey! You only remember me now. I don’t want this kind of charity.” Shang Hongyu pouted in dissatisfaction.

“It’s so good to meet you again,” Zu An said.

“Using the same line twice. How insincere.” Despite her grumbling, Shang Hongyu could still feel Zu An’s overflowing emotions from his embrace. Her expression slowly became gentler. 

A while later, it became Shang Liuyu’s turn to feel uncomfortable. She couldn’t help but remember how Zu An and Shang Hongyu had already gotten intimate before, whereas she felt like an outsider here. 

Thus, she stood up and said, “You two chat. I’ll be heading off first.”

“Ey, why are you leaving?” Shang Hongyu anxiously stopped her. It feels as if I’m chasing her away. This is so weird. 

Zu An also reached out to grab Shang Liuyuu's waist and pulled her toward his body. “Don’t go. We've already been apart for far too long.”

He hugged her tightly. Just remembering the parting they had in the Celestial Court world was enough to fill his heart with pain. 

The two sisters exchanged looks. They could sense each other’s awkwardness. They were close sisters, and they were both in love with Zu An. However, it still felt terribly awkward for their lover to embrace the two of them at the same time. They thought of struggling free, but they saw the pain on their lover’s face and their hearts shuddered.

Both of them asked in unison, “Ah Zu, what happened?” 

“It’s nothing. Let me hug you a while longer. Just a while is enough…” It hurt Zu An each time he had to recount what happened at the Celestial Court world. His heart was already filled with holes.

Shang Hongyu looked at her little sister worriedly. She had never seen him like that before. Shang Liuyu was worried as well, and asked her older sister what they should do with her gaze.

Shang Hongyu didn’t say a word. She gently wrapped Zu An’s body with her beautiful red tail and rested her body on his. “Ah Zu, we’ll be with you no matter what happens.”

Shang Liuyu felt terribly embarrassed, but she also embraced Zu An and said, “Yes, we’ll always be with you.”

Zu An sighed. The Mermaid Queen and Xihe had wanted to be with him too, but there were far too many things that weren’t within their control. 

Sensing his low spirits, the two sisters became even more worried. Something must have happened in the spacetime fragment for him to act like that, but what could it be?

Shang Hongyu suddenly blinked as a smile formed on her lips. “Ah Zu, let’s make a bet. You’ll have to tell me what happened if I can lift your spirits.”

Zu An chuckled. “Just seeing the two of you is enough to raise my spirits.”

“Tsk, how insincere!” Shang Hongyu noticed her little sister’s confused gaze. “Little sister, let me teach you a trick to raise your lover’s spirits.”

Shang Liuyu immediately perked up like a diligent student. She watched as Shang Hongyu straightened her posture and pinned up her wavy hair. Much to her confusion, the latter suddenly dove into the water.

What she witnessed next shocked her. She quickly averted her gaze with a reddened face. 

Zu An gasped. He gadn’t expected Shang Hongyu to be so bold as to do it in front of Shang Liuyu, though he had to admit that it was extremely effective. It was hard for him to remain in low spirits in such a situation. 

Shang Liuyu tried to leave out of embarrassment, but her hand and waist were being firmly held by Shang Hongyu and Zu An respectively. She bit her lips as her face heated up. These two are really a match made in heaven!

Shang Hongyu, as a mermaid, was capable of breathing in water. The way her beautiful tail fin moved in the water was truly mesmerizing. 

“Wuuuu!” Shang Hongyu pinched her little sister as she whispered something to the latter. 

Shang Liuyu pretended not to understand her words. I’m not going to join my big sister in this madness!

To conceal her awkwardness, Shang Liuyu hurriedly changed the topic. “Ah Zu, can you tell us what happened now?”

Zu An was conflicted. It wasn't a problem for him to share the story, but doing it under such circumstances felt weird. However, considering how the two sisters had already gone this far to console him, he felt that it wouldn’t be right for him to not speak up. Thus, he told them what had happened in the spacetime fragment.

The two sisters had weathered countless storms with Zu An, but they were still shocked when they heard about what happened in the spacetime fragment and the Celestial Court world. In particular, they were left with complex feelings when they learned about Xihe and the Mermaid Queen.

Just as they were reaching an entertaining part of the story, Shang Hongyu suddenly stood up with a red tinge on her face. She rubbed her cheeks as she directed a begrudging look at Zu An. “My cheeks are getting numb…”

She nudged Shang Liuyu. “You do it. I’m totally exhausted.”

“Ah? Me?” Shang Liuyu was flabbergasted. This sort of thing… 

Even though Zu An didn’t think that the usually poised Shang Liuyu would agree, he'd gotten so riled up by Shang Hongyu that he couldn’t help but look at her with hopeful eyes. 

Shang Liuyu’s heart beat furiously as her mind went blank. However, Shang Hongyu went ahead to pull her over, not giving her time to think. 

Shang Liuyu ultimately still couldn’t bring herself to do what Shang Hongyu had done. Instead, she raised her slender hand and said, “I… I’ll help you like that…”

Shang Hongyu knew better than to push Shang Liuyu too far. She first guided Shang Liuyu’s hand to its destination before she settled down beside Zu An and smiled. “Tell me, Ah Zu. Does the Sun Goddess really resemble me? Which of us do you think looks better?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2622: The Method To A Reunion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2622: The Method To A Reunion

“You two resemble each other greatly, but you’re more beautiful.” Zu An conceded that Shang Hongyu’s ‘healing method’ was very effective. The nearby water was as clear as glass, and he was in the company of two beautiful mermaids who gently stirred the water with their fins. It was a truly pleasant scene. 

No man could have remained in low spirits under these two women’s ‘consolation’. 

Shang Hongyu harrumphed. “Lies. I bet you’d say something different in front of that Xihe.”

Zu An took a sharp breath of air. Shang Liuyu had never done anything like this before, so her movements were still very rigid. Fortunately, her hands were very soft and beautiful, making it a different kind of experience. “Hongyu, you misunderstand me. Xihe was the Sun Goddess and the Celestial Empress. She was beautiful, but she was also fierce and domineering. She was nowhere as gentle and caring as you.”

“That’s more like it.” Shang Hongyu’s beautiful eyes twinkled with glee, but she quickly put on a stern look once more. “Even so, you just said that Xihe is my previous incarnation. Aren’t you saying bad things about my past self then? I feel like I ought to get angry.”

Zu An was speechless for a moment. He pulled her in and began punishing her with his hands. Shang Hongyu’s vibrant yet playful personality was extremely likable.

Shang Hongyu trembled as she pleaded for mercy. The two played around for a little while before Shang Hongyu suddenly turned to her little sister and asked, “Why are you so quiet? Aren’t you curious about your previous incarnation?”

Shang Liuyu rolled her eyes. Don’t you see what I’m doing? I’m not as thick-skinned as you, to be able to chat while doing such stuff. 

Shang Hongyu laughed. She could see through her little sister’s thoughts, so she quickly reached out to help the latter. “That’s not the right way to do it. Come, let me teach you.”

Shang Liuyu’s face reddened further. It was even more embarrassing to have someone teach her something like that. She instinctively withdrew her hand, but Shang Hongyu anticipated her movement and firmly grabbed her hand. 

“You need to do it like that. It won’t do to be so stiff…”

Shang Liuyu felt as if she was experiencing an entire lifetime’s worth of shame. Shang Hongyu is a lunatic! Why does she have to drag me down with her?!

Even so, thanks to Shang Hongyu’s guidance, she was being far bolder than she usually was. She couldn’t help but be astonished by her big sister’s teachings. I didn’t know there’s so much wisdom behind such things!

She also couldn’t help notice how small her hands were. To think that even mine and big sister’s hands combined wouldn’t be enough!

Shang Hongyu knew how shy her little sister was, so she asked on the latter’s behalf, “Ah Zu, tell us more about Liuyu’s previous incarnation.”

“You can say that she’s the founder of your Mermaid race…” As he reminisced about Gong Suyin, Zu An’s gaze turned gentle.

“I have always been curious as to why there were so few records about the Gong clan. Their lineage has always had few offspring too. That explains it,” Shang Liuyu finally spoke up. 

“You’re usually such a philanderer. Shouldn’t you be more proactive about such stuff? It’s because of your inaction that the Gong clan’s main lineage’s bloodline wasn’t passed down.” Halfway through Shang Hongyu’s words, her face suddenly turned red. “No, it’s a good thing you didn’t have any offspring with her, or else the two of us could be your descendants. That would be a huge mess.”

Zu An was speechless. “What nonsense are you spouting? She was a Gong and you’re Shangs. How can you be blood-related?”

“That’s hard to say. Intermarriages often occur within our five major clans.” Shang Hongyu harrumphed. She looked relieved. 

“What happened next?” Shang Liuyu was more interested in what had happened between Zu An and her previous incarnation than these details. 

“Well…” Zu An sighed. “It ended in tragedy.”

He told them about how Gong Suyin had been cornered and eaten the immortal medicine, which resulted in her ascending to the Frost Palace and how she was trapped there for the rest of her life. The two of them had bade each other farewell and never met again. 

By then, Shang Hongyu was already sobbing. “Ahh, that Miss Gong is too pitiful! How can you seduce her and not take responsibility? You’re terrible!”

Zu An didn’t explain himself. Despite being limited by his circumstances, the fact that he had failed to find Gong Suyin was the truth.

Shang Liuyu shook her head. “I disagree. Your feelings with Miss Gong were mutual, and the two of you were lawfully married as well. She loved you even when you were forced to part ways, and she was holding hope in her heart for an eventual reunion. I think the worst thing that can happen to a person is to lose all hope. I think she was only able to hold on because of the hope you gave her.”

Zu An sighed. “Even hope can't stand the test of time. She waited far too long for me, but I never showed up. I can’t imagine how deep her despair must have been.”

Shang Hongyu shook her head. “She might have thought that your feelings for her had changed and harbored a grudge against you for that.”

Shang Liuyu glanced at her big sister. She’s using this chance to unravel the knots in Ah Zu’s heart for good. She played along with Shang Hongyu and continued, “Two people truly in love wouldn’t blame each other. I have never met Miss Gong before, but she sounds like a wise and sentimental woman. I don’t think her love for you would change even if she felt that you would never meet again.”

Seeing someone with the Mermaid Queen’s face say such words shook Zu An’s heart. He felt as if he was meeting the Mermaid Queen once more, and the latter was sharing her countless years of sentiment. Unable to take it anymore, he pulled her into his embrace. At that moment, the Mermaid Queen and Shang Liuyu overlapped for him. 

Shang Liuyu gently hugged him back without saying a word. 

Only Shang Hongyu was left feeling frustrated. She had to do it alone after Shang Liuyu abruptly retracted her hand. What am I doing here while they're enjoying their pure romance?

“Hey, don’t you think my previous incarnation is pitiful too? She was also alone for tens of thousands of years.”

Zu An burst into laughter and gently embraced her too. 

This scene would have felt heartwarming to both sisters… if not for a certain pole pressing against them. 

“Ah! Now that I think about it, Liuyu, could your recent dreams be related to that Gong Suyin?” In the end, Shang Hongyu still couldn’t bear to give up halfway through and leave Little An like that. 

Shang Liuyu hesitated. She didn’t think it was right to let Shang Hongyu do all the work, so she went back to helping her. “I’m not sure either, but my recent dreams did leave me feeling alone and melancholic. But is it possible to retain past memories after reincarnation?”

Zu An shook his head. “It shouldn’t be possible under normal circumstances. Granny Meng’s soup washes away past memories.”

A thought suddenly struck him. Hold on, I'm the the Necropolis Emperor. I could have made an exception for her. I thought of reuniting with the Mermaid Queen at the Frost Palace, but if she reincarnated, she would have passed through the Netherworld and the Six Paths of Reincarnation. 

Shang Liuyu spoke up, “Ah Zu, you need not be so depressed. You might have failed to meet Miss Gong thus far, but it doesn’t mean you’ll never meet her again. You've returned to the past once; I’m sure you can do it again once you find a way to do so.” 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2623: Betrayal
chapters.body:
Chapter 2623: Betrayal

Zu An widened his eyes. That’s right. Somehow, I kept thinking of reuniting with them in my timeline, but that would be extremely difficult in any case. I should be thinking of ways to return to their timeline instead. 

Time travel was no easy feat, but it wasn't impossible either. Some dungeons and artifacts were capable of time travel, such as the Nature Jade Plate. That artifact had turned back into stone, but there could be other ways to do it. 

The thought of that lifted his morale and dissipated his gloominess, though that immediately evoked exclamations from the two sisters…

The following day, Zu An made his way to the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons with Shang Hongyu accompanying him. 

Shang Liuyu had been swept away by the atmosphere yesterday and didn’t think much into it, but once the deed was done, she felt so embarrassed that she wanted to bury herself. She refused to meet Zu An no matter what he said.

“Under her carefree exterior is an innocent heart. Don’t mind it too much. She’ll get over her awkwardness in a few days’ time,” Shang Hongyu replied with a gleeful smile. She was happy that she had succeeded in teasing her little sister yesterday.

Zu An also smiled gently when he thought about Shang Liuyu’s clumsiness yesterday. It felt as if the veil dividing them had finally been ripped apart then, but their progress was so fast that it seemed to have scared her off. 

“How do you intend to thank…” Shang Hongyu cried out before she could finish her sentence. Zu An had swept her into his arms to kiss her. 

Her body quickly relaxed, and her enchanting eyes glimmered as if dabbed with water. “You bullied me a lot yesterday. Is that not enough?”

Shang Liuyu had been the one stroking the zither yesterday, but as the melody played to its climax, her inexperienced self had failed to dodge in time. In the end, she'd run off out of embarrassment to clean herself up and never returned. 

Shang Hongyu had needed to clean up the situation. Thinking about what happened afterward made her heart beat quickly. 

This temptress, Zu An thought. She cosplayed Xihe and switched between characters yesterday. How am I supposed to endure that?

Unfortunately, they were in the Ocean races’ most sacred Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons. Some of the Mermaid race’s ancestors were buried here. It wouldn’t be right for them to fool around here. 

While advancing, Zu An went up to the statue of the founding Mermaid Queen. He had always wondered why the latter resembled Shang Liuyu so much, but he now had the answer—the statue was sculpted in her likeness. Not to mention he was the one who had supervised this project in the ancient era. 

He gently stroked the Mermaid Queen statue. It had been battered by time, but she still looked as beautiful as ever. 

Shang Hongyu got jealous. “Oh hoh! You sculpted a statue for my little sister’s previous incarnation to remember her by. Why didn’t you do the same for my previous incarnation?”

Zu An sweated profusely. He awkwardly replied, “You were the Sun Goddess and the Celestial Empress in that era. It wouldn’t have been good if our relationship became known.”

“Isn’t it just a human spouse? My sister and I are descendants of the Ocean races, but I don’t see you having any reservations about that.” Shang Hongyu was just joking at the start, but she got emotional halfway through. 

It’s a story of three people, but only I am unworthy of being commemorated. 

Zu An also realized how inappropriate his actions were, so he pulled her into his arms to console her. 

“Forget it. You can walk by yourself. I won’t be accompanying you.” Shang Hongyu gently pushed him away and headed off. A trail of gleaming pearls were left in the dark palace. 

Zu An wanted to stop her, but he didn’t know what to say. She seemed truly upset. 

Even though he and Xihe had shared an intimate relationship, they'd been more like enemies instead of lovers. He'd been nowhere as close to her as Shang Hongyu in this life. But speaking those thoughts aloud would likely only upset her further. He would rather she blame him instead.

Zu An sighed before advancing deeper into the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons. The time loop had been closed, and the World Law Beacon had returned to his hands. However, he had come here today to verify something. When he sensed the aura of the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine lingering in the atmosphere, he frowned. 

He had entrusted the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine’s seed to the Mermaid Queen in the Celestial Court world. However, she'd eaten it to prevent Feng Meng from gaining possession of it, which resulted in her ascending to the Frost Palace. 

When he constructed the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons with the Ocean races, there had been no substitute for the immortal medicine there. Back then, he'd even worried that the disappearance of the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine would affect history.

But somehow, he'd still managed to obtain the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine in this timeline.

There can only be two possibilities. One, there are two Divine Dragon Immortal Medicines. However, immortal medicines are primordial substances, and each of them is unique. 

That leaves only the second possibility. Gong Suyin was here. She probably remembered me talking about that, so she intentionally planted the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine’s seed here to close the time loop. 

The thought that Gong Suyin had visited this place and seen her statue excited Zu An. Does this mean that she broke free of the Frost Palace’s restraints? Did Mo Xi grow strong enough to use ‘Farewell, Nanchao’ on her contract?

Zu An’s excitement heightened. He began searching the area, but in the end, he didn’t find anything. This confused him. If the Mermaid Queen was here, why didn’t she leave anything behind? Was she unable to leave anything here?

He continued advancing with a heavy heart. Soon, he passed through the abyss and entered the Netherworld. He first made his way under the Oblivion Bridge and saw Chu Chuyan still in her cultivation. He briefly hesitated, but decided against disturbing her. 

The Oblivion River was highly damaging to souls. Even with him, the Netherworld’s master, making a special exception for her, Chuyan still needed to remain in a state of hibernation to endure the relentless flow of time. If she woke up, she would experience the real world’s flow of time, and that would only put her in greater pain. 

Zu An quickly made his way to the Reincarnation Platform. He had instructed the Ox Head and Horse Face in the spacetime fragment to leave a message here if anything happened to the Netherworld in the future. 

They should have at least left something behind, right?

Zu An nervously made his way to the discreet corner on the Reincarnation Platform that he had pointed out to them. A second later, his body trembled. 

There was an extremely small word written in the Shaman language. It was inscribed messily, as if it had been done in a hurry. 

‘Betrayal’.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2624: An Enigmatic Intruder
chapters.body:
Chapter 2624: An Enigmatic Intruder

Zu An looked at the messily scribbled word and fell into deep thought. Betrayal? Who was the traitor, and who did they betray?

There were far too many possibilities with that single word that Zu An couldn’t make sense of the situation with his limited information. 

Why did Ox Head and Horse Face leave this single word behind? Did they have no time to leave more information? Did the enemy discover them while they were inscribing this word? Or did some rule or individual prevent them from leaving behind more detailed information?

In any case, the death of the Netherworld’s other deities likely has something to do with this matter.

“Lady Houtu?” Zu An tried calling out, but the Six Paths of Reincarnation continued operating without answering.

Houtu had initially preserved a sliver of her sentience due to her strength after transforming into the Six Paths of Reincarnation, but after many years of operation, her sentience was bound to erode, leaving behind a pure heavenly law. 

Zu An spent a long time pondering things by the Reincarnation Platform. Following that, he went under the Oblivion Bridge once more to visit Chu Chuyan. He spent a while chatting with her despite knowing she was unconscious, but it did calm his heart. 

The next day, he went to the Dragon Palace. To his surprise, Shang Liuyu was waiting in the vicinity for him.

“Big sister Shang!” Zu An was overjoyed. He'd thought that she would continue avoiding him due to what happened the last time. 

Shang Liuyu’s face reddened as she thought of the same matter. She rolled her eyes and said, “Don’t misunderstand, I’m not here for you. My big sister came back yesterday and kept crying nonstop. I asked her what was wrong, but she refused to speak. She was in a good mood when she accompanied you to the Tomb of Ten Thousand Dragons. What happened?”

Zu An was startled. “I’ll talk to her.”

“I’ve relayed the message. The rest is up to you,” Shang Liuyu said before leaving in a hurry. 

Zu An shook his head bitterly, knowing that Liuyu hadn’t recovered from her embarrassment. However, the priority at hand was to talk to Shang Hongyu. 

Following Shang Liuyu’s tip, he went to an island near the Dragon Palace and found Shang Hongyu at a beach. She was resting against a boulder, staring at the sky in a daze. Golden rays shone on the emerald ocean water, and the reflected light made her graceful figure glow. 

Zu An felt as if he was looking at a beautiful portrait. As he walked over, he said sincerely, “You’re beautiful."

“But I have no weight in your heart,” Shang Hongyu lamented. 

That sharp response made Zu An sweat profusely. He sat down next to her and grabbed her hand. He was about to speak when Shang Hongyu suddenly interjected, “Do you think I’m jealous of my little sister?”

“Of course not.” Zu An thought he would get angry if he was faced with the same situation too.

Shang Hongyu lowered her head. “I’m not angry at you or my little sister. I’m angry at myself.”

Zu An didn’t know what to say.

Shang Hongyu looked at him with a miserable smile. “It’s not a tantrum. I’m angry at myself for always meeting you later than others. Not only did I meet you later than my little sister in this world, but I also met you later than the Mermaid Queen in that world. It feels as if I’m always one step behind.”

Zu An suddenly laughed as he pulled her into his embrace. “You’re wrong about that. I met Xihe much earlier than Suyin.”

“Really?” Shang Hongyu narrowed her eyes in suspicion.

“Of course. I met Xihe a long time ago in a dungeon…” Zu An told her about his first meeting with Xihe in that dungeon, including how she'd cornered him. 

Shang Hongyu was thoroughly entertained by that story. “You’re terrible! Using your Golden Phoenix’s ability on your first meeting, making her fall in love with you.” 

Zu An was apologetic about that. “I had no choice. She was going to kill me. That was my only way out.”

“Did you secretly use that ability on us too?” Shang Hongyu eyed him in suspicion. 

Black streaks flashed across Zu An’s face. “Of course not! I told you I had no choice then.”

Shang Hongyu breathed a sigh of relief. “I guess you met my past incarnation much earlier than that Gong Suyin. You might have seen Xihe as an enemy because she was after your life the first time you met, so your feelings for her weren’t as pure as compared to Gong Suyin.”

“Yeah, I can’t deny that,” Zu An replied frankly. “Your previous incarnation was formidable. The first time I met her, I was utterly helpless against her. She even prepared an elaborate scheme the second time we met. I would have been sold out by her without knowing!”

Shang Hongyu suddenly remarked, “I think Xihe ended up selling herself out instead.”

“What do you mean?”

“Do you think a woman would willingly sleep with you if she didn’t like you?”

Zu An fell into deep thought. He had considered that matter too.

Shang Hongyu quickly added, “Do you think she did that under the influence of your Golden Phoenix’s ability?”

Without waiting for his answer, she shook her head and said, “The Golden Phoenix’s ability is formidable, but it could only make her fall in love with you; she still retained her rational judgment. There were ways for her to avoid intimate contact with you while she was disguised as Yumen Beiqing, but she still allowed it. She might have initially allowed it due to the Golden Phoenix’s ability, but over time, you should have been able to tell whether her feelings for you were true or not.”

Zu An nodded. “You’re right. She had many chances to kill me, but she didn’t do it.”

He recalled how the Goddess of Wu Mountain, in the projection she had left behind, said that she, Xihe, and the Mermaid Queen had become close friends. That, in itself, spoke volumes. 

“I don’t know how it was for my past incarnation, but at least in this lifetime, I sincerely love you.” Shang Hongyu raised her head to look at her lover with gentle but determined eyes. 

Zu An leaned in and kissed her.

The two of them cuddled as they thought about their time together. Their hearts felt closer to each other than ever. 

Suddenly, Zu An pushed Shang Hongyu aside and coldly glared in a direction. “Why don’t you show yourself when you’re already here?”

Shang Hongyu was initially confused, but she immediately tensed up when she learned that someone else was here, and she hadn’t noticed the other party’s presence at all. 

“Hm? You sensed me? How is that possible?” a voice echoed. 

A figure slowly revealed itself not too far away. It was a person wearing a bizarre mask and scholar's robes. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2625: Frog In The Well
chapters.body:
Chapter 2625: Frog In The Well

Shang Hongyu immediately put up her guard. Her earlier failure to detect the intruder could be attributed to her putting down her vigilance because she was with Zu An, but despite the other party revealing himself, she was still unable to sense his aura. 

She was the Mermaid Queen, a renowned expert of the Ocean races. It was utterly astounding that she couldn’t sense a person despite the other party appearing before her.

Zu An frowned. “Hm? You aren’t from this world. How did you get in?”

The Cultivation World was a rare Wonderpoint World in the universe. With him activating the world barrier through the World Law Beacon to guard against the monsters’ invasion, it shouldn’t have been possible for beings from other worlds to slip in. 

Did something happen to the barrier? Zu An narrowed his eyes.

Hearing Zu An’s words and sensing his unique aura, the masked man nodded in realization. “Ah, you are the master of this world. Pardon me for intruding. I am a unique entity, which allows me to enter any world. You need not worry about your world barrier. There are only a handful of people in the universe who possess this ability.” 

Zu An examined the other party. As the master of this world, he could utilize resources unthinkable to mortals while he was in this world. Looking through his own eyes, he saw that the other party had no physical body. Beneath the scholar's robe was empty air. 

And yet, the other party was a living entity, existing in a way that was incomprehensible to him.

“What are you doing in our world?” Zu An asked vigilantly. 

“I chanced upon this place while investigating the death of a comrade.” The other party’s attitude was exceptionally respectful upon learning that Zu An was the world’s master. 

“Death of a comrade? What’s your comrade’s name? Let me see if I have an impression of him.” Zu An wondered if one of the experts he had killed was the other party’s comrade. 

“You need not worry. This has nothing to do with you,” the masked figure replied with a smile.

Zu An frowned. What are you doing here if it has nothing to do with me? He suddenly thought of something and turned to Shang Hongyu.

Shang Hongyu also realized what was going on and asked, “Me?”

The masked figure nodded. “Yes, my comrade died at that lady’s hand.”

“Are you mistaken?” Shang Hongyu was baffled. She couldn’t think of anyone related to this mysterious entity. 

The Mermaid race wasn't fond of slaughter, and there was no way she wouldn’t have an impression of such an enigmatic being.

Zu An subtly pulled her behind him as he said, “She couldn’t have killed your comrade.”

But the masked figure confidently replied, “Our race has very few people, so each of our brethren is very important to us. Our bodies have a unique way of imprinting people who kill us, and there’s no way to remove the imprint. I sensed the imprint while passing by this world, so I came in to take a look. I hadn’t expected the murderer to be such a beautiful lady.”

Zu An frowned. There really are all sorts of mysterious skills in the universe. I have to be more careful in the future. “You said a lot, but there’s no way she could have killed your comrade.”

The masked figure assessed Shang Hongyu before nodding. “Indeed, she’s too weak. It doesn’t seem likely that she could have killed my comrade.”

Shang Hongyu was perplexed. She had heard all kinds of evaluations about her—some said she was pretty, others said she lacked decorum, and yet others said she was fierce. This was the first time someone had said she was weak.

But what was even more maddening was that she couldn’t refute those words. The other party was so unfathomably powerful that she was no match for him. 

“It’s good that we have cleared the misunderstanding.” Zu An knew next to nothing about the other party, and his race had a unique way of imprinting people. If things went wrong, it could escalate into a full-blown war. He didn’t want to make such troublesome enemies for no reason. “Since you have come from afar, allow me to welcome you as a host.”

He was hoping to gather more information about the other worlds. He had been to quite a few worlds, but they'd been accessed through dungeons. The only other world he had physically been to was the Monster World, but the Monster World was already on the verge of destruction. 

Just the thought of the terrifying Annihilation Beast made him tense up. There were too many powerful entities in the universe, and he was lacking information about them.

“You have misunderstood. I said that the current her is weak, but that doesn’t mean the past her was weak too.” The masked figure shook his finger.

Zu An’s face darkened. “Are you joking with me?”

“Our racial culture is inclined toward solemnity. We don’t joke around,” the masked figure earnestly replied. “Let me see. Using your world’s words, you can say that her past incarnation was the one who killed my comrade.”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Shang Hongyu’s past incarnation was Sun Goddess Xihe. Could it be… 

Shang Hongyu came to the same conclusion too. She glanced at Zu An with worried eyes.

Zu An squeezed her hand to reassure her before smiling at the masked figure. “Don’t you think it’s too ridiculous to be talking about past incarnations? It’s too far-fetched to use such excuses to accuse her of a crime.”

The masked figure fell silent, as if weighing the risks of confronting Zu An. A while later, he still chose to patiently explain, “Our race’s imprint is a special one. It remains even after the soul has undergone reincarnation, as long as vengeance is not served.”

“Impossible,” Zu An interjected. “Our world observes the law of the Six Paths of Reincarnation. Anyone who has undergone reincarnation has their memories wiped, making them a different person from before. How can you still sense your imprint?”

“It is baffling. I can also sense the unique nature of your world. Indeed, I shouldn’t have been able to sense the imprint after an individual entered the Six Paths of Reincarnation.” Even through the mask, Zu An and Shang Hongyu could still sense the other party’s confusion. “If I have to fathom a guess, it must be because she’s someone special. Either she used a secret art or a special exception was made in the Six Paths of Reincarnation, allowing her to retain something from her previous life. It is because of that connection that I can sense that imprint. Yes, that must be the case!” He started uncertain, but his voice grew increasingly certain.

“The Six Paths of Reincarnation is impartial. It is a fundamental law of this world. How can anyone retain anything from their previous life after undergoing the Six Paths of Reincarnation? Aren’t you going too far by pinning a crime on my friend based solely on your conjecture?” Zu An exclaimed, startled.

However, the other party’s conjecture made sense. Xihe, as the Sun Goddess and the Celestial Empress, could have used a special artifact to determine her future reincarnation, not to mention her close ties with him. It was possible for the Six Paths of Reincarnation to have made a special exception for her.

The masked figure scratched his head. “You’re putting me in a tough spot.”

“She’s my woman, which makes her this world’s mistress. You’re welcomed here as a guest, but if you’re looking for a fight, we’ll entertain you too.” Zu An coldly eyed the masked figure as he wondered if he should apprehend the other party to gather more information. However, the other party’s ability to imprint on others was indeed troublesome to deal with.

The masked figure straightened his posture as his disposition turned cold. “You must have had a smooth-sailing life to become this world’s master, but you’re nothing but a minor border world in the universe. You’re a frog in the well if you think you’re invincible.”[1]

1. A frog in the well refers to someone who is stuck in their little world and doesn’t know how big the world outside is. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2626: Nemesis
chapters.body:
Chapter 2626: Nemesis

The masked figure exuded a formidable aura, and his words were sharp. He thought that this could be an opportunity to let this border world bumpkin know how huge the universe was. Little did he expect to be faced with a sharp blade of sword ki. 

Shang Hongyu was awed by the blinding sword. Ah Zu’s swordsmanship proficiency has advanced considerably. I could still barely understand his swordsmanship before, but now, he has already surpassed this world’s limits. 

She knew that if she were in the masked figure’s place, she would have been sliced in two before she could make sense of what was happening. 

However, her smile soon froze. The masked figure casually tilted his body, and the sword ki simply passed by him.

Zu An frowned too, but his sword ki didn’t stop there. It abruptly changed its trajectory to move upward. 

But once again, the masked figure casually stepped back and avoided the attack.

Zu An unleashed several more attacks, but the other party could always avoid his attacks using the subtlest movements. 

Shang Hongyu was stunned. The first time could have been a coincidence, but the other party pulled it off every time. It showed how sharp the masked figure’s judgment was. She didn’t want to admit it, but the other party’s swordsmanship proficiency likely far surpassed Zu An’s. The difference could even be at a fundamental level. 

Zu An was shaken up too. Could the power levels in the universe be so drastically different?

When he saw Zu An stop his aggression, the masked figure smiled. “Do you see now that my words are no lie…” 

“What you lookin’ at?”

“I’m looking at you, shithead!”

The masked figure was interrupted by the ‘What you lookin’ at’ ability before he could finish his sentence. Zu An didn’t hesitate to use this opportunity to strike. He drew the Tai’e Sword and created countless afterimages to attack the masked figure from all directions. 

Initially, the masked figure could still dodge his attacks with casual steps, but over time, his dodging stances became increasingly flustered. He was no longer as relaxed as before.

Shang Hongyu also began singing, layering debuffs on the masked man. She knew that with her current cultivation, engaging the masked figure close up would only result in her being one-shot, so she wisely focused on supporting Zu An instead. Fortunately, the Mermaid race was skilled in support. 

The masked figure was already flustered from Zu An’s attack. Shang Hongyu’s intervention placed him in a dangerous position. He immediately retaliated by drawing a brush to deflect Zu An’s sword. At the same time, he flipped open a book and chanted aloud, “Fear arrogance and shackle yourself with humility. Fear narrow-mindedness and be all-embracing like the ocean…”

His forceful words evoked a harrumph from Shang Hongyu as fresh blood seeped from her lips. She had suffered a backlash from the other party’s soulspeak.  

“What you lookin’ at?”

“I’m looking at you, shithead!”

… 

Zu An hurriedly interrupted the other party’s soulspeak before rushing to Shang Hongyu’s side to examine her condition. Upon confirming that she hadn’t hurt her cultivation base, he exhaled in relief. 

The masked figure secretly wiped off his sweat. He didn’t use this opportunity to attack Zu An. “How did you know I couldn’t dodge the subsequent attacks?”

His earlier trick had never failed him. He had used it countless times and shattered many dao hearts, leaving his enemies with no fighting spirit. 

Zu An laughed. “I have met and fought many powerful entities, and I don’t believe you’re stronger than them. You probably have an ability to foresee the future, allowing you to predict my attacks, but such abilities either have restrictions or come at a cost. That’s why I sped up my attacks to test your limit. Clearly, I have overestimated you.”

The masked figure fell silent for a moment before replying, “You’re right. I have such an ability. It’s called ‘Constellation Reading’. It allows me to foresee the enemy’s next move. You have an unwavering heart and sharp wits. Such traits are rare in the world. I am in awe.” 

He hadn’t revealed the downsides of the ability, but Zu An had hit the nail on the head.

Zu An calmly replied, “The pleasantries are belated.”

The masked figure held a brush in one hand and a book in the other, looking as relaxed as ever. “You might have seen through one of my abilities, but surely you don’t think you can apprehend me with just this much.”

“How would I know if I don’t give it a try?” Zu An took out a formation plate and moved his hands so quickly that they formed afterimages. In the blink of an eye, he constructed a formation channeling the energies of the nearby islands, mountains, and ocean to trap the masked figure.

As the world’s master, he already wielded the ability to control the laws of the world. With the support of his formation, he could intertwine the formation’s laws with the laws of physics. 

The masked figure suddenly couldn’t sustain his flight anymore and plummeted into the raging ocean. 

“Let history be the mirror that pierces through the veil!” a calm voice echoed.

A light shone amid the waves. The light slowly intensified, which revealed the masked figure’s silhouette in the water. He was upside down like a hanged man, but he remained calm. He flipped open his book as he casually walked on the waves. 

The scenery suddenly distorted. A second later, he suddenly emerged from the formation, regaining his previous stance. 

Zu An suddenly realized something. “You’re a Time Scribe?”

The other party’s clothes and skills resembled that of the Time Scribe he'd met in the Monster World. With this realization, he finally understood why the other party was after Shang Hongyu. Sun Goddess Xihe had killed that Time Scribe in the Celestial Court world, and Shang Hongyu was Xihe’s incarnation. No wonder the masked figure had come looking for Shang Hongyu!

“Time Scribe?” Those words appeared to agitate the masked figure. He vehemently shook his head as he shouted, “No no no, I’m nothing like those hateful things! I am a Mirror Scribe!”

Zu An was startled by the masked figure's intense disgust. “Is there a difference?” Why are you coming after us if you aren’t on the same side?

“Of course there’s a difference!” the masked figure shrieked. “We, the Mirror Scribes, record true history, whereas the Time Scribes alter history at their whim. They are our nemeses, so how can we be the same?”

“Alter history?” Zu An was perplexed. Is what I did in the other spacetimes considered altering history?

“Hold on, how do you know about the Time Scribes?” The masked figure eyed the duo with suspicion. 

“We crossed paths with one recently. You said you wanted to avenge your comrade, so I wondered if it was him.” Zu An and Shang Hongyu exchanged looks.

The masked figure widened his eyes. “Could that fellow be a spy?!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2627: Deity Names
chapters.body:
Chapter 2627: Deity Names

Zu An was speechless. Hold on, so you really aren’t on the same side?

“Can you tell me more about your encounter with that fellow?” the masked figure urged. 

Zu An huffed, “You came here yapping about avenging your comrade. You’re the one who picked a fight, and now you’re also the one who wants to stop the fight. Why should I answer your question?”

The masked figure bowed to Zu An. “I apologize to you for my earlier disrespect and seek your forgiveness. I’ll present you with a satisfactory gift afterward as compensation.”

“What’s the gift?” Shang Hongyu asked. She didn’t want her lover to be fooled by vague terms. 

“I guarantee your satisfaction. I swear on my position as a Mirror Scribe,” the masked figure replied. 

Since the other party had already made such a vow, the duo didn’t dwell on the matter. 

Zu An began recounting his encounter with the Time Scribe in the Monster World. The masked figure raised a few questions along the way, and Zu An answered them all.

After hearing the full story, the masked figure remarked, “Based on what you have told me, that fellow might really have been a Time Scribe. He wielded the Spring and Autumn Brush, the tool used by Time Scribes. But I’m curious to know how you became aware of his identity. He shouldn’t have told an outsider that he was a Time Scribe.”

“I have my ways of finding out.” There was no way Zu An would tell the other party about Mi Li’s existence. “Can you tell me more about the grudge between Mirror Scribes and Time Scribes?”

The masked figure knew someone who had become a world’s master was bound to have many fortuitous encounters. The latter could indeed have encountered a Time Scribe somehow. Since Zu An was unwilling to speak, he chose not to continue probing either. “We might be fellow scribes, but we worship different philosophies and deities. We, the Mirror Scribes, view history as a mirror, and we seek to record objective truths.

“The Time Scribes, on the other hand, have lost their basic principles as scribes and seek to toy with history. Events they pen with their Spring and Autumn Brushes depict a twisted version of the truth, misleading others into forming a false impression.

“That’s why we are absolute nemeses. That fellow was hiding among us for a long time now; there’s no doubt he harbored malicious intent. It’s a relief that you eliminated him, even if unintentionally, or else it could have spelled a disaster for us.”

The revelation that an old comrade had secretly been a Time Scribe made him shudder. To have infiltrated us, he must have been up to something terrible.

Zu An took this chance to probe further. “You mentioned that you worship different deities. What’s the deity you worship?”

He had a feeling that the deity worshiped by the other party wasn't just a minor deity, but a universal deity.

The masked figure frowned. “If you learn their names, you will be dragged into the war among deities. Are you certain that you’re prepared for that?”

Shang Hongyu grabbed Zu An’s hands and looked at him worriedly. Despite knowing the height she had reached in her previous life, she was happy with her current one. Ah Zu is carefree and happy in this world; there’s nothing bad about this. If he gets tied up in other matters, there's a good chance he might have to leave this world. That’s the last thing I want to happen.

Zu An hesitated too. Being this world’s master was a carefree job for him. He wasn’t certain whether he wanted to brave unknown risks. However, this was also his only chance to learn about the deities’ names. Mi Li would never tell him about those beings. But if he confirmed that he wanted to know, this masked figure would tell him. 

He thought about how Jiang Luofu had been summoned by an enigmatic entity, as well as Gong Suyin and the Goddess of Wu Mountain’s unknown plights… There were too many mysteries that he needed to unravel, and he needed greater power to do so. 

“I’m ready, but I have a question. Someone once told me that it’s a taboo to reveal a deity’s name. Obliteration may ensue if one catches the deity’s gaze. If so, why is it that you are able to tell me your deity’s name?” Zu An asked.

“Your friend was trying to protect you. It’ll only bring destruction upon yourself if you learn about a deity’s name when you are incapable of shouldering the burden. I can reveal it because I am a Mirror Scribe. I am responsible for recording the universe’s objective history,” the masked figure replied proudly. “As a world’s master, you are qualified to learn about the universe’s secrets. That being said, given your limited strength, I can only tell you the names of two deities. You are already implicated in this karma, so with the protection of that karma and my unique identity, you should be able to avoid being destroyed by the deities’ overwhelming power.”

Zu An’s face turned serious. Mi Li wasn’t lying to me. Even the preconditions are harsh. 

The masked figure spoke in a tone resembling documentary narrators in Zu An’s previous life, “We, the Mirror Scribes, worship the Deity of Memory, Meng. We record past, future, and erased events.  Be it from memories from a person or a world, we record everything that happens in the universe, including life, death, and the destruction of worlds. As long as our records persist, they will not be truly dead.”

Zu An was taken aback. He had imagined the name of a universal god to be majestic, but it was surprisingly simple. Hold on, would having such a thought be considered blasphemy?

He got nervous for a while there, but fortunately, there was no divine retribution. 

“You have a noble goal,” Zu An sincerely said. Judging from the other party’s passionate tone, he could tell that the latter believed in his cause. 

Their records would no doubt have helped many as well, since the recorded beings and worlds were, in a way, eternal as long as their records remained.

The masked figure was delighted to receive Zu An’s acknowledgment. “Why don’t you join us in worshiping the great Deity Meng and become a Mirror Scribe too?”

“I know too little about the deities. It’d be insincere of me to worship a deity on a whim,” Zu An subtly declined the offer. You must be joking. I don’t even know what’s going on with those universal deities, so how can I haphazardly join a side? Besides, I am at least a minor deity who governs this world’s cycle of reincarnation.

“You’re right. I have gotten ahead of myself.” The masked figure didn’t insist on it. “Next, I will share with you the deity worshiped by the Time Scribes. They believe in chaos and enigma, and their deity’s name is ‘Mi Li’.”

“What did you say their deity’s name was?” Zu An felt as if a hammer had struck his heart. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2628: Tree And Beehive
chapters.body:
Chapter 2628: Tree And Beehive

How could Zu An not be shocked when that name sounded just like big sis empress? He'd initially thought that Mi Li was Qin Shihuang’s empress, but it later became apparent to him that that wasn’t the case. She had always been enigmatic to him. 

Mi Li seemed to know a lot, but getting information from her was tougher than squeezing out toothpaste. She spoke in riddles, which matched the traits of a Deity of Chaos and Enigma. 

While others have grandpas as their cheat, my cheat is one of the most powerful entities in the myriad worlds? The thought shocked Zu An.

“What nonsense are you thinking?” Mi Li’s sneering voice suddenly echoed in his ears. “Do you think I’d babysit you like a nanny if I were truly that Deity of Enigma?”

Zu An thought those words made sense. When he first met her, she'd been sealed in the tomb of Qin Shihuang’s empress and didn’t even have a body. She had to rely on him to find materials to forge a new body for her. It didn’t seem possible for an unparalleled deity to end up in such a plight… 

The masked figure was horrified to hear Zu An’s question. He reflexively looked at the sky before replying with a suppressed voice, “Even as a scribe blessed with the great Deity of Memory’s protection, I can’t keep calling the name of that exalted entity. It’ll be terrible if I draw the deity’s attention. But judging from your reaction, have you heard that name before?”

Zu An replied with a strained smile, “No, I must have remembered wrongly.”

The masked figure sternly warned him, “Remember this. You should avoid listening to any universal deities' names unless their envoys personally reveal it to you.”

Zu An was intrigued. “What would happen otherwise?”

“You might draw the deity’s attention and disintegrate on the spot,” the masked figure replied. 

“Will it always draw the deity’s attention?” Zu An asked. 

“I don’t know. I doubt anyone in the world would dare put their life on the line to test that theory,” the masked figure replied hesitantly.

Zu An thought made sense. It would be better to err on the side of caution. 

The masked figure took out a black-gold card and passed it to Zu An. “I promised you a gift. This is an invitation letter to Dreamland. With it, you can access Dreamland, a place countless people across the myriad worlds wish to visit.”

“Dreamland?” Zu An was perplexed.

“As the name suggests, it is a land of dreams. There is no war there; only happiness. It is said that no matter who you are, you can always find the items you need to fulfill your dreams there. Of course, the more precious the item you require, the greater the price you have to pay,” the masked figure explained.

Zu An’s breathing quickened. Whether it was the spacetime divide with the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain, the materials needed to reforge Mi Li’s body, or Chuyan, who was trapped under the Oblivion Bridge… He had far too many wishes that couldn’t be fulfilled with this world’s power. If Dreamland was truly that incredible, he would absolutely have to make a trip there. 

“Dreamland only opens once every thousand years, and access is based on the invitation letters they distribute. Only those with an invitation letter can enter. There has been bloodshed over the invitation letters all throughout history. Some world emperors even offered to trade a planet for an invitation letter, but there were no takers.”

Sensing Zu An’s astonishment, the masked figure smiled. He was certain that this gift would satisfy Zu An, and his guess was on the mark.

“How can the invitation letters be snatched if Dreamland specifically sent them to certain individuals?” Zu An was perplexed.

“There are no names on the invitation letter. Dreamland only recognizes the invitation letter, not the individual. That’s why I can give this to you,” the masked figure explained.

“It feels as if Dreamland intentionally adopted such a system to stir conflict.” Zu An frowned. He wasn’t fond of such maneuvers. 

The masked figure thought nothing of it. “Think of it as a trial. Someone who gets their invitation letter stolen isn’t worthy of entering Dreamland. Dreamland doesn’t want to waste time on the weak either. Experts are capable of offering more valuable things to it.”

Zu An fell silent. It's the survival of the fittest after all. He glanced at the masked figure and asked, “Based on what you've said, the invitation letter is incredibly valuable. Why do you want to give it to me?”

The masked figure sighed. “I obtained this invitation letter because of my position as a scribe. There are many major events in the myriad worlds waiting to be recorded, and I have another mission on hand. On top of that, I must rush back to inform my comrades that a Time Scribe infiltrated our ranks. My hands are full. It just so happens you did us a huge favor, so it feels right to thank you with this invitation letter.”

Zu An took the invitation letter. It was unlike any material he had ever seen before, resembling a fusion between gold and jade. 

The masked figure addressed his confusion, saying, “The letters are made from Dreamland’s specially produced amber. They cannot be forged by anyone else, which is why the invitation letters are so highly sought after.”

“Thank you. I just realized that I don’t know your name yet,” Zu An said as he stowed away the invitation letter. The other party looked to be a decent person, and it just so happened that he knew next to nothing about the myriad worlds. It would be good for him to make a friend here. 

The masked figure was overjoyed. Scribes like him were obsessed with the notion of making a name for themselves in history. A world's master asking for his name meant that his name could be recorded in the annals, too. He replied, “I am Unblind Zuoqiu.”

“That is a rather scholarly name.” Zu An was perplexed by the name. 

Unblind Zuoqiu was overjoyed to hear the remark. “I like this name too. We, Mirror Scribes, usually pick names from people we revere in history as testament to our mission.”

Zu An felt a headache settling in as Unblind Zuoqiu began talking about the elders in his industry. It was a while later before he found a chance to slip in a question. “Brother Zuoqiu, what major powers are there in the myriad worlds? We don’t get much information here.”

Unblind Zuoqiu replied, “The 'myriad worlds' is a term referring to the collection of all universes. Some barriers are nigh impossible to cross between universes and prevent them from interacting with each other. It is tough to travel to other universes unless your universe has reached a certain level of civilization.

“Your universe used to have a fair number of civilizations, but they were all conquered by the Monster World. With the destruction of the Monster World, your world is now the only one left in your universe.”

“There are many theories about the form of the myriad worlds. One of the more mainstream ideas is the Tree Conjecture. It is said that the Creation Tree has countless leaves on it, and each leaf is a universe. Each universe has many galaxies and civilizations, but it is difficult for one leaf to interact with another leaf.

“I am personally more inclined to see the myriad worlds as a beehive. Each honeycomb is a universe, but there are walls between each honeycomb that make it nigh impossible for two universes to interact.”

This greatly clarified Zu An’s thoughts. “The people who destroyed the Monster World—which deity’s worshipers are they?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2629: The Deity’S Gaze
chapters.body:
Chapter 2629: The Deity’S Gaze

Unblind Zuoqiu was taken aback by Zu An’s question. He hesitantly lowered his voice and replied, “They worship the Deity of Annihilation. I don’t dare to utter his name, and I don’t advise you to seek it either.
