You’ll only bring annihilation upon your world if you draw his gaze…” Fearing that his words had offended the Deity of Annihilation, he anxiously took out a book and began chanting as if to tap into his deity’s protection. 

Zu An was disappointed by the other party’s frightened reaction. He'd been hoping to gather more information about the Deity of Annihilation. The Monster World had nearly destroyed the Cultivation World, but all it took to destroy the Monster World was a subordinate from the Deity of Annihilation. It was hard not to be wary of such a being.

Zu An wanted to ask more about the myriad worlds, but Unblind Zuoqiu said, “The other civilizations of your universe have been destroyed, so there is nothing else for you to learn about. There is also little point in learning about the other universes’ civilizations in advance. You can take a look yourself if there is an opportunity for you to cross universes.”

He took on a solemn tone as he continued, “Brother Zu, you must remember one thing when you travel to other worlds. Learning about secrets comes with risks. Knowing things you shouldn’t know will bring you danger. It’s the same when you leak secrets too.”

“Thank you for your warning, Brother Zuoqiu. I've gotten ahead of myself.” This wasn't the first time Zu An had heard such words. Secrets must be truly dangerous.

“All right, Brother Zu An. I need to take my leave now. May we meet again.” Unblind Zuoqiu clasped his fist as he bade Zu An farewell.

Zu An panicked. “Brother Zuoqiu, shouldn’t you at least tell me where Dreamland is? Didn’t you mention earlier that there are barriers that cannot be crossed between universes?”

Unblind Zuoqiu smacked his head. “Ah, I nearly forgot that you lack common knowledge of the myriad worlds. The barriers between universes are indeed nigh impossible to cross, but there are plenty of adventurers in the myriad worlds who make it their goal to explore the unknown. They have developed a way to cross universe barriers, but I shan’t elaborate on the details.

“The invitation letter you have isn’t for show. Once Dreamland opens, the invitation letters will sense it. All you have to do is to infuse your spiritual energy, immortal ki, chakra… Well, different worlds have different terms for their energy, but they are all fundamentally the same. You just have to use your ki to activate the invitation letter, and a spaceship will come to fetch you.” 

“Spaceship? Is it from Dreamland?” Zu An was taken aback. 

“No, it’s from a universal commercial organization. Dreamland hires them to fetch their guests. This ensures that anyone with the invitation letter can reach Dreamland without a hitch,” Unblind Zuoqiu explained. He suddenly widened his eyes as he murmured, “Ah! I unknowingly divulged more secrets to you again. No, I can’t stay here any longer, or else I’ll be taking a huge loss!”

Despite Zu An’s attempts to keep him, Unblind Zuoqiu’s figure slowly turned transparent before vanishing into thin air. Zu An was stunned.

Unblind Zuoqiu's hurried departure invited Shang Hongyu to remark, “He seems more like a worshiper of the Deity of Commerce than the Deity of Memories.”

Zu An chuckled. “Perhaps there are rules for exchange in the myriad worlds. Secrets are probably very valuable commodities.”

“Ah Zu, do you truly intend to head there?” Shang Hongyu asked worriedly. 

“Yeah. There are far too many people I have to save, and I've already done everything I can in this world. My only option is to seek solutions elsewhere,” Zu An replied. 

“But it’ll be dangerous.” Shang Hongyu bit her lip. She was in no mood to get jealous. 

“The greater the risks, the greater the rewards.” Zu An grabbed her hand. “Don’t worry, Hongyu. Danger has accompanied me for many years now, but…”

Shang Hongyu placed her finger on his lips as she looked at him with worried eyes. “Never lose your fear of danger.”

Zu An’s heart warmed. He knew she was telling him not to raise any death flags here.

“It’s still too early to say for sure. I don’t know when Dreamland is going to open yet,” he said. The invitation letter had a date, but it followed a different format from that of the Cultivation World. 

“How can that Mirror Scribe be so unreliable? He left without properly explaining things,” Shang Hongyu huffed. “The other civilizations likely use a different language, right? How are you going to communicate with them?”

Zu An’s smile stiffened. He had failed to consider this problem earlier. Hm? Hold on. Isn’t it weird I was able to communicate with others in the other worlds I've gone to? Is it because these worlds all fall under the Six Paths? If so, it might not work in other universes. 

“Don’t worry. A spaceship is coming to fetch me, so there must be a solution.” Zu An knew that cultivators of a certain level could communicate through certain laws or telepathy, though it wouldn't be as convenient. 

“I hope so. Let’s discuss this with my little sister.” Shang Hongyu grabbed his arm. Her little sister was still feeling embarrassed over what had happened two days ago, but this wasn’t the time for her to be feeling awkward. 

Zu An nodded as the two of them made their way to the Dragon Palace. On the way, he began chatting with Mi Li, “Big sister empress, are you truly not that Deity of Chaos and Enigma?”

“Are you hoping for it to be me or not?” The red-dressed Mi Li appeared beside him with an unreadable smile. She was a unique being; unless she chose to reveal herself, only Zu An could see her soul. 

“Of course I’m hoping it’s you. With such a thick thigh to latch onto in the myriad worlds, I would have nothing to fear!” Zu An laughed.

Mi Li rolled her eyes. “I also hope it’s me, so I can discipline you for that blasphemous thought.”

All of a sudden, however, her expression warped in shock, and she vanished. 

While Zu An was wondering what had happened, white lights suddenly lit up around him. Suddenly, he was no longer surrounded by emerald seawater, and Shang Hongyu was nowhere to be seen either. He now seemed to be floating in the middle of space. 

What’s going on? I'm this world’s master. How can something ridiculous like this happen when I've already activated the world barrier?

The surrounding white lights converged to form a peculiar pattern, then unprecedentedly tremendous pressure suddenly weighed down on Zu An. He was horrified. He felt as if he was on the verge of being torn apart. 

“You want to latch onto my thick thigh?” an impassive voice boomed.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2630: Conversing With A Deity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2630: Conversing With A Deity

A mini explosion seemed to happen in Zu An’s mindd, warning him about the danger he was in. He felt as if he could disintegrate at the other party’s mere thought. He had never felt so shaken before, even when he was standing against a Celestial Emperor. Never had he felt so overpowered that he couldn’t even summon the willpower to retaliate.

This entity was even stronger than the mysterious being Qiu Honglei had summoned through the Dance Offering to the Universe. Judging from the other party’s strength and tone, they must be the Deity of Chaos and Enigma, Mi Li.

He finally understood why so many people had warned him not to carelessly mention a deity’s name. He hadn’t expected that a mere joke he cracked would catch the other party’s attention. 

He quickly snapped out of his daze and replied, “I just heard what the Mirror Scribe said, and I have to say that I strongly disagree with his view of chaos and enigma. The world is born amid chaos and enigma, and that is bound to be its ultimate state as well. There is no need for events to be recorded so clearly. An overly defined history will only make everything boring. Only vague information shrouded in mystery can bring endless entertaining possibilities.”

His brain was furiously churning to rake up all of the knowledge he had accrued over his several lifetimes to bootlick the other party. He knew that he only had one shot, and if he failed to secure enough goodwill, destruction would be his plight. 

“You seem to believe that I am the Deity of Chaos and Enigma, Mi Li?” The other party’s ethereal voice was tinged with mischief. It sounded feminine, but Zu An didn’t know if it would be right to gender stereotype a deity.  She didn’t speak in the Cultivation World’s tongue, but Zu An could still comprehend her, as her voice seemed to directly reach his soul.

Her words left Zu An with goosebumps. Hold on, did I bootlick the wrong person?

If the other party was the Deity of Memories, Meng, his earlier words would have been incredibly disrespectful. The entire Cultivation World could be destroyed because of what he'd just said!

“Your heartbeat and your profuse sweat show that you’re very nervous, but why don’t you try explaining yourself? Is Mi Li the only one worth fawning over?” the voice spoke coldly. 

“My mortal wits are nothing in the face of a true deity. I’m sure that, with your divine splendor, you can sense the utmost respect I have for you,” Zu An respectfully replied. 

“I only feel your cunning.” She had no corporeal body. All Zu An saw were white lights reminiscent of starlight, moving around and pulsating with different degrees of brightness. That seemed to represent her expression. 

Sweat rained down Zu An’s face. He didn’t dare to think too much, fearing that the other party could peer into his mind. 

“Forget it. You’re in luck. I am Mi Li,” the voice replied. 

I would be luckier if I hadn't caught your attention. Zu An shuddered as soon as the thought popped into his head, and he quickly dispelled it. “I am honored to be in the presence of a revered entity like yourself. May I know if there is anything I can do for you?”

The starlight flickered, as if angered by his disrespectful thought. “Can’t I look for you if I have nothing else to do?”

Zu An sensed that the other party wasn’t as inflexible as he had thought. Rather, she seems a bit…  

Not daring to dwell too much on that, he quickly replied, “Revered deities like you must be extremely busy. It is beneath a mortal like me to take up your time for no reason.”

“Don’t use your shallowness to comprehend beings like us.” That voice harrumphed. “In this world, you are a deity, but don’t you still frolic around with those females?”

Zu An was horrified. It felt like the other party had been observing him for a long time. “With my minor accomplishments in my narrow domain, compared to your exalted self, I am no deity.” 

He was being so subservient that it even disgusted himself, but the omnipresent terrifying aura vanquished such thoughts right away. 

“At least you still have some self-awareness.” The voice chuckled. “I'm here to settle the score with you. That Time Scribe was my worshiper. Tell me, how should I deal with this?”

Zu An trembled. “That Time Scribe’s death has nothing to do with…”

He bit his tongue before he could finish speaking. What if the other party goes after Xihe and Shang Hongyu if I shirk responsibility here?

So, he quickly changed his tune, saying, “It’s a misunderstanding. We didn’t know he was your worshiper.”

“You’re a loyal one. It’s good that you’re willing to bear this karma.” The starlight shimmered dazzlingly. “I don’t want any excuses. You killed my worshiper. If I don’t avenge him today, anyone would dare to hurt my worshipers.”

Beneath the beautiful starlight lurked nearly tangible murderous intent. Zu An racked his brain and replied, “That Time Scribe was one of your countless worshipers. You are the master, whereas he’s your servant. It doesn’t make sense for the master to avenge the servant’s death. It should be the servant avenging the master’s…”

“Hm?”

Zu An nearly choked. “What I’m saying is that this goes against the way of things. Others will wonder who is the master and who is the servant.”

“Are you teaching me how to do things now?” It was impossible to read Mi Li’s emotions from her words. 

“I don’t dare to, but I have an idea in mind.” Zu An’s mind had never churned that quickly before. 

“Speak!”

“It’s just a Time Scribe. You can find someone to fill his place.”

“It sounds as if you want to be a Time Scribe. It’s not that easy.”

“What I previously did, in a way, fulfills the essence of a Time Scribe,” Zu An hesitantly replied. He chose not to fully divulge what had happened in the spacetime fragment and the Celestial Court world to see if the other party was truly omniscient. 

The starlight flickered. A while later, she said, “Indeed, you are a talent in this field. You made a mess of history without changing major events. You’re more outstanding than that buffoon.”

Zu An slowly regained his nerves. His willpower had been sharpened from the countless dangerous encounters he had. By now, he'd already made sense of the situation. She wouldn’t have wasted her breath on me if she was truly here to punish me. This might have been her intention from the start.

“You think I was laying a trap for you?” the voice coldly asked.

Cold sweat rained from Zu An. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2631: Taking A Gamble To Become An Envoy
chapters.body:
Chapter 2631: Taking A Gamble To Become An Envoy

She can read my thoughts!

Zu An quickly stifled that thought. He didn’t dare to carelessly harbor any more thoughts lest he blaspheme the deity before him. “I am in awe of the exalted deity’s omniscience!”

He didn't try to explain himself, instead opting to shrug it off with bootlicking. 

As he expected, Mi Li didn’t pursue this matter. “Not only are you a world's master, but you know how to read and navigate the situation, too. You have the talent to become a Time Scribe. Very well, from this day onward, you shall be a new Time Scribe.”

An old book and a disheveled brush appeared in front of Zu An. These two items looked extremely normal; no one would have taken a second look at them in a bookstore. But as a world's master, he could tell that something was special about them. They exuded a faint, ancient, desolate intent.

He took the book and flipped through it. The river of history seemed to flow through the book; different images flashed before his eyes, only to recede into the book the next second. 

Zu An stared at the blank page before him in a daze. He couldn’t help but wonder if he had been hallucinating.

It was then that he received a series of messages from his Keyboard System. 

Item: Time Annal

Ability: ???

Learnable: No.

Zu An was taken aback. After obtaining the Netherworld Book of Life and Death, he'd gained the ability to consume Rage points to learn abilities from items he came into contact with. The only reason he hadn’t used it more often was because of the astronomical Rage points required to invoke that ability, but never before had an item been unlearnable to him. It’s no wonder she’s an exalted deity of the myriad worlds. 

All of a sudden, he trembled in shock. Ah, she can read my mind. 

“You seem to have something interesting with you.” The starlight flickered with interest. 

Zu An suppressed the thoughts in his mind and replied, “Nothing I have is worth a mention before an omniscient deity like yourself.”

The starlight formed two curved eyes. “That’s true.”

Zu An resisted the urge to retort about the other party’s little girl-like nature and hurriedly changed the topic. “I remember that the Time Scribe could alter everyone’s memories. Why don’t I have any ability at all?”

He had never considered rejecting Mi Li at all. You must be kidding me. I would be out of my mind to reject an entity as powerful as her. Besides, if I can obtain a universal deity’s protection as a Time Scribe, I might be able to march anywhere in the universe with my chest puffed out. 

“Would you like to swap what you have for that ability if you like it that much?” The starlight flickered once more as she spoke with a teasing voice. 

Zu An anxiously changed his tune and replied, “I’ll treasure whatever you bestowed upon me as a treasure, regardless of whether it is good or not.”

The starlight harrumphed. “You aren’t that good at fawning. How can something I bestow upon my worshiper be terrible? Hold the Spring Autumn Brush and the Time Annal at the same time.”

Zu An did as he was told, holding the brush in one hand and the book in the other. Enlightenment immediately came to him. 

Spring Autumn Brush and Time Annal Combination Ability: Exists

As long as it is plausible and reasonable, whatever is written with the Spring Autumn Brush in the Time Annal will become reality.

Note: The content written must be reasonable. If it is too far-fetched or the narrative is too easily unraveled, the ability will be rendered ineffective.

Zu An’s breathing quickened. Isn’t this ability overpowered?

It was similar to his Keyboard Come ability, but the latter required too high a price. Each time he used that ability, he would be left on the verge of death. In comparison, this ability didn’t require him to pay a price. All he needed to do was ensure that the content he penned down was reasonable.

He couldn’t resist asking, “O great Deity of Chaos and Enigma, could I become a revered deity like you by writing it down in this book?”

“What do you think?” the starlight asked. Rather than being angry, she was amused by his boldness. 

“Probably not,” Zu An replied sheepishly. “Will anything I write be realized as long as it is reasonable?”

“You can understand it that way, but you need to be careful when writing about certain entities. The stronger the target, the lower the chances of success. Know that you’ll suffer a backlash if you fail; it could range from severe injuries to instant disintegration. You need to be very prudent with what you write in the Time Annal,” the voice echoed. 

Zu An nodded. If there were no restrictions to their abilities, Time Scribes would have ruled the world by now. The other deities’ worshipers had to have their overpowered abilities too. 

A thought came to his mind and he said, “Let me give it a try.” He picked up the Spring Autumn Brush and quickly wrote a few words.

“You’re already using it now?” Mi Li, despite being a deity, was curious as to what he would write. 

Zu An soon finished writing what he wanted. 

“On a certain day of a certain month of a certain year, Zu An was summoned by the exalted Deity of Chaos and Enigma and earned Her approval. She bestowed him with a standing and power unlike ordinary Time Scribes and conferred him as an Envoy of Chaos and Enigma.”

Other than their worshipers, deities also had envoys representing them, similar to how diplomats represented their respective nations. Needless to say, envoys wielded power and authority far superior to ordinary worshipers.

He was taking a gamble here. He sensed that the other party harbored some goodwill for him, so he pushed his luck a little. Most of what was written here was true anyway, and the fabricated portions, as long as she didn’t object to it, could become reality too. She had her pride to uphold, after all. It wouldn’t look good on her if the ability she had just bestowed failed on the first try… right?

Mi Li snorted, “…You sure are brazen!”

The starlight shook. For a moment there, it resembled a CPU burning out. 

Zu An put on his most sincere smile and said, “O great Deity of Chaos and Enigma, I can feel your magnanimity and the favor you have shown me. Am I mistaken in my words?”

“You are much more interesting than those boring, inflexible fellows, but don’t you think you’re too bold to scheme against me?” 

Starlight abruptly fell on the Time Annal, altering its contents. Zu An lowered his head and saw an additional ‘provisional’ word—'provisional envoy.'

Mi Li continued speaking, “How can becoming my envoy be that easy? But given the good work you have done in those worlds, I’ll let you enjoy being my provisional envoy for now. Don’t worry, you’ll have a chance to become a true envoy as long as you continue doing a good job.”

Zu An felt as if a rock had been lifted from his chest. “Thank you, exalted Deity of Chaos and Enigma!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2632: How Can I Repay Them For Their Sentiments?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2632: How Can I Repay Them For Their Sentiments?

“If you truly wish to thank me, show me more interesting work. It’d be best if you could make a complete mess of history.” The starlight flickered in anticipation of the future. 

Zu An was perplexed. Are you the Deity of Chaos and Enigma or the Deity of Entertainment?

Worried that the other party could read his mind, he quickly halted his train of thought and instead tried to gather some information about Jiang Luofu. “O great deity, I have a companion who seems to have been summoned by a true deity. May I ask who that deity is?”

“Oh? Someone from a border world like yours caught the eye of those fellows?” Mi Li was surprised. 

Zu An recounted Jiang Luofu’s mysterious circumstances before her disappearance.

The starlight shimmered as if checking on something. A while later, the voice said, “I get it.”

Zu An was overjoyed. “May I know who summoned my friend?”

“You’ll know when the time comes,” Mi Li replied.

Zu An nearly choked on his saliva. Ugh, another riddler! “May I know when the right time will be?”

“You said something that I deeply agree with. We shouldn’t confuse the position of the master and the servant. I granted you power and bestowed you artifacts, and here you are, demanding that I find someone for you. Are you the master here, or am I?” The voice turned chilly.

“Of course you are!” Zu An secretly wiped away cold sweat. These deities are unpredictable!

“It’s good that you know. Go alter more entertaining history and satisfy me, and I'll consider telling you,” Mi Li said nonchalantly. 

“Yes!” Zu An tried his luck. “Do you have artifacts that can travel through time? That would make it much easier for me to alter history.”

With such a tool, I could return to the past and reunite with the Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain. He recalled Shang Hongyu’s anger and added another name to the list. And Xihe too.

“Hmph! Trying to use official work to fulfill your private agenda? I don’t care which women you copulate with. As long as you offer me enough entertaining stories, I can let you copulate with all the beautiful women in the myriad worlds.” Mi Li coldly harrumphed.

Cold sweat rained down Zu An. I nearly forgot she can read my thoughts. How am I supposed to guard against this? He had an overactive brain. More often than not, random thoughts would pop uncontrollably into his mind. And the way she phrased it is bizarre too. It’s almost as if she’s rearing a pet. 

“Time-traveling tools fall under a special category. Unlike Time… Hmph, there is much you can’t know about yet. All you need to know is that you don’t need to travel back in time to change history. Everything you do has the possibility to change history. Your trip to Dreamland is a good opportunity. You have to go, and you'd better make it a big event for me. Otherwise, I have no use for you.” The voice gradually faded as if it was leaving. 

Zu An barely blinked before the starlight around him disappeared without a trace. He had returned to the ocean, and Shang Hongyu was worriedly tugging his arm. “Ah Zu? Ah Zu?”

“What’s wrong?” Zu An snapped out of his daze.

“I should be asking you that. You suddenly stopped moving,” Shang Hongyu huffed. 

Zu An widened his eyes. “How long was I gone for?”

“Not long. Around two to three breaths. But that would have been long enough for an enemy to take you down,” Shang Hongyu replied. 

“I was summoned by a deity.” Zu An realized that only his consciousness had been summoned, but he wondered why time hadn’t halted. Won’t I be in danger if the deity abruptly summons me while I'm fighting a life-and-death battle with an enemy? I should ask how she handles such a situation.

“Ah?” Shang Hongyu was stunned. She hadn’t expected to receive such a shocking answer. “What happened?”

“Let’s find Liuyu first, then we’ll talk together.”

Shang Liuyu was still feeling embarrassed and didn’t want to face them, but upon hearing about the otherworldly intruder and Zu An’s meeting with a deity, she hurriedly opened her doors.

Zu An proceeded to share the story of their encounter with the Mirror Scribe and his subsequent audience with a true deity. 

The two women were horrified. “We mustn’t carelessly speak of the deities in the future.”

Zu An chuckled as he took out the Spring Autumn Brush and Time Annal. “It turned out to be a blessing in disguise.”

The two women were stunned when they learned about his new ability. It transcended the possibilities of their current world. 

Zu An wondered whether to take this chance to write about a steamy night with the two sisters… but he quickly stifled that thought. It was only a matter of time before that came to be, so there was no need to rely on such cheats. Besides, he didn’t know whether writing such nonsense in the Time Annal was considered blasphemy. It would be terrible if he angered Mi Li with that. 

“While becoming a deity’s envoy comes with huge merits, it also means that you’ll be embroiled in the conflicts between true deities. That could potentially put you in more danger in the future,” Shang Liuyu reminded him. “Ah Zu, I know you’re determined to head to Dreamland to save the others, but you'll need to avoid flaunting your position. We know too little about the other worlds; we don’t even know what enemies the Deity of Chaos and Enigma has.”

Zu An finally calmed down from his excitement. “Thank you for your reminder, big sister Shang. I was too optimistic earlier.”

They also discussed the potential dangers in Dreamland, but they had too little information to work with. In the end, they could only equip him with a couple of artifacts and pills from the Dragon Palace. Zu An left a few days later. He didn’t know when Dreamland would open, so he needed to make arrangements for his absence. 

Before he left, Shang Liuyu gave him a suit of scale mail, saying, “Ah Zu, my big sister and I wove this armor out of our Mermaid race’s most valuable Heart Guarding Scales. We already intended to do so when you headed to the Monster World back then, but it’s only recently that we finally finished working on it.”

Zu An took the scale mail. Iridescent light flowed through its scales; it looked gorgeous. Despite being made out of scales, it was as smooth and flexible as thin silk, and it didn’t weigh much either.

“How fragrant.” He lifted the scale mail to his nose to take a whiff, an action that caused the two sisters to blush. 

Shang Hongyu gently smacked him. “What are you doing? This scale mail is made not just from our Heart Guarding Scales but the ones left behind by generations of Mermaid Queens. You’re blaspheming our ancestors.” 

Zu An quickly reined in his smile and apologized to the two ladies. He had once received a scale from a certain Mermaid Queen that allowed him to withstand a fatal attack once. This chainmail, on the other hand, was made out of the most important scales from many generations of Mermaid Queens. Its defensive prowess had to be at a ridiculous level. 

The two sisters had practically emptied the Mermaid race’s treasury for him. He was moved. How can I repay their sentiments?

But there were too many things he needed to do, so their time together was destined to be limited. He held the two ladies tightly in his embrace. The two also knew that it could be a long time before they met again, so they tightly embraced him back. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2633: Impetus
chapters.body:
Chapter 2633: Impetus

A while later, Shang Hongyu suddenly stood up and said, “Ah Zu, my little sister has something she wishes to say. I shan’t disturb the two of you.” Following that, she left with a bizarre smile. She even shot her little sister a look before closing the door behind her.

Zu An looked at Shang Liuyu in surprise. “Big sister Shang, what do you want to tell me?”

Shang Liuyu’s cheeks immediately reddened. Her previously relaxed expression suddenly looked incredibly awkward. It was a moment later before she spoke up. “My big sister said that since we have already progressed to this point, we should become a proper couple before you leave, lest…”

“Lest what?” The other party’s shy appearance made her look indescribably delectable to Zu An, so he couldn’t resist teasing her further. 

Shang Liuyu raised her head to look him in the eye. She gently touched his cheeks with her fair hands. “Lest something were to happen to you. I’d live out the rest of my life in regret.”

Those words moved Zu An, and he grabbed her hand. They are truly concerned about me. I’m heading to other universes that we know nothing about; even I don’t know what will happen. 

He pulled her into his arms and said, “I’ll be careful. I have the backing of a true deity now, so the trip won’t be as dangerous as you think it to be.”

“Mm.” Shang Liuyu rested her cheek against his chest and felt his heartbeat. Her heart slowly calmed down. 

“You only mentioned what your big sister thought. How about you?” Zu An asked.

“I…” Shang Liuyu looked conflicted. A while later, she shook her head and replied, “I don’t know.”

“Do you feel as if something is missing?” Zu An asked with a smile. 

Shang Liuyu instinctively nodded. “Indeed, especially after hearing your story with the ancient Mermaid Queen. She might be my past incarnation, but we're still two different people. I envy the deep bond you two shared.”

Her words were vague, but Zu An understood what she was getting at. He and Shang Liuyu had already maxed out their feelings for each other, but they were lacking an impetus to advance their relationship. Were it not for his encounter with the ancient Mermaid Queen, this parting could have served that purpose.

However, women were sentimental creatures. Zu An already had a grasp of Shang Liuyu’s personality from the first time he met her. Beneath her carefree exterior was an innocent girl who harbored fantasies about love. She didn’t want to be outdone by her previous incarnation. 

“You don’t need to feel burdened. We don’t have to rush things and regret it in the future. Wait for my return. We’ll find an auspicious timing and get married…” Zu An’s expression suddenly stiffened halfway through his words.

Pui pui pui! How can I raise such a massive death flag?! Movie characters who promise to marry their lover after returning from a dangerous trip are almost guaranteed to die. How could I make such a low-level mistake?

Shang Liuyu’s face also warped in horror as the same thought occurred to her. “You mentioned before that such words are incredibly inauspicious.”

“Then you should accompany me tonight to console my wounded soul. Don’t worry, I won’t do anything other than hug you.”

Shang Liuyu nodded. It looked as if her cheeks had been applied with the world’s finest rouge. With their parting imminent, she wanted to spend as much time with her lover as possible. They embraced in bed as they shared their yearning for each other. 

All of a sudden, Shang Liuyu shuddered. “Y-You… Why are you touching my foot?”

“How beautiful.” Zu An fiddled with the foot in his hand. He finally understood why there were so many people who were into feet. “Big sister Shang, you don’t know how much I hoped to have such a day with you from the first time we met.”

Shang Liuyu recalled the first time they'd met in a gazebo and smiled gently. “So back then, you were already a scheming pervert.”

“It’s called appreciation for beauty.” Zu An missed the old days. “I wouldn’t have dared to overstep the line back then.”

“But now you dare?” Shang Liuyu regretted it as soon as she blurted those words out. 

“Well, I’d obey if big sister Shang tells me to stop right now,” Zu An said as he leaned in.

Shang Liuyu immediately exclaimed, “Stop!”

Zu An was rendered speechless. “I haven’t even started yet!”

“But you’re… You’re still holding it.” Shang Liuyu could feel the heat from Zu An’s palm against her foot, and that made her heartbeat quicken. 

Zu An was amused by how tense she was. “Does the Mermaid race have sensitive feet?”

Shang Liuyu turned away as she grumbled, “I’m sure you know given how often you play with my big sister.”

Zu An relished in her pleasant, quivering voice. He reflexively gripped her foot even tighter.

Shang Liuyu trembled. She shouted in alarm, “Stop!”

“See, I keep my promises, don’t I?” Zu An chuckled at her flustered response. 

“Remove your hand.” The furiously blushing Shang Liuyu tried to withdraw her leg, but Zu An held it so firmly that she couldn’t pull it out. 

“I only promised to stop when you asked me to. I didn’t say that I would release your leg.”

“You liar! Ahh, don’t… Stop!”

Zu An was amused. “Are you asking me to stop or not?”

“You’re taking advantage of me!” Shang Liuyu gritted her teeth. She lurched forward and bit his shoulder. 

The two of them began bickering. At some point, the room abruptly fell silent. They found themselves so close that they could feel each other’s breaths. 

Shang Liuyu’s beautiful eyebrows fluttered. She suddenly found herself at a loss. 

Zu An leaned in. Shang Liuyu’s breathing quickened, but she didn’t avoid him. 

The two lovers kissed.

Some time later, Shang Liuyu suddenly exclaimed with a quivering voice, “Why are you taking off your clothes?”

“It’s too hot.”

“But you’re taking off my clothes too!”

“Aren’t you hot too?”

“But we’re in the underwater Dragon Palace!”

“How weird. The Dragon Palace feels oddly hot today.”

“No… Stop!”

“Don’t worry, I’ll just cuddle a little.”

“…”

A while later, Shang Liuyu bit her lips. Her eyes glimmered as she murmured, “If you really wish to… I can do it too.”

Her concession left Zu An feeling as if his mind was going to explode, but he shook his head. Perhaps she sounded willing now, but it would leave her with regrets. He didn’t want to leave her with a lifelong regret over a moment of recklessness. 

Shang Liuyu felt his concern, and that tinged her heart with sweetness. But when she lowered her head, she couldn’t help but worry. “But your current condition…”

“You can help me.”

“Ah… It was big sister who did it the last time. Should I call her over?”

“You don’t necessarily have to use…” Zu An leaned in and whispered to her. 

Shang Liuyu was stunned. Her face reddened in embarrassment. “Where do you learn this nonsense from?”

Worried he would get his way, she morphed her legs back into a tail to cut his thoughts short.

… 

The following day, Shang Hongyu and Shang Liuyu escorted Zu An out of the Dragon Palace for thousands of kilometers before reluctantly bidding him farewell. 

Shang Liuyu sighed. “Big sister, do you think I’m useless?”

“Ah? You didn’t give it to him even though it was such a good opportunity yesterday?” Shang Hongyu was taken aback.

Shang Liuyu blushed furiously. “That’s not what I’m talking about! I’m lamenting that I can’t be of any help to him even though he’s venturing into an unknown world.”

“So did you give it to him yesterday or not?” Shang Hongyu was more concerned about that. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2634: Nervousness As Home Draws Close
chapters.body:
Chapter 2634: Nervousness As Home Draws Close

“You ruffian!” Unable to take her big sister’s bold remarks, Shang Liuyu turned her reddened face away and fled. 

“What’s there to be shy about between sisters? Tell me more!” Shang Hongyu chased her, determined to fulfill her desire for gossip. 

Shang Liuyu recalled what had happened last night. 

After she morphed into her mermaid tail, Zu An had suddenly shared a fairy tale called ‘The Little Mermaid’ with her. In the story, a mermaid princess had saved a prince and fell in love with him. To be with him, she'd approached a witch to morph her mermaid tail into legs, only to learn that she had to trade her melodious voice for it… 

It was such a sad story that it had made her tear up, but Zu An went on to say that the mermaid princess had paid such a heavy price for her legs; it was a huge pity for her not to show hers when she had them. 

His argument had stumped Shang Liuyu. In the end, she'd turned her tail back into legs, and somehow, she'd ended up helping him the way he told her to… She could still feel the heat on her feet, and it made her face go red. How can I share something like that with big sister?

“Nothing happened!”

Upon hearing her little sister’s answer, Shang Hongyu got agitated. “How could nothing have happened? What did I tell you before? You should listen to me. You’ll regret it for life if something happens to him out there.”

Shang Liuyu chuckled. “There’s nothing to regret. Our souls are connected. I understand him, and he understands me too.”

“Sure, sure, your love is noble and pure. Only mine is dirty and lustful,” Shang Hongyu huffed. 

“Big sister, that’s not what I mean.” Shang Liuyu grabbed her big sister’s hand to placate her. “Rather than satisfying him on the spot, I thought it would be better to give him something to look forward to. He’d have to stay calm when taking risks so that he can come back safely and claim the prize. Who knows? It might give him a boost of power in his time of need.”

Shang Hongyu suddenly felt jealous. “Sure, you’re the more charming one. He thinks about coming back to you to claim his prize even when he’s struggling outside. Hmph! You know how many women that philanderer has outside? He might meet a beautiful foreign woman in Dreamland and melt in her warm embrace. Do you think he'd still think about you then?”

“Ah Zu isn't that kind of person. I trust him,” Shang Liuyu replied with a smile. “I might not be charming enough alone, but I’m not alone. I have you too.”

“What do you mean?” Shang Hongyu looked at her with confused eyes. 

“I promised him that if he returns safe and sound, we could…” Shang Liuyu reflexively lowered her head as her heart beat furiously. I must have been out of my mind to make such a promise to him last night. 

Shang Hongyu was stunned. Has my elegant little sister always been this bold? She soon remarked with a pout, “Why are you dragging me into your promise?”

“Are you willing or not?” Shang Liuyu grabbed her big sister’s arm and shook it around.

“Pui! I’m not a casual woman.” Images surfaced in Shang Hongyu’s mind and tinged her cheeks red. “Unless you properly beg me then.”

“I’ll have Ah Zu properly beg you.” Shang Liuyu lowered her head in embarrassment.

“No way. I want you to beg me instead. Who asked you to put on a lofty appearance? That just makes me look cheap in contrast. I never thought you would be this bold…”

“Aiyo, my big sister…”

“You aren’t going to change my mind. Be prepared to beg me!”

… 

Meanwhile, Zu An was traveling to the human capital via Rainbow Morph. Along the way, he asked Mi Li about what happened. “Master empress, you’re more incredible than I thought. You sensed a universal deity’s descent and hid in advance.”

“You were so busy playing with the two sisters that you only remembered me now?” Mi Li teased. “I’m nowhere as incredible as you are. You were able to convince that reserved little mermaid beauty to do such things for you.”

That made Zu An remember Shang Liuyu’s delicate feet, which looked like sculpted masterpieces. He let out a cough and hurriedly changed the topic. “Master empress, do you think… that person noticed you?”

“She didn’t,” Mi Li replied without hesitation.

Zu An was taken aback. “Even an existence of that caliber failed to notice your presence?”

“The contract between us is a unique one. No entity aside from you can see me unless I reveal myself on my own accord,” Mi Li declared proudly. 

“Then why did you hide?” Zu An expressed his doubt at her words.

“Your lousy brain could have thought about me at a whim and given me away,” Mi Li huffed. 

Zu An smiled sheepishly. The universal deities' ability to read minds was a fearsome one. “Can they really read our minds? That makes it impossible to conceal any secrets from them, and no scheme can escape their notice!”

“You're a bold one to think about scheming against them!” Mi Li looked at Zu An with eyes of approval. “They are entities standing above the myriad worlds. It won’t be that easy to deal with them. You don’t have to be worried about their mind-reading ability. They won’t constantly read your mind. It’s similar to how you wouldn’t be concerned about what a roadside ant thinks.”

Zu An nodded in realization. So that mind-reading isn’t a passive ability. It needs to be activated. At least the other party isn’t reading my mind all the time. That’s a relief. 

“Have you heard about Dreamland?” he asked.

“I’ve never heard of it,” Mi Li replied without hesitation. 

Zu An asked, “…Unblind Zuoqiu said that Dreamland is famous across the myriad worlds, but you've never heard about it?”

“I told you I lost most of my memories. There are two possibilities. Either I used to know about Dreamland but lost that portion of my memories, or Dreamland didn’t exist back in my era.” The red-dressed Mi Li looked into space in a daze. It was hard to tell what she was thinking. 

Zu An looked conflicted. “Master empress, you’re finally admitting you’re from another world.”

“Haven’t you already guessed as much?” Mi Li chuckled, not denying it. “But my world is intricately linked to yours.”

“Do you really have no connection with those entities?” Zu An asked. 

“I don’t know.” Mi Li shook her head. “I’m not lying to you. I don’t know what memories I've lost.”

Looking at her downcast expression, Zu An consoled her. “Don’t worry, master empress. I've heard Dreamland can fulfill any wish. I’ll find the materials required to reforge your body there. You might regain your memories once you regain your body.”

“At least you have a conscience. Your mind isn’t just filled with thoughts of those women.” Mi Li smiled.

“Aren’t you one of them too?” Zu An murmured. 

Mi Li’s eyebrows shot up. “What did you say?”

“Nothing.”

“Are you planning an uprising against your master now?”

“…”

“Hold on! What do you mean by riding your master? What the heck is in your mind?!”

“See, you can read my mind too. And you still say you aren’t related to that Mi Li.”

“You must be tired of living! What are you going to do if she really comes?!”

… 

Amid merry chatter, Zu An soon arrived at the human capital. Now that he was about to return home, he suddenly felt nervous. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2635: Rebellion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2635: Rebellion

Zu An’s journey to the spacetime fragment and the ancient Celestial Court world hadn't felt long to him, but he'd come back to find that three years had passed in this world. Many things were bound to have changed within three years.

His infant daughter would have already grown up.

He took a deep breath and entered the city gate. He didn’t fly in, as the capital was the stronghold of the human race; it was protected by many formations and experts. He would cause too huge of a commotion by flying in. He wouldn’t be able to quietly do what he wanted once someone recognized him. 

But as he passed by the city gates, he couldn’t help but notice that the soldiers’ inspections were much stricter than usual. That brought a frown to his face. Has Linglong not eradicated the monster remnants yet?

However, the capital was as bustling as usual. The populace didn’t seem fazed, so he didn’t think too much about it. 

He made his way to the Sang manor. It looked the same as ever. Bi Linglong had intended to bestow Sang Hong with a larger manor, but the latter had rejected it. The Sang clan had few family members, so a larger manor would have only felt cold. It wouldn’t be as warm as their current manor. 

Zu An headed straight to the backyard instead of entering via the main entrance—he wanted to give them a surprise. To his surprise, however, there was no one in Zheng Dan and Sang Qien’s rooms.

“Did they go out for a walk?” Zu An was disappointed.

Suddenly, he spotted a fair-skinned girl sitting on the steps, eating something that resembled a black lump. The fragrance of a sweet potato wafted in the air. He walked over and saw the girl wiping her mouth every now and then while munching the sweet potato. This resulted in her chubby cheeks being smeared black like a spotted kitten.

The girl leaped in shock when she realized that someone else was here, and she instinctively hid the sweet potato behind her. But when she saw it was Zu An, she breathed a sigh of relief and said, “I thought it was mother.” She took out the sweet potato and continued munching on it.

“Who’s your mother?” Seeing the girl’s familiar big eyes, Zu An could already fathom a guess about her identity.

“Mother is mother,” the girl mumbled through her full mouth. She thought that this uncle had asked a weird question.

Zu An took a deep breath before sitting down beside her. “Are you Sisi?”

“How do you know?” The girl finally turned away from her sweet potato to look at Zu An, her clear eyes filled with confusion.

Zu An chuckled. “Is the sweet potato delicious?”

“It’s delicious.” The little girl’s eyes lit up as she bragged in glee, “I secretly roasted this in the kitchen. Mother wouldn’t let me roast it.”

“Your mother is just worried that you’ll get hurt.” Zu An leaned closer. “The sweet potato smells good. Can you share some with me?”

The girl warily scooched away. “No way!” No wonder this weird uncle keeps talking to me. He wants my sweet potato!

She quickly stuffed the remaining sweet potato into her mouth, as if frightened that Zu An would steal it from her. It was so scalding that she kept huffing out hot air. 

Zu An found this sight funny, yet worrying. He wiped off the black stains from her cheeks as he said, “Eat slower. It’s hot.”

He took out the candied hawthorn he had just bought from the street. “Do you want it? I’ll give it to you.”

When she saw the delectable candied hawthorn, the girl gulped down her saliva. She instinctively reached out to take it, only to retract it right after. “I can’t. My mother said I mustn’t take food from strangers. The stranger could be a human trafficker.”

That made her look at Zu An with even more vigilant eyes. “Who are you? Why have I never seen you before?”

The word ‘stranger’ caused Zu An’s heart to clench up. I’m a bad father. It 's been so many years that my daughter doesn’t recognize me anymore. 

“Sisi, I heard you secretly ran off to roast sweet potatoes again?” An angry voice filled the air as a beautiful figure rushed over from afar. But when she saw Zu An, she froze on the spot.

The girl was horrified. She'd been racking her brain for an explanation when she noticed her mother abruptly falling silent. 

“Qien’er!” Zu An cried out. It had been several years, but Sang Qien was still as beautiful as ever, although she exuded a much more mature aura now. 

“Ah Zu!” Unable to curb her gushing feelings, Sang Qien rushed into Zu An’s embrace with tearful eyes, hugging him tightly as if worried that this was an illusion and he would disappear when she blinked.

Feeling her intense emotions, Zu An also hugged her tightly.  

The girl was shocked. A moment later, she became so agitated that she burst into tears. “Mother, why aren’t you hugging Sisi? Sisi won’t secretly eat roasted sweet potatoed anymore. You baddie! Let go of my mother!”

Seeing how her daughter was trying to pull Zu An away, Sang Qien bent down and lifted her up. “Sisi, you often ask me about your father. He's finally back. Here, call him ‘Father’.”

“You're my father?” The girl shyly looked at Zu An. She couldn’t imagine that the man who'd tried to dupe her out of her sweet potato was the great hero father that her mother told her about. 

Unable to hold himself back anymore, Zu An embraced Sisi. The latter felt like a meat dumpling; she was soft and fragrant in his arms. He could feel their shared bloodline. 

Sang Qien took out a handkerchief to wipe her daughter’s cheeks. “Look at you. You got so dirty eating a sweet potato again…” She felt a bit frustrated. She hadn’t expected her daughter to look so unkempt during their long-awaited reunion; it made it look as if she hadn’t taken good care of her. 

Zu An chuckled. He took the handkerchief from Sang Qien’s hand to help wipe his daughter’s face. “She’s adorable.”

The tenderness in his eyes brought a warm smile to Sang Qien. 

The girl suddenly stared intently at Zu An’s candied hawthorn. She licked her lips as she asked, “Mother, I can take food from father, right?”

“Eat, eat, eat! Haven’t I told you to be more demure?” Sang Qien was at a loss for words. Clearly, Sisi had brought her many headaches over the years. 

Zu An laughed as he passed the candied hawthorn over. “Of course.”

Seeing that her mother didn’t object to it, the girl’s eyes lit up as she took the candied hawthorn. “Thank you, father!”

Seeing her daughter forgetting about her father once she had food in her hand, Sang Qien looked at Zu An apologetically and said, “Ah Zu, this lass is fond of eating. It may take a while for her to warm up to you.”

Zu An laughed. “I think she’s adorable as she is. Kids are all like that…”

Sang Qien also looked at her daughter with warm eyes. “Indeed. She’s adorable.”

Warmth lingered between them as they chatted about recent affairs. A while later, Zu An asked out of curiosity, “Hm? Why don’t I see Zheng Dan around?”

Sang Qien replied, “Dandan has followed the army to aid the empress in quelling the rebellion.”

“Rebellion?” Zu An was startled. 

Sang Qien explained, “The Zhao clan’s kings are dissatisfied that a woman has taken the throne, and it's been years since we last heard from you. So, King Yan and the other kings staged a rebellion in the name of the imperial grandson to force Linglong to abdicate.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2636: Irrepressible Thought
chapters.body:
Chapter 2636: Irrepressible Thought

“Imperial grandson? Who?” Zu An was confused. 

“It’s Concubine Bai’s son,” Sang Qien explained. “That’s the crown prince’s only child, so he commands legitimacy. On top of that, the crown prince became the emperor later, giving his child more rallying power within the Zhao clan.”

Zu An frowned. Concubine Bai, huh… 

During their trip to Violet Mountain, Zhao Han had sensed his impending death and chosen to prematurely sentence Concubine Bai, a spy of the demonic cult, to death. He'd even instructed Zu An to make the move. It had taken a lot of effort for Zu An to save Concubine Bai by swapping her for someone else.

Later, Concubine Bai had been worried about her son, so she'd snuck back into the capital before going missing. 

Zu An's first thought was that Concubine Bai wouldn’t be the ungrateful type, but on second thought, she cherished her son so much that it wouldn’t be surprising for her to do anything to pave the path for him. 

“Those Zhao clan kings dare to rebel! I must have been too kind back then.” Zu An’s expression turned cold. He had purged Bi Qi and a few other kings from the capital, but he'd chosen to placate the others. From the looks of it, his soft approach had left behind an underlying threat.

Sang Qien let her daughter enjoy her candied hawthorn while discussing with Zu An, “You’d have incited chaos in advance if you killed those Zhao clan kings back then. We wouldn’t have been able to unite against the monsters. But after we curbed the monsters, the Zhao clan’s kings began to covet power. Furthermore, you've been gone for years, and there were rumors that you'd died in the other world. The ambitious ones couldn’t stay still anymore.”

Zu An was apologetic. “It must have been tough. I’m sorry for worrying you.”

Sang Qien smiled sweetly. “We didn't fall for those rumors. We trust that you can overcome any challenge and return safely.”

Zu An’s heart warmed up. He embraced her and relished in the family warmth. No matter the dangers he faced outside, there was someone here waiting for him. 

“Why did Dandan join the battlefield too?” he asked out of curiosity. 

“It’s all because you went against conventions and placed an empress in place.” Sang Qien rolled her eyes. “Your presence back then was too strong, to the point that no one dared say anything, but the moment you were gone, the frustrations immediately came bubbling up.

“Linglong is indeed highly competent, but even she can’t do anything about her sex. Unlike the Fiend races, we don’t have a history of empresses. There was great discontentment among the powers, so much so that they rebelled as soon as the chance arose.

“Even within the imperial court, many officials have been secretly defying her. We couldn't turn a blind eye to her predicament, so we went to help her. Thanks to our connection to you, she didn’t doubt us. Dandan led the sect she created in her earlier years to ensure the smooth flow of supplies to the frontlines. I stayed in the capital to deal with the backend logistics because I was worried about Sisi.”

Zu An fell silent after hearing her story. He could imagine how tough it had been for Bi Linglong. He was too irresponsible, to have dumped everything on her. 

“What about the empress dowager?” he continued probing about the situation.

“The empress dowager and Linglong are tackling the inside and the outside, respectively. Linglong marched onto the battlefield, while the empress dowager stands guard in the capital. If not for the empress dowager, chaos would have broken out in the capital as soon as Linglong left.” Judging by how she addressed the latter by name, Sang Qien had to be on decent terms with Linglong.

Zu An nodded. Other than the stricter inspections at the city gates, there was nothing different about the capital and the populace. Liu Ning is indeed adept at governance. 

“I need to make a trip to the palace,” he said. Only the empress has a bird’s eye view of the situation.

Sang Qien’s expression sank. We’re already parting ways when we've only just reunited. However, she knew that this matter took precedence, so she said, “All right. I’ll wait for you to return.”

Zu An smiled. He grabbed her hand and asked, “Why don’t you come with me?”

His warm hands and gentle eyes dispelled Sang Qien's gloomy mood, and she replied, “Sure!”

But she thought of a dilemma right away. “But there’s no one to take care of Sisi here…”

Zu An lifted Sisi up and said, “She can come with us.”

“But…” Sang Qien wanted to say that this was inappropriate, but on second thought, there should be no place in this world that his daughter couldn’t go. In the end, she only said, “All right.”

So, Zu An carried his daughter with his right hand while holding Sang Qien with his left hand as they made their way to the imperial palace’s main entrance. He didn’t bypass the guards to fly in, but instead strutted in so that more people could see that he had returned. 

Sang Qien felt uncomfortable having her hand held in public, especially in a place like the imperial palace, as their relationship had been secret thus far. She tried to withdraw her hand, but Zu An held her tightly and refused to let go. With her wits, it wasn't too difficult to figure that Zu An was bringing her and their daughter to light through this.

She lowered her head and bit her lips, trying not to let her tears fall. She had already resigned herself to be Zu An’s underground lover for the rest of her life, but which woman wouldn’t want to openly appear before others with her lover? 

A commotion broke out from the palace doors. “It’s the regent! The regent is back!”

Zu An responded with a smile. He was soon escorted into the palace.

The news spread like wildfire, swiftly reaching the capital’s every nook and cranny. 

Liu Ning, who had been busy discussing state matters with the officials, put down her work and rushed out to welcome him. 

Someone secretly reminded her, “Your Majesty, this isn’t appropriate…”

The regent was still a subject. Even the emperor had to pay respects to the empress dowager; it was beneath the empress dowager to head out and greet a subject.

Liu Ning smiled. “The regent isn’t just any subject. He’s worthy of it.”

The crowd in the Imperial Library was taken aback. The empress dowager had had a permanent frown on her face for a while now; this was their first time seeing her smiling so happily. 

She looked ravishing. The sight reminded the crowd that the empress dowager wasn’t old; she was still in her prime and was exuding feminine charms.

Could the empress dowager and the regent…

The officials stifled that thought as soon as it arose, knowing it was taboo. It should be fine as long as the regent doesn’t openly marry the empress dowager. It’s not uncommon for empress dowagers in history to rear lovers anyway. 

Liu Ning lifted her skirt and rushed out of the Imperial Library. There was only a single thought left in her mind, and that was to leap into Zu An’s embrace. Years of separation had simmered her yearning to a boiling point. The rebellion of the feudal kings had also repressed her emotions to an extreme degree. Now, she just wanted to let everything loose. To hell with that empress dowager title, the imperial court, and rumors. I wouldn’t want this damned position at all if not for him… 

But when she saw Zu An holding Sang Qien’s hand and carrying a girl, her raging emotions finally calmed down a little. As she regained her rationality, she understood that it wasn't the right time for her to let her emotions run wild. 

Even so, a thought did surface in her mind: I’m going to have his baby too!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2637: The Regent And The Empress
chapters.body:
Chapter 2637: The Regent And The Empress

Seeing the empress dowager rush out excitedly, only to regain her majestic poise upon seeing their family of three, Sang Qien instinctively released Zu An’s hand. Her womanly instincts told her that Ah Zu and the empress dowager had to have something between them.

She and Dandan had shared their guesses, but because of how sensitive the empress dowager’s identity was, they hadn’t dared to investigate. Even so, at that instant, she confirmed their relationship—there was no need for concrete proof. 

She had spent many years living in the capital and understood how powerful the empress dowager was. She didn’t want to get on the empress dowager’s bad side, lest she implicate her daughter. 

Zu An could never have known that Sang Qien would have thought this far in an instant. Upon seeing Liu Ning, he smiled and said, “Paying respects to the empress dowager.”

He greeted her like a friend, showing no inclination to bow. The surrounding officials didn’t think that there was anything inappropriate about his behavior either. 

“You’re finally back.” Upon seeing Zu An’s warm smile, Liu Ning’s spirits lifted a little. 

“The recent situation must have tired you out.” Zu An thought about the kings’ uprising and figured that Liu Ning and Bi Linglong had to have their hands full dealing with the situation. 

The empress dowager’s eyes flickered. “Indeed, but now that you’re back, I’m sure my fatigue will be dispelled.”

It had been three years. She felt as if there was a flame inside her that urgently needed to be vented. Most empress dowagers who were widowed in their prime reared boy toys and lovers. She was no maiden herself, but the two men in her life had been truly top tier. After the time she'd spent with Zu An, no one else could catch her fancy anymore. 

She'd thought that she would go insane in the last three years. It was almost a relief that the kings’ rebellion had distracted her, or else she wouldn’t have known how to spend the long days without him. 

The officials frowned. Is it just me or does her tone sound off? We must be thinking too much of it, right? There’s no way the empress dowager is flirting in broad daylight. 

Only Zu An knew that was indeed what she meant. His heart quivered. What a temptress. 

But he could only feign ignorance, as there were too many people around. He coughed lightly and asked, “Your Majesty, what’s the current situation?”

“Let’s talk in the Imperial Library.” The empress dowager beckoned the officials to follow her. 

Zu An noticed that the officials present looked unfamiliar and young. The empress dowager explained through telepathy, “The imperial court has been in turmoil in recent years. The old foxes have already lined their pockets. They feign submission, only to act against me in the shadows. Linglong and I had no choice but to nurture the younger officials.”

Zu An nodded. He had been in a high position as well, and that had given him a glimpse into the power struggles in politics.

No matter how ingenious and beneficial a policy was, whether it worked out or not ultimately depended on the executor. Should the wrong person be entrusted with a policy’s execution, they could either execute the policy poorly, or warp its intent to cause outrage toward the ruler. The officials who were currently in power had been nurtured by Zhao Han, so it wouldn’t be easy for the empress dowager and Bi Linglong to bring them over to their side. 

There was a massive sandbox in the middle of the room. Zu An could tell it was a map of several provinces at a glance. Liu Ning must have been discussing curbing the rebellion with the officials earlier.

The officials quickly explained the situation to Zu An.

In the period shortly after his departure, the imperial court had remained stable due to his intimidation. But when two years passed and he showed no signs of returning, rumors had begun spreading. The empress dowager and Bi Linglong tried to suppress them, but it was to no avail.

King Yan and King Guangling had been the first to gather their armies and rebel. They'd quickly gained the support of other grandmaster kings, and the movement had eventually grown into eighteen kings marching toward the capital to demand Bi Linglong’s abdication.

“They even compiled a list of the empress dowager, the empress, and the regent’s crimes…” The officials’ voices trailed off at this point. 

Zu An was stunned. Huh? Did I receive Dong Zhuo’s script?

The officials passed Zu An a scroll submitted by the rebels. He glanced at it—most crimes listed on it were generic, but there was a jarring one accusing him of tainting the imperial harem and having a questionable relationship with the empress and the empress dowager.

He noticed a couple of yellow booklets that appeared to be storybooks circulating in the streets. He picked one up, and the booklet’s title made his eyelids twitch.

‘The Philandering Regent’

‘The Regent’s Romances’

‘The Regent and the Empress’

‘The Regent and My Mom’

… 

The booklets were so bold and ridiculous that even the thick-skinned Zu An blushed upon reading them. Did they install cameras in the imperial palace? How do they know so much?!

The other officials also had weird looks on their faces. They'd thought that the rebels had fabricated these stories to besmirch the regent, the empress, and the empress dowager, and that no one should take them seriously. But now… They couldn’t help but wonder if they were real.

Zu An coughed lightly to change the topic. He probed further about the rebellion and learned that the rebels had built incredible momentum. Zhao Han’s imposing reputation had left a strong impression on the masses, whereas the regent’s power was only known within the capital. Furthermore, it hadn’t been long since the regent came to power, so most people were still inclined to side with the Zhao clan. 

The central army had initially suffered consecutive losses. Seeing that everything was going to crumble, Bi Linglong had joined the frontline to personally lead the campaign. She'd been accompanied by the Qin clan’s two masters, the Murong clan’s father and daughter, Pei You, Gao Ying… Even Xiao Jianren had brought the Embroidered Envoys there to provide intelligence. 

The situation had stabilized, and the central army and the rebel army had come to a standstill at Phoenix Pass outside the capital. The central army couldn’t defeat the rebel army, but the rebel army couldn’t breach Phoenix Pass either. This standstill had lasted for months now. 

Bi Linglong had already done a good job, but the sharper ones could all tell that the imperial court was at a disadvantage. Many neutral parties were keeping an eye on the situation, and if they concluded that the imperial court didn’t have the means to quell the rebellion, they could either join the rebel army or declare their independence. That would pose a lot of trouble.

The empress dowager said, “Someone proposed bringing the Fiends in to quell the rebellion. The Fiend Empress dispatched an envoy over to ask Linglong if she needed help, but Linglong turned her down. The rebels have accused us of colluding with the Fiends, so bringing them in would only reinforce that position. Besides, there is enmity between humankind and the Fiend races. If we let the fiend army in, their soldiers could take the opportunity to harm the populace.”

Zu An nodded. “Linglong made the right call.”

Even though he was the Fiend races’ regent, and the Fiend Empress and the upper echelons on that side were his close friends, he understood that if a war broke out, it would be hard to restrain the bloodthirsty fiend soldiers. 

“I’ll have to personally make a trip to Phoenix Pass,” Zu An said calmly, as if the army of the eighteen kings wasn't worth a second thought.

And yet, none of those present found anything wrong with that. Their previously gloomy faces lit up with smiles, as they were confident that the regent could quell the rebels given his influence and strength. 

The empress dowager coughed. “The rest of you are dismissed. I need to discuss confidential matters with the regent.”

She turned to her lady-in-waiting and instructed her, “Show Miss Sang and little Sisi around the imperial garden. Have the imperial kitchen prepare some snacks for little Sisi.”

Sang Qien was perplexed. What confidential matters do they have to discuss? It feels like she's just evicting us from here. Look at her eyes—she’s on the verge of gobbling Ah Zu up!

Pui! She’s using this as a pretext to fulfill her personal needs.

Despite having guessed the truth, Sang Qien couldn’t fight the empress dowager over Zu An in public. If that were to happen, they would become a laughingstock. Besides, as an official’s daughter, she didn’t stand a chance against the empress dowager. 

Forget it, I’ll turn a blind eye to this on the account that she’s treating Sisi well. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2638: Confident
chapters.body:
Chapter 2638: Confident

After sending away the officials, Sang Qien, and Sisi, the empress dowager dismissed the maids and eunuchs in the room with a wave of her hand. This should have been highly inappropriate, but no one questioned her order. The empress dowager had gained absolute control over the people around her, and her closest eunuchs and maids were aware of her affair with the regent. Sometimes, they'd even had to cover for her.

Once the maids closed the door behind them, the empress dowager rushed into Zu An’s arms. 

Zu An embraced her mature body as he asked with a smile, “Ning’er, what do you need to discuss with me?”

The empress dowager raised her head, resting her lower jaw on his chest as she said with a pout, “You only call me that in private. Why didn’t you call me Ning’er earlier?”

Zu An burst into laughter. “You’re the empress dowager. We need to pay heed to your reputation in public.”

“What about Sang Qien and Sisi? You held their hands in public,” the empress dowager complained. 

Zu An realized that she was jealous. Instead of explaining himself, he leaned in and kissed her. Only a fool would debate about right and wrong at a time like this. What she needs is emotional affirmation. 

The empress dowager moaned. Her lover’s sweet nothings quelled the anger bubbling inside her, and her body gradually relaxed. 

Some time later, the two of them began talking about the kings’ rebellion and the imperial court’s initial defeat. 

“A skilled housewife can’t prepare a meal without rice.” The empress dowager explained the circumstances then. “Rumors of your death in the other world caused hearts to waver. With those under us thinking about who to side with, it’s no wonder our army suffered defeat after defeat. Linglong and I may be capable, but we're reliant on our subordinates to execute our orders. The situation only stabilized when Linglong joined the frontlines while I held fort in the capital.”

“You've worked hard,” Zu An said, grabbing her hand.

The empress dowager burst into laughter, lighting up the solemn imperial library. “Regent, do you want to know what happened in this time?”

“Of course.” Zu An figured that it wouldn’t hurt to know more about his enemies. With the rebellion having built up so much momentum, it was important for him to draw a clear line between friends and foes. 

“Regent, please have a seat. Allow me to slowly explain things to you.” The empress dowager pushed Zu An toward the throne. 

Zu An was initially perplexed as to why he had to sit on the throne for them to talk. Where’s Liu Ning going to sit then?

The empress dowager chuckled. She tied her hair up before ducking under the table. Everything immediately made sense to Zu An then.

The empress dowager had a skilled mouth. She shared the details slowly, but in detail. 

Zu An could finally make sense of the major events that had happened while he was gone. Some time later, the empress dowager leaned toward Zu An’s ear and whispered, “Ah Zu, I want a child.”

“But you’re the empress dowager.” Zu An was put in a tight spot. 

“I don’t care. I want to have your child.” The empress dowager also felt as if something had come over her. She had been the empress for many years, but she'd never been concerned about her lack of offspring. And yet, seeing Zu An carry a girl into the palace struck something deep in her. 

Her repressed emotions suddenly burst out at that instant. She realized that she was fond of children all along too; it was just that she hadn’t been willing to bear Zhao Han’s babies. 

Besides, Zhao Han wouldn’t have let it happen either. The Liu clan had two empresses. If both were to give birth to sons, the Liu clan’s influence would have grown to an extent where it would be hard to keep them in check. Thus, she'd had no choice but to live with it.

However, her desire to become a mother suddenly became ridiculously intense. 

Zu An fell silent. 

Liu Ning’s identity was simply too special. The empress dowager bearing a child would be a massive scandal, even when taking humankind’s long history into consideration. It would be impossible to predict what would happen. 

The empress dowager’s expression sank, but she soon revealed a seductive smile and said, “I’m joking with you. I am still young and haven’t had enough fun yet. Children are a hassle to deal with.”

Zu An could tell she didn’t mean those words, and that she was merely finding an excuse to end the topic. All he had to do was to play along with her.

And yet, he gently caressed her cheeks and nodded solemnly. “All right.”

“All right?” The empress dowager’s voice quivered in disbelief.

“Let’s have a child,” Zu An said with a smile.

The empress dowager was stunned. She never expected him to accept her request. For a moment there, she felt that she hadn’t lived in vain.

Back when they first started, she had still been the lofty empress, whereas he was a lowly Embroidered Envoy. Taking that first step had left her incredibly nervous and conflicted. She had also wondered if he merely coveted her wealth and standing, or maybe just her beauty.

As she grew closer to him, she'd discarded the first possibility. But as for the second possibility, while Zu An had many young, beautiful lovers, she was the only more mature lover he had, even though she could hardly be considered old at all. 

Sometimes, in the dead of the night, she'd wondered if Zu An truly loved her. But she swiftly extinguished this thought whenever it surfaced, as she dared not think deeply into it. She was a mature woman. Her rational mind told her that there was no need to get to the bottom of that; it was enough that they had fun whenever they were together. 

But at this moment, she realized that she had a place in his heart. He cared about her feelings and would think on her behalf. This was enough for her. 

Her mind suddenly cleared up, and she said, “Ah Zu, my identity is sensitive. It’ll bring too much trouble if I have a child. I’m already satisfied enough to hear you say those words. Thank you.”

Zu An pulled her into his embrace. “Don’t worry. I’ll deal with whatever trouble that arises.”

The empress dowager wanted to say more, but he was too domineering. There was also no way she would reject him after three years of separation. She glanced at the throne, and her face turned bright red. This fellow seems to get more excited whenever he sits on this chair. And for some reason, he doesn’t allow me to take off my phoenix gown… 

…

In the rebel army’s commander tent at the distant Phoenix Pass, King Yan looked at the tightly guarded wall and remarked, “Bi Linglong is a capable one. If she had come a day later, I would have already breached Phoenix Pass. The world would have belonged to our Zhao clan.”

Zhao Yuan replied, “No matter how capable she is, she is but a woman. Many in the imperial court refuse to submit to her. You need not worry, King Yan. This standstill is to our advantage. More people will be swayed toward our side. Even those guarding Phoenix Pass aren’t united as one; quite a few people have secretly reached out to us. It’s only a matter of time before we breach the pass.”

King Yan frowned. “A mere Bi Linglong doesn’t worry me. But the longer we stall, the more uneasy I feel. Is it possible that Zu An is still alive?”

That name made Zhao Yuan frown, but he replied, “There’s nothing to worry about, King Yan. Years have passed. He would have long since returned if he was alive. Besides, we've rallied over a million soldiers to our cause. He can’t stop us single-handedly.”

King Yan was still worried. “But that fellow wields terrifying prowess. He might not be no match for our million-troop-strong army in a direct confrontation, but what if he chooses to act against us in the shadows?”

“Don’t worry. I have the means to deal with him even if he returns,” Zhao Yuan replied with a mysterious smile. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2639: The City Is Breached
chapters.body:
Chapter 2639: The City Is Breached

King Yan’s eyes lit up. “Really?”

Zhao Yuan looked at him with deep eyes. “Do I look like the type to joke around?”

“That’s true…” King Yan figured that Zhao Yuan was a highly reliable and calculating individual. “How do you intend to deal with him?”

“It won’t do to reveal the answer beforehand. You’ll naturally know what it is if we ever reach that point.” Zhao Yuan stood up and left. 

After Zhao Yuan was gone, King Yan’s aide walked over and said, “Your Highness, Zhao Yuan is keeping many things from you. He doesn’t seem to respect you.”

King Yan narrowed his eyes. “He must be guarded against.”

The world believed he was leading the rebellion, but the true mastermind was Zhao Yuan. Granted that he had contributed by reaching out to several kings, it was insincere and dishonest from Zhao Yuan’s side to withhold intelligence from him. Who’s the boss here?

Unfortunately, Zhao Yuan had served the imperial guards a few years back and knew the imperial court’s secrets better. On top of that, he had close ties with the other kings and key officials. For the time being, King Yan couldn’t cut off Zhao Yuan yet.

Hmph, I’ll first use him to take down the capital before slowly settling the scores afterward.

He wasn’t worried, as he had the largest army among the eighteen rebelling kings. He had always wanted the throne and had been secretly making preparations for it. The other kings, who had decided to participate in the rebellion on a whim, couldn’t hope to match him. 

… 

In the capital, the empress dowager was still explaining the details of the rebellion to Zu An. She lay in Zu An’s arms like a kitten, her face flushed. Her usual solemnity was nowhere to be seen. Even her voice felt much gentler and sweeter than usual. 

Zu An recalled what had just happened, and he had to concede that the empress dowager was indeed in her prime. He could always go all out with her. 

“Shall I call Miss Sang to join us?” The empress dowager drew circles on her lover’s chest, her eyes reflecting a hint of fear. It has only been a few years, but Ah Zu’s body has become much stronger than before. Can he still be considered a human now?

Zu An huffed. “Don’t change the subject. Let’s continue talking about the rebels. Have they not tried anything else after being stuck at a standstill at Phoenix Pass for several months?”

“Indeed. Linglong and I were worried that they were merely tying down our troops while ravaging the other provinces, spreading chaos everywhere. But they didn’t do so. Perhaps the eighteen kings aren’t as united as we thought,” the empress dowager replied. 

“The rebel army must have smart people in their ranks for their movement to even grow to such a scale. They would have considered doing that, and there must be a reason they aren’t doing so.” Zu An spent a moment in thought before suddenly murmuring, “Could they be confident in taking down Phoenix Pass?”

The empress dowager was startled. “That can’t be. We've already overcome the most dangerous phase, and Linglong is holding the fort at the frontlines. The situation has already stabilized. With Linglong’s ability, the rebel army shouldn’t be able to breach Phoenix Pass.”

“Let’s hope so.” Zu An still felt unnerved despite saying so. “This won’t do. I should quickly make my way to Phoenix Pass.” There were too many people he cared about there. He was worried they would face danger.

As Zu An hurriedly put his clothes on, the empress dowager harrumphed. “What a heartless man. Wiping your hands and leaving right after the deed is done.” Despite her grumbling, she still got up to tidy her lover’s clothes.

Zu An kissed her. “I’ll come back to accompany you once I quell the rebellion.”

The empress dowager walked him to the entrance. She couldn’t leave in her current state, or else anyone who saw her would immediately understand what had just happened. 

She watched as Zu An’s figure disappeared before returning. Barely after taking a few steps, she suddenly realized something and quickly flitted to the bed by the side. She lay down flat and raised her legs against the wall. She couldn’t let any of it go to waste. 

She thought about the earlier warmth and bit her lip. I hope I can have his child this time. 

… 

After leaving the imperial library, Zu An found Sang Qien and Sisi playing in the imperial garden.

Rather than playing, however, perhaps it was more apt to say that they were dining. A magnificent spread had been laid out in a gazebo. Sisi held a chicken thigh in one hand and a sweet beverage in the other while still chewing on a cake. 

Sang Qien sighed as she wiped Sisi’s mouth. “Slow down. No one is going to steal your food.”

Glancing at the nearby maids, who were smiling warmly at them, Sang Qien felt so embarrassed that she wanted to hide in a pit. She felt as if she would die of shame if the empress dowager and Linglong learned about this. 

“It’s good to be able to eat well. It shows that she’s healthy,” Zu An said with a chuckle as he walked over and lifted Sisi up.
