“Father, the food is yummy. Do you want some too?” Sisi asked as she offered him an oily chicken drumstick. 

“Look at how dirty your hands are!” Sang Qien anxiously wiped the grease off Zu An’s robe.

The maids were startled. This girl calls the regent her father! And looking at how Miss Sang doesn’t hesitate to wipe the regent’s clothes, there's no question that they are a family. 

They had guessed that the girl was from a distinguished background, but they'd thought that the regent was only doting on her because of Sang Qien. They hadn’t expected her to be the regent’s blood daughter. They patted their chests, relieved that they hadn’t been perfunctory when serving the mother and daughter duo.

Sang Qien noticed the maids’ expressions and realized what was going on. She wanted to teach her daughter not to recklessly call Zu An ‘father’ in public, but she didn't know how to say it in front of so many people. 

Zu An gestured that it was fine with a shake of his head before munching down on Sisi’s chicken drumstick with a smile. “Mm, Sisi’s chicken drumstick is the best!”

He took such a huge bite that Sisi was startled. Her mouth quivered, and she looked as if she was about to burst into tears. “How can you eat it all? Bad father!”

Her adorable demeanor made Zu An burst into laughter. “Yes, it’s father’s fault. I’ll get ten more drumsticks for you.”

The heartwarming interaction between the father and daughter brought a tender smile to Sang Qien’s face. Hearing Zu An affirm Sisi’s identity lifted a boulder that had been weighing on her heart for many years. 

The maids, however, were on the verge of tears. Are we allowed to know this? Will we be silenced for knowing too much? 

The regent had a righteous reputation, but having worked in the imperial palace for many years, they understood that knowing too much could spell one’s death. 

Looking at the nervous maids, Zu An chuckled. “You may leave first. I’d like to talk to Miss Sang privately.”

“Yes!” The maids exhaled in relief and quickly took their leave. 

Zu An placed his daughter down before the table, allowing her to gorge herself, before heading to the lakeside with Sang Qien. 

“Thank you.” There had been much to say at the back of Sang Qien’s throat, but it eventually came out as those two simple words. 

Zu An embraced her. “I won’t let the two of you suffer any grievances.”

Sang Qien smiled sweetly as she nuzzled her cheek against his chest. All of a sudden, she raised her head and stared at him. “You’re covered in the empress dowager’s scent.”

Zu An sweated profusely. I got careless!

“Did you satisfy the empress dowager?” Sang Qien had initially felt stifled when the empress dowager sent her away, but the earlier interaction lifted her spirits. She was now in the mood to tease Zu An.

“It should be the empress dowager satisfying me.” Zu An harrumphed. 

“Ah?” Sang Qien was initially surprised to hear those words, but on second thought, it made perfect sense. Her face reddened. “You always defy conventions. I wonder what the empress dowager is like when she’s satisfying you…”

“I can call you along if you want to know. The empress dowager was thinking of it earlier.”

“Pui. I’m not going to go crazy with the two of you.”

After some joking around, Zu An finally revealed his purpose. 

Sang Qien was saddened to hear that he was going to leave again, but she replied, “Your worry isn’t unfounded. Dandan and the Crown Prince Consort are there. It’ll be disastrous if something happens. You should hurry there.”

After exchanging farewells, Zu An rose into the sky and made a beeline toward Phoenix Pass. 

The capital had rules against flight. A formation swiftly whirred into action as experts rushed out from all directions to stop the lawbreaker, but when they saw that it was Zu An, they froze up. 

Zu An exchanged pleasantries with them before he morphed into a rainbow and disappeared into the distance. He was intentionally revealing his face to let the entire capital know that he had returned safely. This should make those who are wavering settle down. 

… 

Night descended upon Phoenix Pass. 

Shortly after Bi Linglong completed her military duties and went to sleep, she was awakened by an uproar. She immediately sat upward. An ominous feeling gripped her heart. It was abnormal to hear such a commotion on a silent night. 

Soon after, a golden-armored Murong Qinghe marched into her tent and reported, “Bad news, Your Majesty. The rebels have breached the city!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2640: A Desperate Fight
chapters.body:
Chapter 2640: A Desperate Fight

As soon as she heard the commotion outside, Bi Linglong swiftly put on her clothes. Naturally, her dress here was nowhere as elaborate as when she was in the palace. What she heard shocked her.

“Come in!” 

The guard opened the door, and Murong Qinghe entered anxiously. Her tall stature and her golden armor gave her an air of valiance, but she looked flustered. She quickly repeated what she had just said. 

Bi Linglong’s face darkened. “General Murong, don’t talk nonsense!” Were it not Murong Qinghe reporting this matter, she would have had the other party punished to stabilize the army’s morale. 

Unsurprisingly, the nearby guards and maids began whispering under their breath in panic. 

“It’s true, Your Majesty. Chaos suddenly broke out in the city. I was patrolling the streets when I chanced upon it. The rebel army was in the city. I ordered the troops to stall them before rushing here,” Murong Qinghe said anxiously. “The rebels seem to be after Your Majesty. Your Majesty, you have to retreat somewhere safe!” 

“That’s impossible. Phoenix Pass is tightly guarded. How could the rebel army slip in without any notice?” 

Even though Bi Linglong had always trusted Murong Qinghe, at this very moment, she was secretly hoping that the latter had betrayed her and was relaying fake news. She pushed Murong Qinghe aside and walked out of the room. She was staying at a higher elevation, which allowed her to have a bird’s eye view of the situation in the city. 

Flames blazed furiously in the pass. Screams and cries filled the air everywhere. Soldiers were slaughtering the civilians one-sidedly.

Bi Linglong’s heart sank. Under her leadership, Phoenix Pass had become heavily reinforced. It was inconceivable to her how it could be breached just like that. 

There must be traitors!

That was the only explanation. The rebel army couldn’t have breached their defenses otherwise. 

“Relay my orders. General Gao and General Pei are to lead the troops to stall the enemy soldiers, while the others are to gather at the inner city right away.” 

Bi Linglong knew the troops she had in the city were no weaker than the rebel army’s. The problem was that most of her troops were asleep, whereas the enemy had come prepared. In their current state, they were no match for the rebel army. Her only shot at turning the tables was to rally her troops together.

“Your Majesty, we won’t make it in time.” Murong Qinghe was awed by Bi Linglong’s composure, but as a military veteran, she had to offer her judgment. 

“We have to give it a try nevertheless.” Bi Linglong clutched a pillar so tightly that her fingers turned pale. She understood that there was nothing more fearsome to an army than a night raid.

Panic was infectious. It was hard to gauge how strong an enemy force was when facing a night raid. Without any intelligence, one could easily exaggerate the threat. Once an army succumbed to panic, they were doomed to be obliterated. 

But Bi Linglong had no choice. If the rebel army were to breach Phoenix Pass, the capital would be in danger. At this rate, she could lose the throne. While she had no desire to cling to her position as empress, Zu An had entrusted this country to her. How could she face him if she lost the country in just a few years?

Despite the rumors that Zu An had died in the other world, she believed that the man who had created so many miracles would return alive. 

Just then, a guard rushed over and reported, “Your Majesty, the messenger was killed by a rebel soldier shortly after he was dispatched. We have no way to get in touch with the two generals.”

Bi Linglong felt as if her heart had been plunged into cold water. The worst situation had happened.

“Where are the formation masters? Can’t you send the message directly?!” she roared with gritted teeth. Teleportation formations consumed a huge amount of resources, but this was an emergency. While refining Phoenix Pass’ defensive system in the last few days, she'd set up teleportation formations in several crucial spots so that they could communicate even in such situations. 

“I’ve checked it. Someone tampered with the formations. They wouldn’t activate!” the fully armored Zhang Zitong rushed in and reported. As the commander of the imperial guards, she had to accompany the empress wherever she went.

Bi Linglong was enraged. “Every formation is heavily guarded, yet someone still managed to tamper with them? Where are the Embroidered Envoys? What are they doing?!”

Zhang Zitong’s eyelids twitched. It was a major slip up from the Embroidered Envoys to not send a heads up when something this huge had happened. But she couldn’t help but speak up for her former colleague, saying, “Lord Xiao says that there must be traitors among the upper echelons in the city. He has personally set out to contact the generals, and he warns Your Majesty to think carefully about who to trust.”

Bi Linglong’s face turned ice-cold. For the rebel army to slip into the city and quietly tamper with so many important formations, there had to be several high-ranking defectors. 

The clashing sounds grew closer. Zhang Zitong grew anxious. “Your Majesty, the rebel army is on its way here. We need to retreat right now!”

“Retreat? Where can I retreat to?” Bi Linglong looked bitter. Beyond Phoenix Pass was a plain, so the rebel army could make a beeline for the capital. The imperial court would have no way to stop them anymore.

Zhang Zitong understood that, but she still advised, “Your Majesty, the regent has entrusted me with your safety. As long as you survive this ordeal, we can always come up with something.”

The mention of Ah Zu raised Bi Linglong’s low morale. She took a deep breath and commanded, “Relay my order. Hoist up my personal banner and the White Tiger Banner. Have the soldiers rally toward me.”

The White Tiger Banner could only be used by the emperor. It represented a strong offensive, which meant that she was determined to put up one last fight. 

Zhang Zitong was horrified. “You mustn’t! It will be too conspicuous in the middle of the night. Not only will our soldiers see it, but the rebel army will also know where you are! If they encircle this area, we won’t be able to get away.”

Zhang Zitong was confident she could escort Bi Linglong out of the city if they did it secretly, but the same couldn’t be said if they made a huge fuss about it. 

“Escape? There are still many soldiers fighting in the city. How can I flee on my own?” Bi Linglong’s expression turned cold. “Execute my order!”

Zhang Zitong still wanted to dissuade her, but Murong Qinghe interjected, “I’m willing to put my life on the line with Your Majesty!”

Murong Qinghe grabbed the war horn and blew it. A majestic call echoed through Phoenix Pass. A brilliant yellow banner and the solemn White Tiger Banner were raised at a vantage point in the city. 

The morale of the fleeing imperial soldiers lifted when they saw the banners. “Our empress is still fighting with us!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2641: Reinforcement
chapters.body:
Chapter 2641: Reinforcement

Most of the imperial soldiers had woken up in the middle of the night, hearing that the rebel army had infiltrated the city. They were surrounded by enemies. The commanders couldn’t find their soldiers, and the soldiers couldn’t get to their commanders. Under such circumstances, they could only obey their primal instincts and flee, thus leading to a one-sided slaughter.

Some had tried to rally the soldiers together to resist, but there were too few of them, so the enemies overran them swiftly. Their defeat further lowered the morale of their comrades, leading them to dare not fight back. The only thought left in their minds had been to flee. 

But when they saw the two magnificent banners flying high, and someone shouting that the empress was still fighting, they suddenly felt as if they had found their backbone. 

“Everyone, head to the White Tiger Banner!”

“For the empress!”

… 

The squads that had been wandering around like aimless flies finally found their center and regained their morale. They began rallying around Bi Linglong. 

King Yan, who had been spectating the battle from afar, saw the imperial army slowly regaining their footing and remarked, “Bi Linglong is indeed a talent. Despite being a woman, she shows tremendous courage in a crisis.”

King Guangling, Zhao Yuan, nodded. “She handled administrative tasks well while she was still the crown prince’s consort. Many in the imperial court respect her.”

Everyone had known the crown prince as a fool. Bi Linglong had been the one propping up the Eastern Palace.

“It’s a pity she betrayed the imperial family’s trust. She sided with an outsider instead of our Zhao clan.” King Yan harrumphed.

“You wouldn’t have had a chance if not for that,” King Guangling remarked with a chuckle.

Glee flickered across King Yan’s face, but he quickly restrained himself and said, “I am doing this for the sake of our Great Zhou.”

King Guangling played along. “Your Highness’ loyalty to the country is an example that we should follow.” I wonder who was preparing for a rebellion all this time. Don’t you think it’s a bit thick-skinned to play the good person here?

King Yan knew the other party was retorting in his heart, but he didn’t mind it. He had the highest seniority and commanded the most prestige among the kings, making him the de facto leader of this alliance. 

I ought to thank Zu An. Zhao Han’s bloodline is mostly gone. Once we arrive at the capital, no one can compete with me for the throne. I only need to defeat the imperial army here and kill Bi Linglong… 

The thought of that suddenly made him worried. “Is there any chance of her turning the tables?”

Zhao Yuan replied, “You’re thinking too much. The imperial court has lost its momentum. She’s merely making a desperate struggle. We should be more worried about her escaping amid the chaos. Someone of her ability could make a comeback. We should be glad that she’s insisting on fighting to her death for some cheap sentimentality. This gives us a chance to take her down for good.”

King Yan’s eyes lit up. “Indeed. Everything will be settled if we kill her here!”

He summoned his aide and deployed his elite troops to capture Bi Linglong, promising that the one who accomplished the feat would be conferred a hereditary marquis position. 

The handsome reward made the rebel soldiers’ eyes turn red. They roared in enthusiasm as they charged toward the White Tiger Banner. 

The fighting intensified. The imperial army desperately rallied around the White Tiger Banner, but the rebel army was also making its way over. 

At the city lord’s residence, Zhang Zitong anxiously urged Bi Linglong, “Your Majesty, the rebel army is a mere three hundred steps away from here. If we don’t leave now, it’ll be too late!”

Her body was soaked in blood. She'd been caught in a battle in the brief moment she had left to scout the frontlines.

Bi Linglong coldly eyed Zhang Zitong as she drew her sword. “Captain Tong, if you continue uttering words that shake our army’s morale, I’m going to punish you under military law.”

Zhang Zitong’s heart shuddered. Bi Linglong had commanded an air of majesty even when she was the Crown Prince’s consort; after she became the empress, her disposition had only gotten even stronger. She did not doubt that Bi Linglong would kill her on the spot if she continued trying to persuade her. 

Bi Linglong retracted her gaze and said, “I don’t need you to protect me. Take the imperial guards to the frontlines to fight the rebels. We have to give it our all for a chance to turn the tables around.”

She had been observing the battlefield. Raising the White Tiger Banner had greatly raised the morale of their imperial army, but the rebel army had also come prepared. It also didn’t help that the imperial soldiers were rushing here haphazardly from all directions. Murong Qinghe had led her soldiers to receive the incoming imperial soldiers, but the rebel army was so strong that they quickly fell into a disadvantageous position. 

Bi Linglong keenly knew that their defense line was on the verge of crumbling, and she needed to devote all her soldiers to hold their ground. 

Zhang Zitong wanted to warn the empress that she wouldn’t have guards with her, but she quickly remembered the latter’s earlier warning. She held her tongue and said, “Understood. Imperial guards, Embroidered Envoys, follow me!”

She led her subordinates down to the frontlines. With their efforts, the situation at the defense line stabilized a little. 

Murong Qinghe was astonished to see Zhang Zitong. “You’re here too? What about Her Majesty?”

Zhang Zitong replied with a bitter smile, “Her Majesty sent me here.”

Murong Qinghe fell silent. She knew that given the current situation, there was no other option, and she had to concede that Bi Linglong was a decisive leader. 

Someone in the rebel army bellowed, “Even the imperial guards have been mobilized. They're cornered. Once we breach this defense line, victory will be ours!”

Cheers broke out in the rebel army as they fearlessly charged forth. This was their opportunity to earn a hereditary marquis position; it could turn their fates around for good.

The pressure on Murong Qinghe and Zhang Zitong increased. Their comrades collapsed one after another, and even they were sustaining injuries. In a battle of this scale, unless they were at the regent’s level, an individual’s valiance was inconsequential; not to mention that the rebel army was filled with experts too. The two women exchanged looks of despair.

“Who says we don’t have people?”

Another army suddenly arrived at the scene. It was led by a silver-haired old man—it was Qin Zheng from the Qin clan. He was accompanied by Qin Yongde and Qin Guangyuan. Qin Zheng commanded great prestige in the army, so his arrival greatly lifted the imperial army’s morale. 

A king from the rebel army shouted, “What’s there to fear? That old fart has one foot in the grave! Besides, they don’t have many troops left!”

Qin Zheng had brought his personal elite troops, but they were indeed nothing much compared to the scale of this battle.

“Don’t forget about us!” Pei You and Gao Ying joined the fray with their troops too. Cheers broke out among the imperial soldiers. 

The rebel army’s morale took a hit, but a king quickly yelled that they still had the numerical advantage. They continued their advancement, but with fresh blood joining the imperial army, their momentum was slowed.  

As the situation on the battlefield stabilized, Bi Linglong exhaled in relief. She had overcome the danger for the time being. As more imperial soldiers gathered in the area, they would have a chance to turn the tables around.

Just then, a figure descended from the sky. “Duke, I’ll be entrusting this place to you. I’ll protect Her Majesty!”

It was Du Jian, one of the Eight Dukes. His son had been the empress’ aide in the Eastern Palace. Qin Zheng exhaled in relief and said, “I’ll be leaving Her Majesty to you, Du Jian.” 

He gestured for his subordinates to open a path for Du Jian.

Bi Linglong frowned. “Lord Du, I don’t need any protection. Go and aid General Qin instead.”

Du Jian smiled. “Your Majesty, please leave the battlefield to the soldiers. I’ll take you somewhere safer.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2642: Two Walnuts To Kill Three Officials
chapters.body:
Chapter 2642: Two Walnuts To Kill Three Officials

Bi Linglong rejected the offer without hesitation. “How can I leave when my soldiers are fighting with their lives on the line? Now that Lord Du is here, I should have nothing to worry about. If we lose the battle here, I won’t be safe even if I return to the capital.”

Du Jian tried to persuade her, “Your Majesty is right, but you didn’t consider how the rebel army slipped into the reinforced Phoenix Pass in the first place. There must be a traitor among us, and that traitor is likely to be a high-ranking person. I’m not confident I can protect you against the traitors.”

“Are you saying that there’s a traitor among the other Eight Dukes?” Bi Linglong asked with a frown. Given their high standing and great prestige, she had brought the Eight Dukes with her to the battlefield, as they could stir up problems in the capital otherwise. Their high cultivation could be an asset on the battlefield, too. That being said, she'd remained guarded against them. 

Du Jian’s son had once been an advisor in the crown prince’s Eastern Palace, and she'd been the one in charge of managing the Eastern Palace, so there were some ties between them. Similarly, Imperial Preceptor Yu Rui was from the Yu clan; Zu An was close to Yu Yanluo, and Yu Yanluo trusted Yu Rui.

Thus, Bi Linglong had tasked Du Jian and Yu Rui to keep an eye on Great General Cheng Yuan and Imperial Tutor Zhuang He. There were only four of the Eight Dukes here, but that was because Zu An had already dealt with the other four. 

Are Cheng Yuan and Zhuang He the traitors here?

“They are indeed suspicious. Despite the ongoing crisis, neither of them is stepping forward to hold the fort,” Du Jian replied.

“Old Du, it’s not good to speak behind others’ backs.” A cold harrumph came as a figure descended from the sky—it was Imperial Tutor Zhuang He.

“Indeed. I rushed here as soon as I was done organizing the soldiers. Rather, I find it perplexing how Lord Du rushed here so quickly without doing anything,” another old man said as he walked over from another direction. It was Great General Cheng Yuan. 

Du Jian was unfazed. “I am merely speaking the truth. Phoenix Pass was suddenly breached, and none of the formations in the city are working. There must be a traitor among us. To tamper with the formations and quietly open the city gates; the traitor must be a high-ranking, influential individual.”

Zhuang He’s expression turned cold. “Are you implying we’re the traitors?”

Cheng Yuan also coldly glared at Du Jian with narrowed eyes.

“I’m merely pointing out the most reasonable conjecture.” Du Jian shielded Bi Linglong behind him. “As far as I know, Brother Zhuang had a conflict with the regent a while back and has been harboring a grudge against the latter. I wouldn’t be surprised if you were dissatisfied with Her Majesty too.”

Bi Linglong also remembered that incident. Zhuang He had publicly opposed Zu An, but the latter’s decisiveness in killing the Zhao clan’s Eight Dukes forced him to lower his head. 

Zhuang He’s face reddened in shame. “I did what I did for the sake of the country; it has nothing to do with the regent! Don’t you utter falsehoods to mislead others now!”

“I am being objective here,” Du Jian replied.

Great General Cheng Yuan spoke up too, “Brother Du Jian, how can you say you’re being objective when you’re attacking Brother Zhuang? You are suspicious as well. How did someone tamper with the formations under your watch?”

“The traitors came prepared. I was caught off guard,” Du Jian refuted him. “Besides, you were in charge of guarding the city gates. Who knows whether you let the rebels in?”

“Enough!” Bi Linglong’s face turned frosty. She didn’t trust any of these people fully, but she needed all the manpower she could get right now, especially experts of the Eight Dukes’ caliber. 

People of their stature wouldn’t be tasked with grunt work like guarding the formations and the city gates. They had merely been assigned as the reinforcements if anything went wrong with those areas. 

“We shouldn’t be fighting among ourselves when our survival is at stake! We’ll only become laughingstocks!” 

Du Jian interjected, “Your Majesty, we have to root out the traitor, or else they could turn against us at a critical moment later on, rendering our efforts futile.”

Zhuang He berated him, "You keep talking about catching the traitor, but who knows if you are the traitor here?” 

“What are you saying, you old fool?!” Du Jian roared. The tension between them heightened, and it looked as if they were going to duel each other to the death. 

“Enough!” Their argument made Bi Linglong’s head hurt. Their words made sense, and none was clear of suspicion. With her sharp wits, she quickly came up with a plan. “There’s an easy way to determine who’s the traitor. You three head to the frontlines, and the one who kills the most rebel soldiers is the least likely to be a spy.”

The three old men fell silent. They were awed by Bi Linglong’s wits. The rebel army was already close to victory. Going on a massacre right now was bound to draw the eighteen kings’ ire. The true traitor wouldn’t do something like that. 

Great General Cheng Yuan said, “Your Majesty is wise, but I’m worried that the true spy will hold Your Majesty hostage after I leave. That would be disastrous.”

Du Jian huffed, “Old Cheng, why are you looking at me?”

Zhuang He sneered, “You should know the reason best.”

“You must be tired of living!” Du Jian roared. The two of them began bickering. 

Looking at the three bickering old men chilled Bi Linglong’s heart. Her straightforward ploy should have revealed the traitor. Even if the traitor played along and fought on the frontlines, it would still be to her benefit. And yet, these three were bickering here. Despite their righteous words, they were refusing to execute her order.

This could only mean one thing—they were all traitors! That thought plunged her into despair. Of the trio, she'd trusted Du Jian the most because of his ties with the Eastern Palace. She hadn't expect the other party to betray her. 

But this wasn’t the time to pursue the issue. Her only leverage in this situation was that the trio didn’t seem to know one another’s identities. It could be because they were serving different kings, or that the rebel army hadn’t revealed their presence to the other traitors due to their sensitive identities…

Regardless, she had to find a way to turn the trio against one another so she would have a chance to take them down. 

So, she declared, “Lord Du, General Cheng, apprehend Imperial Tutor Zhuang first.”

Zhuang He was horrified. “Your Majesty, are you suspecting me now?”

“Lord Du is right. You have harbored dissatisfaction against me and the regent, and that makes you the likeliest suspect. You need not worry. I’ll investigate this matter, and I’ll personally apologize to you if you have been framed.” Bi Linglong eyed Du Jian and Cheng Yuan. “What are you waiting for?”

Du Jian and Cheng Yuan were overjoyed. They had been worried about exposing their identities, leading to a two-against-one situation. If they could eliminate Zhuang He first, it would boil down to a one-on-one, thus increasing their chances of victory. Thus, the two of them charged at Zhuang He.

Zhuang He fended against their attacks while shouting, “Your Majesty, you fail to identify who is loyal to you! You’re bringing the empire to ruin!”

Soon, however, he no longer had the energy to continue shouting. Du Jian and Cheng Yuan’s relentless attacks quickly cornered him, to the point that he could lose his life at any moment. 

Bi Linglong calmly watched as Du Jian and Cheng Yuan dished out killing blow after killing blow. Just as I thought.

Just then, a sigh filled the air. “You fools. How can you let a mere lass take you for a ride?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2643: Cornered
chapters.body:
Chapter 2643: Cornered

An old man dressed in a fluttering white robe flew over. He had a smooth complexion. It wouldn’t have been surprising if he had charmed many women in his younger days.

“Brother Yu!” The fighting trio stopped upon seeing him. They dared not continue fighting, as they didn’t know the old man’s stance and were worried that he would stab them in the back.

The man who had just arrived was one of the Eight Dukes, Imperial Preceptor Yu Rui. 

Bi Linglong should have been overjoyed by Yu Rui’s arrival, since considering Yu Yanluo and Zu An’s relationship, the latter should have been an ally. But what Yu Rui had just said revealed too much about his stance.

The other three looked at Yu Rui with confused eyes as they asked, “Brother Yu, what did you mean by that?”

“Exactly as you heard,” Yu Rui huffed. “The three of you are comrades, and yet you let her manipulate you into fighting among yourselves. You must have lived all these years in vain.”

Usually, they would have lost their tempers right away if anyone dared reprimand them like that, but at this moment, they were more concerned about what Yu Rui had just said. They exchanged looks as they wondered… Could it be… 

Yu Rui was worried that these fools would continue fighting, so he clearly spelled it out for them. “King Guangling told me that you secretly pledged your allegiance to different kings, which means we are in the same faction. If we continue fighting amongst ourselves, we’ll only become a laughingstock.”

“What?!” All of them were astounded. Their first thought was to wonder whether Yu Rui had intentionally said those words to bait them into revealing themselves. 

However, they had been the Eight Dukes for many years now, such that they could converse just by exchanging looks. 

“I didn’t expect you, thick-brows, to defect to the rebels too.”

Zhuang He scoffed, “Old Du, your earlier pretense of righteousness nearly scared me.”

Du Jian’s face reddened. “I was worried you two would gang up on me.”

Cheng Yuan laughed. “To be honest, Brother Du, I didn’t expect you to side with King Yan. Otherwise, we wouldn’t have fallen for your deception.”

In Bi Linglong and their eyes, Du Jian had no reason to join the rebels. 

“A hero knows how to read the tides.” Du Jian felt awkward, as his act of betraying his liege was dishonorable. “But I hadn’t expected Brother Yu to side with the rebels.”

Bi Linglong shared the same doubt too. She coldly asked, “Why, Imperial Preceptor Yu?”

Yu Rui fell momentarily silent before replying, “Crown Prince Consort, it’s all over. It’s time for you to surrender. I can guarantee your safety in my name.”

He had chosen to address Bi Linglong as ‘Crown Prince Consort’ instead of ‘Your Majesty’, which showed that he didn’t acknowledge her position. 

Bi Linglong held her blade in front of her as she coldly retorted, “How can I surrender when my soldiers are still fighting?”

Zhuang He laughed. “Do you think you can defeat us single-handedly?”

The others smiled too. Before Zu An appeared out of nowhere, the Eight Dukes had been the strongest experts in the world. They were legendary figures to mortals, and there were four of them gathered here. 

“Someone once told me to not give up, no matter the difficulty faced. If I persist, a day will come when the sky clears.” Bi Linglong’s stern face turned gentle, making her look so beautiful for an instant that it mesmerized the four old men. 

“That’s just meaningless consolation. What can you do even if you persevere now?”

A laugh suddenly echoed from afar as a group of people galloped over from an alley. This group was led by none other than King Yan.

Bi Linglong laughed. “You're too sly, King Yan. It took so much to lure you out. Imperial Preceptor Yu and Lord Du, you have done well.”

King Yan was horrified. He quickly halted his charge and ordered his aides to protect him well.

Zhuang He and Cheng Yuan were enraged as well. “Yu Rui, Du Jian, I knew you two were up to something!”

In truth, they'd doubted that Yu Rui and Du Jian would betray Yu Yanluo, and King Yan’s reaction enlightened them to the truth. To win the favor of their new master, they immediately made their move. 

Yu Rui and Du Jian were stunned. They guarded themselves while explaining, “Don’t listen to that woman’s nonsense!”

Bi Linglong suddenly changed her tune. “Indeed, I was uttering nonsense.”

Zhuang He and Cheng Yuan had initially been doubtful about Bi Linglong’s claim, but the latter’s abrupt change in attitude made them wonder if she was protecting Yu Rui and Du Jian so that they could find an opportunity to strike. It also didn’t help that Yu Rui and Du Jian were formidable rivals. This was a good opportunity to get rid of them, lest there will be two thorns in their back in the future. 

So, they intensified their offense, unleashing killing blow after killing blow. 

Yu Rui and Du Jian realized their words were futile, and that made their anger surge. They decided to fight back. The nearby houses crumbled under the shockwaves from their clashes, and nearby trees were reduced to dust. 

Fortunately, King Yan’s aides stepped forward to suppress the shockwaves, allowing King Yan and Bi Linglong to remain safe. 

King Yan clapped his hands. “Impressive. You are full of schemes. You turned them against one another with just a few simple words… but you remain cornered.”

Bi Linglong fell silent. She had dealt with the four dukes for now, but King Yan had many experts on his side, whereas she only had a couple of guards left. 

Her stalwart guards drew their weapons and stood in front of her, saying, “We’ll guard Your Majesty to our dying breath!”

Murong Qinghe and Zhang Zitong noticed the twist in the situation and wanted to lead their forces back to rescue Bi Linglong, but the rebel army had already received orders to stall them at all costs. 

Bi Linglong understood the situation she was in and sighed softly. Ah Zu, I've done everything I can, but I still don’t see a ray of hope.

She turned to King Yan and said, “King Yan, I have one last question to ask. How did you convince them to betray me?”

Given the situation in the city, the traitors clearly weren’t just limited to these four. 

King Yan sneered, “Demoness, do you think I would let you stall for time? I’ll give you a chance to preserve your dignity and end your own life now. Otherwise, don’t blame me for what happens next.” 

Bi Linglong was indeed beautiful—it was a waste to let her die like that. However, it was too dangerous to keep her alive. He was aiming for the throne, and she outranked him. He could potentially sully his reputation if he didn’t deal with this well. The ideal situation was to have her end her own life. Once the others learned about her death, the imperial army would lose its fighting spirit.

Bi Linglong quietly raised her blade. As the empress, she couldn’t let anyone sully her.

“I’m sorry, Ah Zu.” As she was about to slit her throat, a single teardrop fell from her eyes. 

At her moment of death, however, she seemed to have seen a beautiful rainbow. How gorgeous. If only Ah Zu were here with me to witness this sight… but why is there a rainbow in the middle of the night?

“Didn’t I tell you to never give up?” a gentle voice echoed. It was reproachful, yet sympathetic.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2644: A Frog In The Well
chapters.body:
Chapter 2644: A Frog In The Well

Bi Linglong was startled. Did something happen to Ah Zu? Is that why I'm seeing him now that I’m dead?

But the warm embrace and familiar aura snapped her out of her daze. She murmured incredulously, “Ah Zu, is it really you?”

“It’s me. I came late.” Zu An hugged her trembling body with a fearful heart. He had felt unnerved while he was in the capital, which was why he rushed here right away. Had he been a second later, he would have lost her.

“Regent!” The imperial guards were moved.

When they saw the empress preparing to slit her own throat, they'd felt so indignant that they had followed suit, only for their blades to be knocked away. By the time they recovered, the regent was already standing in front of them. 

On the other hand, King Yan and the others turned ghastly pale. Didn’t they say that this accursed man died in the other world? How did he return?

“It's been a while, King Yan. Why are you leaving without greeting me?” After consoling Bi Linglong, Zu An turned to the old man who was planning to slip away. 

King Yan’s body stiffened up. He awkwardly turned around and said, “You have become more spirited over the last few years.”

Zu An laughed. “You must be wondering why I didn’t die in the other world.”

King Yan laughed sheepishly. “Regent, you are as humorous as ever.”

Zu An’s expression suddenly darkened. “I’m not in the mood to joke with you.”

He wasn’t planning to waste his breath arguing with King Yan here. Countless soldiers were dying due to the ongoing war below. So, he flew into the air and roared, “STOP!”

His thunderous voice could be heard clearly by every soldier despite the chaotic battlefield.

“It’s the regent! The regent is back!”

Many in the imperial army cheered in delight when they saw Zu An. In contrast, the rebel soldiers exchanged frightened looks. The meat grinder that was the battlefield abruptly stalled to a halt. 

Zhang Zitong wiped off the blood on her face as she looked at the glowing figure in the sky, who resembled a deity descending to the mortal world. Ah, the regent is so dashing.

Even Murong Qinghe’s eyes twinkled as well. Soldiers respected the strong, and her brother-in-law was the strongest expert out there. However, she soon remembered something that made her face redden, and her expression became conflicted. 

Bi Linglong lost her usual air of majesty, displaying the pure joy of a woman in love. Whenever Zu An was with her, all problems faded away. 

After stopping the battle, Zu An turned his attention back to the four members of the Eight Dukes. “As the foundation of the empire, why didn’t you aid the empress earlier?” 

The four of them opened their mouths, but they didn’t know what to say. 

Bi Linglong was worried that the others would take advantage of his ignorance, since he had just arrived and was oblivious to the situation, so she said, “Ah Zu, they have defected to the rebel army.”

Zu An nodded. “I see. How perplexing. You have already reached the pinnacle of wealth and power within the empire, so how did King Yan convince you to join his side?”

He didn’t look at Zhuang He and Cheng Yuan, as it wasn’t surprising for those two to rebel. Rather, he was baffled that Du Jian and Yu Rui had betrayed Bi Linglong as well.

Yu Rui and Du Jian fell silent. 

It was Zhuang He who spoke up. He knew that given their past history and this incident, Zu An wouldn’t let him off, so he said, “What other reason could I have? You disappeared for so many years that everyone thought you were dead. I have a huge clan behind me. It’s only normal for me to find another way out.”

“Is that all?” Zu An frowned. He looked at Yu Rui in disappointment. He had always treated the Yu clan well because of his relationship with Yu Yanluo. He hadn’t expected Yu Rui to backstab him at a time like this.

Yu Rui took a deep breath and said, “It’s too late for words.” His aura suddenly surged. 

Bi Linglong was startled. She sensed Yu Rui’s cultivation rising beyond the might he had displayed thus far. His aura was even more intense than Zhao Han’s. 

The other members of the Eight Dukes were startled before chuckling. “So you made a breakthrough too.”

Their auras also surged, rising all the way up to the pinnacle of Earth Immortal as they spread out to encircle Zu An. They had hidden their cultivation earlier to use it as a trump card to catch their opponent off guard, but now that Yu Rui had shown his hand, there was no reason for them to continue concealing their strength. 

King Yan was overjoyed. He hadn’t expected there to be such a twist in the situation, though he couldn’t help but wonder how these fellows had grown so fast as to even surpass his cultivation. The Eight Dukes had always been formidable, but times had changed. As a feudal king, King Yan had access to far more cultivation resources and cultivation manuals. He'd thought that he had become the most influential figure, beneath only Zu An. 

The rebel soldiers cheered in delight. In contrast, the imperial soldiers looked grim. Can the regent overcome this crisis?

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up as he assessed the four people. “Is this the basis of your confidence? I must say that your growth has been abnormally fast.”

Fusing the worlds together had greatly enriched this world’s ki, but the resources these four had were nowhere on par with Zhao Han's. It didn’t make sense for them to have surpassed Zhao Han. 

Zhuang He gleefully replied, “Regent, you might have easily crushed the Earth Immortal Imperial Tutor Zhao back then, but times have changed. Within the last three years, our cultivation has grown by leaps and bounds. We have surpassed not just Zhao Chen but the former emperor too. It’s no longer your world!”

Clearly, no one recognized that fool, Zhao Ruizhi, as a legitimate emperor. By former emperor, they meant Zhao Han.

Cheng Yuan warned, “Don’t let your guard down. The strength he displayed when he quelled the Doomsday Spirit and the Plague Monarch is not to be underestimated.” 

Despite their vigilance, they weren’t worried. Zu An was formidable, but the four of them had already surpassed Zhao Han. On top of that, they had the massive armies of the eighteen rebel kings backing them. Losing this battle was unthinkable to them. 

Du Jian finally spoke up. “Regent, the empire belongs to the Zhao clan. You have gone overboard with your actions. Why don’t I propose a deal? If you leave right now, we will ensure your safety and not pursue your crimes. On top of that, you can retain your king title too. This way, we can avoid meaningless casualties. Won’t that be for the best?”

He figured it would still be ideal to avoid a fight, or else they would have to pay a hefty price. Considering how he had betrayed Zu An, there was a chance that Zu An, when cornered, could choose to drag him down to hell with him. 

King Yan didn’t like that suggestion. He wanted to overturn it, but he was worried that if he pushed too far, Zu An would go after him first. It’s better to concede for now and slowly erode his influence over time. I can use the strength of the entire empire to settle the scores with him later on. 

“The empire belongs to the Zhao clan?” Zu An acted as if he had heard the most hilarious thing in the world. His expression turned cold as he scoffed, “The world belongs to me.”

Cheng Yuan harrumphed. “Aren’t you being too arrogant, regent?”

Zu An suddenly sighed. “Your world is too small. You’re frogs in a well, gazing upon the moon. Your ignorance is what gave you the courage to oppose me. If you had further advanced your cultivation, you would have understood the vast difference between us.”

He gently waved his hand. A massive golden palm suddenly appeared in the sky and instantly crushed the four dukes into dust. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2645: Shadow Group Revealed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2645: Shadow Group Revealed

Loud exclamations rippled through the battlefield. The abrupt twist in the situation had shocked everyone. Ordinary soldiers wouldn't understand the difference in the cultivation levels of both sides, but they could still feel the immense pressure the four dukes had exerted in that instant. Even the strongest expert in their respective armies wouldn’t stand a chance against entities of that level. It would take an army on an open field to subdue such an opponent. 

And yet, the regent had crushed all four of them at once?

The imperial soldiers weren't too surprised, as they had previously witnessed Zu An’s prowess in the capital. They cheered in delight at his feat. 

The rebel soldiers, however, were mostly from other provinces. They had heard rumors, but they'd thought they were exaggerated, especially with the kings downplaying Zu An’s strength. This sight crushed their morale. How were they supposed to fight someone so unbelievably powerful?

Even Bi Linglong was dumbstruck. She'd been worried when the four dukes emanated auras surpassing even Zhao Han’s. While she had always known her lover was formidable, the latter appeared to have gotten far stronger in the past few years. 

But Yu Rui is from the Yu clan. Won’t this put Madam Yu in a tough spot?

King Yan was flabbergasted. When he saw how strong the four dukes were, he felt both envy and relief. He'd thought that if they combined their prowess, the four would perhaps able to take down Zu An. He hadn’t expected such an outcome.

At that instant, he doubted the decades he had spent cultivating. What’s the point of cultivating when people like Zu An can just crush us at a whim? 

But when he recalled how he had ruled with unquestioned authority in his fief, he realized he wasn't the incompetent one. It was this Zu fellow who was inhuman!

Zhao Han had been the dominant power of his era, but now, Zu An was proving to be far stronger than that. 

“It’s a misunderstanding, regent.” King Yan, with his high stature, was accustomed to others fawning over him. He hadn’t expected a day would come when he would be fawning over others, let alone that he would be such a natural at it too. 

“Misunderstanding? Your rebellion has cost the lives of countless civilians and soldiers, but you’re shrugging it off as a misunderstanding?” Zu An didn’t even raise his hand, but King Yan floated up from his tight encirclement of guards. He kicked his legs frantically while clawing at his neck, as if an invisible hand was strangling him. 

King Yan finally experienced the terror the four dukes had experienced earlier. The other party gave off such intense pressure that despite his cultivation, he felt like an infant. A warm stream flowed from his crotch, and an awful stench wafted. Yet due to the overwhelming terror paralyzing his head, he didn’t feel the slightest shame. 

He frantically racked his brain for an excuse. Fortunately, his brain seemed to function better when his life was at stake, and he found a scapegoat to blame everything on. “Zhao Yuan instigated me!” 

“Oh?” Zu An loosened the restraint on King Yan. “Where is he?”

King Yan gasped for air like a drowning person who had just been brought to shore. “He stayed outside the city, to receive incoming troops. He told me to join the battle here to build my name.”

He secretly cursed Zhao Yuan and his ancestors as he spoke. That fellow is a schemer! Now that I think about it, he was digging a pit for me!

Worried that this wouldn’t be enough to convince Zu An to spare him, he added, “Regent, I thought you met with a mishap in the other world, which was why I foolishly fell for Zhao Yuan’s tricks. I have always been worried… No, I have always thought that you would overcome the hurdles in your way and return safely! But Zhao Yuan said that he had ways to deal with you even if you returned alive.”

The more King Yan spoke, the angrier he felt. That fellow said he had a way to deal with Zu An, but I don’t see him doing anything at all. Why did I foolishly fall for his nonsense?

A sigh suddenly echoed and a voice said, “King Yan, that’s behavior unworthy of a ruler.”

A figure slowly walked over from the distance. It was King Guangling, Zhao Yuan.

King Yan retorted, “Talk is cheap. Why don’t you go deal with him?” He had just witnessed the deaths of four powerful dukes before being lifted like a little chick. Only someone who had been in the same position would understand the despair he felt. 

Zhao Yuan looked at Zu An with conflicted eyes. “Regent, you have grown more imposing over the last few years.”

Zu An was surprised by Zhao Yuan’s composed attitude. “You don’t seem flustered.”

“Why did you think I would be flustered?” Zhao Yuan calmly replied with a smile. 

“I was wondering how the four dukes could have grown so quickly. I can now see it's due to your credit,” Zu An replied. When he saw the calm Zhao Yuan, the doubt he had been harboring was answered. 

“The regent is as sharp as ever.” Zhao Yuan smiled.

King Yan was horrified. This fellow helped the four dukes surpass Zhao Han? Why didn’t he help me too? Hold on… If he could help others make a breakthrough, he must be even stronger himself!

He thought back about the times he had put on airs before Zhao Yuan, and the latter had played along with him. He suddenly felt incredibly embarrassed. Damn it! I must have looked like a clown to him! 

Suddenly, a shadow on the side wall came to life and attacked Zhao Yuan. Even its sword was dyed black, reflecting not the slightest light. 

This sight shocked many. Zhao Yuan was currently alone without his guards. This assassin had truly found the best opportunity to take him down. It was a wonder how the assassin had known Zhao Yuan would appear here in the first place.

Could the regent have arranged for this? No, there’s no reason for someone as powerful as the regent to resort to such tactics. 

While the crowd was making sense of the situation, a dull thud echoed. The assassin had been sent flying, with fresh blood spurting from his mouth. 

Many of the experts present rubbed their eyes in bewilderment. They didn’t even see how Zhao Yuan made a move. But what they were more concerned about was the assassin—who would dare assassinate Zhao Yuan under such a setting?

The black cloth veiling the assassin’s appearance had been knocked off, revealing a pale face mired in regret and indignation. It was an unfamiliar youth, but he commanded the disposition of a top-notch assassin. 

But Zu An recognized the youth. It was the person he had spared at the Lake of Hatred during the Devil Sect’s infighting—Jing Li, the son of the Skysplitting School’s sect master. What is he doing here? Could it be…

“You’re courting death!” Infuriated, Zhao Yuan thrust his palm at the other party.

As if knowing he wouldn’t survive this ordeal, Jing Li turned to Zu An and shouted, “Zhao Yuan is the Shadow Group Master!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2646: Trump Card
chapters.body:
Chapter 2646: Trump Card

Zu An didn’t want to see someone he knew dying just like that, so he casually raised his hand and pulled Jing Li to his side, thus saving the latter from Zhao Yuan’s fatal attack. He was astonished to hear Jing Li’s words, repeating, “He’s the Shadow Group Master?”

The Shadow Group was the most elusive assassin organization in the world. Even the Embroided Envoys could only find their branches, but not the identity of its master. While there had been clues suggesting the Shadow Group Master was in the imperial palace, that conjecture had never been verified. 

Thinking about it, Zhao Yuan was an imperial relative and a commander of the imperial army. He should have wielded considerable influence, yet he had always maintained a low profile and had hardly any presence. This had benefited him. He alone had remained standing when all the renowned figures in the imperial palace had met with mishaps. Were it not for the previous rebellion, the imperial army would still have been under his control. 

Many things would suddenly make sense if he was the Shadow Group Master.

The others also stared at Zhao Yuan in astonishment. None of them had expected him to be the most secretive person in the world. 

Zhao Yuan broke his composure and glared at Jing Li with vicious eyes. “I should have never spared a bastard like you.” There was no point refuting it now, after all.

His admission caused a huge commotion. The Shadow Group had held a resounding reputation before Zu An’s rapid ascent in the capital. Many influential figures in the imperial court had feared that their throat would one day be slit by a Shadow Group assassin.

King Yan looked at Zhao Yuan with fearful eyes. He hadn’t expected the person he had recently spent so much time with to be such a terrifying individual. And I kept bragging in front of him!

“You seem confident.” Zu An was surprised by Zhao Yuan’s frank admission. It seemed the latter had something to fall back on. 

“This world would have been mine if not for your sudden emergence.” Zhao Yuan shook his head regrettably. 

A thought surfaced in Zu An’s mind. “You don’t seem to just be referring to this rebellion.”

“Of course not. Your appearance foiled my careful arrangements.” Zhao Yuan had bottled these thoughts up for a long time, and he could finally vent them. “I was a distant imperial relative. The throne wouldn’t have been mine even if a thousand emperors came and went. But were it not for your intervention, with the previous emperor’s passing, the crown prince’s weakness, and the conflict between the Crown Prince Consort and the empress, they would have fought each other to the death without any outsider meddling.

“The distinguished King Qi’s lineage always coveted the throne. There were also many ambitious ones among the other kings, be it the previous King Wu or the current King Yan. Chaos should have befallen this world a long time ago!”

Zu An sneered, “Only then would you stand a chance.”

“Your intervention resulted in King Qi’s faction’s premature retirement. The Crown Prince Consort and the empress, despite their long-standing feud, made peace and became allies. I would have never expected you to conquer those two tigresses…”

Zu An’s face reddened. “Don’t talk nonsense!” He had to be careful, since they were in public. 

Bi Linglong noticed many gazes falling on her. She tried her best to maintain her composure, but she wouldn’t hesitate to dive into a hole if she could. 

Zhao Yuan continued, “You suppressed King Wu and dealt with the previous emperor’s sons. You unraveled many of the conflicts among major clans in the capital. My many years of effort went down the drain because of you.”

“You sought to turn the major clans and princes against one another, so you could swoop in and reap all the benefits afterward. The main imperial lineage had to suffer a hit before a distant imperial relative like you could stand a chance.” Zu An could guess the other party’s plan. 

“Indeed. Your appearance changed it all.” Hatred flickered through Zhao Yuan’s eyes. “But heaven isn't blind. I finally found an opportunity to incite Bi Qi to make a move against the Liu clan, thus finally putting my plan into action. I never expected you to foil it yet again.”

Bi Linglong widened her eyes. The previous chaos in the capital had resulted in the decimation of the Bi clan and the Liu clan. So this fellow was the one behind it!

Zu An frowned. “Weren’t the monsters behind that? You defected to the monsters?”

“The monsters? The likes of them aren’t worthy of my loyalty.” Zhao Yuan placed his hands behind his back as he spoke arrogantly. 

“Let me see the basis of your confidence.” Zu An unleashed a blade of golden sword ki at Zhao Yuan. The attack was only intended to sever Zhao Yuan’s arm. There were too many things that he needed to question the latter about. 

A black aura suddenly manifested around Zhao Yuan and neutralized the golden sword ki. 

The rebel soldiers cheered in delight. Their leaders were mostly Zhao clansmen, and they understood that their defeat spelled death. Even though Zhao Yuan was the Shadow Group Master, he was still an imperial relative. His victory was more advantageous to them than Zu An’s. After witnessing Zu An’s overwhelming strength, they had given up hope, but the way Zhao Yuan had easily neutralized Zu An’s attack brought them renewed hope. 

King Yan further lowered his head in shame. 

On the other hand, Zu An remarked in astonishment, “Annihilation power?”

This power wasn't foreign to him. The Monster World had been destroyed by a terrifying Annihilation Beast, an expert in the path of annihilation. Those walking the path of annihilation believed in the annihilation of the universe itself, and they were feared in the myriad worlds. Zu An hadn’t expected Zhao Yuan to wield annihilation power!

Zhao Yuan was surprised. “I never expected you to have heard of my master. If you’re willing to join us, I can speak to my master on your behalf and have him accept you as a disciple.”

Spatial tears opened in the vicinity. Black-clothed assassins emerged from the tears and surrounded Zu An. They were shrouded in black auras suffused with annihilation power. 

Most spectators were astounded. They didn’t know what annihilation power was, but they recognized the black-clothed assassins as elites of the Shadow Group. Each elite assassin in the Shadow Group was a legendary figure who wielded the power to kill a king. 

Despite their fearsome reputation, most people didn’t think they posed a threat to the regent. However, the auras they were currently emanating surpassed even those of the four dukes. 

This sent everyone into a frenzy. Is the Shadow Group that powerful?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2647: Mark Of Annihilation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2647: Mark Of Annihilation

The battlefield had no lack of outstanding cultivators who had trained for many years, yet they paled far in comparison to these nameless assassins who'd popped out of nowhere. They couldn’t help but wonder if they had cultivated in vain. 

Only Zu An could tell these assassins from the Shadow Group hadn't truly reached their present level. Their cultivation had been forcefully raised by annihilation power. It would be impossible for them to further advance their cultivation. That said, he didn’t know if there was a growth system for universal deities’ disciples. If the Path of Annihilation’s deity bestowed them with more divine power, they could potentially grow faster than him. 

These elite assassins stood in a formation that suppressed him. 

Zhao Yuan revealed a victorious smile. “Regent, I do admire you despite us being enemies. In just a few years, you rose from a nobody in a distant city to this. It wouldn’t be far-fetched to say that your talent is unmatched in human history.”

“You know too little about human history to say that.” Zu An thought about the astounding figures he had met in his travels through spacetime. “You don’t need to flatter me. I have no compliments for you.”

Zhao Yuan’s smile stiffened, and his expression turned cold. “Do you think you are invincible? Indeed, you might be so by human standards, but that’s because you have never witnessed the glory of true deities.”

His gleeful attitude left Zu An speechless. I wonder how he’ll react if he learns that a universal deity has ‘visited’ me. 

But there was no reason for him to share that with Zhao Yuan. Instead, he said, “I heard you staged this rebellion in the name of the imperial grandson. Are you holding Concubine Bai and her son hostage?”

“Hostage?” Zhao Yuan was perplexed. “I have long heard about your lustful nature, but I didn’t expect you to be thinking about Concubine Bai when you’re already at death’s door.”

The crowd frowned. Some were perplexed, as it had been a long time since they had heard from Concubine Bai. Those privy to insider knowledge wondered if she had been sentenced to death. But the majority was just astonished to hear the news. 

There’s something between the regent and Concubine Bai too? The novels on the market are still too conservative!

Bi Linglong frowned. Back then, that woman was the crown prince’s concubine. To think that she’d compete with me over Ah Zu now. I guess we’re fated to be enemies. 

Zhang Zitong felt indignant. These people keep slandering the regent. I would know best whether he’s lecherous or not! I dropped hints toward him so many times, but he showed no inclination to take advantage of me. He’s obviously a gentleman!

Hold on… Could it be that he’s just not attracted to me?

Her expression suddenly collapsed at that realization.

Murong Qinghe looked at the figure in the sky and shook her head. My brother-in-law would have been perfect if not for his philandering ways. Big brother Chu is more devoted, but…

“Death’s door? You’re very confident that victory is in your grasp.” Zu An chuckled.

“Does the regent not sense the anomaly in your body? This is disappointing.” Zhao Yuan shook his head in contempt. 

Bi Linglong anxiously looked at Zu An, wondering if something had happened to him. 

“Are you referring to the weakening of my defense, agility, and divine sense?” Zu An calmly replied. That was the effect of the formation constructed by the elite assassins. 

He was in no rush to make his move. He was about to head to Dreamland, where he would encounter experts from other worlds and disciples of other universal deities. He could deal with experts from the Path of Annihilation better if he could learn more about them here. 

“You seem to be probing our abilities?” Zhao Yuan saw through his thoughts and sneered. “Regent, you are bold, but you made a miscalculation. You have been afflicted with the Mark of Annihilation, which is widely feared in the myriad worlds. While you were stalling for time, we stacked the marks to the maximum, weakening you by half. Surely you don’t think you can deal with us with just half your strength?”

“The regent is weakened by half?” The crowd was stunned. 

The military also had formations that could amplify ally damage and weaken enemy defense, but often, both sides would possess similar methods, so the eventual buff was usually no more than 20%. Furthermore, these formations rarely worked on experts. Military formations took time to construct, and no expert would be so dumb as to wait on the spot for the formation’s completion. 

It hadn’t been long since the elite assassins encircled the regent. There shouldn’t have been enough time for them to even complete the formation!

Do they have a secret art that allows them to directly apply the effect to the regent?

“Mark of Annihilation? What an interesting ability.” Zu An merely found the inexplicable power influencing his physical state interesting.

His abnormal composure stoked Zhao Yuan’s unease. Not daring to wait anymore, he ordered, “Attack!”

The ten elite assassins abruptly vanished. 

“Careful! That’s the specialty of the Shadow Group’s assassins, Void Weaving!” a few people in the crowd exclaimed. 

A few of the ladies exchanged looks, as they could sense one another’s concern for Zu An, but this was no time for them to be distracted. Their attention swiftly returned to the battle in the sky. 

Zu An frowned. This wasn't his first time dealing with such methods; the dark elves specialized in this too. Given his cultivation and grasp of the world’s will, he should have been able to track their movements even when he was weakened by the Mark of Annihilation and they were hiding in the void.

And yet, he couldn’t sense the elite assassins at all. 

Is the annihilation power concealing their presence?

Zu An wasn’t worried. He was capable of slipping into the void too. He also vanished on the spot, entering the void’s chaotic flow. He was about to search for the elite assassins when a slender sword suddenly emerged from his blind spot to strike his vitals, almost like a venomous serpent. 

But Zu An merely laughed. Hmph, I was only afraid that you’d continue hiding. Swordsmanship had little meaning to his current self.

That elite assassin suddenly found his lethal thrust reversing direction to slit his neck. It was abrupt, and he had no idea how it even happened!

But Zu An frowned right after. For some reason, his life force was dissipating.

Just then, Zhao Yuan sneered from the void, “Ah, I forgot to warn you. When they kill a person who inflicted the mark on them, someone afflicted with the Mark of Annihilation will lose a tenth of their life force. I dare you to kill them all.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2648: Tribute
chapters.body:
Chapter 2648: Tribute

Zu An was startled. I’ll lose a tenth of my life force if I kill an assassin, and ten assassins placed the Mark of Annihilation on me earlier. Won’t I be dead if I kill all of them?

The abilities of the path of annihilation were downright underhanded. It was fortunate he was learning about them here, or else it would have been worse if he had been caught off guard by a stronger enemy in Dreamland. 

Two black figures suddenly flanked him and attacked his vitals. These people were the most elite assassins of this world—they understood their craft and knew how to efficiently kill or incapacitate their target. 

Zu An’s expression darkened. He raised his hand and struck the two assassins. The two assassins didn’t have time to react before they collapsed to the ground, dead.

He wasn’t so dumb as to blindly trust the enemy’s words; it would be wiser for him to verify the facts himself. 

Soon, however, he felt two-tenths of his life force seeping away. Zhao Yuan wasn’t lying about the Mark of Annihilation’s ability. 

The way Zu An effortlessly dispatched the three elite assassins sent a shiver down the spines of the rest. There was a vast disparity in their fighting prowess. Even when buffed by annihilation power, they were still no match for him.

So, they opted for long-range attacks instead, but these tools couldn’t hope to hurt Zu An. He neutralized them with ease, too.

“How can you be so powerful despite having lost half your strength?” Zhao Yuan roared angrily.

He couldn’t make sense of the situation. He'd thought that their annihilation power buff, their numerical superiority, and the Mark of Annihilation debuff were enough for them to rival Zu An. It should have been an easy victory for them. So why is he still making quick work of us?

Zu An didn’t bother answering Zhao Yuan’s question. They could have stood a better chance against him had they been in another world, but in this world, he was god. 

Meanwhile, he'd figured out another function of the Mark of Annihilation—it allowed the caster to locate the target. He was traversing the void, yet the assassins could locate and launch a coordinated attack against him. It was as if they had planted a tracker on him. 

He decided not to hold back anymore. He raised his hand and declared, “Void traversal is forbidden in this land.”

This wasn't soulspeak—he was rewriting the world laws with his authority as the world's master. 

Zhao Yuan and the other seven assassins reappeared in midair. Their bodies were weirdly distorted, likely because they had been squeezed into small spatial cracks. They hadn’t expected to be revealed at Zu An’s command. 

Initially, the crowd in Phoenix Pass had been worried when all of them suddenly disappeared. The rebels were worried about Zhao Yuan, whereas the imperial court was worried about Zu An. The Shadow Group’s elite assassins were notorious, and they wielded the fearsome annihilation power. How could they not be worried? 

When they saw Zu An reappearing safe and sound, whereas the elite assassins had lost three of their comrades, the imperial forces breathed a sigh of relief. 

Zu An flitted to Zhao Yuan’s side and struck his head, not giving the latter a chance to react. He couldn’t be bothered to spare Zhao Yuan’s life, knowing that the latter would remain relatively intact under the annihilation power’s protection. It would be easier to question him with his authority as the netherworld’s master. 

But his body soon stiffened. He realized that the person he had just struck wasn't Zhao Yuan, but a nearby elite assassin. 

The elite assassin’s eyes were popping out. He had no idea how he had suddenly gotten here when he should have been some distance away. 

Zu An frowned. He glanced at Zhao Yuan. That fellow must have used a secret art to swap places with his subordinate, turning the latter into his scapegoat. 

“You are truly…” Before he could finish speaking, his eyes suddenly narrowed, and he anxiously dodged to the side. 

The elite assassin he had just smacked to death abruptly exploded, and the terrifying shockwaves that ensued distorted even the surrounding space. 

Zu An could have easily avoided the explosion, but he could sense how powerful the shockwaves were. If he left the explosion be, the entirety of Phoenix Pass would be destroyed, and many would lose their lives. He raised his hand and sealed off the surrounding space to limit the explosion radius, but the more he tried to restrain it, the greater the backlash he felt. It also didn’t help that the explosion was imbued with annihilation power. Evidently, this body explosion was also an ability from the path of annihilation. 

Even with Zu An’s strength, the tremendous shockwave still tore through his finger webbing, and blood seeped from the corner of his lips.

Zhao Yuan’s eyes lit up. “I see the regent has much that he cares about. That simplifies things.” As the Shadow Group Master, he knew that assassins excelled not in their physical prowess, but in exploiting all factors to create the ideal conditions to assassinate their target. 

Zu An’s expression turned cold. “You don’t even hesitate to sacrifice your subordinates. Many of your brethren are in the rebel army. They would have been caught in that explosion too.”

“Don’t try to drive a wedge between us. Those who pursue the path of annihilation should be prepared to die at any time. To us, death is not an end, but rather glory.” Zhao Yuan gazed down on Phoenix Pass. “It’s their fate if they get caught in our battle and die. All beings eventually have to die anyway.”

Many rebels cursed him for being so heartless as to leave them in the lurch, but Zhao Yuan was unfazed by their remarks.

Zu An sighed. “If you truly believed that death is an honor, you wouldn’t have used your subordinate as a scapegoat earlier. You don’t seem to be a devout believer of annihilation.”

Zhao Yuan looked flustered. “Don’t you dare insult me! You have no right to doubt my faith in my master!”

He took out a dagger and plunged it into his chest. “Master, I offer you my fresh blood. Please grant me power.”

The remaining elite assassins also plunged their daggers into their chests and murmured some obscure chant. 

The crowd in Phoenix Pass was taken aback. They wondered if Zhao Yuan and his elite assassins were out of their mind. 

But Zu An frowned. He suspected that this was a skill to offer their blood essence in exchange for a temporary burst of power. 

Flames enveloped Zhao Yuan and the seven elite assassins, and their auras grew two times stronger. Black runes appeared on Zhao Yuan’s face, making him look even more creepy and deranged than before. “Regent, you may be strong, but you are ultimately mortal. The likes of you can’t hope to withstand my master’s might!” 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2649: Frog In A Well
chapters.body:
Chapter 2649: Frog In A Well

Zu An observed the assassins’ transformation. It’s similar to the Devil Sect’s blood ignition, where they ignite their blood essence to temporarily raise their fighting prowess, but it's much more advanced. They've grown two times stronger. 

Those from the path of annihilation are troublesome to deal with.

At Zhao Yuan’s order, the assassins formed a sword formation and attacked Zu An. Each of their blades ignited with a peculiar flame that was suffused with violent energy. 

Zu An was starting to find the situation a little tricky.

The others in Phoenix Pass could only see shadows flashing across the sky to attack Zu An; the elite assassins had attained a speed beyond what the spectators could follow. 

Bi Linglong’s hand was soaked in sweat. She had already reorganized the imperial army by now, and she turned to the Qin clan’s two dukes and asked, “Can the formation masters in our army support the regent?”

The two dukes exchanged looks. Qin Zheng said, “Their strength is beyond our understanding. It’ll be hard for our formation masters to intervene in a duel of this caliber. If we carelessly make a move, we might distract the regent instead.”

Bi Linglong frowned. She thought hard and ordered, “Evacuate our men from Phoenix Pass. Get them away as far as possible.”

“But that would mean leaving brother-in… the regent alone with the rebel army!” Murong Qinghe was worried. 

Zhang Zitong said, “General Murong, Her Majesty is right. We aren’t of any help to the regent here, and it’s probably the same for the rebel army. If anything, our presence here might only hinder the regent, as he'd have to protect us.” 

Murong Qinghe was no fool. She recalled what Zhao Yuan had said and knew it wasn’t wise to linger here. 

Soon, Bi Linglong began arranging for the imperial army to leave the battlefield, while she and a few other commanders scaled a faraway mountain to watch the battle. 

The rebel kings wanted to pursue them, but after witnessing what had happened earlier, none of their men were willing to move, except for a few die-hard loyalists. A couple of silver taels per month wasn’t enough for them to lay down their lives.

Even the loyalists realized that the others weren’t going to join the pursuit, and challenging the imperial army with their meager strength would be tantamount to suicide. Thus, they had no choice but to let the imperial army retreat. When the imperial army retreated from Phoenix Pass, they were initially perplexed, but since the goal of this battle was to claim Phoenix Pass, they thought it was a good thing anyway. 

Several kings excitedly ordered their subordinates to occupy Phoenix Pass’ vital chokepoints in case the imperial army regretted their decision and returned. They had tasted despair when the regent returned earlier. They were relieved to see Zhao Yuan and his team of assassins turning the tables on the regent.

They weren’t bothered about Zhao Yuan’s identity as the Shadow Group Master, since he was also a member of the Zhao clan. They didn’t think he would mistreat them. In contrast, Zu An would claim their lives if he won! This prompted them to cheer Zhao Yuan on. 

In the sky, Zu An defended against the elite assassins’ sword formation as he said, “The people below are cheering hard for you.”

When he saw how light-hearted Zu An was, Zhao Yuan’s expression turned grim—the latter was still in the mood for idle chatter. “How are you still fine despite having so many Marks of Annihilation layered on you?”

He had been confident in his chances of winning this battle after his blood tribute greatly raised his strength. Initially, the battle unfolded the way he'd expected. He and his assassins’ sword formation suppressed Zu An, forcing the latter into a defensive stance. 

But he soon noticed something amiss. They had the upper hand, but somehow, it wasn’t translating to victory. Zu An could always find ways to neutralize their offense. Chances were that he was using this as an opportunity to decipher their abilities. 

How is this possible? I layered five Marks of Annihilation on him, weakening him by half! On top of that, he killed three of my assassins—that should have cost him 30% of his life force. He was also injured by the earlier explosion. He should be at his limit now!

What’s going on here?

He recalled what Zu An had said when he killed the four dukes: ’Your world is too small. You’re frogs in a well, gazing upon the moon. Your ignorance is what gave you the courage to oppose me. If you had advanced your cultivation further, you would have understood the vast difference between us.’

Zhao Yuan shuddered. He suddenly had a feeling that Zu An was as unfathomable as the sky.

No, that can’t be. I've obtained the power of a true deity. How can a mortal compete with that? He must be blowing his horn. He’s feigning infallibility to erode our fighting spirit. 

Thus, he howled, “He’s already at his limit! We just need one last push to bring him down!”

Elite assassins like them had been honed to be unwavering, and Zhao Yuan’s words further raised their morale. They charged at Zu An like shooting stars. 

An inferno gushed outward each time Zu An deflected their attacks with his Tai’e Sword, and sparks of flame fell from the sky and landed in the rebel army. These sparks unleashed destructive waves of flames as soon as they touched the ground. Soldiers afflicted with the flames, including experts, tried to put them out, but they couldn’t be extinguished. It didn’t take long for the troops to be reduced to ashes. 

Soon, the falling sparks engulfed the entirety of Phoenix Pass in flames.

The imperial army was horrified. Fortunately, Her Majesty ordered our retreat, or else the consequences could have been dire.

Zu An frowned. “At this rate, your people are going to suffer heavy losses.”

Except for the higher-ups, most soldiers in the rebel army were here because they had no choice but to obey orders.

Zhao Yuan sneered, “You should surrender if you feel sorry for them. Otherwise, there will only be more casualties.” We might be on the same front now, but in the future, the other kings will become my rivals. If I weaken their armies now, no one will be able to defy me in the future. 

The conversation in the sky shocked and horrified the rebel army. Some cursed Zhao Yuan for his heartlessness, while the others begged the regent to save them.

Zhao Yuan laughed even harder. “You hear that? They're begging you to save them. Are you going to leave them in the lurch?” It’ll be in our favor if Zu An gets distracted trying to save them. 

Zu An harrumphed. “I can do that easily.”

The sky suddenly turned dark. A thunderous crackle boomed, and torrential rain fell right away. The flames engulfing Phoenix Pass were swiftly extinguished. 

Deafening cheers broke out across Phoenix Pass. Even several high-ranking officials in the rebel army shouted "Long live the regent!"

Zhao Yuan was dumbstruck. He knew this was no ordinary rainwater—ordinary water couldn’t hope to put out their annihilation flame. More than that, this rainwater didn’t just extinguish the flames in Phoenix Pass; it also put out the ones blazing from them. 

The spectating imperial army breathed a sigh of relief. 

Zhang Zitong squealed, “The regent is the strongest!”

Bi Linglong nodded in agreement as a blissful smile formed on her lips. 

Murong Qinghe watched the imposing figure and suddenly blushed. 

… 

“You… can control this world’s weather?” The drenched Zhao Yuan stared at Zu An in disbelief. He sensed inexplicable law power emanating from the rainwater. 

“In this world, I am god.” Zu An stood towering in the sky with lightning crackling behind him, outlining his cold expression. 

It was at this moment that Zhao Yuan finally understood that he truly was a frog in a well.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2650: Double Agent
chapters.body:
Chapter 2650: Double Agent

Zu An knew that now that they had acquired annihilation power, Zhao Yuan and the others couldn’t be underestimated. They would have made troublesome foes anywhere else. But he held the World Law Beacon of this world, making him the true god here. He could even alter the laws of this world. 

He could have altered the world laws anytime to cancel the Marks of Annihilation’s weakening effect. Similarly, the elite assassins’ deaths siphoning away his life force didn’t pose a problem to him either. He could easily regenerate it with his Primordial Origin Sutra. 

He'd only been entertaining Zhao Yuan and his subordinates to gather more information about the path of annihilation. Now that he had gotten what he wanted out of them, it was time to draw the curtains on this farce. 

Sensing Zu An’s aura swiftly recovering, Zhao Yuan and the others descended into despair. 

He was toying with us all this time!
