As the Shadow Group Master, Zhao Yuan had never tasted despair no matter how strong his opponent was. He knew an assassin’s true strength didn’t lie in direct confrontation; he simply had to engineer the most advantageous circumstances to eliminate his target. 

Even so, this time, he collapsed into true despair. 

After acquiring annihilation power, he'd thought he was strong enough to dominate the world, not to mention that he had the aid of the Shadow Group’s top ten elite assassins. They had also grown by leaps and bounds after acquiring annihilation power. He'd thought, Surely we can bring down a mortal with the power of a true deity? 

He hadn’t expected the world to be so cruel. Is a true god’s power inferior to that of a mortal?

With his elite assassins losing their fighting spirit, he knew it was pointless to continue this fight. So, he gritted his teeth and said, “Concubine Bai and the imperial grandson are in my hands. Are you going to abandon them?” 

Zhang Zitong glanced at Bi Linglong. Her Majesty and Concubine Bai used to be sisters in the Eastern Palace. Is history going to repeat itself?

Many in the imperial army’s upper echelons looked at Bi Linglong too. They knew the two women weren’t on good terms. 

The gazes on her made Bi Linglong frustrated. She wanted to say something but couldn’t find the words, so she decided to wait for Zu An’s decision first. 

Zu An frowned. “The previous emperor secretly sentenced Concubine Bai to death. How did you find her?”

“There’s nothing in the world the Shadow Group can’t find. When it comes to gathering intelligence, we’re far superior to the Embroidered Envoys.” 

Zhao Yuan’s words angered Xiao Jianren and Zhang Zitong, but they had to concede that the Shadow Group was indeed not inferior to the Embroided Envoys when it came to intelligence, and they were even a notch higher when it came to confidentiality. 

“Where’s Concubine Bai?” Zu An asked.

“Let us go, and you’ll see her.” Deep down, Zhao Yuan knew that he was only inferior to Zu An; he was strong enough to tower over everyone else. As long as he avoided Zu An, he could still live a carefree life. And if he continued praying to the Deity of Annihilation, perhaps he could just gain new powers to turn the tables on Zu An. 

Zu An shook his head. “Your rebellion has caused the deaths of many innocent civilians and soldiers. I can’t let you go.”

“If I die here, those two won’t live either!” Zhao Yuan anxiously exclaimed.

“I doubt that.” Zu An figured he could sieve through the memories of Zhao Yuan’s soul after killing him. He could figure Concubine Bai’s whereabouts by doing that. 

Zhao Yuan suddenly laughed maniacally. “Concubine Bai, it looks like your regent doesn’t care about you. You can come out now.”

His words shocked both the rebel army and the imperial army. Is Concubine Bai not being held hostage?

A woman dressed in white slowly walked out from a house in Phoenix Pass. She looked frail. Her beautiful face contrasted with the surrounding blood and flame remnants, which made her look even more pitiful. 

“Concubine Bai!” Many recognized the beautiful woman. The dimwitted crown prince was lucky to have such a beautiful concubine!

Unlike the authoritative Crown Prince Consort, her frail stature evoked a man’s desire to protect. 

“It has been a while, regent.” Concubine Bai looked at the man in the sky with conflicted eyes.

“Concubine Bai, if you convince the regent to let us go, I’ll ensure your son’s safety.” Zhao Yuan said. 

Many soldiers in the rebel army cursed Zhao Yuan. We’re fighting against the empress in the imperial grandson’s name, yet you’re holding the imperial grandson hostage? This is ridiculous!

Zu An descended to Concubine Bai’s side. “Who could have thought we would meet again under such circumstances?”

Hesitation flickered across Concubine Bai’s face as she secretly sent him a telepathic message, “Be careful, regent. Zhao Yuan intends for me to find an opportunity to assassinate you.”

“I know.” Zu An smiled. “Why else would I come here?”

“You already know?” Concubine Bai was taken aback. 

“He wouldn’t have let you move independently if you were truly a hostage.” Zu An sighed. “You’re also from the Shadow Group, aren’t you?”

Concubine Bai looked at him with a bitter smile. “Indeed. I am the Shadow Group’s Holy Maiden, as well as its eleventh elite assassin. It’s quite a coincidence. Back then, you were the eleventh Embroidered Envoy, and I was the eleventh assassin.”

Despite having figured out her identity, Zu An still had questions. “But you're Yun Jianyue’s junior. How did you get involved with the Shadow Group?” I always thought she was a spy planted by the Devil Sect in the imperial palace. Is she a double agent?

“The Shadow Group has its roots in the Devil Sect’s Skysplitting School.”

Concubine Bai didn’t explain in detail, but that was all Zu An needed. There had been infighting in the Devil Sect when he was at the Lake of Hatred. There, he'd learned about the relationship between the Shadow Group and the Skysplitting School and released the son of the Skysplitting School’s master, Jing Li.

Jing Li had been investigating the Shadow Group Master all this while. Due to this link, he was probably the only one in the world who could have uncovered Zhao Yuan’s true identity. 

Seeing the hushed conversation between Concubine Bai and Zu An, Zhao Yuan began losing his patience. He shouted, “What are you waiting for?”

Concubine Bai suddenly dropped to the ground. Zu An reflexively reached out to support her, but she suddenly whipped out a blue, glowing dagger and stabbed it into his chest. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2651: The King Of Assassins Becomes A Joke
chapters.body:
Chapter 2651: The King Of Assassins Becomes A Joke

This shocked everyone present, especially Bi Linglong and the other ladies. They had been jealous when they saw Concubine Bai and Zu An acting friendly with each other. Who could have thought Concubine Bai would suddenly turn against him like that? They were so nervous that they thought their hearts would beat out of their chests. They had never worried about Zu An losing to a man, but he was so philandering that it felt inevitable that he would lose to a woman.

Zhao Yuan smiled in glee. This was the trump card he had prepared just in case. As the Shadow Group Master, he knew an assassin’s ability couldn’t be evaluated by their cultivation. More often than not, a frail beauty could be more useful than a powerful cultivator. 

Concubine Bai was the card he had reserved to deal with Zu An. He had spent years investigating Zu An’s weakness. The latter was indeed a formidable foe, both in cultivation and wits, but he had a fatal weakness—his lustfulness. 

Zhao Han had secretly sentenced Concubine Bai to death, but Zu An had taken on a huge risk to save her. That suggested he had special feelings for her. Thus, Zhao Yuan had begun formulating this plan. The dagger was smeared with the most formidable poison developed by the Shadow Group. 

But his smile soon stiffened up. Concubine Bai had thrust the dagger toward Zu An, but the latter stood up casually as if nothing had happened. Zhao Yuan's gaze moved downward, only to realize that the dagger hadn’t pierced Zu An’s skin. 

Concubine Bai was baffled too. His body is even harder than metal!

While she hadn’t exerted her full strength, it should have been enough to cut his skin. Of course, she hadn’t smeared the lethal poison on the dagger either. The dagger’s blue glow was a gimmick.

Zu An smiled. “You don’t have to go through so much trouble. I can help you rescue your son.”

Concubine Bai had just requested for him to put on an act with her. Zhao Yuan was holding her son hostage, forcing her to carry out this assassination. But Zu An hadn’t played along with her script.

Zhao Yuan’s face darkened. He exclaimed angrily, “You bitch! You’ll never see your son again!”

He uttered something to the elite assassins, and their eyes suddenly reddened with madness. They let loose loud screeches as they abruptly charged at Zu An.

“Careful! They're going to detonate themselves to bring you down with them!” Concubine Bai anxiously warned him. 

Zu An also sensed the threat they posed. By the time he held Concubine Bai closer to him, the assassins were already in their vicinity. 

Unlike before, they weren’t planning to fight. Their bodies swiftly swelled up and exploded. The simultaneous explosions of the remaining elite assassins triggered a chain reaction, culminating in a humongous explosion far stronger than the one before. A black hole suffused with destructive energy appeared in the sky. It exerted an incredible suction force that pulled the houses and trees into it. 

The rebel kings and their aides had been the closest to Zu An—they'd remained on standby in case an extra hand was needed to take Zu An down. The turn of events scared them out of their wits. They desperately fled for their lives, but they couldn’t outrun the suction force. Despite their best attempts to run forward, their bodies were dragged backward. 

As the black hole got bigger, even the experts in their ranks succumbed and flew into the sky too. Out of despair, some of them begged Zu An to save them. 

But Zu An didn’t have the energy to entertain them. His mind was focused on altering the surrounding world laws. If he couldn’t stop the black hole in time, Phoenix Pass and the hundreds of thousands of soldiers in the vicinity would be annihilated here. 

A massive hand suddenly manifested in the sky. It grabbed the black hole, and the two vanished into the void together. 

Even though Zu An had transported the black hole into the void in the shortest time possible, Phoenix Pass was still destroyed. The rebel army also suffered heavy losses.

“Hahaha! Bastard Zu, look who I have in my hands!” A frenzied laugh echoed. Zhao Yuan held Bi Linglong before him, with a sword pressed against her neck. 

As the Shadow Group Master, he needed to be resourceful. Concubine Bai was merely one of the hands he had prepared; the other was Bi Linglong. He knew Bi Linglong was on close terms with Zu An, so much so that there were novels about them on the streets. While his subordinates were exploding, he'd charged into the imperial army’s ranks and grabbed Bi Linglong. After what happened earlier, he'd had little confidence that the explosions would work.

Many people had been guarding Bi Linglong, but none were powerful enough to stop Zhao Yuan. In the blink of an eye, he already had Bi Linglong in his grasp. He immediately turned to Zu An, only to see the latter easily neutralizing the explosion. That filled him with fear and relief. Good thing I had a backup plan.

“Your Majesty!” Shocked and horrified, Zhang Zitong, Murong Qinghe, and the others quickly surrounded Zhao Yuan. They wanted to attack him, but they were worried that he would hurt Bi Linglong. 

Zhao Yuan didn’t spare a glance at Bi Linglong’s guards. He cleared his throat and addressed Zu An, “Let me go and promise not to pursue me. I’ll let her go once I’m somewhere safe.”

He thought of forcing Zu An to break his arms, but he was worried that the other party would give up on Bi Linglong if he forced him too far. He had heard about Zu An’s many lovers, and while Bi Linglong was beautiful, she was merely one out of many in his massive harem. 

Zhao Yuan didn’t want the crown anymore. After the consecutive blows he had received, his ambition had been quelled. He just wanted to survive and find somewhere peaceful to live out his life. Being the Shadow Group Master or the emperor was no longer important to him. 

Of course, he wouldn’t release Bi Linglong even after reaching somewhere safe. He never believed his enemy’s promises. Only with the hostage in hand would his safety be guaranteed. 

Concubine Bai looked at Zu An apologetically. Even though she wasn't the culprit here, he wouldn’t have been distracted if not for her. She secretly warned Zu An, “Big brother Zu, Zhao Yuan doesn’t believe in anyone. It is unlikely he will release big sister Linglong after getting to safety. It’ll be more dangerous if you allow him to take big sister Linglong away.” But she couldn’t think of any solution at hand. 

In contrast, Zu An remained perfectly calm. One step at a time, he slowly made his way toward Zhao Yuan. 

“Stay away! I won’t show mercy.” Zhao Yuan panicked. He tightened his grip on his sword, causing a blood slit to appear on Bi Linglong’s fair neck. 

The chilling touch of the blade sent goosebumps all over Bi Linglong’s body, but she still calmly shouted to Zu An. “Don’t worry about me, regent. This man is the source of all this chaos. We have to kill him for the peace of the world!”

“I didn’t expect a woman like you to be so brave. What a pity he’s a sentimental man. I doubt he’ll bear to lose a beauty like you.” Zhao Yuan laughed, but he was uncertain. He was worried that Zu An would forsake his lover and attack him. 

Bi Linglong wanted to say more, but Zu An stopped her with his gentle eyes. He turned to Zhao Yuan and said, “I don’t think you understand what it means to be god.”

Zhao Yuan felt uneasy. He tightened his blade further, holding Bi Linglong firmly in place. 

All of a sudden, however, he sensed something amiss. He lowered his head and found that instead of Bi Linglong, he was holding a broken pillar.

Bi Linglong was standing next to Zu An!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2652: Clearing All Threats
chapters.body:
Chapter 2652: Clearing All Threats

“How’s this possible?” Zhao Yuan panicked, his eyes brimming with confusion. 

As the king of assassins, he had absolute control over his body. He was confident that he could dictate the life or death of anyone within a one-meter radius of him. Even when Zu An appeared, he'd still been confident he could kill Zu An if the latter got within a thirty-centimeter radius. 

If someone had told him that an enemy could easily rescue a person he was holding hostage, he would have never believed it. What did it mean to be the king of assassins? It meant no one could rescue a person under his control!

That was the basis of his confidence, which was why he'd dared to negotiate with Zu An after holding Bi Linglong hostage. 

What's going on? Was it all my delusion? Reluctant to believe this was real, he even tried stabbing the pillar in his grasp. It was indeed no illusion. What he was holding was a rotten pillar.

It wasn't just him; the crowd was also flabbergasted. 

Murong Qinghe and Zhang Zitong rubbed their eyes before exchanging confused looks. They had seen Zhao Yuan holding Bi Linglong hostage. It was one thing for Zhao Yuan to hallucinate, but how could all of them be hallucinating together?

Bi Linglong felt as if she was dreaming. It was just a second ago that Zhao Yuan had his blade pressed against her neck, and she had already resigned herself to death. She'd even considered slitting her neck against the blade so that she wouldn’t be a liability to her lover. But then, the next second, she suddenly appeared by his side. 

She reflexively touched her neck and felt the thin cut on it. That confirmed she wasn’t hallucinating. What’s going on?

Everyone present was full of questions. 

Zu An sighed. “I told you, I call the shots in this world.”

After obtaining the World Law Beacon and the acknowledgment of the world’s will, he'd been granted the equivalent of a superuser privilege. He could freely alter the world laws, though only to a certain degree—it couldn’t threaten the functioning of the world. 

To be exact, it was the equivalent of having access to a game’s backend engine, allowing him to freely alter data. Even moving mountains and seas wasn’t beyond him, let alone substituting Bi Linglong with a pillar.

In this world, he was no longer at the level of a simple cultivator anymore. Other cultivators had to rely on fighting to obtain what they wanted, but he could solve problems with a simple thought. 

He'd still struggled to use these privileges before, but after his encounter with the deities in the ancient Celestial Court and the Deity of Enigma and Chaos, Mi Li, his grasp on power and laws had risen to another level. It helped him decipher his privileges in this world. 

But he could only access these godlike abilities because he was this world’s master, which meant that he couldn’t use them anywhere else. Had he bumped into Zhao Yuan in another world, it wouldn’t have been such an easy fight.

That said, even the experience of being a god in a little world was meaningful. It deepened his understanding of power and laws, allowing him to deduce the abilities of the mythological and universal deities. 

“So this is the power of a god…” Zhao Yuan looked crushed. He knew he was cornered, and there was no way Zu An would spare him after everything he had done. 

“How tragic it is to be mortal!” He slit his throat with his blade. He looked at Zu An and Concubine Bai with a mocking gaze as life seeped from his body. “I won’t let you interrogate me. Concubine Bai, you'll never see your son again!” Thanks to the annihilation power's enhancement, he could still speak so much despite having slit his throat.

Concubine Bai exclaimed in horror. Her face turned ghastly pale. 

Zu An stared at Zhao Yuan with an enigmatic smile. “Do you think death is the end for a god?”

Now that death was drawing close, Zhao Yuan had been fearless, but Zu An’s words made him panic. Could it be… 

The truth soon became apparent. Zu An casually waved his hand, and a transparent soul emerged from Zhao Yuan's body. The latter looked back at his corpse, followed by his current transparent form that no one else could see. He was shocked and frightened. “This is… my soul?”

Zu An smiled. “I should have told you that I’m also the netherworld’s master. Deceased beings fall under my jurisdiction.”

Zhao Yuan was horrified. “NOOOOOO!”

Zu An turned to Concubine Bai and said, “Don’t worry. He hid your child in a small town not too far away from Phoenix Pass. I’ll send someone to rescue him.” He called Zhang Zitong over and relayed the instructions to her.

Zhang Zitong excitedly replied, “Consider it done!” 

I’m the first one the regent thinks of when he needs something done! He's given me the location and the defense layout. With the imperial guards and the Embroidered Envoys under my command, I should just buy a slab of tofu and bang my head on it if I can’t rescue the hostage despite that. 

Zu An turned back to Concubine Bai and said, “The Shadow Group fed him a poison to keep him under their control, but you don’t need to worry. I already know the antidote’s concoction method. I’ll hand it to someone from the academy later.”

Concubine Bai was overjoyed. She kneeled to him and said, “Thank you, Zu… Regent!”

Bi Linglong frowned. She wanted to say something, but she held her tongue. 

Zu An supported Concubine Bai to her feet. Then, he turned to address the rebel soldiers in Phoenix Pass. “You have brought chaos to the empire by aiding the rebellious kings, but they have been purged now. I’ll give you a chance. Lay down your weapons, and I’ll spare your lives as long as you haven’t hurt any innocent civilians.”

“Thank you, regent. Long live the regent!”

Now that Zhao Yuan was dead and Concubine Bai had submitted, who would dare to continue the rebellion, especially after what they had just witnessed? Right now, they only felt thankful to the regent for his magnanimity. 

Listening to the cheers from the crowd, some imperial officials secretly glanced at Bi Linglong. When they saw her looking at her lover with adoring eyes, they burst into laughter. In history, officials who were so celebrated by the people drew their liege’s suspicion and would eventually be purged, but no one thought anything was wrong with what had just happened. 

Zu An entrusted the two dukes from the Qin clan with the important responsibility of processing the rebel army’s surrender, while he greeted the officials and caught up with old friends like Gao Ying and Pei You. It had been years since they had met, so there was a lot to chat about.

It took a while before Bi Linglong got some private time with Zu An. “Ah Zu, I don’t think we should spare Concubine Bai’s son.” Fearing that he would misunderstand, she quickly added, “I’m not on good terms with Concubine Bai, but I’m not saying this out of a personal vendetta. That child is Zhao Han’s descendant. Others might seek to use him when he’s still young, and if he learns that you killed his father later when he grows older, he might turn against you. It wouldn’t be wise to keep him around.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2653: Repaying Gratitude With Enmity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2653: Repaying Gratitude With Enmity

Bi Linglong’s concern wasn't unfounded. Any rational politician knew what the right thing to do was. Sparing such a person and having them around was a ticking time bomb. 

Zu An, as a transmigrator, knew that better than anyone. There were too many examples in history. Dorgon from the Qing dynasty had accrued tremendous power, but despite his many years of dedication, he'd still failed to conquer a woman with a child. Furthermore, he was also a regent. 

Sensing Zu An’s hesitation, Bi Linglong continued with a hushed voice, “Ah Zu, I know this would make it hard for you to face Concubine Bai. You can entrust it to your subordinates and blame it on the Shadow Group and Zhao Yuan. It wouldn’t be surprising if they pulled something while you were trying to rescue the imperial grandson. Concubine Bai wouldn’t be able to blame it on you.”

Zu An shook his head. “I know you’re doing this for me, and under normal circumstances, I should heed your advice. However, I don’t have to worry about such things anymore. Others don’t have the power to threaten me. You don’t have to be too worried about it.”

Bi Linglong was taken aback. She belatedly remembered the strength he had displayed—he seemed more like a god than a mortal. Unlike the other politicians in history, he didn’t have to worry about others stabbing him in the back, as no one could threaten him anymore.

Despite knowing that, Bi Linglong remained worried. “But I still think that…”

Zu An gently grabbed her hand and said, “Linglong, I don’t want to be a cold-blooded machine of politics. If that’s how I treat Concubine Bai and her child today, won’t I do the same to the rest of you if a conflict of interest occurs in the future?”

Bi Linglong was startled. A gentle smile slowly formed on her lips. “I understand. I was too narrow-minded. You’re more like a being of flesh and blood than the others.”

Being from a major clan and selected to be the crown prince consort from a young age, she often dealt with hypocritical officials who wanted to exploit her value. Over time, she'd become a political machine that acted to prioritize her interests and constantly questioned others’ motives. It was a tiring way to live, but she hadn't thought there was anything wrong with that…

…until she met Zu An.

His unusual way of handling situations and his incredible vibrance had made her realize what it meant to be human. That was the trait that had attracted her. She hadn’t expected him to remain the same despite having grown so much. 

She suddenly felt fearful. Earlier, she hadn’t realized that she had tried to change the man whom she loved so much. It was a relief that he'd held onto his beliefs. 

Many people had seen her entering Zu An’s room. While there were no lack of rumors speculating her relationship with Zu An, she still felt embarrassed dwelling in the same room with him for too long. Then, it just so happened that a guard reported Concubine Bai’s request for an audience, so she took this opportunity to take her leave. 

While she was heading out, she passed by Concubine Bai, and directed an amicable smile at the latter. She felt apologetic to the latter for her earlier suggestion. 

Concubine Bai reflexively responded with a smile of her own. Before she entered the room, she couldn’t resist the urge to look at Bi Linglong’s departing figure. Worry flickered in her eyes. Even though the two ladies were amicable on the surface, there was bad blood between them due to their rivalry in the Eastern Palace. This seemed to be the first time Bi Linglong was taking the initiative to smile at her. Why did she smile at me? And why do I sense an apology in her gaze?

Concubine Bai had dwelled in the imperial palace for many years, and her association with the Devil Sect and the Shadow Group made her extremely sensitive to her surroundings. A thought surfaced in her mind. In truth, this worry had been on her mind all this time, but she knew Zu An and didn’t think he was such a person. But a person’s conviction can be slowly eroded by pillow talk, and my son’s birthright must be extremely troublesome for them. 

Her heart was gripped by nervousness. The first thing she did upon seeing Zu An was to drop to her knees.

Zu An was dumbstruck. “Concubine Bai, what are you doing?”

Tears glimmered in Concubine Bai’s eyes. “Big brother Zu, please spare the child’s life. I’m willing to devote my life to you to repay this favor.”

She didn’t address him as the regent—she was hoping to use their ties to move him.

The women of the imperial palace can’t be underestimated. Concubine Bai must have realized the danger her child is in.

“Concubine Bai, you need not do this. I won’t make things difficult for a child.” Zu An tried to pull her up to her feet. 

But Concubine Bai remained on her knees. “Big brother Zu, I’ll take my child into the mountain forest and live out our lives in seclusion. We won’t become a threat to you.” She had seen far too many broken promises in the imperial palace to believe his words. 

Zu An smiled. “You don’t have to do that. It wouldn’t do for a child to live in seclusion in the mountains.”

Seeing Zu An’s refusal to budge, sorrow welled up in Concubine Bai’s heart, and tears flowed from her eyes. “Big brother Zu, I know his existence itself is a threat, but he’s my flesh and blood. I don’t know what to do either. Wuuu…”

The tear stains on her cheeks when she first walked in had been a performance. As an elite assassin, she knew there was nothing more formidable than a woman’s tears. She knew how to use her frail appearance to her advantage to evoke a man’s desire to protect. But she was truly undergoing a mental breakdown now. She understood the severity of the matter, and she could find no reason for Zu An to spare her child. As she spoke, she lost her confidence and eventually succumbed to despair. 

Seeing the weeping Concubine Bai, Zu An sighed. “You must be curious about what Linglong told me earlier.”

Concubine Bai cried even harder. She could fathom a guess, as she would have made the same suggestion had she been in Bi Linglong’s place. 

“She advised me to spare you and your son. She said that I’m already powerful enough not to have to worry about the two of you. It would also settle the rebellion,” Zu An consoled her.

“Ah?” Concubine Bai was dumbstruck. She hadn’t expected her long-time rival to speak up for her. 

Zu An pulled her to her feet and wiped off her tears. “We've known each other for many years. Do you seriously think I'm a vicious person?”

Concubine Bai finally believed his words. She hugged him in delight as she said, “Thank you, big brother Zu.”

Feeling her tears on his robes, Zu An knew she was letting her true emotions through. He didn’t push her aside, and instead patted her shoulder to console her. 

Concubine Bai suddenly lifted her head and looked at Zu An with teary eyes. “Big brother Zu, I want to have your baby.”

Zu An was stunned. Hold on! I helped you out of goodwill, but you’re lusting over my body?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2654: Unexpected Request
chapters.body:
Chapter 2654: Unexpected Request

Zu An smiled awkwardly. “You don’t have to do that. I’m helping you based on our past ties and my principles. What would I be if I took advantage of your weakness to exploit you?”

“You aren’t exploiting this. I’m doing this of my own volition,” Bai Rouxue hurriedly explained. “And I’m not doing this to repay your favor either, but… but…” 

Seeing her stuttering and reddened face, Zu An asked, “But what?” 

Bai Rouxue mustered her courage to look at him. “For as long as I could remember, I was a tool for the Devil Sect, and I later became the Shadow Group’s Holy Maiden. My life has always been about completing the missions issued to me. I've never lived for myself before.

“I didn’t like that dumb crown prince, but I had to marry him for my mission. But he was too slow-witted to understand the affairs between a couple. Zhao Han emphasized the tradition of having the oldest son succeed the throne to curb King Qi, who was in the line of succession, but that meant the crown prince couldn’t be changed.

“However, the crown prince was too dumb to leave descendants behind. Zhao Han thought it would be better to have an intelligent imperial grandson to placate the officials, so he turned his eyes to me.”

Pain and resentment flickered across Bai Rouxue’s face. Tears began streaming down her cheeks. 

Zu An sighed. “It’s over now. You don’t have to think about that anymore.”

Bai Rouxue sobbed as she continued recounting the past. “Big sister Linglong should have been the ideal choice. She was the crown prince consort, so it would grant further legitimacy to the imperial grandson if he descended from her. However, she happened to be away then…”

She laughed miserably. “Now that I think about it, she was from a noble lineage. Zhao Han would have had to think twice about her backing before making a move on her. It’s much easier to trample on people without a background like me.”

Zu An sensed the resentment in her words and said, “You must have suspected that Linglong was aware of this, or was perhaps even one of the masterminds. That must have been why you've been on bad terms all these years.”

“I dared not think that, and the matter has long passed too. There’s no point thinking about all those things.” Being a spy in the imperial palace, Bai Rouxue had always been treading on thin ice, so she dared not express her emotions lest it give her away. This was her first time venting. 

“You've misunderstood her. You aren’t the only sacrifice here.” Zu An told her about how Zhao Han had planted a sliver of his soul in Zhao Ruizhi’s body so that he could possess it later, and Bi Linglong was the empress he had chosen. 

Bai Rouxue was horrified. “Zhao Han, that bastard!”

Zu An fully agreed with her. “He was indeed a bastard.”

“I've misunderstood big sister Linglong for so many years!” Bai Rouxue felt ashamed of herself, especially when she thought about how Bi Linglong had spoken up for them earlier. 

“Don’t think too much about it. It’s all over now. You can make peace with her from now on.” There was something Zu An was intrigued about. “If you hate Zhao Han that much, why do you still care about that child?” 

Bai Rouxue revealed a wretched smile. “My life has been dark for a long time. I didn’t like the child at the start, but he was still the child I conceived after ten months of hardship. Over time, he became my pillar of emotional support. He was the reason I held on for so many years.” 

Zu An understood where she was coming from. That child had been her light for many years now. She couldn’t just give up on him. 

“But I have another reason to hold on now.” Bai Rouxue looked at Zu An with a reddened face. “I thought you were a special man from the first time I met you, but even so, I didn’t expect you to take such a huge risk to save me when Zhao Han sentenced me to death…”

She'd had a good impression of Zu An all along, and that incident tipped the scales. She would have long since confessed to him if not for her involvement in the Shadow Group. But after everything that had happened afterward, she had to swallow down those words. 

“You and Zhao Yuan played me like a fool back then,” Zu An remarked bitterly. Zhao Yuan had confronted him several times back then, nearly bringing the matter of him harboring Bai Rouxue to light. It was only now that he realized they'd been in cahoots. 

Bai Rouxue was apologetic. “Zhao Yuan planned to abandon me. He figured having the imperial grandson in his hands was enough for him. He didn’t expect you to rescue me. He decided to go along with you and play the role of a loyal subject. Zhao Han had already sensed his impending doom then, and he knew the Shadow Group Master was likely hiding in the imperial palace, so he was suspicious of everyone. Zhao Yuan’s maneuvers won him Zhao Han’s trust.” 

Zu An was impressed. It was amazing that Zhao Yuan had succeeded in fooling the cunning Zhao Han, who had the world dancing in the palm of his hand then. 

Bai Rouxue lowered her head sorrowfully. “Big brother Zu, you have many gorgeous women, whereas I'm a woman with a child. I know I’m beneath your notice.”

Tears streamed down her cheeks yet again. It was supposed to be a performance to evoke pity, but sadness swiftly welled up in her heart. Why didn’t I meet him first?

She and Bi Linglong had been the crown prince’s consorts, and they'd met Zu An at the same time. She had also been closer to Zu An back then. So why had he ended up together with Bi Linglong, while she landed in this plight?

She would have felt envious in the past, but now that she knew Bi Linglong had nothing to do with her fate and had spoken up on her behalf, she couldn’t bring herself to hate the latter anymore. She could only blame her harsh fate. 

Zu An tried to wipe her tears, but he quickly retracted his hand when he realized it was too intimate. Instead, he said, “Don’t put yourself down. You’re gorgeous too. You and Bi Linglong are known as the capital’s two flowers.”

“But you don’t like me.” His consolation only further stoked Bai Rouxue’s sadness. 

“Who says I don’t like…” Zu An blurted out, only to realize the inappropriateness of his remark. He'd intended to say he thought she was beautiful, but that didn’t sound right either. 

But Bai Rouxue’s eyes lit up. “Really?”

Her hopeful eyes stifled Zu An’s explanation. He couldn’t bring himself to pop her bubble.

Bai Rouxue smiled radiantly. “Don’t worry, big brother Zu. I don’t covet a proper title. I’ll always be waiting for you. It’ll be enough for me if you visit me whenever you miss me.”

Just then, a guard reported Murong Qinghe’s arrival. Bai Rouxue took this chance to flee with a reddened face. Murong Qinghe noticed Bai Rouxue’s flustered look, which prompted her to look at Zu An with questioning eyes.

Zu An knew she was having a misunderstanding, but it was tough to explain this. So, he cleared the air with a light cough and asked, “Qinghe, do you need something from me?”

Murong Qinghe hesitated before finally gathering her courage. “Brother-in-law, can I hug you?”

Zu An was speechless.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2655: Request
chapters.body:
Chapter 2655: Request

“This… doesn’t seem appropriate,” Zu An replied awkwardly. Despite having once undergone the most intimate ritual, that was due to Chu Youzhao’s scheming. She was the Chu clan’s daughter-in-law, so it wouldn’t do for him to carelessly hug her.

“My request might be unusual, but I truly wish to hug you.” Murong Qinghe was already embarrassed to make that request, and insisting on it sapped all her courage dry. 

A thought surfaced in Zu An’s mind. “Did you argue with Youzhao?”

He had rushed here from the capital when he sensed that Phoenix Pass was in danger, so he hadn't had time to drop by the Chu manor. As such, he hadn’t met Chu Youzhao yet and didn’t know how their relationship had progressed over the years. 

“You should know why there’s a conflict between us.” Murong Qinghe was from a military clan, after all. She quickly overcame her embarrassment and regained her courage. 

“It has been years since we last met, so how could I know?” Zu An could fathom a guess, but it wouldn’t be good to speculate without speaking to Chu Youzhao first. 

“Is that so?” Murong Qinghe murmured before abruptly leaping into his arms to embrace him.

It was so abrupt that Zu An’s protective ki layer reflexively activated, jolting her back. He hurriedly reined in its strength, but it was still too much for her to bear. With a dull thud, Murong Qinghe spewed fresh blood. She collapsed on the floor, severely injured.

Zu An was both angry and worried. He rushed forward to circulate her ki and treat her injuries while exclaiming, “What are you doing? You would have been dead if I hadn’t held back in time.”

Instead of saying a word, Murong Qinghe took this chance to embrace him. Tears flowed down her eyes as she muttered, “It was indeed you that night…”

Zu An was stunned and awed by her intuition. She can tell just from a hug?

“I’m sorry. It was an accident…” he tried to explain, but he didn’t know how to go about it. 

“I know. It’s all big brother Chu… No, it was big sister Chu’s arrangement,” Murong Qinghe replied miserably. 

“You already know?” Zu An was taken aback. 

“How can I not know? She might have fooled me at the start, but the truth became apparent after I was married. Besides, that night was… I didn’t think too much at the start because I was too embarrassed, but in hindsight, that couldn’t have been big brother Chu’s body.” Murong Qinghe wiped her tears. “Since then, big brother Chu has never shared a room with me. I thought he was scorning me and was heartbroken. Fortunately, big brother Chu hinted at the truth to me because he was worried I would resort to something extreme. It was only then that I realized what happened on the night of my marriage.”

“Why did you still test me earlier?” Zu An was curious.

For some reason, Murong Qinghe’s face reddened further. “Youzhao refused to tell me that man’s identity, but it wasn’t difficult to figure out what happened that night. Given my years of ties with Youzhao, I doubt she would just find anyone to take advantage of me.” 

“You are the closest man to her, and you happened to be in the neighboring courtyard that night. I tried asking her if it was you that night, but she didn’t give me an answer. That’s why I needed to verify it myself.”

Zu An was intrigued. “You were able to tell with just a hug?”

Murong Qinghe’s face reddened. She turned away. “You wouldn’t know. You aren’t a woman.”

It was too embarrassing for her to say it aloud. That night had been too special for her. There was no way she could forget that sensation. 

“I’m sorry. I had too much to drink that night, and I didn’t know Youzhao would do something like that,” Zu An replied apologetically. 

“I know. You were a victim too, and she kept you in the dark as well.” Murong Qinghe had already figured out what the Chu clan was planning back then, so she didn’t blame him for it. 

The word ‘victim’ sounded off to Zu An. Had it come from him, it would have felt as if he were complaining after taking advantage of her. He asked, “You were hurt the most in this matter. Do you hate Youzhao for it?”

“Of course I do. I hate her for being a woman and not a man.” Murong Qinghe bit her lips. “I should have known earlier. There were times when big brother Chu felt too weak to be a man, but I fancied her for her weak, scholarly disposition.”

Zu An had to concede that the Chu clan had outstanding genes. The three sisters were incredibly good-looking. Chu Youzhao, when disguised as a man, looked like a dashing gigolo. Murong Qinghe wasn’t the only woman who had fancied him then. It could have also been because Murong Qinghe had been in the military for too long and had seen too many coarse men, thus making the gentle and graceful Chu Youzhao more appealing to her. 

“Now that I think about it, there were many signs. Big brother Chu asked me several times…” Murong Qinghe suddenly fell silent here.

“What did she ask?” Zu An was intrigued by her peculiar reaction.

“Nothing.”

Murong Qinghe suddenly remembered how Chu Youzhao had once asked her if she would have fallen in love with big brother Zu had she met him first. She hesitated briefly, though she chose Chu Youzhao in the end. Big brother Chu must have been confirming my feelings then. 

When she thought about how much Chu Youzhao cared about her, her heart ached. 

“Are the two of you on bad terms after talking it out?” Zu An han’t seen Chu Youzhao anywhere in Phoenix Pass.

“I don’t blame big brother Chu. I know she had no choice either. I only blame fate for playing such a prank on us.” Murong Qinghe sighed. 

Zu An felt the same too. She’s a good person. She still thinks on Chu Youzhao’s behalf despite having such a thing happen to her. 

“Big brother Zu, big brother Chu is a pitiful one too. She has to conceal her identity and live as someone else. Even her marriage was an act.”

“She is a pitiful one. But Linglong ascending to the throne will be the start of all kinds of changes. I'll push for women's rights, so that they can inherit noble titles and assets. This way, those who face the same plight won’t have to go through the same suffering,” Zu An replied. 

Murong Qinghe’s eyes lit up. “Are you doing this for big brother Chu? It looks like her feelings for you didn’t go unreciprocated.”

“Ah?” Zu An was taken aback.

“Youzhao harbors deep feelings for you as well, but she has to play the role of the Chu clan’s young master, and you’re her brother-in-law. She had no choice but to suppress her feelings,” Murong Qinghe said with a sigh. “I did think it was weird how big brother Chu was attached to you. It only made sense to me when I learned that she was a woman.”

“She might have deceived me, but after everything we've gone through, I believe our feelings for each other are real.” Murong Qinghe looked intently at Zu An. “You were the only one she would have chosen to take her place for that night, and I believe she would have rather been the one to accompany you that night.”

Zu An coughed. “I am her brother-in-law, after all.”

Murong Qinghe narrowed her eyes. “That means nothing. Sisters sharing a husband are praised. The previous emperor’s wives are from the Liu clan. Such things are also common among nobles and officials.”

“Why are you suddenly turning the topic to me?” Zu An felt awkward. “What are your plans with Youzhao?”

“We’ll just continue like that. If we part ways, her identity will be exposed. I don’t want to put big brother Chu in a difficult position. Besides, I've already said that I’ll be with her no matter what happens,” Murong Qinghe said with conviction.

It was then that she suddenly changed her tone and said, “Big brother Zu, even though that night… I hope you don’t take me for a casual woman. Don’t look for me again in the future!”

Zu An replied solemnly, “Of course!” I’m also not the kind who will casually take advantage of others. 

Then, Murong Qinghe whispered with a reddened face, “But it’ll be fine if big brother Chu agrees to it.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2656: This Is Improper
chapters.body:
Chapter 2656: This Is Improper

Zu An was baffled. What do you mean, it’ll be fine if big brother Chu agrees to it?

Despite being a youngster too, he struggled to understand what was going through the heads of youths nowadays. 

Shouldn’t Murong Qinghe be angry at being deceived about her marriage? She looks a bit angry, but she seems to still care for Chu Youzhao a lot. Despite knowing Chu Youzhao is a woman, she still takes her for her lover and husband. 

And she’s even willing to let Chu Youzhao get together with other men?

Zu An was confused. Is my understanding of love too narrow?

Seeing Zu An’s reaction, Murong Qinghe realized how culturally shocking her earlier words were. So, she added with a whisper, “You’re an exception. I wouldn’t let big brother Chu be with anyone else.” 

“I’m moved by the feelings you have for each other.” Zu An didn’t know how to reply to those words. 

“These are my earnest thoughts. I haven’t even told big brother Chu about it.” Murong Qinghe bit her lips. “Brother-in-law, you mustn’t tell big brother Chu about this. I’m still feigning anger at him.”

It took a while before Zu An squeezed out an answer. “Alright.”

This little couple sure knows how to play around.

Murong Qinghe suddenly looked shy. “How much longer are you planning to embrace me, brother-in-law?”

Zu An was speechless.

He impatiently snorted, “I’m treating your injuries!”

Murong Qinghe's injuries from the earlier jolt were so severe that Zu An had only barely stabilized her condition now. 

“You’ll have to rest up for the next few days. You mustn’t get into any fights. It shouldn’t take long for you to make a full recovery.” Zu An pulled her to her feet before releasing her. Her body feels young and vibrant, perhaps because she spends her days in the military.

“Thank you, brother-in-law. I was joking with you earlier, though. You can hug me all you want.” Murong Qinghe mischievously blinked at him. She seemed to be in a good mood. 

“Scram.” Zu An chased her away with a huff. This lass doesn’t know she’s playing with fire. If it had been in the past, she would have been gushing after being treated by my primordial ki. 

Now that Zu An had become the world’s master, he could treat her using the world laws, thus avoiding the primordial ki’s side effects.

Murong Qinghe ran outside. Her head suddenly popped back into the room as she said, “Brother-in-law, don’t forget about our secret agreement.”

Zu An’s eyelids twitched. She looked so wretched earlier that one would have thought she was living in misery all these years, but it looks like she's been enjoying herself in her way. 

A smile lingered on Murong Qinghe’s lips even after leaving the room. She murmured, “My dumb brother-in-law.”

What had worried her in recent days wasn't Chu Youzhao, but who it had been that night. Even though Chu Youzhao had implicitly hinted at that person’s identity to her, she dared not believe it until she confirmed it herself. Now that she had confirmed it truly was big brother Zu, it felt as if a boulder had been lifted from her heart. She couldn’t tell if her feeling for him was love, but she wasn’t averse to it. 

… 

Zu An’s door suddenly opened, and a beautiful figure walked in, asking, “What secrets do you have with little Qinghe?”

Zu An raised his head to see a gorgeous, oval-shaped face. He exclaimed in delight, “Dandan!”

He rushed forth to embrace her. He'd been worried that she had met with danger, because he hadn’t seen her anywhere in Phoenix Pass. Later, he'd learned that she was in charge of transporting rations from Bi Linglong, and wasn’t in Phoenix Pass then. 

“Ah Zu!” Zheng Dan hugged him tightly.

It had been three years since they last met. While she was confident in his abilities, she couldn’t stop worrying since he was marching into danger. She had woken up from nightmares countless times, after which she would solemnly pray for him. She had rushed here as soon as she received news that Phoenix Pass was in trouble, only to learn that her lover had descended like a deity and promptly dealt with the rebels. Overjoyed, she headed right over to look for him. 

The two of them hugged for a long time before Zheng Dan coyly grumbled, “I can’t catch my breath.”

Zu An was startled. “Was I hugging you too tightly?”

“You’re covered in other women’s perfume.” Zheng Dan’s eyes glimmered. She leaned in and sniffed him. “I smell Linglong’s fragrance. This Hundred Flowers Fragrance smells unfamiliar, but I’m guessing it belongs to that Concubine Bai. Hm? There’s Qinghe’s scent too. Tsk tsk tsk, you’re a beast to go after your sister-in-law.”

“I’m not Giggs,” Zu An huffed. “Don’t talk nonsense. Qinghe was injured, so I was treating her earlier.”

It wouldn’t be good for him to share what had transpired between him and Murong Qinghe, lest he ruin her reputation.

“Your treatment method must have made her swoon.” Zheng Dan knew best just how potent his primordial ki was.

“I'm the world's master now. I can avoid that side effect when treating others,” Zu An replied awkwardly. 

Zheng Dan’s eyes lit up. “I heard from others about how cool you were in that battle, something about you becoming a god. Is it an ability from being this world’s master? Tell me more about it.” 

She had been working hard in recent years, evident in the rapid progression of her cultivation, but there was no way she could match the growth of a monster like Zu An. This was a good chance for her to learn about the higher cultivation levels. So, Zu An embraced her and began sharing everything that happened over the past three years with her. Time passed swiftly. 

Halfway through, Bi Linglong came in to report about the imperial court’s affairs, only to be quickly drawn in by his stories as well. Her eyes shimmered in excitement, making even the plum makeup between her brows look vibrant. “Compared to what you went through, my life as the empress has been boring. I wish I could have been there with you.”

“Indeed. It feels like everything we've done in the last few years was just playing house.” Zheng Dan felt the same too. She had organized the capital’s underground powers and worked with Little Qien to help Bi Linglong stabilize her position and deal with the rebels. They'd thought that they had done a lot, but compared to what Zu An had faced, it looked like a minor squabble. 

“If not for your hard work, the world would have been in chaos. You shouldn’t downplay what you'be accomplished. It hasn’t been easy on you either,” Zu An replied. He had put them through a lot by entrusting everything to them. 

“Hmph! I bet you left us here because you didn’t want us getting in the way of you courting other worlds' ladies!” Zheng Dan harrumphed. “I noticed you were vague when talking about the Goddess of Wu Mountain, the Mermaid Queen, and the Sun Goddess… I bet there's something between you and them!”

Bi Linglong nodded in agreement. 

Zu An sweated profusely. There was no way to conceal things when someone understood him too well. It took him a long time to placate the two of them. 

Bi Linglong suddenly stood up and said, “It’s getting late. I should get going. Dandan, it's been a long time since you last met Ah Zu. There must be many things you’d like to talk to him about, so I won’t impose any longer.”

Unexpectedly, Zheng Dan grabbed her hand and smiled mischievously. “Isn’t it the same for you? There must be many things you would like to say to him as well.”

“Even so, given my identity, I shouldn’t stay here for too long.” Bi Linglong had many sentiments she would like to share with Zu An, but her rational mind held her back. She was now the empress. It wouldn’t do for her to stay over in Zu An’s room under public view, or else the novels in the streets would become even more explosive. 

Zheng Dan laughed. “Please! Who doesn’t know you’re the regent’s woman? Now that Ah Zu has displayed his overwhelming prowess, it wouldn’t look out of place for you to be with him. Rather, it would be weirder for you not to be here.”

Bi Linglong’s heart skipped a beat, and her face heated up. She hesitated for a moment before nodding. Then, she stared intently at Zheng Dan. 

Having become close friends in recent years, Zheng Dan immediately understood her intention. She angrily lashed out, “Are you chasing me away now? That’s too much! Don’t even think about it. I’m staying here tonight as well!”

Bi Linglong’s face turned bright red. She stood up to leave. “This is improper!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2657: Guilty Conscience
chapters.body:
Chapter 2657: Guilty Conscience

Zheng Dan moved quickly to grab her arm. “What’s improper about this? It’s been a long time since you last met. Others will understand.”

Bi Linglong got agitated. “That’s not what I’m talking about! I’m talking about… being here with you!”

Zheng Dan’s face turned severe. “I didn’t know you looked down on me, Your Majesty. You must think that I’m not qualified to be your sister.”

Bi Linglong exclaimed, “Don’t mock me with such bitchy words. You know what I mean.”

Shortly after Zu An’s departure, the capital had been in disarray, and the Bi clan had been purged in the previous capital upheaval. While Bi Linglong did have allies, there weren’t many people she could rely on. Sang Qien and Zheng Dan had helped her a lot in recent years. One had wisdom, and the other was skilled at socializing. The two of them had helped her resolve many problems. After working together so many times, they had long become friends. With her wits, she could tell that Zheng Dan was just playing with her.

“If you don’t mean that, why don’t you want to be here with me?” Zheng Dan harrumphed.

“It’s different!” Bi Linglong felt as if she was going to be driven mad. She looked to Zu An for help. “Say something, Ah Zu!”

“I think Dandan’s words made sense,” Zu An replied with a straight face. He secretly shot Zheng Dan a thumbs up. You’re the best!

Bi Linglong was dumbstruck. She was about to explode from anger. “You two are in cahoots!”

“It’s only a matter of time before such things happen. You just have to get used to it,” Zheng Dan replied with a grin. 

Bi Linglong widened her eyes in astonishment. “Hold on, you've already… tried it before?”

Zheng Dan smiled back at her without saying a word. 

But Bi Linglong caught her drift. The next question that popped into her mind was with whom Zheng Dan had done it together with, only to remember how close she was with Sang Qien. And to think I thought they looked closer than sisters! I finally understand why! 

She angrily glared at Zu An. You depraved man!

The maids outside waited for a long time, but the empress didn’t come out. They exchanged perplexed looks. 

The empress’ personal maid was worried. Your Majesty, do you still remember your position? You mustn’t be led astray by the regent! She glared at the other restless maids and told them not to fidget around, before arranging for some of them to return first to change shifts.

Some maids were confused. Why do we need to do shifts? It’s just waiting for a couple hours.

Bi Linglong wouldn’t bring powerless maids with her to the battlefields; these people were meticulously chosen because of their ability to double as guards. Standing for a few hours was no trouble for them.

“A few hours?” the personal maid sneered. These people don’t know how ridiculous the regent’s stamina is. 

It was a relief that the regent had constructed a barrier around the room to prevent any noise from getting out, or else she wouldn’t know how to deal with the situation. 

Some time later, Zhang Zitong rushed back, only to be blocked at the doorway. Startled, she explained, “I have something I need to report to the regent urgently.”

“Commander Zhang, I’m afraid it’s inconvenient for the regent to meet you right now,” the personal maid replied. 

Zhang Zitong recognized the empress’ personal maid and asked, “Is Her Majesty inside?”

The personal maid nodded.

Zhang Zitong immediately figured out what was happening inside, and that made her gnash her teeth. The empress pulled the rug out from under me while I was busy with official business. Indeed, all women lust for the regent’s body. 

She coughed lightly before ordering, “Guard this place properly. Don’t let anyone other than the empress enter the room, or else I’ll hold you accountable!”

She was their superior, so she had the right to issue commands to them. Hmph, it’s one thing for the empress to claim the regent’s body, but I won’t allow other vixens to join in.

Her arrangement made perfect sense, making it impossible for anyone to see through her motive. Even she herself was impressed by her wits. 

The maids exchanged perplexed looks. Considering that Zhang Zitong was their superior, as well as the regent’s and the empress’ aide, they secretly informed her, “The Sang clan’s young mistress is here too.”

Zhang Zitong was speechless. She stared at the room in shock. She knew how much of a vixen Zheng Dan was, but Bi Linglong had always been dignified and demure. To think that Bi Linglong would play along to Zheng Dan’s tune!

The other guards nodded in agreement. They'd been equally shocked earlier too. 

Zu An had constructed a one-way sound isolation barrier to avoid being oblivious to the happenings outside, so Bi Linglong heard the conversation outside loud and clear. She angrily bit her lover’s shoulder. “It’s all your fault! How embarrassing!”

The clean reputation she'd maintained over the years had gone down the drain. She wanted nothing more than to hide in a hole right now.

Zheng Dan mischievously blew at her ear. “Who dares tease our empress? You can issue an edict to execute them.”

“The first one I’ll execute is you!” Bi Linglong exclaimed. A ghost must have possessed her to have actually chosen to stay behind!

“Big brother Zu, she’s bullying me!” Zheng Dan shook Zu An’s arm as she lodged a complaint. 

Her voice was so cloying that Bi Linglong, despite being a fellow woman, shuddered. Do men like her type? Ugh, I can’t copy that even if I want to. 

Zu An was in a good mood. “You can bully her back.”

“Yes, my regent.” Zheng Dan’s hands shot for Bi Linglong’s body, causing the lofty empress to squeal nonstop. 

… 

The following morning, the radiant sun cast its rays on Phoenix Pass. Peace had returned after yesterday’s battle, and everyone in the city seemed to radiate bliss from their faces. Quite a few people were still discussing the regent’s valiance. 

Bi Linglong, who was surveying the surroundings, leaped in shock. She was afraid that someone had heard their voices last night. However, she quickly realized they were referring to Zu An’s battle with Zhao Yuan and the Shadow Group, and she breathed a sigh of relief. 

It’s all that vixen Zheng Dan’s fault! I lost my dignity as an empress last night! Does she usually go that far with Sang Qien too? 

Thinking about what happened yesterday made Bi Linglong blush furiously. I didn’t think there was such a side to Little Qien beneath her demure front. I should advise Ah Zu to calm down a little lest he hurt his body. 

But considering her lover’s unique constitution, her worries were likely to be unfounded. 

Zhang Zitong looked at Bi Linglong in admiration. Your Majesty is truly self-disciplined. She didn’t sleep a wink last night, and she could barely walk when she left the room in the early morning. Yet, she still perked herself up and insisted on doing an inspection. Is that her guilty conscience at work?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2658: Two Kings And Three Respects
chapters.body:
Chapter 2658: Two Kings And Three Respects

Bi Linglong had been at her limit. She'd been planning to do a quick customary check before returning to rest, lest she make a fool of herself.

But as time passed, she felt a warm surge of energy rising from her abdomen before diffusing into her limbs and marrows. Incredibly pure natural ki nourished all her meridians, causing her cells to buzz to life. Her exhaustion faded, and she even felt as if her cultivation had advanced a level. She was initially startled, but she soon realized what had happened and blushed. 

Ah Zu, as the world’s master, was worthy of being called a god. The things he had in his body were probably more effective than the treasures and spirit springs that cultivators fought over. 

She bit her lips. Now that there’s such a function to him too, those vixens will be sticking to him all day long. That made her jealous.

This was the first time the soldiers, who were used to Bi Linglong’s regal air, saw her coy side. Her peachy skin and her vibrant plum blossom mark complemented each other, causing many to stare at her in a daze. Even Zhang Zitong, who had been jealous the entire night, had to concede that Bi Linglong was truly beautiful. Even a woman would be mesmerized by her, let alone that philandering regent. 

“Commander Zhang, have you found Concubine Bai’s son?” Bi Linglong suddenly asked while walking on the street.

“Yes. We ambushed one of the Shadow Group’s hideouts in accordance with the regent’s intelligence. The place was heavily guarded by the Shadow Group’s assassins, but the regent provided us with their defense layout. We would be unworthy of being your imperial guards if we still failed at that mission.”

Bi Linglong gestured for others to distance themselves with a wave of her hand. She looked intently at Zhang Zitong before saying, “Commander Zhang, you mentioned the regent several times in your report. I can sense your deep admiration for him. Are you fond of the regent?”

Zhang Zitong’s heart thumped furiously. Oh no, is the empress jealous? Is she going to eliminate potential rivals now?

It was normal for consorts and concubines to scheme against each other and even poison imperial offspring.

Zhang Zitong reacted fast and replied, “You have misunderstood, Your Majesty. I merely respect the regent a lot.”

“Are you certain you aren’t fond of him?” Bi Linglong assessed Zhang Zitong with an unreadable smile. “I’ll tell the regent to assign you to another post and bestow you with a marriage.”

“No!” Zhang Zitong exclaimed. 

Looking at Bi Linglong’s playful gaze, she hesitantly added, “Which woman wouldn’t be fond of an unparalleled hero like the regent?”

Bi Linglong was moved by those words too. “Indeed, which woman wouldn’t be fond of him?”

Not only does Ah Zu stand at the peak of power, he is also the strongest man in the world. Most important of all, he’s so suave!

It was not just once or twice she had seen the maids secretly discussing the regent with glowing eyes. Zu An already had enough lovers; the thought of having to deal with more women who approached him with ulterior motives gave her a headache. 

“Commander Zhang, I’ll assign you a mission. Make sure to protect the regent well. Don’t let women with ulterior motives get close to him.”

Zhang Zitong was momentarily stunned before her face lit up in delight. “Yes, Your Majesty!”

At the same time, she was perplexed why the empress wasn’t worried about her. The empress must have entrusted me with this mission because she sees me as one of her own. Maybe she has acknowledged that I’d eventually become one of the regent’s women. The thought of that made her excited. 

Just then, Zu An’s deep, magnetic voice echoed, “Come here, Zitong.”

Zhang Zitong jolted in shock. She looked around her, but Zu An was nowhere to be found. Am I having auditory hallucinations because I’m missing him too much?

“What are you thinking? Come to the city lord manor right now!” Zu An huffed. 

It was only then Zhang Zitong realized that Zu An was talking to her remotely. That awed her. The regent is truly powerful. 

Noticing her astonishment, Bi Linglong asked, “What’s wrong?”

Zhang Zitong replied, “The regent has summoned me over. He probably has something to ask me.”

“It’s likely regarding Concubine Bai. I’ll head there with you.” Bi Linglong was concerned about this matter too. 

Zhang Zitong couldn’t turn her down, so the two of them went to meet Zu An together. 

Looking at the empress’ silhouette, Zhang Zitong chuckled under her breath.This must be a one-of-a-kind situation in history—the empress paying a visit to the regent, though I doubt anyone sees a problem in that after the previous capital upheaval and the recent battle at the Phoenix Pass. 

They arrived at the city lord manor, where they saw Concubine Bai seated in the main hall, anxiously waiting for them. When she saw the two of them, she hurriedly stood up and greeted, “Paying respects to Your Majesty.” She nervously looked at Zhang Zitong, her fingers clutching tightly onto her skirt.

“You may dispense with the formalities.” Bi Linglong supported Concubine Bai upright. She happened to meet Zu An’s gaze, which instantly reminded her about what had happened last night, prompting her to avert her gaze. I wonder where Zheng Dan is… 

Zu An also remembered the passionate night they'd shared, and his heart tingled. He quickly reined himself in with a cough before asking, “Zitong, how was the mission last night?”

Zhang Zitong had to hold herself back from rolling her eyes. I was going to report this matter last night, but you were busy messing around in your room. Even so, she couldn’t say those words aloud. Instead, she reported what she had just told Bi Linglong in detail. 

When she learned her son was safe and sound, Concubine Bai breathed a sigh of relief. 

“Take Concubine Bai to meet her child later,” Zu An instructed. “I’ll also entrust you with the concoction of their antidotes. You can go to the Embroidered Envoys and the academies.”

“Thank you, regent!” Concubine Bai was grateful. 

“Since Linglong is here too, shall we discuss how we should settle them?” Zu An proposed. 

“I am a sinner, so I don’t dare to hope for much. All I ask for is a small courtyard in the capital,” Concubine Bai meekly said. 

Bi Linglong’s wariness soared. She wants a courtyard in the capital? That’s as good as telling Zu An to visit her whenever he can! I’d be a fool to give them a house so they could have a tryst with each other. 

So, she replied with a smile, “Sister, I can’t wrong you two. You both have special standing. There are similar precedents in history. For instance, there was a system of ‘two kings and three respects’ in the ancient era. Each dynasty conferred the royalties of the previous two dynasties as nobles and granted them fiefs. Given your stature, you can pick any province of your liking, and I’ll confer it to you.”

It had become paramount to change the dynasty. After consecutive rebellions, Zu An knew better than to give the Zhao clan another chance. His kindness would only bring more conflicts to the world. After just having quelled the Zhao clan’s rebellion, his prestige was at an all-time high; few would dare to oppose him at this juncture. He was determined to clean things up for Bi Linglong before leaving this world. 

Concubine Bai’s son was the successor of the previous dynasty. His young age and relationship with Concubine Bai made it easier to deal with him than the others from the Zhao clan. Bi Linglong’s proposal was thorough, having considered different aspects of the issue. Her wealth of experience in governance showed in her decision-making.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2659: Old Ties
chapters.body:
Chapter 2659: Old Ties

Concubine Bai interjected, “But that’s as good as conceding that the new dynasty succeeds the Zhou Dynasty. Wouldn’t that be unfair to big brother Zu?” 

“That’s true.” Bi Linglong agreed.

Zu An had saved the world from the monster invasion, and practically all powers in the world recognized him as their lord. Not even legendary emperors in human history had achieved the same height as him. He didn’t have to inherit the Zhou Dynasty’s mantle; it was beneath him to do so.

Zu An laughed. “Why would it be unfair to me? I’m not the emperor.”

Bi Linglong rolled her eyes. “Who else, other than you, is worthy of being the emperor?”

“You’re doing a good job as the empress.” Zu An shook his head. He had no interest in being the emperor. His eyes were set on the stars, the myriad worlds. Being a nation’s emperor held little significance to him. 

“Not at all. It all went up in smoke within two years after you left,” Bi Linglong said with a sigh. She was proud and confident in her governing ability, but there had been many frustrations in recent years that made her realize that there was little she could do alone. 

“They only acknowledge me because of you. You’re more suited to be the emperor. With the prestige you command, everyone will bow down to you willingly.”

She had ordered the Embroidered Envoys to spread news about Zu An’s immense accomplishment in driving back the monsters, building up his immense prestige among the populace. In truth, she had been preparing for this day.

“I’ll have to leave for a long time very soon. How can I be a good emperor when I can’t be present?” Zu An looked at Bi Linglong. “Linglong, you’ve already done very well. I believe that you’re a good empress. Now that I’ve cleared up all the underlying risks, you won’t have to worry about another rebellion anymore.” 

Bi Linglong knew Zu An wasn’t the type to be bound to one place, and being the emperor would only be a constraint on him. She bit her lips and glanced at Concubine Bai and Zhang Zitong. After some hesitation, she asked, “Big brother Zu, have you considered how the others might feel if you make me empress? Won’t they be upset and dissatisfied?”

She was only one of his many lovers, and his other lovers were outstanding in their own right too. They could be dissatisfied with him giving so much to her. In particular, the big miss from the Chu clan. They might have separated peacefully, but she’s still his legal wife. 

Furthermore, there are also other lovers whose capability and beauty don’t pale in comparison to mine. What right do I have to be the empress? What if others turn against me, and this just leads to disharmony in Ah Zu’s household?

Zu An burst into laughter. “You don’t have to worry about that. They’re cultivators, and they have their pursuits too. I’ll help them realize their dreams. Of course, Little Qien and the others can assist you with your work too.”

“But I still don’t think I’m suited…” Had it been years ago, Bi Linglong would have taken the position without hesitation. Back then, she’d prioritized her clan above all else, and she also wanted to exact her vengeance.

But now, all those things felt fleeting to her. She would rather spend her time with her lover. 

Zu An interjected, “Have you not considered a bigger problem? If I become the emperor, who should become the empress?”

Concubine Bai and Zhang Zitong were taken aback. They looked at Bi Linglong with gossipy eyes. Neither of them dared harbor thoughts of becoming the empress, but would Bi Linglong, as the current empress, lower herself to be an ordinary concubine?

And if Bi Linglong wanted to be Zu An’s empress, the other ladies wouldn’t necessarily let things slip. Being the nation’s empress was one thing, but being Zu An’s empress was another matter. The latter meant being Zu An’s legal wife; the others wouldn’t give up on that position so easily.

Bi Linglong was also stunned, as she realized how tricky the situation was. If Zu An became the emperor, she would be at a disadvantage competing for the empress seat. 

It’d probably go to the big miss of the Chu clan. She is the legitimate wife, after all. 

But the thought of lowering herself to serve tea to other women pricked her pride. 

Noticing her dilemma, Zu An laughed. “Let’s not dwell on this. It’s too troublesome for me to be the emperor. It’ll be better for you to be the empress and bring prosperity to the people.”

Bi Linglong’s lips twitched, but she decided not to dwell on the topic. She took her leave with Concubine Bai so the two of them could discuss a legal way to settle her and her son without undermining the regent. 

After they left, Zu An turned to the long-legged guard off to the side and said, “Zitong, there seem to be words on the tip of your tongue. Feel free to say whatever you have in mind.”

Zhang Zitong hesitated, but she spoke up anyway, “Regent, I don’t think what you’re doing is fair.”

“Oh? How am I being unfair?” Zu An was surprised.

“During the founding of the Zhou Dynasty, the Zhou clan went after the preceding Meng Dynasty’s imperial family. Many Meng clansmen and their subjects died as a result. And yet, you showed such compassion to the Zhao clan.” Zhang Zitong’s eyes reddened.

She must have been reminded about the casualties suffered by her Zhang clan protecting the old imperial grandson. Zu An walked over and patted her shoulder. “You don’t think I’ve killed enough Zhao clansmen yet?”

He hadn’t gone after them out of a grudge, but there was no denying that he had killed many Zhao clansmen.

Zhang Zitong was startled. She belatedly remembered how the regent had previously purged respected and influential Zhao clansmen in the previous capital uprisings. That greatly placated her. “I wasn’t thinking clearly, regent.”

Zu An smiled. “Concubine Bai’s son is a child. We shouldn’t continue the bloodshed for the world’s stability. However, your words do remind me of something. Contact the Hub of Freedom and have them create a list of the Meng Dynasty’s survivors. I’ll acknowledge them and grant them a fief where they can live openly.”

Zhang Zitong’s eyes lit up. “Is that for real?”

“Of course it is. Do you think I’d lie about this?” Zu An huffed.

“I’ll do it right now!” Feeling conscience-stricken, Zhang Zitong meekly looked at Zu An and said, “Regent, I haven’t contacted them since I started following you. You aren’t… testing me, right?”

Zu An replied, “…Do you think I’d need to do something like that?”

Zhang Zitong beamed. “That’s true. But regent, those people would rather have you become the emperor and restore the Meng Dynasty’s glory.”

Zu An shook his head. “Dynasties come and go; this has been the unchanging way of the world. I don’t intend to get involved in such matters, so don’t mention it ever again.”

“Yes, I won’t mention it again.” Zhang Zitong was disappointed.

Zu An relented with a sigh. “Zitong, I’m not going to get involved with the Meng Dynasty, but I won’t forget the Zhang clan’s sacrifice. I’ll build a monument for the Zhang clan so that the world remembers their heroic deeds even after ten thousand years. Also, I’ve never thought of you as a subordinate, so you don’t need to be so uptight around me.” 

Those words evoked Zhang Zitong’s past memories, and tears glimmered in her eyes. She kneeled and kowtowed to him. With a hoarse voice, she said, “I thank you on their behalf, regent.” 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2660: Acquaintances
chapters.body:
Chapter 2660: Acquaintances

Zu An hurriedly pulled Zhang Zitong up to her feet. “You don’t need to do something like that! I owe your Zhang clan a lot. I haven’t properly repaid you yet. Is there anything you want? I’ll fulfill it as long as it’s within my means.”

He wasn’t the real imperial grandson, but it was thanks to the Zhang clan that he’d grown up safely. He was indebted to the Zhang clan for their sacrifice.

Feeling the heat of Zu An’s palms on her arms made Zhang Zitong’s heart race. The regent has touched me! Through her excitement, she heard Zu An’s words, and that made her heart swell in bliss. “Can I ask for anything?”

“You can give it a try. There are very few things that are beyond me now.” Zu An smiled. Despite everything he had been through, he still enjoyed her reverent gaze. 

Zhang Zitong’s breathing hastened. Her face reddened as she thought about something, but she suppressed that thought and said, “I wish to continue serving you as your guard. No, not just a guard, but… What was the term you used back then? Yes, secretary. That.”

Zu An was stunned. “You’re already my guard and secretary. That isn’t much of a wish. Why don’t you pick something else?”

But Zhang Zitong shook her head. Her mind was made up. “That’s what I want!”

She knew that Zu An’s pond was filled with ferocious, gigantic sharks, and a shrimp like her would only be swallowed whole if she joined the battlefield. But if she became his secretary, no matter how many lovers he had, she could remain the one closest to him.

Those sharks might even have to fawn over me instead!

The thought of the lofty empress, noblewomen, and holy maidens fawning over her made her so happy that she nearly burst into laughter. 

Zu An couldn’t understand her bizarre wish, but since it wasn’t anything difficult, he accepted her request. “Alright. Let me know if there’s anything else you want.” Although, he didn’t think this would be enough to repay the Zhang clan.

“This is enough.” Zhang Zitong grinned merrily.

… 

After organizing the rebel army in Phoenix Pass, Zu An and Bi Linglong brought the rebel kings to the capital as prisoners. The political situation immediately stabilized. Many major clans had been secretly liaising with the rebels in search of a way out, but with the rebellion quelled, no one dared to harbor traitorous thoughts anymore. 

It also helped that Bi Linglong had publicly burned the letters she obtained from the rebel army, declaring that she would only punish the masterminds. That won her tremendous support from the people. 

Zu An entrusted all administrative work to Bi Linglong, including the clean-up and the transition of dynasties. Meanwhile, he went to the courtyard on top of Mount Yuquan and gathered all the teachers there. 

Other than Second Senior Drunken Sword Immortal Xiao Yao, who was wandering the world, Sixth Junior Painter Yu Yanluo, who was with the Fiend races, and Ninth Junior Bard Shang Liuyu, who was with the Ocean races, the rest were all present.

“Paying respect to the libationer!” All of them bowed to Zu An.

To them, his position as the libationer was more important than his post in the imperial court. They keenly noticed that Jiang Luofu hadn’t returned with him, and that made their hearts sink in sorrow.  

Zu An knew they had misunderstood, so he quickly told them about the events during the trip and where Jiang Luofu was likely to be.

“A universal deity?” The crowd was startled. 

They were the old libationer’s disciples, masters of their respective fields. They were knowledgeable. But learning about universal deities and the battle against Zhao Yuan at the Phoenix Pass made them realize that they were still frogs in a well.

Even so, this only sparked their enthusiasm to get to the bottom of things. 

Knowing they were a bunch of obsessed academics, Zu An warned them, “Don’t recklessly dive into this. Catching the attention of a universal deity is more likely to bring destruction, though if you happen to be suited for their path, it is possible for them to bestow you with power instead, such as in Zhao Yuan’s case.”

Qi Yaoguang blinked. “Is the libationer telling us to look into deities whose dao aligns with ours, so we can have a higher chance of getting acknowledged?” 

Zu An nodded. “It would be best for you to avoid getting involved with them, but I know you’re devoted academics. You won’t let an opportunity to uncover the unknown slip past your fingers, so I won’t waste my breath here.” 

Yan Xiangu frankly replied, “I’ve been pursuing the heavenly dao for a long time now. I can’t stop now that a path has emerged. It might be fraught with dangers, but I have no regrets dying while in pursuit of the truth.” Despite his emaciated physique, he looked unwavering in his stance. 

“Great senior is right!” The others voiced their agreement. They weren’t to give up on their paths for safety. 

But Ji Dengtu was more worried about something else. “Why don’t I see Xiaoxi? Don’t tell me you lost Xiaoxi on top of my little aunt?” He lurched forth and grabbed Zu An’s collar. His voice was quivering. 

The others frowned. If anyone else dared to treat the libationer in such a manner, they would have intervened right away, but Ji Dengtu had a special relationship with him. 

“Brother Zu, what about my big sister?” Xie Xiu asked. A third-generation disciple like him wasn’t qualified to attend this meeting, but he was Zu An’s friend and Xie Daoyun’s little brother, so Zu An had called him over too. He was only speaking up now because he hadn’t dared to interject when his elders were talking. 

Yan Xiangu also asked, “Indeed, Libationer. Is Daoyun safe?” He, too, was worried about his disciple’s safety.
