“You don’t have to worry. They’re safe. I’ll bring them over right now.” Zu An wasn’t angry even though Ji Dengtu’s saliva was splattering all over his face. They had, after all, forged a strong bond over erotica back then. 

The crowd was perplexed. Right now? How? Are they in this room too? But we went out to welcome the libationer together earlier. He didn’t bring anyone else other than the beautiful guard with long legs. 

Zu An abruptly paused for a second before saying, “Xiaoxi is fine, but Xie Daoyun’s situation is a little unique. You should prepare yourselves mentally.”

“Ah?” Yan Xiangu and Xie Xiu’s faces paled. 

“Ah? Is she crippled, poisoned, or in a coma?” Xie Xiu asked about a series of frightening possibilities. 

“That’s not it. She’s fine, it’s just that her appearance has changed.”

“What do you mean, her appearance has changed? Is she disfigured?”

Zu An had no idea how to explain this, so he decided to let them witness it in person instead. Thus, he began communing with the world’s will.

There were a few flashes of light, and several figures suddenly appeared in the house. 

“Xiaoxi!” Ji Dengtu nearly burst into tears when he saw Ji Xiaoxi.

There was also the well-endowed Pei Mianman, the innocent yet seductive King Wu Consort, and a woman who was dressed like an ancient shaman but emanated a familiar scholarly air.

“Where’s my big sister?” Xie Xiu panicked when he didn’t see his big sister. 

“Xiu’er, it has only been a while. Have you already forgotten me?” Xie Daoyun had been chatting with her Fiend race friends when she was suddenly transported here. She heard her little brother’s words and instinctively reached out to pinch his ears. 

“Ah?” Xie Xiu, Yan Xiangu, and the others froze up. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2661: Seed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2661: Seed

“Huh? Who are you?” Xie Xiu immediately retreated like an alarmed rabbit while staring at her with wary eyes. 

Xie Daoyun belatedly remembered that she was still assuming Yumen Beiqing’s appearance. Frustrated, she said, “I’m your big sister. It’s a long story.”

“Brother Zu, who is she?” Xie Xiu refused to believe it. He thought Zu An was making fun of him. He wondered if a mishap had happened to his big sister, prompting Zu An to hire an actress to fool him.

Yan Xiangu was perplexed too. Other than their similar dispositions, he saw no similarity between Xie Daoyun and the woman in front of him.

Xie Daoyun harrumphed. “You brat. You have two moles on your left bottom. You have been afraid of ghosts from a young age, and you always pestered the maids to accompany you because you didn’t dare to sleep alone. You peed your bed even when you were already seven…”

Xie Xiu anxiously interjected, “Stop, stop, stop! I believe you now!”

No one knew about his dark history better than his big sister. An impostor wouldn’t have known about all these things. His face darkened. I shouldn’t have questioned her identity. Ugh, I’m sure news will spread soon, and the reputation I have built up for myself will be ruined. 

Xie Daoyun just bowed to Yan Xiangu, and the latter tested her on talismans and formations, specifically knowledge that only they knew. From that, Yan Xiangu confirmed that she was Xie Daoyun… but he still couldn’t understand how her appearance could have changed like that.  

“Libationer, what’s going on?” 

“She is now the Fiend races’ esteemed Saintess Yumen Beiqing…” Zu An explained.

“That elusive Yumen Beiqing from history?” Those present in this meeting were well-read; they had naturally heard about the legendary Yumen Beiqing. They hadn’t expected her to be Xie Daoyun. 

“It is truly baffling. Yumen Beiqing must have been Xie Daoyun’s previous incarnation. An accident might have happened while she was transmigrating into the past using the Nature Jade, resulting in such an outcome,” Zu An summarized the situation. 

The crowd was in shock. Had they not witnessed it with their own eyes, they wouldn’t have believed such a thing could have happened. 

“I hadn’t expected the concept of past incarnations to be real,” Hei Baizi remarked. 

Qi Yaoguang’s eyes twinkled in excitement. “I wonder who my previous incarnation was.”

… 

Unlike the curious crowd, Xie Xiu was more concerned about his big sister’s appearance. “Will my big sister have to live out her life with this appearance?”

Zu An was at a loss too. “We might find a solution in the future.”

Xie Daoyun spoke up, “Big brother Zu, it’s not a big deal. After returning to this world, I found out that I’d appear as Yumen Beiqing in the day, but revert to my original appearance at night.”

“Your appearance reverts?” Zu An was overjoyed to hear that. 

Xie Daoyun nodded. “I initially thought I had reverted to my original appearance for good, but at daybreak, I transformed into Yumen Beiqing yet again. I wasn’t comfortable with it at the start, but I soon got used to it. I did spend a substantial time as Yumen Beiqing back in the past, so it feels as if both of them are me.”

Zu An deduced that returning to this world might have reawakened Xie Daoyun’s identity, but there was no way to say to be sure whether she would completely revert back in the future. 

Xie Xiu was stifled. “Ah? Doesn’t that mean I can only meet my big sister at night?”

Xie Daoyun huffed, “I’m still your big sister in the daytime!”

“But whoever marries you in the future will be marrying two people at once,” Xie Xiu said. He noticed his big sister glancing shyly at Zu An and frowned. “Brother Zu, did you… get together with my big sister while you were in the past?”

Zu An was awed by Xie Xiu’s sharp judgment as a brothel regular. The latter could keenly sense the difference in their intimacy level.

He was about to answer when the furiously blushing Xie Daoyun exclaimed, “You brat! What nonsense are you spouting? I’ll wallop you!”

Xie Xiu fled in a fluster. “You can wallop me, but you should at least inform our father about something this important!”

Zu An’s head hurt. Having too many lovers was troublesome too. I should find time to pay a visit to Lord Xie.

Ji Dengtu took the chance to ask, “Brat, what happened with the Cruel Poison Constitution you mentioned earlier?”

As a renowned physician, he could sense a terrifying poison that sparked fear from the bottom of his heart in his daughter’s body. 

“You don’t have to worry. I’ve found a solution for her Cruel Poison Constitution. We’ve gathered ingredients to concoct a pill that will aid Xiaoxi in cultivating the poison dao. This could be a blessing in disguise; her cultivation will grow by leaps and bounds.” Zu An recalled that one of the ingredients was the Defying Spirit Tears, and that caused his heart to squeeze. 

“Poison dao?” Ji Dengtu frowned. “Will that prevent her from marrying and having a child?”

As a father, he was more concerned about his daughter’s safety and happiness. He knew that poisonous creatures tended to store poison in their reproductive organs, so he was worried. 

“I don’t think so…” Zu An had never considered this, but this solution had been offered by Shennong’s successor and affirmed by a Celestial Emperor. They would have brought it up if there was such a huge downside to the solution. 

Ji Dengtu was angered. “What do you mean by ‘you don’t think so’?”

Ji Xiaoxi exclaimed in a fluster, “What are you saying, daddy? I won’t talk to you anymore!”

Being a physician herself, she had also considered this issue. But the Cruel Poison Constitution was too unfathomable; it was already a blessing for her to have found a way to survive, so she didn’t dare to hope for more. 

Not to mention big brother Zu is this world’s master. He wouldn’t be fazed by my poison, so that isn’t a problem at all. 

There was no way she could explain that in front of so many people, especially given her shy nature. Unable to take the embarrassment, she ran out. 

“My good daughter, I was wrong! Let me feel your pulse…” Ji Dengtu chased after his daughter. 

Zu An took this chance to settle Pei Mianman and the others in the academy. 

Yan Xiangu and the others seemed to have returned to the old days when they first enrolled in the academy, pestering Zu An to tell them more about the ancient era and the deities. 

Zu An’s wealth of experience had given him a deeper insight into strength, cultivation, and laws. His answers could always shake Yan Xiangu and the others to their core. Eventually, they decided to gather all the academy’s students to listen to Zu An’s lecture. 

Zu An knew he hadn’t been properly doing this job as the libationer, so this time, he tried his best to clear everyone’s doubts in order to sow a huge dao seed here. This way, even if something happened to him in Dreamland, the cultivators of this world would still find a path forward. 

The lecture lasted for three whole days. It wasn’t that Zu An refused to continue, but the crowd needed time to digest what they’d already learned. 

He had already sown the seed anyway, so it was only a matter of time before it flowered. 

Yan Xiangu and the others had only previously recognized Zu An as the libationer because that was the old libationer’s order. They’d respected him, but it was only out of admiration for his strength and character. 

Yet now, they were bowing to him as disciples. They viewed him as their mentor from the bottom of their hearts. 

Zu An returned to the courtyard. He suddenly sensed something and turned to the side, where he saw a figure dressed in a snowy-white robe.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2662: A Virtuous Woman Fears A Persistent Suitor
chapters.body:
Chapter 2662: A Virtuous Woman Fears A Persistent Suitor

The courtyard at the peak of Mount Yuquan was tranquil. The lotus pond in the courtyard blossomed all year round, and the red koi swimming in it were as good as spirit pets. Anyone who came here would be awed by the sight.

But the picturesque scenery paled in comparison to the beautiful figure standing in it. She had long, fluttery hair, brows reminiscent of the distant mountains, and a pair of bright eyes. Her fair neck resembled that of a graceful crane, and she wore a white robe resembling snow. Her very presence formed the most picturesque painting. 

Moonlight shone on her, and she naturally commanded the limelight.

“Big sister Yan!” 

Zu An was overjoyed to see the other party. He hadn’t expected to reunite with her here. She felt familiar to him because he thought about her frequently, but upon further thought, it had been a long time since they last met. 

“It was a wonderful lecture. Even I benefited greatly from it. I was your elder, but you can become my master now.” Yan Xuehen had a pleasant voice, but her tone was colder than the night wind. 

Even so, Zu An didn’t think too much of it. Her character archetype was that of a snow immortal; he would be more surprised if her personality suddenly changed.

“I dare not be your master. You are my master.” Zu An walked over with a grin. 

But Yan Xuehen subtly side-stepped to maintain her distance from him. “The world of cultivation has never been about age or seniority. It’s about how far you have advanced. Your cultivation far surpasses that of the past Zhao Han and the Fiend Emperor. You are qualified to be the master of all experts in this world, including me.”

Sensing her distance, Zu An frowned. He flitted forth and embraced her. “It’s been a while, but you’ve changed quite a bit.”

Yan Xuehen wanted to dodge, but it was beyond her means to avoid him. His warm embrace brought a frown to her face. “You’re using your skills to deal with me now?”

“You wanted to dodge,” Zu An replied brazenly.

“You have reached the peak of humankind, so why are you still acting like a thug?” Yan Xuehen’s voice quivered. She tried to struggle free but to no avail. In the end, she coldly said, “Regent, please have some self-respect. I am Chuyan’s master, and this is the academy’s sacred land…”

Whoosh!

She was kissed before she could finish speaking. To make things worse, the other party made a loud smooching sound, as if pulling a prank on her. 

Her fair skin was swiftly dyed red. “You!”

The White Jade Sect’s disciples admired Yan Xuehen’s beauty, but they found her too cold. She never showed any expression. Many had fantasized about how mesmerizing she would be if she smiled, but no one had ever seen her smile, let alone blush.

Yet in front of Zu An, she always showed sides unknown to others.

Looking at her angry yet embarrassed expression, Zu An thought to himself that this was probably the peak of beauty. He couldn’t take it anymore and stole yet another kiss from her. 

Yan Xuehen couldn’t struggle free of him, so she put on a stern face and said, “I’m indeed no match for you now, but are you the type of person to take advantage of women like this?”

Zu An chuckled. “Of course I am. For many years now, I’ve dreamt of going onto the streets and teasing fair maidens!”

Yan Xuehen was left speechless, utterly shocked by his shamelessness.

She helplessly replied, “Ah Zu, didn’t we agree to forget about the past and remain as normal friends?”

“This is normal for me.” Zu An was unfazed. 

Yan Xuehen was starting to get frustrated. “Do you hug normal friends like that too?”

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehan for +22… +22… +22… 

Zu An wouldn’t hug male friends like that, but he had no qualms with hugging female friends in such a manner. That said, he wouldn’t dare say those words aloud.

Yan Xuehan sighed yet again. “Ah Zu, I am Chuyan’s master.”

“So what?” Chuyan knows about us, but I promised her that I’d keep it from Yan Xuehen. I shouldn’t backstab her now.

“Stop messing around.” Yan Xuehen glared at him. 

“If I remember correctly, you promised me a hundred times. Surely a sect master like you won’t renege on your promise, right?” Zu An was helping jog her memory of their previous meeting. 

“A hundred times? I only promised thrice…” Yan Xuehen blurted out, only to realize right away she had fallen for Zu An’s scheme. She angrily pummeled her fist on his chest.

Seeing that she had returned to normal, Zu An burst into laughter.

After the two bickered a little more, Yan Xuehen said while gasping for air, “Can’t you release me first? I have something to say.”

She had already glanced outside a couple times, worried that others would request an audience with their libationer. 

“Do you still acknowledge the three times?” Zu An took the chance to ask. 

Yan Xuehen gritted her teeth. Seeing how he refused to let go, she had no choice but to reply with a “Mm”.

Her embarrassed response further stoked Zu An’s fondness. Yan Xuehen had become colder after several years of absence, but that was due to the advancement of her cultivation rather than their relationship.

She cultivated the Unshakable Daoist Manual, which required one to cast away their feelings. If he’d let her have her way earlier, their relationship could have had to stop here. 

Fortunately, the old idiom rang true—even the most virtuous woman feared a persistent suitor. 

Yan Xuehen eyed the man beside her with conflicted eyes. A long time later, she asked, “Have you found a way to save Chu Chuyan?”

“I’ve found Chuyan and stabilized her condition for the time being, but it would be a stretch to say she’s living.” When he talked about Chuyan,  Zu An’s smile vanished.

“What happened?” Yan Xuehan anxiously grabbed his hand. 

“You don’t need to be so nervous. Chuyan currently exists as a soul, and her condition isn’t as bad as you imagine. For the time being, the situation is under control…” Zu An moved to explain about the Naihe Bridge and the Oblivion River.

Even though she was supposed to have cast away her emotions, tears glimmered in Yan Xuehen’s eyes. “Chuyan is suffering there alone. She is too pitiful.”

She suddenly raised her head and declared, “I want to meet her.” She was worried about Chu Chuyan and wanted to see how she was faring. 

“That’s the Netherworld. The living can’t get there.”

“Aren’t you the master of this world and the Netherworld? You must have a way!” Yan Xuehen looked at him with hopeful eyes.

Zu An pondered for a while. “There is a way—soul projection—but it’s fraught with dangers…”

Yan Xuehen firmly replied, “I’ll do it!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2663: Condition
chapters.body:
Chapter 2663: Condition

“If you get careless, your soul could disintegrate.” Zu An grimly warned Yan Xuehen. 

Upon reaching the Earth Immortal realm, cultivators gained the power to draw their souls out of their bodies. The stronger they were, the further their souls could travel from their bodies. 

But that was limited to the mortal world, which was filled with ki that could nourish one’s soul. Even so, they had to be extremely careful of weapons that dealt with the soul, and their bodies had to be protected properly too. A soul, no matter how powerful, would disintegrate if it lost a body to house itself. 

The fact that they were heading to the Netherworld, which could potentially corrode their souls, didn't help either. 

“No matter how dangerous it is, I have to go. I have been worried about Chuyan all these years. I have browsed countless records but couldn’t find a way to save her. I finally have some news about her now. I won’t be able to rest at ease unless I see her in person.” Yan Xuehen looked anguished. 

Judging from her emotional fluctuations,  Zu An could tell that this was a knot in her heart. Yan Xuehen thought that their relationship was what had caused Chu Chuyan’s mishap. If she couldn’t resolve it in time, it could grow into an inner demon, and that would be the greatest possible bane in her cultivation method. 

Thus, Zu An said, “Alright.”

“When do we make a move?” Yan Xuehen breathed a sigh of relief.

Zu An looked at the night sky and said, “It’s going to be the ‘Zi’ hour[1] soon. The yin energy will be the most concentrated then; it’ll be best to head to the Netherworld at that time.”

He gave Yan Xuehen a brief overview of the Netherworld and the things she had to look out for lest she suffer a fright later on. 

Yan Xuehen calmly listened to him. Shortly after, she began meditating under his guidance, and by channeling the method he had imparted to her, her soul began drifting out of her body. 

Staring at her face close up, Zu An was mesmerized by her beautiful appearance and her unique transcendent disposition. No wonder even someone as powerful as Zhao Han had courted her back then. 

Yan Xuehen suddenly opened her eyes and noticed his scrutiny. That made her face redden. “I have another question. Will it be safe to leave my body here?”

She’d had many suitors, which cultivated her cautious personality. She was worried about leaving her body here after projecting her soul out. 

Zu An smiled. “Don’t have to worry. This is the safest place in the academy, and I’ll also set up formations here. No one will be able to reach your body. I’ll also be able to return here right away if anything happens. As long as I focus, I can keep tabs on anything happening in this world.”

Yan Xuehen was relieved. At the same time, she looked forward to reaching his level. “The records detail an unparalleled cultivation level, but for many generations, we cultivators thought it was just the ancestors’ imagination. I never thought you’d reach it.”

“Call me master from now on, and I’ll teach you,” Zu An said with a grin. 

“Pui!” Yan Xuehen spat before shyly looking away. 

Even though she had said that Zu An was now qualified to be her master, she’d intended it to refer to a solemn impartation of cultivation insights. But judging from Zu An’s tone, he was likely planning to exploit that position to take advantage of her. There was no way she could accept that. 

Zu An knew how shy she was and was worried her emotional fluctuation could affect her soul’s stability, so he stopped teasing her. “Are you ready? Let’s begin.”

“All right.” Yan Xuehen closed her eyes. Under Zu An’s guidance, she murmured an incantation, and soon, a faint silhouette stood up from her body.

Yan Xuehen thought it was strange to see her own body sitting on the bed. “Your method of soul projection seems different from projecting the yin soul and the yang soul.”

Zu An altered the surrounding environment to protect her soul while explaining with a smile, “There is a slight difference. The yin soul and the yang soul are just parts of the soul, whereas this soul projection draws out the entirety of the soul…”

Looking at Yan Xuehen’s soul, Zu An was perplexed as to why the projected souls he had seen were all wearing clothes. In theory, souls should have been bare and without adornment. 

It must be their subconscious mind at work. They instinctively feel that they should be clothed even when projecting their souls, which results in them appearing that way.

Yan Xuehen was enlightened by Zu An’s talk about souls. I should be able to make a breakthrough in my cultivation if I go into seclusion after I return… 

When she saw how earnestly he was taking this, her expression became gentler. He looks dashing when he gets serious. 

If she knew what Zu An was thinking, however, her lower jaw would drop to the ground. 

“Aren’t you going to project your soul too?” Yan Xuehen asked curiously.

Zu An smiled as he reached out to grab her. “Living beings are forbidden in the Netherworld, but I’m an exception. Come, let me guide you.”

Yan Xuehen didn’t turn him down. Being in soul form left her feeling insecure, and she instinctively wanted to stay close to Zu An. Her body shuddered when she grabbed Zu An’s hand. “This feels weird.”

“Your senses are more pronounced in soul form…” Zu An replied. He was reminded about how his soul had fused with Jiang Luofu’s in the Monster World—that had been an unforgettable experience to him. 

I wonder if she’s safe… 

Sensing something strange, Yan Xuehen asked, “Are you thinking about other women while holding my hand?”

She regretted blurting that out right away. It was unlike her to say such words. 

But Zu An was overjoyed. “I didn’t know big sister Yan got jealous too.”

“I am merely keeping an eye on you on Chuyan’s behalf. As her master, I ought to stop you from philandering while she’s not around,” Yan Xuehen replied coldly.

“Of course, of course.” Zu An could hardly suppress his smile. He leaned closer and said, “I have been feeling lonely now that Chuyan isn’t with me. Master, shouldn’t you take her place and accompany me to keep me from philandering around?”

“How can you be this shameless?” Yan Xuehen exclaimed angrily. She’d never realize a soul could blush as well. 

“Shouldn’t you go all the way when helping someone? If you refuse to help, I’ll have to look for other people. You’d have failed your duty to Chuyan, no?” Teasing the ice immortal was more fun than Zu An had thought. 

Yan Xuehen blinked. Something felt off about his words, but she couldn’t put her finger on what it was. “Just because I’m keeping an eye on you on Chuyan’s behalf doesn’t mean that I’ll help her with… this.”

“Why? If you take care of me, I won’t have the energy to look for others. You’d be doing Chuyan a huge favor here.”

Yan Xuehen was shocked to find herself moved by his words. This makes sense. It’d be better to keep him by my side than to let him philander outside. I think Chuyan would rather he be with me than other ladies outside. At least we’re close… 

That thought made her face redden. She angrily punched the man beside her and exclaimed, “You nearly misled me! You would still have the energy to find other ladies even if Chuyan was with you!”

She had a lasting impression of his neverending stamina. She had spent many nights exhausted over that. It would be impossible to satisfy him with just the two of them. 

Zu An’s eyes lit up. “It sounds like you’ve considered it.”

Yan Xuehen was on the verge of dying from embarrassment. She decided to just give in. “If you promise not to look for anyone else other than Chuyan in the future, I can… take care of you on her behalf.”

1. 11PM to 1AM. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2664: Don’T Even Think About It
chapters.body:
Chapter 2664: Don’T Even Think About It

“Sure!” Zu An accepted without any hesitation.

Yan Xuehen was dumbstruck. She angrily exclaimed, “How can you make a promise you won’t keep?”

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +66… +66… +66…

She didn’t think Zu An would give up on the forest for Chu Chuyan and her. 

“How do you know I can’t do it if you don’t give it a try?” Zu An looked at her with a smile. 

Yan Xuehen averted her gaze. She was no fool. Playing along with him was as good as whacking a dog with a meat bun—she would just get gobbled up. 

The two of them soon arrived above the sea. Yan Xuehen suddenly said, “I heard you’re on good terms with the Mermaid Queen. Shouldn’t you pay her a visit now that you’re here?”

Zu An was surprised. “I didn’t expect the aloof Immortal Yan to show interest in mortal affairs.”

Yan Xuehen’s heart skipped a beat. “Hmph! Who in the world hasn’t heard of your philandering affairs?”

She would never admit to collecting all the news relating to him over the years. In fact, upon hearing about his return, she had rushed here from the White Jade Sect without rest. 

“It isn't safe for a soul to remain outside the body for too long. I need to bring you back as soon as possible lest complications occur.” Zu An wouldn’t be so foolish as to bring a woman to meet her love rival. That would be as good as stomping on his own foot.

Yan Xuehen didn’t intend to meet the Mermaid Queen either; she wouldn’t know how to respond in the latter's presence anyway. Without revealing their relationship, she would be forced to watch their public display of affection as an elder. It was too embarrassing for her to reveal their relationship, on the other hand.

“I heard the Mermaid Queen used to be the Dragon Emperor’s wife? Don’t you already have many lovers by your side? Why do you still go after other people’s wives?” Yan Xuehen thought her dao of clear-mindedness, which she had spent many years cultivating, would shatter when she asked this question. 

He already has Chuyan, Miss Pei, the Devil Sect’s Saintess Miss Qiu, and me… Why does he still go after other people’s wives? It’s not just the Mermaid Queen. There’s also the empress and the empress dowager.

She had been reading the novels about the regent’s romances sold on the street.

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +110… +110… +110…

Her only relief was that she wasn’t in any of those books, or else she would be too embarrassed to face the world. 

Zu An felt awkward too. “It was a coincidence. When I first arrived at the Ocean races' territory, someone was impersonating the Dragon Emperor, and she was escaping from pursuers…”

He roughly described his first meeting with the Mermaid Queen by the seaside tavern back then. 

That changed Yan Xuehen’s impression of the Mermaid Queen. “It sounds like you aren’t at fault here. But why do you keep bumping into such incidents?”

Zu An was frustrated too. “I don’t know either. It’s the same between us too. You were initially after my life as well…”

The embarrassed Yan Xuehen chomped on him before he could finish speaking. Her face was all red. He’s like a magnet for women.

Before they knew it, they arrived at the abyss leading into the Netherworld.

When she looked at the pitch-black abyss before her, Yan Xuehen was no longer in the mood to bicker with Zu An. The dread she instinctively felt made her lean closer to Zu An.

Zu An wrapped his arm around her waist. “Stay within a meter of me. Living beings are forbidden from the Netherworld. You must be careful not to be afflicted by its death energy.”

“Mm.” Understanding the severity of the situation, Yan Xuehen obediently stayed within Zu An’s arms.

She wasn't averse to physical contact with Zu An, but her position and the habit she had cultivated over many years made her self-conscious about it. But if there was a legitimate reason, she didn’t mind being publicly intimate with Zu An. 

Zu An leaped into the abyss with Yan Xuehen in his arms. A light emanated from his body and enveloped the two of them.

Yan Xuehen glanced at the man beside her. She thought about their encounters, and the frost in her eyes melted into tenderness. She embraced him tightly while resting her face on his chest. Even if it’s just for a short while, he belongs to me now. 

It didn’t take long for them to land on the ground. Zu An gently said, “We’re here.”

Yan Xuehen looked up and assessed the surroundings. “Is this the Netherworld?”

The world was shrouded in a gray mist. Zu An had told her that was death energy, which could kill any living being upon contact. While she was assessing the surroundings, her body suddenly shivered. She bolted away from Zu An’s arms while staring at him in shock. 

“What’s wrong?” Zu An was stunned. 

“I don’t know. You suddenly felt very frightening. Every bit of my soul is telling me to stay away from you.” Yan Xuehen had no idea why she had such a feeling. Zu An was powerful, but it wasn't his first time holding her. She shouldn’t have been frightened of him.

However, this was primal fear.

Zu An understood what was going on right away. “I'm the master of the Netherworld, wielding the power insignia of the afterlife. All ghosts in the Netherworld naturally fear me…”

He cast a skill that shrouded him in light, isolating the terrifying pressure he emanated.

Yan Xuehen breathed a sigh of relief. She wiped away her sweat. “I've never felt that scared before. I felt utterly helpless, as if I would live and die at your whim. It was terrifying.”

Zu An leaned in with a smile. “It’s not as if that's never happened before.”

Yan Xuehen’s face turned completely red. “You’re so hateful!”

She also remembered how he had driven her to her limit back then, to a point where she begged him for mercy. Even her voice lost its usual coldness and then became cloying. 

Zu An's interjection did dispel much of her fear, however. It quickly dawned on Yan Xuehen that even though he appeared to be teasing her, he seemed to have his own reason for doing so. 

They made their way to a black pass. Yan Xuehen looked dazed as she murmured, “Is that the legendary Gate of Hell…”

This place was often mentioned in daoist records and folklore, but it was often shrugged off as a legend. She'd never thought a day would come when she was standing before it.

Zu An nodded. He introduced to her the Netherworld’s buildings, the distant Yin Mountain, and the Yellow Spring. 

Yan Xuehen studied her surroundings with curious eyes as she asked, “Why is there no one here? Ah… I mean, why are there no netherworld guards around?”

“There seems to have been a major incident in the Netherworld at some point in history, resulting in the disappearance of the netherworld guards. The place is still functioning thanks to the Netherworld Book of Life and Death and its intrinsic laws managing the ghosts, though.” Zu An remembered about the message left behind by the missing shamans, Ox Head, and Horse Face, and he couldn’t help but worry. 

Yan Xuehen sensed his worry and held his hand. “Ah Zu, I have been cultivating hard the past few years too. My cultivation has far surpassed the old Zhao Han. From now onward, I’ll help you no matter what you’re facing.”

Zu An was moved. Yan Xuehen was one of the female powerhouses standing at the peak of the world. Rather than being reliant on others, she would rather help shoulder her lover’s burden. He hugged her and whispered, “Will you help me when I’m with Chuyan…”

Yan Xuehen pushed him aside before he could finish speaking. The utterly embarrassed ice immortal’s complexion had turned completely pink. “Don’t even think about it!”

“I’m talking about cultivation. What are you thinking about?” Zu An looked at her with a mischievous smile. 

Yan Xuehen realized she had fallen for his trick and angrily pummeled him. For the first time in a very long time, laughter filled the desolate Netherworld. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2665: It’S All Acting
chapters.body:
Chapter 2665: It’S All Acting

As they walked down the Yellow Spring Road, Yan Xuehen noticed bizarre red flowers growing by the roadside. She stopped to take a closer look. “Are these the legendary Red Spider Lilies? How beautiful.” 

“They are indeed beautiful.” Zu An agreed. These Red Spider Lilies were the only plants in the Netherworld. Each time he passed by this sea of flowers, it raised his mood a little despite the stifling atmosphere.

“When Chuyan obtained the Pure World Icelotus’ intent back then, Miss Pei simultaneously obtained the Paramita Butterfly’s intent. Could her Paramita Butterfly be related to these Red Spider Lilies?” Yan Xuehen suddenly remembered something. 

“Her Paramita Butterfly does indeed resemble these Red Spider Lilies. I’ll bring her here when an opportunity arises.” With this experience, Zu An would be able to bring Pei Mianman here to view the Red Spider Lilies in the future. 

“I’d like to have a look at Miss Pei’s Paramita Butterfly, too.” Yan Xuehen felt goodwill toward Pei Mianman, not just because the latter's was Chu Chuyan’s close friend but also because of their interactions. Compared to the other women whom I haven’t met, Pei Mianman is much better… 

“I also hope for such a day to come.” Zu An smiled. Pei Mianman’s Paramita Butterfly was usually hidden in her body. It would only appear on her waist when her blood was racing quickly. The only way for Yan Xuehen to see it would be if we were together in bed… 

Yan Xuehen looked at him with a questioning gaze, sensing something amiss from his expression. 

The two of them ventured further into the Netherworld. Knowing she would soon meet Chu Chuyan, Yan Xuehen’s initial curiosity about the Netherworld gave way to nervousness. She bit her lips. She was probably the only master in the world who was nervous to meet her disciple. 

Soon, they arrived under the Bridge of Helplessness. 

When she looked at the blood-colored Oblivion River Water, Yan Xuehen’s face paled. With her sharp eyes, she could see the distorted faces of vengeful spirits within. Has Chu Chuyan been living in such a vile environment?

A seven-colored light glowed under the Bridge of Helplessness, forming an independent space. The racing Oblivion River seemed to avoid that space. But Yan Xuehen couldn’t see anything there. 

Zu An belatedly remembered the Bridge of Helplessness’ rules—those standing on the bridge couldn’t see those under the bridge. Thus, he smeared his finger across her eyes. 

When Yan Xuehen opened her eyes once more, she could finally see a pure white lotus pad inside the seven-colored light. A beautiful woman was seated on the lotus. She was either cultivating or in a deep sleep.

It formed a stark contrast to the Oblivion River. On one side were the vengeful spirits of hell, whereas on the other side was a heavenly fairy. Her presence somehow dampened the ferocity of the Oblivion River. 

“Chuyan…” Yan Xuehen’s voice quivered at the familiar sight. If not for Zu An holding onto her, she might have even stepped into the Oblivion River. 

“She’s in a deep sleep.” Zu An patted her hand, as if telling her to calm down. 

“How long does she have to remain in that state?” Yan Xuehen’s voice was trembling. She had noticed vengeful spirits in the Oblivion River swiping their claws at Chuyan, wanting to drag her down into the abyss with them. It was just that the seven-colored light stopped the vengeful spirits and disintegrated them.

The seven-colored light and the white lotus appeared to be protecting Chu Chuyan, but there were countless vengeful spirits dwelling in the Oblivion River. Yan Xuehen was worried about leaving her here. 

“A thousand years… No, she has 997 years left.” Zu An’s voice was light-hearted, but he didn’t look happy in the slightest. He thought that time was passing too slowly. 

“It takes so long?!” Yan Xuehen was agitated. “Aren’t you the world’s and the Netherworld’s master? Can’t you find a way to save her?”

Zu An shook his head. “She chose to spend a thousand years under the Oblivion Bridge in order to not forget me. This is a fundamental law governing the Netherworld; I can't alter it, at least not with my current ability.”

Yan Xuehen sighed. “What a silly child. If you hadn’t become the Netherworld’s master,  you wouldn’t have met her and learned about the thousand years of anguish she would be going through.” 

It would take an extreme degree of obsession for someone to withstand such a long trial. Many couples who had promised eternity to each other eventually succumbed to the years of suffering under the Bridge of Helplessness, and they could only watch in despair as their other half forgot about them. Their love turned into hatred, and they joined the vengeful spirits dwelling in the Oblivion River. 

“Indeed. She’s a fool.” Zu An felt a tight squeeze in his chest when he thought about the worst possibility. 

“It’s as if you’re fated for each other. She sacrificed so much for you, and you just happened to become the Netherworld’s master, which granted you the power to protect her.” Yan Xuehen felt apologetic. The greater the feelings they had for each other, the greater the guilt she felt. 

Chuyan loves Ah Zu so much. Who am I to get stuck between them?

“What happened between us is fate too.” Zu An sensed Yan Xuehen’s low spirits and hugged her from behind.

Startled, Yan Xuehen tried to struggle free from his arms, protesting, “Don’t mess around. Chuyan is right there!” She couldn’t overcome the mental barrier even though Chu Chuyan was unconscious. 

“Don’t worry. She can’t see a thing…” Zu An was halfway through his words when his smile suddenly froze in place. He noticed Chu Chuyan’s eyebrows fluttering. 

Then, Chu Chuyan opened her eyes and looked coldly at the two of them. 

Zu An: “…”

Yan Xuehen was on the verge of losing her mind. She'd almost struggled free of Zu An’s embrace, but she couldn’t tell when Chu Chuyan had woken up. If she saw what happened earlier…

She eyed the Oblivion River. For a brief moment, she even considered jumping in and ending it for good.

Chu Chuyan was the first to break the silence. “Ah Zu, master…” Her voice was feeble, and she sounded groggy. 

Zu An was both alarmed and overjoyed. He grabbed Yan Xuehen and brought her to the lotus pad inside the seven-colored light. 

Yan Xuehen shoved Zu An aside as soon as she got a stable footing. She anxiously explained, “Chuyan, don’t think too much into it. Ah Zu wasn’t hugging me. He was just…” Her mind went blank. 

“I wasn’t thinking much of it. Flight is forbidden above the Oblivion River. Master, you couldn’t have gotten here if Ah Zu didn’t carry you over.” Chu Chuyan blinked. “Master, are you calling him Ah Zu too? The two of you have gotten much closer.”

Yan Xuehen’s heart beat furiously. She awkwardly twirled her hair to conceal her awkwardness. “Is that so? It just caught on with me after hearing you call him that.”

Zu An nearly burst into laughter. Chu Chuyan was feigning ignorance to tease Yan Xuehen despite already knowing about their relationship. He'd already felt sympathy for Yan Xuehen when Chu Chuyan previously warned him not to give her away. 

Yan Xuehen looked conflicted, not knowing that Chu Chuyan was making fun of her. 

Unable to bear watching this anymore, Zu An wanted to tip off Yan Xuehen, but when he saw Chu Chuyan’s warning glare, he quickly sealed his lips. Chu Chuyan was going through a lot for him. It wouldn’t hurt for her to find some entertainment to tide her over through a thousand boring years. 

Yan Xuehen was oblivious to it. To not give herself away, she quickly changed the subject. “Chuyan, I never expected your seclusion to almost end up as eternal farewell between us.” Her emotions poured out, and tears streamed down her cheeks. 

Chu Chuyan knew that emotional fluctuations were taboo to their cultivation; her master had to be truly agitated to behave in such a manner. She thought about the time she had spent with Yan Xuehen, how the latter had taken care of her in the sect, as well as the bizarre experiences she'd had in recent years.

She suddenly felt incredibly aggrieved, diving into Yan Xuehen’s arms and crying, “Master!”

The master and disciple cried for a long time while Zu An watched them in a melancholic mood. 

Chu Chuyan had been extremely composed since their last reunion, but she was after all a young lady. It wouldn’t do for her to continue suppressing all these emotions inside her. 

However, she had been independent from a young age. She wouldn’t break down even in front of her mother, Qin Wanru. It was only to Yan Xuehen, who was like both a master and a mother to her, that her true emotions would flow out. 

After the two ladies vented their emotions, Zu An asked curiously, “Chuyan, weren’t you in hibernation? Why did you suddenly wake up?”

Yan Xuehen tensed up too. What if she saw what happened earlier?

Chu Chuyan rolled her eyes in annoyance. “You still have the cheek to ask that? Why didn’t you wake me up when you came to the Bridge of Helplessness after returning from the past? Were you planning to leave just like that again if I didn’t wake up?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2666: Turning Tables
chapters.body:
Chapter 2666: Turning Tables

“I know it isn’t hard for you to fall into a deep sleep. I feared things would get harder for you if I woke you up, so I chose not to disturb you,” Zu An explained.

“What’s the point of sleeping for a long time if you aren’t around? I’d rather wake up and spend each day with you slowly.” Chu Chuyan glared at him grudgingly. She took his decision not to wake her to heart. 

Yan Xuehen was surprised. She had never seen Chu Chuyan speaking in such a manner. The latter had always been cold-natured, and everyone in the White Jade Sect said Chuyan resembled her master the most. Who could have known she could utter such sweet words?

But then again, even her own Unshakable Daoist Heart had wavered, let alone Chu Chuyan’s.

Even so, it feels weird seeing this young couple murmuring such sweet nothings to each other, especially when the man is my man, too. She was shocked when that thought popped into her head. She hurriedly drove it out. 

While embracing Chu Chuyan, Zu An noticed Yan Xuehen’s discomfort. He was also feeling uncomfortable, especially since he could tell Chu Chuyan was intentionally doing this as an act in front of her master. 

Yan Xuehen will probably explode if she finds out that I helped Chu Chuyan fool her, but if I reveal it now, it’ll also cause an uproar… 

He could only try his best to balance both sides. He broke the awkwardness with a light cough and asked, “Chuyan, how did you know I was here?”

Chu Chuyan chuckled. “I’d be as good as dead if I didn’t hear a thing when you were speaking so much. However, it takes some time for me to wake up. A certain fellow had already left when I last woke up, leaving me to deal with this Oblivion River alone.”

Sensing her grudge, Zu An felt apologetic. “I should have just woken you up if I'd known.”

“I was worried you’d leave right after seeing me again, so I set up a formation to wake myself up as soon as you arrived. It’s a relief that I did that, or else you might have left after seeing me once more.” Chu Chuyan eyed Yan Xuehen as she spoke, thinking to herself that Zu An had likely brought the latter here to tour the area. 

Haaa, why would someone as incredible as my master fall for this pig?

Yan Xuehen’s heart skipped a beat. Can her formation record information? If so, she would have seen Zu An hugging me earlier… The thought of that was driving her insane, causing her to continue prattling, “Chuyan, I came here to meet you. I only looked for Ah… him to bring me here.”

Chu Chuyan eyed Yan Xuehen in surprise. “Master, you used to hate Ah Zu. Your attitude toward him seems to have changed a lot.”

Yan Xuehen anxiously explained, “I was worried you’d be deceived by his lies—that would have hindered your cultivation. Now that I know him better… I have to admit he’s a decent person. My view of him is different now.”

Chu Chuyan was speechless. You didn’t want me to be deceived, but you ended up getting deceived yourself.

After being kept in the dark for so long, she wanted to return the favor, so she had to keep her thoughts to herself. She harrumphed. “What’s decent about him? He’s full of flaws, especially his philandering ways. His eyes wander the moment he sees someone beautiful.”

Yan Xuehen agreed with those words. That fellow would be perfect if he weren't so lustful.

What Chu Chuyan said next nearly made her heart leap out of her chest. “Master, you’re also incredibly beautiful. I hope Ah Zu hasn’t done anything rude to you?”

“He’s been very proper around me…” Yan Xuehen said with a guilty conscience. She reflexively thought about Zu An’s barbaric ramming, and that made her face heat up. 

“Is that so?” Chu Chuyan assessed her playfully before turning back to Zu An. “Ah Zu, you mustn’t bully my master, all right?”

“Of course. I respect Sect Master Yan as my master, too.” Zu An decided to shut his mind down and not think too much about it. These two women are putting their acting skills into play. I could tear up. It’s just that the information asymmetry placed Yan Xuehen in a disadvantageous position right from the get-go.

Yan Xuehen felt as if a storm was raging in her usually calm mind. How can Ah Zu be so thick-skinned as to say such words? He respects me as his master too? Do you ride on your own master?

Chu Chuyan grabbed Yan Xuehen’s hand and gently said, “Master, I can’t be with him for now. You have to help me keep an eye on him, lest I find myself with an army of sisters when I return.” 

Yan Xuehen’s guilt was at an all-time high. How can I let down Chuyan’s trust in me? I’m not worthy to be someone’s teacher. I can’t continue hiding this anymore. “Actually, Chuyan…”

Chu Chuyan saw through her intention and interjected, “Ah, I nearly forgot. That fellow wouldn’t be tied down so easily. I heard the stories you shared with me the last time you were here. You got involved with more women in the past, didn’t you?”

Yan Xuehen’s courage was shattered by Chu Chuyan’s interjection, though at the same time, she was also curious about what Zu An had gone through.

“There’s no new woman. They're all people I know,” Zu An replied sheepishly. With two incredibly beautiful women staring at him simultaneously, he was bound to feel guilty even if he had done nothing wrong. 

It was fun watching the master-disciple duo playing against each other. No wonder ancient emperors were so fond of inciting infighting among the princes and the crown prince. Otherwise, the princes could unite against them. 

“How can you already know people from the past?” Chu Chuyan doubted his words. 

“I thought I shared the details with you?”

“I was deeply asleep. I could only sense someone talking to me, but I don’t remember the details.”

Zu An had no choice but to share the details with her again.

Yan Xuehen was flabbergasted. She'd thought their experiences in the dungeon were shocking, but to think that such mystical encounters were commonplace for him. No wonder he grows so quickly!

“Manman was the ancient Goddess of Wu Mountain, whereas Teacher Shang was the ancient Mermaid Queen…” Chu Chuyan was astonished to realize that the people around her had such secret identities. 

“Did you meet my previous incarnation? How about my master’s?” Chu Chuyan asked curiously. 

Yan Xuehen was alarmed. “Why are you dragging me into this?” But she was intrigued too. Somehow, it feels weird if he got together with someone who resembles me in the ancient era. 

“I didn’t see your previous incarnations.”

Chu Chuyan was disappointed. “Looks like Manman and Teacher Shang are more special…”

Zu An huffed, “They might be their previous incarnations, but I’d say that they're different people. Beneath their identical appearances lie completely different personalities.”

“You must have enjoyed it,” Chu Chuyan scoffed. 

Zu An was speechless. There’s no way to placate you, is there?

Yan Xuehen burst into laughter. Looks like Chuyan is the only one who can curb him. 

She was about to ask more about the deities when Chu Chuyan suddenly said, “Ah Zu, I’d like to talk to my master in private.”

Zu An was curious to know what she was going to talk about, but it wouldn’t do for him to eavesdrop when she was asking for privacy. Thus, he nodded and flew off. 

Now that they were alone under the Bridge of Helplessness, Yan Xuehen suddenly felt nervous.

“Master, there’s something weird about you,” Chu Chuyan suddenly remarked.

“What’s weird about me?” Yan Xuehen felt her hair stand on end. 

“I’m usually the one nervous around you, so why are you nervous around me now? That’s how the women Ah Zu had outside usually react whenever they see me,” Chu Chuyan assessed her playfully.

“Ah?” Yan Xuehen’s mind went blank. It’s over. She knows. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2667: A Higher Level
chapters.body:
Chapter 2667: A Higher Level

Many thoughts flashed through Yan Xuehen’s mind. Awkwardness and embarrassment gushed into her mind, overwhelming her.

But Chu Chuyan suddenly grabbed her. “Master, I know you’re worried about me, but you can put your mind at ease. I’m already used to it. It’s not that different from when I was cultivating in seclusion in the White Jade Sect; it’s just a change of environment. You know I prefer quiet. Staying here might be an ordeal to others, but it’s not difficult for me.”

“Ah?” Yan Xuehen blinked. Is that what she wants to say? She finally understood what it meant to have a guilty conscience. After breathing a sigh of relief, her conscience got to her, and she said, “Chuyan, I…”

But Chu Chuyan didn’t give her the chance to speak. “Master, I wasn’t joking about that. I won’t be able to accompany Ah Zu for a thousand years, so can you take care of him on my behalf?”

Yan Xuehen panicked. “How can I take care of him…”

“Ah Zu is strong, but he’s still young. As the saying goes, ‘deity at a whim, demon at another’. Master, you should stay with him and guide him. If he strays down the wrong path, I fear that no one will be able to stop him,” Chu Chuyan said. 

The serious talk cleared Yan Xuehen’s mind a little. She solemnly nodded. “Alright.”

As the leader of the orthodox path, the duty to uphold justice was ingrained in her. What Chu Chuyan mentioned could potentially happen, and that would spell a calamity for the world. 

Seeing her master’s grim expression made Chu Chuyan snicker on the inside. I know Ah Zu’s personality well. He might stray when it comes to women, but he won’t make a mistake in other matters. 

She had only said that to give Yan Xuehen a valid pretext to stay by Ah Zu’s side. When she first learned about her lover getting together with her master, she was angry, but many things had happened since. She'd gone on a long journey and even traveled along the river of time under different identities. This was minor compared to everything she'd been through.

Furthermore, she knew her lover well. He was a philanderer at heart, and it didn’t help that he was handsome and powerful. It would be hard for other women not to be charmed by him. I’d rather my master benefit than other strangers out there. At least we’re keeping it within our lineage. My master is the most outstanding woman in the world. Countless heroes were awed by her back then and courted her. She only remained single for so long due to our cultivation methods and her personality. 

Chu Chuyan had previously wondered what man could win over her master, and had even tried finding a dao companion for her. Who could have known that her master, who had shown no interest in other men, would fall for her lover? Should I be proud or angry?

To top it off, those two had only gotten together due to her ‘Snow Lady’ condition back then. It was a trick of fate for her to have played matchmaker between her master and her lover. 

Many years of interacting with her master gave her a good grasp of her master’s temperament. She knew her master couldn’t overcome her guilt, and it was evident from the other party’s conscience-stricken expression and distracted attitude. This matter had to have been weighing down on her mind. 

Intense emotional fluctuation was a taboo for her master’s cultivation method. At this rate, it could become an inner demon. 

But Chuyan knew her master cared about her reputation and was easily embarrassed. If she was too direct, it wouldn't be beyond her master to draw her sword and end her life on the spot. So, she had to advance slowly and let her master slowly grow accustomed to this relationship. 

Haaa, my lover and my master are hooking up behind my back, and I still have to give them a push. This sure is hard on me. She felt exhausted.

But when she thought about how she was playing her lofty master like a fiddle, she soon smiled with glee. I bet she’ll show the most interesting expression when the truth comes out…

The corners of her lips curled upward. She didn’t feel as indignant anymore. 

Yan Xuehen grabbed her disciple’s hand as she vowed, “Chuyan, I’ll find a way to free you."

Chu Chuyan wanted to tell her master not to concern herself with something that even Zu An was helpless about, but she was worried that such words would wound her master’s pride. So, she replied with a smile, “Thank you, master.”

The two of them chatted for a long time. By the time she came out to beckon Zu An over, Yan Xuehen’s eyes were red. She said, “She wants to meet you.”

“You cried?”

Zu An instinctively tried to wipe her tears, but Yan Xuehen stepped back while nervously looking at the Bridge of Helplessness. Despite knowing that Chu Chuyan couldn’t see them here, she felt awkward.

Before flying under the Bridge of Helplessness, Zu An cast a spell on Yan Xuehen to protect her soul. 

“I told my master to take care of you. She agreed,” Chu Chuyan said with a smile.

“How’s that possible?” Zu An narrowed his eyes skeptically. “Did you tell her the truth?”

“Of course not,” Chu Chuyan scoffed. “She still doesn’t know I know, so don’t you dare tell her. Otherwise, I’ll never forgive you.”

“Fine…” Zu An couldn’t understand what was going through her mind, but he could only go along with her. I’ll leave it up to time to resolve this issue.

Chu Chuyan rested her head on Zu An’s chest. “Ah Zu, I’m worried about your trip to Dreamland…”

“It could be a fortuitous encounter too. Either way, it’s something I’ll have to overcome. We can’t remain stuck here, or else we’ll be helpless if trouble comes knocking.” Zu An thought about the monsters and the Annihilation Beast that had destroyed the Monster World.

This was a Wonderpoint World, but that could still be insufficient against the universal deities. Given that Zhao Yuan and the others had obtained a universal deity’s annihilation energy, his gut feeling told him it was a matter of time before more trouble arrived. 

Besides, there was something he wanted in Dreamland. 

The two of them cuddled and chatted for a long time. However, it was soon about to be daybreak in the mortal world. Chu Chuyan was worried that Yan Xuehen’s soul would sustain damage if she stayed in the Netherworld for too long, so she reluctantly bade farewell.

While Zu An was taking Yan Xuehen out of the Netherworld, the latter suddenly said, “Ah Zu, about the universal deities you mentioned earlier, I think I came into contact with a similar power before.”

“What?” Zu An was startled. “Which deity did you come into contact with?”

Yan Xuehen was uncertain. “I don’t know. I didn’t see its physical form. It was a pitch-black world with a tinge of white around it. I sensed a peculiar power. It was even stronger than the Absolute Dispassion that I pursue.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2668: A Stronger Fourth Universal Deity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2668: A Stronger Fourth Universal Deity

“A pitch-black world, white light…” Zu An was at a loss. He had never heard of such a deity. He hurriedly asked, “What were the circumstances leading to that encounter?”

Yan Xuehen pondered it. “I was flipping through an ancient notebook in our sect’s library, searching for a way to save Chuyan, when I was transported into that pitch-black world. I felt unprecedented, immense pressure weighing down on me, making me shudder. I also felt a void that could make a person forget everything… It’s hard to describe that sensation with words.”

Zu An continued asking, “What was the ancient record you were reading?”

“The notebook contains the insights of an elder from history whose identity is unknown. Its contents are all over the place, mentioning an even higher level than Absolute Dispassion. That book has been shrugged off as nonsense, left in a corner to collect dust. While searching for a way to save Chuyan, I couldn’t leave a single stone unturned, so I browsed through it too.”

The daoist sects had always thought that the highest level achievable was Absolute Dispassion, but that unknown author claimed there was a higher level. Unsurprisingly, his words were shrugged off as nonsense.

As Yan Xuehen shared the notebook’s contents, Zu An frowned. The details were all over the place, sounding like a lunatic’s ramblings. It was understandable why the text had been so poorly regarded across the generations. 

The past Zu An might have shrugged it off as a lunatic’s delusion as well, but now that he had more information about higher levels, he thought about another possibility. “That unknown elder might have unknowingly caught a universal deity’s attention, but the universal deity didn’t favor him and chose not to bestow him power.

“His mind and cultivation weren't strong enough to withstand the universal deity’s pressure, causing him to go insane. That could be why his notebook resembles a madman’s ramblings.”

Yan Xuehen was taken aback. “Is catching a universal deity’s attention that dangerous?”

“That’s also what I’m perplexed about.” Zu An assessed Yan Xuehen. “You might have caught that universal deity’s attention after reading that notebook because your daos are aligned, but you fared much better than that elder—you didn’t lose your mind. However, I don’t sense special powers within you either. It doesn’t seem as if that universal deity bestowed you with power.”

For instance, Zhao Yuan and the Shadow Group’s assassins had been aligned with the Deity of Annihilation’s dao and were bestowed with its power, resulting in the rapid advancement in their cultivation. 

Yan Xuehen had grown significantly stronger, but that was the result of her aptitude and the enrichment of the world’s ki. It didn’t seem to be the deed of a universal deity. But if she hadn’t been bestowed with the universal deity’s power, she should have gone insane from the latter’s terrifying pressure. Her current situation was perplexing. 

“Even so, something seems to have changed within me. I can sense a higher level than Absolute Dispassion, and my cultivation speed is faster than ever. Sometimes, when I cultivate too quickly, I enter a state of emptiness. It feels as if I've cast aside my human emotions, and everything has become meaningless.”

Zu An widened his eyes in realization. “No wonder you were so distant when we first reunited.”

“Rubbish. It has nothing to do with that!” Yan Xuehen averted her gaze.

“It has everything to do with it,” Zu An replied seriously. “I thought it was because you'd reached a higher level in your Unshakable Daoist Manual, but thinking back on it, it was more likely that you were affected by your audience with the universal deity.”

“But I didn’t meet any deity then.” Yan Xuehen was nervous. They were dealing with deities here, after all. She feared that she would end up in the same situation as that elder. 

“That’s why I’m confused…” Zu An pondered about the matter, but he knew too little about universal deities to say for sure. So, he telepathically called out, “My beautiful master, do you know which deity did big sister Yan encountered?” 

Mi Li lazily replied, “You only call me when you need me.” She manifested beside Zu An as she spoke, though only Zu An could see her. 

“I didn’t want to disturb your rest,” Zu An replied with a fawning smile. 

“Aren’t you disturbing me now?”

“Desperate times call for desperate measures. Master, do you know which universal deity Sect Master Yan encountered? Is she in any danger?”

Mi Li sneered, “Tsk. You were calling her big sister Yan earlier, only to draw a line before me and call her Sect Master Yan now. Are you worried that I’d be jealous?”

“Of course. You’re big sister master. How can I call other people big sister too?” Zu An replied earnestly. 

“You have a sweet tongue.” Mi Li chuckled. “I overheard your conversation. There is indeed a universal deity who fits that profile, but I can’t say for certain. That universal deity is known to be disinterested in mortal affairs, so I can’t imagine him gazing upon a mortal.” She sounded confused as well. 

“Disinterested in mortal affairs?” Zu An narrowed his eyes. “That resembles the state of Absolute Dispassion that Sect Master Yan strives for. Could the alignment in their daos and that elder’s notebook have drawn the universal deity’s gaze?”

“That is possible.” Mi Li nodded.

“Who’s that universal deity?” Zu An asked.

“You’re getting ahead of yourself. Do you think I can carelessly reveal a universal deity’s name?” Mi Li scoffed. 

“That’s true.” Zu An nodded.

“But you don’t need to worry. It would make sense for that woman to not be bestowed with power if it was that universal deity who gazed at her. That deity is known to be disinterested in everything. He has never actively bestowed power to his disciples,” Mi Li replied.

Zu An was still worried. “What a wasted opportunity. I've heard that most people only receive a universal deity’s gaze once in their lifetime, and other universal deities steer clear of such individuals.”

Yan Xuehen had a chance to meet a universal deity but didn’t receive any power. Does this mean she won’t be able to obtain the powers of other universal deities either?

“Who says there’s only one chance to receive a universal deity’s gaze? That might be true for most people, but such rules don't apply to special entities. Besides, having a single universal deity isn’t a bad thing. Yan Xuehen’s universal deity is an extremely strong entity, even stronger than the ones you know of.”

Zu An was stunned. The universal deities he currently knew about were the Deity of Memory Meng, Deity of Chaos and Enigma Mi Li, and the Deity of Annihilation. And yet, none of the three was as powerful as the universal deity whom Yan Xuehen had encountered?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2669: Forgetting Everything
chapters.body:
Chapter 2669: Forgetting Everything

“But Sect Master Yan got nothing out of that encounter,” Zu An pointed out. 

“Nothing? Just sensing that universal deity’s power is a huge benefit. Yan Xuehen said herself that her cultivation speed is much faster than before,” Mi Li lazily replied. 

“Are there any disadvantages to this?” Zu An was still worried. 

“There are. Under that universal deity’s influence, Sect Master Yan may lose her human emotions and forget many things. There might come a day when she forgets about you too,” Mi Li gloated.

“What?!” Zu An was horrified. “Are there any solutions?”

“What solutions can I possibly have? This is a universal deity’s dao influence. If I could interfere in that, I’d be a universal deity too.” Mi Li rolled her eyes.

There was a long moment of silence before Zu An raised his head to look at Mi Li. “Does that mean I can only save her if I become a universal deity?”

“Perhaps.” Mi Li sounded unsure. She looked at him playfully. “Despite your carefree attitude, you go all out whenever something involves your lovers. Do you think it’s that easy to become a universal deity?”

“Humans ought to have something to work toward,” Zu An replied. 

Mi Li stared at him in a daze before snapping out of it. “That’s a good attitude. I believe you’ll eventually succeed one day.”

Zu An eyed her skeptically. “Are you keeping something from me?”

“What could I be keeping from you?” Mi Li yawned. “You interrupted my sleep. I’m going back into hibernation. Don’t disturb me if there isn’t anything important.” She disappeared in a puff of smoke.

Zu An frowned. He felt there was something amiss about Mi Li’s earlier expression, but there was nothing he could do when the other party refused to speak. 

“What’s wrong, Ah Zu?” Yan Xuehen called out. 

“Ah, it’s nothing.”

“You were in a daze earlier. Did you figure out which universal deity I met?” Yan Xuehen asked nervously. 

After some thought, Zu An decided to reveal some information. “I don’t know that universal deity’s name, but he appears to be a formidable being. You probably caught his attention because his dao aligns with the Absolute Dispassion you're pursuing…”

He shared what he had just heard from Mi Li. Yan Xuehen was a top-notch expert and had a strong dao heart. Rather than concealing things from her, it would be better to give her a heads up so she could prepare herself in advance. 

“You’re saying I might start forgetting things…” Yan Xuehen’s face paled. Despite pursuing Absolute Dispassion, she was seeking only to forget some human emotions, not to relinquish all her emotions, bonds, and memories. 

“These side effects might only start to show as you advance further in that universal deity’s dao,” Zu An explained. 

Yan Xuehen was silent for a long while before letting out a sigh. “What’s the point of becoming strong if I have to forget everything? Can’t I pursue a different dao from that universal deity?”

“We don’t know enough about universal deities to know the implications. Don’t worry too much for now. On the whole, this is good news for you. Let’s go with the flow and see how it goes for now.” Zu An patted her hand and consoled her. 

“How can this be good news? At this rate, I’ll forget about Chuyan and you…” Yan Xuehen’s face reddened as soon as she blurted those words out. 

Zu An was overjoyed. He embraced her and said, “I didn’t know big sister Yan cared so much about me.”

“You’re overthinking it.” Yan Xuehen tried to push him aside out of embarrassment. 

But Zu An held her tightly. “Stay close to me. You might get sucked in by the Netherworld otherwise.”

“Nonsense! We’re already out of the Netherworld,” Yan Xuehen grumbled. 

Zu An belatedly realized they were currently flying above the sea. He calmly replied, “You’re still in your soul form. The slightest carelessness may result in the dissipation of your soul. We might be out of the Netherworld, but you still shouldn’t let your guard down.”

Yan Xuehen bit her lips and held her tongue, allowing Zu An to hug her as he pleased. But when she felt his breathing against her neck, she couldn’t bear it anymore and complained, “Stop staying so close to me.”

Zu An sighed. “You might forget me in the future. I’m afraid I might lose you if I stray further.”

Yan Xuehen’s heart softened. She also feared this situation. But if I can forget everything, perhaps I can put an end to this ill-fated entanglement…

Her attention was quickly drawn back to the present. “Where do you think you’re touching?!”

“I don’t want to lose you.” Zu An made her anger dissipate with a single line. 

There was a long silence before Yan Xuehen replied, “Me neither.”

After hearing her true feelings, Zu An couldn’t hold himself back anymore and sealed her lips. 

Yan Xuehen’s body stiffened, but it soon fell limp. A ridiculous thought flashed across her mind. Chuyan told me to take care of him. Does this count too?

But she was more concerned about something else. If I’m going to forget everything in the future, I should cherish every day I have with him. 

Jolts washed over her like relentless waves. She was taken aback. “Why does it feel so intense…”

“Souls are more sensitive,” Zu An explained. 

“How would you know…” The truth dawned on Yan Xuehen as soon as she asked the question. He must have done this with other women before!

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +22… +22… +22…

But her fury was swiftly quelled by his tenderness. 

Zu An took her to the clouds. It was freezing up there, but cultivators of their level were impervious to the cold. On top of that, the warmth radiating from Zu An’s body made Yan Xuehen feel sluggish. 

“How beautiful!”

“How beautiful…”

The two of them remarked in unison. 

Yan Xuehen was startled. Her remark was directed at the starry sky, but Zu An’s remark appeared to be directed at her. Unbeknownst to her, Zu An had unfastened her robe. 

She clutched her belly and crossed her legs out of embarrassment. With the starry night sky reflected in her eyes, she whispered, “Only two more times.”

Zu An nearly burst into laughter. He hadn’t expected her to be keeping count, though he knew that she was the kind of person who couldn’t put down her pride unless given a legitimate excuse.

“Alright.” Needless to say, he wouldn’t argue about the validity of their agreement right now. 

Yan Xuehen tightly embraced the man before her while staring at the stars in a daze. Somehow, she felt bolder than usual. The rules and restrictions she usually imposed on herself faded away. Her cheeks reddened like peach blossoms. With a wave of her hand, she wrapped them in a layer of fog. 

Zu An was surprised. “Aren’t you blocking the beautiful night sky?”

“It feels weird to do it in the wild…” Yan Xuehen’s voice grew meeker with each word. She knew it was unlikely for others to bump into them at this altitude, but she felt insecure. 

Zu An agreed with her. Who knows if there are entities in the sky looking at us? So, he wove the clouds together to form a miniature world around them. 

Yan Xuehen was surprised by the familiar scenery. “It’s the White Jade Sect!” Even her room had been reconstructed in full.

“Would you feel more comfortable being in your room?” Zu An asked with a tender smile. 

Yan Xuehen felt embarrassed. You’re making me more nervous with this atmosphere. But she knew he meant well, so she chose not to say anything to dampen the mood. 

Zu An also projected his soul and walked over to her side. 

Yan Xuehen felt her head spin. This fellow has no sense of shame. I was wearing clothes when I projected my soul, but he… 

“Since you don’t want to be in your room, let’s head to the White Jade Sect’s other landmarks,” Zu An said with a mischievous laugh. He lifted her and carried her to the square in front of the sect hall. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2670: I’Ll Never Forget You
chapters.body:
Chapter 2670: I’Ll Never Forget You

Despite knowing this wasn't the real White Jade Sect, the sight of the familiar square still made Yan Xuehen nervous. “What are you doing?”

“I knew Sect Master Yan wouldn’t allow me to do it in the White Jade Sect, so I could only construct another one.” Zu An gleefully toured the area with her in his arms. “Does it look similar?”

Similar? It’s identical! Yan Xuehen thought to herself. She was awed by his ability to remember the details despite having only been there once. 

But recalling his purpose for recreating this scene made her embarrassed and angry. He's the god of this world now, yet he’s using his powers for such nonsense!

“Don’t do it here. It feels weird.” Yan Xuehen’s fair skin was now so red that it looked as if blood would ooze out of it. 

“Is it because there are no disciples here? That isn’t a problem.” Zu An waved his hand, conjuring figures wearing the White Jade Sect’s uniforms and carrying the White Jade Sect’s swords.

Yan Xuehen was stunned. She felt as if she had really returned to the White Jade Sect, especially when two nearby disciples noticed her and walked over to greet her.

“Paying respects to Sect Master Yan.”

Yan Xuehen was so embarrassed that she hurriedly buried her head in Zu An’s arms, not daring to reply. If there were a hole in the ground, she would have leaped into it without any hesitation. 

She knew that these figures were fake, evident from their spiritless eyes, but Zu An’s ability to reconstruct the White Jade Sect and its disciples was still ridiculous. The thought of the usually stoic Sect Master Yan being carried into the White Jade Sect’s most sacred hall and bullied in front of its disciples made her tremble nonstop.

“Are they not real enough for you? Unfortunately, I can’t construct real humans yet. Why don’t I take you back to the White Jade Sect? We should be able to get there in the blink of an eye,” Zu An whispered to her. 

“No!” Yan Xuehen exclaimed. She would die of shame if she returned to the White Jade Sect in her current state. 

But when she saw Zu An’s mischievous grin, she knew right away that he was teasing her. 

“You!” Yan Xuehen was so angry that she bit his shoulder. He’s terrible! No wonder Chuyan was fooled by him!

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +55… +55… +55…

Zu An laughed. With a wave of his hand, the surrounding ‘White Jade Sect disciples’ dissipated. “Looks like you’re satisfied with this place.”

Yan Xuehen gritted her teeth. She couldn’t say she was dissatisfied, or else Zu An would probably just bring her back to the White Jade Sect. He had always been fearless and brazen, and now that he had grown this strong, no one could stop him anymore. 

She thought about Chuyan’s request and earnestly lectured him, “Ah Zu, with great power must come great responsibility. You mustn’t use your abilities on such nonsense.” She thought that she had to correct Zu An lest he go overboard. It would be a huge pity if he went down the wrong path. 

Zu An kissed her ear and whispered, “I’ll stop messing around… if you promise me.”

The sensation made the sensitive Yan Xuehen shudder. After hearing his words, her face heated up. “How can you threaten me? How are you any different from the Devil Sect then?”

Zu An burst into laughter. “Oh, there is a huge difference. Countless Devil Sect experts have dreamed about taking advantage of the leader of the orthodox sects, the White Jade Sect’s Immortal Yan. None of them have succeeded to date, but I did.”

Yan Xuehen groaned in agony. This fellow is so shameless that there’s no reasoning with him!

“Fine. Do whatever… you want. But you mustn’t speak like that ever again.”

“It’s a deal.”

…

Yan Xuehen had never observed the White Jade Sect from this angle, but very soon, she learned the number of glass tiles on top of the White Jade Sect’s gates, the number of bricks in front of the hall, and the number of candles and incense sticks inside the hall…

In the end, she mustered her final sliver of demureness and meekly said, “You've already used up the three times I promised you…”

Zu An was amused. “This only counts as once.”

Yan Xuehen became agitated. “How can this count as once? From the gate until now…”

She was too embarrassed to continue speaking. This fellow is like a demon king! 

Seeing the lofty immortal revealing such mortal emotions further stoked Zu An’s excitement. He hugged her tightly in his arms. “I’ll make sure you never forget about me.”

Yan Xuehen realized that his actions were driven by the fear that she might forget him one day. That filled her heart with tenderness. She gently accepted everything and said, “Ah Zu, I’ll never forget you.”

…

Meanwhile, Pei Mianman looked at the ethereal fairy from the peak of the academy’s Mount Yuquan. “Sect Master Yan?”

She'd been in the Devil Sect when Zu An suddenly transported her here. It was to let Ji Xiaoxi and Xie Daoyun reunite with their family members. She was heartened by the sight, but she was also envious of the kinship they shared. 

She'd wanted to have a private chat with Zu An, but the latter had gone on to lecture for three straight days in the academy. She couldn’t find an opportunity to approach him. 

She'd excitedly rushed over here to look for Zu An when he finally concluded his lecture, but when she entered, the latter was nowhere to be found. Instead, there was a beautiful woman. 

She'd thought that Zu An was hiding a lover, but at second glance, she realized that the woman was Yan Xuehen and was taken aback.

She knew Yan Xuehen was Chu Chuyan’s master, and she was aware of Yan Xuehen’s resounding reputation in the Dao Gate and among the orthodox sects. However, her impression of Yan Xuehen shattered when she bumped into her getting steamy with Zu An.

“Sect Master Yan?” Thinking it would be inappropriate to just turn around and leave, Pei Mianman tried calling out to her. Is she here for a secret liaison with Ah Zu? But he isn’t here. 

But the other party didn’t respond, leaving Pei Mianman perplexed. With her cultivation, even if she was asleep, she should have awakened when I called out to her. 

Taking a closer look, she noticed Yan Xuehen’s snowy skin had turned red, and her breathing was ragged. She seemed to be moaning in pain.

“Is she having a cultivation deviation?” Pei Mianman was taken aback. Be it for Chu Chuyan or Zu An, she couldn’t turn a blind eye to this. 

She thought about treating Yan Xuehen to stabilize her condition, but she was worried that her intervention could worsen the situation instead. This made her panic. 

Yan Xuehen was panicking too. She could vaguely hear sounds, and that made her pale in horror. “Ah Zu, I think there’s someone next to my body.”

Zu An smiled. “Don’t worry. You’re fine.”

He already knew that it was Pei Mianman, which was why he hadn’t activated the formations. 

But Yan Xuehen was still worried. “There really is someone there! It seems to be Miss Pei.”

Knowing he couldn’t hide it anymore, Zu An projected the situation inside the room with a wave of his hand. “See, you aren’t in danger. She won’t hurt you.”

Yan Xuehen nearly fainted. She was already embarrassed about doing it with Zu An, and it definitely didn’t help that a junior was looking at her! Her soul was thousands of kilometers away, but the soul and the body were intricately linked. 

If Pei Mianman figures out what’s going on, I should just smash my head into a block of tofu!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2671: Exposed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2671: Exposed

Zu An was startled when he sensed the anomaly in her body. He had no choice but to give in. “All right, let’s rush back.”

Yan Xuehen angrily bit him. “I’ll never forgive you if she finds out about us!”

Zu An looked at her. “She's already seen us together.”

“You still dare to speak!” Yan Xuehen felt as if she would lose her mind. This fellow sounds so gleeful about that!

“Relax. She won’t notice anything amiss,” Zu An said with a smile. All of a sudden, however, his expression stiffened. Why is she here?

A beautiful figure flitted across the moonlit sky. The academy’s defensive formations didn’t affect her in the slightest. It didn’t take her long to arrive at Mount Yuquan’s peak. 

The formations Zu An had constructed around the courtyard were about to automatically attack her when she harrumphed. “Is this how you treat a guest?”

Sweat rained from Zu An’s forehead. He quickly halted his formations remotely.

The beautiful figure nodded in satisfaction. “That’s more like it.” She walked into the courtyard.

Sensing something amiss, Yan Xuehen hurriedly asked, “Who is it?” 

Zu An had no idea how to explain, so he said, “Take a look for yourself.”

He projected the situation in the courtyard into the air, revealing a long-haired, red-robed beauty.
