Yan Xuehen was speechless. She furiously pummeled Zu An’s body. “Return right now! That demoness will figure it out if she sees it!” 

The red-robed beauty was Sect Master Yun Jianyue. Unlike Pei Mianman, Yun Jianyue was a master of seductive arts. With her wealth of experience, she could figure out what was going on at a glance. 

The two had been archenemies for many years now. The thought of Yun Jianyue learning about her relationship with Zu An sent shivers down Yan Xuehen’s spine. 

Zu An laughed. “Don’t worry. No one's thoughts will go in that direction. Besides, Manman will stall her.”

Yun Jianyue entered the courtyard.

A vigilant voice echoed from within. “Who is it?”

Pei Mianman rushed out, only to be taken aback when she saw the red-robed beauty. “Sect Master Yun!”

Yun Jianyue hadn’t expected someone else to be in the courtyard. Her face heated up. Good thing I didn’t say anything intimate earlier. I’d die of embarrassment if others knew about it. “Miss Pei.”

Looking at the long-haired silhouette under the moonlight, Pei Mianman had to concede that Yun Jianyue was truly gorgeous, especially with her imposing disposition as the master of the Devil Sect. That sparked her vigilance. Why would she visit Ah Zu in the middle of the night? There’s no way that lustful Ah Zu can resist a beauty of her caliber!

In the past, she would have never thought of that, but what happened with Yan Xuehen made her realize that everything was possible. She asked, “What brings Sect Master Yun here?”

Yun Jianyue had met Pei Mianman before, but each time they met, she couldn’t help but wonder what the latter had eaten to grow so big. She had an impressive figure too, but it was lacking compared to this young lady.  

She keenly sensed animosity in Pei Mianman’s words. I won’t stoop to getting into a catfight with my disciple’s friend. The only one who's a match for me is that stone cold woman… but there’s no way that woman would have anything with Ah Zu. 

She had considered matchmaking Zu An and Yan Xuehen so she wouldn’t be the only one to die of embarrassment if her relationship with Zu An was exposed. Unfortunately, she hadn’t had a chance to do so thus far. 

“I heard Zu An had returned, so I came here to ask about Honglei.”

Pei Mianman secretly breathed a sigh of relief. “Don’t worry, Sect Master Yun. Ah Zu has managed to save Honglei—I met her a few times myself. She’s still in a feeble state, as the immortal medicine isn’t enough to heal her impaired soul. She needs plenty of rest to recover, but she should be almost well now.”

Yun Jianyue was overjoyed. “That’s wonderful news. I’d like to meet her. Where’s Zu An? It’s been a while since I arrived, so why is he not here?”

“Ah Zu isn’t here.” Pei Mianman was perplexed about that as well. 

“He isn’t here?” Yun Jianyue was confused. She sensed another presence inside the courtyard, but there were too many defensive formations here to tell who it was. 

She'd thought that Pei Mianman had been messing around with Zu An, and had come out here to drive her off. She could understand why Pei Mianman would do that, but she couldn’t accept Zu An implicitly allowing it to happen. 

Her anger surged. “Regent, don’t you think it’s inappropriate for you to remain hidden when a friend has come to visit?” 

Zu An sighed. She must be truly angry to address me as ‘regent’. 

Yan Xuehen was perplexed. Why is Yun Jianyue angry?

“Sect Master Yun, you've misunderstood. Ah Zu isn’t inside…” Pei Mianman explained. 

The lack of response from Zu An further stoked Yun Jianyue’s anger. She thrust her palm, destroying the windows by the side. “Putting on airs with me? Regent…”

She froze before she could finish speaking. The person in the room wasn't Zu An, but someone else beyond her wildest imaginations. “Ice sculpture, what are you doing here?”

But Yan Xuehen didn’t respond. 

Pei Mianman walked up to her with a frown. “Sect Master Yun, do you now believe me when I say that Ah Zu isn’t here?”

“Where is he? And why is this woman here?” Yun Jianyue’s head was spinning. 

“I don’t know either. I just arrived.” Pei Mianman was worried. “I tried calling out to Sect Master Yan, but she hasn't responded. Now and then, her face turns red and her breathing becomes ragged, as if she’s suffering cultivation deviation.”

Yan Xuehen’s reputation as an ice-cold beauty was so striking that it was unthinkable for her face to turn red. 

“Her face turns red?” Yun Jianyue narrowed her eyes in doubt. She walked over to inspect Yan Xuehen’s condition. 

Yan Xuehen trembled. Her mind turned blank. “I’ll kill myself if she finds out about it!”

She looked so serious that Zu An didn’t dare continue teasing her. With a flash of rainbow light, they returned to Mount Yuquan.

Yan Xuehen returned her soul to her body. She tried to stand up and walk away, but her legs caved in and she fell to the side. Fortunately, Pei Mianman quickly grabbed her arm to support her. “What’s wrong, Sect Master Yan?”

Yan Xuehen's face was burning red. She dared not reply, fearing that her voice would sound too coy and give her away. 

Yun Jianyue’s gaze alternated between her and Zu An. Looking at Yan Xuehen’s perplexing behavior, a thought popped into her mind. “What were the two of you… doing?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2672: Different Intentions
chapters.body:
Chapter 2672: Different Intentions

Yan Xuehen was scared out of her wits. Having dedicated her life to fighting Yun Jianyue, she would be lucky if she got away with a tattered reputation after stealing her disciple’s man.

Zu An spoke up and said, “I took Sect Master Yan’s soul to the Netherworld to visit Chuyan.”

“Soul projection?” Pei Mianman widened her eyes in realization.

“Can a living person’s soul enter the Netherworld?” Yun Jianyue asked in astonishment. 

“It shouldn’t have been possible under normal circumstances, but with my current strength, I can bring a person in, though it’s only limited to the soul,” Zu An replied gleefully. He had to be the only human in the world capable of this feat. 

“That’s wonderful! I want to go to the Yellow Spring Road to examine those Red Spider Lilies.” Pei Mianman was overjoyed. She hadn’t been able to do much with her Paramita Butterfly. Taking a look at the Red Spider Lilies could help her reap some benefits. She turned to Yan Xuehen and asked, “Sect Master Yan, did you see the Red Spider Lilies? Are they beautiful?”

Yan Xuehen had enough time to regain her composure. “Yes, they are unforgettably beautiful.”

Pei Mianman’s eyes twinkled in excitement. She was already imagining how romantic it would be for her to stroll through a field of Red Spider Lilies with her lover. 

But Yun Jianyue wasn’t that easily fooled. She eyed the two of them and asked, “Are you sure that’s all?”

Zu An replied with a smile, “What else could there be?”

Yun Jianyue pushed on with narrowed eyes, “A person’s senses are heightened in soul form. Did you do anything impolite to the ice sculpture?”

Before Zu An could reply, Yan Xuehen harrumphed. “What do you mean by that, demoness?” She was nowhere as confident as she appeared, but she knew it would appear more suspicious if she didn’t fight back when her archenemy was slandering her. 

Zu An secretly shot Yan Xuehen a thumbs-up. Who could have thought the cold Yan Xuehen would be such a wonderful actress? Is acting second nature to all women?

Yun Jianyue burst into laughter. “What else could I mean? I don’t believe you two didn’t get intimate while he was escorting you to the Netherworld.”

Yan Xuehen replied with a cold face, “Indeed, but we have nothing to hide.”

There was no way to hide this matter. The other women would know right away when Zu An took them to the Netherworld. It would be unwise to lie about this matter. Please forgive me, founder of daoism…

For some reason, she had been lying more than ever after meeting Zu An. 

Zu An was worried that Yan Xuehen couldn’t cope with the situation alone, so he helped to explain, “She needed to remain within a meter of me in the Netherworld, so some physical contact was inevitable. I’d never disrespect Chuyan’s master.”

That single line made the three women present blush. 

Yan Xuehen thought about how she had been thoroughly taken advantage of. 

Yun Jianyue felt conscience-stricken. She was Qiu Honglei’s master, after all. Hmph, this won’t do. I must make sure that stone cold woman suffers the same plight before our relationship is revealed, or else I’ll become a laughingstock.

Pei Mianman alternated her gaze between Zu An and Yan Xuehen. She knew that the two of them had a thing, so she decided to quietly watch their performance. I’m not surprised by Ah Zu’s acting skills, but Yan Xuehen usually appears so dignified and upright. I didn’t expect her to be such a good liar too. 

Yan Xuehen was starting to feel uncomfortable. She quickly replied, “Regent, thank you for bringing me to meet Chuyan. I should return to my seclusion now. I shan’t impose on you any further.”

She turned around and left.

Zu An panicked. He couldn’t bear for her to leave so quickly. 

However, Yun Jianyue moved even faster. “Ice sculpture, we have known each other for many years now. Isn’t it impolite of you to leave just like that? You should stay a few more days.”

Yan Xuehen’s face reddened. More like being played with for a few days if I choose to stay. But she refused to give in. “I have nothing to say to you. Or are you looking for a fight?”

“Sure. I’ve been wanting to challenge you to a fight—my cultivation has advanced a fair bit recently. You won’t chicken out, will you?” Yun Jianyue sneered.

Yan Xuehen couldn’t stop herself despite knowing it was a taunt. “Hah, of course not.”

“Then let’s fight right now.” Yun Jianyue rolled up her sleeves.

Pei Mianman secretly laughed. The leader of the Devil Sect and the leader of the orthodox sects, yet they're acting like a pair of countryside aunties. 

Yan Xuehen looked away. “Not now. My soul is still affected by the Netherworld’s death energy. I’ll need some time to recuperate.”

Her legs were still trembling from the earlier ordeal. She wouldn’t be a match for the demoness in her current state. 

“Fine, I shan’t take advantage of your weakness. I’ll wait a few days for you here till you’re ready to challenge me,” Yun Jianyue replied. 

Zu An also interjected, “Sect Master Yan, you should stay here a couple days more. There are many things regarding Chuyan and the universal deities that I need to discuss with you.”

Yan Xuehen snorted in her mind. What an obvious lie. In truth, she couldn’t bear to leave either, so she used her duel with Yun Jianyue as an excuse to stay.

Zu An settled her down in the neighboring room. This was the old libationer’s residence. Out of respect for the old libationer, there were no other residences in the vicinity other than his. 

Yan Xuehen didn’t want to interact with anyone else in the academy. She figured that Ah Zu wouldn’t mess around when there were other people in the courtyard, so she agreed to it. She quickly found an excuse to take her leave and rest in her room. 

I feel as if my body is in tatters even though it was just a union of souls… 

Yun Jianyue looked at Yan Xuehen’s fleeing silhouette and frowned. “Something feels off about the ice sculpture.”

Pei Mianman, who had guessed the truth, looked at Zu An and snorted. 

Feeling conscience-stricken, Zu An asked Yun Jianyue about the reason behind her visit.

“What else? I’m here to look for my darling disciple. Do you think I’d be here for you?” Yun Jianyue couldn’t reveal their intimate relationship as Pei Mianman was around too. 

Zu An smiled sheepishly. “I just visited Honglei not long ago. She’s on the verge of a full recovery.”

“I can’t rest at ease until I see her in person.” Yun Jianyue was nervous. Qiu Honglei had left home and met with a mishap after witnessing her affair with Zu An. This matter had been weighing on her mind. 

Zu An nodded. He understood how she felt, so he took off his pendant. A cave paradise soon manifested in the courtyard. He brought the two women inside. 

A beautiful figure rushed out from the place and leaped into Zu An’s arms. “You heartless thing. You haven’t visited us for a long time now. Did your feelings change?” She pummeled him as she spoke.

Zu An felt a headache settling in. That tone… It can only be Black Jing Teng. 

When they saw the beautiful lady dressed in black lace acting coyly around Zu An, Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman’s faces darkened. 

You have successfully trolled Yun Jianyue for +66… +66… +66…

You have successfully trolled Pei Mianman for +66… +66… +66…

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2673: Laying It Out
chapters.body:
Chapter 2673: Laying It Out

Black Jing Teng noticed the other two women present and pouted in displeasure, but she quickly changed her expression and coyly nuzzled against Zu An’s chest. “Ah Zu, why don’t you bring them in to play together? I don’t mind it.”

Yun Jianyue was speechless. What do you mean by play?

Pei Mianman was startled. Of all people, Miss Jing has to crack a joke with Sect Master Yun. That’s a bona fide demoness there!

But she quickly remembered that Jing Teng was a sealed demoness herself and chuckled under her breath.

Zu An felt a headache setting in. “Stop messing around. I brought them here to visit Honglei.”

Black Jing Teng huffed. “You still dare to speak about that! Our agreement is for you to occasionally drop by and nourish us if we help you take care of her. We fulfilled our end of the bargain and even gave our house to her, yet you hardly visited us at all.”

Yun Jianyue snorted at Zu An. “Take your time nourishing her. I’ll check on Honglei.”

You have successfully trolled Yun Jianyue for +77… +77… +77…

Pei Mianman rolled her eyes too. “I’ll check on Honglei too.”

You have successfully trolled Pei Mianman for +77… +77… +77…

While Zu An was feeling stressed out, Black Jing Teng visibly turned white. 

“Little sister, you’re kicking up a fuss again.” White Jing Teng’s disposition was conspicuously different. She was elegant and dignified, completely unlike the eccentric woman from earlier.

Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman instinctively halted their footsteps. They knew that the two sisters were sharing a body, but it was still shocking to see it. 

White Jing Teng directed a nod at the two women. “Pardon us for earlier. She’s still young and immature. I hope you won’t hold it against her.”

Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman were rendered speechless. Aren’t you the same age as your little sister? And the two of you are old enough to be our ancestors!

But it didn’t seem appropriate to hold a grudge when White Jing Teng was earnestly apologizing to them, so they replied, “Of course not, Miss Jing.”

Black Jing Teng angrily protested inside, “What do you mean, immature? Those two women are our love rivals! Why are you being so polite to them? We should reveal Yun Jianyue’s affair with Ah Zu and have those two cows fight it out, so we can sit back and reap the benefits.”

They had noticed the intimate interactions between Zu And and Yun Jianyue the last time the latter came to visit Qiu Honglei. It wasn't hard to guess their relationship from that. 

White Jing Teng suppressed her little sister’s urge to drop the explosive news, and instead gracefully smiled at the two women. “Come, let me take you to Miss Qiu.”

At the same time, she reasoned with her little sister, “That’s too shallow. They'd soon realize that we were driving a wedge between them. Have you considered how Ah Zu would view us?”

Black Jing Teng was dissatisfied. “Are you going to watch as Ah Zu flirts with them?”

“Why resort to despicable means when we can just watch it play out?” White Jing Teng replied with a mysterious smile. “This is a hidden card that only we know about. We should hold onto it for now.”

Black Jing Teng was taken aback. “Big sister, you’re more evil than me.”

… 

Jing Teng led the way. Looking at her graceful gait, Pei Mianman murmured, “That waist and that bum… If I were a man, I wouldn’t be able to stand it.”

Yun Jianyue was worried too. Ah Zu has so many top-notch beauties around him. How can Honglei fend against them alone? Should I lend her a helping hand?

Her face heated up as soon as that thought popped into her mind, and she cursed at herself. Jianyue, when have you become as hypocritical as that stone cold woman? Do you truly intend to help Honglei?

The door to the distant wooden cottage creaked open, and a beautiful figure walked out. “Big sister Jing, Ah Zu…”

Qiu Honglei had been resting when she heard voices outside, so she came out to take a look. Halfway through her greetings, she noticed Yun Jianyue and froze up. “Master…”

Her heart was plunged into turmoil. She didn’t know how to face Yun Jianyue. Should I call her master or big sister?

She had always respected her master, and her master took good care of her too. But whenever she recalled the sight of her master and Zu An getting together above the Lake of Hatred, her heart still hurt.

Pei Mianman was unaware of the tension between the master and disciple. She rushed forward to support Qiu Honglei. “Honglei, you've gotten skinnier. It feels as if you'd topple over in a stiff breeze. Was the food I brought you not to your taste?”

Pei Mianman had accompanied Zu An several times when he visited Qiu Honglei. The two of them knew each other from their time at Brightmoon City. There had been conflicts between them then, but after everything they had been through, those had been long forgotten. 

“Manman~” Qiu Honglei grabbed Pei Mianman’s hand and smiled. “I haven’t had much of an appetite recently.”

They had quickly grown closer due to the frequent visits.

“That’s how it is when recovering from an illness. Try going for some bland food first and rest well,” Zu An said. 

Qiu Honglei nodded. She knew her master was here too, but she didn’t know how to face her. 

Pei Mianman didn’t notice the anomaly between them. “Honglei, Sect Master Yun is here to visit you. She's been here before, but you were still unconscious then.”

Qiu Honglei’s heart quivered. In the end, she called out, “Master~”

She couldn’t talk about her master’s relationship with Ah Zu here, as there were others around. 

“Honglei!” Unable to hold herself back anymore, Yun Jianyue embraced Qiu Honglei. “I’m sorry for putting you through so much misery.”

The guilt she had been suppressing for many years poured out, and tears streamed down her cheeks. She believed that if not for her affair with Zu An, her disciple wouldn’t have left the Devil Sect and met with a mishap. 

Feeling the moisture on her shoulders, Qiu Honglei remembered the care and concern her master had shown her. Had things gone awry, they might have never met again. That caused her to burst into tears as well. “Master…”

Seeing this, Pei Mianman turned to Zu An and said, “We should give them some space.”

As he knew about the situation between them, Zu An was hesitant.

White Jing Teng nodded too. “It has been a long time since the two of them met. They must have a lot to say to each other. We shouldn’t disturb them.” She took the lead by walking away.

Zu An could only follow suit. He wished Yun Jianyue good luck before leaving as well. 

Yun Jianyue felt nervous. She had never been so nervous, not even when she was faced with mountains of corpses and seas of blood, or even Zhao Han. She'd never thought she would be afraid to face her disciple. 

The door closed, and the room plunged into silence. The crying master and disciple stepped away from each other and straightened their postures. 

It was Yun Jianyue who first broke the silence. “Honglei, I've been worried about you. It’s a relief that Ah Zu found the immortal medicine and saved you.”

“Ah Zu is indeed capable,” Qiu Honglei muttered. “How else could he have won your heart?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2674: Battle Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2674: Battle Plan

Yun Jianyue’s expression stiffened. She bitterly said, “I’m sorry, Honglei.” 

Seeing her proud master apologizing in such low spirits softened Qiu Honglei’s heart. “Master, can you construct a formation to prevent sounds from getting out?”

Yun Jianyue knew that Qiu Honglei was going to lay things out. She was flustered, but she didn’t turn down the request. “Alright.”

She constructed a barrier. “They can’t listen to our conversation anymore.” Though I won’t be able to stop Ah Zu if he wants to eavesdrop.

Qiu Honglei looked at her. “Master, how did you… get together with Ah Zu?” That was what she was most curious about. Her master wasn’t the type to stoop to stealing her disciple’s man. 

“I sustained severe injuries during Lu Sanyuan’s rebellion. To save me, Ah Zu…” Yun Jianyue began.

Qiu Honglei interjected before Yun Jianyue could finish speaking. “I know about that. He did it to save you, and I don’t blame either of you for that. But that wasn’t your first time, right? You know what I’m getting at.”

Yun Jianyue’s face heated up. She had imagined this situation countless times and thought about how to deal with it, but now that she was faced with it, she found that her mind went blank instead. In the end, she sighed and replied, “It started with a misunderstanding…”

She explained what had happened after Zu An absorbed Sir Fu’s ki.

Qiu Honglei’s face darkened. “Ah Zu forced himself on you?”

Yun Jianyue shook her head. “He was suffering from an inner demon from his breakthrough to essence form stage, but I couldn’t have helped him if I wasn’t willing too.”

Qiu Honglei’s heart swelled with jealousy upon learning the full story. “You were already together that early on. I was the third party…” Even though I came first… 

Yun Jianyue anxiously exclaimed, “That’s not it, Honglei. I got in between your feelings. I'm the despicable one.”

“Master, don’t put yourself down. It’s beyond humans to control their feelings.” Qiu Honglei sighed. “It’s all Ah Zu’s fault for being a philanderer.”

Yun Jianyue looked at her worriedly. “Honglei, you can punish me however you wish to, but you’re currently in a feeble state. You shouldn’t bottle up your feelings. You’ll only hurt yourself.”

Qiu Honglei smiled. “Master, do you know what I’m the angriest about?”

Yun Jianyue bitterly replied, “I failed to uphold my duties as your master and stole your man. Anyone would be angry about that.”

“That’s not it,” Qiu Honglei replied, much to Yun Jianyue’s bewilderment. “I’m angry that you chose to keep it from me.”

Yun Jianyue was speechless.

“It’s one thing for Ah Zu to keep it from me, but master, why did you keep it from me too?” Qiu Honglei’s eyes glimmered, making it impossible to tell how she was feeling. 

“How could I talk about that?” Yun Jianyue was frustrated as well. “I wanted to shrug that incident off as a beautiful mistake and erase Ah Zu from my life, but things happened afterward…” 

Qiu Honglei sighed. “Ah Zu is indeed an unforgettable man, not to mention he’s shameless and tenacious. He wouldn’t give up on a beauty like you.”

“You know him well.” Thinking about how the culprit behind this mess had escaped scot-free made Yun Jianyue grit her teeth in anger.

You have successfully trolled Yun Jianyue for +33… +33… +33…

“Master, it’s one thing if this happened to Chuyan and Sect Master Yan, but I’m disappointed that you were so indecisive and even chose to hide it from me.” Qiu Honglei sounded aggrieved. “We are the Devil Sect’s demonesses, practitioners of the Bewitching Dance! We should be free from the shackles of social convention. We've seen plenty of such things from the sisters around us. Do you think I’d be fazed by your relationship with Ah Zu?”

“I have disappointed…” Yun Jianyue noticed something amiss halfway through her sentence. “Ah?”

Qiu Honglei bit her lips. “I mean it, master. I would have been jealous and angry if I were Ah Zu’s only woman, but he’s a philanderer! He has countless lovers, and they're all beautiful and outstanding women. I can’t deal with them alone. We could have worked together if you had told me earlier. The other women out there wouldn’t have stood a chance against us!”

Yun Jianyue blinked. She tried touching Qiu Honglei’s forehead. “Are you serious?”

“Of course!” Qiu Honglei exclaimed. “Such things are common in our Devil Sect! There are also similar instances between fellow disciples, blood-related sisters, and even… You're the master of the Devil Sect, so why are you following the hypocritical conventions of the orthodox sects?”

Yun Jianyue awkwardly replied, “But it’s a different story. Our sect members do those things to attain greater power or to vent their lust, whereas we…”

“True love, huh?” Qiu Honglei interjected. 

Yun Jianyue couldn’t take it anymore. “Fine, you’re even teasing me now! Don’t you feel the same toward Ah Zu too?”

“Of course I do,” Qiu Honglei replied with tender eyes. “I believe Ah Zu feels the same about us too. So, we can’t let Ah Zu do worse than those scum in our sect. Good people deserve to be bullied.”

Her twisted logic left Yun Jianyue’s head reeling. “Honglei, you have a novel perspective.”

Qiu Honglei crossed her arms. “Master, Ah Zu is always looking to fool around. If we were going to have another sister, do you think I’d prefer it to be a stranger or you?”

Yun Jianyue’s heartbeat hastened. “Honglei, do you really not mind?”

“What I mind is you not telling me the truth. We could have stopped other vixens from slipping in through the cracks.” Qiu Honglei lay in her master’s arms with a tear flowing down her cheek. 

How could she not mind? But the other party was her master. Yun Jianyue had taken good care of her, doted on her, taught her how to cultivate, and provided her with a safe shelter.

While disciples of the Devil Sect were indignant that the orthodox sects labeled them as demonic, deep down, they knew that it was a fitting label. The Devil Sect believed in the survival of the fittest, and the ends justified the means. With Qiu Honglei's beauty, were it not for her master’s protection, some elder in the Devil Sect would have long used her as a cauldron for nourishment.

For the longest time, her master was her sky. It was home to her wherever her master was. Having been orphaned at a young age, Yun Jianyue was more than a master to her. She didn’t want her master to be burdened by guilt for the rest of her life and perhaps even leave an inner demon behind. Their ties couldn’t be allowed to fracture over a man. 

Although, she meant what she had just said too. Zu An was indeed a terrible philanderer. Rather than benefiting outsiders, she would rather let her master in on the pie. 

Yun Jianyue tightly embraced her disciple. Having raised Qiu Honglei, she could see through the latter’s intentions. She murmured under her breath, “Thank you, Honglei.”

All of a sudden, Qiu Honglei frowned. “Oh no!”

Yun Jianyue was stunned. “What’s wrong?”

“We can’t let others know about this, especially not Sect Master Yan and Chuyan. Otherwise, we’ll never be able to lift our heads in front of them.”

Yun Jianyue had fought with Yan Xuehen all their lives, whereas Qiu Honglei and Chu Chuyan were rivals, as they were both the Holy Maidens pushed forth by their respective sects. 

“Don’t worry, I’ll keep my distance from Ah Zu to keep them from learning about it,” Yun Jianyue replied. 

Qiu Honglei rolled her eyes. “Master, do you think I’m trying to pressure you using the same hypocritical tactics that those orthodox sects? It goes against our Devil Sect’s style to retreat in the face of a challenge. We should be proactively making a move instead!”

Yun Jianyue’s heart stirred. “You mean…?”

Qiu Honglei’s eyes twinkled. “We’ll drag Sect Master Yan down too. Let’s see who mocks whom then. We're the Devil Sect’s demonesses; such things are no big deal to us. They, on the other hand, pride themselves on their principles. They're the ones who will be shamed!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2675: Master And Disciple United
chapters.body:
Chapter 2675: Master And Disciple United

Yun Jianyue’s eyes lit up. “As expected of the disciple I raised. You had the same thought as me!” 

Qiu Honglei was surprised. “You also thought of that, master?”

Yun Jianyue nodded awkwardly. “I couldn’t stand the thought of being mocked by that ice sculpture for the rest of my life, so I thought I’d make her like me. Neither of us can mock the other then. I didn’t think that deeply into it, but I guess it’d indeed be more embarrassing for them.”

Qiu Honglei was enthused. “Master, how’s your progress?”

Yun Jianyue frowned. “My gut feeling tells me something is off about that ice sculpture and Ah Zu, but I can’t put my finger on it.”

“Tell me about it. What feels off?” Qiu Honglei asked with widened eyes. 

Yun Jianyue shared the earlier incident, especially how unnatural Yan Xuehen looked when she returned to her body. “Their explanation made sense, but something seems to be off.”

Qiu Honglei nodded in agreement. “Indeed. How can Ah Zu not be tempted by someone as beautiful as Sect Master Yan? I didn’t think about it back then, but if he dares to touch even you, master, what else would he dare not do?”

Yun Jianyue’s eyebrows shot up. “You lass! You’re making fun of me!”

Qiu Honglei hugged her arm and coyly replied, “Master, I’m just stating facts here. Don’t take it to heart.”

Yun Jianyue continued, “Ah Zu has always been fearless and brazen. It is indeed likely for him to have made a move on that ice sculpture. But I've known that ice sculpture for many years. She isn’t the type who would get involved with her disciple’s man…”

Qiu Honglei grudgingly murmured, “Master, are you conceding that you’re inferior to Sect Master Yan in that aspect?”

Yun Jianyue harrumphed. “You said it yourself. We’re the Devil Sect’s demonesses. It’s more like us to be daring in that regard. How can you say I’m inferior to her?”

“Master, you've finally regained your usual flair.” Qiu Honglei chuckled as she leaned against Yun Jianyue and enjoyed the warmth they shared. This isn’t that bad either. I can be with master forever, and we can work together to deal with love rivals too.

“Honglei, what should we do to make that ice sculpture accept Ah Zu?” Yun Jianyue was vexed. She'd harbored that intention for a long time now, but she hadn’t found a way to achieve that. 

“Shall we work together to knock out Sect Master Yan, strip her bare, and put her on Ah Zu’s bed? I don’t believe Ah Zu can hold himself back.” Qiu Honglei’s eyes twinkled. 

Yun Jianyue looked at her disciple with a frown. She’s even more extreme than me. 

“That won’t work. That ice sculpture is no weaker than me. If we work together, we can defeat her easily, but capturing her is a different story. Besides, I doubt Ah Zu will fall for it. He is lustful, but he isn’t the type to take advantage of others in their moment of weakness, not to mention that ice sculpture is Chu Chuyan’s master.”

Qiu Honglei nodded. Ah Zu is indeed not that kind of person. 

“Should we injure Ah Zu and have Sect Master Yan offer herself to save him, just like what you did back then?” Qiu Honglei proposed. 

Yun Jianyue gritted her teeth. She’s doing this on purpose, isn’t she? “Ah Zu is now so powerful. How can we injure him?”

“That’s true.” Qiu Honglei nodded. “Since there’s nothing we can do about Ah Zu, should we try drugging Sect Master Yan instead? Our Devil Sect’s Yin Yang Path has some potent medicine. Even you nearly fell for it back then, master. You should have some of it on you, right?”

Yun Jianyue was speechless.

Fang Zhonghe from the Devil Sect’s Yin Yang Path was fond of concocting such medicine, and she did have some on hand. However, she still turned down the tempting proposal. “That won’t do. That ice sculpture practices the Unshakable Daoist Manual; the medicine might not work on her. Even if it works, Ah Zu can heal her. We have to find a way to make her do it willingly. Otherwise, she’ll claim we schemed against her, and it won’t be fun to mock her after.”

Qiu Honglei giggled. “Master, I have to say you’re wrong. Considering Sect Master Yan’s personality, there’s no easy way around this, so we’ll have to do it step by step. Ah Zu can cure her poison, but that will require physical contact. Sparks are bound to arise from that. Even if they observe etiquette, their relationship won’t be as pure as it used to be.”

Yun Jianyue’s eyes lit up. “Your suggestion makes sense. Why didn’t I think about that?”

Qiu Honglei nuzzled against Yun Jianyue with an excited grin. “Master, when are we doing it?”

“You focus on your recuperation. Leave this matter to me,” Yun Jianyue replied. She already had an idea in mind. 

When Yun Jianyue left the room, Zu An walked over and whispered, “Big sister Yun, how is it?”

Yun Jianyue knew what he was getting at, so she let out a sigh and said, “Honglei is still angry at me.”

Zu An gently comforted her. “It can’t be helped. You’re more than a master to her, and such a thing happened. Don’t worry too much. She just needs time for her feelings to settle down.” 

Yun Jianyue was secretly delighted. She was probing Zu An to see if he had eavesdropped on their conversation, and it seemed the latter hadn’t. This made things easier for her. She said, “I’m thinking of staying here to accompany Honglei. Is that fine with you, regent?”

Zu An was overjoyed to hear that news. “Of course.”

Yun Jianyue’s expression suddenly turned serious. “Don’t let others know about our relationship, lest it affect Honglei.” Especially not Yan Xuehen.

“That goes without saying,” Zu An replied. “There aren’t many rooms in the courtyard. I’ll have to place you next to Sect Master Yan.”

“That would be best.”

“???”

“I mean, we are already familiar with each other.”

“You aren’t planning to fight and tear the courtyard down, are you?”

“Don’t worry, I won’t go that far.”

… 

Black JIng Teng was observing the two of them from afar. They sure know how to act.

Zu An noticed how she had looked at the two ladies. He'd been planning to head out to settle Yun Jianyue into her room, but it wouldn’t be appropriate for him to just leave right now, as he hadn’t visited Jing Teng in a while. So, he decided to gather them together in the room and share what happened with them. 

Qiu Honglei saw her master returning and initially thought their secret ploy had been exposed. She only breathed a sigh of relief afterward, upon learning that wasn’t the case. 

“Shouldn’t we call the ice sculpture here to listen too?” Yun Jianyue asked. 

Zu An shook his head. “I’ve already shared it with her on our way to the Netherworld.” I doubt the shy Yan Xuehen is in the mood to meet anyone after what happened earlier. 

“You must have chatted a lot on the way,” Yun Jianyue replied with a tinge of jealousy. 

Black Jing Teng asked, “Big sister, could there be something between Ah Zu and that Yan woman?” 

White Jing Teng telepathically replied with a graceful smile, “It doesn’t matter. We’ll just watch the show.”

Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei were initially distracted, as they were busy considering how to scheme against Yan Xuehen, but their attention was soon drawn to Zu An’s story. Even though he had returned safe and sound, their hearts still thumped anxiously when they heard about his close shaves. In particular, they were shocked when they learned about universal deities. 

Yun Jianyue remarked, “Actually, I sensed a powerful will summoning me a while back. I don’t know if it was one of the universal deities you talked about.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2676: A New Universal Deity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2676: A New Universal Deity

“What?” Zu An was taken aback. Yan Xuehen had previously received an audience with a universal deity and was on her way to forgetting everything. Who knew what kind of side effects Yun Jianyue would suffer? “Which universal deity did you encounter?”

He figured he could look after her if it was Mi Li, but on second thought, Yun Jianyue’s dao was nothing like Mi Li’s. The chances were unlikely. 

Yun Jianyue’s face reddened. She began to stutter.

“Master, there are no outsiders here. Who is it?” Qiu Honglei said. The other two ladies here were fellow ‘sisters’, so there was no need to hide things from them. 

Yun Jianyue hesitantly replied, “I didn’t see the universal deity; I merely sensed his presence. He seemed to be the Deity of Desires. I might have caught his gaze due to the special nature of my cultivation skill.”

The others exchanged glances. No wonder she’s embarrassed. It is indeed… embarrassing. 

The awkward looks made Yun Jianyue more self-conscious. “Don’t misunderstand. There are more desires than just lust, such as greed, jealousy, and gluttony. They're all different types of desires…”

The others played along with her and nodded.

Yun Jianyue finally caved in and exclaimed, “Fine! I caught his gaze because of what you’re thinking. I’m the first person in the Holy Sect’s history to master the Voice of the Devil, after all.”

It was hard to tell who succumbed and laughed first, but the others began trembling uncontrollably too. 

Zu An tried his best to suppress his laughter as he asked worriedly, “Has there been anything different since? Have you gained any new abilities, and do those abilities come with side effects?”

“I have indeed gained a new ability.” Yun Jianyue bit her lips. “I can stir a person’s lust much more easily now.”

Qiu Honglei was stunned. Hold up, that makes it easier for us to pull off our plan! To think I was worried about whether the Yin Yang Path’s medicine would work on Sect Master Yan!

“Really?” Jing Teng was intrigued. “How does it compare to Ah Zu’s special ki?”

The mention of that ki made all the women present blush. All of them had been victims of that. 

“It’s compa…” Yun Jianyue was about to answer that it was comparable when it struck her. “Ah Zu’s special ki? What’s that?” I almost let it slip. I am Honglei’s master and Ah Zu’s elder. I shouldn’t know what effects his special ki has.

Qiu Honglei quickly added, “Ah Zu’s ki is rather unique. It’s normal that you don’t know about it, master.”

Jing Teng narrowed her eyes. Interesting. They've already patched things up?

Black Jing Teng scoffed, “That master and disciple duo are so perverted. We were at odds for so long when we fell in love with Ah Zu at the same time.”

White Jing Teng sneered, “Is it? I thought you were primed to snatch whatever good things I have.”

Black Jing Teng was indignant. “Pui! I was the first one to go after him!”

While the two sisters were arguing, Pei Mianman asked, “Does it work on both sexes?”

Yun Jianyue’s face reddened. “It does…”

Qiu Honglei suggested, “Shall we give it a try?”

“On whom?” Yun Jianyue asked.

Ah Zu is out of the question. His ox-like stamina is no joke. What if he runs amok? 

Honglei isn’t an option either. She’s still recovering. 

Jing Teng is a senior, and for some reason, I’m a bit intimidated by her. 

Thus, she turned her gaze to Pei Mianman. 

When she realized that everyone was looking at her, Pei Mianman blushed. “Hold on. Why are you looking at me?” 

Yun Jianyue hugged Pei Mianman. “Manman, why don’t you give it a try? Ah Zu is here anyway. He can help you if anything happens.”

Pei Mianman’s face was so red that it looked as if one could squeeze blood from it. This was too embarrassing!

Qiu Honglei blinked in confusion. “Master, when did you become so close to Manman?”

Their closeness didn’t seem feigned, but one was the Devil Sect’s master whereas the other was a second-generation disciple of the Dao Gate. That was quite an uncanny combination. 

Sensing her confusion, Pei Mianman replied with a grin, “Ah, I haven’t told you yet. Honglei, your master and I are cousins.”

“Sisters?” Qiu Honglei was baffled.

Yun Jianyue nodded. “Manman’s mother is from the Yun clan too. She is my and Yuqing’s aunt. That makes Manman and me cousins.”

Pei Mianman hugged Qiu Honglei and mischievously said, “Honglei, now that I’m your master’s sister, shouldn’t you call me aunt?”

Qiu Honglei felt giddy. I merely slept for a while. How did my master become my sister, and my sister become my elder?

Yun Jianyue snatched her disciple back. “Manman, Honglei is still recovering. Let’s talk about this another day.” If we really wanted to go down this rabbit hole, we would all be fellow sisters.

Black Jing Teng remarked, “What a messy relationship they have.”

White Jing Teng agreed. “Indeed…”

Black Jing Teng took this chance to take control of their body. “Since no one wants to be the test subject, I’ll sacrifice myself.”

From her black stockings, the crowd knew that the little sister had taken control. 

Black Jing Teng had a volatile personality, but she was the ideal test subject among them. Yun Jianyue accepted her offer and placed her finger on the latter’s body. 

Black Jing Teng was still smiling when her face suddenly turned red. Her knees began knocking together. She looked toward Zu An with teary eyes. “Ah Zu~”

Her coy voice induced goosebumps in the other women. The others wondered if this was how she spoke whenever she was in heat with Zu An. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He suppressed his lewd thoughts and focused on investigating Yun Jianyue’s new ability. “How are you feeling? Are you able to control yourself? When did the ability start taking effect…”

Unfortunately, Black Jing Teng was in no state to answer his question. She instinctively nuzzled against him. “Ah Zu…”

The others were stunned. Yun Jianyue’s ability is formidable!

Jing Teng had fallen to her ability despite not being an entity made out of flesh and blood. 

Yun Jianyue coughed. It would be inappropriate to continue staying here, so she said, “I shan’t interrupt the two of you. I’ll take a rest outside first.”

Qiu Honglei’s face reddened too. “Master, it has been a while since I saw the outside world. Why don’t you take me outside?” It would be awkward to stay here and hear her lover getting steamy with another woman. 

Pei Mianman quickly spoke up too. “Honglei, I’ll support you.”

The three women tactfully left the cave paradise. 

Black Jing Teng pushed Zu An to the ground, her eyes glistening with lust. “There’s no one to disturb us now.”

Zu An was speechless. Why do I feel like a tool here?

Many years ago, when he was still a shut-in, his greatest wish had been to have a harem. Now that his wish had been fulfilled, he realized that having a harem wasn't easy at all. It was only recently that he'd had an unforgettable, soulful interaction with Yan Xuehen, but now, he had to deal with Jing Teng. 

Even horse breeders wouldn’t push their steeds this far!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2677: Chill
chapters.body:
Chapter 2677: Chill

Yun Jianyue took Qiu Honglei to the lotus pond outside to enjoy the mountain breeze and the academy’s night scenery while chatting about recent affairs. 

Qiu Honglei’s heart swelled with bliss. While Zu An occasionally took her out to breathe in some fresh air too, she had been too weak to admire the world’s beauty. Now that she was on the verge of a complete recovery, everything looked different to her.  

While they were chatting, Yun Yuqing rushed over after hearing the news. Xie Daoyun and Ji Xiaoxi were busy having their family reunion, while Yan Xuehen was still overcoming her awkwardness. She was the only one left on the mountain peak. 

She brought a thin blanket with her and wrapped it around Qiu Honglei. “Honglei, you’re still recovering. You mustn’t catch an illness at this point.”

“Thank you, Aunt Qing.” Qiu Honglei had known her master had a younger sister, but there had been no need for her to interact with Yun Yuqing as the two sisters had been estranged. But now that her master and Yun Yuqing had patched things up, she had to make sure to maintain etiquette. 

Yun Yuqing was gorgeous. Qiu Honglei thought, No wonder she was known for her beauty back when she was King Wu’s consort, and Ah Zu fancies her. 

This complicated relationship made Qiu Honglei feel awkward. Yun Yuqing was her aunt, but both of them were Zu An’s lovers, which made them sisters. But if they looked at it in terms of who had gotten together with Zu An first, she should have been Yun Yuqing’s older sister instead. But she couldn’t bring herself to tell an elder to call her ‘big sister’. 

My seniority has been slipping while I was unconscious. Ugh, what a huge waste. Even Manman has become my aunt, and now there’s another, Yun Yuqing. How vexing!

She was tempted to drag Chu Chuyan here to experience the same frustration. 

Yun Yuqing smiled. “Don’t call me aunt. That sounds weirdly old. We’re a family now. You can call me big sister instead.”

Qiu Honglei replied with a smile, “Shouldn’t you be the one to call me big sister?”

Yun Yuqing’s smile froze up, but she quickly replied, “That works too. It’d be great to have a big sister look after me.”

Yun Jianyue couldn’t take it anymore and interjected, “Preposterous. What are the two of you doing?” If Yun Yuqing calls Honglei big sister, won’t I have to start calling Honglei big sister too? There’s no way I can do that!

Pei Mianman could hardly hold her laughter in. “It’s right anyway. You can call her aunt, and she’ll call you big sister. I don’t see a conflict here.” It doesn’t affect my standing anyway.

Qiu Honglei hugged Yun Yuqing’s arm and coyly said, “Aunt Qing, don’t mind me. I was joking.”

It belatedly struck her that she would be putting her master in a tough spot if Yun Yuqing called her ‘big sister’. By the same logic, she would have to call Chu Chuyan ‘big sister’ in the future too. They were both the Holy Maidens of their respective sects, and the victor between them hadn’t been decided yet. She would bring shame to the Devil Sect if she admitted defeat in this field. That was unacceptable to her. 

This little hiccup helped to mellow the mood, and the group chatted harmoniously.

Worried that Qiu Honglei couldn’t take the cold, Yun Jianyue had been grabbing her disciple’s hand and transmitting ki to her. 

Pei Mianman smiled. “Leave it to me.” She conjured a flickering black flame at her fingertip and lit a few bonfires. It didn’t take long for the gazebo to become warm.

“Thank you, Manman.” Qiu Honglei could feel the concern others were showing her, and it brought warmth to her heart. 

She had heard plenty of stories about her Devil Sect peers fighting over men. They would go as far as to beat their rivals to death. It was good that they didn’t have to scheme against each other for Zu An’s favor. I wonder where Ah Zu found these wonderful women. 

Yun Jianyue showed interest in Pei Mianman’s black flames. “These flames carry the racial traits of the Devil races, but it isn’t as simple as that. It’s truly incredible…”

Just like that, they chatted for the entire night. It was only when they heard the roosters’ calls and saw the brightening hue on the horizon that they exchanged bewildered looks.

“They aren’t done yet?” someone in the group suddenly said. 

All the women sighed. The man in the room was their lover, after all. The thought of him spending the night with another woman still sparked their jealousy, especially since they showed no signs of stopping. 

“I’ll go check things out. It’s about time for Honglei to rest anyway,” Yun Yuqing said.

Qiu Honglei’s face reddened. “I’m fine. I can rest with master.” She didn’t want to be used as an excuse here.

Yun Jianyue wanted to discuss the details of their scheme against Yan Xuehen with Qiu Honglei, so she played along and said, “I’ll bring Honglei into the room to rest first.”

Just then, the cave paradise opened. Zu An staggered out while holding the walls for support. When he saw the women staring at him, he quickly straightened his posture and awkwardly asked, “You aren’t asleep yet?”

The women felt anger boiling within them. Yun Jianyue couldn’t lower herself to complain, so she glanced at her disciple and urged her to say something. 

Qiu Honglei thought about the lengths Zu An had gone to save her and the meticulous care he'd shown her over the years. She couldn’t bear to say anything to him, so she turned her gaze to Yun Yuqing. Isn’t this the time for you to speak up?

Yun Yuqing felt indignant. Unlike you people, I was King Wu’s consort. We might not have done anything, but it makes it hard for me to complain about Zu An’s philandering ways. She turned her gaze to Pei Mianman.

Pei Mianman was speechless. You’re backing down now after all your big talk? But she was indeed displeased, so she asked, “Same to you. Aren’t you tired after doing it until now?”

Her words made Zu An feel even more awkward. Before he could say a word, Pei Mianman stood up and left. “I’ll head back in to rest.”

Qiu Honglei nodded. She didn’t want to get involved in their fight. 

Yun Yuqing took her leave too. While she was walking by Zu An, she remarked with a chuckle, “Don’t go overboard lest you hurt your body.”

Now that he was alone, Zu An rubbed his waist. Black Jing Teng is a little temptress. She’s far more brazen than her big sister, daring to do everything. Each time she’s at her limit, she pushes White Jing Teng out to take the blow and returns only after resting up.  

Isn’t it peculiar how, despite sharing the same body, the two sisters regain their stamina whenever they switch personas? How in the world do they do it?

He had to concede that his experience with Jing Teng was one-of-a-kind. The seamless transition between the dignified big sister and the eccentric little sister was an experience that couldn’t be described with mere words.

Under the influence of Yun Jianyue’s ability, White Jing Teng had been far more proactive than usual. She'd even pulled out her trump card—white stocking on one leg and black stocking on the other, simultaneously showcasing the traits of both sisters. It had quite the visual impact. 

That, together with his earlier experience with Yan Xuehen, had driven Zu An to his limit. I mustn’t mess around like that anymore. 

Meanwhile, Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei discussed their plan in their room. 

Qiu Honglei was worried. “Ah Zu mentioned that Sect Master Yan has obtained a universal deity’s power too, further dampening her emotions. Master, will your ability work on her?”

“I’d have to give it a try to know.” Yun Jianyue was excited to pull one over her long-time rival. 

In the neighboring room, Yan Xuehen felt a chill running down her spine. How weird. Why am I feeling cold?

She was aware of the earlier gathering in the gazebo, and she'd considered heading out to meet them. However, she'd decided against it in the end. They're all that fellow’s lovers. That demoness happens to be their kin, but what reason do I have to join the group?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2678: Battle
chapters.body:
Chapter 2678: Battle

In the neighboring room, Qiu Honglei eyed the wall and asked, “Master, could Sect Master Yan have overheard our words?”

Yan Xuehen was a renowned expert in the world of cultivation. It wouldn’t be surprising if she had the means to eavesdrop on their conversations.

Yun Jianyue proudly replied, “Don’t worry, I'm here. If she can overhear our conversation that easily, I might as well surrender to her.”

Qiu Honglei’s heart was put at ease. Master and Sect Master Yan have always been equally matched. There should be no problem if she said so. “Master, I’m also worried about something else. You saw Ah Zu’s earlier condition too. Miss Jing is simply too… energetic. I’m worried that Ah Zu’s body won’t be able to take it.” 

Yun Jianyue frowned. “You’re worried about that fellow? He’s built like a bull!”

Lewd memories flashed through Qiu Honglei’s mind, and her face reddened. “But I've never seen him so weak before.”

Yun Jianyue nodded. “That’s true. We can’t underestimate those two sisters.”

The two of them fell silent. Considering their master-disciple relationship, it felt weirdly awkward for them to be talking about their lover’s prowess in bed. 

Yun Jianyue changed the subject with a cough. “Your worry isn’t unfounded. It won’t be good if he fails to perform after we've drugged that ice sculpture. Let’s nourish him well tomorrow and keep an eye on the other women, lest they make a move.”

Qiu Honglei’s face reddened. “I’ll have to trouble you for that, master. It’s not convenient for me to move around, as I’m still recovering from my injury.”

Yun Jianyue rolled her eyes. “There’s nothing difficult about that. You can stall Miss Pei by chatting with her. Leave Yun Yuqing to me. As for Miss Xie and Miss Ji, they shouldn’t be a problem since they're busy with their family reunion.”

Qiu Honglei thought those words made sense. Master and Manman might be cousins, but they are from different generations. There are more common topics between me and Manman.

They continued discussing their plan to deal with Yan Xuehen. They were both intelligent individuals, and it didn’t take them long to devise a feasible ploy.

Zu An had barely rested for two hours when the teachers and students from the academy began streaming in to visit him. He couldn’t tell them that he was exhausted from his night activities, so he could only muster his energy to deal with them. Fortunately, he'd recovered most of his energy with just two hours of rest. 

After he was done dealing with the academy’s affairs, Yun Yuqing entered the room with a bowl of chicken soup. “Ah Zu, you have been working hard recently. You need some nourishment.” 

Zu An had expended too much energy in recent days, and the aroma of the chicken soup whetted his appetite. He took two sips, and his eyes lit up. “Did you make this? It’s delicious!”

“I can brew one for you every day if you like.” Yun Yuqing thought about how Yun Jianyue had secretly offered her pointers. Blood is indeed thicker than water. 

She had never thought about personally cooking for Zu An. She'd been born into affluence and her family had plenty of chefs serving them. They had never needed to cook for themselves. But Yun Jianyue had told her that if she personally cooked for Zu An, the meaning would be different.

Yun Yuqing hadn’t expected that her elder cousin, despite being a feared demoness, had such a meticulous side. Even she hadn’t thought about brewing chicken soup to nourish Zu An. 

Zu An took a few more sips. “It has a unique taste. What did you add to it?”

“Ginseng, deer antler, epimedium, cistanche, morinda root, eucommia bark, cynomorium, goji berry, red dates…” Yun Yuqing recounted the herbs she had used. This was Yun Jianyue’s recipe. The Devil Sect’s demonesses sure are knowledgeable.

Zu An was speechless. No wonder I feel a heat in my stomach after drinking the contents. He put the bowl aside and said, “There’s no need to add so many herbs.”

“Ah? You don’t like it? Big sister Yue says it’s beneficial for you.” Yun Yuqing was stunned.

Zu An was taken aback. So, it was Yun Jianyue’s idea? What is she planning, feeding me so much nourishment? Does she also intend to…

Yun Yuqing picked up the bowl and personally fed him. “Ah Zu, you need nourishment. You have been working hard in recent days, including lecturing in the academy…”

Zu An wanted to turn her down, but his stomach rumbled. He was feeling hungry, so he took the bowl and continued drinking. By the time he was done, his body felt warm.

Exclamations suddenly echoed outside. 

“They're fighting!”

Alarmed, Zu An rushed out. Standing atop two of Mount Yuquan’s peaks were a black figure and a white figure—Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen. 

“What are you doing?” Zu An shouted. I know you're rivals, but do you have to come to blows each time an opportunity arises?

Yun Jianyue replied, “This is between me and her. Ah Zu, you stay out of it. We agreed on this battle yesterday. I don’t mind postponing it another day if she hasn’t rested enough.”

“There’s no need for that.” Yan Xuehen’s voice was as cold as ever.

She'd had to postpone the fight yesterday because her legs were wobbling from Zu An’s pounding. She would have embarrassed herself if they fought there and then. But a night of rest coupled with Ah Zu’s rich ki nourishment put her in a better state than ever. She was determined to teach Yun Jianyue a lesson and let everyone in the academy know that the demoness was beneath her. 

It was rather perplexing how, despite practising the Unshakable Daoist Manual, she couldn’t control her emotions whenever Yun Jianyue was involved. 

The tense atmosphere between the duo stirred a commotion in the academy. Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen were renowned figures in the world. In particular, Yan Xuehen was viewed as a fairy and enjoyed great popularity. A showdown between these two was a huge deal. News quickly spread in the academy, and soon, even people from the capital rushed over. 

“Waaa, Sect Master Yan’s skin is as smooth as rumored. No wonder she’s known as a fairy!”

“Of course! Don’t you know that back then, she was lauded as the number one beauty in the world of cultivation?”

“You’re blaspheming the fairy! Fairy Yan is devoted to pursuing the dao. She has no interest in such mortal comparisons.”

“I didn’t expect Sect Master Yun to be that beautiful.”

“Indeed. I have always heard she was a demoness who doesn’t hesitate to kill others, so I thought she would be a hideous monster with a bulky body. I didn’t expect her to be so beautiful. In terms of looks, she surpasses even Fairy Yan.”

“Nonsense. You clearly don’t have an eye for beauty. Nothing can surpass Fairy Yan’s transcendent beauty.”

… 

Just their presence was enough to divide the crowd into two factions. 

Yun Jianyue finally made her move. She unleashed a crescent moon that flowed in a beautiful arc before slicing through the mountain peak Yan Xuehen stood on. 

Yan Xuehen flitted into the sky, avoiding the attack. At the same time, frost enveloped the mountain Yun Jianyue stood on. 

Zu An’s eyelids twitched. At this rate, they’ll destroy the academy!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2679: Losing Control
chapters.body:
Chapter 2679: Losing Control

Zu An raised his hand and enveloped the nearby mountain peaks in a thin golden layer before shouting, “Please fight within this arena. Don’t go overboard in the fight.”

There was no lack of experts among the spectators in the academy, and they were all astonished by the strength of the golden layer. The academy often held tournaments as well, in which they inscribed similar formations on the dueling rings to prevent the shockwaves from destroying the academy’s buildings and harming innocent spectators. But the formations only spanned the size of a training field at most, and even so, they were always prepared days in advance. 

However, the libationer had constructed such a massive formation in the blink of an eye, and it was strong enough to withstand attacks from top-notch experts like Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun. 

The morning sun shone on the peaceful mountain valley, cloaking the lush mountain peaks in a golden sheet. 

Two beautiful figures glared at each other in midair. Their brief clash had left the two of them shocked. They'd both thought that given their recent breakthroughs and the power bestowed by their respective universal deities, it would be an easy fight, but the other party had also grown beyond their imagination.  

Under their glares, the air became so tense that it felt almost viscous. Upon sensing their murderous intent, the birds residing in the mountain forests fled in fear.

Yan Xuehen raised her hand, and her Flying Snow Sword emerged from its sheath. Its blade resembled a slender ice crystal that glimmered brilliantly under the sun. The light reflected could easily affect most opponents, but it wasn’t enough to even faze Yun Jianyue.

Yun Jianyue responded by lighting an ancient lantern. The flame inside the lantern was tiny, looking as if it would be extinguished at any moment, but its gentle brilliance instantly enveloped all the nearby mountain peaks. Even the sun paled in comparison to it. It felt as if the day had suddenly turned into night, and the ancient lantern was the moon. 

Yan Xuehen’s Flying Snow Sword was no longer as brilliant as before, but she didn’t lose her composure. The sword vibrated, transforming the gentle mountain breeze into a powerful frigid gale. Snow began raining from the sky, cloaking the mountain peaks.

She slowly made her way toward Yun Jianyue, her every step leaving a beautiful ice crystal pattern. The air between her and Yun Jianyue froze. The incredible cold was reminiscent of a prison; it could even freeze the body and blood of a top-notch expert. On top of that, sharp ice crystals manifested out of thin air and whizzed toward Yun Jianyue.

“Your Snowflake Sword has advanced another level,” Yun Jianyue said with a chuckle. She gently stepped on an ice crystal before her silhouette blurred like fleeting moonlight. 

The omnipresent ice cage could indeed trap all things tangible, but how could it imprison moonlight? Yun Jianyue’s body was incredibly fluid, dodging the incoming ice crystals with graceful movements. 

Awed exclamations echoed from the academy. The younger students gulped. 

“How beautiful…”

As someone who had mastered Bewitching Voice, Yun Jianyue’s every gesture and chuckle could siphon away a person’s soul. Now that she had received the Deity of Desires’ bestowment, even without her intentionally seducing them, normal people couldn’t hope to resist her charms.

As she flitted across the air, Yun Jianyue left silhouettes in her wake. By the time she appeared once more, she was already standing in front of Yan Xuehen. Her crescent glaives were as thin as a cicada’s wings, looking as if a glowing silver fluid was flowing within them; they seemed to be imbued with the moon’s power. They moved in a perfect arc, forming yet another full moon in the sky. 

But the top-notch experts present sensed the danger of the full moon—it was formed by compressing immense force together. It surreptitiously swept toward Yan Xuehen’s waist, slicing through the raging snowstorm that was protecting her. 

Yan Xuehen channeled the Flying Snow Sword, choosing to counterattack instead of dodging. Its sword tip precisely struck the edge of the full moon.

A weaker cultivator would likely have chosen to strike the moon’s body, but its weak spot lay in its perimeter. There was a crisp sound, and the moon shattered into specks of light. 

Awed exclamations echoed outside the mountain valley. None of the spectators thought they could resolve this crisis as gracefully as Yan Xuehen. 

Qiu Honglei tensed up. “Will master be alright?”

Yun Yuqing patted her head and said, “Don’t worry. Big sister won’t lose that easily.”

Yun Jianyue dashed through the specks of light and swiftly approached Yan Xuehen like a specter. It was as if she had cast aside her corporeal body, her steps leaving behind graceful black afterimages. No more than a handful of people in the academy could see her true body. 

The earlier full moon attack had been a feint. She'd known that Yan Xuehen could unravel her attack, but the shattered specks of light would seep into the latter's body and slow her ki circulation for an instant. That was more than enough time for her to strike.

Yun Jianyue quickly weaved a tight moonlight net resembling a lover’s embrace with her crescent glaives. Her slashes were imbued with the ancient lantern’s moonlight energy, granting them incomparable sharpness and a tinge of law that affected its target’s movements and mind.

The mountain valley turned into her moonlight domain. No one except for Zu An could make out her silhouette, for every beam of moonlight was her! 

Yan Xuehen’s expression turned grim. She held the Flying Snow Sword in front of her while manifesting swords of ice behind herself. The crisp buzzing of a blade echoed in the air. Her hair and robe fluttered as snow shrouded her body, forming a massive avatar of ice.

As she swung her blade down, the avatar behind her fused into her Flying Snow Sword, and she unleashed a powerful burst of blue sword ki that sliced the moonlight in two. The sword ki even reached the clouds on the horizon, cutting them in half as well. 

All the spectators stood up and craned their necks to look at the distant blue hue with shocked eyes. If not for Zu An’s barrier, the capital could have been sliced in half as well. 

Even Zu An was taken aback. The earlier slash had nearly shattered his barrier. It was a relief that he had reacted quickly and reinforced it. He had used his godlike powers to isolate this area into a mini world, but Yan Xuehen’s sword had the power to slice through that world! Her growth far surpassed his wildest imaginations. 

“Master!” Qiu Honglei grabbed her sleeves. Even Yun Yuqing wasn’t as confident as before. 

“Don’t worry, she’s fine,” Zu An said. 

The brilliant moonlight in the mountain valley suddenly turned red. Even the moon in the sky transformed into a blood moon. A coy laugh echoed from the blood moon. “Ice sculpture, I didn’t expect your swordsmanship to have reached this level. I underestimated you.”

The crowd looked over. A beautiful figure was seated in the blood moon. Her sleeves had disappeared due to the earlier sword slash, but her fair arms, coupled with her amazing figure, left her oozing with seductive charm. 

Zu An’s head hurt. This woman is unknowingly emanating the Deity of Desires’ power, and it’s affecting everyone in the academy!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2680: Mutually Injured
chapters.body:
Chapter 2680: Mutually Injured

It would be disastrous if everyone in the academy and perhaps even the capital went on a rampage, so Zu An took out his Violet-Gold Alms and lightly knocked on the bowl. A calming reverberation echoed through the academy, calming the fervent minds of the crowd. 

The crowd widened their eyes in astonishment. They were all thinking, What got over me? I wanted to charge over as if I had lost my mind. No wonder she’s known as a demoness. Just looking at her made me lose control of myself. 

The older students silently circulated their ki to calm themselves while instructing their juniors to close their eyes and not look anymore. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. The Violet-Gold Alms protected one’s soul from beguilement and damage. Yun Jianyue’s desire energy likely fell under the field of beguilement. 

It was fortunate Yun Jianyue hadn't actively channeled that power, and the desire energy was divided across many individuals over a huge area, so its effect on each individual was limited. Thanks to that, the Violet-Gold Alms easily shook them out of their daze. Otherwise, they would have been in trouble.

Yun Jianyue was startled by the chaos, but she proceeded to laugh coyly afterward. That seduced quite a few people in the crowd yet again, so Zu An had to raise the frequency of his knocking on the Violet-Gold Alms to dispel the effects. 

Qiu Honglei was perplexed. While master is skilled in the art of seduction, she doesn’t like to resort to that, which is why she usually puts on a fierce appearance. Yet she's now embracing the character of a temptress. Is it because Ah Zu opened her horizons, or is it the influence of the Deity of Desires?

Yan Xuehen frowned too. “Demoness, you have changed.”

“Aren’t you the same too? Your sword isn’t as emotionless as before. Did you recently meet someone you fancy?” Yun Jianyue chuckled coyly.

The spectators didn’t think much of it; they thought this was just a way for the demoness to shake Fairy Yan’s mind.

But Yan Xuehen felt conscience-stricken. Her expression turned cold, and she thrust her blade toward the blood moon. A resounding buzz echoed from her sword as blue frost rippled into her surroundings. Everything in the vicinity froze, and ice crystals reminiscent of diamonds dangled in midair. 

“Why are you in such a rush?” Yun Jianyue chuckled, though her eyes narrowed ferociously. 

The blood moon behind her suddenly flowed like a fluid, gushing forth and devouring the ice crystals in its way. The malevolent blood sea and the frigid ice crystals clashed and eroded each other. A thick fog broke out into the surroundings. It felt as if hell had manifested on earth. 

Many spectators turned pale in fright. Their earlier lewd thoughts had vanished without a trace. 

Zu An laughed. So, fear can dispel lust. He wondered whether to bring Yun Jianyue to the Netherworld to see the different hells. It could help her achieve a breakthrough in her Corpse Mountain Blood Sea domain. But on the other hand, that could affect her ‘desire’ dao cultivation. 

Yan Xuehen’s reckless attack put her at a disadvantage in this clash. Her skirt was nicked, revealing her fair legs.

The crowd trembled in excitement. It was just her thighs, but the demure Sect Master Yan had never revealed them before.

Black streaks appeared on Zu An’s face. I feel like I’m the one losing even though they're the ones fighting. He could only console himself with the thought that the ladies in his previous world were dressed far more scantily than this. 

The two women in the sky didn’t have the leisure to think about such things. They continued clashing inside the fog. 

Yan Xuehen lived up to her reputation as the strongest expert of the Dao Gate. She knew she had slipped up and opted to maintain a defensive posture to slowly regain her momentum. 

A while later, Yun Jianyue fully fused with the blood moon. All the moonlight and blood light converged together to form a single line. It pierced through the thick fog and locked onto Yan Xuehen, leaving her no room to escape. 

Yan Xuehen’s expression tightened. As she became one with her sword, her Flying Snow Sword buzzed louder than ever. A beautiful blue hue reminiscent of ten-thousand-year-old ice glowed around her. Whatever the blue hue touched froze over. 

The clash between the blades produced a sound that couldn’t be fully described with words. It resembled the call of a phoenix, or the clash between two stars.

Everyone’s hearts shuddered. This sight inexplicably deepened their understanding of the sword and the saber.

Time seemed to stop at this moment. There was no earth-shaking explosion; just the intersection and destruction of their energies and laws. The mountain range was split in two. One half turned into an ice field, whereas the other was dominated by the blood moon and sea.

The two women harrumphed before retreating a hundred meters away. Trails of blood seeped from the corners of their lips. 

Yun Jianyue’s hair and clothes were coated in white frost. Even her breath immediately froze into ice crystals. 

Yan Xuehen’s face and body turned red. A blood moon vaguely manifested on her body. 

Alarmed, Zu An quickly stepped in between them and declared, “Let’s end this fight here. There’s no point continuing the fight, as your abilities are comparable to each other. At this rate, it’ll only end in mutual destruction.” His voice was firm and left no room for negotiation.

The crowd wasn’t surprised, as Zu An was unquestionably the strongest expert in the world. Most cultivator ranking boards didn’t include his name, as it was meaningless to do so. The gap between the first and the second place was too huge. Thus, his verdict was credible. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were both on close terms with Zu An, and they themselves knew that he was speaking the truth, so they clasped their fists and took their leave. 

It was only when Zu An lifted the barrier that the crowd realized that there was a clear boundary within the mountain range. On one side were red trees and flowers, and molten flowing earth resembling the blood moon. On the other side was a pristine ice field. The vibrant mountain peaks had turned barren as a result of the fight. 

Zu An turned to the crowd and said, “I believe most of you have gained something from witnessing the fight, but calm down and slowly unravel what you have learned. I’d recommend you go into seclusion to ponder hard. You may go now.”

With a wave of his hand, the desolate mountain peaks became verdant once more.

The crowd was awed. They bowed to him before taking their leave. As they left, they couldn’t help but discuss the battle and Zu An’s miraculous restoration of the mountain peaks.

Zu An quickly returned to the courtyard and first made his way into Yun Jianyue’s room. Qiu Honglei was so anxious that she was on the verge of crying, whereas Yun Yuqing was helping Yun Jianyue channel her ki to heal her injuries. 

“You’re severely injured…” Zu An wanted to help her, but Yun Jianyue stopped him with a look.

“Don’t reveal our relationship. I have Yuqing and Honglei here to aid me, but the ice sculpture is alone. You should help her instead,” Yun Jianyue secretly relayed a message to him via her ki. 

Zu An was taken aback. “Do you mean it?”

“Of course! Now that Chuyan isn’t with her, she’s alone. What do you expect her to do alone?” Yun Jianyue quickly replied. “As much as I want to defeat the ice sculpture, I don’t intend to take advantage of her weakness.” 

“But you’re severely injured. Yuqing’s ki might not be enough.” Zu An was still worried. 

“Manman is over there. Call her over to help me. The two of them should be enough to treat my injuries,” Yun Jianyue said. 

Zu An agreed. Yun Yuqing and Pei Mianman weren't strong enough individually, but if they combined their strength, they could handle it. Besides, Yan Xuehen needed help as well, so Zu An told Yun Jianyue to take care before rushing to the neighboring room. 

Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei exchanged expectant looks. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2681: Anxious
chapters.body:
Chapter 2681: Anxious

Qiu Honglei was taken aback. She sent a voice transmission and asked, “Master, have you already used your ability on Sect Master Yan?”

When she saw the two of them fighting, her heart had nearly leaped out of her chest. She'd been worried danger might befall her master. Yet her master had still managed to use that kind of ability in that tight situation?

“Of course. Why else would I push myself so far?” Yun Jianyue was gleeful because she had gotten to Yan Xuehen.

“Master, don’t take such risks ever again!” Qiu Honglei glared at Yun Jianyue. The thought of her master becoming severely injured made her shudder in fear. 

“Don’t worry, I've been through plenty of danger in my life. I know what I’m doing.” Yun Jianyue harrumphed, unhappy that her disciple didn’t share her excitement in having succeeded in their plan. 

“What are you murmuring about over there?” Yun Yuqing asked.

“Nothing. Just something worth celebrating.” Yun Jianyue smiled like a sly fox.

“Tell me about it,” Yun Yuqing realized her big sister had been less ferocious but more seductive recently. 

“I’ll tell you when the time is right.” Even in her moment of glee, Yun Jianyue knew some things couldn’t be said out loud. 

“Tsk. Stingy!” Yun Yuqing was dissatisfied, but there was nothing she could do about it. 

Meanwhile, Zu An entered the neighboring room and saw Pei Mianman treating Yan Xuehen. Pei Mianman was relieved to see Zu An. “Ah Zu, you came just in time. Sect Master Yan is severely injured, and I’m afraid I alone am unable to treat her.”

Zu An nodded. “Leave her to me. You help your big sister.”

“Ah?” Pei Mianman was taken aback. Her gaze alternated between the two. Wouldn’t it be inappropriate to leave a man and a woman alone like this? She knew about their relationship, but the others were still oblivious to it. If she left the room, it could stir rumors.

Zu An harrumphed. “Your big sister is calling you over. Yuqing can’t treat Sect Master Yun alone, so they need you there to help her.”

Yan Xuehen opened her eyes. “Thank you, Manman.”

Pei Mianman pouted. I thought Yan Xuehen was a reserved person, but she’s driving me out now? This adulterous couple! They can’t wait to fool around as soon as Chuyan is gone! She finally replied, “We’ll be right next door. Call us if you need anything.”

She couldn’t reveal her knowledge about their relationship, so she could only subtly remind them to take note of their surroundings. If they were too loud, the others could hear what was happening from next door. She shot a glare at Zu An before leaving the room.

You have successfully trolled Pei Mianman for +66… +66… +66…

Zu An was startled. He'd come here to treat Yan Xuehen, only to be treated as a pervert.

He walked up to Yan Xuehen and carefully grasped her hand. “Is there a need for the two of you to go this far and get yourself injured like that?”

Yan Xuehen tried to withdraw her hand, but Zu An refused to release it. She thought about the extreme intimacy they had shared yesterday and decided to let him do as he pleased. “Hmph, why don’t you go reprimand that demoness instead?”

“It’s not a reprimand. I’m just worried about both of you,” Zu An gently replied. 

“You’re rather concerned about the demoness.” Yan Xuehen glanced at him. With her sharp instincts as a woman, she sensed something amiss. 

Zu An was alarmed. He couldn’t give Yun Jianyue away without her agreement. “What are you thinking? Aren’t I here to treat your injuries?”

Yan Xuehen felt a tinge of sweetness in her heart. At least I’m better than the demoness in this regard. 

But her face heated up right after. How can I harbor such thoughts? She would mock me if she knew about this.

“It’s a good thing I was around, or else you’d have gone down together,” Zu An said worriedly as he channeled a steady stream of warm ki into her body to treat her injuries. 

It hurt him no matter who got injured, not to mention there was a good chance of the two of them going down together. The thought of that frightening possibility made him tempted to reveal his relationship with both of them. That way, he could become their battlefield instead. But considering their relationship as long-time rivals, that would be a huge mess too.

“So be it. I can’t lose to that demoness!” Yan Xuehen harrumphed.

Zu An burst into laughter. “You’re usually unfazed by everything, but you lose your composure as soon as Yun Jianyue is involved.”

Yan Xuehen frowned. “I don’t know why either. Perhaps we were enemies in our previous lives.”

Her emotions went haywire whenever she thought about the demoness. That reminded her of something, and she eyed Zu An warily. “Doesn’t your recovery ki carry the power of the Golden Phoenix? Are you sure it’s fine?”

Her vigilant attitude made Zu An laugh. “Have you forgotten I'm now the god of this world? I can erase the side effects of my ki in this world. Don’t think too much and focus on recuperating from your injuries.”

“Mm.” Yan Xuehen breathed a sigh of relief and began channeling her ki. 

With Ah Zu’s help, I should recover faster than the demoness. Just you wait and see!

The thought of that brought a smile to her lips.

But why does Ah Zu’s ki feel so hot…

In the neighboring room, Yun Yuqing pinched Yun Jianyue’s waist and said, “Focus, big sister! At this rate, you’ll suffer a cultivation deviation!”

Yun Jianyue smiled sheepishly. She was too focused on the situation next door to focus on her own treatment. Yun Yuqing and Pei Mianman were top-notch experts as well; they could sense the anomaly in her condition.

“Don’t worry, I’ll be fine even if the treatment is delayed.” Yun Jianyue was overflowing with curiosity, but Zu An had constructed a barrier to isolate noises from the neighboring room, so she was unable to hear what was happening on the other side.
