She had to time herself well to catch them in the act. If she moved too early, it would alert them and make them vigilant. If she moved too late, they would snap out of her ability. 

She thought hard about it and decided to wait a little longer. Ah Zu shouldn’t be that fast. 

She'd even confirmed that Ah Zu had drunk plenty of the nourishing chicken soup with Yun Yuqing beforehand. He should have recovered his stamina, which was squeezed dry by the Jing Teng sisters last night.

Yun Yuqing suddenly asked, “Do you think… they'll do something next door?”

The room plunged into silence. 

Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei thought their ploy had been exposed. 

Pei Mianman wondered if she had accidentally let the news slip. Even though she was angry when she learned that Zu An had gotten together with Yan Xuehen, she wasn’t so tactless as to infringe on their privacy. Although, she'd secretly revealed it to Chu Chuyan so that the latter wouldn’t be hurt too deeply. 

The reactions were so huge that Yun Yuqing felt embarrassed. “It goes without saying that I trust Sect Master Yan’s character, but she’s so beautiful, and Ah Zu is… I fear that Ah Zu might make a move in the heat of the moment…”

Pei Mianman replied with a straight face, “Don’t worry, Ah Zu won’t force himself on others. Nothing will happen unless Sect Master Yan is willing.”

She wasn’t lying. She knew Ah Zu’s character well, and judging from her expression back then, Sect Master Yan was clearly a willing party. This is in line with Ah Zu’s character, but I must say that Sect Master Yan is a bit hypocritical.

Yun Yuqing chuckled. “Then I guess nothing can possibly happen. Sect Master Yan is such a reserved person!”

Yun Jianyue exchanged amused looks with Qiu Honglei. Well, you never know!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2682: How Can I Resolve The Situation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2682: How Can I Resolve The Situation

In the neighboring room, Yan Xuehen’s injuries recovered at a visible pace under the effects of Zu An’s ki. It wasn't her first time witnessing this, but she was still awed by his pure ki. 

“I doubt I could ever cultivate my ki to this extent.” Yan Xuehen, despite being a proud person, couldn’t help but feel demoralized. She felt that she couldn’t reach this level even if she continued cultivating for a hundred or even a thousand years. 

Zu An smiled. “Don’t put yourself down. I only reached this level because I obtained this world’s Law Beacon and became its master. Unless you obtain a Wonderpoint World’s Law Beacon as well, it’ll be hard for you to reach the same level as me. On top of that, this power is only limited to this world.”

His concern warmed Yan Xuehen’s heart. “I understand. Thank you for your reminder.”

The most important thing to a cultivator was their dao heart. If their dao heart wavered, their cultivation would collapse. 

“There’s no need for words of gratitude between us. It sounds so distant.” Zu An tenderly organized Yan Xuehen’s unkempt hair. When he saw her pale face, his heart ached. 

Yan Xuehen embarrassedly eyed the wall and said with a hushed voice, “Be careful, they're just next door. I… won’t be able to face anyone if they learn about it.”

She could have constructed a barrier to hinder Yun Jianyue’s prying eyes if she was in her normal state, but now that she was severely injured, she didn’t have the strength to maintain such a barrier. 

Zu An burst into laughter. “Don’t worry. I’m not that careless. They can’t hear anything next door.”

Yan Xuehen breathed a sigh of relief. A second later, she murmured, “You seem awfully experienced. You must have done such things often.”

The vibrant expressions on the cold beauty made Zu An’s heart quiver. She’s truly as beautiful as a fairy. “Are you jealous?”

“Jealous? I’m helping Chuyan keep an eye on you,” Yan Xuehen replied with a straight face. As she recovered from her injuries, her face regained its redness, making her look coy. 

“Indeed. You’ll have to take care of me more now that Chuyan isn’t around.” Zu An wasn’t bothered by Yan Xuehen’s more vibrant expression; he attributed it to her feeling vulnerable due to her severe injuries. Besides, they were in an intimate relationship. It was normal for her to be more vibrant when there were no outsiders around.

“How should I take care of you?” Yan Xuehen stared at him with eyes brimming with emotions. Zu An was moved. 

“Chuyan is my wife. Since you’re taking care of me on her behalf, it’s only right for me for you to take care of me like a wife caring for her husband.” Zu An grabbed her hand. Despite her frosty exterior, her hand was warm and soft like fine jade. Yan Xuehen is truly blessed by the maker. 

“There are plenty of people taking care of you like a husband. Didn’t the Jing Teng sisters take very good care of you just last night? Yun Yuqing even prepared a bowl of chicken soup for you earlier—it was filled with tonics that boost your vitality,” Yan Xuehen sneered as she shook his hand off. 

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +33… +33… +33…

Zu An burst into laughter. I didn’t expect her to be keeping track of these things despite appearing so aloof. She must have been keeping an eye on everything happening around me. He pulled Yan Xuehen into his arms and asked, “Big sister Yan, are you jealous?”

His masculine aura made Yan Xuehen shudder. She instinctively pushed Zu An aside. “Stop it. You’re here to treat me. You can’t stay here for too long, or else they’ll suspect something.”

“Don’t worry, they don’t know how long it’ll take for me to treat you.” Zu An sensed her tone loosening up. 

“Aren’t you planning to treat the demoness later? She’ll know right away.” Yan Xuehen sneered. Ah Zu came here first because I’m closer to him, but Yun Jianyue is Miss Qiu’s master, making her Zu An’s friend. He won’t turn a blind eye to her plight.

“I’ll go slower later. They won’t notice a thing,” Zu An scoffed. She gets easily affected whenever Yun Jianyue is involved.

“Are you sure you wouldn't be going slower to spend more time with her? She might be my nemesis, but I have to concede that she’s gorgeous, not to mention she’s the only woman in the history of the Devil Sect to have mastered the Bewitching Voice. Countless men would kneel under her skirt at a wag of her finger. You saw how the crowd reacted during our duel earlier.”

Yan Xuehen blushed after saying those words. It sounds like I’m jealous, but I’m just stating facts!

Zu An was both surprised and overjoyed. While Yan Xuehen would give in to him in a moment of passion, she was still demure on the whole. She had never acted so coyly to him before. The change in her attitude delighted him.

“Do you think I’d have to resort to such underhanded means?”

“Sure, sure. All it takes is a word from you—princess and holy maidens would line up for a chance to serve you,” Yan Xuehen scoffed as if she had just downed an entire vat of vinegar. “Who knows? That demoness might throw herself at you too.”

She suddenly narrowed her eyes and asked, “Has that demoness ever tried to seduce you?”

She had never considered this possibility, but Zu An’s words reminded her. With Ah Zu’s standing, which woman could resist him now? Yun Jianyue is a demoness from the Devil Sect, and she specializes in seducing men. He’s a philandering pig, and Yun Jianyue is so beautiful…

All those thoughts spurred her panic. 

“What are you thinking? Sect Master Yun is Honglei’s master. How could we have such ties?” Zu An secretly wiped away cold sweat. Yun Jianyue had also sternly warned him not to divulge their relationship, or else she would turn against him. Thus, he had no choice but to lie to Yan Xuehen. 

But he knew it was only a matter of time before the truth came to light, and that gave him a headache. How am I supposed to resolve the situation then?

Both Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue would blame him for keeping secrets from them. It wouldn't be beyond them to fling their sleeves and leave him for good.

“And I am Chuyan’s master!” Yan Xuehen harrumphed. Her face reddened right after she blurted out those words. She tugged her clothes as she felt inexplicable heat rising from her body.

“It’s different. That was a coincidence.” Zu An could only explain it that way, but he was soon mesmerized by her beauty. Her glistening lips made his mouth go dry.  

While he was thinking about how to approach her without angering her, she suddenly leaned in on her own volition and gently kissed him. 

“Ah Zu, you can’t get together with that demoness. I don’t know what I’ll do otherwise.” Yan Xuehen looked at him with pleading eyes. 

Zu An’s heart quivered. Do I have to continue deceiving her and resolve this issue in the future? But both sides have warned me not to reveal our relationship. What am I supposed to do?

“If you’re struggling to resist that demoness’ seduction, you can… look for me. I’ll take care of you on Chuyan’s behalf,” Yan Xuehen said as she shyly buried her face in his chest, not daring to look at him anymore.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2683: From Hell To Heaven
chapters.body:
Chapter 2683: From Hell To Heaven

“Really?” Zu An was exhilarated. He had never expected Yan Xuehen to be so proactive. 

Yan Xuehen’s heart was beating quickly. She also hadn’t expected herself to utter such bold words. She sensed something was amiss, but somehow, she didn’t think her words were excessively overboard even though they stepped on the line. 

And I’d let Chuyan down if Yun Jianyue gets to him.

From a personal standpoint, she also didn’t want there to be anything between Zu An and Yun Jianyue, or else just the thought of it could anger her to death.

“Of course.” Her voice was barely audible, but it couldn’t escape Zu An’s ears. 

He burst into laughter as he pulled her into his arms. His cold lover felt a lot warmer than usual.

Yan Xuehen felt dazed. She normally wasn't averse to being intimate with Zu An, but something just felt off today. 

Zu An leaned in and kissed her. She moaned and reciprocated the kiss. He thought that he had finally melted the ice beauty’s heart. She’s so much more proactive than usual.

Yan Xuehen felt bliss surging into her heart. I was deceiving myself all this while by putting distance between us. This passionate kiss felt like the most intoxicating wine in the world. She felt as if she was going to melt from the heat. 

And this intoxication effect was as much physical as it was psychological. The realization of that made her embarrassed. What's come over me? Forget it. It's already happened anyway. There’s no reason for me to continue pushing him away. Till Chuyan returns, I’ll help her fulfill her duties as his wife… 

The thought of Chuyan jolted her heart. She suddenly felt a wave of intense shame. 

She had practiced the Unshakable Daoist Manual, after all. Clarity returned to her head, and she belatedly realized how intertwined their bodies were. Taken aback, she exclaimed, “Ah Zu, are you sure… you didn’t use that golden phoenix ki on me?”

“Of course not.” Zu An was stunned. He thought she was just embarrassed. “Calm down, big sister Yan. It’s a normal reaction in the heat of passion. I like you, and you like me too. That’s all that matters.”

Yan Xuehen was confused. He has no reason to deceive me. He might be lustful, but I trust his character. He won’t use such despicable means to take advantage of me. Could this be my true will… 

It’s all Ah Zu’s fault! He messed around too much after we returned from the Netherworld. Otherwise, I wouldn’t be feeling this way with just a mere touch.

The thought of what happened in the Netherworld made her heart beat even faster. Her usually cold eyes overflowed with tenderness and shyness. 

The two of them naturally fell on the bed together. Out of embarrassment, Yan Xuehen put down the blinds as Zu An pushed her down. 

…

A while later, Yun Yuqing’s anxious voice echoed, “Bad news, Ah Zu! Something happened to big sister Yun!”

Qiu Honglei’s anxious voice followed. “Master is at her limit!”

They barged into the room without waiting for a response. The door was locked, but a normal lock couldn’t hope to stop the two of them. 

Pei Mianman looked upon the situation worriedly. She knew about Zu An and Yan Xuehen’s relationship. Things would get troublesome if the two of them were doing something intimate in the room. But Yun Jianyue’s injuries had suddenly worsened. She couldn’t find a way to help her, so she had no reason to stop these two women from seeking Zu An’s help. 

Yan Xuehen exclaimed in horror. She anxiously sat up and grabbed a blanket to cover her body, but her smooth shoulders and exquisite collarbones remained exposed. On top of that, her fair skin was dyed red. Anyone witnessing this scene would know what had happened right away. 

Looking at the three women who had barged into the room, her face turned pale. It’s over!

It was one thing for her reputation to crumble, but the White Jade Sect’s honor would be sullied, and Chuyan would become a laughingstock too. On top of that, Yun Jianyue would mock her to no end. The thought of that filled her heart with despair. She instinctively grabbed her Flying Snow Sword to slit her throat. She could think of no solution other than ending her life and cleansing her shame with her blood. 

But Zu An moved faster than her. He grabbed her sword and exclaimed, “Big sister Yan, what are you doing?”

Yan Xuehen’s eyes were cloaked in despair. “Ah Zu, let me die.”

Zu An quivered upon seeing her current state. I considered coming clean with them, but now, I’m glad I didn’t do so. “Don’t worry, big sister Yan. They didn’t see anything.”

“How could they have not seen anything? They…” Yan Xuehen pointed at the three women who had just barged into the room, only to raise her eyebrows in astonishment. 

None of the three women were moving. Their postures were fixed at the moment they barged into the room—their legs and hands hung in mid air, and their expressions were frozen in place. They looked like three statues. But Yan Xuehen could tell they were living beings. Not even the greatest sculptor in the world could create a masterpiece as lifelike as this. 

Zu An explained, “I paused their time. They haven’t realized what’s going on in here.”

“Really?” Yan Xuehen was overjoyed. It felt as if she had been lifted from hell into heaven. 

“Of course.” Zu An smiled. “With my recent breakthrough and my grasp of the World Law Beacon, I am god in this world. I can do things beyond your wildest imagination. Pausing their time is nothing in comparison.” 

Yan Xuehen blinked as spirit returned into her eyes. “Are there no restrictions?”

“Of course there are. I could easily pause their time because they're standing next to one another, so the implications on the world’s cause and effect are limited. But if I expanded the scale to the academy and the capital, the implications would be so great that they could plunge this world into chaos or even unravel it,” Zu An explained. 

Yan Xuehen looked at Zu An with envious eyes. “I can’t imagine how anyone can defeat you in your current state. Even universal deities must only amount to this much, no?”

Any cultivator would dream of attaining the abilities Zu An wielded.

Zu An shook his head. “My abilities are only limited to this world. I won’t be able to use them outside of this world. In comparison, the universal deities can exert their unparalleled powers across the myriad worlds.”

Yan Xuehen was startled. She wanted to further discuss this topic, but the cold wind blowing against her skin was a reminder of her current plight. She felt so embarrassed that she quickly put on her clothes. 

Zu An frowned. “You don’t need to be in such a rush. Time flows differently for us right now. We can… stay like that for a little longer, and it would only be an instant for them.”

Yan Xuehen spat. “No way!”

Even if the three women couldn’t see it, it was still as good as a public execution for her to get steamy with him in front of them. 

“Why did you all suddenly go quiet? Did you see something?” Yun Jianyue’s voice suddenly came from outside. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2684: Doubt
chapters.body:
Chapter 2684: Doubt

Yan Xuehen had barely breathed a sigh of relief when she heard her nemesis’ voice. She was so nervous that she could almost feel her heart leaping up her throat. She anxiously put on her clothes, but she was in such a fluster that she buttoned them incorrectly.

Zu An was amused by her panic. He reached forth and helped her rebutton. 

But that only made Yan Xuehen more nervous. How can I explain this if she witnesses this sight?

“Don’t worry, she won’t be able to get in,” Zu An reassured her. 

Yan Xuehen realized Zu An must have used his time stop ability again and exhaled in relief. Her face reddened. “I’ll do it myself…” I’ll probably run out of strength quickly if I let this fellow tidy up my clothes for me. 

Zu An watched her put on her clothes with a grin. He found it a beautiful scene to behold. Yan Xuehen was so embarrassed by his intense stare that she turned away from him.

“Are you ready?” Zu An asked. 

“Stop!” Yan Xuehen hurriedly interrupted him. Under Zu An’s perplexed gaze, she quickly channeled her cultivation method. After a few circulations, her redness receded, and she regained her usual icy mountain-like coldness. 

Zu An was startled. He thought about her earlier act of passion, and the sharp contrast made his heart skip a beat. No wonder so many people in my previous life enjoyed the dere tropes! There's an inexplicable charm to it.

“Don’t you give me away later!” Yan Xuehen glared at Zu An as she instructed him. 

“Don’t worry, I’m a veteran myself,” Zu An promised, thumping his chest. 

Yan Xuehen felt a twinge of jealousy. “You must have a lot of experience doing this.”

Zu An was speechless.

When Yan Xuehen was finally done with her preparations, Zu An snapped his fingers, and time reverted to normal for the others in the residence.

“Ah Zu, my master…” Qiu Honglei said as her eyes swiftly scanned the room. She was stunned when she saw a fully-clothed Yan Xuehen resting on a chair and Zu An sitting a distance away, remotely treating her injuries.

Her master had hinted for her to head over to catch them in the act, thinking Zu An and Yan Xuehen should have started on the 'action'. She'd felt embarrassed and wanted her master to do it instead, but she couldn’t defy her master and had no choice but to half-reluctantly, half-excitedly head over. 

She'd thought a huge scandal was awaiting her, but it was the opposite of what she expected. After my master boasted so much about her ability…is this all? Master, you’re so unreliable!

Meanwhile, Pei Mianman was perplexed. She knew about Yan Xuehen and Zu An’s relationship. Even if they dared not do anything in the room, they wouldn’t be so distant from each other as if they were strangers. Alhough this wasn’t a good time to raise her doubts.

“How’s your master?” Zu An asked. 

Qiu Honglei felt a tinge of guilt under Zu An’s gaze. Fortunately, Yun Yuqing spoke up. “A problem cropped up with big sister Yun’s injuries. I don’t know why, but her condition suddenly worsened. We had no choice but to seek your help.”

“Are you sure? She looks fine to me,” Zu An said as he looked at the doorway. 

Qiu Honglei and the others turned around and saw Yun Jianyue rushing over. “What’s taking all of you so… long?”

Yun Jianyue froze when she saw everyone looking at her. She quickly scanned the room and saw Yan Xuehen properly clothed, emanating her usual ice sculpture aura. She was stunned. How is this possible? 

“Big sister Yun, how are you…” Yun Yuqing was bewildered. 

Yun Jianyue quickly cleared the air with a laugh. “Somehow, I managed to calm the energy rampaging inside my body. I was worried you would disturb their treatment, so I rushed over to stop you. Looks like I came too late.”

Yun Yuqing breathed a sigh of relief. “I’m glad you’re fine, big sister.”

Yun Jianyue responded with an unnatural smile. She was feeling conscience-stricken. 

Qiu Honglei wished there was a hole she could jump into. I’d be too embarrassed to face Ah Zu if he learns about our plan.

Pei Mianman alternated her gaze between the two pairs—Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei, and Zu An and Yan Xuehen. She could already guess the reason behind Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei’s anomalous behavior, but she couldn’t understand how Zu An and Yan Xuehen were so prim and proper. 

What made Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei suspect them of being in a relationship? If I hadn’t witnessed it myself, I wouldn’t have believed that the icily cold Sect Master Yan would have an affair with Ah Zu.

She considered warning Zu An about it, but on second thought, she realized this had nothing to do with her. I should just enjoy the show. The thought of that brought a smile to her face. 

After the farce settled down, Yun Jianyue and the others quickly returned to their room. Yan Xuehen looked at their departing silhouettes and murmured, “Ah Zu, I think the demoness is after me.”

“Don’t think too much into it. It’s just an accident.” Zu An had come to the same conclusion, but he was on close terms with Yun Jianyue too. He had already made an exception by siding with Yan Xuehen once. If he continued that way, he wouldn’t be able to face Yun Jianyue anymore.

“Ah Zu, are you sure you didn’t use your special ki on me?” Yan Xuehen asked.

“I didn’t. It was just a natural reaction in the heat of the moment,” Zu An reassured her. No wonder Yan Xuehen was acting abnormally earlier. Yun Jianyue must have used her ability. What’s that woman up to?

After returning to her room, Yun Jianyue found an excuse to send away Yun Yuqing and Pei Mianman before asking her disciple with a hushed voice, “Honglei, are you sure you didn’t see anything when you entered the room?”

“Both of them were properly dressed and observing propriety. I told you Ah Zu was a gentleman.” Qiu Honglei had intended to complement Yan Xuehen too—she acknowledged the latter's character even though they were enemies. It was unlikely their plan would ever come to fruition. However, she decided otherwise lest she agitate her master.

“Gentleman…” Yun Jianyue was baffled. My silly disciple, you must now know how wild he is. He grabbed my hair and rode me like a horse!

She was confident that Zu An was incapable of resisting Yan Xuehen’s allure, and she had successfully planted her desire energy in Yan Xuehen. So what went wrong?

A thought suddenly popped into her head. “You were away for quite a while. Did you really not see anything at all?”

Qiu Honglei was baffled. “You went over shortly after we went there.”

“Nonsense! You were away for at least ten minutes. That’s why I came over to look for you,” Yun Jianyue refuted.

“That can’t be. You came over shortly after we arrived at Zu An’s room. That’s why Yuqing and Manman were baffled. You can ask them if you don’t believe me.” Qiu Honglei was confused by her master’s remark.

It wasn't without reason that Yun Jianyue was one of the world’s top experts. After a moment of contemplation, she smacked her thigh and exclaimed, “I get it now! There really is something between them!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2685: We Have The Upper Hand
chapters.body:
Chapter 2685: We Have The Upper Hand

“What makes you say that, master?” Qiu Honglei was confused. After what happened earlier, she didn’t harbor any hopes. 

Yan Xuehen appeared to be unfazed by the mundane, and for many years, she had been uninterested in romantic relationships. On top of that, she was Chu Chuyan’s master. It was unthinkable for there to be anything between her and Zu An. 

“Think about it. You were there for ten minutes, but it felt like an instant to you. No one else could have affected your senses under Ah Zu’s watch. Only he himself is capable of that.” Yun Jianyue’s eyes flickered with a brilliant light. 

“Does Ah Zu wield such an ability?” Qiu Honglei was baffled. She had been unconscious for so long that she had no idea what abilities Ah Zu currently wielded.

“He has mentioned many times that he’s the god of this world, and that there’s nothing he’s incapable of.” Yun Jianyue started getting excited halfway through her sentence as if she had discovered uncharted territory. 

“Master, you’re saying…” Qiu Honglei quickly guessed what her master was driving at.

“Indeed. Why would Ah Zu warp your senses if they were innocent? Thinking back, it felt unnatural how distant they seemed,” Yun Jianyue sneered.

“Sect Master Yan is cold-natured; she avoids physical contact with men. Ah Zu is a gentleman, so it would make sense for him to observe propriety,” Qiu Honglei pointed out.

“What poison did that fellow feed you to make you think that he’s a gentleman?” Yun Jianyue huffed. “Besides, it’s not as if he's never hugged that ice sculpture before. Do they need to maintain such a distance between each other?”

“They hugged before?” Qiu Honglei was stunned.

“Of course! He hugged me with one hand and her with the other.” Yun Jianyue had a dangerous look in her eyes. Why didn’t I think about that earlier? If he dares to lay his hands on me, there’s no reason he wouldn’t dare lay his hands on that ice sculpture!

She had been too convinced that nothing could happen between him and that ice sculpture beforer, but that fellow’s flirting skills surpassed common sense. He could very well have his way of dealing with Yan Xuehen.

“Ah?” Qiu Honglei was baffled. A man in the world actually simultaneously held my master and Sect Master Yan in his arms?

“Have you forgotten about it? It was when we had a showdown against Zhao Han in that dungeon. Zu An held us both in his arms, but things were urgent back then, so I didn’t think too deeply into it. There could have been something between them then.” As she spoke, Yun Jianyue got increasingly excited. “Think about it. That ice sculpture is someone who would rather die than violate propriety. I should have known when she allowed Ah Zu to touch her!”

Qiu Honglei was baffled. Hold on, you've already been sharing a husband since then? She still found it hard to believe that the lofty Yan Xuehen would get together with Zu An. “This is all your conjecture. We don’t have any proof.”

“That’s disappointing.” Yun Jianyue paced around the room with a vexed heart. 

“Master, are you sure your ability is that potent? Sect Master Yan looked completely unfazed earlier.” Qiu Honglei was still reeling from the awkwardness of barging into their room earlier. 

“If that fellow helped her, it would have been easy enough for the ice sculpture to overcome this crisis. How dare that fellow collude with the ice sculpture to deceive me? Doesn’t that mean he cherishes the ice sculpture more than me?” The furious Yan Jianyue suddenly looked deflated, and her face turned pale.

Qiu Honglei was horrified. “What’s wrong, master? Did your injuries relapse? I’ll look for Ah Zu right away!”

She was about to rush to the neighboring room when her master grabbed her. 

“I’m fine. It’s just that… I've fought with that ice sculpture all my life, but I still lost to her in the end. And it was a crushing defeat.” Yun Jianyue looked forlorn, as if her life had lost all meaning. 

Do I have to prostrate myself to that ice sculpture in the future? The thought of having to serve tea to the other party made her feel so terrible that she would rather die. Qiu Honglei was already a step behind Chu Chuyan, with the latter claiming the title of Zu An’s official wife. If she lost to the ice sculpture too, that would spell a complete loss for them both, master and disciple!

Yun Jianyue felt so awful that she wanted to hug her disciple and cry, but she was worried the ice sculpture would overhear and make fun of them. 

Qiu Honglei knew about the rivalry between her master and Yan Xuehen, so she could immediately guess how her master was feeling. She chuckled. “Master, you don’t need to be so pessimistic. Things aren’t as bad as you think.”

“What do you mean?” Yun Jianyue grabbed Qiu Honglei’s arm like a drowning man clutching onto a straw of hope.

Qiu Honglei laughed on the inside. My master usually puts on a domineering front, commanding deference from the world. Yet she seems like a fragile-hearted girl here. “Think about it, master. While it’s true Ah Zu didn’t tell you about his relationship with Sect Master Yan, he also didn’t tell Sect Master Yan about his relationship with you.”

Yun Jianyue’s eyes lit up. “Indeed! That ice sculpture would have used that to mock me if she knew about my relationship with Ah Zu! You’re right. That ice sculpture definitely doesn’t know about my relationship with Ah Zu!”

Seeing her master had regained her fighting spirit, Qiu Honglei continued, “Ah Zu chose to withhold the truth from both parties, which shows that he isn’t biased. Rather, now that we've figured out the truth about Sect Master Yan while the latter is still kept in the dark, we have the upper hand!”

The more Qiu Honglei spoke, the angrier she felt. She puts on a holier-than-thou attitude as if the secular world is beneath her, yet she turns out to be a bitch who steals her disciple’s man. 

She snuck a glance at her master and secretly apologized for inflicting collateral damage on her. My master is from the Devil Sect, so it’s understandable for her to do that. But Sect Master Yan is the leader of the orthodox sects, and she often preaches kindness, morality, and propriety. Doesn’t she feel ashamed at all?

Yun Jianyue burst into laughter. “Well said, my good disciple! We have the upper hand now!”

She excitedly hugged her disciple and kissed her. “I didn’t raise you all these years in vain! That ice sculpture’s precious disciple isn’t around to help her. As long as we join hands, she won’t be a match for us!”

Qiu Honglei looked perplexed. Her master had taught her many seduction skills, and they had once boasted that either of them could easily make any man in the world fall head over heels for them. And yet, they now had to join hands to fight for a man. 

Yun Jianyue suddenly thought about something and harrumphed. “Hmph! What’s Ah Zu thinking by keeping both sides in the dark? Does he intend to use this information asymmetry to keep us fighting while he reaps the benefits of both sides?” That thought irked her. 

Qiu Honglei gave the matter some thought before shaking her head. “Ah Zu isn’t that kind of person. He probably doesn’t know how to approach this matter. The world knows you've been fighting with that Yan woman for a lifetime now. If he revealed the truth, it would be one thing for us since we’re from the Devil Sect, but that Yan woman, who prides herself on being the leader of the orthodox sects, might end her life out of shame.”

Yun Jianyue’s eyes lit up. “That’s not a bad idea. Let’s hash it out with that ice sculpture. Let’s see if she can keep up her act!”

Qiu Honglei anxiously stopped her. “We mustn’t do that! If something happens to that Yan woman, Ah Zu will blame us. Taking a step back, what if she simply denies it? We don’t have any evidence, and with her stellar reputation, the world is more likely to believe her than us.”

Yun Jianyue frowned. “You’re right. She's kept up that pretense for so long that it's given her a lot of credibility. The whole world has fallen for her stink act.”

She scoffed, “Our Devil Sect is known for our seduction arts, but I think that ice sculpture’s skills surpass ours. Look at how she's charmed the entire world!”

Qiu Honglei agreed too. The master and disciple continued grumbling about Yan Xuehen for a long time before both of them let out a sigh.

“What should we do next?” Qiu Honglei asked.

Yun Jianyue pondered before replying, “After today’s incident, that ice sculpture and Ah Zu will be guarded against us. We won’t be able to find evidence ourselves.”

“Should we ask Aunt Yuqing for help?” Qiu Honglei asked. 

Yun Jianyue shook her head. “That won’t do. She’s my little sister. They'll be guarded against her, too.”

She spent a moment in thought before asking, “What about Miss Pei?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2686: Coming Clean
chapters.body:
Chapter 2686: Coming Clean

“Manman?” Qiu Honglei frowned. “I don’t think it’ll work. She's been good friends with Chu Chuyan since their time at Brightmoon City. There’s no way she would help us deal with Chu Chuyan’s master.”

“Just because they're good friends doesn’t mean they're on good terms. If they are that close, why would Manman get together with Ah Zu even though he’s married to Chuyan?” Yun Jianyue pointed out. 

“There must be a reason behind that.” Qiu Honglei rolled her eyes. “Didn’t you get together with Ah Zu despite knowing my relationship with him too?”

Yun Jianyue was speechless. Gah, it feels like I've shot myself in the foot. 

Qiu Honglei quickly grabbed Yun Jianyue’s arm and said, “Master, I’m kidding. Manman is your younger cousin, so she should be closer to us. Let’s do as you say and ask her for help.”

Yun Jianyue’s complexion finally improved. This lass would have never dared to joke with me like that in the past. It’s all Ah Zu’s fault!

“You ask her. I’m not close with Manman, and you've known her longer.”

Qiu Honglei was baffled. “She’s your younger cousin! How are the two of you not close?”

Yun Jianyue replied sheepishly, “I can’t shrug off the feeling that she’s a junior. It’s embarrassing to talk about such matters with her. It’s better for you to do it.”

Qiu Honglei belatedly remembered that others still didn’t know about her master’s relationship with Zu An, so she had no choice but to take on the job. I must have owed my master a lot in my previous life. Not only did I get my man taken by her, but I even have to cover her ass. 

So, Qiu Honglei went out and visited Pei Mianman’s room.

Pei Mianman rushed over to support her. “Honglei, you should take it slow. You've only just recovered.”

Her concern brought a smile to Qiu Honglei’s face. “Thank you, Manman, but I am still a cultivator. I’ve been resting too much in recent years, so I’d like to walk around more.”

The two of them exchanged some pleasantries. Pei Mianman sensed that Qiu Honglei had come with a purpose, so she probed about that. 

Qiu Honglei was put on the spot, but she still replied, “Manman, what do you think about Ah Zu and Sect Master Yan?”

Pei Mianman was taken aback. “Why do you suddenly ask that?” Have they figured out Ah Zu’s relationship with Yan Xuehen?

Qiu Honglei didn’t notice her anomalous response. “I just think that they look too distant.”

Pei Mianman breathed a sigh of relief. “Do they?” More like negative distance. 

“Didn’t you see how Ah Zu was sitting five meters away from Sect Master Yan when treating her earlier in the day? I’m worried there’s a conflict between them.” Qiu Honglei wasn't so foolish as to reveal that she suspected them of having an affair right away. 

Pei Mianman scoffed in her heart. I bet Ah Zu didn’t expect his cautiousness to backfire on him. 

“Sect Master Yan values propriety and avoids physical contact with men. Ah Zu probably kept his distance because of that.” Pei Mianman couldn’t help but recall intimate images of the duo as she said those words. Honestly, I can’t understand how Yan Xuehen fell for Ah Zu.

Yan Xuehen hadn't just been putting on an act all these years. She was true to her reputation, but Zu An had somehow managed to melt even the icy mountain.

Qiu Honglei was aware of Yan Xuehen’s reputation, and she knew no one would believe her if she said that Yan Xuehen had seduced her disciple’s man. On the contrary, people would think that she was slandering Yan Xuehen. Thus, she could only remark, “Sect Master Yan is simply too powerful, so much so that my master went all out and accidentally released her desire energy in the duel. We were worried that she would be affected, but fortunately, she looked fine earlier on. Her self-discipline is truly formidable.”

She didn’t voice her suspicion, but instead subtly revealed the key phrase ‘desire energy’. She knew Manman was smart enough to draw a link. She’s Ah Zu’s woman and Chuyan’s best friend. She wouldn’t turn a blind eye to this matter!

Unsurprisingly, Pei Mianman’s complexion darkened. I was wondering why she and Ah Zu looked so formal in the room. I bet something indescribable must have happened in there earlier. They must have used some method to deceive our eyes…

They chatted a while longer before Qiu Honglei took her leave, knowing that Pei Mianman had to be harboring doubts now. 

After she walked out the door, she hid in the corner and secretly observed Pei Mianman. It was only when the latter rushed out of the door that she finally left with a satisfied smile. I am indeed a Devil Sect demoness. Somehow, I feel excited doing this. 

Meanwhile, Pei Mianman finally found Zu An. The latter was sending off a few imperial court officials who had come to visit. 

Zu An smiled and stepped forward with open arms to embrace her. “Manman, you’re here.”

Pei Mianman stepped aside and avoided his embrace. 

“What’s wrong, Manman?” Zu An was taken aback. 

“Ah Zu, I won’t say anything about your philandering ways, but why do you have to get involved with Sect Master Yan, of all people?” Pei Mianman finally let it off her chest.

Zu An replied bitterly, “Would you believe me if I said it happened by coincidence?” He knew Pei Mianman had seen them that day. 

“It doesn’t matter what I think. The question is whether others believe you or not.” Pei Mianman took a deep breath. “How much longer do you intend to conceal this matter?”

“I don’t know.” Zu An sighed in resignation. “You also know Yan Xuehen’s position. It doesn’t matter to me whether this gets revealed or not, but I fear she won’t be able to take the backlash. She might just end her life out of shame.”

“You two should never have overstepped your boundaries!” Pei Mianman was both angry and agitated. 

Yan Xuehen’s reputation was too resounding. She was the leader of the orthodox sects, the incarnation of justice, the divine fairy who practiced the Unshakable Daoist Manual… It would be no exaggeration to say that the world already saw her as a saint.

The higher a person stood, the greater they stood to fall. It would be a huge scandal if she were exposed to be in a relationship with a man, not to mention that it was her disciple’s man. Even Pei Mianman felt stifled thinking about it, let alone Yan Xuehen.

Zu An asked, “Manman, why are you suddenly asking me about this?”

“You shouldn’t have done it if you didn’t want others to know,” Pei Mianman coldly uttered. “I’m here to warn you that others have started suspecting your relationship with her. It won’t be long before they arrive at the truth. You should mentally prepare yourselves for that.”

Then, she left without giving him a chance to ask further questions. She wasn't going to betray Qiu Honglei. 

Zu An fell into deep thought. He could guess what was going on. 

Qiu Honglei and Yun Jianyue barged into the room earlier today to catch us. I was still overjoyed when they patched things up, but to think they would join forces to pull this off right after. 

Did they find out about me and Yan Xuehen?

No, that can’t be. If she had conclusive evidence, Yun Jianyue would have confronted Yan Xuehen right away. They're probably just suspecting it… 

Should I pull both sides together for a show of hands?

No, that won’t do. Their situations might be similar, but Yun Jianyue is from the Devil Sect and has less regard for her reputation. Yan Xuehen is at a disadvantage… 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2687: A Shocking Question
chapters.body:
Chapter 2687: A Shocking Question

It had been a long time since Zu An felt so frustrated over a problem ever since his breakthrough. He felt an unprecedented sense of helplessness. He thought for a long time but couldn’t come up with a solution, so he decided to just take it one step at a time. 

Since Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei suspected Yan Xuehen, Zu An thought he should warn the latter, or else she would be disadvantaged when dealing with them. 

Yan Xuehen was appalled by his return, worrying that others would notice their relationship. However, when she learned the reason behind his return, she was shocked. “Huh? Is there something wrong with the demoness? Why is she doing that?”

She had doubted Yun Jianyue at the start, but she dropped her suspicion after Zu An promised her that nothing was amiss. From the looks of it now, the demoness had indeed come prepared.

“It’s all your fault. She must have started suspecting us after our trip to the Netherworld.” Yan Xuehen thought about the things he had done along the way, and her face turned so red that one could almost squeeze blood out of it.

She couldn’t even stand upright then. Given how sharp the demoness was, she was bound to have sensed that something was amiss. 

“I suspect she intends to drag you down.” Zu An was aware of their relationship, and that allowed him to better guess her thoughts. 

Yan Xuehen calmed down and thought about it. “Yes, you’re right. The demoness is capable of that. She probably plans for us to embarrass ourselves, so she can mock me for that…”

She felt stifled. I should be angry about that, but it just so happens I have an affair with Zu An. The demoness hit the nail on the head by chance!

Zu An couldn’t help but wonder if the two women would gang up against him if they learned about each other’s relationship with him. The ensuing drama that could arise from that made him shudder. 

“What’s wrong?” Yan Xuehen was perplexed by his response. 

“It’s nothing. We should tread carefully for now. She’ll probably let it slip when she fails to find any evidence,” Zu An consoled her. 

“Easier said than done!” Yan Xuehen hissed. “She wields desire energy now. Who knows when she’ll use it against me?”

It finally made sense why she had been so affected earlier.

“You have something similar to void energy too. You fell for her trap because you were unguarded, but now that you know what she’s up to, you should be able to neutralize her desire energy with your void energy,” Zu An replied. 

Yan Xuehen nodded. Fighting spirit blazed in her eyes. “I’d like to see whether my dao is stronger, or hers!” 

Zu An thought, They're truly a match made in heaven. Neither side refuses to admit defeat.

Yan Xuehen suddenly narrowed her eyes. “I’m in too much of a disadvantage if I remain in a passive position. This won’t do. I should fight back too!” She could turn a blind eye to such things when it came to anyone else, but somehow, she couldn’t let herself lose when it came to Yun Jianyue.

Zu An was rendered speechless. Yun Jianyue’s action has already left me flustered. If Yan Xuehen makes a move too, given their wits, they might quickly realize our relationship, and that would spell the end of the world!

He said, “I’ll take her away with me. That way, she won’t be able to deal with you.”

Yan Xuehen turned to him. “Where do you plan to take her?” There was a tinge of jealousy in her voice. 

“Her Corpse Mountain Blood Sea Domain resembles the Netherworld. That would be the perfect excuse to take her on a tour in the Netherworld. She won’t suspect a thing,” Zu An replied. 

“You want to take her to the Netherworld?” Yan Xuehen raised her voice. “That means she would have to project her soul and stick close to you?”

Zu An was astounded. He was in an intimate relationship with Yun Jianyue, so he didn’t think much about embracing her. But to Yan Xuehen, who didn’t know about their relationship, that was overstepping a boundary.

He coughed. “What are you thinking? I’m also bringing Manman with us to observe the Red Spider Lilies. With her around, nothing can possibly happen.”

Yan Xuehen exhaled in relief. The atmosphere won’t be as chummy with someone else around. However, she still felt jealous. “Our previous duel ended with a draw. With her wits, it’s likely that she’ll advance her domain to a higher level after your trip to the Netherworld. By then, I might no longer be a match for her.”

She glared at him grudgingly as if blaming him for helping an outsider against her.

“I’m doing this to stop her from going after you.” Zu An lifted her chin. “Big sister Yan, you look so adorable when you’re jealous.”

“Who’s being jealous?” Yan Xuehen looked away in embarrassment. Why do I keep acting like that in front of Zu An?

To conceal her awkwardness, she changed the topic. “Forget it. Take her to the Netherworld if you want to, but can you leave Miss Jing here to accompany me?”

“Why are you looking for her?” Zu An was worried that a fight would ensue.

Yan Xuehen harrumphed. “I can’t end up lagging behind that demoness. Miss Jing has deep ties with the Dao Gate, and her knowledge runs deep. There are many things I’d like to consult her on. It might help me take a step forward.”

She also wanted to go to the Netherworld to meet Chuyan, but she dropped that thought because she was worried that Yun Jianyue would see through them. I can get Ah Zu to take me to the Netherworld whenever I want anyway. There’s no need for me to go with her. 

Zu An’s heart was put at ease. “That’s a good idea. Jing Teng does command vast knowledge.”

Other than Mi Li, Jing Teng was the most knowledgeable person around him. Her wealth of life experience and experience in suppressing the giant tomb made her a living encyclopedia. Besides, Qiu Honglei was still too frail to head to the Netherworld. It would be good to leave Jing Teng here to take care of her. 

Zu An paid a visit to Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman to inform them about his plan.

Yun Jianyue wanted to plot against Yan Xuehen, but she knew advancing her cultivation was more important, so she agreed with Zu An's decision. Meanwhile, Pei Mianman could guess Zu An’s intentions, but she didn’t hesitate to accept the offer because she wanted to see the Red Spider Lilies in person. 

Thus, Zu An quickly made preparations before taking Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman’s souls to the Netherworld.

Yan Xuehen took this chance to consult Jing Teng about her dao. But Jing Teng didn’t seem interested in engaging with her. 

Yan Xuehen sensed something was amiss and asked, “Miss Jing, is there something on your mind?”

Jing Teng replied, “Miss Yan, I have a question. Do you think a master can share a husband with her disciple?”

Yan Xuehen’s face turned pale. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2688: Suspicion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2688: Suspicion

Yan Xuehen’s face paled in horror. She'd thought that the demoness had only guessed the truth coincidentally, but who could have thought that even Jing Teng, who spent her days in the cave paradise, had figured it out too? Didn’t that mean the other ladies knew about it too? They're probably just keeping quiet about it out of respect for me. 

She felt as if she had been plunged into an ice chamber. She reached for her sword to end her life once and for all. 

Jing Teng eyed her sword and asked, “What are you doing? Even if you’re not on good terms with Yun Jianyue, you shouldn’t make a move against her while her soul isn’t around.”

“Ah?” Yan Xuehen was baffled. 

“I knew I shouldn’t have told you that!” Jing Teng glared at Yan Xuehen as if the latter were a despicable rat who took advantage of other people’s weakness.

Yan Xuehen was bewildered. She noticed Jing Teng’s stockings turning black—it was the younger Jing Teng who was talking to her. 

“You’ve misunderstood. I wasn’t going to make a move on Yun Jianyue.” Yan Xuehen knew the younger Jing Teng was eccentric like a demoness and didn’t want to provoke her.

Black Jing Teng smiled. “I knew you weren’t that kind of person. You wouldn’t resort to such underhanded means even when dealing with someone who stole your disciple’s man.”

Yan Xuehen was speechless. Aren’t I the one who stole my disciple’s man? Who are you referring to?

“I didn’t expect a lofty expert like Yun Jianyue to do something that despicable, but what’s more maddening is how Qiu Honglei let things slip despite it all. She continues to maintain close ties with her,” Black Jing Teng huffed.

“You’re saying Yun Jianyue…” Yan Xuehen narrowed her eyes. It was plenty obvious what Black Jing Teng was driving at. 

Many thoughts surged into her mind, and the doubts she'd previously harbored suddenly made sense. She'd always thought there was something amiss between Zu An and Yun Jianyue—they looked like friends, but they seemed extremely comfortable with each other. She had previously attributed that to Yun Jianyue’s eccentric personality as a demoness and didn’t think in that direction, but if even she could get together with Zu An, there was no reason the demoness couldn’t do so as well. 

Zu An had hugged them both in the dungeon back then, but she hadn’t suspected a thing due to the urgency of the situation. But now that she thought about it, the demoness had never come into contact with other men in her life, let alone allowing someone to grab her waist. 

No wonder Ah Zu maintains a neutral stance and covers up for her. So that’s the reason…

As shock washed over her heart, Jing Teng’s stockings reverted to white. She apologetically said, “Please don’t misunderstand, Miss Yan. My little sister was talking nonsense.”

Yan Xuehen had gotten used to the two sisters’ role switching. “Your little sister wouldn’t make such a remark out of nowhere. You must know something.”

White Jing Teng was put in a spot. Knowing the other party wouldn’t let things slip, she acquiesced and said, “I overheard Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei’s conversation. They were talking about something about stealing their disciple’s man. My little sister might have misunderstood because of that. Unfortunately, we don’t know the details about the matter.”

Black Jing Teng angrily roared, “Why aren’t you telling her the truth? That Yun woman stole her disciple’s man—no, she stole our man too! So what if I rat her out?”

White Jing Teng huffed, “A lady doesn’t gossip about others behind their back.”

Black Jing Teng rolled her eyes. “I've never been a lady. You can keep your holier-than-thou attitude and watch in resignation as someone steals your man!”

“Ah Zu has never been a devoted man. Are you only realizing that now?” White Jing Teng retorted.

Black Jing Teng was at a loss for words. “It’s one thing for Ah Zu to be like that, but what’s the deal with that Yun woman? She’s Honglei’s master! How can she do that? I won’t stand for it!” After years of living and chatting together, she had gotten close to Qiu Honglei.

Looking at her furious little sister, White Jing Teng could only change tactics to convince her. “Haven’t you considered that Ah Zu might blame us if we did something? Not only would Yun Jianyue hate us, but Qiu Honglei might not be thankful either. The other ladies would also be wary of us. We'd become everyone's enemy. There wouldn’t be a place for us.”

Black Jing Teng was taken aback. She didn’t mind being hated by the other ladies, but she was horrified by the possibility of Zu An being averse to her. 

Seeing that her little sister had calmed down, White Jing Teng turned to Yan Xuehen and continued, “This is probably a misunderstanding. I doubt it’s as my little sister says. Miss Yan, please don’t take it to heart.”

Yan Xuehen responded with a strained smile. She chatted a little longer before excusing herself. 

I thought there was an affair between Ah Zu and Yun Jianyue, but it seems like the person Yun Jianyue was talking about is me. In the end, I only went in one big circle. 

I guess there’s no way to continue hiding my relationship with Ah Zu. I should talk to him when he returns. 

But she recalled Black Jing Teng’s words. Could there really be something between Yun Jianyue and Ah Zu? If that’s the case, Ah Zu won’t tell me the truth, and he won’t help me either… 

Yan Xuehen suddenly felt vexed. She decided to have a chat with Xie Daoyun and Ji Xiaoxi. They had been traveling with Ah Zu, so they had a better understanding of what he'd been through in recent years. Unlike Yun Yuqing, those two ladies would be closer to her. 

… 

Meanwhile, Zu An took Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman’s souls to the Netherworld. He instructed the two of them to stay close to him to protect them from the death energy. 

Yun Jianyue snorted. We’re so close that our ears and hair are pressed against each other, and that ice sculpture still says they're innocent?

Pei Mianman didn’t notice these details, as she had something weighing on her mind. She regretted giving a heads-up to Zu An. It felt awkward for her to share a space with Yun Jianyue now.

Sensing the tension, Zu An said, “You don’t have to worry. I can sense anything that happens at the mountaintop residence. Your bodies will be safe.”

Yun Jianyue smiled. “That’s the safest place in the academy, and that ice sculpture is there too. There’s no reason for me to worry.”

Pei Mianman was surprised. “Aren’t the two of you rivals? Will she protect your body?”

“We are enemies, but I acknowledge her character. She wouldn’t resort to despicable means to defeat me. If our positions were switched, I’d protect her body too,” Yun Jianyue replied without any hesitation.

Pei Mianman was baffled. What a weird relationship they have. I don’t know if they're enemies or friends. She was so intent on cornering Yan Xuehen just a moment ago. Not knowing what to do about this situation, she said, “Ah Zu, I’d like to visit Chuyan first.”

That would be an opportunity for her to catch up with Chuyan and consult her on how to deal with this matter. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2689: Coming Clean
chapters.body:
Chapter 2689: Coming Clean

Zu An accepted Pei Mianman’s request. He'd previously been worried about bothering Chu Chuyan, fearing that she would be in greater pain if she woke up, but from their previous interaction, he'd learned that she was happy to wake up and chat with people she knew. 

Yun Jianyue also spoke up. “I’ll join you.”

Pei Mianman looked at her questioningly, causing her face to heat up. I can’t say that we’re all sisters, right? It’ll help to dampen the blow if we at least get familiar with each other. 

Yun Jianyue wasn't afraid of Chu Chuyan, but considering the latter’s special standing, she wanted to avoid unnecessary trouble. To keep Pei Mianman from thinking too much, she explained, “I’d like to see the legendary Bridge of Oblivion.”

Zu An chuckled in his heart. I didn’t expect big sister Yun to have such an adorable side to her. 

The two ladies initially had their minds occupied, but they soon found themselves awed by the Netherworld’s scenery.

“I’d never have expected the legendary Netherworld to be our playground.” Yun Jianyue turned to Zu An. This fellow is extraordinary. No wonder he has so many women charmed. At this point, she suddenly glared at Zu An and asked, “Ah Zu, do you have an affair with that ice sculpture?”

It was much more discreet to communicate via souls, so there was no need to worry about Pei Mianman overhearing them.

Zu An sighed. “Would you believe me if I said no?”

“Of course not!” Yun Jianyue harrumphed. You’d obviously cover up for that ice sculpture if there’s something between the two of you.

“See. Why bother asking me if you won’t believe me?” Zu An sighed.

“Looks like there really is something between you two!” Yun Jianyue sneered.

“There isn’t.”

“I’m sure of it!”

“There isn’t.”

… 

This argument continued until they reached the Bridge of Oblivion.

The two ladies, despite having gone through a lot, were taken aback. The Oblivion River’s blood-tinged yellow river emanated a bloodthirsty aura that made their souls tremble even from a far distance away. They wouldn’t want to approach it even if no one told them about the Oblivion River’s traits.

Taking a closer look, they saw countless warped faces in the water. Those were the vengeful spirits cursed to suffer in the Oblivion River for eternity. But sitting at the center of this terrifying river of death was a sacred lotus flower. A beautiful figure could be vaguely spotted amid its iridescent lights, adding some vibrancy to the oppressive scene.

“Chuyan is too pitiful.” Pei Mianman bit her lips. She'd already heard about Chu Chuyan’s condition, but the shock of witnessing it in person still left her feeling melancholic. 

Even Yun Jianyue, who was accustomed to seeing mountains of corpses and rivers of blood, fell silent. She felt a tinge of regret. Did we go too far plotting against them in the last two days? That ice sculpture is hateful, but her disciple is innocent.

Zu An gently called out to Chu Chuyan. The lotus flower in the Oblivion River blossomed, and its seven-colored divine light gradually expanded. The beauty lying within opened her eyes. 

“Ah Zu!” Chu Chuyan was overjoyed to see Zu An. She hadn’t expected them to reunite so quickly. 

“Chuyan!” Zu An noticed the joy in her eyes and thought that he should visit her more often. 

Chu Chuyan noticed his company and embarrassedly called out, “Sect Master Yun, Manman…” Would they mistake my interaction with Ah Zu as a declaration of dominance?

Yun JIanyue nodded in acknowledgment. 

Pei Mianman excitedly called out, “Chuyan!”

She tried to dash to Chu Chuyan’s side. Horrified, Zu An stopped and warned her, “You mustn’t get afflicted by the Oblivion River, or else it’ll wash away your memories and turn you into one of its countless vengeful spirits.” 

Chu Chuyan offered the same warning too. 

Pei Mianman remarked hoarsely, “Chuyan, you've really suffered.”

She was close friends with Chu Chuyan. While she'd initially had ulterior motives for approaching her, they became friends after years of company. The knowledge that her best friend had to stay in a place like this for a thousand years filled her with irrepressible sorrow. 

Chu Chuyan smiled. “Manman, don’t worry about me. I’m protected by the Pure World Icelotus, and Zu An is taking care of me too. It’s not as bad as you think.”

Chu Chuyan’s consolation only made Pei Mianman feel even more melancholic. 

Yun Jianyue tugged Zu An’s sleeves and said, “Why don’t we let them chat in private?”

She could have used this opportunity to express concern and forge closer ties with Chu Chuyan, but her pride wouldn’t let her do so. There were no ties between them on the surface, so if she proactively approached Chu Chuyan, it would make her seem like the weaker one.

Zu An could also tell that Chu Chuyan and Pei Mianman had many things to talk about, and their presence here was a hindrance, so he imbued Pei Mianman’s soul with a portion of his energy to protect her before taking Yun Jianyue elsewhere.

After those two departed, Chu Chuyan asked teasingly, “Manman, isn’t Ah Zu caring?”

Pei Mianman’s face reddened. It was inappropriate of her to get together with Zu An, considering that he was her best friend’s man. “Chuyan, I…”

Chu Chuyan raised a hand to stop her apology. “Manman, do you think I’d be concerned about this after everything I've been through?”

Pei Mianman thought that made sense. It was the same for her too. The Myriad Worlds were so humongous that such social propriety just felt insignificant in comparison. “Chuyan, I was being narrow-minded.”

Chu Chuyan smiled. They exchanged some pleasantries before she suddenly asked, “I heard Ah Zu met someone known as the Goddess of Wu Mountain in their previous life. Is that your previous incarnation?”

“That thing makes me feel so stifled. I don’t remember anything about my previous life. It feels like Ah Zu had another relationship behind my back, but that person is somehow ‘me’, so I can’t get mad at that…” Pei Mianman began venting to Chu Chuyan. It felt as if they had returned to their old times in Brightmoon City, when they would spill their hearts to each other.

… 

A while later, Pei Mianman suddenly remembered her reason for coming here and asked, “Chuyan, what kind of person do you think your master is?”

Chu Chuyan was taken aback, but she replied with a smile, “My master is a wonderful person. If not for her, my life would have gone down a different path. The world thinks that she’s a lofty ice immortal incapable of human emotions, but I know my master is full of feelings. It’s just that she's sealed away her heart for her cultivation, which makes her appear cold to others.”

Pei Mianman hadn’t expected Chu Chuyan to have such an evaluation of her master. She further probed, “Do you think your master would fall in love with a man?”

“Under normal circumstances, no, but there’s no way to say for certain when it comes to feelings.” Chu Chuyan’s clear eyes seemed to peer right into the truth.

Pei Mianman hesitantly asked, “What kind of man do you think could catch your master’s eye?”

“Someone like Ah Zu,” Chu Chuyan replied.

Pei Mianman was flabbergasted. “You already know about it?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2690: Solution
chapters.body:
Chapter 2690: Solution

Chu Chuyan revealed a faint smile. “You abruptly asked me about my master, and most people wouldn’t talk about my master and romance in the same breath. You wouldn’t have asked such a question if you hadn’t noticed something.”

Pei Mianman felt a mixture of awkwardness and awe. “I was worried you would be hurt, but I didn’t think it would be fair for you to be kept in the dark either. That’s why I hesitated.”

“You’re a good friend,” Chu Chuyan said with a heartened smile. She could tell whether Pei Mianman was trying to drive a wedge between her and her master or was genuinely worried about her.

“How did you know? Did Ah Zu tell you?” Pei Mianman was curious. 

“He doesn’t have the guts,” Chu Chuyan scoffed. “I discovered it myself.”

“You aren’t angry at all?” Pei Mianman was surprised by how nonchalant Chu Chuyan was.

“How can I be angry? The two of them got together because of me,” Chu Chuyan remarked with a sigh. 

Pei Mianman was taken aback. Is Chuyan into being cuckolded? Did she secretly push me and Ah Zu together back then too? Am I just a toy for those two?

Judging from her expression, Chu Chuyan could tell she had misunderstood. She rolled her eyes and said, “What are you thinking? It’s because I was the Snow Lady at a certain point in spacetime!”

Pei Mianman finally realized what Chu Chuyan was getting at. She sheepishly replied, “Ah, I think you mentioned that before.”

“That’s why I only have myself to blame. After becoming the Snow Lady, I lost my memories and hunted them down, leaving them with no choice but to resort to that to rescue me.” Chu Chuyan had been magnanimous when she consoled her master, but it was still a blow to her. That was why she was ranting to her good friend now.

Pei Mianman secretly chuckled. “Indeed, you can’t blame them for what happened.”

“That’s why I feel so stifled. I want to vent but I can’t find anyone to vent to!” Chu Chuyan smacked her thighs whenever she thought about this. 

“So, you’re permitting their relationship?” Pei Mianman was perplexed. “I can’t imagine your master’s reaction to that.”

“She doesn’t know that I know,” Chu Chuyan replied with a sigh. 

Pei Mianman was stunned. You’re wilder than I thought!

Chu Chuyan explained, “You know my master too. She’s lofty and cares about her reputation. I’m worried she’ll do something extreme if this matter is revealed.”

Pei Mianman nodded in understanding. “That’s possible.”

Yan Xuehen was too famous. The world saw her as a paragon of purity. No one would accept her getting into a relationship with a man. 

Had her partner been anyone else, she could have just weathered the storm and it would all eventually pass. But of all people, her partner was Ah Zu, her own disciple’s husband. 

Pei Mianman could already imagine how the world would react. They would say that she appeared pure on the surface but secretly schemed to steal her disciple’s man—making her worse than the Devil Sect’s demonesses.

That was what the world was like. They were much more tolerant of villains than good people. A villain would be praised for doing something good for once, whereas a person who had been good their whole life would be eternally condemned for committing the slightest misdeed. 

“That’s why I hid the truth from my master. I told Ah Zu to find a way to help her come to terms with this.” Chu Chuyan felt tired. She was the one being cuckolded, yet she still had to clean up after them. 

Pei Mianman was stunned. “So Ah Zu knows that you know!” She gritted her teeth in anger. So Ah Zu had Chu Chuyan’s permission all this time. To think I was worried for him!

Chu Chuyan suddenly tensed up. “Was it not Ah Zu who told you about it? How else did you learn about their relationship?”

Pei Mianman told Chu Chuyan about how Yun Jianyue and Qiu Honglei had tried to solicit her help to investigate Zu An and Yan Xuehen’s relationship. 

Chu Chuyan was so agitated that she stood up from the lotus and nearly fell into the Oblivion River. “This is bad! Sect Master Yun is my master’s nemesis. My master will be in trouble if she learns the truth!”

Pei Mianman interjected, “Despite Sect Master Yun’s reputation as a demoness, I don’t think she’s a bad person after getting to know her. I don’t think she’ll corner Sect Master Yan.”

Chu Chuyan paced around the lotus. “Sect Master Yun can be magnanimous at times, but that's toward others and not my master. My master and her are true mortal enemies. For many years, she's failed to outdo my master. She won’t miss a chance to mock my master. My master can remain nonchalant before anyone but her. Faced with Sect Master Yun’s ridicule, I suspect she would rather die than suffer the shame.”

Pei Mianman’s head hurt. “But Sect Master Yun and Honglei are suspecting their relationship, whereas Sect Master Yan knows nothing about the situation. She’s at a disadvantage.” 

Chu Chuyan frowned. She pondered for a while before asking, “Manman, can you help my master? Didn’t Sect Master Yun send you to investigate my master? You placate them for now and find a chance to subtly tip off my master.”

Pei Mianman was conflicted. “Have you forgotten that Sect Master Yun is my older cousin?”

“Aren’t we sisters too?” Chu Chuyan blinked. “Not to mention we’re close friends on top of that. You don’t intend to side with me?”

Pei Mianman was rendered speechless. “Fine, I’ll help you this once. But only this once!” Just thinking about the matter gave her a headache. 

“That won’t do,” Chu Chuyan anxiously spoke up. “It doesn’t seem like Ah Zu has unraveled my master’s concerns yet. You need to help him.”

“How can I help?” 

“Help them normalize their relationship, so my master can be with him openly.”

Pei Mianman glared at Chu Chuyan begrudgingly. “Chuyan, have you forgotten Ah Zu is my man too? You want me to help another woman claim possession of my man?”

“Manman, I know you’re the best. Please, I’m begging you. I’ll owe you a favor. I’ll help you do one thing in the future too,” Chu Chuyan said coyly. 

Pei Mianman was reminded of their days in Brightmoon City. They'd often chatted like that in private too. That softened her heart. “Fine, I’ll give it a try, but this matter is too tricky to deal with. I don’t even know where to start.”

Chu Chuyan fell silent. “I’ve been thinking about ways to normalize their relationship, and I do have an awful idea in mind.”

“What is it?” Pei Mianman was intrigued. 

Chu Chuyan’s eyes gleamed. “What if Sect Master Yun got together with Ah Zu too?”

Pei Mianman was speechless.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2691: Battle Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2691: Battle Plan

Pei Mianman was shocked beyond words. Sister, are you really into being cuckolded? 

Chu Chuyan embarrassedly explained, “Desperate situations call for desperate measures. I've thought hard, but I couldn’t find a way to unravel the knot in my master’s heart. One day, it occurred to me that my master’s biggest fear is Sect Master Yun. If Sect Master Yun were in the same plight, neither of them could mock the other. That would resolve the issue.”

Pei Mianman took in a deep breath. “Chuyan, I want to ask you a question, but I hope you won’t get angry.”

“What is it?”

“Are you helping your master, or do you want Honglei to experience having her man be snatched by her master too?” 

Chu Chuyan’s expression stiffened. It was a long while before she exhaled. “I wouldn’t have realized it if you hadn’t mentioned it. I might really have harbored such a thought subconsciously. Honglei and I are the representative disciples of our respective factions, and we've been competing for the longest time. I never expected that notion to be so deeply embedded within me that I wasn’t even aware of it.”

Indeed. If this matter were to be revealed, my master and I wouldn’t be able to face anyone. Qiu Honglei and I were the top disciples of our respective factions, and there's never been a victor between us. The thought of losing to her here makes me feel uncomfortable.  

Chu Chuyan’s frank confession left Pei Mianman at a loss for words. A long time later, she exhaled deeply and said, “I didn’t think you would harbor the same thought as them. It feels like the so-called 'Holy Maiden' and 'Demoness' titles are just a difference in faction.”

Chu Chuyan smiled. “Human hearts are complicated. How can it be easy for a mind to become truly unshakable?”

“But how can I help you with that?” Pei Mianman felt extremely awkward. The person she had to matchmake here was her lover!

“I would have done it myself if I could leave this place.” Chu Chuyan looked at her nearly transparent body in misery. 

Pei Mianman’s heart shuddered. She has to remain here for a thousand years. That can’t be easy. The least I can do is to help her with her request… It was still awkward, but she figured she ought to do something for Chu Chuyan. “I can help you, but this is unfair to Sect Master Yun. I feel like I’m corrupting a virtuous maiden.”

“Do you think Sect Master Yun doesn’t have feelings for Ah Zu?” Chu Chuyan asked with bright eyes that seemed to see through all facades. 

“Ah?” Pei Mianman was startled. “Do you know something?”

Chu Chuyan shook her head. “I was bored under the Oblivion Bridge, so I began thinking about Ah Zu’s lovers and when each pair got together. I noticed there was something amiss between Ah Zu and Sect Master Yun.”

Pei Mianman was stunned. She hadn’t expected that her usually cold-natured friend would think about such things. “Ah Zu said that you were hibernating.”

“It’s a thousand years. I have more than enough time to hibernate, so I try to wake up every now and then,” Chu Chuyan replied. 

“Aren’t you lonely?” Pei Mianman felt worried for Chu Chuyan, especially when she looked at the terrifying surroundings. 

“I’m used to being alone,” Chu Chuyan replied with a smile. “Besides, it’s interesting to think about those matters.”

Pei Mianman felt conscience-stricken. Did she also think about when I got together with Ah Zu?

“I thought that if even my master could get together with Ah Zu, no one in the world would be safe from Ah Zu’s charms.” Chu Chuyan said. “The first person that popped into my mind was Sect Master Yun. Considering Ah Zu’s personality, do you think it’s possible for there to be nothing between them?”

“Your argument is weirdly convincing.” 

Pei Mianman racked her mind. There was indeed an inexplicable intimacy between Zu An and Yun Jianyue. Given Yun Jianyue’s reputation and position as Qiu Honglei’s master, she had never thought of such a possibility before. 

But on second thought, those factors meant nothing at all. Even Yan Xuehen got together with Ah Zu, for god’s sake!

“We don’t have to resort to forceful measures. If they're innocent, given their cultivation, nothing will happen between them no matter how we scheme.” Chu Chuyan smiled. 

Those words convinced Pei Mianman. She no longer felt guilty. “Since you've been thinking about this for so long, do you have a plan in mind?”

“Not at all.” Chu Chuyan shook her head. She looked at Pei Mianman and said, “I’m leaving it in your hands. With your wits, I’m sure you’ll find the perfect plan.”

Pei Mianman replied, “…Chuyan, are you messing with me now?”

She'd thought Chu Chuyan had already planned everything out, and that she would only need to execute the plan. It turned out that she was both the brains and the brawn of the operation!

Chu Chuyan felt apologetic. “Manman, I’m not messing with you. There’s only so much I can do without knowing the situation on the ground. The two of them are smart people. Whatever I can come up with here won’t escape their notice. But it’s different for you, not to mention you’re Sect Master Yun’s younger cousin. I’m sure you’ll find a feasible solution.”

Pei Mianman rolled her eyes. “So you also know she’s my older cousin!”

“But I think we’re closer.”

“Tsk. Scram!”

… 

While the two of them were chatting, Yun Jianyue was raising the pressure on Zu An. “There must be something between you and that ice sculpture!”

Zu An felt helpless. “You’re overthinking things.”

He was getting goosebumps all over his body. He couldn’t sell out Yan Xuehen unless she agreed to it. It would be the same if Yan Xuehen questioned him about Yun Jianyue too. 

Yun Jianyue was confused. She had questioned him many times along the way, but Zu An persisted in his stance. That made her confidence wane. Still, she persisted. “Do you earnestly have no feelings for that ice sculpture? We might be enemies, but I have to concede that her charm is incredible. Countless men in the world fancy her, you know?”

“But she’s Chuyan’s master, after all…” Zu An replied. 

“Hah! And I am Honglei’s master.”

“Given Sect Master Yan’s personality, do you think it’s possible?”

“I wouldn’t have considered this possibility if it were any other man, but you are a master at this. Who knows whether she’d fall for you?”

“…”

Zu An coughed. “This is a rare chance to explore the Netherworld. You should be focusing on this Blood Sea Hell instead of such unnecessary things. Manman is calling me.” With that, he flitted off.

Yun Jianyue looked at the Blood Sea Hell before her with a frown. 

Zu An flew to the Oblivion Bridge to bring Pei Mianman to the Yellow Spring Road, where she could examine the Red Spider Lilies. 

Just as he was relieved to be freed of Yun Jianyue’s interrogation, Pei Mianman suddenly asked, “Ah Zu, is there something between you and big sister Yun?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2692: It’D Be Rude Not To Return The Favor
chapters.body:
Chapter 2692: It’D Be Rude Not To Return The Favor

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat, but he maintained a straight face. “Why do you ask?”

“Your relationship with Sect Master Yun seems to transcend that of ordinary friends.” Pei Mianman couldn’t tell him that it was Chu Chuyan’s conjecture. 

“We've braved life and death situations in dungeons together. We’re bound to be closer than friends,” Zu An replied with a chuckle. “Don’t think nonsense and focus on the Red Spider Lilies. Let’s see if they can resonate with your Paramita Butterfly.”

He had already gotten numb to the grilling. At this rate, he could get PTSD from this. I have to find a way to resolve this matter as soon as possible, or else it’ll remain a huge landmine.

His composed attitude made Pei Mianman wonder if Chu Chuyan had been overthinking things. But she suppressed her doubt, walked into the field of Red Spider Lilies, and examined the flowers close up. 

Zu An quietly left the area so that she could focus on her cultivation. 

He returned to the Oblivion Bridge to accompany Chuyan. They chatted for a while before he finally asked, “Chuyan, did Manman say something to you earlier?”

“It was just some usual talk between close friends.” Chu Chuyan blinked. “What’s wrong? Did you do something wrong?”

“Of course not…” Zu An replied sheepishly. Maybe I should start revealing the truth about Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue, or else I might go crazy at this rate! His only concern was that the damage to Yan Xuehen would be far greater than to Yun Jianyue. 

Yun Jianyue would be embarrassed, but Yan Xuehen would take the brunt of the blow and be condemned by the world. There was no way Yan Xuehen would be able to withstand the shame. 

Haa, this is the downside of building up a good guy persona. 

Chu Chuyan noticed Zu An’s unnatural attitude but didn’t expose him. Instead, she began chatting with him about other subjects. 

Time swiftly flew by. Zu An eventually had to reluctantly bid Chu Chuyan farewell to take Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman out of the Netherworld, lest its death energy harmed them. 

Along the way, he asked, “How was it? Did you grasp anything?”

Yun Jianyue’s cheeks heated up. Her mind had been too set on Yan Xuehen’s affairs for her to calm down, so she replied ambiguously, “I felt something, but I might need a few more trips.”

Pei Mianman replied, “It’s the same for me too, so I might need more time.” 

Zu An nodded. “Don’t worry about it. I’ll take you here a few more times before I leave. It’ll be best if you can make a breakthrough by then.”

His words reminded them that he could have to head to Dreamland at any time, and that left them with heavy hearts. 

Yun Jianyue harrumphed. “You talk as if you’re leaving a will. It’s just some Dreamland. I’ll charge into even mountains of knives and seas of flames with you.”

Pei Mianman glanced at Yun Jianyue. She wouldn’t have thought much about those words under normal circumstances, but after her earlier conversation with Chu Chuyan, she couldn’t help but think that those words sounded like a confession. 

“It’s too dangerous over there, and only I have an invitation letter. You should focus on your training instead. You might be able to help me in the future,” Zu An consoled Yun Jianyue with a smile. 

Yun Jianyue felt a twinge of guilt. Her lover was about to embark on a dangerous journey, but her mind was still on bickering with the ice sculpture. Hmph! I guess I’ll let things slip if that ice sculpture lowers her head to me. 

Pei Mianman was thinking along the same lines too. Ah Zu is about to leave. I should at least resolve things on this side so he can leave with peace of mind. That made her more motivated to carry out the task Chu Chuyan had entrusted to her. 

Zu An brought the two ladies back to the peak of Mount Yuquan and returned their souls to their bodies. As soon as they opened their eyes, they saw Yan Xuehen.

“Ice sculpture, why are you staring at me?” Yun Jianyue felt awkward. 

Yan Xuehen smiled. “It’s nothing. It just occurred to me that I know very little about you.”

“What a ridiculous thought,” Yun Jianyue scoffed before leaving to look for Qiu Honglei. She was going to ask her disciple if Yan Xuehen had discovered something. 

Zu An was perplexed too. “Sect Master Yan, something feels different about you today.”

“After thinking things through, I’m in a better mood.” Yan Xuehen didn’t mention Yun Jianyue, knowing Zu An wouldn’t admit to it. 

White Jing Teng had shrugged off the matter as her little sister’s misunderstanding, but Yan Xuehen was no fool. After she returned to her room and calmed down, she'd quickly realized that the other party couldn’t have been talking about her. 

There was no reason for Black Jing Teng to have gone through the trouble to tell her that. She had to have other intentions in mind. Thus, Yan Xuehen had deduced that the Jing Teng sisters must have sensed something peculiar between Yun Jianyue and Zu An, and thus put on an act to pit her against Yun Jianyue. 

Well, I shall fall for it!
