Her relationship with Zu An had been suffocating her. She was fond of Zu An, but she was terrified about what could happen if their affair was exposed. Despite her wits, she couldn’t think of a solution to resolve this matter. Her stress had nearly caused her to manifest an inner demon. 

But upon learning that Yun Jianyue could be involved with Zu An, she'd suddenly seen the light at the end of the tunnel.

So the demoness is guilty too! 

Thinking about how they had toyed with her made her clench her teeth in anger. 

How could Ah Zu not tell me about his affair with the demoness?!

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +110… +110… +110…

Zu An was bewildered. Why is she suddenly angry at me? Is she jealous that I spent time with Yun Jianyue and Pei Mianman instead of her?

“The imperial palace sent a letter over. The empress and the empress dowager wish to discuss matters with you. The Qin clan and the Chu clan are also looking for you,” Yan Xuehen said. 

Ah, so it’s because of that. Zu An couldn’t say too much as Manman was still around, so he simply exchanged some pleasantries before taking his leave. 

After he left, Yan Xuehen turned to Pei Mianman and asked, “Manman, is there something on my face?”

“Sect Master Yan looks more ravishing than ever. Did something joyous happen?” Pei Mianman asked with a smile. 

Yan Xuehen’s face reddened. She replied with a tinge of guilt, “Looks are of secondary concern to cultivators. Did you meet Chuyan?”

“I met her. We even talked about you.” Pei Mianman scoffed in her heart. Hah, I see that cultivators are all good actors.

Yan Xuehen’s heart skipped a beat. “What did she say?”

“She said that you’re a good master and that the two of you are on good terms. She told me to take good care of you while she’s away,” Pei Mianman said. 

“Chuyan worries too much. I’m not a child anymore. What’s there to take care of?” Yan Xuehen was perplexed.

“You never know.” Pei Mianman smiled enigmatically before taking her leave. 

Yan Xuehen felt as if Pei Mianman was trying to say something, but she shrugged it off for the time being as there were more important matters for her to attend to. That demoness used her desire energy to scheme against me. It’s time for me to return the favor. 

She had prepared a gift for Yun Jianyue while the latter's soul was out. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2693: Tit For Tat
chapters.body:
Chapter 2693: Tit For Tat

Meanwhile, Yun Jianyue entered Qiu Honglei’s room. The latter woke up from her meditation and welcomed her with a smile. “Master, were there any gains from your trip?”

Yun Jianyue shook her head. “I could hardly calm down thinking about the ice sculpture. I felt some inspiration from observing the Blood Sea Hell, but I couldn’t make a breakthrough.”

“Master, don’t push yourself too hard,” Qiu Honglei consoled with a smile. “Such spurs of inspiration happen by chance. There’s no way to force it.”

Yun Jianyue’s heart warmed up. She hugged Qiu Honglei. “My dear disciple is the best. You know how to console me.”

The two of them had previously hugged often. The master doted on the disciple, and the disciple relished in the master’s care and concern. But now that they'd become sisters, it felt weird for them to continue hugging like this. Thus, they quietly parted from each other with unnatural looks.

Qiu Honglei broke the awkward silence and asked, “Master, did you meet Chuyan?”

“I did…” Yun Jianyue told Qiu Honglei what she'd seen underneath the Oblivion Bridge. 

Qiu Honglei felt wistful. After learning about the latter's plight, she couldn’t bring herself to continue competing with Chu Chuyan anymore. All she felt was sympathy. The thought of spending a thousand years alone under the Netherworld Bridge could send shivers down anyone’s spine.

After learning about what she had to go through, anyone with the slightest kindness in them wouldn’t harbor enmity toward Chu Chuyan. 

“Yan Xuehen is too much, capitalizing on this opportunity to steal her man! How despicable!” Yun Jianyue scoffed.  

Qiu Honglei burst into laughter. My master just can’t stop harping about Yan Xuehen. If not for their long-time grudge, I would have thought they were in love with each other. 

“It would be a surprise if Sect Master Yan were truly in a relationship with Ah Zu.” Even now, Qiu Honglei still doubted Zu An and Yan Xuehen’s relationship. Her impression of Yan Xuehen as a pure and virtuous person was simply too overwhelming. 

“But that ice sculpture behaved weirdly when we returned.” Yun Jianyue thought about her encounter with Yan Xuehen earlier and frowned.

“What happened?”

“I suspect she knows about my relationship with Ah Zu.”

“That can’t be. Only I know about your relationship with Ah Zu, and you wouldn’t tell anyone else.” Qiu Honglei was taken aback. 

“I hope I’m overthinking things. That ice sculpture is no fool. By now, she might have figured out that I did something to her,” Yun Jianyue said grimly.

“You have to tread carefully, master. Don’t turn from the hunter to the hunted.”

“Don’t worry, I’m not that dumb.”

“Master, what should we do next?” Qiu Honglei asked. 

“I've already used desire energy on that ice sculpture once. She might notice it if I make another attempt, and Ah Zu is no fool either. He might have fallen for it the first time, but he’ll realize it if it happens again.” Yun Jianyue frowned. “We have to plan our next move carefully.”

They brainstormed many ideas, but discarded every one in the end. Ultimately, they couldn’t decide on a course of action. 

Seeing that it was getting late, Yun Jianyue retired to her room. She didn’t rest right away, but instead inspected her room. Soon, she noticed a projection mirror fragment planted discreetly in the corner of the room. She would have never seen it if her guard was down.

“That conniving ice sculpture! She planted this thing here to film me and Ah Zu!” Yun Jianyue was glad she had sensed something amiss with the ice sculpture, or else she would have fallen for this ploy. 

She scanned the room once more to make sure there were no other projection mirror fragments. Hmph, I won’t give that ice sculpture a chance to film me with Ah Zu. She crushed the projection mirror and drifted off to sleep.

In the neighboring room, Yan Xuehen suddenly opened her eyes and smiled. That demoness is as sharp as I thought, but she still fell for my trick.

She was indeed the one who'd planted that projection mirror fragment in Yun Jianyue’s room, and she had made sure to hide it in a discreet location. But after years of rivalry with Yun Jianyue, she knew how difficult of a foe the latter was better than anyone.

The projection mirror fragment was a feint to lull Yun Jianyue into a false sense of security. She would have never guessed that the projection mirror fragment was just the start of the trap. As soon as she crushed the projection mirror fragment, the trap went into motion.

Yan Xuehen knew her cultivation was, at most, on par with Yun Jianyue's. No matter how meticulous her ploy was, it would have been unlikely for her to completely escape Yun Jianyue’s detection. She was confident in her skills, but she wasn’t arrogant. Thus, she hadn’t done everything by herself, and had instead sought Jing Teng, Xie Daoyun, and Qi Yaoguang’s help. 

Of course, Jing Teng wouldn’t admit she was trying to deal with Yun Jianyue, making Yan Xuehen think, Those two are always playing good cop, bad cop; it’s hard to tell what they're thinking.

She'd also consulted Xie Daoyun and Qi Yaoguang for this matter. The former’s aptitude in formations surpassed hers, whereas Qi Yaoguang could provide a mysterious oracular energy that made the formation even more unfathomable.  Neither Xie Daoyun nor Qi Yaoguang had thought too much about what Yan Xuehen was up to because of her virtuous image. If anything, they were flattered that she was consulting them, and they'd naturally given their all to assist her. 

Therefore, Yun Jianyue wasn't just up against Yan Xuehen alone, but the combined powers of the Jing Teng sisters, the formation master Xie Daoyun, and the mystery master Qi Yaoguang. It wouldn’t be surprising for her to be caught in their trap. 

Indeed, Yun Jianyue thought she'd foiled Yan Xuehen’s ploy, so she lowered her guard and went to sleep. She descended into a dream that Yan Xuehen had meticulously woven for her. 

In her dream, she saw the sky split apart, and a peculiar ship descended. An envoy on the ship claimed that he would bring Zu An to Dreamland. Everyone was reluctant to part with Zu An, but the envoy suddenly declared that Yun Jianyue was one of the guests as well. It turned out catching the gaze of a universal deity had won her an invitation letter too.

She was surprised and overjoyed. She even shot a provocative look at Yan Xuehen, and the latter’s look of defeat filled her with glee. I finally outdid her at something!

She boarded the ship with Zu An, and the two of them set off to Dreamland. Along the way, they were attacked by an Annihilation Beast. It was comparable to the one who destroyed the Monster World; it made quick work of the ship crew. 

She fought hard against the Annihilation Beast with Zu An and managed to drive it off, but the fight left Zu An severely injured. He was on his last breath. 

Yun Jianyue wept uncontrollably. “Ah Zu, what do I have to do to save you?”

“Dual cultivation…” Zu An only managed to utter those words before falling unconscious. 

Yun Jianyue caught his drift and began taking off her clothes. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2694: Confession
chapters.body:
Chapter 2694: Confession

Meanwhile, Yan Xuehen was in the neighboring room. The air before her rippled like water before forming a mirror. The images in the mirror reflected what Yun Jianyue was experiencing in her dream. 

Even Yan Xuehen was awed. I’d have never been able to pull this off without Jing Teng, Xie Daoyun, and Qi Yaoguang’s help. A person reacts the most genuinely in an illusion like this. 

She tightly grabbed her sleeves. She had never felt so nervous before. On one hand, she could claim the upper hand against the demoness if she could acquire evidence of Yun Jianyue’s affair with Zu An. On the other hand, she was worried that there was really something between Yun Jianyue and Zu An.

Her relationship with Zu An defied convention and left her constantly nervous, but it also filled her with bliss. There were even times when she felt a sense of superiority over Yun Jianyue. But that meant nothing if Yun Jianyue was with Zu An too. The thought of sharing a man with the demoness made her feel worse than death.

The scenes revealed in the mirror made her breathing hasten. 

The relationship between Yun Jianyue and Zu An clearly transcended friendship, but again, given that they'd only had each other in a foreign land, it was normal for them to be more intimate than usual. 

When the scene cut to Zu An getting severely injured by the Annihilation Beast and needing dual cultivation with Yun Jianyue to survive, Yan Xuehen's heart pounded nervously. She wanted to see what Yun Jianyue would do here. 

When Yun Jianyue unhesitatingly took off her belt, Yan Xuehen's heart nearly leaped out of her mouth. Could they be…

In the other room, Yun Jianyue suddenly stopped her movements. She sensed something was amiss, but she couldn’t put her finger on it. There seemed to be something wrong, but when she thought back on it, everything appeared to make sense.

She glanced at the unconscious Zu An and fell into deep thought.

… 

In the imperial palace, the Empress Dowager and Bi Linglong were overjoyed by Zu An’s visit and welcomed him warmly. 

Zu An initially chatted joyously with them, but when he learned that neither of them had invited him over, his expression suddenly collapsed. But Yan Xuehen said… 

He closed his eyes and focused his senses. He felt a peculiar formation pulsating from the residence on top of Mount Yuquan. He immediately understood what was going on. 

He felt angry but amused. It turned out Yan Xuehen had sent him away so he wouldn’t get in the way of her ploy. Who could have thought the upright Yan Xuehen would play such a trick?

I can’t return right away and expose Yan Xuehen, or else it could wound her reputation and evoke suspicion. After some thought, he decided to send a voice transmission to Yun Jianyue. With his current cultivation, he could easily pull that off without alarming Yan Xuehen.

Yun Jianyue already harbored doubt about this dream, and Zu An’s reminder further convinced her that this was a ploy. Her face warped murderously. “Who dares to make a fool out of me?!”

A devastating blood sea and blood moon manifested around her and frenziedly destroyed everything in the vicinity. 

Such illusion formations only worked if the other party had their guard down. Once the target realized it was just an illusion, the formation could no longer trap her. Thus, Yan Xuehen’s mirror shattered. 

Yun Jianyue traced the ki pulsation to her room and was infuriated. She charged at Yan Xuehen while roaring, “Ice sculpture! I didn’t expect you to be this despicable!”

Yan Xuehen was bewildered. I was just a step off. Why did she suddenly regain composure?

“Hah, who could have expected the proud master of the Devil Sect to be willing to use her body to save someone? Have the two of you been together all this time?” Yan Xuehen was also livid when she thought about what had happened nearing the end of the illusion.

The sheer feeling of shame further fanned Yun Jianyue’s anger. I nearly fell for this woman’s ploy! “Hah, when did I do that? Someone who got together with her disciple’s man shouldn’t be maligning others!”

“Nonsense!”

“Back at you!”

The two of them exchanged jabs while crossing blows. The residence was protected by formations, but how could those formations withstand the shockwaves produced by these two top-notch experts? It didn’t take long for them to destroy the building.

Qiu Honglei, Pei Mianman, and the others rushed over and were horrified. “Master (Cousin Yun), Sect Master Yan, what are you doing?!”

Even the academy’s teachers also rushed over. Now that outsiders were around, both women tacitly chose not to mention Zu An anymore and instead focused on crossing blows. The entire residence was soon leveled. 

Many people from the academy felt a pinch in their hearts. This residence had been personally built by the old libationer, and many considered it to be a sacred place. To think it would be destroyed like that!

But no one dared to complain about it. The two women were too terrifying. Their enmity was well-known, and no one wanted to get on their bad side. 

Fortunately, a seven-colored light descended from the sky shortly after. Zu An had returned. 

“Libationer!” Those from the academy were relieved. If you'd taken any longer, our academy would have been destroyed by those two dinosaurs!

With Zu An standing between them, both Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue stopped their attacks and looked away with a harrumph. It was as if they couldn’t hold themselves if they saw each other’s faces.

“What happened?” Zu An asked. 

“You ask her!” Yun Jianyue huffed.

“Says the guilty one!” Yan Xuehen scoffed coldly.

“What do I have to be guilty about?”

“You know it deep down.”

“You think you’re in the right after scheming against me?”

“I’m merely returning the favor.”

… 

Xie Daoyun and Qi Yaoguang were baffled. Ah! Sect Master Yan solicited our help to deal with Sect Master Yun!

They'd been flattered when Yan Xuehen knocked on their doors. They had always looked up to her, so they naturally did everything they could to help her. Who could have thought she would use them against Sect Master Yun?

Mount Yuquan’s residence ended up getting destroyed because of that. We have a responsibility to pay for that. 

Zu An sighed. He restored the residence with a wave of his hand. 

The crowd had already witnessed plenty of his methods before, but this sight still made their eyes turn red in enthusiasm. They excitedly asked him how he'd pulled it off.

Zu An settled the crowd down before calling Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue over. He intended to mediate between them, but the two women ignored him. They were both already convinced he was in a relationship with the other woman and would side with her, so they returned to their rooms and slammed the door. 

Zu An felt his head hurt. 

Pei Mianman secretly laughed under her breath. “Ah Zu, I’ll talk to them on your behalf.”

“I’ll be troubling you.” When it came to matters of the heart, Zu An’s strength was of no use. 

Pei Mianman first made her way to Yun Jianyue’s room. Since they were cousins, the latter didn’t turn her away. 

“There must be something between that ice sculpture and Ah Zu, or else she wouldn’t use such a method to test me.” Yun Jianyue gritted her teeth in anger.

Pei Mianman thought about what Chu Chuyan had told her. She took a deep breath and decided to get to the point. “Big sister, let’s put Sect Master Yan aside for now. Are you in a relationship with Ah Zu?”

Yun Jianyue immediately panicked. “Don’t talk nonsense, Manman. How can there… possibly be anything between me and that fellow? I’m talking about that ice sculpture now.”

Pei Mianman sighed. “Big sister, you can’t cover fire with paper. Can’t you tell me the truth?”

Yun Jianyue’s face flickered with several emotions. Seeing that things had gotten to this point, she decided not to argue her way out of it and instead asked, “How did you find out about it, Manman?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2695: A Mess
chapters.body:
Chapter 2695: A Mess

No words could describe the intensity of Pei Mianman’s feelings. She'd just been giving it a try, but who could have expected Yun Jianyue to admit to it?

So I am a fool! Chuyan noticed it from so far away, yet I didn’t realize a thing at all despite being right by Ah Zu’s side!

To add insult to injury, Yun Jianyue is my older cousin! But I didn’t sense a thing at all!

That darned Ah Zu! He’s already with Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen, but he didn’t utter a word and let them fight it out!

You have successfully trolled Pei Mianman for +666… +666… +666…

She forced a smile and said, “Cousin, don’t underestimate a woman’s sixth sense.”

She was rational enough to not reveal that she'd heard it from Chuyan, or else the other party would surely blow up. She hesitantly continued, “Does Honglei already know about this?”

Yun Jianyue looked awkward for a brief moment before replying with a subtle “Mm.”

Pei Mianman was stunned yet again. A while later, she sighed and said, “The two of you must be close.” How can you not be close? You’re already close enough to share a man!

However, after having spent so much time with Qiu Honglei, she couldn’t say that she was surprised to hear that answer. She knew how much Yun Jianyue meant to Qiu Honglei.

But that only made her more stifled. So I’m the only one kept in the dark!

“Honglei is a good child. I've let her down.” Yun Jianyue sounded guilt-ridden. 

Pei Mianman scoffed in her heart. And I’m your younger cousin, remember? You secretly hooked up with my man. Shouldn’t you feel guilty towards me too? 

But it was already chaotic enough without her sticking her foot into it, so she decided to let the master and disciple sort it out by themselves.

Yun Jianyue nervously grabbed Pei Mianman’s hand and said, “Manman, who else knows about this? I don’t mind being embarrassed, but I can’t let Honglei be mocked alongside me.”

“Don’t worry, I’m the only one who knows.” Pei Mianman knew what she was worried about. “At this point, Sect Master Yan only suspects it. She isn’t certain about your relationship with Zu An.”

Yun Jianyue breathed a sigh of relief. As long as that ice sculpture doesn’t know. She quickly added, “Manman, you must know why I requested your help last time now. You have to help me, or else Honglei and I will be in trouble.”

Pei Mianman hesitated for a while but eventually decided against revealing Yan Xuehen’s relationship with Zu An. That would cause so much damage to Chu Chuyan and Yan Xuehen that she had to think things through first.

“Let me calm down first.” She waved her hand as she left the room in a daze. 

Chuyan asked me to deal with Yun Jianyue, whereas Yun Jianyue asked me to deal with Yan Xuehen. Does no one remember Ah Zu is my man too?! What the hell is this?!

She headed straight to Zu An’s room. She was going to have a good talk with the culprit behind this huge mess. 

“Manman, why were you so angry earlier?” Zu An stepped forward to embrace her, only for Pei Mianman to shove him aside and glare at him coldly. 

Zu An was perplexed. “Did something happen?”

“You should know better than anyone else!” Pei Mianman glared at him with so much rage that it wouldn’t be surprising if she ripped off his flesh.

“So you know about it.” Zu An knew she had come from Yun Jianyue’s room, so it wasn’t hard for him to figure out what had happened. 

Pei Mianman couldn’t take it anymore. “How could you get together with Sect Master Yun and Sect Master Yan? They're Honglei and Chuyan’s masters! What the hell are you thinking?!”

“The heart defies logic.” Zu An didn’t explain how they had ended up together. 

Pei Mianman retorted, “…Big words, but you’re just a pervert, aren’t you?”

Zu An was silent.

Pei Mianman suddenly tensed up. “Hold on. You didn’t do anything with my master, did you?”

Zu An was dumbstruck.

His bewildered expression made Pei Mianman burst into laughter. I’m overthinking things. My master might have been a beauty in her younger years, but she’s no longer this fellow’s type anymore. She quietly asked her master for forgiveness in her mind. 

She was feeling extremely conflicted now. On the one hand, she was glad she didn’t have to face the same dilemma that Chu Chuyan and Qiu Honglei had gone through. On the other hand, she was worried that if those two cooperated with their masters, she would be at a disadvantage.

She quickly shook those thoughts away from her mind and asked, “What do you intend to do now? How are you going to settle this?”

Zu An looked lost. “I've been conflicted about this too.”

“What’s there to be conflicted about? Call them together and thrash it out!” Pei Mianman harrumphed. “Can’t you see they're already pouncing on each other, all because of you? If you let things continue at this rate, there’ll be no way to resolve this.”

Zu An’s reluctant expression brought a frown to Pei Mianman’s face. She continued, “Do you enjoy seeing them compete over you so you can relish in the benefits? You bastard!”

Zu An angrily replied, “Do I look like that kind of person? I've considered that. It might be possible for Yun Jianyue—she’s from the Devil Sect and doesn’t conform to conventions, so the world won’t mock her for this—but what about Yan Xuehen? She would have to endure not just Yun Jianyue’s mockery, but the world’s scorn too.”

Pei Mianman was taken aback. She'd forgotten about that issue. So that’s what he’s worried about. I misunderstood him. 

She thought hard but couldn’t think of a solution, and that vexed her. “Of all things, why did Yan Xuehen have to build up a pure, virtuous persona?” If you went with that persona, you should have never snatched my man!

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. “Manman, you don’t know how frustrated I was, being unable to find a person to confide in. It's wonderful to have you strategize for me.” 

“Who’s strategizing for you? I’m only helping because Chuyan asked me to!” Pei Mianman blurted out in a fit of anger.

“Chuyan?” 

Pei Mianman clasped her mouth, realizing she had accidentally given away crucial information. There was no point hiding it anymore, so she told him about Chu Chuyan’s request.

Zu An was moved. “Chuyan…”

“You should be glad to have a wife like her.” Pei Mianman harrumphed.

Zu An knew she was jealous, so he embraced her and said, “It’s my fortune to have your company.”

Pei Mianman wanted to maintain a cold expression, but Zu An was so skilled that she soon succumbed and started laughing. “Stop messing around. It’s itchy…”

They wrestled for a bit before resuming their discussion.

“Why don’t I talk to big sister Yun first? She’s a good person at heart. I don’t think she’ll corner Sect Master Yan,” Pei Mianman proposed.

“Are you certain she’ll spare Yan Xuehen?”

“…”

Pei Mianman thought about their long-time grudge. Yeah, it’s unlikely my cousin would hesitate to use this against Sect Master Yan. Even if my cousin held back, Sect Master Yan could feel insulted that she was ‘spared’ by her rival. Who knows what extreme things she could do?

This frustrated her a lot. She exclaimed, “You deal with your mess!”

She turned around and left. He’s the one reaping the benefits. Why should I clean up after him?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2696: The Corruption Of The Fairy And The Demoness
chapters.body:
Chapter 2696: The Corruption Of The Fairy And The Demoness

After the heat of her anger faded, Pei Mianman slowly calmed down. Chuyan entrusted this task to me. I don’t want to let her down. So, she began contemplating a plan in her room.

Unlike the others, her spectator viewpoint allowed her to see things from an unbiased perspective. It didn’t take her long to grasp the crux of the matter. 

Qiu Honglei and Chu Chuyan have accepted their masters’ actions. Yun Jianyue might use this against Yan Xuehen, but that could still be kept under control. The real problem is how the world would react to this matter. 

Yan Xuehen had always been a righteous figure. If this matter became public, she would be condemned, and given her personality, there was a good chance she would end her life to cleanse her shame. 

Since that’s the case, we just have to control the story. 

A thought popped into her mind. If Yan Xuehen would be condemned here, what if we just move elsewhere? Ah Zu can take them to the Fiend side, where the culture is more open. Yan Xuehen might be less reserved there. 

Ah Zu hasn’t seen the little Fiend Empress, Yu Yanluo, and Xue’er for some time anyway…

That thought filled her heart with jealousy. It’s one thing for me to help other women court my man, but to have to push him into the arms of other women on top of that… Unlike Chuyan, I'm not into cuckolding. 

She immediately dropped the idea. Since they have to remain here, I can only try the other plan…

A few days passed. Zu An was meditating in his room when an anxious Yan Xuehen suddenly knocked on his door. He was perplexed. It was rare to see such an expression on her. He asked, “What happened?”

Yan Xuehen quickly shut the door, took out a book, and passed it to him. “Take a look at this!”

Confused, Zu An took the book and saw it was one of the trendy novels sold on the streets. 

Its title was ‘Fairy Plummets to the Secular World—The Regent Claims the White Jade Sect’s Master and Disciple’. Its cover depicted two cold ladies. They didn't look anything like Yan Xuehen and Chu Chuyan, but the similarity in their dispositions was uncanny. Anyone looking at the cover would immediately be reminded of Yan Xuehen and Chu Chuyan. 

Zu An flipped open the novel, and it only further affirmed his guess. It showed how he'd first courted Chu Chuyan, and later, how he'd coincidentally gotten together with Yan Xuehen. 

His eyebrows shot up. He had to concede that the author had done a good job of stoking the readers’ emotions. Its content was mostly guesses, but it was somewhat close to the truth. What a prodigy!

But seeing how agitated Yan Xuehen was, he consoled her. “Don’t worry, there are many such novels circulating in the market. There are also books about the empress and empress dowager too. No one will take them seriously.”

“It’s different!” Yan Xuehen’s face reddened. “There have been books about us in the past, but those were obviously fake. No one would take them seriously. But it’s different this time.”

She snatched the book over and flipped the pages. “This, this, and this… It’s exactly what happened to us!”

Zu An took the novel back and carefully scanned it with his divine sense. Only then did he realize that the novel was meticulously written to mix fact and fiction. Outsiders would think it was a mere story, but the involved parties knew better. 

“Some of the guesses just happened to hit the mark. Most of the content is still gibberish,” Zu An continued. 

“No, the author must be someone around us. Someone familiar with us.” Yan Xuehen clenched her jaws. “It could be that demoness…”

Before she could finish her sentence, the door was suddenly blasted open. Yun Jianyue marched into the room with a book in hand. “Ah Zu…”

She noticed Yan Xuehen and quickly swallowed her words. Instead, she asked, “Ice sculpture, what are you doing here?”

Yan Xuehen keenly noticed Yun Jianyue calling Zu An ‘Ah Zu’. They must be in an intimate relationship. She sneered, “Why can’t I be here? What are you doing here then?”

Yun Jianyue instinctively hid the book behind her. “Hmph! Why should I explain myself to you?”

Her action couldn’t escape her nemesis’ notice. Yan Xuehen exclaimed in shock, “Did you also…”

She belatedly realized how inappropriate her words were and bit her tongue. 

Yun Jianyue also belatedly noticed the book in Zu An’s hands and exclaimed, “Someone wrote a story about you too?” She snatched the book from Zu An’s hand. 

Yan Xuehen also snatched the book that Yun Jianyue was hiding. She was taken aback when she saw the book title: 'The Demoness’ Corruption—The Regent Conquers the Master and the Disciple at the Lake of Hatred.'

Yun Jianyue’s eyebrows also shot up when she saw the book title, but she was more curious about the book’s contents, so she quickly browsed through it. The same went for Yan Xuehen. 

Soon, they had indescribable looks on their faces. 

Yun Jianyue harrumphed. “You sneaky ice sculpture! I didn’t expect that beneath your pure and virtuous cover, you were so licentious!”

Yan Xuehen felt a mix of awkwardness and shame, but she didn’t let it show. “Hmph, this book about you goes even further. Is it all true?”

Yun Jianyue was rendered speechless. She couldn’t possibly admit to it before finding concrete evidence of Yan Xuehen’s relationship, so she argued back, “This book is elaborately described. I doubt such details could have come out of nowhere.”

Yan Xuehen obstinately held onto her point. “Are you really in that kind of relationship with him?”

Yun Jianyue was vexed. She wanted to admit it and then use it to mock Yan Xuehen. They might have done the same thing, but by virtue of their positions, Yan Xuehen would be more humiliated than her. 

However, she gave up on that notion, fearing that Yan Xuehen would refuse to confess to it. That would put her in a bad position. So, she turned to Zu An and asked, “You tell us who’s in a relationship with whom!”

Yan Xuehen glared at Zu An, warning him not to spout nonsense. 

Zu An was vexed. “Those money-minded merchants probably came up with this nonsense based on hearsay. Don’t take it to heart. I’ll investigate this matter and clear your names.”

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue exchanged looks. They felt a twinge of guilt. How can our names be cleared when these are all true? But neither one could lower her head in front of her nemesis.

Yan Xuehen scoffed, “Regent, I hope you can deal with this matter promptly to uphold the White Jade Sect’s honor.” She flung her sleeves and left. 

Yan Xuehen’s reaction felt so cold and distant that it made Yun Jianyue doubt herself. Could my guess be off the mark?

That said, she couldn’t be outdone here. “Hmph, our sacred sect also has a reputation to uphold! If you can’t resolve this, I’ll have my men look into it and let the culprit have a taste of our sect’s methods!” With that, she also marched away in a huff. 

Her practically tangible killing intent confused Yan Xuehen. Could there truly be nothing between them?

Regardless of how they viewed this matter, the novels had already spread throughout the capital. Everyone was talking about them. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2697: The Source
chapters.body:
Chapter 2697: The Source

If this world had Google, the two novels would have been the most searched topics in recent days. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were among the strongest experts in the world. One was the top figure of the orthodox sects, whereas the other was the master of the Devil Sect. Furthermore, they were both incredible beauties who commanded great popularity. If Yu Yanluo was the dream lover of the secular world, the two of them were the dream lovers of the cultivation world. 

Yun Jianyue had many fans. She was the number one goddess in the Devil Sect and the underground world. There were even some in the orthodox sects who had been charmed by her. It wasn't without reason that she was known for her seduction arts. 

Countless people had had their hearts broken when she first made her debut as a cultivator. They had known she was a venomous rose, but she was so alluring that they couldn’t stop themselves from falling for her. Who knew how many people fantasized about her in the dead of night?

But it would be heresy for someone from an orthodox sect to fall in love with someone from the Devil Sect, let alone its master. Thus, no one dared to reveal their crush for Yun Jianyue in public, resulting in her appearing to be slightly less popular than Yan Xuehen. 

Either one becoming taken would have been explosive news, but to think that they would both be taken at the same time... And to the same man, no less! What was even more shocking was that the man was their disciples’ man too! This was a massive scandal of unprecedented proportions!

Everyone in the capital was shocked beyond words. The first thing they asked each other upon meeting was, “Have you read it?” 

In taverns, people argued with puffed cheeks.

“It must be fake! It has to be fake! How can a fairy like Sect Master Yan fall for a man?”

“She might be a fairy, but she’s still a woman. The regent is the greatest man in the world. It isn’t surprising for her to fall in love with him.”

“It’s one thing if it’s any other man, but the regent is her disciple’s husband! How can she steal her disciple’s man?”

“What’s so surprising about that? Take a look at the historical annals! It’s common and even praised for sisters to share a husband in imperial harems and noble clans. Why can’t a master and a disciple share a husband then?”

“Such a line of thought is depraved! You’re a scourge to society!”

“You’re being immature, clutching onto meaningless values. Do you think the guy is taking advantage of them? Across history, those who joined the imperial harem or married into a noble clan as sisters did so to tie down the man.” 

“Given the regent’s unprecedented accomplishments and power, it isn’t a stretch to say that he's the most powerful man in history. He’s so famous as a philanderer that the White Jade Sect’s Holy Maiden can’t tie him down alone. That’s why Sect Master Yan had to join the fight to boost their chances at victory. You’re merely looking at the surface!”

“Sure, you’re the only one who gets it. Do you think Sect Master Yan is bothered by such secular things?”

Due to their differences in opinion, both sides quickly came to blows. Supporters of both sides stepped forward to help out, causing the tavern to devolve into chaos.

… 

Such incidents were occurring throughout the capital and its surrounding provinces. The contents of the novels were being swiftly propagated all over the world. 

In the Chu manor, Chu Youzhao angrily exclaimed, “To think I admired and respected Sect Master Yan so much! I didn’t think she would steal my big sister’s man while my big sister isn’t around!”

Chu Huanzhao shared the same thought. “Indeed! Our brother-in-law is just too outstanding. Those vixens have been finding ways to throw themselves at him.”

The thought of losing her brother-in-law to those women made her so angry that she flailed the Wailing Whip, causing the air to crack. Had she not known she wasn’t a match for them, she would have confronted Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue.

Murong Qinghe interjected, “Those are just rumors. They might not necessarily be true.”

“Which darned merchant came up with these novels? I’ll let them have a taste of my whip if I ever get my hands on them!” Chu Huanzhao flailed her whip around as she grumbled. “They wrote so much about my brother-in-law. Why aren’t they writing about my little aunt then?”

Chu Youzhao blurted out, “Who says they aren’t? There are plenty of novels like…” She nearly ran back to her room to take out her prized collection.

Chu Huanzhao harrumphed. “Those are old books! There hasn’t been any decent work in the past two years. It's been all about other women.”

Murong Qinghe felt perplexed. She should have gotten angry listening to her ‘husband’ discussing this, but somehow, the thought of her brother-in-law made her blood rush. My brother-in-law is a great man. It’s not a surprise that so many women have fallen for him.

In the mountain behind the academy, a few teachers were gathered in a secret chamber to discuss something important.

Qi Yaoguang was awed. These seniors and juniors of mine usually have their hands full with their research. It’s no easy feat to gather them together, and there are usually latecomers. Yet everyone arrived on time today. They must be dying to gossip.

Seeing how her austere seniors were too awkward to kickstart the conversation, Qi Yaoguang decided to break the ice. “Do you think the rumors about the libationer and Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun are true?” 

“It’s just nonsense made up by those booksellers for money,” Seventh Senior Wang Shuyang said with a dissatisfied look. He was an honest person, and Zu An’s pointers had helped him open new grounds in agriculture. That led him to think that Zu An was being maligned. 

“That’s not necessarily the case.” Yin Shi stroked his beard. “The content is too vivid to be based on just sheer imagination. There are even details we didn’t know about, yet it makes perfect sense. Don’t you find that intriguing?”

“Hmph! What’s there to be intrigued about? It must be real! That little pervert…” Second Senior Ji Dengtu had to swallow his remaining words when he found himself glared at by the others. “It’s not as if you don’t know his personality. He wouldn’t give up on women as beautiful as Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue.”

Zu An had used a lot of his paper to produce top-quality erotica back then. To be honest, the current novels in the market were just repetitions of the works Zu An had produced back then. If you ask me, it might be possible that Zu An wrote those novels himself to let the news out. 

I have to keep Xiaoxi away from him. Someone as innocent and pure as Xiaoxi can’t possibly beat a love veteran like him!

“But Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun aren’t just anyone, not to mention their disciples are in a relationship with the libationer. This is… unthinkable.” Fifth Senior Hei Baizi spun a go piece between his fingers, looking visibly shaken up.

The wheelchair-bound Fourth Senior Shen Xuzi suggested, “Why don’t we just ask him straight?”

The crowd rolled their eyes. Shen Xuzi has been spending too much time around puppets.

Qi Yaoguang turned to Yan Xiangu. “Big senior, why don’t we ask Daoyun?”

Xie Daoyun was in a relationship with Zu An, and they had been together for some years. 

“I’m not sure if that’s a good idea…” Yan Xiangu said, but he still ignited a talisman paper to summon Xie Daoyun over.

The others laughed in their hearts. Who could have thought our austere big senior would be curious about this too?

Xie Daoyun soon arrived at the scene. Her face turned red when she heard their question, and she exclaimed, “I don’t know!”

… 

In the imperial palace, Bi Linglong’s expression was colder than the night of the winter solstice. “Have you tracked down the source of those novels yet? Who is behind them?!”

“Yes, we have tracked down the source.” Zhang Zitong had the weirdest look on her face. “But they seem to be from the academy.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2698: Can’T Miss The Opportunity To Mock
chapters.body:
Chapter 2698: Can’T Miss The Opportunity To Mock

Bi Linglong was speechless. She had thought of countless possibilities, such as a plot to besmirch Zu An by the imperial clan’s survivors, or the Shadow Group, or the Hub of Freedom, but it had never crossed her mind that the books would be from the academy.

The academy was Zu An’s territory! Everyone there respected him, and it was unthinkable for anyone there to pull this off. 

“I thought I made a mistake too, but after capturing several booksellers, I was forced to conclude that the books are indeed coming from inside the academy. They each thought they were the exclusive publisher, so they worked overnight to release more goods in the market,” Zhang Zitong explained.

“Did you find out who in the academy is behind this?” Bi Linglong asked.

“That person is highly elusive. They concealed themselves, so none of the booksellers know their identity. It appears that they are a formidable cultivator,” Zhang Zitong reported with a frown. It looked as if she had a theory in mind, though.

Bi Linglong knew what Zhang Zitong was getting at. The academy was Zu An’s territory; no one would dare work against him there. 

Is the culprit Ji Xiaoxi or Xie Daoyun… It can’t be Ji Xiaoxi. I've only met her a couple of times, but she doesn’t seem capable of this. Her father is capable of it, but I heard he’s a slave to his daughter. It’s unlikely he would do this. 

That makes Xie Daoyun the more likely culprit… but why would she do this? She's already been together with Ah Zu since returning from the ancient era. Did she sense something between Ah Zu and Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue and lose her rationality?

That stoked Bi Linglong’s wariness. If she can deal with Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue today, who knows when she’ll go after me? 

She gave the idea more thought and decided against it. Xie Daoyun was the Fiend races’ Saintess in the ancient era. She led the fiends in the fight against the monsters. Someone like that wouldn’t be so reckless.

But if it isn’t her, who could it be? Did Ah Zu spread the news himself?

Zhang Zitong probed, “Your Majesty, do you think the contents of the novels are true?”

Bi Linglong harrumphed. “Of course they're true.”

Zhang Zitong was perplexed. “But I can’t imagine Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun being in such a relationship with the regent. Their disciples…”

“That fellow is a walking incubus. Which woman in the world can resist him?” Bi Linglong said vexatiously. That fellow just keeps expanding his harem. How am I supposed to deal with this?

No woman in the world would want to share her lover with others. 

Zhang Zitong’s face reddened as she felt a twinge of guilt. I have to admit that the regent is indeed dashing…

“That fellow is a master philanderer! How can Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue withstand his abilities?” The more Bi Linglong spoke, the more frustrated she felt. 

Annoyance flickered through Zhang Zitong’s eyes. Why is the regent such a gentleman around me then? I've already hinted toward him many times! Though she would never dare say those words aloud to Bi Linglong. 

Sensing Bi Linglong’s anger, Zhang Zitong tried to change the subject. “Should I continue investigating this matter?”

“Don’t bother. Why should we worry about him when he’s not even worried himself?” Bi Linglong turned around and returned to her room. She couldn’t tell if Zu An had intentionally released the news or not. It would be awkward if it turned out to be him. 

She considered seeking verification from Zu An, but she was too angry to do so right now. 

… 

A new novel was released before daybreak. The plot was even more explosive this time, delving into Yan Xuehen’s motive for going after her disciple’s man. 

She had fought against Yun Jianyue for a long time, with both sides refusing to admit defeat. However, her disciple was now in a bad condition and couldn’t be with the regent. This was a disaster, as her disciple’s relationship with the regent was bound to cool off at this rate. On top of that, she discovered that Yun Jianyue had gotten with the regent. That sparked her sense of danger. For her disciple’s happiness and her reluctance to concede her charm was beneath her rival’s, she began to seduce the regent.

Realization dawned upon the world. It had previously confused them why the highly respected Sect Master Yan would do something like that, but the explanation, as ridiculous as it was, sounded plausible. 

No wonder Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun came to blows recently. It was over a man!

On Mount Yuquan, Yun Jianyue nearly blew up after reading the sequel. “Rubbish! She got together with Zu An before me! How could our rivalry be the motivation for their relationship?” She smashed the book into dust. 

Qiu Honglei gulped before meekly whispering, “Master, you might have been ahead of her.”

“Impossible! The ice sculpture must have been with Zu An before me! The more I think about it, the more certain I am!” Yun Jianyue paced around the room angrily. “If I ever learn who’s behind these books, I’ll rip them apart!”

Pei Mianman cowered. She coughed lightly and excused herself.

“Help me check out the situation on the ice sculpture’s side.” Yun Jianyue was still relying on Pei Mianman for intelligence, thinking the latter was trustworthy since they shared blood ties. 

Pei Mianman was on good terms with Yan Xuehen and Chu Chuyan, so it was easy for her to win their trust. 

Pei Mianman’s expression crumbled as soon as she left the room. Ugh, it’s been so tough on me! Do you know how hard I have been working to clean up your mess? And now, I even have to fear for my life!

She shook those thoughts out of her head and went off to meet the next batch of booksellers. 

Her series had sparked such widespread interest that demand for her books shot through the roof! It was to the extent that resellers were listing the books at astronomical prices! All the publishers in the capital and nearby provinces wanted a slice of the pie, so there was no lack of collaborators for Pei Mianman to choose from. 

As such, the third book was released before the readers could even digest the contents of the first two books. It was even more erotic. 

It detailed how Yan Xuehen had seduced the regent while her disciple wasn’t around. She'd approached the regent under the pretext of discussing her disciple’s affair, then moved on to consulting the regent about cultivation. Once they became close, she invited the regent to her room in the middle of the night to discuss secret manuals. The regent didn’t suspect her intentions and did his best to impart everything he knew to her.

But Yan Xuehen intentionally dressed scantily, sometimes appearing before the regent in her pajamas. She dropped hints at every turn of the corner. One night, she spiked the regent’s drink with aphrodisiac. The regent finally succumbed. Their discussions about secret manuals moved onto the bed, and it became a truly intimate sharing session. 

The details were so explosive that blood would uncontrollably spurt from one’s nose. Needless to say, the novel caused a huge fuss. No one could have imagined the virtuous and pure Yan Xuehen to be so licentious. 

On Mount Yuquan, Yun Jianyue clutched her belly and burst into laughter. “Hahaha, the publishers are doing a good job with the novels! I knew the ice sculpture has been faking it all these years. Someone is finally seeing her true face!”

Qiu Honglei scoffed in her heart. Just moments ago, you were berating the publishers for coming up with nonsense. 

Yun Jianyue couldn’t hold back anymore. She grabbed the book and marched off to Yan Xuehen’s room. How can I miss such a good opportunity to mock her?

Yan Xuehen was reading the same book in her room. Like Yun Jianyue, she was concerned about the latest development and had bought the newest sequel right away. The content made her face turn red, and she trembled in anger.

Bam!

Someone kicked her door down. Yun Jianyue appeared by the doorway with an evil smile. “You’re much wilder than I thought, ice sculpture!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2699: Evoking Revulsion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2699: Evoking Revulsion

Yan Xuehen slammed the table. “What nonsense are you spouting, demoness?!”

Yun Jianyue gestured to Yan Xuehen’s book with her chin. “You’re asking the obvious. Aren’t you reading it yourself?”

“This is utter nonsense! How can anyone believe it?!” Yan Xuehen’s face heated up as she hurriedly stowed away the book. She hadn’t expected the other party to catch her with it. 

“Is it? I thought it was written quite well. It depicts you accurately,” Yun Jianyue said gleefully. 

“Accurate, my ass…” Yan Xuehen was so angry that she uttered curses. She wondered how a human could achieve true indifference. Recent events had been challenging her tolerance.

But the years she'd spent cultivating weren’t in vain. It didn’t take her long to compose herself and retort, “Are you acknowledging the novels’ depiction of you too?”

Yun Jianyue’s smile froze. “Ridiculous! It should go without saying that the parts about me are all lies.”

“What double standards!” Yan Xuehen scoffed. She was feeling humiliated due to the novels, but seeing Yun Jianyue in the same plight comforted her. 

“Hah! How much of those novels is about me? Compared to you, the stuff about me is a drop in the bucket?” Yun Jianyue had honed fast wits from years of rivalry with Yan Xuehen. “Besides, I'm the Devil Sect’s demoness. Shouldn’t it be normal even if I rear some male slaves? This is nothing for me!

“You, on the other hand, pretend to be some fairy untouched by secular desires, only to play such games behind everyone’s back. Don’t you feel ashamed calling your disciple’s lover into your room in the middle of the night?” Yun Jianyue clicked her tongue in disdain.

Yan Xuehen couldn’t take the insult. Her flying sword flew out of her sheath, and snow rained within the room. Countless ice shards shot toward Yun Jianyue at the speed of light. 

Yun Jianyue was already prepared for this attack. She drew her crescent wheels, and the two of them came to blows. 

Boom!

It didn’t take long for the residence to crumble under the shockwaves. 

Many people from the academy rushed over to spectate the battle. 

“Look, they are fighting again!”

“It must be over the libationer.”

“Shouldn’t the libationer deal with it?”

“How is he supposed to resolve this conflict? He can’t side with either of them. Speaking of which, have you read today’s novel?”

“I did. It’s so explosive! I didn’t expect them to be so wild…”

… 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue’s eyebrows twitched uncontrollably. Even though the crowd had suppressed their voices, their discussions couldn’t escape the pair's ears. 

Killing intent flared from Yun Jianyue. Had it been anywhere else, she wouldn’t have hesitated to teach them the consequences of gossiping about the master of the Devil Sect. Even Yan Xuehen nearly went on a rampage. 

They would only embarrass themselves if they continued fighting, so they tacitly stopped the fight and flew off, leaving the residence in a mess. 

Not long later, a new novel surfaced. 

This time, the protagonist was Yun Jianyue. It detailed her frustrations in being unable to surpass her long-time rival—her prestige and popularity even paled in comparison to Yan Xuehen’s—so she decided to go about it from another direction. Since both their disciples were the regent’s lovers, she thought she could at least defeat Yan Xuehen in the field of romance. So, she leveraged her strength as the only person in the history of the Devil Sect to have mastered seduction arts to seduce the regent, to give her disciple a helping hand. 

But she had underestimated the strongest person in the world. Not only did her seduction arts fail, but she even suffered a backlash and uncontrollably fell in love with the regent. Due to the special nature of her seduction arts and the regent’s charm, she became his plaything.

She was the Devil Sect’s most outstanding demoness. Her looks, figure, and skills were impeccable. Even the regent was infatuated with her. The book elaborated about how Yun Jianyue used the techniques passed down through generations of the Devil Sect’s Holy Maidens to please the regent. It also depicted the regent’s immense strength and how he made her submit. 

Yan Xuehen, who had still been brooding over the last book not long ago, was overjoyed. She happily took the book to Yun Jianyue’s room. Meanwhile, Yun Jianyue was throwing a tantrum after reading the book’s contents. 

This culminated in yet another huge fight. They learned their lesson and fought in the suburbs. There were fewer spectators this time, but their clashes caused such a huge commotion that it inevitably drew experts over. 

Even Zu An rushed over to check on them, fearing they would kill each other. 

When they saw Zu An, the two ladies felt conscience-stricken. They felt as if the crowd was looking at them and Zu An with gossipy eyes, which prompted them to escape despite Zu An calling them to stop. They were worried the crowd would assume the rumors were true if they exchanged even a single word with Zu An here.

Zu An shook his head bitterly. The crowd looked at him with eyes of awe, admiration, disdain, and so on.

Zu An returned to Mount Yuquan’s residence and called Pei Mianman over. “You're behind the commotion, right?”

Pei Mianman stuck out her tongue. “Look how hard I have to work to clean up your lousy mess!”

“What are you thinking? How can you make those things public?” Zu An was frustrated. The capital and the academy were in an uproar. Many people had secretly approached him to verify if the rumors were true. Just dealing with those people was troublesome enough. 

“Do you intend to hide it forever?” Pei Mianman asked. “It’s only a matter of time before everyone finds out, so we might as well take the initiative to reveal it. At least we’ll have some control over the situation this way.”

“Control?” Zu An was starting to get an idea of what was going on.

Pei Mianman nodded. “That’s right. Haven’t you noticed how, despite the huge commotion this scandal has caused, there aren’t many people criticizing Yan Xuehen or Yun Jianyue?”

“No criticisms? There are plenty of people saying they're shameless!” Zu An had been paying close attention to the crowd’s responses.

“I was the one who spread those criticisms,” Pei Mianman calmly replied. 

Zu An was speechless.

“Someone else would have said those words either way. I’d rather take control of the narrative and make the criticisms even uglier. That would evoke the crowd’s revulsion and encourage them to speak up for the two,” Pei Mianman replied. 

“The 50-cent army!” Zu An was shocked. He hadn’t expected Pei Mianman to learn these tricks by herself. She would have been a marketing genius in the modern era!

Pei Mianman had no idea what the ‘50-cent army’ referred to, but she continued her explanation. “I intentionally exaggerated the contents of the novels. The first two books are 30% true, but the subsequent ones are less than 10% true. Some people are even questioning the credibility of the novels now.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. All the talk about Yan Xuehen dressing scantily and drugging him just sounded too far-fetched. The recent book about Yun Jianyue was highly exaggerated too. Its content was explosive enough to draw eyeballs, but the smarter ones had already calmed down and shrugged off the books as the publishers’ money-making scheme. 

Pei Mianman continued, “Under such a setting, the extreme rhetoric I sowed on the streets would evoke the masses’ revulsion. Even those who truly intend to besmirch Sect Master Yun and Sect Master Yan will be ignored. The masses are beginning to understand and even side with them. When the time is ripe, I’ll give it one last push so that the masses will come to terms with this matter.”

Zu An was awed. “Manman, you’re a genius!”

Pei Mianman dodged his embrace and harrumphed. “With your wits, you should have thought about that too; it’s just that you didn’t dare to take the gamble.”

Zu An fell silent. He had indeed been worried about the situation going out of control. 

“It’s too early to celebrate. I've dealt with the public opinion for you, but you’ll still have to find a way to make them come to terms with this matter,” Pei Mianman coldly pointed out. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2700: Choose One
chapters.body:
Chapter 2700: Choose One

In the coming days, the discussion about the novels only intensified, and it spread like wildfire across the world. All manner of talk and rumors surfaced. Some said Yan Xuehen was shameless. Some remarked that Yun Jianyue was a demoness and the root behind this mess. Quite a few people questioned the novels’ veracity, pointing out they were riddled with flaws and claiming that they were written just to draw attention. But others thought the novels were true.

A few nosy people wanted to investigate the truth, but none dared to confront Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen for it. The former was notorious; only those with a death wish would dare ask her such a question. The latter had a reputation to uphold, so people couldn’t bear to ask her. Of course, some didn't care about her reputation, but they couldn’t find her. 

So, people sought verification from Zu An instead. 

Annoyed by the questions, Zu An escaped the mess under the pretext of paying a visit to the Fiend races. Neither confessing nor denying the matter would be right at this juncture anyway. 

But that only made the crowd speculate more. Those who believed the novels to be fake were baffled that no one had stepped forward to suppress the rumors, be it the people in question, the imperial palace, or the academy. 

The smarter ones slowly realized the truth. Could there be something among the three of them?

The bulk of the populace, however, didn’t have access to as much information, and most people would only believe such a matter to be true if they saw it with their own eyes. 

Under Pei Mianman’s media manipulation, most people still believed the novels to be fake. Furthermore, there were so many discussions and conspiracies surrounding the trio that if the truth ever came to light, they would likely just shrug it off as another attempt to draw attention. Even if the truth eventually came to light, this three-sided relationship would be so over-discussed that there wouldn’t be any overly extreme criticisms directed at Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue anymore.

Zu An was awed. Manman is truly a prodigy. She found a solution to a problem that has confounded me for so long. All that’s left is to untangle the knots in Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue’s hearts. 

But this won’t be easy either…

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were extremely smart individuals. After the initial shock, humiliation, anger, fear, and all sorts of emotions, they slowly regained themselves and noticed something amiss. 

Outsiders wouldn't know whether the novels’ content was real or not, but the two of them knew better. The initial books were 30% truth mixed with 70% falsehood, but the sequels became 10% truth and 90% falsehoods. 

It must be an inside job—outsiders wouldn’t have known such details about us. Clearly, someone is plotting something. But who could be behind this?

Naturally, the first person who came to mind was Zu An. He was the most likely mastermind behind this mess! His motive was also clear. It was to first deliver a strong shock to numb the crowd, so they could stand together under broad daylight one day. 

If the details about me are real, the details about the demoness (ice sculpture) must be real too, right?

They didn’t seek Zu An for confirmation as they were still reeling from embarrassment. Besides, they figured that Zu An would feign ignorance and not betray them to the other party, and vice-versa.

Since that was the case, it would be easier to just ask each other. So, they each headed out to confront each other, only to bump into the other midway. They were initially taken aback, but they quickly realized what was going on.

“Things have already gotten to this point, so I’ll get to the point. Are the details surrounding Zu An and you true?” Yan Xuehen asked directly. 

Yun Jianyue hesitated. She normally wouldn’t have admitted to it, but it couldn’t be more obvious that Yan Xuehen was in the same plight as her—they were both just short of the final confirmation. There wasn't much point in denying it anymore.  She had thought she could mock Yan Xuehen over this matter, but the world had already rained all kinds of criticisms on them. To make things worse, most people favored Yan Xuehen. There was no point in further mocking Yan Xuehen when both of them had heard it all.

So, she frankly admitted to it and said, “That’s right. What about you?”

There was a moment of silence before Yan Xuehen replied with a subtle “Mm”.

I knew it! Ah Zu has been with her for some time now, but he kept it from me! I must have looked like a fool in his eyes! Yun Jianyue narrowed her eyes.

Yan Xuehen sighed. “Demoness, we've been so busy fighting between us that we didn’t know someone else was watching us make a fool out of ourselves from the shadows.”

Yun Jianyue felt vexed too. “Was he behind the novels too?”

“Most likely so.” Yan Xuehen’s eyes turned cold. 

The two women each felt anger surging in their chest. No wonder he escaped to the Fiend races' side! He’s avoiding us!

“What do you intend to do?” Yun Jianyue asked.

“I’ll be making a trip to the Fiend races to demand an explanation,” Yan Xuehen replied coldly.

“Yes, he owes us an explanation!” Yun Jianyue clenched her teeth. “Shall we head off now?”

Yan Xuehen nodded. She flashed into the sky and rushed to the northern Fiend races. Yun Jianyue kicked off the ground and flew alongside Yan Xuehen, not lagging behind in the slightest. 

Both of them kept opening and closing their mouths hesitantly along the way. They had never felt so awkward before. After fighting for their entire lives, they would never have thought that they would end up with the same man!

Yun Jianyue was the one who broke the silence in the end. “When did you get with him?”

Yan Xuehen’s face reddened. She wasn’t fond of sharing such private details. “You ask him.”

Yun Jianyue’s eyebrows shot up. This woman is as hateful as ever.

“Fine. I’ll ask him in person. Let’s see who he chooses. This should draw the curtains on our many years of enmity,” Yun Jianyue sneered. You want me to share a husband and become sisters with this ice sculpture? Dream on!

She knew that men preferred Yan Xuehen’s type, so it was more likely for Zu An to choose Yan Xuehen over her. But so what? Even if I lose, I shall lose with my head held high. 

Yan Xuehen was taken aback. She hadn't thought Yun Jianyue would go this far. Is she that confident that Zu An would choose her? After a brief silence, she said, “Don’t bother, I’ll back out. Things have never been possible between him and me.”

She didn’t want to just be a choice for someone, and she had never planned to openly be with Zu An either. Thus, she figured it would be best for her to quietly back out. 

Yun Jianyue was infuriated. “What do you mean by this, ice sculpture? A win is a win; a loss is a loss. I don’t need your charity!”

Yan Xuehen calmly replied, “I don’t want to compete with you in this.”

Yun Jianyue suddenly fell silent. She felt the same way too. That bastard is at fault! “Aren’t you just afraid of losing to me?”

“I’m afraid of losing to you? What a joke.” Yan Xuehen could never remain calm when it came to her rivalry with Yun Jianyue. “You’re a proud person. Are you truly willing to become a choice for him?”

“That’s simple. We’ll let him determine the victor between us first before dumping him,” Yun Jianyue coldly replied.

Yan Xuehen was taken aback. As expected of the master of the Devil Sect! She considered it for a while before nodding. “Alright.”

The two women angrily flew toward the Fiend races’ imperial court to look for Zu An. Their hearts were heavier than ever. 

Zu An was busy catching up with the little Fiend Empress when he sensed something and mentally prepared himself for what was about to come. Shortly after, two beautiful women appeared before him. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue glanced at the little Fiend Empress. Their complexions alleviated a little when they saw she was properly dressed. They turned to Zu An and got to the point.

“Choose one.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2701: Heart Turning Cold
chapters.body:
Chapter 2701: Heart Turning Cold

The little Fiend Empress was startled. The gossip had already spread to the Fiend races. She had intended to ask Zu An about this matter when the other two stars came knocking. There’s no need to ask anymore. This matter must be real. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Before he could say a word, another chirpy voice echoed from outside.

“Ah Zu, please tell me that the rumors about you, Sect Master Yan, and Sect Master Yun aren’t real…” A woman rushed into the room, only for her voice to abruptly stop when she saw the two women in the room. She froze up out of awkwardness.

Yan Xuehen recognized the woman. The latter had been Chu Chuyan’s personal maid, as well as a spy from the Shi clan—the elven princess Snow. Her appearance made the situation even more awkward. 

Trailing behind Snow was a woman who was so beautiful that she mesmerized even Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen—the Medusa Queen, the woman who had once been the capital’s number one beauty, Yu Yanluo. 

The two women hadn’t expected so many spectators for their confrontation with Zu An. They felt startled and incredibly awkward. 

“Ah Zu~” Yu Yanluo’s beautiful face lit up in delight when she saw Zu An, but she quickly realized there were others in the room too. She bowed to the two women and said, “Paying respects to Sect Master Yan and Sect Master Yun.”

Her etiquette was so on point that it could have been a textbook example. No wonder the capital’s men were so enchanted by her back then. 

“Madam Yu!” the two women returned the greeting.

The atmosphere became peculiar. They'd come to settle things with Zu An, only to be surrounded by his lovers. Yan Xuehen glared at Yun Jianyue, urging her to move things along.

Yun Jianyue felt vexed. Why do I have to do the talking? Don’t you have a mouth? 

That said, she knew the reticent Yan Xuehen wouldn’t even be able to squeeze out a fart in such a setting, and the awkwardness would suffocate them to death before they got anything done. So, she turned around and walked out. 

Yan Xuehen followed suit. This is the first time Yun Jianyue hasn’t looked so annoying to me.

Zu An was speechless as the other ladies looked at him with gossipy eyes. 

He coughed lightly. “Give me a moment. I’ll be right back.”

After he left, the little Fiend Empress, Snow, and Yu Yanluo gathered to gossip. Despite their different personalities, it was human nature to gossip. 

“I didn’t think the rumors were true.”

“I knew it. How could Ah Zu overlook those two gorgeous women around him?”

“But they are Chuyan’s and Honglei’s masters. Isn’t that weird?”

“I remember the two of them are mortal enemies. Ah Zu has quite the problem to settle here.”

The little Fiend Empress animatedly added, “Before you arrived, those two demanded for Ah Zu to choose between them!”

The eyes of the other two women lit up. “Woah! Things are getting heated up!”

Unable to withhold their curiosity, they quietly snuck to the doorway and pricked up their ears. 

…

On the other side, Yun Jianyue dismissed the guards outside before taking in a deep breath. “Make your choice.”

Her voice was quivering. As the Devil Sect’s master, she had weathered countless storms, but she had never felt so nervous before. She already had an inkling that Zu An would choose the ice sculpture over her, and that thought pricked her heart. 

I've fought with the ice sculpture my entire life. Who could have thought I’d lose to her on this?

She realized that Zu An not choosing her would hurt her far more than losing to the ice sculpture. She fully understood the risks of forcing Zu An with an ultimatum, but her pride wouldn’t allow her to be a coward and continue dragging things out. 

Yan Xuehen felt nervous too. She was aware that her cold nature pushed others away from her, and that she was nowhere as passionate as the demoness. 

He might be displeased with me for continuously pushing him away… but this works too. This relationship should never have come to be. I’ll find a quiet mountain valley and devote the rest of my life to my cultivation. 

That was what she told herself, but there was an inexplicable pain in her heart that made her anxious. The fact that she felt such intense emotions despite having cultivated the Unshakable Daoist Manual spoke volumes. 

As they awaited his answer, the two women looked at Zu An nervously.

Zu An felt his throat turning dry, but he answered, “You should also know that I don’t like to choose. If I have to, I’ll choose to take it all.”

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were speechless.

You have successfully trolled Yan Xuehen for +666… +666… +666…

You have successfully trolled Yun Jianyue for +666… +666… +666…

This fellow is ridiculous!

Yan Xuehen coldly spat, “Impossible!”

Yun Jianyue scoffed out of sheer anger, “Dream on!”

How can I share a husband with that ice sculpture? And clearly, she feels the same way, too.

The spying crowd inside the main hall also spat in disdain.

“Ah Zu is shameless.”

“That ruffian-like personality is exactly like him.”

… 

Zu An sighed. “We don’t have to make things so tense.”

“You knew how things are between us, but you still chose to withhold the truth and play us like fools.” Anger seethed in Yan Xuehen’s eyes.

“I was afraid of hurting you. You're Chuyan’s and Honglei’s masters. Unless the two of you allowed it, I couldn’t reveal our relationship to others.”

“Is it? How do you intend to explain the novels? Are you forcing us to accept this situation?”

“I wasn't the one behind those novels. It was Manman.”

The women in the main hall exchanged looks as if they had just learned something unbelievable. None of them could have guessed that Pei Mianman would be behind those novels! 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were shocked too. They hadn't thought it would be her.

“She wouldn’t do something like that without your permission.”

“Actually, she received a request from Chuyan. On top of that, considering her ties with big sister Yun and Honglei, she also wanted to resolve your reservations.”

Yun Jianyue fell into deep thought. Manman was indeed her blood-related cousin, and she was on close terms with Yan Xuehen’s disciple as well. She did have the motive to pull off something like that. 

Yan Xuehen was astounded. “It was Chuyan’s request? Why would she… make such a request…” Her voice quivered as she spoke.

Zu An sighed. “You remember the Great Snowy Mountain’s Snow Lady, right? That was Chuyan after being sent through spacetime. She recalled everything afterward…”

Yan Xuehen’s face paled after hearing his explanation. “Chuyan, I have wronged you…”

She turned around and left, not wanting to stay here any longer.

Zu An was startled. He quickly reached out to grab her.

Yun Jianyue’s face crumbled. She understood that Zu An had made his decision, and her heart felt chilled. She turned around and flew off in the opposite direction.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2702: Confession
chapters.body:
Chapter 2702: Confession

But shortly after Yun Jianyue flew off, her surroundings suddenly retreated in the opposite direction. Before she knew it, she had returned to her original position. It went without saying that Zu An had dragged her back. 

Yun Jianyue was speechless. Well, this is awkward. 

She glanced at the ice sculpture, followed by the pairs of eyes hiding behind the door, and anger suddenly surged within her. “Bastard Zu, don’t think you can humiliate me like that just because you’re stronger than me!”

She raised her hand to end her own life. 

Zu An hadn’t expected her to react so explosively. Horrified, he flashed to her side and grabbed her hand. “That’s not what I mean, but we should at least properly talk things out before you leave.”

“Didn’t you already choose the ice sculpture? I have no reason to remain here.” Yun Jianyue’s heart quivered as she spoke. Tears would have streamed down her face if not for her pride. 

“That wasn't picking between you two.” Zu An was also at a loss. How was he supposed to know that consoling one side would only hurt the other side?

Yan Xuehen’s heart shook. She wasn't so dense as to think that Zu An was choosing her by grabbing her hand, but hearing him speak it aloud still hurt her. 

The three women in the main hall were thoroughly immersed in the drama.

Snow uttered jealously, “He’s holding Sect Master Yan in one hand while embracing Sect Master Yun with the other. I reckon Ah Zu is the only one capable of that in the entire world.”

The little Fiend Empress snorted, “It doesn’t look like he’s going to get his way this time. Who knows? He might even lose the two of them for good!”

“Surely not?” Snow remarked. “Who do you think Ah Zu will choose?”

Even the usually quiet Yu Yanluo couldn’t resist the urge to speculate, “It could be Sect Master Yan. Her transcendent aura poses a fatal allure to men.”

“My bet is on Sect Master Yun. Not only is she the valiant master of the Devil Sect, but she’s also passionate beyond measure. No man can resist the immense satisfaction of conquering a woman like her.” The little Fiend Empress was more fond of Yun Jianyue, who walked the same path as her. 

Snow frowned. “I don’t think Ah Zu is capable of choosing one at the other’s expense.”

… 

“Then what’s your answer?” Neither Yan Xuehen nor Yun Jianyue wanted this ambiguous relationship to drag on. They wanted a conclusion and to settle things once and for all.

Zu An was frustrated. He'd been so focused on resolving the problem of public opinion that he'd neglected their own will. He'd thought that he could slowly coax them one by one, but little had he expected the two of them to confront him at once, and three more women were watching the drama on top of that. There was no way to resolve this privately.

Yan Xuehen sighed. “Since you’re incapable of making a decision, let's just end this. We should never have been together in the first place.”

Yun Jianyue shared the same thought. She also found it shameful to snatch her disciple’s man. Perhaps this was a good opportunity to take a step back and just remain friends. 

Zu An was about to speak when he sensed something and looked at the sky.

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue initially thought he was trying to change the subject, but they quickly sensed something and looked up as well. They could sense a ship coming from outer space. 

Zu An reached into his robe to retrieve Dreamland’s invitation letter. It was emitting a faint glow—a sign that it had been activated. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue knew about Dreamland and were taken aback. Could that be… 

A man who was dressed in a way reminiscent of a dignified Victorian butler appeared not too far away from Zu An. He was momentarily mesmerized by the two women beside Zu An, but shame soon flickered through his eyes. He bowed to Zu An and said, “Pardon my breach of etiquette, esteemed guest. Your female companions are so beautiful that I lost myself for a moment despite my strict training.”

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue’s faces heated up. It felt good to be complimented. They wanted to clarify that they weren’t Zu An’s companions, but they didn’t want to embarrass him in front of an outsider. In the end, they chose to hold their silence. 

“You are?” Zu An asked despite having guessed the answer. 

“I am Employee Number 9527 from the Universal Conglomerate. We have received a request from Dreamland to fetch invited guests, and I am responsible for fetching you.” The butler-like employee glanced at Zu An’s invitation letter with a smile. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue’s hearts quivered. They hadn’t expected their day of separation to come so quickly. They were no longer in a mood to compete with each other. 

The three women in the main hall also lost interest in the drama and ran out. “Ah Zu!”

They looked worried. 

Yun Jianyue asked, “Can he bring others with him?”

Yan Xuehen’s eyes lit up too. She was worried about letting Zu An go alone.

Employee 9527 shook his head. “I’m sorry, but Dreamland strictly permits entry only to invitation holders.”

All the women looked disappointed. 

Zu An asked, “Can you give me some time? There are some matters I still have to attend to.”

Employee 9527 looked troubled. “I’m afraid I can’t do that. I wouldn’t have minded waiting days or even half a month for you, but there are other esteemed guests onboard too.” 

Zu An nodded. There are other guests onboard? This works too, since I can gather information about the myriad worlds from them. I’m practically venturing into the unknown now. “It won’t take that long. Just two hours or so.”

“That should be fine. I’ll inform the other esteemed guests. Please activate the invitation letter once you’re ready.” Employee 9527 bowed before disappearing into thin air.

“Ah Zu…” Snow couldn’t conceal her worry. She hugged Zu An tightly, fearing that he would disappear forever if she let go. 

Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue were left in an awkward position. They released Zu An’s hands, thinking that it was inappropriate for the four of them to be hugging together.

“Don’t worry, this is a fortuitous encounter too. I’ll be careful.” Zu An gently patted Snow’s shoulder. He also felt reluctant to part ways with her. 

“I’d rather you be safe here than risk your life outside,” Snow said with a hoarse voice.

Yu Yanluo and the little Fiend Empress nodded as well, agreeing with Snow’s words. 

Zu An sighed. “We can’t hole ourselves up like ostriches. It’ll be too late for regrets if greater danger strikes in the future and we’re incapable of protecting ourselves. Besides, there are still people here who need saving. I might find solutions at Dreamland.”

Be it Mi Li’s body, Chu Chuyan, the Goddess of Wu Mountain, or Chang’e, there were too many things he needed to do. 

The others knew he was determined to make the trip, so they didn’t try to further dissuade him. Instead, they reluctantly bade him farewell. 

Zu An made his way up to Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen. Despite their imminent separation, he still didn’t know what to say. 

Yan Xuehen quietly spoke up. “Now that we’re about to part ways, none of those things are important anymore…”

She was no longer in the mood to force him to choose between them. There were many words at the tip of her tongue, but she didn’t know how to voice them. 

Yun Jianyue suddenly spoke up. “You little brat! Take care of yourself. If you return safely…”

She briefly paused here as she squirmed awkwardly, though her gaze remained firmly on Zu An. “As long as you return safely, I’ll even accept sharing a husband with this ice sculpture.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2703: Raising Flags Everywhere
chapters.body:
Chapter 2703: Raising Flags Everywhere
