Shock rippled through the crowd. No one had expected the domineering Devil Sect’s master to be willing to share a husband, but on second thought, this was to be expected. All of the women gathered here were extraordinary in their ways, but they'd still fallen for Zu An anyway. 

But we didn’t expect her to be able to put down her enmity with Yan Xuehen for him. She must have truly fallen in love with Ah Zu. She must be trying to motivate him so he’ll grit his teeth and get through any danger he faces in Dreamland. 

The ball had been thrown into Yan Xuehen’s court. All the gazes on her made her nervous. In truth, she didn’t think her concerns mattered now that Zu An’s safety was at stake. She might have said something similar to Yun Jianyue in the heat of the moment if they were alone, but how could she do so in front of so many people?

Amid her hesitation, her face turned pale. It suddenly occurred to her that she had lost. 

The demoness has the guts to confess to Ah Zu in public, yet I’m unable to do the same. Everyone will think that my feelings for Ah Zu are less than hers. Even if Ah Zu understands my quandary, he might still feel uncomfortable about it.

Yan Xuehen felt bitter regret, but her pride wouldn’t allow her to change her tune. As a result, she just stood completely still on the spot. She felt so awkward that her fingernails were digging into her palm. 

Zu An smiled. He grabbed both Yun Jianyue and Yan Xuehen’s hands and said, “Big sister Yun has said such words, and big sister Yan has implicitly acknowledged it. Looks like it’s a blessing in disguise for me.”

Yan Xuehen felt warmth in her heart. Ah Zu is as caring as ever. He resolved my regret and awkwardness with such ease. Now that I think about it, he was also involved in Manman’s actions. He's put a lot of thought into resolving my reservations. 

Having thought things through, she didn’t shake off his hand. The warmth transmitted through his palm made her nervous heart slowly calm down. 

Yun Jianyue eyed their hands in astonishment. Having been enemies for so many years, she could see through Yan Xuehen’s inner turmoil. I thought ice sculpture would shake off his hand. Tsk, I've already won here, but Ah Zu is taking such good care of her. 

That made her heart boil with anger. 

You have successfully trolled Yun Jianyue for +110… +110… +110…

“Hmph, we’ll talk about this when you return.”

The little Fiend Empress and the others secretly chuckled. Well well, you can’t expect maintaining a harem to be easy, right? Did I accept him too easily back then? I should have put him through a bit more, considering his philandering ways!

Having finally calmed down, Yan Xuehen worriedly said, “There’s no saying what you might face at Dreamland. You should bring some defensive artifacts with you.”

She regretted being so caught up in this matter that she'd forgotten to forge defensive artifacts for him. So, she took off her Flying Snow Sword and handed it over. “Ah Zu, your Blue Luan Sword Formation becomes stronger with more weapons. This Flying Snow Sword is a treasure passed down through the generations in our White Jade Sect. It should be of some help to you.”

The other women were shocked. The Flying Snow Sword had been with Yan Xuehen since she first entered the world of cultivation, yet she was giving it to a man now? Who was the one who said she practiced the Unshakable Daoist Manual?!

Yun Jianyue smiled. “I can’t be stingier than the ice sculpture. My Crescent Moon Blade won’t be of much use to you, so you can have my Empress Lantern instead. It can temporarily imprison an enemy.” 

Seeing the other women reaching for their most prized treasures as well, Zu An hurriedly stopped them. “I appreciate your sentiments, but I've made ample preparations. I’ll feel more at ease if you keep these treasures with you.”

“But…” 

Yan Xuehen wanted to argue, but Zu An interjected, “You've been gazed upon by a universal deity. There’s no saying when you might encounter danger. Your fighting prowess will be greatly reduced without your weapons, and that’ll only make me worry about your safety.”

Hearing those words, the others didn’t insist on it. “You must take care of yourself!”

All the women had countless words to say to him, but they were too embarrassed to speak up in front of everyone else. 

Zu An smiled, and their surroundings suddenly changed. All the women realized that they were alone with their lover; they couldn’t see anyone else. 

Snow’s heart thumped wildly. Did Ah Zu choose to bid me farewell first? Moved, she leaped into his arms, only to fall right through.

Zu An explained, “I'm currently in soul form. This way, I can bid all of you farewell at once.”

Snow pouted. I thought I was the special one. But with their separation imminent, she didn’t want to squander the precious time they had left on jealousy, so she spilled her heart out to him.

The same thing was happening in every corner in the world. All of Zu An’s lovers saw him, and their eyes reddened in sorrow. The more sentimental ones couldn’t stop their tears from flowing. Zu An could only console them over and over again. 

Pei Mianman’s eyes were reddened too. “You don’t have a conscience! I've been working myself to the bone to settle those two women for you, but you’re dumping me now that the problem is almost resolved.”

“I didn’t expect Dreamland’s courier to arrive right now either," Zu An said.

Zu An was planning to update her about Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue, but Pei Mianman interjected, “I don’t want to spend the precious time we have left talking about other women.”

Zu An’s heart melted. He held her with his soul, and the two of them soaked in each other’s warmth. 

In the imperial palace, Bi Linglong was worried. “Ah Zu, I’m worried that I can’t hold the fort when you’re gone. I won’t be able to face you if I lose your empire.” 

Zu An smiled. “This is your empire, not mine. You've been doing a good job as the empress.”

“No, this empire belongs to you. I'm merely taking care of it on your behalf.” Bi Linglong regretted not having his child. If something happens to him… Touch wood! What am I thinking? Ah Zu will surmount all dangers! 

In the Ocean races’ dragon palace, Shang Liuyu and Shang Hongyu gathered around Zu An. “We could have fought alongside you if we were stronger.”

“You shouldn’t rush your cultivation. With your talent and fortuitous encounters, I believe it shouldn’t take long before you make a breakthrough.” Zu An knew about their past lives and understood that they were destined for greatness.

Beneath the Bridge of Helplessness, Zu An said with a worried expression, “Chuyan, you're the one I worry about the most. There’ll be no one to talk to you once I leave.”

Only he had the power to bring others into this land of the dead. Once he was gone, Yan Xuehen, Pei Mianman, and the others wouldn’t be able to visit Chu Chuyan anymore. 

A tear trickled down Chu Chuyan’s cheeks. Her eyes brimmed with concern and worry. “That’s why you have to return safely, or else I’ll be alone for a thousand years. I don’t know if I’ll have the courage to last that long without you.”

Zu An felt a sharp pain in his chest. His eyes hardened in determination. “Chuyan, I’ll be back. Wait for me!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2704: Inauspicious Omen
chapters.body:
Chapter 2704: Inauspicious Omen

As a transmigrator who had watched plenty of movies in his previous life, Zu An was familiar with movie tropes. If a character took out a photograph and said that they had loved ones waiting for them at home, that they would return home to get married after this last operation, they were pretty much doomed to die later in the show. Those were all death flags. 

Zu An thought about his earlier farewells with the ladies and shook his head with a bitter smile. It’s like I’m doing a speedrun for collecting death flags. 

But now that he was going on a long journey, that was the least of his concerns at the moment.

He had also sent a sliver of his soul to gather the academy’s teachers to leave some final instructions. He entrusted the leadership of the academy to Yan Xiangu. The latter was the old libationer’s first disciple, and had the greatest strength and the highest prestige, so no one objected to the appointment. 

The teachers gave their cherished treasures to Zu An for his self-protection. Even Ji Dengtu, who often scoffed at him, took out a bottle of pills and said, “Brat, these are pills that Xiaoxi and I came up with recently. They work much better than the other pills we've made. I didn’t want to give them to you, but Xiaoxi insisted on it. Now that I’ve done so, you better not die outside and waste my pills.”

Zu An knew Ji Dengtu had a sharp tongue but a soft heart. The latter was clearly worried about him but refused to admit it. Thus, he replied, “Don’t worry, I can’t bear to leave Xiaoxi behind. I’ll come back to look for her.”

Ji Dengtu was so angry that he threw a tantrum. It was almost as if he could already see his beloved daughter being taken away by Zu An.

You have successfully trolled Ji Dengtu for +119… +119… +119…

Amid the uproar, Qi Yaoguang said, “Libationer, I’d like to have a private word with you.”

The others were astounded. Has our little junior sister fallen in love with the libationer? Even Yan Xuehen and Yun Jianyue have fallen for him, so I guess it’s not that surprising for our junior sister to harbor feelings for him too.

Thus, they excused themselves with a smile to give them some privacy. 

Ji Dengtu’s anger flared. Is this brat a walking aphrodisiac? Even our little junior sister has fallen for his tricks! This won’t do. I must knock some sense into Xiaoxi so she doesn’t get duped by this philanderer!

You have successfully trolled Ji Dengtu for +444… +444… +444…

Only Zu An sensed something was amiss. “Yaoguang, you seem a little frail.”

“I knew I couldn’t hide it from you.” Qi Yaoguang coughed. She gave up on her disguise, and her face immediately turned ghastly pale. “Don’t worry, libationer. I merely used my divination.”

Zu An quickly channeled a surge of pure ki into Qi Yaoguang’s body. Only then did her complexion finally recover a little. He was perplexed. “Someone of your cultivation shouldn’t become so weakened from a divination. Your earlier condition was so severe that it felt as if even your lifespan was affected.”

Qi Yaoguang smiled. “That would be the case for other matters, but when divining the libationer’s safety, the price was bound to be heavier.”

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected her to go this far for him. 

Instead of dwelling on this matter, Qi Yaoguang waved her sleeves and conjured an image of a massive vortex. There was a skeleton at its center. She said, “This is the result of my divination. I hesitated for a long time whether to tell you, but I thought I should at least give you a heads up so you could take precautions.”

“What does that mean?” Zu An looked at the image. The vortex induced a feeling of vertigo, and the skeleton inside looked jarring too. This looked terribly inauspicious. 

“In the field of divination, bones represent death. I’m not too sure what that vortex means; it could represent water or some sort of danger.” Qi Yaoguang had a worried frown. 

Zu An sighed. “Am I destined to die on this trip?”

Qi Yaoguang hurriedly shook her head. “Divination only shows the most likely flow of events. Your trip will be fraught with danger, but heaven’s will is fleeting. There will always be a sliver of hope. Fate isn't cast in stone. Libationer, you mustn’t give up hope.”

Zu An smiled. “I’m not the kind to surrender to fate. You may put your mind at ease, Yaoguang.”

Qi Yaoguang breathed a sigh of relief. “Libationer, will you harbor a grudge against me for sharing an inauspicious divination with you at this juncture?”

“Why would I harbor a grudge against you? I'm more than grateful to you for sacrificing your lifespan to warn me of danger,” Zu An replied gently. “I’ll be leaving soon, so I can’t help you any further. Seek Ji Dengtu and Xiaoxi’s help, and request some medicinal herbs from Bi Linglong. They can help you recover from your depletion.”

Qi Yaoguang smiled. “Don’t worry, libationer. It’s not my first time peering into heaven’s will and suffering such a backlash. May you have a smooth journey.”

… 

Zu An bade farewell to his friends and lovers with his countless soul fragments. He had never felt time pass so quickly before. In a flash, it had already reached the time he had agreed on with Employee 9527. He returned his soul fragments into his main body and activated his invitation letter. 

Soon, Employee 9527 appeared. “Esteemed guest, this way please!”

A cloud appeared beneath Zu An’s feet. He stepped onto it, and the two of them swiftly flew into space. He looked at the world beneath him with wistful eyes. There was no greater grief in the world than separation. 

Soon, they arrived before a boat.

Zu An was surprised. He'd been expecting a high-tech spaceship, but it looked no different from an ancient wooden boat—and on top of that, it didn’t look big. As he boarded the boat, he asked in surprise, “I thought you said there were other guests. Where are they?”

He didn’t have to scan the area with his divine sense. The boat was small enough for him to see everything at a glance. 

“This is a dispatch boat. The ship heading to Dreamland is docked at another world.” Sensing Zu An’s confusion, Employee 9527 elaborated, “All kinds of danger exist in the Myriad Worlds, so the coordinates of each world are kept secret. Most guests don’t want their world coordinates to be known to others, so we employ isolating measures to ensure client confidentiality.”

Zu An was taken aback. He had failed to consider this due to his lacking experience. It hadn’t been easy for him to eradicate the Monster World and restore peace. Should their coordinates be exposed, the annihilation worshipers or other dangerous entities could set their eyes here, and they would never have peace ever again.  

That made him vigilant. “But you know the coordinates of all the guests.”

Employee 9527 smiled. “You can put your mind at ease, esteemed guest. Our Universal Conglomerate enjoys a stellar reputation across the Myriad Worlds. We have never leaked our guests’ secrets in the billions of years of our existence.”

“Billions of years…” Zu An was taken aback. “It looks like the Universal Conglomerate is extremely powerful.”

Employee 9527 smiled proudly. “Our conglomerate exists wherever civilization exists and the stars shine on.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2705: Three Pieces Of Information
chapters.body:
Chapter 2705: Three Pieces Of Information

Zu An was taken aback. A single universe was already unimaginably huge as it was, and there were multiple universes on top of that. To think the Universal Conglomerate would have branches everywhere! This was proof of their strength.

“Doesn’t that mean that you’re highly respected within the conglomerate?”

9527 wasn't a small number, but it was nothing compared to the sheer number of planets out there in the Myriad Worlds. Having such a number showed that the employee was of considerable stature within the conglomerate. 

Employee 9527’s face beamed with pride. “Esteemed guest, you’re flattering me.”

Zu An took this opportunity to shoot a question. “May I know which universal deity is protecting the Universal Conglomerate?”

The Universal Conglomerate couldn’t have grown so big without the aid of a universal deity. 

Employee 9527 smiled. “This isn’t a secret in the Myriad Worlds. Our Universal Conglomerate worships the Deity of Order, and we maintain our principles to ensure fair trade. That’s also why different universes are willing to trade with us.”

Zu An nodded. I’ve learned about another universal deity. The Deity of Order… He didn’t ask about the Deity of Order’s name, knowing it could draw the latter’s gaze.

“Sit well, esteemed guest. We’ll be setting off.” Employee 9527 took out a formation disc and began controlling it.

Zu An studied the formation disc, curious to know how it worked. He quickly realized that the formation disc contained not just formations, but also some kind of technology. But even though the world he had transmigrated from, Earth, was a technological civilization, he wasn't a scientist. His high school-level scientific knowledge wasn't enough for him to comprehend the principles of the formation disc. 

Soon, the boat set off. Zu An took one last look at the Cultivation World, wondering when he would return.

The boat initially moved slowly, but a light barrier soon formed around the boat, and it began moving at an incredible speed. The surroundings began glowing brightly as the boat seemed to travel through pockets of space. 

Zu An would never have thought that this shabby-looking sailboat could travel at such fast speeds. Despite being on the boat, he felt no turbulence whatsoever. On top of that, he noticed that the boat wasn't just flying straight but traversing through ‘barriers’.

Top experts of the Cultivation World could also temporarily travel in space, but no matter how long they flew, all they saw was the bleak void and desolate planets. They wouldn’t see such a brilliant sight. 

Sensing Zu An’s bewilderment, Employee 9527 explained with a smile, “This is spatial leap technology. It allows one to move at extreme speeds while traversing through normal world barriers.”

Zu An was awed. This technology is far superior to that of my previous world.

Employee 9527 soon stowed away the formation disc, and the boat automatically traveled toward the assigned coordinates. 

“How much longer will it take for us to arrive at the temporary world?” Zu An asked. 

“Using your world’s time calculation method, around two hours,” Employee 9527 replied politely. 

“That’s long!” Zu An was surprised that it would take that long despite them traveling so quickly. The temporary world had to be extremely far away from the Cultivation World. 

“This much precaution is required to ensure the safety of our guests.” Employee 9527 first verified that the boat was functioning properly before saying, “There’s still some time before reaching the temporary world, so why don’t we make a deal?”

Zu An was surprised. “What deal?”

Employee 9527 didn’t answer the question. Instead, he asked, “What do you think is the most important thing you need when exploring the Myriad Worlds?”

“Information,” Zu An replied without hesitation.

“Indeed.” Employee 9527’s eyes gleamed with approval. “Most people would answer 'strength', but there is bound to be someone stronger or possessing an ability that curbs you in the Myriad Worlds. That’s why it is important to collect intelligence beforehand to avert danger.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. “I presume you’re intending to sell information to me. Does the Universal Conglomerate know everything in the world?”

Employee 9527 shook his head. “The Myriad Worlds are vast. As formidable as our conglomerate is, we aren’t omniscient.”

He suddenly changed his tone and continued with a proud smile, “But we can answer most questions that people have.”

Zu An was awed. “May I know what currency the Universal Conglomerate uses?”

Employee 9527 replied with a smile, “There are many worlds across the universes, each one having their own currency. Unfortunately, what is valuable in one world can be commonplace in another. For instance, diamonds can be valuable gemstones in some worlds, but there are also worlds where diamonds rain from the sky.

“To deal with that issue, our Universal Conglomerate trades using credit points. All worlds trade with us using credit points, and we transport their resources to other worlds to sell and earn a profit.”

“That sounds fair.” Zu An wasn’t surprised. There were similar concepts in his previous world’s sci-fi novels.

“Of course it is. Our Universal Conglomerate will never take advantage of our customers!”

“What can I use to trade for credit points, and how many credit points would a piece of information cost?”

“Ores, weapons, armor, and cultivation methods can all be traded for credit points. However, your world isn’t particularly strong, so I fear you will struggle to earn enough credit points for even a piece of information. Secrets and intelligence are the most valuable assets in the Myriad Worlds.” Employee 9527 briefly paused before adding, “But if you’re willing to stake your World Law Beacon, you should be able to earn a decent amount of credit points.”

Zu An calmly replied, “You just mentioned that the Universal Conglomerate doesn’t take advantage of its customers. Do you think I don’t know how much a Wonderpoint World is worth?”

His friends and lovers resided in the Cultivation World. He wouldn’t sell it to anyone else even if it wasn’t a Wonderpoint World.

“This is a normal haggling method; it doesn’t defy our conglomerate’s principles.” Employee 9527 realized that his explanation wasn’t convincing, so he added, “But I was indeed a bit abrupt. Since this is our first meeting, in accordance with the Universal Conglomerate’s rules, I can provide you two free pieces of information. I’ll add an extra one in as a sign of my goodwill. I hope you will see our Universal Conglomerate in a positive light in the future.” 

In the Myriad Worlds, those who possessed a Wonderpoint World were all formidable powerhouses. While the man before him appeared weak at the moment, his potential wasn't to be underestimated. It would be wise to befriend him while he’s still weak. 

He'd thought he could exploit Zu An’s ignorance to buy the Wonderpoint World, but his recklessness had ended up piquing the other party’s displeasure. If the other party was vindictive, it could spell trouble for the Universal Conglomerate in the future. Resolving a future grudge with an additional piece of information was a worthwhile trade. 

Zu An’s expression alleviated a little. “My gratitude to you and the Universal Conglomerate.”

He pondered for a while before asking his first question, “What universal deities are there in the Myriad Worlds?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2706: Twelve Universal Deities
chapters.body:
Chapter 2706: Twelve Universal Deities

That question had been on Zu An’s mind for a long time. The Cultivation World lacked information about the Myriad Worlds, and Mi Li had been ambiguous about it. I hope that the Universal Conglomerate won’t disappoint me. 

Employee 9527 was startled, not expecting the question. He remarked, “Most people in the Myriad Worlds avoid discussing the universal deities, fearing it will bring disaster. However, our Universal Conglomerate deals with intelligence as well. We have the Deity of Order’s protection and the other universal deities’ tacit approval. We have permission to talk about them.” 

Zu An was awed. The Universal Conglomerate was able to obtain the tacit approval of the universal deities? They're stronger than I thought.

“This piece of information should have been worth a huge fortune, far beyond what most people can afford. I feel like I’m taking a huge loss here.” Employee 9527 felt deflated. 

Zu An smiled. “It’s my fortune to befriend you.”

Employee 9527’s awful complexion finally alleviated. This business could be worthwhile if he could earn the friendship of a future top expert. With this thought in mind, he began speaking, “There are thirteen universal deities logged in our records. It’s possible some universal deities might have passed away, and it’s also possible for there to be universal deities that we aren’t aware about. The Myriad Worlds are a huge place, after all.”

“I’d love to hear about them.” Zu An pricked up his ears. 

Employee 9527’s expression turned solemn. He took out a small table and placed all kinds of offerings on it. 

Zu An was surprised. This resembles some of the rituals recorded in the Baopu Sutra.

Employee 9527 took out an artifact and waved it around Zu An and himself. A peculiar melody played, and droplets of water began falling out of nowhere.

Zu An felt revitalized, as if he had been cleansed inside out. He was surprised. This must be some kind of sacred water or spirit spring water with the power to purge evil. It must have been worth a fortune.

Employee 9527 took out three incense sticks, which automatically lit up without him doing anything. He first prayed to the void before passing the incense to Zu An. “From my mouth to your ears, whatever I tell you today must never reach a third person’s ears, or else you’ll bring calamity upon yourself.”

“Of course!” By now, Zu An understood that secrets had value in the Myriad Worlds, and they could bring danger upon one too. He followed the other party’s instructions, taking the three incense sticks, praying to the void, and placing them on the table.

When he saw that the incense sticks didn’t go out, Employee 9527 breathed a sigh of relief. He began speaking, “First, there is the Deity of Memory, who records all information in the Myriad Worlds. There is a divergence among his worshipers. Some believe in only recording important figures, items, and events; anything else is a waste of time and effort. In any case, the Deity of Memory’s worshipers boast the widest span of knowledge and intelligence in the Myriad Worlds, with some of them knowing as much or even more than the Universal Conglomerate.” 

Zu An nodded. The Mirror Scribe he'd met, Unblind Zuoqiu, worshiped the Deity of Memory, and had given him the invitation to Dreamland. 

“There’s also the Deity of Chaos and Enigma. This deity…” Employee 9527 briefly paused to contemplate how to word his statement. “This deity’s jurisdiction appears to overlap with the Deity of Memory, but they are the opposite of each other.

“The Deity of Memory’s worshipers seek to record events happening in the Myriad Worlds, whereas the Deity of Chaos and Enigma’s worshipers seek to corrupt… Cough cough, I mean, alter events. There are many things now for which we don’t know whether they have truly happened or have been altered.”

The corners of Zu An’s lips slowly crept upward. Looks like my universal deity is quite the troublemaker. He had unintentionally carried out the duties of a Time Scribe, and he realized that his universal deity was very misunderstood.

Often, there wasn’t a clear distinction between truths and lies. Sometimes, beautiful lies were much better than harsh truths. Until the dust settled, it was possible to switch the truth with a lie. 

“Naturally, the Deity of Memory’s worshipers and Deity of Chaos and Enigma’s worshipers are nemeses.” Employee 9527 appeared to be holding himself back. “The Deity of Chaos and Enigma’s worshipers are often madmen or lunatics. They act without rhyme or reason, so we, the Deity of Order’s worshipers, are on bad terms with them too.

“You should steer clear if you ever encounter one, or else it could lead to endless trouble. Fortunately, the Deity of Chaos and Enigma has very few worshipers, so it’s unlikely you’ll ever meet one.”

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up. He hadn’t expected his universal deity to have such poor reputation, such that even her worshipers were practically outcasts in the Myriad Worlds. It’ll be a huge mess if Employee 9527 ever learns about my identity.

Employee 9527 suddenly asked out of curiosity, “I’m guessing you’re curious about the universal deities because you have encountered their powers. May I know what deity you worship?”

Zu An was briefly stunned. But his brain whirred to action, and he quickly replied, “I am the Deity of Memory’s worshiper.”

That Time Scribe had been impersonating a Mirror Scribe. It must be pretty common for Time Scribes to disguise themselves as Mirror Scribes. 

Employee 9527 was surprised. “I didn’t expect you to be the Deity of Memory’s worshiper. We might be able to trade information in the future.”

“Of course,” Zu An replied. I bet you’ll have the most interesting look on your face when you discover my identity in the future. 

Employee 9527 continued, “Our conglomerate worships the Deity of Order. Order is necessary in the Myriad Worlds, or else there’ll only be slaughter and calamities.” 

Zu An nodded in agreement. 

“Speaking of calamities, there is the Deity of Annihilation. He believes that civilizations, people, and all living beings are the scourge of the Myriad Worlds, depleting its essence. His worshipers seek to bring annihilation upon everything. Needless to say, they are widely feared in the Myriad Worlds.”

“I have encountered his worshipers in the past,” Zu An replied grimly. “May I know the name of the Deity of Annihilation? What powerful entities are there under him?”

The more information he gathered, the more prepared he would be to deal with the Deity of Annihilation. 

Employee 9527 shook his head. “The ritual grants us some level of immunity, but mentioning their names will still draw their gazes. That could spell the end of us.”

“I was too reckless.” Zu An was startled. So the Universal Conglomerate is fearful of this too.

“If you want information about the Deity of Annihilation, that will count as the second question,” Employee 9527 said with a smile.

“It’s fine. I’ll investigate it myself in the future.” Zu An had better questions to ask than to squander this precious opportunity. There was still time anyway. 

Regret flickered through Employee 9527’s eyes. “Let’s resume our topic. Next is the Deity of Nebula. Little is known about him, for those who seek to understand him will be assimilated and lose all feelings, becoming one with the nebula.” 

“In a way, he is more mysterious than the Deity of Chaos and Enigma, and he has very few worshipers. Some speculate that the Deity of Nebula is either the origin or the end of the Myriad Worlds. He is omnipresent, yet no one has ever seen him.”

Zu An heart shuddered. This resembled the universal deity whom Yan Xuehen had come into contact with. 

“What if one becomes his worshiper?”

Employee 9527 grimly replied, “That’s not a good thing. His worshipers can gain tremendous power, but they lose everything in the end. That’s why no one wishes to embark on that path. Almost all worshipers of the Deity of Nebula stumble into that path by accident, but by then, it is already too late for them to back out. They are doomed.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2707: The Death Of Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2707: The Death Of Death

Zu An’s heart sank into the abyss. When he first reunited with Yan Xuehen, the other party had appeared to be on the verge of ‘forgetting’ him. She'd mentioned coming into contact with a power that appeared to be even more advanced than Absolute Dispassion.

Absolute Dispassion already makes one forget many emotions; wouldn’t a higher level mean forgetting everything? That resembles the state of nebula that Employee 9527 described earlier.

“Is there no way for those who have already embarked on this path to avoid a tragic end?”

“Based on the current information we have collected, there is no solution.” Zu An’s agitation sparked a thought in Employee 9527’s head. “Esteemed guest, do you have a friend who is on that path too?”

Zu An nodded. Employee 9527 sighed. 

As expected of an entity who possesses a Wonderpoint World. He might be weak now, but he already harnesses tremendous fortune. Even the people around him have such extraordinary experiences. 

It’s extremely rare for anyone to step onto that path, whether knowingly or unknowingly.

The Deity of Nebula was feared, but his worshipers indeed wielded incredible fighting prowess. Becoming his worshiper was considered a fortuitous encounter that many envied in the Myriad Worlds.

“Please don’t be too worried, esteemed guest. Those who have embarked on this path will inevitably descend deeper into the abyss, but there is some time before they lose themselves,” Employee 9527 said in consolation.

Zu An was still worried. I'll have to tell Yan Xuehen about this when I return. She must stop practicing her Unshakable Daoist Manual. I should also warn Chuyan against following her master’s footsteps. 

Employee 9527 proceeded to introduce another universal deity, “While the Deity of Nebula induces his worshipers to lose themselves and see no meaning in the world, it’s the opposite for the Deity of Bliss and Desire. He seeks ultimate bliss. He believes that all beings have desires, but these desires are inhibited by restrictions we impose on ourselves. Only by releasing our innermost desires can we enjoy the most fundamental bliss in the world.”

Zu An remarked, “The Deity of Bliss and Desire must have many believers.”

It resembled the Devil Sect’s creed, though this was at a much higher level.

Employee 9527 burst into laughter. “Indeed. No matter the world, it is difficult for one to resist bliss and pleasure. The Deity of Bliss and Desire has many worshipers. I’ll also offer you an additional piece of information—among his worshipers are special entities known as Bliss Demonesses. They are in another class. Even the greatest experts take pride in sharing a night with a Bliss Demoness. They say there’s no greater thrill in the world.”

Judging from the other party’s envious look, Zu An could tell that Employee 9527 had never tasted that kind of sensation before. He took this opportunity to ask, “May I know what the traits of a Bliss Demoness are?” 

Since Employee 9527 was already volunteering information on his own accord, it would be foolish of Zu An not to probe deeper. More importantly, he was worried that Yun Jianyue would become a Bliss Demoness. What if it comes with side effects as well?

“A Bliss Demoness can bring bliss to others, but she also has a fundamental need for bliss. For that reason, renowned Bliss Demonesses tend to have a long list of lovers.” Employee 9527 revealed a look that all men understood. “If you ever encounter a Bliss Demoness, you might be able to snag her without taking the initiative.”

Zu An was speechless. He was at a loss for words. He'd thought it was already bad enough for Yan Xuehen to have caught the Deity of Nebula’s attention, but to think that Yun Jianyue was worse off! I have to stop her from venturing deeper into this path!

“Is it possible to veer onto another path after becoming a Bliss Demoness?” Zu An hurriedly asked.

“Similar to becoming a Deity of Nebula’s worshiper, once a person becomes a Bliss Demoness, they won’t be able to break free anymore. Besides, that is a blissful thing for them. They can’t bear to let go of that happiness, so why would they veer off?” Employee 9527 suddenly thought about something and asked in astonishment, “Do you, by any chance, know a Bliss Demoness too?”

Zu An replied with a sigh, “I have a lover who has received the Deity of Bliss and Desire’s gaze. I don’t know if she’ll become a Bliss Demoness.”

Employee 9527 was astounded. “Amazing! You have both a Deity of Nebula’s worshiper and a Bliss Demoness with you!”

He was glad that he had invested in Zu An. No wonder he became a Wonderpoint World’s master. He wields incredible luck and potential.

“But that doesn’t sound to be a good thing,” Zu An exclaimed with a frown. 

Employee 9527 stared at Zu An. Well, I don’t think it’s necessarily a bad thing for her, though it is a bad thing for you. You’re going to be cuckolded real bad. Of course, he knew better than to say those words aloud.  

“Don’t worry too much, esteemed guest. There are all kinds of peculiar abilities in the Myriad Worlds. There might be something out there that can suppress her desire.”

Zu An was speechless.

Employee 9527 quickly changed the subject. “Moving on, there is the Deity of Longevity. All civilizations in the Myriad Worlds, no matter how prosperous or prestigious, have an intrinsic desire for longevity, especially those in positions of power. Their strong desire for eternal life created the Deity of Longevity.”

Zu An was taken aback. “Are there beings in this world who can live forever?”

In human history, Qin Shihuang had sought the immortality medicine, and the other emperors had likely tried the same too. The greater their power and authority, the more they feared death. 

“It’s possible to live for a very long time—the Deity of Longevity can greatly extend a person’s lifespan—but death remains inevitable. Death is a fundamental law that has existed since the creation of the Myriad Worlds.” Employee 9527 knew Zu An possessed exceptional luck, so he gave the latter a bonus piece of information. “The Deity of Longevity created different immortal medicines that can resurrect a person, allowing them to live a second life. Each one can send experts into a rampage vying over it.”

Zu An was surprised. He hadn’t expected the immortal medicines to be from the Deity of Longevity.

“With longevity naturally comes death, but there are rumors that the Deity of Death is dead. However, he still has some worshipers. Perhaps a new Deity of Death might be crowned in the near future,” Employee 9527 said. 

“The Deity of Death can die too?” Zu An was baffled.

“Of course. Some say he was killed by the Deity of Annihilation. Some say he was devoured by the Deity of Nebula. There are also speculations that he died naturally. No one knows the truth.” Employee 9527 decided to sell Zu An a favor and provide him with more information.

A thought popped into Zu An’s mind. The Netherworld was emptied out. Could that be due to the demise of the Deity of Death?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2708: The Most Beautiful Woman In The Myriad Worlds
chapters.body:
Chapter 2708: The Most Beautiful Woman In The Myriad Worlds

Zu An had been wondering what had happened in the Netherworld to cause it to be emptied overnight. The Netherworld had been protected by the Shaman races, ghost deities, and the Six Paths of Reincarnation. It could even stand its ground against an invasion from the Celestial Court. 

But the inscription on the Reincarnation Platform was so brief that it appeared that the Netherworld had been destroyed in a very short time, such that even Ox Head and Horse Face didn’t have time to leave complete information.

So, he asked Employee 9527 about it. He thought that it was worth a question. 

“The Netherworld?” Employee 9527 frowned. “It sounds like it could be related to the Deity of Death, but my apologies, the Universal Conglomerate has no information about that. Perhaps you might have better luck with the Deity of Memory’s worshipers. Since I’m unable to answer this question, I’ll let you ask another one.”

Zu An was intrigued. “Have you memorized all the Universal Conglomerate’s information?”

It was amazing how Employee 9527 could answer his questions right away each time. 

“Of course not.” Employee 9527 smiled. “Who knows how many civilizations there are in the Myriad Worlds? I can’t possibly remember everything. We’re implanted with a chip that grants us restricted access to the conglomerate’s database, depending on our access rights, so that we can strike deals.”

“Could your access rights be insufficient to access that piece of information?” Zu An asked, refusing to give up. 

“That’s not the case here.” Employee 9527 shook his head. “The database will notify us as such if our access rights are too low to access a piece of information. In such cases, we can contact a colleague with higher access rights to handle the exchange. However, the database indicated that it doesn’t have the information you’re looking for.”

“I see.” Zu An was disappointed. At the same time, he was amazed by how the Universal Conglomerate had access to not only cultivation treasures but also sci-fi technology. No wonder their business got this big. 

Employee 9527 continued, “All beings fear death. Some overcome it by pursuing longevity, maximizing their individual potential. Others go a different route, pursuing quantity over quality. They frantically reproduce to ensure the continuation of their race and bloodline.

“Naturally, there is also the Deity of Reproduction. As long as a single member of their race survives, they will be able to make a comeback. Such swiftly reproducing races are a huge headache for countless civilizations.”

Zu An was reminded of sunfishes and Antarctica krill from his previous life—those were animals who relied on mass reproduction to ensure the survival of their species. To think there would be such races in the Myriad Worlds too!

“Reproduction isn’t just limited to biological reproduction. For instance, the Deity of Reproduction’s worshipers from technological civilizations may also engage in actions such as cloning,” Employee 9527 explained. 

Zu An was reminded of a famous anti-fan of a gaming company in his previous life. The gaming company had kept reporting the anti-fan’s social media account to stop him from spreading negative information, but a new one would just pop out right after. The gaming company couldn’t stop the anti-fan no matter what it tried.

I guess that anti-fan should count as a Deity of Reproduction worshiper. 

“Next is the Deity of Love and Beauty. Her worshipers believe the meaning of life is beauty. They pursue everything beautiful, be it in appearance, buildings, sceneries, or the universe. In technological civilizations, it can even be applied to physics and mathematical equations. Often, they judge an equation by its aesthetics before it is proven to be true.”

Zu An was baffled. Huh? That works too?

“Unlike other universal deities, the Deity of Love and Beauty is the only confirmed female deity. It is also recorded in our database that she is the most beautiful woman in the Myriad Worlds. However, it has been a long time since anyone has seen her.” Employee 9527’s eyes shone as he spoke about the Deity of Love and Beauty. It seemed as if his greatest regret was having yet to see her.

“Are the other deities genderless?” Zu An asked out of curiosity.

“Of course. Universal deities can’t be judged in the same way as normal beings. Gender holds no meaning to them,” Employee 9527 replied.

I guess the Deity of Love and Beauty must be truly narcissistic to insist on existing as a woman.

“If you obtain news about the Deity of Love and Beauty, make sure to sell it to me. Such information will land me consecutive promotions in the Universal Conglomerate. You can trust me to compensate you handsomely for that,” Employee 9527 said with beaming eyes.

Zu An smiled. “You’re overestimating me. I doubt someone from a remote world like me will stumble upon the Deity of Love and Beauty when no one else in the vast Myriad Worlds knows about her whereabouts.”

“That’s hard to say. You are blessed with tremendous fortune.” Despite his words, Employee 9527 didn’t think Zu An would be lucky enough to stumble upon the Deity of Love and Beauty either. He continued his introduction, “Up next is the Deity of Knowledge. All civilized races hold knowledge in high regard and seek to understand the universes’ fundamental laws. The Deity of Knowledge’s worshipers are more common in technological civilizations.

“The Universal Conglomerate hires the Deity of Knowledge’s worshipers to resolve our problems. The chips in our heads that allow us to access the database were developed by them.” 

Zu An figured the world he'd lived in prior to his transmigration could be considered a technological civilization too. In fact, most cultivators devoted their lives to comprehending dao. That was pursuit of knowledge, too. 

“There’s also the Deity of Protection. From ants to higher entities, all beings have things they seek to protect. Naturally, there is bound to be a Deity of Protection as well.”

Zu An guessed that Mohism’s believers from his previous life would worship the Deity of Protection if they knew about his existence. Curious, he asked, “Then what does the Deity of Protection himself seek to protect?”

Employee 9527 was taken aback. “I don’t know. Some say he has devoted himself to protecting the Myriad Worlds to keep them from being destroyed, but who would have the ability to destroy the Myriad Worlds?”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Even if the sky collapsed, there were universal deities to shoulder the burden. These were problems for them to worry about, not him. 

“I have already answered one of your questions. What else do you want to ask?” Employee 9527 felt as if he was taking a huge loss. The information he had just revealed far surpassed the value of a single question.

“I’d like information about Dreamland.” There was a lot of things Zu An wanted to know about, but his priority at hand was still learning about Dreamland. Qi Yaoguang’s prophecy had piqued his vigilance. This trip was likely to be fraught with danger. He should first overcome the crisis at hand before thinking about other matters. 

Employee 9527 frowned. “The scope of your question is too big. I feel like I’m taking a huge loss here.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2709: No Survivors
chapters.body:
Chapter 2709: No Survivors

Zu An clasped his fist and said, “It’s truly my fortune to have met you. I’ll etch in my mind the help you've provided me.”

He had indeed taken advantage of the other party by posing questions with wide scopes. Thus, he thought it was only right for him to provide the other party with some emotional value too.

It worked. Employee 9527’s frown was alleviated as he began explaining, “Dreamland is a highly elusive place with little ties to the outside world. There is a long interval between each opening, and the interval varies. A long time ago, it didn’t even open once every ten thousand years, but more recently, it has been opening once every millennium.”

“I heard that Dreamland can fulfill any wish. Is that true?” Zu An was curious.

“Not even universal deities can fulfill any wish,” Employee 9527 replied with a smile. “But Dreamland is indeed a mystical place. Our information indicates that it can fulfill wishes within a reasonable degree, especially since most people’s wishes aren’t that hard to fulfill. If one has the resources to deal with our Universal Conglomerate, we can fulfill most of their dreams, too.”

Zu An frowned. “But unlike the Universal Conglomerate, Dreamland isn’t an organization with connections and resources across universes. It can’t fulfill wishes the same way the Universal Conglomerate does.”

“Indeed. We have been investigating the methods employed by Dreamland to fulfill wishes, but we haven’t been able to figure it out,” Employee 9527 remarked.

“Not even the Universal Conglomerate has been able to figure it out?” Zu An was taken aback. To think there are things that even the most powerful conglomerate is unable to figure out!

“We have had employees who entered Dreamland, but most did not return. Those who returned struggled to explain Dreamland’s inner workings. Furthermore, the interval between each Dreamland opening is too long, and the place doesn’t seem too dangerous. In the end, we decided not to bother with it,” Employee 9527 explained. 

“Did no one return with news from the previous Dreamland opening?” Zu An frowned. Dreamland was only sounding more and more baffling to him. 

“No one returned from Dreamland during its last opening,” Employee 9527 replied grimly.

“Huh?!” Zu An was shocked. “Doesn’t that make Dreamland public enemy number one? Why did you mention it didn’t seem too dangerous earlier?”

“Those people chose to stay in Dreamland voluntarily.” Sensing Zu An’s doubtful gaze, Employee 9527 elaborated, “They contacted their friends and family beforehand to inform them of their decision to stay in Dreamland.”

“Could they have been coerced into it?”

Employee 9527 shook his head. “That can’t be. There are powerful experts among those who chose to stay in Dreamland. Putting aside whether Dreamland has the means to simultaneously coerce so many people, there should be no way for them to pull that off without any anomalies.”

“And this is not the first time something like that has happened. The last few times it opened, most people were reluctant to leave Dreamland. Think about it—it’s a place where dreams can be fulfilled. It’s only normal for one to not want to leave.”

“Based on what I have heard, a corresponding price has to be paid for a wish to be fulfilled. It’s not like Dreamland is operating a charity. I doubt Dreamland will continue to fulfill all their wishes just because they choose to stay there forever,” Zu An said.

Employee 9527 was awed. “There would be far fewer wars in the world if most people could be as rational as you. You are right, but you have to put yourself in their shoes. The temptation is not that easy to resist.”

Zu An was alarmed. I mustn’t fall for Dreamland’s temptation. There are too many people waiting for me back home. “Do you know which universal deity Dreamland falls under?”

“As I told you, we know very little about Dreamland,” Employee 9527 said. “Our staff has discussed this matter, and we are split into two main factions. One faction believes Dreamland belongs to the Deity of Bliss and Desire because it fulfills wishes. It’s hard to imagine anyone other than the Deity of Bliss and Desire accomplishing such a feat.”

Zu An nodded. Many people have refused to leave after setting foot in Dreamland. That fulfills the trait of losing oneself in desire.

“The other faction thinks they belong to the Deity of Memory,” Employee 9527 assessed Zu An’s reaction as he spoke.

“The Deity of Memory?” Zu An was taken aback.

“Indeed. Among the Deity of Memory’s worshipers is a branch that specializes in crafting dreamscapes. Some of our staff believe the easiest way to fulfill a person’s dream is within a dream,” Employee 9527 replied with a smile. “You are a Deity of Memory worshiper. I thought you would have heard about this.”

Zu An shook his head. “As you know, I've been living in a remote corner. It’s only in recent days that I learned more about the Myriad Worlds. This is the first time I’m hearing details about Dreamland, so there’s no way I could have known such information beforehand.”

“That’s true.” Employee 9527 didn’t doubt Zu An. “Since you’re a Deity of Memory worshiper, you might have a huge advantage in Dreamland if it is under the Deity of Memory’s jurisdiction.”

Zu An responded with a smile, but his heart swelled with bitterness. But I’m a Time Scribe instead of a Mirror Scribe. That puts me on the opposite side of the Deity of Memory! Who knows what fate will befall me if I get discovered?

“Please let me know if you ever find out which universal deity Dreamland worships. I’ll compensate you generously,” Employee 9527 said. “The same applies to any valuable information. All information about Dreamland is worth a lot.”

“Of course. It’s my fortune to have befriended you. But how should I contact you in the future? Should I visit one of the Universal Conglomerate’s branches?” Zu An asked. 

“You can reach me by reporting my employee number at any branch, but that’s too troublesome.” Employee 9527 handed him a little cube. “You can use this to contact me instead.” 

Zu An was intrigued. The cube resembled an antiquated Nokia phone, but it only had two buttons. One resembled the pick up button, and the other resembled a hang up button. 

Employee 9527 taught him how to use it. “You might not be able to use this inside Dreamland. That place is too enigmatic. Our conglomerate sent employees in with this kind of communication tool. We were still able to reach them at the start, but we soon lost contact.”

Zu An’s heart shuddered. Looks like this trip is going to be fraught with danger. I need to be extremely careful. Seeing that there was no more useful information about Dreamland, he decided to pose his third question. “I have a friend who is trapped in a place for a thousand years…”

Upon learning Dreamland could potentially only be able to fulfill wishes within a dream, Zu An became worried it couldn’t resolve his problems, so he decided to pose his question to the Universal Conglomerate too. He could always find the solution for Mi Li’s body elsewhere. 

As for the issues concerning the Goddess of Wu Mountain and Chang’e, he dearly hated the fact that he couldn’t ask more questions.

Employee 9527 quickly searched the database before replying, “Saving your friend is both easy and hard.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2710: Spaceship
chapters.body:
Chapter 2710: Spaceship

“What do you mean by that?” Zu An asked.

“The situation you have described is the result of a special world law. Being the master of the world grants you control over its world laws, but you are unable to rewrite them. You can directly rewrite them once you become strong enough, thus saving your friend. Most people struggle to reach that level, however, which is where the difficulty lies,” Employee 9527 said. 

“That said, the Myriad Worlds are a huge place. You don’t have to reach that level yourself. You don’t even require the help of a universal deity; there are experts beneath them capable of that feat as well. Otherwise, you can also achieve the same effect through the use of a treasure.

“Still, you’ll need luck to stumble upon an expert of that caliber, and even so, you’ll likely have to pay a hefty price to solicit their help. That’s why I said it can be both easy and hard.”

Zu An nodded, having understood what the other party was saying. He asked out of curiosity, “May I know how power levels are classified in the Myriad Worlds? How strong of an expert do I need to find to save my friend?”

“There are countless worlds and civilizations in the Myriad Worlds. Your world has its own standards for classifying cultivation levels, but the names and standards can be very different in another world. Besides, abilities can complement and curb one another—an expert in one world can be curbed by a weak ability in another world. Who’s to say who is stronger?”

Zu An frowned. This made things tricky.

“A simpler evaluation method gradually arose as civilizations interacted, such as planet level, star level, galaxy level, and universe level… It measures the rough extent of one's destructive prowess. Beneath planet level are the weaker cultivators in each civilization. In the context of the Myriad Worlds, they are all ants. You’re roughly at planet level right now.

“But these distinctions are vague and inaccurate. The Myriad Worlds is huge, as I have said before. Through the use of mystical artifacts or special abilities, a planet level can defeat a star level.”

Zu An knew Employee 9527 was consoling him, but he didn’t pay it much heed. His fighting style was indeed reliant on special abilities. He wondered where the celestials in the ancient era fell in this classification method. 

“How strong would one have to be to rewrite the world laws trapping my friend?”

Employee 9527 pondered before replying, “That’s hard to say. Some people are strong but lack in their grasp of laws. Some people are weak but specialize in this field. I’m also not sure how strong the law is, as I have never been to the place you mentioned. However, I reckon star level should be enough…”

“Are there different strengths to world laws, too?” Zu An asked.

“Of course! Stronger worlds are bound to have stronger spatial laws than weaker worlds. The gap between planet levels can sometimes be larger than the gap between a planet level and a star level,” Employee 9527 explained.

Zu An recalled his understanding of the world from his previous life and nodded in realization. Many planets in the universe were as large as stars, but they didn’t metamorphose into stars because they lacked something critical or needed more time to build up. These planets could be thousands of times larger than other ordinary planets. 

“I have answered your three questions. Just on time. We’re reaching our destination soon.” Employee 9527 got up and controlled the boat using his formation disc. 

A white light flashed ahead of them, and an entrance appeared.

Zu An was surprised. He was certain there had been nothing ahead of him earlier. He probably wouldn’t have noticed anything amiss even if he flew past this area countless times. 

As the boat entered the door, their surroundings suddenly lit up. They transited from pitch-black space into a vibrant world with a blue sky, white clouds, and warm sunlight. This world had no ground, or perhaps the clouds were the ground. 

A massive ship floated above the clouds. It was so majestic that it made the boat they were riding appear like a little dinghy. What surprised Zu An was that the ship resembled the spaceships he had seen in his previous life’s sci-fi movies, just that it looked even more streamlined and high-tech. Its black metallic body gleamed with a brilliant luster, and there was a huge image on it that appeared to be the Universal Conglomerate’s logo.

Zu An’s instinct told him that the ship had terrifying offensive prowess, and that it wouldn’t be a good idea to make an enemy of it. 

Employee 9527 tapped on the formation disc. A hole appeared beneath the massive spaceship, and a brilliant light shone on their boat. 

Zu An’s surroundings blurred as the boat was sucked into the massive ship. The first thing he saw inside was a dock, with many boats in place. Uniformed staff members had their hands full inspecting the boats. 

Zu An was perplexed. “Why are there such huge differences in the boats?”

Some looked to be built in the feudal era, whereas some were high-tech spaceships. It was bizarre seeing them side by side. 

“Our Universal Conglomerate deals with all kinds of civilizations across the universes, including those specializing in technology and magic. To cope with different potential risks, we don’t limit our arsenals to just that of a single civilization, or else it would be disastrous if we encounter one that happens to curb it,” Employee 9527 replied smoothly. Clearly, Zu An wasn’t the first guest to have asked this question. 

Zu An was surprised. “Are there civilizations that are completely immune to technology or magic?”

“The universe is huge. There will always be enemies beyond your imagination. For instance, your Cultivation World has ghosts and apparition entities that are immune to physical attacks,” Employee 9527 explained grimly. “Since we operate across universes, there is no harm in being more prepared.”

Even an organization as huge as the Universal Conglomerate had such great respect for the Myriad Worlds. Zu An’s expression turned even more severe as he prayed that his luck wouldn’t be too bad.

“Follow me.” Employee 9527 led Zu An into the spaceship. They first passed through an automatic door that verified their identity, followed by several lifts. Along the way, Employee 9527 asked out of curiosity, “Hm? Based on what I know, your Cultivation World has never come into contact with a technological civilization before. You seem rather nonchalant about all these things, as if you are already used to it.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2711: Fatal Mistake
chapters.body:
Chapter 2711: Fatal Mistake

Employee 9527 had fetched countless people before, and even the most composed person from a cultivation-esque world tended to ask questions about the spaceship and everything about it. And yet this man was so calm. This was at odds with the intelligence he had received.

Could he be a spy sent by other powers? That sparked Employee 9527’s vigilance. 

Sensing the other party’s body tensing up, Zu An quickly explained, “I chanced upon a dungeon connected to a technological civilization, so automatic doors and elevators aren’t foreign to me.” He wouldn’t easily reveal that he was a transmigrator, so it was fortunate there was another convenient excuse here.

Employee 9527 breathed a sigh of relief. “I’d expect no less from a Wonderpoint World. It is linked to so many different dungeons. Most worlds hardly have dungeons at all.”

Zu An was surprised. Are dungeons that rare? I guess the Cultivation World is hiding many secrets. Worried the other party would continue probing, he quickly changed the topic. “How many guests are there on this ship?”

“There are four other guests. You’ll be able to meet them very soon.” Employee 9527 gestured to the facilities and began introducing them. “That is our practice room. It provides our guests with ki, spiritual energy, battle energy, mana, or anything they require for their training. You should cultivate several times faster in there than usual.

“Alternatively, you can also set the training room to ‘Combat Mode’. The system will randomly choose a battlefield and an enemy for you. You can fight to your heart’s desire without worrying about dying.”

“That’s incredible!” Zu An was amazed. 

It would take an immortal-tier weapon or a top-notch dungeon to achieve these effects in the Cultivation World, yet this was merely an ordinary training room on this spaceship. This went to show just how powerful the Universal Conglomerate was. 

“It is a long journey, after all. It’s our pleasure to provide our guests with the greatest degree of convenience possible. Most of our guests are disciplined and seek to constantly train to grow stronger. Forcing them to idle is worse than death to them. Moreover, they can also vent inside the training room, making it less likely for them to cause trouble on the ship,” Employee 9527 replied with a smile. “You can give it a try when you have time. Don’t worry; all of our facilities, including food, are free. Dreamland has already paid the tab on your behalf.”

Zu An nodded. I should give it a try, since I’ve reached a bottleneck in the Cultivation World. This is a chance for me to get a glimpse of other combat styles in the Myriad Worlds, too.

“Aside from training rooms, we also have beaches, hot springs, swimming pools, and other amenities. You can relax there when you’re fatigued,” Employee 9527 said as he gestured to the other amenities.

Zu An was surprised. Space should have been precious on the spaceship, yet there were so many indulgent entertainment facilities in it. The Universal Conglomerate sure is wealthy!

Employee 9527 suddenly lowered his voice and chuckled. “If you are seeking another kind of fun, you can try out our spa massage room. Our attendants are meticulously-selected beauties, coming in every style you can think of. Your imagination is the limit there! Of course, that will be charged separately.”

Zu An was speechless.

Out of curiosity, he asked, “Do you have that many attendants on standby on every ship?” 

They had encountered some female staff members along the way, but they were far from being as diverse as Employee 9527 described. 

Employee 9527 laughed. “There’s no way we would provide so many female attendants on every ship. Our protocol is to first confirm the guest list before summoning them over using our teleportation technology. It looks like you’re interested. I’ll send you a list later. You can browse their 3D holograms. You won’t be disappointed!”

Zu An smiled awkwardly. “I’ll pass today. I’m a little tired.”

You must be kidding! I have many lovers waiting for me at home. How can I go philandering around here?

Employe 9527 wasn’t surprised by his answer. “I understand. It’s your first time venturing beyond your world, so there must be a lot on your mind right now. If you do feel lonely at any point in time, feel free to tell me.”

Zu An responded with a sheepish smile. 

“That’s the canteen. Right ahead of us is the bar. Our guests frequently drink and chat there while enjoying the scenery outside.”

Employee 9527 brought Zu An into an exquisite bar. It was graceful and quiet. There weren’t many seats, and they were scattered around to grant privacy among the guests. Its walls were floor-to-ceiling glass panels that allowed one to see the scenery outside clearly. 

But Zu An was in no mood to admire the scenery outside; he was more interested in the guests at the bar. 

Seated closest to the entrance was a peculiar youth with seemingly translucent skin and no mouth. His eyes were completely black, with no irises. Or rather, it was practically filled with irises; as upon careful examination, Zu An noticed specks of light inside the youth's eyes that were reminiscent of the night sky.

Despite looking young, his hair was sparse like that of a middle-aged man with a receding hair pattern. It felt as if a gust of wind could pull away his hair. Zu An wondered if the youth was a stressed-out programmer on the 996 schedule[1]. 

Seated by the glass panels was an old woman dressed in rags. A glance was enough to make Zu An’s stomach churn. He had never seen such an ugly woman. Her face was covered in meat lumps of all shapes and sizes; anyone with trypophobia[2] would convulse on the spot. 

Zu An quickly averted his gaze and turned his attention toward the old man seated in the innermost area of the bar. The latter was also hideous with glaring scars on his face, but he could easily be considered dashing compared to the old woman. He seemed crippled as he was seated in a wheelchair. 

But Zu An didn’t dare to underestimate the old man, as the latter was shrouded in a translucent flame that felt extremely intimidating even tens of meters away. 

“%￥@*！#￥【、】+1…!” The old man harrumphed before taking his leave. 

It was as if the wheelchair was part of his body, wheeling him out of the bar without being maneuvered.

Zu An was baffled. He couldn’t understand what the other party was saying. He turned to Employee 9527 with confused eyes, only to see the latter looking a little awkward. The old man must have uttered something unpleasant. 

A voice echoed in Zu An’s mind. “He said ‘Here I was wondering how incredible a master of a Wonderpoint World must be, but he turned out to be a weakling.'”

Zu An was speechless. He tracked the voice and realized the one who had just spoken was the youth with transparent skin and sparse hair. He was baffled as the youth had no mouth. 

Sensing his confusion, the youth replied, “I’m conversing with you through psychic waves.” He shook his head and turned around to continue enjoying the scenery, seemingly having lost his interest in Zu An. 

The old woman covered in meat lumps cackled as she limped her way out of her seat with the aid of a walking stick.

Zu An realized he had made a fatal error. There could be many languages even within a single world, let alone across the Myriad Worlds, yet he'd overlooked this issue. This could make his trip to Dreamland far more dangerous. 

1. An intense schedule first popularized in mainland China, known as “996” - or 9 am to 9 pm, six days a week. In other words, it's a 72-hour work week. ☜

2. Trypophobia is an aversion or strong disgust experienced when viewing clusters of small holes or bumps, not necessarily a fear of holes themselves. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2712: A Deity Of Love And Beauty Worshiper
chapters.body:
Chapter 2712: A Deity Of Love And Beauty Worshiper

Employee 9527 had been talking to him in his world’s language, so Zu An had neglected the communication barrier. It was only when he failed to comprehend what the person in the wheelchair was saying that this occurred to him.

The most important thing on a battlefield was intelligence, but how was he to procure information when he couldn’t speak their language? If he couldn’t understand what anyone was saying, the only fate awaiting him was death. If only he had realized this problem beforehand, he could have consulted Employee 9527 about this. Who knew how much it would cost if he posed that question now?

Employee 9527 led him into an empty room next. Noticing Zu An’s bewilderment, Employee 9527 explained with a smile, “Our guests come from different worlds. It is difficult to come up with a standardized room design because of their different interests and lifestyle habits, so we decided to let our guests arrange their own rooms instead.”

“Arrange their rooms?” Zu An was taken aback. 

Employee 9527 lightly tapped on a crystal ball on the table beside him, and the crystal ball lit up. “Place your hand on the crystal ball and imagine a room you feel comfortable in.” 

Zu An placed his hand on the crystal ball, and the scenery abruptly changed. It first transformed into his room in Brightmoon City, then the residence on Mount Yuquan, and then his room in the imperial palace… 

Taken aback, he hurriedly retracted his hand.

Employee 9527 chuckled. “You can design your room however you want to. I shan’t inconvenience you any further with my presence here. If you’re hungry, you may head to the canteen or call for room service. I wish you a pleasant journey with us.”

He was about to leave when Zu An stopped him and voiced out his concern. Then, he asked, “How do the other guests overcome the communication barrier?”

“There are mainstream languages that experts from each world use to converse with one another.” Employee 9527 was startled. “Doesn’t your world have such language books?”

Zu An shook his head. “Our world has never interacted with other civilizations except in dungeons.”

The Monster World was the only other civilization they had interacted with. Thinking back, he had learned the demon language back then for that purpose, though the demon language was unlikely to be a mainstream language in the Myriad Worlds. 

“That complicates things. I was able to converse with you because we knew which world you were from through the invitation letter, and the conglomerate assigned me a translator for your language.” Employee 9527 retrieved a small chip from his throat. What he spoke next sounded guttural; Zu An couldn’t comprehend at all. He quickly pasted the chip back on his throat and continued, “My apologies; that was a demonstration.”

Then, he continued, “I have backup translators, and I can give one to you in view of our friendship. However, our translators only cover the languages of the other guests and the major powers. It can’t translate the languages of the other civilizations.”

Zu An was delighted. “I will surely repay this favor in the future!”

Employee 9527 was worried. “Don’t thank me yet. Our translators are connected to our spaceship’s database—that’s how it does its live translation. But Dreamland is a peculiar place where our signals are unable to reach. Our translators will not work in there.”

“Do you not have offline translators?” Zu An asked.

Employee 9527 was surprised that Zu An knew about that. “Our conglomerate has offline translators, but they are very expensive. Information is the most valuable commodity in the Myriad Worlds, and the number of civilizations and languages in the Myriad Worlds is as many as the stars in the sky. Offline translators can only cover some of the mainstream languages, and even so, the information they contain is already colossal.

“You’ll have to trade for one with resources, as you don’t have any credit points yet. What do you plan to use for the trade? I’ll apply for an offline translator for you right now, but the processing time for valuable commodities tends to take longer. It’ll be best if you can find something comparable for the exchange before my superiors process the request.”

He quickly took his leave afterward.

Zu An fell into deep thought. What do I have that can interest the Universal Conglomerate? They will be interested in the Wonderpoint World, but I can’t use that for the trade. They should be interested in an immortal medicine too, but… 

While he was deep in thought, he heard knocks on his door.

“Who is it?”

“I heard there’s a new guest on the ship, so I came here to greet you.” It was a feminine voice that sounded sweet and energetic even through the door. 

Zu An’s recent experiences left him in no mood to philander. He wanted to turn the other party down, when it suddenly occurred to him that the other party was speaking his world’s language. Thus, he opened the door and saw a beautiful woman dressed in red standing outside. 

Even Zu An had to concede that the other party was truly gorgeous. Few in the Cultivation World could rival her looks. 

The woman’s eyes lit up upon seeing Zu An. “You look dashing. Completely unlike the others.” 

Zu An was speechless.

He couldn’t agree with her lest he unknowingly offend the other guests. “Miss, are you a passenger too? Why didn’t I see you at the bar earlier?”

The red-robed woman pouted. “A glance at those fellows is enough to make me lose my appetite for three days. There’s no way I would share a space with them.”

Zu An thought about the people he had met in the bar. The black-cloaked old woman was hideous, and the wheelchair-bound old man was disfigured. The only one who looked normal was the person with translucent skin but balding hair.

“I am Firework. What’s your name?” The red-robed woman suddenly looked at Zu An with widened eyes, her black, curly eyebrows fluttered in place. 

“You’re indeed as beautiful as fireworks. I'm Zu An,” Zu An replied. It felt pleasant to talk to a beautiful woman. 

“You’re good-looking and have a glib tongue. The only thing you’re lacking is a good name.” The red-robed woman harrumphed. “Do you intend to continue chatting with me here? Aren’t you going to invite me in for a seat?”

“That might not be appropriate.”

“What’s up with all these useless rules? I just want a place to sit down so that we can converse.”

It would be impolite for Zu An to turn her down when she had already said as much, so he invited her into his room while remaining vigilant. Someone who could board this spaceship was unlikely to be naive. She could be plotting something. 

“Is this your world’s design aesthetic? I see beauty principles being applied everywhere. When do you plan to bring me over for a tour?” The red-robed woman looked at the room’s layout and nodded in approval. 

Zu An was using his room in Mount Yuquan’s residence. He replied with a smile, “Could you be a worshiper of the Deity of Love and Beauty?”

The red-robed woman blinked. “Isn’t it rude to ask about a woman’s faith right away? But since you’re so good-looking, I shall make an exception and answer your question. Yes, I worship the Deity of Love and Beauty, and your appearance fulfills the requirement to be a worshiper, too. Would you like to join my faith?”

Zu An was speechless. He hadn’t expected the woman to ask him to change his faith right away. 

He coughed softly. “I heard the Deity of Love and Beauty has already passed away.”

This was a good chance to fish for some information. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2713: Doubtful Intent
chapters.body:
Chapter 2713: Doubtful Intent

“Who says she’s dead? She just hasn’t shown herself for many years.” The red-robed woman harrumphed. “Besides, love and beauty will persist even if she passes away. It only means that there’s a chance for worshipers like us to become a universal deity.”

Zu An nodded. By now, he understood that every universal deity represented a different path. The daos had existed before the universal deities came into being; the latter merely represented the end of their respective paths. 

A universal deity’s death merely meant that the highest entity on their respective path had vanished, but the path itself still existed. It was just a question of which worshiper would reach the endpoint first and become the new universal deity.

“So, which universal deity do you worship?” the red-robed woman asked out of curiosity.

“The Deity of Memory,” Zu An replied. The Deity of Chaos and Enigma sure is pitiful. Their worshipers have to pretend to be other universal deities’ worshipers lest they get pummeled.

The red-robed woman immediately became vigilant. “I forbid you from recording my ugly sides.”

“Didn’t you say we’re friends? I wouldn’t record a friend’s ugly sides,” Zu An replied with a smile.

The red-robed woman finally smiled. “You’re a decent lad. You’re much more interesting than those companions of yours.”

Zu An knew she was referring to other Deity of Memory worshipers. Worried that he would give himself away if this conversation persisted, he changed the topic and asked, “How do you know my world’s language?”

Employee 9527 had mentioned that only he had the translator because he was in charge of these guests. The guests were relying on their own methods to communicate. The wheelchair bound old man and the black-robed old woman didn’t understand his language, but after seeing how weak he was, they couldn't be bothered to learn. 

“Fluttering Baldie taught me,” the red-robed woman replied with a grin. Her eyes were curved into crescents, making her look exceptionally beautiful.

Zu An was confused.

“You dumbo. It’s that translucent being.” Firework put her hands on her waist and harrumphed.

“Is that your nickname for him?”

“That’s his own name!” 

Zu An nearly burst into laughter. He wouldn’t have expected that balding translucent fellow to have such a name. But again, it wasn't long ago that he'd met ‘Unblind Zuoqiu’ too. People in the Myriad Worlds weren’t particular about their names. Names like ‘Firework’ appeared to be the convention.

“Does he know a lot of languages?” Zu An remembered how the other party had conversed with him through telepathy, looking extremely enigmatic. 

“Of course! He’s a Deity of Knowledge worshiper, pursuing all knowledge and wisdom. Fluttering Baldie specializes in languages. It’s not just limited to your world; there is practically no language he doesn’t know in the Myriad Worlds.” Firework assessed Zu An’s room layout as she replied. 

Zu An was surprised. He hadn’t expected the balding middle-aged man to be so formidable. Employee 9527 had just told him that the Universal Conglomerate’s translator only supported the languages of mainstream civilizations, but the balding middle-aged man knew all kinds of remote languages!

He remarked, “Brother Baldie is incredible.”

“He’s not that amazing.” Firework casually sat on a table and shook her legs. “The hair of the Deity of Knowledge’s worshipers becomes sparser as they advance their cultivation. Knowledge and wisdom are highly demanding on brain power, so they need to channel every bit of their energy to support their brain. Hair follicles, which compete for nutrition, are useless to them.”

Zu An was speechless. No wonder he looks like those 996 programmers. 

“You should have noticed the circle of hair around his head. He hasn’t reached the highest level. You have to tread carefully if you encounter a Deity of Knowledge worshiper with no hair. You won’t be a match for them,” Firework warned.

Zu An nodded as he took her reminder to heart. He remembered his earlier quandary and asked, “May I know how you convinced him to teach you our language? Do worshipers of the Deity of Knowledge seek to sow the seeds of wisdom and knowledge?”

“That’s impossible. Why don’t you get that cripple and old hag to ask him and see if he teaches them? The Deity of Knowledge’s worshipers are the stingiest people you’ll ever know. Whomever knows more gets to advance, so why would they go around spreading knowledge?” Firework puffed up her chest gleefully. “He taught me because I’m adorable and beautiful. No one across the universes doesn’t appreciate beauty. That’s why I’m advising you to embrace the Deity of Love and Beauty.”

Zu An was stunned. How am I supposed to learn that? Fluttering Baldie might have a peculiar appearance, but based on our previous encounter, he’s clearly a man. There’s no way he would be seduced by me. It doesn’t matter how handsome I am.

“Do you know the other two’s background? They don’t look easy to get along with.” Zu An recalled the animosity emanated by the other two, particularly the wheelchair-bound old man. He figured that he needed to be guarded against them.
