Employee 9527 couldn’t go against his professionalism and privately reveal other guests’ personal information. It was fortunate that Zu An had bumped into this red-robed woman. 

“That old hag is revolting. She is the very antithesis of our Love and Beauty Dao. I can’t be bothered to comprehend someone as disgusting as that. She probably specializes in poison, and those pustules are probably the side effects from dabbling with poisons.” Firework looked so disgusted that it looked as if she would puke if she said a single word more.

“As for that cripple, he’s probably Deity of Annihilation worshiper. He gives off an air of desolation as if he’s going to destroy the world. Those people are lunatics.” Firework shook her head. “There are so many beautiful things and people in the world, yet they want to destroy everything. Ridiculous!”

She turned to Zu An and said, “I heard you offended him. You’re dead meat. The Deity of Annihilation’s worshipers are vindictive. It might be inconvenient for him to make a move against you on the Universal Conglomerate’s spaceship, but he might go after you as soon as you move from here. But again, Annihilation worshipers don’t follow common sense. He might just find a chance to assault you on the spaceship. You should tread carefully.”

Zu An’s heart was chilled, but having weathered many storms, he didn’t fluster. He continued calmly, “This isn't my first time crossing paths with an Annihilation worshiper. He can come at me if he wants to.”

Firework clapped. Her eyes twinkled in glee. “Such manliness! I love it!”

Zu An was speechless.

Had it been another man, he would have been seduced by her beauty and incredible acting skills, but he'd dealt with his fair share of demonesses and temptresses. He could tell that she was putting on an act right now. Even if she was a Deity of Love and Beauty worshiper, there was no way she would treat him so well just because he was good-looking.

Firework suddenly added, “It’s too dangerous to make an enemy out of an Annihilation worshiper. It’d be best if you can resolve your grudge in advance. I heard that cripple is just jealous of your Wonderpoint World. Why don’t I talk to him on your behalf and tell him the way to obtain a Wonderpoint World, so he can give up on it?”

Zu An scoffed on the inside. So you’re after my Wonderpoint World. Thus, he feigned ignorance and asked, “Is a Wonderpoint World very special? Don’t the rest of you have one too?”

Awkwardness flickered across Firework’s face. “It is indeed special. Wonderpoint Worlds have many spatial rifts that allow them to swiftly connect to lost worlds. Every lost world is a fortuitous encounter. Aside from that, Wonderpoint Worlds are also absolutely safe. It is impossible to breach a Wonderpoint World without its master’s permission. That cripple wouldn’t have been able to get to you, but now that you have left your turf, it’s a different story.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2714: Cross-Dressing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2714: Cross-Dressing

Zu An’s heart shuddered. This is indeed an underlying threat. “If a Wonderpoint World’s master is killed outside, can the murderer claim possession of the Wonderpoint World?”

Firework pondered about it before replying, “That will depend on where its master kept the World Law Beacon. If he carries it around with him, his enemy can steal and take over the Wonderpoint World. Most people wouldn’t dare target a Wonderpoint World’s master, as most of them are powerful experts, but your situation is peculiar…” 

“Because I’m weak?” Zu An replied calmly as if he wasn’t speaking about himself. 

“You are indeed weaker compared to other known Wonderpoint Worlds’ masters, but as a Wonderpoint World’s master, you are bound to harness tremendous luck and special abilities. That cripple won’t be able to get you that easily,” Firework consoled him. 

“Aren’t you worried about offending him by divulging this information to me?” Zu An asked out of curiosity.

“You look better than him. As a Love and Beauty worshiper, it goes without saying that I ought to help you.” Firework smiled with twinkling eyes. 

“Do you have more information on him?” Zu An figured Firework was after his Wonderpoint World as well. 

“I heard he’s a renowned villain in one of the galaxies. He has committed many evil deeds, including theft, arson, and genocide. I abhor people who destroy beautiful things in the world.” Firework harrumphed. “He has three sworn siblings who are Annihilation worshipers too. You should be glad they failed to obtain Dreamland’s invitation letters, or else you wouldn’t have stood a chance against all four of them.”

“Do you know what abilities he has?” Zu An asked.

Firework shook her head. “I only know that he specializes in flames. Most Annihilation worshipers are such experts, as flame is the most optimal tool for destruction. I’m not sure about his other abilities. Most experts are extremely secretive about their abilities lest others curb them. Only those in the same galaxy might know a thing or two.”

Zu An nodded. Lucky it’s not my first time encountering an Annihilation worshiper, so I won’t be clueless if we come to blows. 

“Why is Miss Firework going to Dreamland?”

“Because it’s fun. It only opens once in a thousand years. It’s interesting just thinking about it.”

Zu An hesitantly asked, “I heard most people who entered Dreamland in its last few openings never left it.”

“Who would make things difficult for an adorable beauty like me?” Firework put up a ‘V’ sign beside her face. She did look adorable. “Besides, won’t you protect me if something happens?”

Zu An smiled. “I’ll do my best to protect you since we’re friends, but I fear I might not be strong enough to help you when the moment comes.”

“Don’t be disheartened. I believe you’ll do fine. I have met many people, but few are able to remain composed in the face of danger like you. I believe you'll be able to deal with whatever comes your way.” Firework patted his shoulder.

“But I’m facing a quandary now…” Zu An summarized his communication issue. 

“And here I was wondering what could have troubled you. That’s easy. I can just ask Fluttering Baldie to teach you,” Firework replied with a chuckle. She grabbed Zu An’s hand and led him outside. “He's much more useful than the Universal Conglomerate’s offline translators. Their translators don’t even cover remote languages.”

Zu An eyed the other party’s hand. It was very smooth, but it felt inappropriate for her to be holding his hand when they had only just met. 

Firework brought him to Fluttering Baldie’s room and knocked on the door.

“Who is it?” Fluttering Baldie’s wary voice echoed from within.

“Open up, Baldie. It’s me,” Firework said.

Zu An was baffled. In our world, it would have been extremely rude to call someone a baldie, even if he is truly a baldie. But then again, Knowledge’s worshipers gradually lose their hair as they gain more knowledge. It could instead be flattering to call them that. 

The door soon opened, and the balding middle-aged man excitedly rushed out. “Miss Firework, what brings you here so late at night…”

He swiftly noticed Zu An and their linked hands. His face twitched. “What’s he doing here?”

“We’re here to ask a favor. Can you teach him the languages of other worlds?” Firework asked with a smile. She emanated a youthful aura that made it hard to reject her.

Fluttering Baldie’s face flickered. A while later, he murmured, “I refuse.”

Firework was taken aback. “Why?”

Fluttering Baldie turned around and walked away from the door. “I’ll only teach you.”

The door slammed closed behind him.

Firework and Zu An were both speechless.

As Firework followed Zu An back to his room, she murmured in bewilderment, “This is weird. Fluttering Baldie was quite friendly when I talked to him earlier. Why is he suddenly so hostile?”

Zu An lifted their joined hands. “Could this be the reason?”

Firework was perplexed. “What’s wrong with me holding your hand? You’re beautiful; you conform to the standards of our Deity of Love and Beauty. I wouldn’t have held anyone else’s hand.”

“Could Fluttering Baldie be jealous?” Zu An couldn’t tell if Firework was just innocent, or if all Love and Beauty worshipers were so thick-headed. 

“Jealous?” Firework was confused. “We aren’t lovers. Why is he getting jealous?”

Zu An retracted his hand, not knowing how to explain it to her. 

Firework didn’t dwell on the matter. She settled down on a chair and rested her head on her hands. “This is troublesome. Let me see if there are other solutions.”

Zu An eyed her clothes. “Actually, I have an idea.”

“Oh?” Firework was surprised to hear that. Even I’m at a loss, but this fellow has an idea? Are masters of Wonderpoint Worlds that resourceful?

Zu An hesitantly asked, “Miss, can you lend me a set of your clothes?”

Firework was stunned. Her fair complexion swiftly reddened, and she exclaimed, “What are you trying to do?”

You have successfully trolled Firework for +110… +110… +110…

Zu An knew his request sounded perverted, so he hurriedly explained, “Please don’t misunderstand. Fluttering Baldie mentioned earlier that he’s only willing to teach you, so I was thinking of wearing your clothes to impersonate you.”

Firework rolled her eyes. “Are you taking me or Fluttering Baldie for a fool? Do you think you can impersonate me just by wearing my clothes?”

“I have an ability that allows me to disguise myself as women, but I need their clothes,” Zu An replied awkwardly.

Firework’s eyes lit up. “You have such an ability?”

She would have never been willing to give her clothes to a man whom she had just become acquainted with, but Zu An’s ability piqued her interest so much that she reached into her spatial storage and passed him a set of her clothes. She was curious to see how he would accomplish that feat. 

“Don’t blame me for turning against you if you dare fool me!” As she passed her dress over, Firework’s face reddened. 

Zu An nodded as he took out Pin Ru’s Wardrobe. To Firework’s bewilderment, he placed her dress into the wardrobe. He considered asking her to excuse herself for a while, but after some thought, he decided to just step into the wardrobe to change his clothes. “This wardrobe is made out of some special material…” 

Firework was assessing the wardrobe when the doors suddenly opened. When she saw someone identical to her walking out, her eyes nearly popped out. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2715: Warning
chapters.body:
Chapter 2715: Warning

“Who are you?” Firework was taken aback.

“I am Zu An,” Zu An replied with a smile.

Firework felt chills running down her spine. “Your voice…”

Zu An's voice remained the same despite having disguised himself as Firework using Pin Ru’s Wardrobe. But he took out the Kawaii Waifu Voice Changer and called a staff member over.

“Miss Firework, is there anything you need?” the attendant respectfully bowed. 

Firework was baffled. She wasn’t the one who'd called the staff member over. After the staff member was dismissed, she examined the item in Zu An’s hand and asked, “Is this a voice changer?”

“You can say so,” Zu An replied sweetly.

Firework was baffled. It was the same voice as hers! She snatched the voice changer over and studied it. “But the voice isn’t coming from the machine.”

“It’s coming from my throat,” Zu An replied with a smile. 

Firework walked over and placed a finger on his throat. “Huh? I don’t see a voice changer here.”

They were so close that Zu An could smell her fragrance. He instinctively took a step back before replying, “This is my voice now.”

Firework’s eyes flickered as she tried to make sense of the situation. “Your ability requires you to first fool a person with that voice changer before it can take effect, right?”

“You’re sharp-witted,” Zu An complimented her. He didn’t think there was a need to conceal this piece of information. 

“That’s an interesting ability.” Firework scrutinized Zu An from head to toe, but she couldn’t find anything that could give him away. “I've seen similar abilities, but it’s usually not too difficult to see through them upon close inspection. But you look as if you were carved from the same mold as me!” 

Curious, she first squeezed his chest before attempting to lift his skirt to inspect beneath.

Horrified, Zu An hurriedly backed off while holding his skirt down. “What are you doing?”

“I’m checking if you've turned into a woman.”

Zu An was speechless. This woman knows no shame. 

Not wanting to dwell on this topic, he asked, “Do you think Fluttering Baldie will teach me if I ask him in my current state?”

“Of course. You’re sporting the look of an unrivaled cosmic beauty right there! How can he possibly reject you?” Firework gleefully harrumphed.

“Let me give it a try.” Zu An wanted to dive into it, since the journey wouldn’t take too long. 

“Hold on,” Firework suddenly stopped him with a frown. “You aren’t planning to do anything weird with that baldie, are you?”

Zu An rolled his eyes in annoyance. “I'm not that perverted.”

“Tsk! I can tell from your demeanor that this is far from your first time cosplaying as a woman. If you aren’t a pervert, no one else is.” Firework clicked her tongue.

Zu An was stunned.

“This won’t do. Just thinking about it sends a shudder down my spine. I can’t let you sully my reputation.” Firework crossed her arms as if overwhelmed by goosebumps. She passed him a pendant. “Wear this.”

“What is it?” Zu An asked as he received the pendant. 

“Think of it as a camera. Ah, it's similar to your world’s projection mirrors. It allows me to see what’s happening on your side, lest you use my appearance to do something perverted with that baldie.” Firework’s vibrant, subtle expressions fully showcased her youthful cuteness.

Zu An wanted to turn down her request, but he thought it would be ungrateful of him to do so. Besides, there was no way he would do anything with that baldie, so he had no qualms letting her watch their interactions. So, he wore the pendant around his neck.

The sharp-eyed Firework remarked, “Oh? You’re already wearing another pendant. Let me take it for you lest that baldie notice something.”

Without waiting for Zu An’s answer, her hand swiped for his pendant.

The vigilant Zu An dodged her hand by turning sideward. “Don’t worry about it. I’ll just put it in my clothes.”

He tucked the pendant under the dress. How can I entrust the pendant to someone else when the Jing Teng sisters are in there?

Firework was surprised. “You’re stronger than I thought.”

Even though she hadn’t gone all out earlier, her movements were so fluid that most people would have fallen for it. Yet Zu An had avoided her swipe with astounding ease. Such skills could only be honed by weathering many life-and-death encounters. 

That made her smile deepen. Looks like I've put my bet on the right person this time. That cripple is going to suffer a huge setback. 

“It’s only because you went easy on me.” Zu An smiled. “I’ll be heading over now.”

Firework waved her hand. “Go ahead. I’ll wait for you here.”

Zu An felt uncomfortable leaving a stranger alone in his room, but on second thought, even though this room was styled identically to Mount Yuquan’s residence, it didn’t contain any of his possessions. There were no secrets to be leaked in this room, so he decided to leave her be.

He soon arrived outside Fluttering Baldie’s room. He knocked on the door and was met with an impatient voice asking, “Who is it?”

“It’s me~” Zu An was amazed by how sweet Firework's voice was. 

The door swiftly opened. The sight of Firework brought a smile to Fluttering Baldie’s face, but he quickly remembered something and scanned his surroundings.

“I didn’t bring that person with me.” Zu An knew what he was searching for. 

“That’s good.” Fluttering Baldie’s smile deepened. “Miss, what brings you here?”

“You said you weren’t willing to teach that man, so I came back alone to learn by myself.” Firework’s voice was so sweet and cutesy that Zu An was getting goosebumps from hearing himself speak. Hmph! I’ll tolerate it for the sake of learning other languages!

Fluttering Baldie was perplexed. “I thought you weren’t interested, little sister Firework?”

“Can’t I change my mind? Are you going to teach me or not?” Zu An crossed his arms coyly.

Meanwhile, Firework watched everything through her screen. She was so amused that she rolled around on Zu An's bed. I must record this piece of dark history and use it against that fellow in the future. What fun!

Fluttering Baldie was mesmerized by the two massive volumes propped up by Zu An’s crossed arms. “I’ll teach you. I’ll teach you.” He led Zu An into his room and closed the door.

Zu An’s heart shuddered. I won’t let myself be humiliated if this fellow tries anything, even if I don’t learn any languages at all. 

Firework lay on Zu An’s bed as she kicked her legs in midair. “Should I help Zu An if Fluttering Baldie tries to take advantage of him?”

She thought it would be interesting to see two men getting steamy, but at the same time, she would feel as if she was also being taken advantage of, since Zu An was disguised as her.

Zu An surveyed Fluttering Baldie’s room. It could hardly be called a room; it felt as if he was standing in the middle of space with brilliant stars around him. He could even clearly see several galaxies in the distance.

Fluttering Baldie noticed her expression and smirked in glee. “Knowledge worshipers have already explored most worlds. Only the boundless universes are worth further exploration.”

Zu An took this chance to shower him with compliments before requesting him to teach him other languages once more.

But Fluttering Baldie replied, “Miss Firework, I hope you won’t think I was being stingy earlier. Further association with that man could put me at risk of danger. I heard Fire Knight contacting his companions using a remote language. They will soon make a move. That lad isn’t even a match for Fire Knight alone; he won’t stand any chance when Fire Knight’s companions arrive. If you continue hanging out with him, you'll also be implicated.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2716: Crash?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2716: Crash?

That fellow is called some knight despite being wheelchair bound? That has to be a joke, right? Zu An retorted in his mind. Or is his wheelchair his mount?

“When do they plan to make a move?” Zu An asked.

“Tomorrow.” Fluttering Baldie puffed out his chest. He was proud that he could answer the beauty’s question. 

Zu An was confused. “But I… Cough cough, that newcomer is still on the Universal Conglomerate’s spaceship. Does Flame Knight intend to make an enemy out of the Universal Conglomerate?”

“Who says they're going to attack the spaceship? Flame Knight is going to sneak his comrades in and quietly get rid of that fellow. The Universal Conglomerate won’t be able to do a thing without evidence,” Fluttering Baldie said. “Not to mention Dreamland implicitly allows others to steal their invitation letters. That Zu An or whatever only has his weakness to blame.”

Zu An frowned. He felt that something was amiss. There’s no need for Flame Knight to call his companions over just to deal with me. Could he be a surprisingly cowardly person despite his arrogant attitude?

Fluttering Baldie reined in his thoughts and said, “Let’s forget about that fellow and talk about languages. Miss, what languages do you know?”

The only language I know other than the Cultivation World’s language is the monster language… 

He risked exposing his identity if he said that, but he, as Firework, couldn’t say that he didn’t know any languages at all. A thought suddenly popped into his mind and he said, “I know Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean…”

Fluttering Baldie was merely asking that question in passing, but Zu An’s answer shocked him. “Miss Firework, you’re well-learned! I've never heard about those languages before. Can you try speaking a sentence or two for me?”

Firework perked herself up. This fellow is more mysterious than I thought.

“(Chinese) Ni hao…”

“(English): Hello world…”

“(Japanese) Yamete kudasai…”

“(Korean) Shibal[1]…”

Fluttering Baldie’s eyes lit up. “Those are indeed new languages! May I know if those words all mean the same thing?”

“You could say so.” Zu An only knew those words from watching clips. He was worried his ignorance would show if Fluttering Baldie continued dwelling on the matter, so he grumbled, “Aren’t you supposed to be teaching me? Why am I teaching you instead?” 

He was relieved that Fluttering Baldie, despite being known to be knowledgeable in all languages across the Myriad Worlds, happened not to know those few. It must have been either because Earth wasn't in the known Myriad Worlds, or that it had been countless years since Earth had gone extinct, so no one bothered to pick up their languages.

Both were depressing news to him, though.

When she saw how coyly Zu An was behaving, Firework’s hair stood on end. That crossdresser is faring too well. There’s no way it’s his first time doing this. However, her mind was swiftly drawn back to her face. “Aww, I’m so beautiful!”

Fluttering Baldie was mesmerized. He hurriedly apologized, “Pardon me, I got ahead of myself. Let me teach you first before further consulting you about those languages. So, what languages do you want to learn?”

“The more the better,” Zu An replied without hesitation.

Fluttering Baldie’s eyes lit up. “I’m sure Knowledge’s brilliance will shine on someone as studious as Miss Firework.”

“Are those languages easy to learn? How long will it take?”

“Of course they are! There’s nothing more interesting in the world than learning languages,” Fluttering Baldie replied with a smile. “For starters, we’ll start with the hundred mainstream languages, followed by the ten thousand common languages in powerful universes. Then, we’ll move on to the billions of languages used by the more famous galaxies…”

“Stop, stop! Why are there so many languages?” Zu An was horrified.

“That’s not a lot in the context of the Myriad Worlds. Every universe has many galaxies, and every galaxy has many planets. There are more civilizations in the Myriad Worlds than you can count. A billion languages isn’t that many.”

Zu An frowned. “That sounds fair. How long will it take for me to learn the hundred mainstream languages?”

Fluttering Baldie continued, “The hundred mainstream languages have been localized into countless more dialects. Our most talented Knowledge worshiper took 23 years to learn them. I’m embarrassed to say I’m lacking; it took me 26 years to learn them, putting me in third place in history. Miss Firework, I believe that you won’t fare any worse than me, given your wits.”

Zu An was stunned. “…You’re saying 26 years is already fast?”

“Of course it is! 26 years is nothing but a snap of the fingers to the universe,” Fluttering Baldie replied with a smile. “Besides, Miss Firework, when I’m with you, time can only pass too fast.”

Firework immediately sat up. “How disgusting!” She stared nervously at the video. If you dare let that baldie take advantage of you, I’ll immediately run over and expose you!

To her relief, Zu An stood up and said, “I’m feeling tired. I’ll learn another day.” 

You must be joking with me! 26 years? I don’t have that much time! I guess I’ll have to strike a deal with Employee 9527.

Fluttering Baldie anxiously tried to stop him. “If it’s too much trouble, Miss Firework, there’s a simpler way you can learn the languages within a short time.”

“What is it?” Zu An was taken aback. 

“We can resonate our brain waves. That’ll allow you to access my knowledge and learn the Myriad World’s languages,” Fluttering Baldie said. 

“Forget it.” Zu An frowned. This kind of resonance was similar to mental sex. He still remembered the intense stimulation he'd felt when he joined his soul with Jiang Luofu’s and Yan Xuehen’s. Putting aside the fact that it would reveal his identity and secrets, there was no way he could tolerate having this kind of interaction with another man.

Fluttering Baldie looked at her departing silhouette as he took a deep whiff of her lingering fragrance. His eyes were downcast with melancholy. I was too anxious. I hope Miss Firework doesn’t mistake me for a pervert.

As soon as Zu An returned to his room, Firework harrumphed. “You should be glad you didn’t accept that fellow’s offer, or else you’d never hear the end of me!”

Zu An took off the dress and reverted to his original appearance. “No wonder you rejected his offer. There’s no easy way to learn those languages.”

Firework chuckled under her hand. “You’re stuck in a mental rut. Why do you think those are the only options? Do you think it took me that long to learn your language?”

“That’s right. How did you learn our language so quickly?” Zu An belatedly recalled this detail. 

“Knowledge worshipers can fuse their knowledge into a page. You just have to burn that page with your spiritual energy or ki, and you can temporarily acquire the relevant knowledge. All you have to do to resolve your problem is convince him to give you a page about languages, right?” Firework rolled her eyes. 

Zu An was overjoyed to hear that. “Why didn’t that baldie mention it?”

“Because you prattled on about learning those languages. To a Knowledge worshiper, such a shortcut isn’t considered learning. I didn’t expect the two of you to be so dumb as to cause such a huge misunderstanding,” Firework huffed impatiently.

Zu An laughed sheepishly. “I guess I’ll have to find another time to approach him.”

“You owe me one for using my identity.” Firework hurled her fist playfully. 

“Of course. I’m grateful for your help.” Zu An bowed deeply.

Firework’s eyes curled into twinkling crescents. “That’s more like it. You'd better not turn me down when I ask you for help.”

Zu An was about to answer when their surroundings abruptly shook. Caught off guard, both of them lost their balance and fell together. A beautiful woman was in his arms, but Zu An wasn’t in the mood to enjoy it. “What happened?”

Firework’s expression looked grim as well. “Did the spaceship bump into something? This is bad! Are we going to crash?”

1. The joke here is that the latter two mean something completely different. 'Yamete kudasai' is formal Japanese for 'please stop', but in this context it probably refers to the phrase being used in a lewd context. Meanwhile, 'shibal' in Korean is a curse word roughly equivalent to 'fuck', so he's basically doing the joke of teaching someone the wrong word in a foreign language so they'll embarrass themselves. -Editor Felis ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2717: Assault
chapters.body:
Chapter 2717: Assault

Zu An and Firework dashed out of the room and saw the previously moving scenery outside come to a halt. The spaceship had stopped moving.

When Zu An first boarded the spaceship, Employee 9527 had triggered the self-destruction sequence for the temporary world before the spaceship drove off into a series of spatial leaps. Iridescent lights had colored the scenery outside the window along the way, looking as if countless shooting stars were passing by. There was no speed gauge, but Zu An could sense just how fast the spaceship was moving then. 

But everything had now come to a halt. This was Zu An’s first inter-universe travel, but he could tell something huge had happened.

You have successfully trolled Fluttering Baldie for +444… +444… +444…

Zu An turned around and saw Fluttering Baldie had left his room too and was glaring at him. The latter must have witnessed him leaving the same room as Firework and gotten jealous. 

A warning siren blared, as red lights flickered brightly in the corridors.

“Enemy attack! Enemy attack! All troops are to make their way to the warehouse!” 

Firework was amused by the ongoing events. “Ha. Someone dares to touch the Universal Conglomerate’s spaceship. Interesting.”

Fluttering Baldie walked over and said, “Miss Firework, you should return to your room. It’s dangerous out here.”

Firework was unfazed. “It’s much more interesting out here. Why should I return to my room? You can return to your room if you’re afraid.”

Fluttering Baldie’s transparent face visibly reddened. 

Zu An was speechless. Isn’t he a Knowledge worshiper? Why can’t he see past love? But on second thought, it could be because they had devoted themselves to academics that they were vulnerable to matters of the heart.

“Should we watch the commotion?” Firework asked Zu An with an excited gleam in her eyes. 

“Sure.” This was a good opportunity for Zu An to see how others fought in the Myriad Worlds.

Firework smiled sweetly as she dragged Zu An toward the warehouse.

Fluttering Baldie looked like a pitiful dog abandoned by its owner. I know Love and Beauty worshipers are drawn to beautiful things, but I’m known to be dashing among Knowledge worshipers. Many of my peers are jealous of my fluttering hair.

You have successfully trolled Fluttering Baldie for +55… +55… +55…

Firework led Zu An to an elevated platform near the warehouse. In the distance, they saw a hole in the spaceship’s wall.

Although calling it a hole wasn't quite the most accurate, as it resembled a wormhole more. All sorts of monsters rushed out from the wormhole and charged at anyone in sight. Many staff members of the Universal Conglomerate were already collapsed in a pool of blood, while those who had just arrived were busy fighting against the monsters. 

The monsters came in all shapes and sizes, but none of them surprised Zu An. All of them conformed to the traits of the countless monsters he had seen in Baopu’s tomb and the Monster World. What he was more interested in was the Universal Conglomerate’s fighting style.

Employee 9527 was clashing with the largest monster using a Star Wars-esque lightsaber. 

The monster had the upper body of a beautiful, middle-aged woman. Her hair was tied into a graceful bun, and her makeup looked seductive. Her lower body, however, was that of a giant octopus with countless tentacles that flew toward Employee 9527. 

Employee 9527, with his outstanding swordsmanship, could slice off a tentacle with each slash of his lightsaber, but the octopus lady would just regenerate them, rendering his efforts futile. 

The spaceship’s staff members were also busy dealing with the other monsters. They wielded guns that shot out red beams. Any monsters struck by the red beams were left with a bloodied hole in their body.

Zu An figured the Universal Conglomerate was more inclined toward the technological side.

These laser beams are similar to sword ki but faster. 

He noticed that some of the monsters moved astonishingly fast, evading the laser beams with all kinds of maneuvers before pouncing on the staff members and tearing them into shreds. 

But then again, the laser beams lack the flexibility of sword ki and have a predictable trajectory. 

“Aren’t we going to help them?” Zu An couldn’t bear seeing so many staff members dying. 

“Isn’t it more fun to watch the show? Fighting and breaking out into a sweat isn’t beautiful,” Firework replied with a smile. 

Zu An was stunned.

In the end, he still intervened when a young staff member was on the verge of being bitten to death by the monsters. He first exerted a suction force to drag the staff member away from the monster before unleashing sword ki to burst that monster’s head. 

“T-t-thank… you…” The staff member was so gripped by fear that he stuttered. 

“Take care of yourself.” Zu An dragged the staff member aside before engaging other monsters.

Firework stomped her feet in frustration. “Doesn’t that fellow know that he’s giving away his precious information?”

A black flash of lightning suddenly surged out of the wormhole and shot straight toward Zu An’s back. 

“Be careful!” Firework shouted, but the enemy was so fast that her words failed to reach Zu An before the attack landed. 

Years of dancing with danger had honed Zu An’s instincts. Despite not seeing anything, he performed a sideward instantaneous movement that allowed him to dodge that fatal attack.

Pah!

The black flash of lightning slowed down, revealing himself to be a bird-headed, human-bodied monster. His clothes appeared to be woven out of feathers, making him resemble an owl. He was wielding a silver hook that struck the spaceship’s floor after missing Zu An, ripping open a huge hole.

Zu An had noticed that the spaceship was made out of a special alloy comparable to the Cultivation World’s immortal-grade weapon materials. On top of that, it was inscribed with technological runes and layered with magic formations, granting it amazing defensive prowess. He himself would have struggled to breach it. 

And yet, the birdman tore it apart as easily as ripping paper in half. 

The birdman was enraged at having his attack foiled. He morphed back into a black surge of lightning to continue attacking Zu An. 

Zu An didn’t dare let his guard down. He performed a series of instantaneous movements to dodge the birdman’s attacks. He did manage to avoid the attacks, but there were many close shaves where the slightest misstep would have cost him his life. 

A resounding laugh echoed. A wall collapsed, and a fatty strutted in. “Number Three, you’re struggling with that brat? Let me do it if you can’t!” As he said those words, he snapped the necks of two staff members. 

Meanwhile, the staff members were toppling over from the powerful suction force coming from the hole ripped into the spaceship. Some of them failed to stand their ground and were dragged out into space.

Employee 9527 was enraged. He sliced over a dozen tentacles in a single cut before hurling out a net from his sleeves to trap the octopus lady. Then, he flashed toward the spaceship’s hole, took out a light whip, and rescued his colleagues from the void of space. After that, he tossed a lump of paste at the hole. It swiftly enlarged and mended the hole. 

Zu An, who was eyeing the situation with his peripheral vision, was awed by the Universal Conglomerate’s advanced technology. 

Just then, the octopus lady screeched, “Stop wasting time, Number Four! I’m at my limit!”

It turned out that the net was contracting around her. It cut into her massive body, causing green blood to seep out. 

“Hahaha! It’s all down to me in the end!” The fatty laughed. He sprinted forth, displaying agility that was at odds with his massive physique. He dashed to the octopus lady’s side, pulled out a pair of massive scissors, and cut open the net. 

The octopus lady quickly ripped the net off herself. She inspected the lacerations on her body and roared in anger, “I’ll massacre everyone on this spaceship!” Her tentacles swiftly extended and randomly lashed out in all directions. Some of the tentacles flew toward Zu An, too. 

Zu An was focused on his fight with the birdman when several tentacles suddenly flew over, while his instantaneous movement happened to be on cooldown. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2718: We’Ll Die Together
chapters.body:
Chapter 2718: We’Ll Die Together

Just as the tentacles were about to coil around Zu An, several red firecrackers suddenly descended upon them, blasting them apart into bloody mist.

Startled, Zu An looked up and saw a red-robed woman smiling brightly at him. He felt moved. Regardless of her motive, she has helped me a lot. 

The octopus lady screeched as she hurriedly retracted her tentacles. Those firecrackers were fueled by Firework’s blood essence, so their flame couldn’t be easily put out. They spread toward the octopus lady’s body so quickly that she had to sever her tentacles. To make things worse, she realized she couldn’t regrow them anymore. 

“You damned lass!” The octopus lady glared venomously at Firework. She shot a tentacle toward the ceiling and swung herself toward Firework as if she were Tarzan.

“Be careful!” Zu An wanted to help her, but he was stopped by the birdman’s silver claws.

The birdman burst into laughter. “Go easy on that little beauty, second sister. I’ll let her have a taste of my cannon later!”

The octopus lady scoffed, “I’ll laugh if you’re blown up by her firecrackers instead.”

The birdman laughed. “Doesn’t that make it more exciting?”

Firework’s gaze turned cold, though her smile persisted. “Why don’t I give them to you two since you like them so much?” She produced two lit firecrackers and tossed them over. 

The birdman was unfazed. “Even your firecrackers look so beautiful.”

He swiped his silver claws at the two firecrackers, but to his shock, the firecracker behind suddenly accelerated and bumped into the one in front, causing an abrupt change in the latter’s trajectory and speed. 

He couldn’t react in time, and the firecracker struck his thigh. He screamed in agony. Having seen what happened to the octopus lady, he quickly ripped off his pants and tore off a huge swathe of his flesh. Blood dripped from his leg. 

Firework clapped her hand in amusement. “Is that exciting enough for you?”

The birdman’s face twisted in fury. “You bitch! I’ll make you suffer a fate worse than… AH!”

He screamed once more, as Zu An exploited his momentary lapse in judgment to launch an attack. He tried his best to protect his vitals, but he ended up losing an arm in the process. 

“Incredible, big brother Zu!” Firework exclaimed with a smile. He has a conscience. At least he knows how to stand up for me.

The octopus lady was startled. This woman still has the energy to chat despite facing our combined onslaught. I can’t underestimate her. “Third brother, quickly subdue that brat so we can deal with this bitch together.”

“Alright.” The birdman clenched his jaw as he quickly regrew his arm. 

Zu An was taken aback. These monsters are too tenacious!

In the Cultivation World, cultivators beyond a certain level could regrow their limbs too, but it usually took months of rest. It was inconceivable for anyone to regenerate that quickly there.

“Be careful, big brother Zu. These monsters only die when you destroy their energy cores. You can think of energy cores as the equivalent of your world’s demon cores, and their energy cores are located in different places,” Firework reminded Zu An.

The octopus lady was infuriated. “You talk too much!”

She thrashed her tentacles at Firework, but the latter was somehow able to weave between the gaps of the tentacles as if she were an agile bird. Despite the furious onslaught, none of the attacks were connecting. 

This lass is still holding back. I wonder how strong she truly is. I’m lucky she’s still fooling around. 

She gave up on capturing Firework, and instead focused on stalling the latter until her allies came over to reinforce her. By then, they could work together to capture the woman. 

Number Four isn’t a match for 9527, but he should be able to stall him for the time being. Number Three should be stronger than that brat, but for some reason, not only is he struggling to bring that brat down, he’s even showing signs of losing. 

Fortunately, my subordinates have the upper hand over the Universal Conglomerate’s employees. They don’t seem to have staffed this spaceship with many fighters. 

Furthermore, our eldest brother hasn’t made a move either.

Employee 9527 angrily roared at the other staff members. “How did these fellows slip in here? Why didn’t our protection system work?”

The Universal Conglomerate’s spaceship was equipped with advanced technology that could  detect and destroy enemies from a long distance away, but the defenses had never indicated an enemy presence. They'd only noticed the enemies when the latter were already aboard.

That spelled one possibility—there was a spy among them!

Who could it be? Did the enemy buy off one of our staff members…

Two more people appeared on the platform. It was the old woman with meat lumps on her face and the wheelchair-bound Fire Knight. 

“Tsk tsk tsk. What a commotion!” The old woman’s voice sounded more jarring than a crow’s cawing. 

“A commotion it is indeed.” Flames flickered eerily in Fire Knight’s eyes. 

The old woman turned to Employee 9527 and said, “It seems you aren’t able to take care of this trouble. Should I help out?”

Employee 9527 had also realized that the situation was beyond his control. “The Universal Conglomerate will be grateful to you if you lend a helping hand.”

“I have no interest in the intangible. I’ll help you if you give me what I want,” the old woman coldly demanded. 

Employee 9527 hesitated. That’s too valuable. But hearing his comrades’ screams of agony helped him make up his mind quickly. If the monsters get their way, and I lose the spaceship, it’ll be over for me even if I escape with my life. “Fine, I’ll give you what you want.”

“Smart. Heh! There are enough living entities here for me to create my new medicine.” The old woman opened her arms and murmured an incantation. Soon, a black fog gathered around her.

Psh!

Her body suddenly trembled. She looked down and saw a bloodied spear lodged in her chest. She turned her head around in disbelief. Holding the other end of the spear was the wheelchair-bound Fire Knight. 

“Why?” the old woman asked in confusion.

“I can’t let you foil my plan, can I?” Fire Knight replied. 

The old woman looked at the monsters below and widened her eyes in realization. “I see. They're your subordinates. Very well. Let’s all die together.”

Her body exploded into a thick black fog that swiftly spread into the surroundings. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2719: Impervious To Poisons
chapters.body:
Chapter 2719: Impervious To Poisons

Zu An was startled. The black-robed old woman had only become terribly disfigured because she dabbled with poisons. A poison released by a dying poison specialist was bound to be extremely potent. Few aboard the spaceship would survive it.

He was about to shout out a reminder when flames suddenly enveloped the old woman, vaporizing the black fog while incinerating her body to ashes.

Fire Knight sneered, “I knew you specialized in poison. Did you think I wouldn’t be on guard against this move?” 

Fire was the counter of most poisons, bacterias, and viruses; not to mention his was no ordinary fire. 

“All hail our boss!” The fatty, the octopus lady, and the birdman cheered. 

Employee 9527 finally understood how these monsters had slipped onto the spaceship. They had a partner onboard! He had treated Fire Knight as a VIP, but the latter trampled over his goodwill in such a manner. That made him incredibly angry. “Fire Knight, do you know the consequences of raiding a Universal Conglomerate spaceship? There will be no place for you in the Myriad Worlds!”

Those words were true. The Universal Conglomerate would obliterate Fire Knight even if he was ten times stronger than he currently was.

Fire Knight burst into laughter. “We Annihilation worshipers are always ready to return to our master’s embrace. Our accomplishments will be worthwhile even if it means we’ll eventually die under the Universal Conglomerate’s vengeance. I shall offer all living beings on this spaceship to our master, so he’ll know how sincere our faith is!”

Zu An’s heart sank. Such fellows are the most troublesome of all. They're so stuck in their beliefs that they don’t consider the pros and cons of their actions. 

Firework laughed. “You speak as if you’re a devout believer, but you just covet Zu An’s Wonderpoint World and our invitation letters to Dreamland.”

The others widened their eyes in realization. It belatedly struck them that Fire Knight’s companions didn’t have invitation letters, but if they robbed the spaceship’s passengers, the four of them could head to Dreamland together.

After all, there was no name on Dreamland’s invitation letters. 

Fire Knight glared at Firework with flames blazing in his eyes. “You damned lass! You’re the only one I struggle to see through on this spaceship. What’s your background?”

Firework weaved through the octopus lady’s tentacles like a butterfly. “What background could I have? I am merely the most adorable and beautiful lady in the universe.”

The crowd was speechless.

“Hmph! Conceal your identity all you like; it makes no difference to me!” Fire Knight harrumphed.

Fire blazed forth from his wheelchair’s handrest, converging into spears that shot toward Firework.

Firework was able to cope with the octopus lady’s attacks with ease, but Fire Knight’s interference put her in a dangerous position. The fire spears were precisely timed and aimed to seal off her escape. On top of that, she seemed to be fearful of the fire, causing her movement to slow. It seemed to be only a matter of time before she succumbed to the enemies’ onslaught.

A lightsaber suddenly appeared from the side and deflected some of the fire spears. Employee 9527 knocked back the fatty and swung his saber down on Fire Knight. At the same time, he conjured dozens more lightsabers that simultaneously assaulted Fire Knight. 

Fire Knight continued to relax in his wheelchair as he spread a fan of fire with a casual wave of his hand. With a single strike, he melted over half of Employee 9527’s lightsabers. 

“How’s that possible?” Employee 9527 was startled. He couldn’t believe what was happening. 

Zu An frowned as well. Ranking in the top 10,000 of the Universal Conglomerate, Employee 9527 was no weakling. His assessment of Employee 9527 was that the former was no weaker than Fire Knight. It was baffling how one-sided the fight had turned out. 

Fire Knight unleashed another wave of fire to knock down the remaining lightsabers. Even the one Employee 9527 held in his hand melted. 

Employee 9527 collapsed to the floor, spewing a large mouthful of blood. He glared at Fire Knight with indignant eyes. “You despicable wretch!”

Fire Knight scoffed, “Don’t you think we’d have made thorough preparations before making a move against the Universal Conglomerate? Though I didn’t expect your antidote serum to be so strong as to delay my second sister’s poison for so long.”

The octopus lady snickered. “Do you think I’m so weak as to let you slice so many of my tentacles for free?”

It finally dawned upon Employee 9527 that the octopus lady had intentionally allowed him to hurt her because her poison needed to be catalyzed by her blood. Before he knew it, he had already fallen to her poison. 

His complexion turned awful. She thrashed around the battlefield earlier, waving her tentacles everywhere. If I was inflicted with her poison, doesn’t it mean that…

Before he could finish that thought, he heard weapons falling to the ground. The Universal Conglomerate’s staff members collapsed one after another, moaning in pain. The poison had sapped their stamina, rendering them incapable of fighting.

Firework was also stunned. “Crap.” She fell from the sky before she could react. 

“You wretch! Not that smug anymore now that the tables have turned, huh? I’ll make you suffer a fate worse than death!” The octopus lady stared at Firework’s beautiful face with jealous eyes as her tentacles shot for the latter. She was going to show Firework the true prowess of her tentacles. 

There was suddenly a flash of black light. The birdman flitted over and swiped Firework away. “Big sister, we agreed that this beauty is mine. I can’t let you ruin her before I have a taste.”

But his expression quickly darkened as he realized there was no one in his arms. Firework had been pulled away by a weird suction force coming from Zu An!

Zu An used the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra to pull Firework into his arms, saving her from those two monsters’ demonic claws.  

Firework’s face briefly turned red, but she quickly snapped out of it and asked, “Why aren’t you affected by the poison?”

Even experts of her and Employee 9527’s caliber had fallen for the poison, let alone others on the spaceship. It was baffling how this fellow was unfazed. 

Zu An was perplexed as well. His unique constitution and the Primordial Origin Sutra made him impervious to poison, but he hadn’t expected this trait to work against the poisons from the experts of the Myriad Worlds, too. The Primordial Origin Sutra is stronger than I thought. 

The others on the spaceship were also astounded.

“Boss, that fellow is unfazed by my poison!” the octopus lady exclaimed.

“He has an instantaneous movement ability that allows him to dodge Number Three’s attacks,” the fatty said while waving his scissors.

“He has a skill that allows him to absorb energy and generate a powerful suction force. You must tread carefully around him,” the birdman added.

The trio were slowly forming an encirclement around Zu An. Fire Knight stared at Zu An before he remarked, “I’ve underestimated you, brat.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2720: The Kill
chapters.body:
Chapter 2720: The Kill

Thanks to Employee 9527’s translator, Zu An could now understand Fire Knight’s words. 

Firework whispered, “Take me away with your instantaneous movement. You aren’t a match for them alone.”

They needed to get to somewhere safe where she could neutralize the poison before they would stand a fighting chance.

Zu An didn’t respond to her. Instead, he eyed the monsters attacking the poisoned staff members. All of the Conglomerate’s staff members had lost their fighting ability, rendering them helpless to the monsters’ assault. It didn’t take long for a couple of them to be torn to shreds. 

Zu An’s expression darkened. These staff members had treated him kindly on the journey. He couldn’t just turn a blind eye to their plight. 

Golden sword ki suddenly appeared in mid air and impaled the closest monster. The remaining monsters screeched as they fled from the Conglomerate’s staff members to dodge the barrage of sword ki. 

Firework was frustrated. “Are you stupid? You can’t save them even if you stay behind! Ugh, it’s too late to escape now!”

There was a good chance they could have escaped from the encirclement if Zu An had employed his instantaneous movement earlier. But now that the four had completed their encirclement, there was no way they could escape anymore! 

Zu An calmly replied, “I can’t turn a blind eye to their plight.”

It wasn't out of sheer recklessness that he chose to stay behind. He knew that even if he broke free of the encirclement, he would still be trapped on this spaceship. If they got stranded in bleak space, not only would he be unable to get to Dreamland, but even survival would be an issue. 

It would thus be wiser to stay behind and fight back. If he rescued the Conglomerate’s staff members, he could resume his journey to Dreamland. 

The Conglomerate’s staff members looked at Zu An with grateful eyes. Had they been in Zu An’s place, they didn’t think they would be able to make the same decision.

Employee 9527 sent a telepathic message over. “I appreciate your goodwill, but you aren’t a match for them. Find an opening to escape to the spaceship’s tail. You’ll find a few escape pods there. Should you escape safely, please report this matter to the Universal Conglomerate. We’ll be thankful to you.” He was strong enough to withstand the poison to relay the message.

Zu An was surprised. He hadn’t expected his kindness to secure him an escape route, though the odds of him being able to take said route were now extremely slim… 

Fire Knight harrumphed coldly. “I didn’t expect you to have such useless compassion. You might have obtained a Wonderpoint World by chance, but it’ll just become a stepping stone for me.”

“What does a hideous thing like you know? Our big brother Zu is a great man, unlike despicable scums like you!” Firework argued back despite her earlier grumbling about Zu An’s decision.

Fire Knight’s flames quivered. “You little bitch! I shall show you what true agony is!”

Birdman anxiously interjected, “Boss, you agreed to give her to me.”

The octopus lady harrumphed. “Get lost! I want her as well. I shall stuff her full of my tentacles!” Her tentacles flailed nonstop out of excitement, as if she was imagining something in her mind. 

Zu An felt the lady in his arms trembling. I didn’t expect her to get frightened by that.

Birdman roared in anger, “Second sister, the people you've toyed with become big enough to stuff a train in! How am I supposed to play with them after that?”

The octopus lady rolled her eyes. “It’s not my problem that you’re small.” 

The birdman was speechless.

Fire Knight angrily smacked the wheelchair and said, “Shut up! Why are you fighting among yourselves when we haven’t dealt with the enemy yet? Capture them first. Second sister, you can have the guy. Third brother, you can have the woman.”

The octopus lady’s eyes lit up. She turned to Zu An and licked her lips with her crimson tongue. “This lad doesn't look too bad either. Deal!”

Zu An was stunned. The birdman was a pervert, but the octopus lady was even worse, preying on both men and women alike. Who knew how many people had been stuffed to death by her? And she’s eyeing me now.

Zu An’s expression turned severe. He was in a grim situation; the four of them were extremely strong. He had no intention of letting anyone pop his ass cherry.

The octopus lady was the first to make a move. She weaved dozens of her tentacles into a net and cast it over Zu An.

“Quick, dodge to the back left, then head toward the back right…” Despite being poisoned, Firework’s combat sense remained. She quickly gave out orders in hopes of creating an escape chance. 

But Zu An remained still as the tentacles loomed over him.

Firework was flustered. Is this fellow scared stiff? Why isn’t he dodging? The thought of the miserable plight that would befall her made her face turn cold. If it comes to that, I’ll bring them down with me.

Since she used firecrackers as her weapon, it went without saying that she had some powerful explosives on her for defensive purposes. If those monsters dared to touch her body, she would immediately ignite them. 

The only regret she had was that she would be blown apart. Having her body scattered around didn’t align with her sense of aesthetics.

The octopus lady revealed a perverted grin. They're now both in my grasp! To avoid any mishaps, she sprayed a pink venom at the duo. 

Fire Knight was baffled. That fellow might not be strong, but there’s no reason for him to surrender without putting up a fight. Why isn’t he fighting back?

His vision suddenly blurred, then pink venom suddenly sprayed all over him. Countless tentacles tightly wrapped around his body as their suction pads relentlessly suckered onto every inch of his skin. Despite his mental fortitude, his mind was shaken up. 

Fire Knight was stunned. I've seen how my second sister toys with her prey, but I never thought a day would come when I’d be a victim of that. As he unleashed flames from his body, his anger flared.

The octopus lady screeched in agony as she hurriedly retracted her tentacles, but most of them still ended up charred. Sensing Fire Knight’s rage, she anxiously explained, “Boss, I don’t know what happened. My attacks were aimed at that fellow…”

“Be careful, second sister!” the fatty shouted from some distance away. 

He'd seen the brat switching places with their boss at the very last moment, but that wasn't all. The brat proceeded to charge at the octopus lady, but his second sister was too caught up in having attacked their boss to notice that. So, he had to quickly shout a warning.

By the time the octopus lady registered the warning, Zu An was already at her flank. It was too late for her to dodge his subsequent attack. Even so, she wasn’t worried. She would only lose a few tentacles at worst. With her strong regenerative prowess, she would recover right away. 

“You rascal! You made me offend our boss! I’m going to stuff you…”

Her body suddenly jolted. All her tentacles fell limp, and she plummeted to the ground. 

She was dead.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2721: Target The Weakness
chapters.body:
Chapter 2721: Target The Weakness

Everyone present was baffled by that sight. Just a second ago, the octopus lady had still been extremely smug, only to drop dead right after. 

Even Employee 9527 was baffled. He had fought the octopus lady earlier, and even though she wasn’t a match for him, her powerful regeneration made her a troublesome foe to deal with. In the end, he'd even fallen to her poison. 

Zu An was clearly weaker than the octopus lady, so how could he instant-kill her?

Did Miss Firework secretly make a move? Did she pretend to fall for the poison?

“Second sister!” the scissors-wielding fatty exclaimed in shock. He rubbed his eyes in disbelief.

The birdman was bewildered. He wasn't on good terms with the octopus lady as the latter often snatched his women, and he had been coveting her position as number two for a while now, but seeing her drop dead all of a sudden still filled him with melancholy. 

Only Fire Knight had seen what happened. Zu An had whipped out a pitch-black knife to cut one of the octopus lady’s tentacles. Black runes immediately appeared all over her body, and she died right away despite her tremendous regeneration. 

He yelled, “His knife has an instant-kill effect. Avoid it at all costs!”

The other monsters were shocked. They finally understood what had happened.

Zu An sighed. He'd been planning to assassinate the other monsters with the Poisonous Prick, but little had he expected to be seen through right away. 

Earlier, he'd swapped positions with Fire Knight right before the octopus lady’s tentacles connected, causing the attacks to fall on Fire Knight instead. At the same time, he'd exploited the octopus lady’s lapse in attention to charge up to her and wound her with the Poisonous Prick. 

He had witnessed Employee 9527’s earlier fight with the octopus lady, so he'd known about her tremendous regenerative prowess and that it would be difficult to kill her with ordinary means. Fortunately, his Poisonous Prick happened to curb that.

Let’s see how you regenerate when Poisonous Prick kills you in an instant!

In truth, he hadn’t been certain if it would work out. Perhaps these skills had worked on enemies in the Cultivation World and the Monster World, but they could have fallen short when dealing with even stronger enemies in the Myriad Worlds. 

And yet, everything had still turned out fine. 

Ideally, Zu An would have assassinated the strongest Fire Knight with the Poisonous Prick, but the latter had unfathomable strength and was enveloped in flames. He doubted he would be able to hurt the latter. 

Rather than risk revealing his trump card but failing to achieve anything, he'd decided to take the safer route and eliminate an enemy first.

The octopus lady was the second strongest in the group, and her tentacles were simply too disgusting. Even he was intimidated by them, let alone Firework. The last thing he wanted was for those tentacles to touch his body. It also helped that the octopus lady’s flailing tentacles had been spread throughout the warehouse, making it easier for him to stab her. 

Firework stared at the man holding her in astonishment. A smile emerged on her lips, and she remarked, “Big brother, you’re incredible.”

Fire Knight asked with a coarse voice, “How could you have moved me? That’s a skill at the level of a world law! And that sure-kill dagger too! How can someone as weak as you possess so many overpowered skills and treasures? I get it now. I should have expected this much from the master of a Wonderpoint World. No wonder Wonderpoint World masters across the Myriad Worlds are so powerful. Those places are indeed littered with treasures.”

The birdman’s eyes lit up. “Boss, have we struck it rich this time?”

Other Wonderpoint World masters were top-tier powerhouses, whereas this fellow was pathetically weak. While he did have some troublesome skills, those wouldn’t pose a threat once they learned about and guarded against them.

It’s me, not we. If I monopolize his Wonderpoint World, skills, treasures, and weapons, it will only be a matter of time before I reach a level on par with the other powerhouses of the Myriad Worlds! Although Fire Knight would never say those words aloud. 

He twisted his wheelchair handle. The sharp spikes at the back of his chair unleashed intense flames, making him look like a blazing sun. The flames first enveloped the warehouse before morphing into floating flowers. 

He smiled in satisfaction as he said, “I've sealed off the area. There’s no way he can escape now.”

That was indeed the case, as the surrounding spatial ki had turned viscous. It would be impossible to overcome this seal unless one was stronger than Fire Knight or adept at spatial formations. 

“Be careful, big brother. This is a very powerful spatial seal. It’ll greatly diminish the effectiveness of your movement skills and instantaneous movement. You have to take that into account when you fight them,” Firework reminded Zu An.

Zu An nodded. It wasn't his first time encountering such spatial seals, so he wouldn’t make such an elementary mistake. 

There was no question these monsters were stronger than him, but strength wasn't the sole determining factor in the outcome of a fight. For instance, oxen were much stronger than lions and tigers, but they were often still hunted down by the latter.

Combat experience, artifacts, abilities, and skills could easily affect the outcome of a battle. 

Judging from the earlier clash, Zu An could tell that he wasn't as weak as he thought at first, and the experts of the Myriad Worlds weren’t as strong as he imagined, either. He would struggle against them, but he still had a shot at victory.

“Number Three, Number Four, you two strike together. I’ll control the formation from the side. The battle will be over as soon as he reveals an opening!” Fire Knight ordered the others. 

He was a wary person, and he didn’t dare to let his guard down despite being the stronger one here. Despite the storms he had weathered, he'd still been startled by Zu An’s position swap ability and sure-kill dagger. If the latter had more of such artifacts, there was a chance that he would be defeated. It was safer for him to dispatch his underlings first to probe Zu An’s trump cards.

The birdman could tell what their boss was thinking, and the truth was that he was also intimidated by Zu An after witnessing how his second sister had died earlier. However, he didn’t dare to refute his boss in public. So, he racked his brain and sent a telepathic message, “Number Four, our boss’ seal has limited the effectiveness of my movement skills. You’ll take the front later, and I’ll support you from the flank.”

“Okay! Let’s avenge second sister!” The fatty flailed his big scissors as he snipped at Zu An from afar. 

Alarmed, Zu An hurriedly dodged to the side. A huge gorge appeared where he'd been standing before, as if invisible scissors had snipped it open. 

“Eat my scissors!” 

The fatty displayed agility that contrasted with his massive physique, releasing snip after snip at Zu An. The birdman also launched a barrage of claw swipes at Zu An from all directions. 

With a flick of his wrist, Zu An whipped out the Tai’e Sword to deflect the attacks. My movement speed has been reduced by around 30%. That makes it much harder to cope with their attacks.

Fire Knight frowned. Even though Zu An looked flustered, he still dodged all of the attacks with his delicate footwork. How does he wield such formidable combat ability when he’s so weak? Only someone who often treads along the boundary of life and death could hone such combat sense. I can’t drag this on anymore!

So, he shouted, “Aim for the weaker one!”

The birdman and fatty caught his drift and directed their attacks at Firework. 

This immediately put Zu An at a critical disadvantage. He was already barely holding on, and having his weakness targeted broke the delicate balance. Gashes appeared on his body. If not for his sharp combat sense, he would have been crippled by now. 

His blood splattered on Firework’s face. She shouted angrily, “You’re renowned figures in the Myriad Worlds! Have a sense of shame!” 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2722: Verge Of Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2722: Verge Of Death

The birdman laughed. “What nonsense! Fighting is all about winning. Only fools would insist on fighting against their enemy’s strengths instead of exploiting their weaknesses.” 

Firework was rendered speechless. There were many places in the Myriad Worlds that practiced the law of the jungle, where the winner took all. There was nothing wrong with what the birdman had said.

Nevertheless, she was still angry because these bastards were using her to threaten Zu An.

The birdman snorted, “Yes, continue watching me with that look of disdain. I want those eyes on me when I’m playing with you later.”

Firework and the onlookers were stunned.

Even the fatty was vexed. “Stop rambling, or else I’ll snip off your dick!”

The birdman felt a chill between his legs, knowing his thick-headed fourth brother wouldn’t hesitate to do what he said.

Zu An keenly sensed his lapse in attention and exploited the opportunity to charge out of their encirclement. He circled behind the birdman and used Hundredwarble’s mental attack. 

Piercing pain assaulted the birdman’s head. He screeched in agony as he tried to escape in a fluster, knowing a follow-up attack was coming, but he was still a step too late. Zu An was already swinging the Tai’e Sword at his neck. 

“Audacious!” The Fire Knight had been keenly watching the battle from the side. There was no way he would give Zu An the chance to kill another one of his companions. He quickly conjured a fire lance and hurled it out. 

He intentionally avoided the vitals as he was worried Zu An would use the position swap skill, but the fire lance was still strong enough to stop Zu An’s sword. That would no doubtbuy enough time for them to launch a decisive attack to kill Zu An off.

“What you lookin’ at?”

“I’m looking at you, shithead!”

The exchange left everyone in the warehouse baffled. It was beyond their imagination why those two would suddenly exchange jabs.

Fire Knight was dumbfounded, too. He had no idea why he had blurted out a response at this critical moment, causing him to be a split second late in rescuing his third brother.

“Shit!”

But he quickly noticed Number Four charging at Zu An with his giant scissors, and that made him heave a sigh of relief. He might have stopped me for an instant, but he won’t be able to stop Number Four. 

“Be careful of his soulspeak!” Fearing his words would be relayed too slowly, Fire Knight chose to inform his companions via telepathy instead.

The fatty nodded in response. As long as he rescued Number Three, they would be able to overcome this crisis and slowly turn the tides on Zu An. But the next moment, he suddenly lost track of Zu An’s whereabouts. 

Fire Knight’s anxious voice echoed in his head, “Be careful!”

Alarmed, the fatty quickly pulled back his scissors to protect himself. He figured that Zu An would resort to the sure-kill dagger to bring him down. 

His armor was crafted out of 81 layers of his shed skin; its thickness wasn't to be scoffed at. Even if Zu An plunged his dagger into the armor, it wouldn’t reach his flesh. Instead, that would be an opportunity for him to snip off Zu An’s head, or at least cripple him. 

But what happened next threw him off guard. Zu An didn’t switch to his sure-kill dagger, but instead continued attacking with his sword. 

The fatty's abrupt switch from offense to defense and his lapse in judgment ultimately created an opening. Right before he could close his scissors, the Tai’e Sword slipped in and stabbed him.

Pu!

Blood spurted out, but it immediately froze into a flower. It was the Snowflake Sword, paired with the Snow Phoenix’s ability! Zu An’s recent exchanges with Yan Xuehen had helped him fully comprehend the essence of frost, allowing him to completely encase the fatty in ice in an instant. 

In the next second, intense flames gushed. The Fire Knight’s intervention was on time, unlike before. He carefully controlled his fire to thaw his companion while avoiding further damage.

The fatty gasped for air, his body drenched in sweat. He knew how much of a close shave that had been. Despite his boss’ timely intervention, Zu An’s sword ki had still seeped into his body and suffused it with bone-piercing frost. Perhaps he'd survived that attack thanks to his thick layers of skin armor, but he'd lost his combat ability. 

Meanwhile, Zu An quickly flitted out of the warehouse with Firework in hand. He'd chosen to wound the fatty instead of killing him to bait Fire Knight into intervening, thus gaining a chance to break free of the encirclement. 

Fire Knight blazed with anger—literally. They'd had the decisive advantage, but somehow, Zu An had managed to kill one, wound one, and slip away. 

“Big brother, that lad broke free of your spatial seal. He must be adept in spatial formations!” the birdman exclaimed.

“Shut up! I can see that.” Fire Knight’s eyes flickered in rage, but moments later, he chuckled. “Very well. This only makes the hunt more meaningful. The more secrets he holds, the greater the rewards we’ll reap from him. We’re in the middle of nowhere, and he isn’t strong enough to traverse space yet. He might be able to escape from the warehouses, but where can he go?”

“Our boss is wise!” The birdman fawned on Fire Knight. 

Fire Knight ordered him, “Take care of Number Four and tie the others up. I’ll chase him.”

The birdman was taken aback. “Boss, that fellow is extremely sly…”

Fire Knight’s eyes turned cold. “Do you think I’d lose to him?”

“Of course not! Boss, I’m sure you’ll catch him without a hitch,” the birdman hurriedly changed his tone.

Fire Knight harrumphed. His wheelchair burst forth like a valiant steed to chase Zu An. 

The earlier clashes had revealed that Zu An’s foundation was beneath his; it was just that the latter possessed bizarre skills that made him a tricky opponent. Not only had his comrades failed to be of help, but under the influence of those bizarre skills, they were even a hindrance.

That was why he chose to pursue Zu An alone. 

Zu An flitted through the spaceship with Firework in his arm while searching for the escape pods Employee 9527 mentioned. 

Firework stared at him with glowing eyes. “Big brother, you’re incredible. With your cunning schemes, you played those monsters like fools.”

It would take sharp combat sense and great intelligence to come up with such an intricate ploy in the middle of a battle. 

“I was lucky they fell for my tricks twice. From this point on, things will only get harder,” Zu An replied. 

Just then, he heard a whimper. He rammed down a door and saw Fluttering Baldie lying in the corner of the room, breathing shallowly. There was a gaping hole in the latter's chest, and blue blood covered his translucent body. 

“What happened to you, Brother Baldie?” Zu An was taken aback. No wonder I didn’t see him despite the huge commotion earlier. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2723: Possession
chapters.body:
Chapter 2723: Possession

“It’s you?” Fluttering Baldie noticed Firework in Zu An’s arm and stiffened up. 

“What happened to you, baldie?” Firework eyed the gaping hole in Fluttering Baldie’s chest. 

“Fire Knight assaulted me.” Fluttering Baldie sighed. “I was dumb. I gloated when I heard Fire Knight was assembling his comrades to deal with this fellow over here. Little did I know he said those words aloud to make me lower my guard. His goal was the spaceship and all of us from the start. I realized that too late.”

Zu An sighed. Had the guests on the spaceship cooperated with the Universal Conglomerate, Fire Knight and his comrades wouldn’t have stood a chance. It was a pity they'd been divided, so Fire Knight took them down one by one.

“What happened, Miss Firework?” Fluttering Baldie finally noticed something was amiss with Firework. It doesn’t look like they're embracing to flaunt their love. 

“I fell for an octopus demoness’ poison.” Firework’s eyes blazed with animosity. I wouldn’t have ended up in this embarrassing situation if not for that!

“Ah. Their poison is colorless and odorless; it’s hard to guard against it. It robs you of your strength and renders you incapable of circulating your ki. However, there’s nothing to worry, as the poison clears within a day,” Fluttering Baldie said, tapping into his vast knowledge reserve. 

“What’s the point of saying that now? We’ll all be dead by then!” Firework rolled her eyes. He was aware of Fire Knight’s movements but didn’t warn us about the octopus demoness’ poison. He probably doesn’t have good intentions in mind. 

Zu An interjected, “Fire Knight is still chasing us. Brother Baldie, I’ll take you with me.”

He held Firework with one hand and lifted Fluttering Baldie with the other. 

Firework pouted. It’s doubtful whether we’ll be able to outrun Fire Knight by ourselves, so why are we picking up more baggage? That said, given how he had wanted to save the Conglomerate’s staff members earlier, she knew it was unlikely Zu An would give up on the baldie. 

Zu An flitted across the spaceship following Employee 9527’s directions. He even found a hidden passageway, to the others' surprise.

Soon, they arrived at the dock where the escape pods were kept. Zu An first placed Firework into an escape pod before proceeding to move Fluttering Baldie.

But Fluttering Baldie shook his head and said, “I won’t be able to make it. You go ahead…”

Firework retorted, “You should have said so earlier then! Why are you only saying that now?”

“All beings are primed with survival instincts, and I’m no exception,” Fluttering Baldie replied with a bitter smile. “I was clutching onto a sliver of hope earlier, trying my best to recover, but my life force has been severed. Unless I can get a legendary immortal medicine, there’s no hope for me.”

Zu An fell silent. He didn’t have any immortal medicine on him, and he wasn’t so naively compassionate as to use it on a mere acquaintance. 

Fluttering Baldie bowed to Zu An and said, “I’m in awe of your magnanimity in putting aside our misgivings to save me.” 

He retrieved a cluster of blue light from his head. Despite not having a corporeal form, it looked as beautiful as a gemstone. 

“I've devoted myself to studying languages across the Myriad Worlds, and I have accomplished a fair bit.” Fluttering Baldie smiled in glee, proud of his work. “You disguised yourself as Miss Firework to learn the languages of the Myriad Worlds. I have no other way of repaying you, so I’ll give you everything I've learned lest my many years of research go to waste.”

Zu An was astonished. “You know about that?”

Fluttering Baldie sighed. “I am a Deity of Knowledge worshiper. I would be a fool if I couldn’t even see through that afterward.”

That Firework he had met in his room had been overly friendly, and there were some differences in their speech patterns and gestures, too. It was just that lust had gotten the better of him, so he hadn't thought too much into it.

Now that he met the real Firework once more and saw how cold the latter was, he'd connected the dots and realized that the Firework he had met in his room was fake. 

“My apologies…”

Fluttering Baldie interjected, “You don’t need to apologize. It was a good move to deal with Deity of Knowledge worshipers like me. I admire your street smarts. If you truly want to do something for me, please complete my thesis, place my name on it, and present it before the academics of the Myriad Worlds…”

His voice grew increasingly weaker before finally fading away entirely. 

The blue light floated up to Zu An.

“That’s a valuable treasure. It’s the crystallization of the knowledge he's accrued in his lifetime. He devoted his life to studying the Myriad Worlds’ languages; there’s no one who knows languages better than him. With that, you can become proficient in countless languages right away,” Firework said.

“How do I use it?” Zu An asked.

“Place it on your forehead, and it’ll fuse into your mind,” Firework explained. “You have to do it quickly. Once it’s out of the body, it’ll dissipate quickly. It’ll be a huge pity if that happens.” 

It would help Zu An tremendously if he grasped more languages before arriving at Dreamland, so he placed the blue light on his forehead. A cooling sensation seeped into his forehead, and he felt a trove of knowledge flowing into his mind.

“AH!”

Zu An clutched his head in pain. The sheer amount of knowledge appeared to have dealt a heavy blow to his consciousness, putting him in a groggy state. The next second, he found himself standing in the middle of a vast space, with knowledge of different languages surging around like a rapid river.

He felt as if needles were pricking his head. He had no choice but to calm his mind and quietly accept the inflow of knowledge. 

All of a sudden, however, the river parted, and a transparent light figure emerged. 

Sensing the foreign presence, Zu An opened his eyes and called out, “Fluttering Baldie?”

It was indeed Fluttering Baldie, but his body was even fainter, as if he was just a soul entity. 

“It’s been a long time since I felt this young. Your cultivation might be weak, but your body is astoundingly powerful. It harnesses untold potential. More importantly, Miss Firework fancies you,” Fluttering Baldie said with a smile. 

Zu An was alarmed. Fluttering Baldie had turned from a weak, dying victim into a sinister, triumphant villain. He swiftly figured out what was going on and asked, “You’re trying to possess me?”

“You aren’t too dumb. It’s too bad you let your greed get the better of you. You wouldn’t have fallen for such a simple trick if you didn’t covet my knowledge,” Fluttering Baldie replied gleefully. He'd thought he would die, but the tides had turned faster than he thought. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2724: Unexpected Reunion
chapters.body:
Chapter 2724: Unexpected Reunion

“I made you lower your guard by exploiting my dying state.” Fluttering Baldie was proud of his deception. “You can be glad you haven’t lived in vain. It’s your honor to have a soul as intelligent as mine occupying your body.”

Zu An sighed. “You should be the one feeling flattered. You’re going to use my body to approach Miss Firework, aren’t you?”

Fluttering Baldie’s face reddened at having his thoughts exposed. “Miss Firework is fond of you. It’ll be easier for me to get close to her with your body.”

Zu An scoffed, “Do you think Miss Firework truly likes me?”

“Is that not the case?”

“You clearly don’t know women.”

Those words enraged Fluttering Baldie. “Hmph, the barks of a loser! Once I take over your body,  I’ll be able to hold her openly.” He morphed into a shadow and lurched toward Zu An.

Clang!

The deep reverberation of a bell echoed. A purple-gold alms bowl appeared above Zu An, emitting a golden dharmic note that cleansed the dark shadow.

Fluttering Baldie snorted, “Using such a mediocre trick against me?”

The shadow rapidly expanded until it covered the alms bowl. The golden light dimmed as if corrupted, and the reverberating dharmic note gradually faded into silence.

Crk crk crk…

Cracks appeared on the alms bowl until it shattered into dust. Zu An’s treasure was nowhere enough to withstand Fluttering Baldie’s powerful soul energy. 

The shadow morphed into a fearsome storm cloud that seemed to herald a devastating tornado. Zu An looked small and insignificant before the storm, and it felt as if it would only take a whiff for him to be devoured whole.

Just then, a woman in a red dress manifested beside Zu An.
