“Huh. How did you get involved with such a powerful enemy?” The woman was none other than Mi Li. She'd been awoken by Fluttering Baldie’s powerful soul pulsations. 

Zu An smiled. “You appeared at a good time. That fellow wants to possess me.”

“He wants to possess you?” Mi Li’s gaze turned cold. Her soul was bound to Zu An’s, which meant that she would die if Zu An’s soul was possessed. 

“Oh? It’s a rare twin soul—a man and a woman on top of that! That explains how you were able to disguise as Miss Firework so convincingly. It’s going to be a feast for me!” The wind transformed into a demonic face that greedily lunged at the duo. 

Mi Li stood still before the approaching demonic face. Meanwhile, Zu An sweated profusely, to the point where he couldn’t hold himself back from making a move anymore. 

Just then, Mi Li pursed her lips and made the gentlest suction sound, as if taking a sip of tea. 

The ferocious demonic face warped in fright. “This stature! It can’t be…”

He desperately retreated, but where could he escape to? The shadow swiftly flew into Mi Li’s mouth, and even the demonic face began trembling intensely. 

A pair of hands grew out of the face and attempted to claw forward as if to grab onto something. But no matter what he did, he couldn’t escape the fate of being devoured by Mi Li. 

In the blink of an eye, Mi Li had already consumed everything. She let out a burp. “Not too bad.”

Zu An was stunned.

This was beyond his imagination. He'd thought there would be an intense battle, but Mi Li had easily devoured Fluttering Baldie’s soul as if it were nothing at all. “My beautiful master, shouldn’t you explain things to me?”

“It would have been hard for me to deal with him if he was in his normal state, but he happened to be a soul entity. Whether a possession succeeds or not depends on the rank and not the volume of the soul. He was weaker than me, so his only fate was to be consumed,” Mi Li replied. 

Zu An asked, “What does the ‘stature’ he mentioned earlier mean?”

“It refers to how high one stands. To explain it in your words, it's similar to the concept of dimensions. A higher dimension being is bound to crush a lower dimension being. His stature was lower than mine, so he was unable to hurt me, but I could easily hurt him.” Mi Li yawned. “I’m going to take a nap and digest his soul energy.”

She went back to sleep.

Zu An was speechless. I feel as if she’s hiding something from me, but I can’t force her if she doesn’t want to speak. 

When he opened his eyes once more, he was faced with Firework’s anxious expression. She asked, “Big brother, are you okay?”

“I’m fine,” Zu An replied. 

He immediately noticed the massive trove of knowledge in his mind, containing information about languages across the Myriad Worlds. He now grasped all the languages Fluttering Baldie had devoted his life to studying. 

“It’s all my fault. I didn’t expect that damned baldie to be this cunning!” All of a sudden, Firework coldly eyed Zu An. “Hold on. Are you that damned baldie?”

Zu An laughed. “Don’t worry. He did try to possess my body, but failed.”

“Are you sure?” Firework remained skeptical. 

“Of course I am.”

Firework pondered for a while before finally smiling once more. “Your eyes are still as pure and innocent as before. Alright, I believe you aren’t that damned baldie.”

Zu An was stunned, but he wasn’t in the mood to bicker with Firework. Perhaps it was due to the earlier possession attempt or the flood of information in his mind, but he was feeling incredibly weak. What the hell? Mi Li was nourished by Fluttering Baldie’s soul, but somehow I’m feeling exhausted.

Even so, there was no time for him to dwell on that. There was an abrupt spatial warp, and Fire Knight appeared at the entrance on his wheelchair, saying, “I’ve finally found you two.”

With a wave of his hand, he unleashed fire arrows, destroying all the escape pods.

Zu An used his final sliver of strength to pull Firework out of her escape pod, lest she get taken out in an explosion. 

“It’s all that baldie’s fault!” Firework cursed. If not for him, we would have been gone by now! She whispered to Zu An. “Reach into my clothes. I have two larger firecrackers in there. Find a chance to hurl them at that bastard. We’ll drag him down to hell with us!”

Zu An was speechless.

“You want to drag me down? Don’t even think about it!” Fire Knight harrumphed as he hurled two fire chains at the duo. Zu An and Firework felt their bodies being weighed down, preventing them from making the slightest move.

Firework’s expression collapsed into despair. Zu An frowned contemplatively. 

Fire Knight was about to drag his two captives back when he suddenly turned around and shouted, “Who is it?”

Zu An and Firework looked over. Another wormhole-like entrance appeared on the wall, and a woman dressed in white walked out.

Zu An was stunned. “Xuehen?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2725: More Terrifying Than The Deity Of Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2725: More Terrifying Than The Deity Of Death

It was as if the woman had just walked out of a painting. Her robes were as white as snow, and her hair fluttered gracefully. She commanded an ethereal air. 

Firework’s eyes lit up. “She was practically born to be a Love and Beauty worshiper!”

The woman was so beautiful that even other women were drawn to her. 

Upon hearing Zu An’s voice, the white-robed woman looked over with impassive eyes devoid of the slightest emotion.

Xuehen was as cold as frost, but the time she had spent with Zu An breathed some life into her. In contrast, this woman felt empty like the void. Even a glance at her induced nihilism. It felt as if there were no meaning to life, death, and conflict. 

“Who are you?” Fire Knight asked grimly. He couldn’t read the woman who had just appeared, and that placed great pressure on him. 

The woman stretched her hand out. “Hand over Dreamland’s invitation letter.”

Her words came out awkwardly, as if it had been a long time since she last spoke, but her voice was still very pleasant-sounding. 

Fire Knight sneered. They had come here to steal the other passengers’ invitation letters, yet someone else dared to rob him? This was ridiculous.

“What if I refuse?” Fire Knight snorted. If not for him struggling to get a read on the woman’s strength, he would have already made a move. 

“Then I’ll have to make a move.” The white-robed woman sighed. She took a step forward, and her aura abruptly surged. 

Fire Knight was taken aback. The woman was stronger than he had expected. His eyes flickered as he said, “Hold on. Those two have invitation letters too, and they’re powerless to resist now. You can have their invitation letters.”

With his second sister dead, the old woman’s and Fluttering Baldie’s invitation letters were enough for his other two brothers to use. It didn’t matter whether he had Zu An’s and Firework’s invitation letters or not. 

He had a fearsome reputation as an Annihilation worshiper, but that didn’t mean he sought to resolve everything through violence. He saw no point in confronting a powerful expert over a matter that was easily resolved. 

The white-robed woman glanced at Zu An and Firework. 

Zu An felt a little awkward as he was still carrying Firework. Despite knowing the other party wasn’t Yan Xuehen, he couldn’t help but feel that he was caught in the act of adultery. 

Firework felt nervous as well. Even Fire Knight was too much for them to deal with, and this mysterious woman just had to appear out of nowhere. It was like the saying ‘trouble comes in pairs’.

The white-robed woman eyed them impassively before turning back to Fire Knight. “I don’t want theirs.”

“…So you’re just after me!” Fire Knight roared in anger.

He was reluctant to pick meaningless fights with experts, but that didn’t mean he was afraid of trouble. Dozens of fire chains shot forth from his wheelchair, sealing off all of the white-robed woman’s escape paths. 

“Be careful!” Zu An exclaimed. Despite knowing the woman wasn’t Yan Xuehen, he was still concerned because of their resemblance. 

Firework grumbled coyly, “Big brother, you can’t just fall for her just because she’s gorgeous. Have you forgotten you’re still holding me?”

Zu An was speechless.

The white-haired woman stood still as if she couldn’t see the fire chains flying toward her. Soon, the chains shackled her body. 

Fire Knight smiled triumphantly. I thought she was some powerful expert, but that’s all she amounts to… 

Yet his smile quickly faded. After shackling the woman’s body, his fire chains didn’t stop; they passed right through her! It was as if her body was incorporeal.

Unwilling to accept the result, Fire Knight shot out more chains, but the other party stood still and allowed the chains to pass through her. She wasn’t hurt whatsoever. 

“How is this possible?!” Fire Knight was flabbergasted, as if he had witnessed the inconceivable. 

The white-robed woman took a step forward and the surrounding fire chains all shattered, including the ones shackling Zu An and Firework. She lifted her sheathed sword, and an incomparably sharp wave of sword ki surged toward Fire Knight. 

Fire Knight was horrified. Not daring to hold back, he unleashed all his flames to manifest a humongous fire skeleton head to rip the sword ki apart.

Faced with the fire skeleton head, the sword ki resembled an ant confronting a whale. It felt as if even a blow from the whale could cause the ant to disintegrate. And yet, when both sides clashed, the more intimidating fire skeleton head was sliced in half like butter coming into contact with a red-hot knife. 

The sword ki surged forth with unstoppable momentum, slicing Fire Knight and his wheelchair in half. The corpse swiftly disintegrated into flames. 

The white-haired woman frowned. She sensed that Fire Knight wasn’t dead yet. Thus, she turned around and walked away.

Zu An hurriedly shouted, “Miss, what’s your name?”

His encounters with the Goddess of Wu Mountain and the Mermaid Queen made him wonder if there was any connection between Yan Xuehen and the woman before him. Not only did they look identical, but they had strikingly similar dispositions, too. On top of that, they wielded the same void divine power.

The white-robed woman glanced at him with a frown before walking away to pursue Fire Knight. 

Noticing Zu An’s daze, Firework remarked, “Hey! She might be beautiful, but you don’t have to fawn on her that much. I’m not bad myself, but I don’t see you being that proactive!”

There was no reason for Zu An to tell her about Yan Xuehen. Now that he was freed from the fire chains, he took a brief moment to recuperate before chasing after the white-robed woman with Firework in his arm.

Firework was taken aback. “Wait wait wait! This is our chance to escape! Are you squandering this opportunity to head back and look for her?”

Zu An hurriedly replied, “She resembles a friend of mine. I have questions to ask her.”

“Tsk!” Firework rolled her eyes. “That’s what all men claim when they see a beautiful woman. Can’t you come up with a better excuse?”

Zu An ignored her. “I shouldn’t implicate you in this. Do you want to stay here?”

“I’m coming with you.” Firework hurriedly explained herself. “I’m still under the effects of the octopus demoness’ poison. I’d be dead meat if even a small fry stumbled in here.”

Zu An thought her answer made sense, so he continued giving chase while carrying her. 

When they arrived at the warehouse, they were shocked by what they saw. The noisy warehouse had turned eerily quiet, and the army of monsters had collapsed in puddles of blood. 

Employee 9527 and a few other Conglomerate’s staff members were still alive, but they were frozen stiff in shock, as if they had just witnessed something unbelievable. 

Fire Knight and the birdman were standing in two corners of the warehouse. Fire Knight, having lost his wheelchair, was now using two spears as crutches for support. The birdman was backed against the warehouse wall while holding his silver claws tightly. 

They were covered in so many wounds that fresh blood flowed profusely from them, but they didn’t have the energy to tend to their injuries. They stared nervously at the white-robed woman standing at the center of the warehouse. 

As beautiful as the woman was, to them, she was even more terrifying than the Deity of Death. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2726: Golden Emblem
chapters.body:
Chapter 2726: Golden Emblem

An intense battle had broken out in the short time Zu An and Firework were making their way here. Fire Knight’s group had suffered heavy losses. 

The white-robed woman sensed the pair’s arrival and glanced backward. Her gaze mainly fell on Zu An, as she sensed something peculiar about him. 

Noticing her lapse in attention, the birdman quickly charged toward the wormhole in the distant wall. He specialized in speed, and he was giving it 120% out of desperation to escape.

Half of his body managed to slip into the wormhole when a sharp wave of sword ki sliced through him. The other half failed to enter the wormhole and squirmed ceaselessly on the spot. 

Those in the warehouse gasped.

The half body tried to regenerate, but the sword ki was imbued with peculiar energy that hindered regeneration. Instead, a tinge of gray began spreading from the wound and swiftly enveloped the entire half.

Then, the half disintegrated into dust, not leaving any traces behind. 

The remaining people in the warehouse were speechless. The white-robed woman’s blade is terrifying! Wait a moment, she didn’t even draw her sword!

Fire Knight remained on the spot. My foolish third brother. Do you think I would have stayed still if it was possible to escape? 

He picked up his crutches and put them together to form a fire lance. Intense flames shrouded his body. They formed new legs that allowed him to stand up and clothed him in fire armor that made him resemble a general on the battlefield. The terrifying pressure he emanated suffocated most of the people in the warehouse. 

“It’s been a while since I felt such pressure. Miss, I have you thank you for sparking such excitement in me.” Fire Knight pointed his fire lance at the white-robed woman. “I am Fire Knight. Draw your sword!”

The white-robed woman calmly replied, “You aren’t worthy.”

Fire Knight and the onlookers were speechless.

Big sister Yan is so domineering! Zu An thought.

Firework looked at the white-robed woman in awe and admiration. 

Fire Knight sneered in anger, “Very well. Let’s see how much longer you can keep your sword sheathed!”

As he charged at the white-robed woman with his lance, he manifested a giant avatar of a fire-armored general. Together, they thrust their lances toward her.

Employee 9527 was horrified. This isn’t good! He knows he’s doomed, so he’s planning to bring all of us down with him! No matter the outcome of the battle, the spaceship won’t be able to take the brunt of his attack. If the spaceship explodes, no more than a handful of us can survive. 

Zu An anxiously placed Firework down by the side and channeled his Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra. He was trying to absorb the shockwaves of their clash, lest the spaceship be destroyed. 

The white-robed woman frowned. She could see through Fire Knight’s intention. She raised her sheath and gently flicked it; her sword popped out by an inch. A cold glint of light shot out. 

Much to Fire Knight’s shock, the massive fire avatar imbued with annihilation power was sliced in half. He had no time to react before he was obliterated by the cold glint of light, too. The light harnessed a peculiar power that was able to extinguish the flames, so the disaster was averted. 

Those from the Universal Conglomerate cheered in delight, but they soon felt worried, as it struck them that they still didn’t know whether the white-robed woman was friend or foe. Even though she had killed Fire Knight, there was no telling whether she would go after the rest of them, too. 

Even Zu An was nervous. He knew he wouldn’t stand much of a chance against the white-robed woman.

Just then, the white-robed woman walked over and carefully assessed him. 

Firework nervously spoke up. “Miss, we aren’t from the same group as Fire Knight.”

The white-robed woman ignored her and asked, “Why do you carry nebula power?”

Zu An was stunned. Nebula power? Is she referring to the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra? Or was I afflicted with Yan Xuehen’s nebula power due to our frequent bonding?

The white-robed woman carefully examined him before shaking her head. “Perhaps I was seeing things.”

She walked over to Fire Knight’s corpse, picked up the invitation letter that had fallen to the ground, and asked, “This spaceship is heading to Dreamland, right? May I have a room, too?”

Employee 9527 hurriedly nodded. “Of course!”

He’d been worried that the white-robed woman would deal with the rest of them as well, so it was a huge relief to hear her making such a simple request. Did she cause such a huge commotion just to steal Fire Knight’s invitation letter? Wow, Fire Knight is a pitiful one. He wanted to steal the other guests’ invitation letters, only to get robbed himself. 

Employee 9527 requested for Zu An to get some antidotes for them from the medical bay. He wouldn’t dare ask the white-robed woman to run an errand for him. 

The Universal Conglomerate had prepared antidotes for common poisons. These antidotes weren’t specifically tailored for the octopus demoness’ poison, but at least it would restore their mobility. 

Soon, Employee 9527 settled the white-robed woman in the most luxurious guest room. He considered asking the latter about her background, but she showed little interest in conversing with him, so he decided to keep his questions to himself. He stationed staff members near her room to attend to her every need. 

Only then did he rush out to meet Zu An. He said amicably, “Pardon me for only attending to you now, Brother Zu.”

Zu An replied with a smile, “Don’t worry about it. It’s important to attend to that lady first, or else she might destroy the spaceship with a slash.”

Employee 9527 smiled sheepishly. “I’m glad you’re so understanding, Brother Zu. I’m truly grateful for your help. We would have all been dead if not for you.”

Zu An clasped his fist and said, “The one who saved you was the white-robed woman.”

Employee 9527 shook his head. “That’s not true. My colleagues and I would have been massacred by those monsters if you hadn’t put your life on the line to protect us before the white-robed woman arrived. Ah, you asked about a mainstream language offline translator earlier. That’s usually worth an exorbitant sum, but I’ll request for one to be given to you for free as compensation.”

Zu An smiled. “There’s no need for it. I’m well-versed in most languages now.”

“Oh? What do you mean by that?” Employee 9527 was confused.

Zu An told Employee 9527 about how Fluttering Baldie had intended to possess him, but it ended with him inheriting Fluttering Baldie’s knowledge about languages instead. 

Employee 9527 was appalled to hear that. “That baldie is despicable! He’s a disgrace to all Deity of Knowledge worshipers!”

Zu An wasn’t bothered by it. “There are good and bad people among each universal deity’s worshipers. It would be unwise to stereotype them based on the black sheep.”

“Brother Zu is magnanimous. No wonder you have a Wonderpoint World despite your young age.” Employee 9527 pondered for a moment before taking out and passing over a golden emblem. “Please take this instead. It represents our Universal Conglomerate’s friendship. We only give it to people whom we owe a huge debt to. With this golden emblem, all Universal Conglomerate branches across the Myriad Worlds will treat you as a VIP.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2727: Forgetting Everything
chapters.body:
Chapter 2727: Forgetting Everything

The golden emblem was made from a special alloy, inscribed with the Universal Conglomerate’s logo. Light flowed on its surface, and it felt cool to the touch. It seemed to have a calming effect as well.

“Our emblems are exclusively forged in our headquarters, and only a small number of employees are authorized to inscribe the relevant details. Outsiders can’t hope to recreate it,” Employee 9527 explained with a smile. 

Unless he did it willingly, Fire Knight and the others could only obtain the emblem mold from him, which wouldn’t be sufficient to be recognized by the Conglomerate.

Zu An was surprised. It sounded as if very few people had the right to forge the emblem, and someone ranked 9527 shouldn’t have had that opportunity. It suggested that Employee 9527 could be of surprisingly high standing in the Conglomerate. 

But since Employee 9527 wasn’t saying a thing, Zu An couldn’t probe either.

It was a close encounter, but Zu An had gotten a lot out of it. He had become proficient in countless languages in the Myriad Worlds, and that was a huge advantage when it came to collecting intelligence. 

The golden emblem was a huge bonus, too. Having witnessed firsthand how affluent the Universal Conglomerate was, he knew it would be tremendously beneficial to forge close ties with them. Not to mention this wasn’t just an informal relationship, but a formal one affirmed by the emblem. 

“Thank you.” Zu An accepted the emblem with a smile. 

Employee 9527 bowed to Zu An and said, “Have a good rest, Brother Zu. I’ll have to clean up the mess on the spaceship, so pardon me for not being able to accompany you.”

Zu An bade Employee 9527 farewell. He pondered for a moment before deciding to visit the white-robed woman’s room.

The maids outside the room greeted him warmly; they were grateful to him for saving them. One asked, “Benefactor, are you here to pay a visit to the miss? Shall we inform her of your arrival?”

Zu An was still hesitating over how to approach this matter when the white-robed woman called out, “Who is it?”

Since she had discovered him, Zu An replied, “I am Zu An. I have something I’d like to ask.”

There was a brief silence before the door opened. A cold voice echoed, “Come in.”

The maids stepped aside to let Zu An in. 

Zu An was surprised when he saw the room’s layout. Employee 9527 had assigned the white-robed woman the best room out of gratitude for her help and fearfulness of her strength, but the arrangement was surprisingly humble.

It was an empty room with a bed. Even from afar, Zu An could feel a chill emanating from the bed, as if it were the Cold Jade Bed from Return of the Condor Heroes.

Zu An stared at the bed after entering the room, not saying a word despite claiming to have something to ask. That brought a frown to the white-robed woman’s face, and she asked, “What do you want to ask?” 

Awkwardness flashed across Zu An’s face. “I’d like to ask your name.”

The white-robed woman was speechless.

Zu An also realized his question was abrupt, so he hurriedly clarified, “Please don’t misunderstand. I happen to have a friend who resembles you greatly, so I came here to ask out of curiosity.”

“Your flirting methods are outdated,” the white-robed woman replied calmly as if nothing in the world could faze her.

“I’m speaking the truth.” Zu An knew his words didn’t sound credible, so he paced around the room before asking, “Miss, are you a Nebula worshiper?”

The white-robed woman stared at him quietly, looking as if she would evict him at any moment. 

Zu An hurriedly added, “That friend of mine unintentionally caught the Deity of Nebula’s gaze. She emanates an aura very similar to yours, though she’s nowhere as strong as you.”

The white-robed woman’s interest was finally piqued. “Oh? What’s your friend’s name?”

“Her name is Yan Xuehen.” 

When he answered the question, Zu An felt both expectant and nervous. It could be a similar case as the Goddess of Wu Mountain and the Mermaid Queen, where the white-robed woman was related to Yan Xuehen, but it could also be that the white-robed woman was another form of Yan Xuehen, similar to Chu Chuyan and the Snow Lady.

Even though it hadn’t been long since he left the Cultivation World, something could have still happened to Yan Xuehen in the meantime. That had been the case with Chu Chuyan back then.

“Yan Xuehen…” the white-robed woman murmured the name before shaking her head. “I’ve never heard of her.”

Zu An exhaled in relief, though he also felt a tinge of disappointment. 

“I am Tingxue,” the white-robed woman added.

Zu An was surprised. He hadn’t expected her to reveal her name. She has a ‘Xue’ in her name, too. Is she really not related to Yan Xuehen?

“Are you on close terms with that woman?” The white-robed woman quietly looked at him. 

“We are extremely close. She’s my lover, and we are mutually in love…” Zu An replied with a heavy heart. He regretted that he couldn’t spend more time with his lovers before leaving. 

“I can tell. I sense nebula energy from you. You must have gotten it from her,” the white-robed woman said.

Zu An smiled awkwardly.

“What a rare case.” The white-robed woman paused. She struggled to speak fluently as she rarely spoke. “Unlike other universal deities, the Deity of Nebula rarely gazes upon anyone or bestows their power. I was the only one for many years. I never expected there to be someone else.”

Zu An replied bitterly, “But this isn’t a good thing. I heard those treading down this path are doomed to become one with the nebula. The deeper they comprehend this dao, the more they lose their emotions and forget about everyone around them.”

The white-robed woman was speechless.

Zu An belatedly realized he was disparaging the other party. “My apologies, I wasn’t talking about you…”

But the white-robed woman interjected, “You’re right. I have forgotten about many things and many people.”

This was the first time she’d shown a hint of emotion. 

“…” Zu An tried probing, “Did you want to forget about those things?”

The white-robed woman shook her head. “I have already forgotten. I don’t know if those memories were happy or sad, though those emotions mean nothing to me now, so there is no meaning in feeling conflicted over this. I just feel a tinge of regret now and then.”

Zu An saw Yan Xuehen’s future in her, and that made his heart ache. “Miss, how long did it take for you to begin losing your emotions and memories after catching the Deity of Nebula’s gaze?” He wanted to gather more details so he could help Yan Xuehen. 

“I’ve forgotten about that, too.” The white-robed woman looked at him as if she was talking about something unrelated to her.

“…” Ugh, she doesn’t know anything at all. Since they were having a pleasant conversation, Zu An decided to probe further. “Miss, why did you go after Fire Knight?”

“They destroyed a world. A girl from that world gave me a flower not long ago. She was dead by the time I arrived,” the white-robed woman lowered her gaze as she replied. 

Zu An widened his eyes. For a moment there, he felt a sliver of rage from the white-robed woman. She hadn’t completely lost her emotions; they had just become very faint. 

“Such lowlives ought to be ripped into pieces!” Zu An had witnessed the miserable plight that befell destroyed worlds, and he thought people who committed such massacres should suffer even after death. 

Unfortunately, he’d been so shocked to see ‘Yan Xuehen’ that he forgot to imprison the souls of Fire Knight and the others in the eighteenth level of the Netherworld. Come to think of it, I don’t think I sensed their souls earlier. It’s as if the white-robed woman destroyed even their souls.

“I thought your goal was their invitation letter,” he eventually continued.

“It was indeed one of my goals.”

“…Why do you want to head to Dreamland?”

—

Translator’s Thoughts:

Nebula, in this case, is often translated as Primordial Chaos in most Chinese novels. It refers to the state of the world, before worlds were formed, before heaven and earth are separated, and everything is just a huge mess of dust and energy lingering around. 

There is no good translation for this term that accurately captures the connotations - so take Nebula in this case with a pinch of salt, that it’s not an entirely scientific term but also comes with fantasy/folklore connotation as well. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2728: Dreamland
chapters.body:
Chapter 2728: Dreamland

The white-robed woman looked at him. Her eyes were as deep and mesmerizing as deep space, but they carried an air of serenity even as she replied, “None of your business.”

Zu An was speechless. Considering the woman's nature and that they weren’t on close terms, her answer wasn’t surprising, but being rejected by someone who resembled Yan Xuehen still felt terrible.

The white-robed woman turned around to look out the window, seemingly deep in thought.

The atmosphere became awkward. Zu An didn’t think it was appropriate for him to continue lingering in the room, so he bade his farewell and took his leave. The woman didn’t say anything else, as if nothing piqued her interest.

While Zu An was returning to his room with a melancholic heart, he stumbled upon a beautiful, red figure leaning against the wall along the corridor. Firework looked at him with an unreadable smile as she asked, “She didn’t let you in?”

Zu An harrumphed. “I got into her room.”

“And yet you’re out so quickly. You must have failed to satisfy her,” Firework teased. 

“…” That’s an innuendo, right? Zu An opened his room door as he asked, “Do you need something from me?”

“Can’t I spend time with you?” Firework naturally followed him into the room. She continued with a dissatisfied pout, “Don’t you think it’s too much to forget about your old lover as soon as you find someone new? Just a while ago, I was still your little sister, but look at how distant you are now.”

Zu An was speechless. “When did I call you little sister?”

“I called you big brother. Doesn’t that make me your little sister?” Firework replied matter-of-factly.

Zu An was speechless.

“Fine, I’ll stop teasing you.” Firework took a seat on a chair behind him and massaged his back with her little hands. “Did you manage to gather any information about the white-robed woman?”

“You’re interested in her?” Zu An closed his eyes and enjoyed the massage. He knew that Firework harbored an ulterior motive, but it was hard to turn down an understanding little sister like her. 

“Of course. Someone as beautiful as her ought to worship our great Deity of Love and Beauty,.” Firework’s eyes gleamed with excitement. She was already imagining the immense contribution she would accrue by leading the beauty into the universal deity’s embrace. 

“You’re going to be disappointed. Tingxue is a Nebula worshiper,” Zu An replied with a sigh.

“You’ve gotten her name, too? You’re quite the charmer, aren’t you?” Firework whistled before sighing as well. “I expected as much. Her cold attitude reeks too much of Nebula. What a pity I failed to get to her before Nebula.”

Zu An asked, “Is there no way to lead a Nebula worshiper onto another path?”

Based on the intelligence he had gathered thus far, most universal deity worshipers had a chance to switch their faith after paying a price. 

Firework shook her head. “It’s impossible. All Nebula worshipers are doomed to lose their emotions and forget everything, until they return to Nebula’s embrace.”

Zu An was stunned. Tingxue’s fate sounded like an omen of Yan Xuehen’s future, and that left him with a heavy heart. 

“Did you find out why she’s heading to Dreamland?” Firework asked.

Zu An shook his head. “I don’t know.” He still felt a little stifled thinking about Tingxue’s cold tone. 

Firework burst into laughter. “Heh. Looks like she doesn’t like you that much either. You should know who's a true friend, who's been through thick and thin with you.”

“Oh? But I don’t know why you’re heading to Dreamland either.”

“I’ll tell you my reason if you tell me yours.”

Zu An didn’t mind revealing his reason. “I heard Dreamland can fulfill all wishes. I’m looking for some special materials and ways to save my friends.”

“I bet they're all women,” Firework scoffed. “Despite your honest front, you’re quite the philanderer.”

Zu An just responded, “…I’ve told you my reason. What about you?”

Firework replied, “Well, I think it’ll be fun there.”

Zu An’s resulting stare left her embarrassed, so she quickly added, “Eesh, I knew you wouldn’t believe me. Fine, fine, I’m heading there to uncover Dreamland’s secrets.”

“Secrets? What secrets?” Zu An was perplexed. 

“Have you wondered what kind of entity has the power to fulfill all wishes?” Firework asked. 

“Universal deities?”

“Not even universal deities possess such power.”

Zu An took that information to mind. I guess even universal deities have limits. “Does this mean the rumor that Dreamland is capable of fulfilling all wishes is a lie?” He had been skeptical about this, but he was still disappointed by the news, as there were questions he needed answers to. 

“It’s not necessarily a lie. Dreamland opens once every thousand years, but no one has ever accused its wish-granting ability of being a lie before,” Firework said. “That’s what bewilders me. On top of that, no one returned from the last entry. Everyone is curious about what happened. I’m not the only one who wants to investigate the matter.”

“Wouldn’t it be wiser for Dreamland to lie low if it did something unforgivable to the previous batch of invitees?” Zu An was intrigued. 

“Perhaps they're simply confident, or there's a compelling reason why they have to do it. We’ll know once we get there,” Firework said. “Once you get there, don’t trust anyone’s words.”

“Not even yours?”

“You’re terrible~”

The spaceship arrived at the galaxy where Dreamland was located. Even Tingxue couldn’t resist the urge to head to the front of the spaceship to take a look at the famous Dreamland. However, the galaxy was enveloped in iridescent lights, making it impossible to see within.

“This is why Dreamland remains a mystery despite having opened many times in the past,” Firework explained. 

“Since guests have to be transported to Dreamland each time it opens, the major powers should already know Dreamland’s location and attempt to investigate it, no?”

“Did you think that Dreamland stays in one place? The Universal Conglomerate once tried recording Dreamland’s coordinates, but when they returned later, the area was empty. Many theorize that Dreamland doesn’t stay in a single location; it’s constantly moving, and it only reveals its coordinates whenever it opens.”

Zu An frowned. “But Dreamland has opened many times before. Can’t we calculate the trajectory of its movements by recording its coordinates each time it opens?”

“Major powers have tried that, but they can’t find a pattern in the trajectory of its movements. They eventually concluded that Dreamland is no ordinary planet. The movements of planets and even entire galaxies can be calculated, but somehow, they couldn’t do the calculation for Dreamland.” Firework looked at the iridescent lights in excitement. I can finally get to the bottom of this. 

“It truly is a mysterious place!” Zu An recalled Qi Yaoguang’s divination, and his heart clenched up. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2729: Space Elevator
chapters.body:
Chapter 2729: Space Elevator

The Universal Conglomerate’s staff members matched Dreamland’s radio frequency and requested entry. Moments later, Dreamland’s staff members granted them permission. 

The iridescent lights parted, revealing a passageway. The spaceship flew inside as instructed. Everyone on the spaceship examined their surroundings, but they couldn’t make out much through the thick layer of light.

Some time later, the area in front of them opened up, and a planet came into sight. A massive spaceport was present at the outer perimeter of the planet, and the spaceship was directed to stop there.

Employee 9527 walked up to Zu An and the others and said, “Esteemed guests, we’ll have to bid you farewell here as we aren’t allowed to enter Dreamland. Dreamland’s staff members will be leading you in. Be assured that we’ll be here to transport you back to your planets once you're done, provided you’re willing to leave.”

Zu An’s heart quivered. Many people had become reluctant to leave Dreamland after entering it, so he needed to be vigilant in case the same happened to him as well. There were many important people waiting for him at home.

After the spaceship docked, the doors opened, and the Conglomerate’s staff members guided the guests out. 

Employee 9527 snuck over to Zu An’s side and said, “Brother Zu, take care of yourself!” He was also worried that Zu An wouldn’t return.

Zu An nodded. “Thank you. I’ll surely return, and I’ll be troubling you to send me back.”

“Haha, leave it to me!” Employee 9527 patted Zu An’s arms before waving him farewell. 

Zu An alighted from the spaceship and gazed at the extravagant spaceport before him. I wonder what kind of technology they've employed here. Somehow, we’re able to walk normally here even though the port isn’t enclosed. 

The different types of spaceships docked in the spaceport hinted at the diverse civilizations spread across the Myriad Worlds. It was weirdly beautiful. He even spotted a worm spaceship, something he had only seen in a sci-fi movie before. 

He had to concede that the people in his previous world were very creative, to have created content that so strikingly resembled real life. Bizarre entities alighted from different spaceships, and they were quickly welcomed by the spaceport’s staff members. 

Interestingly, the spaceport was able to dispatch staff members who corresponded to the guests’ races, and they could always converse fluently in the other party’s native language. This amazed the guests. No wonder people say it’s a place that can fulfill miracles.

The staff member who welcomed Zu An’s group was a human man. He flipped through his notebook and remarked in surprise, “Hm? There should be two more guests, right?” 

“A mishap befell them. They won’t be able to make it in,” Firework replied with a smile. 

“I see. Please follow me.” The staff member was already accustomed to such situations. Along the way, he politely asked, “Would you prefer to first have some afternoon tea at our spaceport, or would you like to head straight to Dreamland Hotel?”

“Of course we’ll head straight to Dreamland Hotel. I can’t wait to see the legendary Dreamland.” Firework’s voice quivered in excitement.

“You won’t be disappointed with Dreamland,” the staff member puffed up his chest and replied. He then turned to Zu An and Tingxue. “What about you two?”

“I’ll head straight to Dreamland Hotel, too,” Zu An replied. The spaceport was extravagant, but how could it compete with the true Dreamland?

Tingxue stood coolly without saying a word, but she appeared to implicitly agree with Zu An. 

“Please follow me.” The staff member led the way.

Zu An examined the surroundings, though his focus was more on the other guests. Those bizarrely-dressed guests vocalized weird sounds that he wouldn’t have been able to understand before, but to his delight, he could understand everything that was being said. 

Fluttering Baldie’s knowledge of languages is indeed amazing.  

Some guests carried both curiosity and respect for Dreamland, and they couldn’t wait to explore the place. They discussed where to head next.

Meanwhile, others grimly warned their companions to remain vigilant. Unlike the ignorant newbies, they understood the dangers that lurked within Dreamland. 

A small group of people looked relaxed and self-assured. Zu An couldn’t overhear their conversation, which suggested that they were all powerful experts. They were aware of the dangers surrounding Dreamland, but they were confident in their strength.

Soon, Zu An arrived before a giant building. Confused, he asked, “I thought we’d be riding on a flying ship or something?” 

The staff member replied with a smile, “To elevate our guest experience, Dreamland has chosen to employ space elevators instead of using flying ships as a transportation medium.”

He ushered the guests toward the elevator lobby and pressed the down button. Soon, the elevator door opened. “Please enter.”

Firework looked at the elevator’s transparent glass walls, followed by the thick wires extending beneath the spaceport into the planet. She hesitantly asked, “Are you sure your elevator is reliable?”

The staff member replied, “Esteemed guest, please put your mind at ease. Our space elevator has never malfunctioned to date. Our space elevator also functions as a mini flying machine if anything happens, so you’ll land safely no matter what happens.”

Tingxue walked into the space elevator first without saying a word. She sat by the window to admire the scenery outside. It wouldn’t hurt her even if the space elevator suddenly exploded anyway.

Firework stuck out her tongue before dragging Zu An in with her. 

Tingxue suddenly turned over, eyed their connected hands, and asked, “Are you two a couple?”

“None of your business.” Firework harrumphed. She'd wanted to convince Tingxue to worship Love and Beauty alongside her, but when she learned that the other party was a Nebula worshiper, she lost her interest.

Once a Nebula worshiper, always a Nebula worshiper. 

“I’m merely curious. He mentioned that he has a wife.” Tingxue turned her head back and reverted to being an ice sculpture.

Zu An was speechless.

Firework was baffled too. She tugged Zu An’s arm, tiptoed, and whispered to him, “Is that woman jealous?”

“That’s impossible!” Zu An huffed. A Nebula worshiper wouldn’t harbor such feelings. 

Firework thought that made sense.

Just then, the space elevator began moving. Her attention was immediately drawn to the scenery, her curious gaze flickering all around.

The spaceship traveled so smoothly that Zu An didn’t feel the slightest shake. 

A sweet AI voice played to inform them about things they ought to take note of. At the same time, a massive screen appeared on the elevator door, playing Dreamland advertisements.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2730: Big Miss
chapters.body:
Chapter 2730: Big Miss

Firework looked at the advertisements and laughed. “I hadn’t expected Dreamland to hire her for their promotional videos. She’s the Fairy Galaxy’s hottest female celebrity, though I think big sister Tingxue is much more beautiful than her.”

Tingxue turned to her and said, “You’re beautiful too.”

Firework’s eyes lit up. “It’s my greatest honor to receive big sister Tingxue’s compliment.”

Zu An couldn’t be bothered to join their conversation, so he made his way to the window and saw many similar elevators descending at the same time. The journey was so smooth that he wouldn’t have known that they were descending if he hadn’t looked outside. 

Dreamland must be a technological civilization. It would take advanced technology to construct such amazing systems. They might even be ahead of those advanced civilizations I saw in sci-fi films from my previous life.

It would have been great if he could have scrutinized Dreamland in its entirety from above, but the iridescent lights and clouds hindered him from doing so. No wonder so little is known about Dreamland. They're truly secretive.

The further they descended, the thicker the fog became. It came to a point where they couldn’t see the scenery outside anymore. Zu An decided to sit down on a chair. 

Firework continued chatting with Tingxue, though she did most of the talking. Zu An wondered how she could hold so many words in that little mouth of hers. 

There was a slight shake, and the elevator door opened.

“Esteemed guests, welcome to Dreamland!”

Staff members were already waiting outside. 

Zu An and the others stepped out of the elevator. They were probably at a transport hub to receive guests from the space elevator and transport them to their final destination. 

“Esteemed guests, we have three hotel styles for you to choose from—the fiery beach, the serene mountain, and the fleeting clouds. May I know which one you prefer?” the staff member said with a smile. 

Zu An frowned. “Don’t all guests stay together?”

“We understand every guest has their individual preferences, so we’re doing our best to fulfill our guests’ needs within the scope of our abilities,” the staff member replied. “Be assured that the hotel style only determines the location and environment of your stay. You’ll receive the same service and information.”

They understood what the guests were worried about. 

“It has to be the fiery beach for me,” Firework said with glowing eyes. 

“Alright.” The staff member turned to Tingxue and Zu An. “What about you two?”

“I choose…”

Firework interjected before Tingxue could finish her sentence. “Big sister Tingxue is with us. She’ll be taking the beach too.”

Tingxue opened her mouth, but she didn’t refute Firework’s words in the end. Someone of her nature would have preferred the mountain or the clouds, but for some reason, she instinctively didn’t want to pick the clouds. She'd been about to pick the mountain when Firework chose on her behalf.

It was all the same for her, so she couldn’t be bothered to argue.

Zu An chuckled. “I’ll pick the beach too.”

Firework grinned brightly. 

“This way, please.” 

The staff member led them out of the building and onto a convertible jeep. Looking at the brilliant sun and the tropical forest, Zu An had a bizarre sensation of having returned to his previous life.

Firework stood up, raised her arms, and cheered in excitement.

The staff member driving the jeep didn’t stop her despite her seemingly dangerous actions. These guests were all experts from all over the Myriad Worlds. They would emerge unfazed even if a massive truck crashed into them. 

Unaccustomed to Firework’s rowdiness, Tingxue shifted to the side, which brought her closer to Zu An. Her cold aura cooled the scorching weather for him.

Zu An sighed. He had experienced something similar from Yan Xuehen and Chu Chuyan, and he couldn’t help but wonder when they would be reunited. He turned to the staff member driving the jeep and asked, “I heard Dreamland has the power to fulfill wishes. May I know where I can gather more information about this?”

He wanted to know if he could save Chu Chuyan, and if so, what price he would have to pay. 

“There’s no rush. Please have a rest at the hotel first. We’ll make arrangements accordingly once our big miss’ coming-of-age ceremony concludes,” the staff member replied with a smile.

“Big miss?” Zu An was surprised.

“Yes, she’s the doted-on daughter of the incumbent master of Dreamland. She’ll be reaching adulthood in a few days. It coincides with your arrival, so you can attend her coming-of-age ceremony, too.”

Zu An frowned. He had no interest in Dreamland’s big miss. If anything, he was unhappy that he was getting delayed over this matter. 

Firework, on the other hand, was hyped. “Is your big miss beautiful?”

The staff member was stoked to receive that question. “Our big miss is the most beautiful woman in Dreamland!”

“Oh? Is she as beautiful as our big sister?” Firework hugged Tingxue’s arm as she asked.

The staff member glanced at Tingxue via her rearview mirror and was enchanted. “She’s as beautiful as this esteemed guest.”

“Tsk. You’re just giving me the politically correct answer.” Firework harrumphed in disbelief. 

The staff member quickly changed the topic to not offend her. “Our big miss will be conducting Dreamland’s rite after her coming-of-age ceremony. She’ll answer your questions then.”

Zu An could tell that Dreamland had planned it as such, as there was no point in probing now. I’ll have to wait for that rite or something. 

The jeep soon arrived at the seaside. Dashing men and beautiful ladies sunbathed on the beach. It was a beautiful sight. 

Firework whistled. “I made the right choice. Let’s play at the beach once we settle into our rooms.”

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up when he thought about Firework and Tingxue wearing bikinis. That made his heart pump vigorously. No wonder they say all men are pig trotters. 

Tingxue frowned, but she didn’t respond either. She wasn’t too interested in this matter. 

The jeep stopped before a hotel. Looking at the extravagant hotel, Firework murmured, “It’s a bit tacky, but I like how bling-blingy it is.”

Zu An was awed by the hotel’s size. Even with his divine sense, he couldn’t perceive its entirety. It was almost as if this hotel were a world of its own. 

“This way, please.” The staff member stepped forward to lead the way. “Please take your invitation letter to the receptionist to register.”

Right as they entered the hotel, a commotion broke out in front of them. A guest was fumbling around while exclaiming, “Someone stole my invitation letter!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2731: One Punch Man
chapters.body:
Chapter 2731: One Punch Man

A hulking man with goat horns was patting down his body while glaring at those around him, as if trying to determine who stole his invitation letter. 

The female receptionist was put on the spot. “Esteemed guest, we need your invitation letter to register you.”

“You sent the invitation letter over. Surely you must have a record of it? You would know whether I have the invitation letter or not if you just look it up,” the hulking man said with a harrumph. 

“My apologies. Our invitation letters are unnamed,” the female receptionist explained. 

“The Universal Conglomerate’s staff members who drove me here can testify for me. So can your staff members who escorted me from the spaceport to the space elevator,” the hulking man said in displeasure.

“My apologies, but our staff members won’t vet your invitation letters. You have to register with your invitation letter here,” the female receptionist gave a formal response.

“Apologies, apologies! All you can do is apologize. How do you intend to resolve this matter?” The hulking man lost his patience. “I still had the invitation letter with me when I alighted from the spaceship, but it suddenly disappeared when I passed through your spaceport and space elevator. I suspect your staff members stole my invitation letter!”

The female receptionist’s expression turned cold. “Please do not malign our staff members. They would never steal our guests' possessions. We only recognize our guests by their invitation letters and not by their faces. If you do not have an invitation letter, I’ll have my colleague send you back to the spaceport. In the meantime, please wait by the side so that you don’t hinder the guests behind you from registering.”

The hulking man was enraged. “Do you know who I am? How dare you speak to me like that!” 

Four Dreamland guards walked over. “Guest, please calm down!”

The guards tried to move the hulking man aside, but the latter unleashed a terrifying aura and shook them off. “Scram! Who do you think you are, to dare lay your hands on me?!”

Zu An was astounded. The four guards were extremely powerful—personnel dispatched by Dreamland to maintain order here were bound to be anything but weak. Yet the four of them combined were still no match for the hulking man. The aura he'd unleashed was even stronger than Fire Knight’s.

The other guests watched the conflict with interest. This was a chance to gauge how strong Dreamland was. 

The hulking man proudly declared, “I am the master of nine stellar systems. How dare you lackeys attempt to manhandle me? I’ll spare you out of respect for Dreamland. Hurry up and register me!” 

He cast a sweeping gaze at the surrounding crowd and continued, “Pray that I don’t find out who stole my invitation letter, or else I’m going to rip that person to shreds!”

Some guests were intimidated by his threat, but others remained calm, and yet others responded with disdain. 

It was then that a lazy voice echoed, “What happened here?”

The crowd parted to allow a tall, beautiful figure to enter. There were two things about her that immediately caught people’s attention. 

One was her impeccable thighs—they were long, but they perfectly filled out her tight jeans. The other was her tight shirt that resembled that of an office lady, though it looked more casual. 

Dreamland should give fitting uniforms to their employees. Her clothes are too small for her! Look at those buttons! They're on the verge of popping out. Ugh, it’s hard not to imagine just how huge she must be. It’s both annoying and amazing how the shirt doesn’t give us a peek of what’s beneath. 

Her sexy yet conservative clothes made it hard to get a grasp of her personality. She had a beautiful face, too, but it was nowhere as eye-catching as her incredible figure and tight clothes. 

She had a lollipop in her mouth, and she exuded an air of laziness, discipline, seductiveness, and innocence… It was hard to imagine how all these traits could appear in a person simultaneously. 

Firework nudged Tingxue. “Big sister Xue, I knew there were no good men in the world. Look at how they're gaping at that woman.”

Tingxue maintained a look of impassiveness, not replying to that remark. 

Zu An’s face heated up, knowing Firework was talking about him. “I’m trying to assess how strong she is.”

“So what’s your verdict? E or G?” Firework rolled her eyes.

Zu An was speechless.

“Captain Yani, someone is causing trouble here.” The female receptionist quickly filled the woman in about the chain of events. 

The hulking man was enraged. “Don’t talk nonsense, lass! Who’s causing trouble? You’re the ones intentionally making things difficult for me.”

“I am the guard captain, Yani. Guest, you may direct your problems to me.” Yani stepped in front of the female receptionist to protect her. 

The hulking man looked at Yani’s voluptuous figure and coughed softly. “Fine, I’ll drop this matter on Captain Yani’s account. Hurry up and register me. I want to rest now. Captain Yani, may I have the honor of sharing dinner with you?”

Yani waved her hand. “Guest, please register with your invitation letter first. I’ll consider whether to share dinner with you afterward.”

The hulking man’s expression darkened. “Are you toying with me now? You already know that someone has stolen my invitation letter.”

Yani’s expression turned cold. “If you don’t have an invitation letter, you aren’t a guest of Dreamland. Are you going to leave by yourself, or do I have to send someone to escort you out?”

“You bitch!” No one dared to challenge the hulking man in the stellar systems he ruled over. He had never suffered such grievances before. Unable to take it, he swiped his hand at Yani. 

Since they refuse to listen to logic, you shouldn’t blame me for resorting to violence! I bet this voluptuous lass will be fun to play with.

None of the guests tried to play the hero. They wanted to see how strong the guard captain was, so that they could gauge Dreamland’s strength.

Yani responded by hurling out a simple fist. 

The hulking man narrowed his eyes in astonishment. He realized he had made a grievous mistake, but it was too late for regret. Yani’s fist collided with his, causing his fist to burst into a bloody mist. 

But the attack didn’t stop there. Yani’s fist continued advancing toward his head and popped it like a watermelon. 

The powerful shockwave from her fist smashed a hole through the clouds and the atmosphere, revealing vast space and the worlds beyond. It took a while before the worlds faded from sight, and the clouds converged to cover the hole.

The noisy hotel lobby plunged into silence.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2732: Dreamland’S Service
chapters.body:
Chapter 2732: Dreamland’S Service

The crowd was stunned. 

Despite knowing that Dreamland had to possess formidable military might to have survived so many years, they hadn’t expected this curvaceous woman who looked like a trophy wife to be this powerful. 

She'd shattered the atmosphere with a single punch, revealing countless worlds in the universe. Her strength was top-notch even in the Myriad Worlds. Most experts of this caliber were famous kings in the Myriad Worlds, but who could this woman be?

The previously amused spectators were now horrified and grim. They wondered if they would meet the same fate as the hulking goat-horned man if they were faced with that punch, and their answer wasn't optimistic. Only a handful could remain composed.

Yani lazily eyed the crowd. “You’re welcome in Dreamland, but please don’t cause any trouble. We have fine wine for friends, but fists for enemies.”

She waved her fists intimidatingly, prompting many guests to retreat several steps for fear of ending up in the same plight as the hulking man. 

Her threat worked, as the previously chaotic scene immediately became orderly. No one else dared to cause trouble.

Firework murmured in awe, “That woman is incredible. Big sister Xue, can she beat you?”

Tingxue didn’t respond, though her expression remained calm. 

Firework had expected such a response, so she turned to Zu An and asked, “How many strikes of hers do you think you could withstand?”

Zu An shook his head. “I wouldn’t last even a single strike.”

“No, I’m sure you can handle her.” Firework enjoyed Zu An’s look of confusion as she gleefully continued, “Someone with as many wives as you is bound to be good at humoring women. Yani, as formidable as she is, is ultimately a woman. We’re counting on you to settle her, so we can have a smooth-sailing journey in Dreamland.”

Zu An was speechless.

“Why are you staring at me? If Yani were a dashing man, I’d have made a move.” Firework harrumphed. 

Even Tingxue looked over. Zu An couldn’t be bothered to respond to Firework's nonsense, though. 

Soon, it was their turn to register. As they handed their invitation letters over, they couldn’t help but be more awed by Yani’s incredible figure now that they were looking at her up close. She was very tall, standing at least at 1.9 meters, if not two meters. 

Standing next to her, Firework looked like a potato. She was more beautiful, but height was a powerful weapon when two people were put in the same frame. Seemingly oblivious to this, she leaned over to Yani and asked, “Miss, do you have a boyfriend?”

“I don’t. Do you plan to introduce one to me?” After confirming their invitation letter, Yani dropped her frosty demeanor and responded to Firework’s question with a smile. 

“What do you think about him? His heart fluttered earlier when he saw your earlier punch.” Firework dragged Zu An forward. 

Zu An was speechless. What’s wrong with this woman?

Yani assessed Zu An. “He’s indeed dashing, but I’ll be too busy maintaining Dreamland’s order to date anyone.”

That was a subtle rejection, but Firework acted as if she didn’t get it. “Does that mean you’ll be keeping all of us safe?”

“Of course. We are obliged to ensure the safety of our guests,” Yani replied.

“That works! Look at how weak he is compared to the experts around us. It’s as if he'll disappear with a poof if someone tries to harm him. You should protect him at close range lest that happens,” Firework said.

The weird looks cast by the other guests made Zu An feel incredibly awkward. He quickly dragged Firework back and said, “Miss, please don’t mind her. My little sister is joking.”

But Yani chuckled. “I’m afraid that with the responsibilities I shoulder, I can’t protect you at close range, but you can take this. Press it if you’re in danger, and I’ll rush over right away.”

She handed a coinlike item into Zu An’s hand. It was a signal transmitter, and she would sense it if he pressed it. 

Zu An wanted to say more, but Firework shoved him back and said, “Thank you, big sister Yani. My big brother will send you an invitation when you have more time.”

Zu An was speechless.

After they left, another bold customer tried the same method on Yani. Despite her ferocity, her figure was nothing short of incredible. Winning over an expert like her would be quite the thrill.

But Yani roared with a darkened expression, “Scram!”

“Right away!”

The other guests burst into laughter. 

“Do you think anyone can pull off that move?”

“You should take a look in the mirror!”

A hotel staff member led Zu An’s group to their rooms. Firework had requested for them to be placed next to one another. Zu An’s room was in the middle, Tingxue's was on the left, and Firework's was to the right. 

“Let’s head to the beach together after changing our clothes!” Firework told the other two before closing her door. 

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He was indeed tempted. 

Unfortunately, Tingxue responded by slamming her door shut. Zu An shrugged before closing his door too. 

Despite being located next to one another, their rooms were 200-square-meter luxury villas. The floor-to-ceiling glass walls ensured that the room was well-lit while granting a good view of the scenery outside.

Profound spatial technology had been used to expand the rooms. They could have been bigger, but they were kept at their current size for maximum comfort, lest the emptiness feel jarring. 

Zu An opened the cupboard and found all kinds of clothes inside. They were automatically adjusted to suit the guest’s size. It was also then that a voice recording played, informing him that he could request clothing of any style, and the hotel would tailor them on the spot and deliver them right away. 

“As expected of Dreamland.” Zu An had no interest in clothes, but he could already imagine the neighboring Firework squealing in delight. He took some time exploring his room before lying on the bed, picking up an introduction booklet, and browsing through it. 

A hologram was projected from the booklet, and it prompted him to select a language before introducing the room facilities to him. Zu An thought to himself that even imperial palaces weren’t as comfortable as this. 

However, his attention was soon drawn to a specific function. The hotel provided a list of portraits of beauties to choose from if the guests were feeling lonely. 

“These beauties are renowned celebrities in their respective worlds. They are contracted by Dreamland to fulfill your demands. You may view their holographic information and introduction by tapping their portrait.

“Once a beauty is chosen, we will send them to your room in the shortest time possible. There is no need to worry about a breach in privacy; they will not remember what happens in the room.

“Also, all expenses incurred within the hotel will be shouldered by Dreamland.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2733: Missing
chapters.body:
Chapter 2733: Missing

Zu An was amazed. 

No wonder this place is called Dreamland. It is indeed a dream to men. Hold on, what about female guests? Are their lists filled with all kinds of hot male stars? How can a gentleman like me read this kind of nonsensical content?

He tossed the list aside and continued observing the other guests on the beach outside his windows. It broadened his horizons—there were people of all shapes and sizes. 

There was a four-legged shark wearing sneakers and holding a wooden bat, a wooden puppet with a massive head, a flying crocodile, an elephant covered in thorns… There were all kinds of civilizations in the Myriad Worlds, so much so that they stretched the limits of the imagination. 

A while later, he started getting distracted. He eventually returned to his bed and picked up the list. I’m not being lustful. It’s just a way to investigate other civilizations in the Myriad Worlds.

He took a casual flip and was awed by the variety before him. They boasted different charms, be it innocent or seductive, slim or curvaceous… It helped that the list was neatly categorized, so he could easily pick based on his preferences. 

There were also different lists such as ‘Trending’, ‘High Reviews’, ‘Popular’, ‘Most Clicks’, ‘Monthly Board’, ‘Daily Board’, etc.

Zu An’s eyes were opened. He clicked on the ‘Overall’ ranking, and those on the list were indeed gorgeous. It wasn’t a surprise, since they had survived harsh market testing.

Huh? Why don’t I see that female celebrity from the space elevator?

He tapped on the celebrities’ profiles and soon noticed a pattern. 

In the Myriad Worlds, the primary factions were the Starcloud Empire and Freedom Confederation. Both powers controlled many universes, and the sheer number of planets under their control was uncountable. They were hostile to each other, but they were also equally matched. 

Other than these two major powers, there were also many influential powers, such as the Universal Conglomerate. 

There were also many different types of civilizations, such as pure cybernetic civilizations made up entirely of robots. They boasted high learning ability and had their consciousness linked to the internet, making them terrifying. 

There were also reptilian, arthropod, and fungal civilizations. In particular, planets populated by the latter were to be feared.

… 

The celebrities on the list could satisfy every racial fetish a person had. Zu An was, once again, awed by how lust was a strong motivator for progress. 

In my previous life, many guys self-learned Japanese through adult films. To think I’d be learning about different civilizations across the Myriad Worlds via this. 

A thought soon occurred to him as he flipped through the list of female celebrities. While some of them carried unique racial traits, he couldn’t help noticing most of them were humanoid, but why would that be the case?

Is it a coincidence or an inevitability… 

While Zu An was pondering that question, someone knocked on his door. Firework called out, and Zu An instinctively rushed over to open the door. 

When he saw what was on the other side, he was stunned. 

Firework was wearing an adorable yet sexy bikini. Her slender waist, fair belly, and long, slender legs perfectly encapsulated the concept of youthful vibrance. 

“Does your room have…” Firework was about to ask a question when she noticed the list in his hand. Her eyes widened into a glare. “Shame on you! What nonsense are you reading…”

“I was doing research…”

Firework interjected before he could finish his piece. “Of all things you could research, why are you researching that! I noticed that too when I was changing my clothes. I wondered if you’d be ensnared by that, so I came over to take a look. I knew it! Big brother, you’re a disappointment!”

“…” I really am doing research. Knowing she wouldn’t believe him either way, Zu An asked, “Is it also female celebrities on your side?”

“Of course not. It’s a bunch of pale-faced moochlords. Ugh, how disgusting. None can match you, big brother.” Firework harrumphed. She snatched his list, stuffed a firecracker into it, and tossed it onto the ground.

With a loud explosion, the list was blasted to bits.

Zu An was speechless. My research material!

“Stop looking at that. Big sister Xue and I look much better. Let’s go to the beach. Hurry up and change your clothes. I’ll go call big sister Xue.” Firework pushed him before the wardrobe before running over to knock on the next door. 

Zu An was stunned. This woman just makes herself comfortable with anyone. 

There was no way to continue his research after his list had been destroyed. Maybe I can gather more information by interacting with other guests on the beach. He changed into leisurely beach shorts and headed to the neighboring room. 

Firework eyed his perfect abs, and her face reddened. “You’re surprisingly ripped.”

Zu An ignored her remark and asked, “Is Miss Tingxue not in her room?”

“She isn’t opening her door.” Firework pouted. An idea popped into her mind, and she said aloud, “Big sister Xue, big brother Zu has changed his clothes and is waiting for you too.”

The door soon opened. “I told you I’m not going…”

Tingxue noticed their revealing beachwear and blushed.

Firework chuckled. “I guess big brother Zu’s name works the best.”

Zu An eyed Tingxue. She has already lost her emotions. Surely not.

Tingxue coldly replied, “I don’t like that kind of clothing. Don’t bother calling me anymore.” She slammed her door shut afterward.

Zu An felt a mixture of disappointment and relief. He was disappointed at not getting to see her in a swimsuit, but he was also relieved that no one else could see someone who resembled Yan Xuehen wearing that. 

Firework shrugged. She stuck out her tongue adorably and said, “Since she isn’t going, let’s go by ourselves.”

Zu An let her drag him to the beach. He took out the towel he had prepared beforehand and draped it on her.

Firework laughed. “What’s wrong? You can’t bear others seeing my skin?”

Zu An was surprised by his own actions as well. As a transmigrator, he had been to swimming pools, beaches, and saunas many times before. He was more than accustomed to such skimpy wear. Why would I subconsciously try to cover her?

Firework was delighted to see him stumped. Just then, she noticed his pendant and reached out to grab it. “Ooh, this pendant is gorgeous.”

Zu An instinctively guarded the pendant, which made Firework harrumph. “How stingy! Why are you so nervous about the pendant? Did your girlfriend give it to you?” 

Zu An answered with a nod. The Jing Teng sisters were still residing in the pendant. 

Only guests carrying invitation letters were allowed in Dreamland. It was forbidden to bring anyone else along. Fortunately, their true body was a talisman paper, so there was no need to worry about anyone finding them. 

Upon hearing his answer, Firework’s mood plummeted. 

The two of them made their way to the beach amid silence. Whistles could be heard now and then from their surroundings—many men were mesmerized by Firework’s beauty.

Firework walked straight toward the sea, tossing her towel onto the beach before diving into the water, swimming around energetically like a dolphin. This reminded Zu An of Shang Liuyu and Shang Hongyu. The sight of them frolicking in the water was enchanting. 

A while later, however, Zu An’s eyes narrowed. It had been a while since Firework surfaced from the water, and he couldn’t see her silhouette in the sea either!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2734: Rescue
chapters.body:
Chapter 2734: Rescue

Zu An rushed to the seaside. “Firework, where are you?”

He infused ki into his voice, such that even people fifty kilometers away would be able to hear him. Unfortunately, there was no response. 

He leaped into the sea and spread his divine sense outward. When he detected a massive shadow several kilometers away, his expression darkened. The shadow couldn’t have been a hotel guest, and there wasn't supposed to be such a behemoth loitering on the beach near the hotel. Thus, he anxiously flew over.

Just then, there was an explosion. Tsunamis over fifty meters tall gushed from the sea. Guests on the beach exclaimed in astonishment, having noticed that something was amiss.

Zu An was startled. The explosion had come from Firework’s firecrackers. She must have met with danger! Knowing this wasn’t the time to be wondering where she hid her firecrackers in her skimpy bikini, he rushed over. 

He spotted Firework’s silhouette amid the waves. She was desperately swimming toward the shore, but a massive shadow flickered behind her, and she was dragged backward. 

Zu An quickly drew his sword and slashed at the shadow, his sword ki slicing the sea in two. 

The crowd could finally see what the shadow was. It was a mountain-like sea monster emanating such a terrifying aura that the guests escaped to the hotel in a fluster. There were hardly any weaklings in Dreamland, but no one wanted to fight this behemoth from the sea. 

A few people watched Zu An with curiosity. That fellow doesn’t seem strong, so how is he able to unleash such tremendous force with his sword as to slice the sea in half?

Pu!

Zu An’s sword ki landed on the sea monster, causing it to bleed profusely. However, the creature was so huge that it was unfazed by wounds that would usually be fatal. It wriggled and dove into the deeper sea, displaying speed that was at odds with its massive size.

Zu An activated the Crown of the Ocean Deity’s ability. Wherever the sea monster dove, the seawater automatically parted. 

Boom!

With no seawater to support it, the monster crashed right into the seabed. Fresh blood oozed from its mouth, nose, and various parts of its body. If they had been further away from the shore, where the seabed was deeper, this maneuver could have taken the monster’s life.

Zu An flitted over and was about to grab Firework’s hand when the sea monster suddenly emanated a peculiar light, and in the next instant, it disappeared with Firework.

Zu An was shocked. That appeared to be teleportation. The sea monster had to have set a safe teleportation point somewhere in the sea, so it could teleport there whenever its life was in danger. 

Zu An combed the sea, but despite searching tens of thousands of kilometers, he found no traces of the sea monster and Firework. He knew what he was doing was the equivalent of searching for a needle in the haystack, but he couldn’t sit still when his companion was in danger.

He used the Sea God Crown’s ability to commune with nearby sea creatures, and he soon found a lead.

He advanced another twenty kilometers and stumbled upon an area of the sea that was bombarded by intense lightning. There was some kind of formation there. This left him with no choice but to return to the hotel first.

The only silver lining was that the sea monster didn’t intend to hurt Firework, or else she would have been gone in a chomp. 

On the way, Zu An bumped into a white silhouette—Tingxue. She said, “I just arrived. I lost your trail.”

“Thanks for coming!” Zu An had been searching the sea, so it was normal for her to be unable to find him. “Let’s head back and talk.”

When they returned to the hotel, they saw infuriated guests questioning the hotel staff, asking how there could be such dangerous sea monsters lurking in the vicinity of the beach. It was an unfamiliar lady this time, but it could be them next time!

Guard Captain Yani tried her best to maintain order while placating the guests. “Please be assured that the area around our beach has always been safe. We implemented anti-shark nets and defensive formations within a ten-kilometer radius…”

Unfortunately, the crowd wasn’t going to let things slip. They furiously questioned her about the sea monster. They had seen it with their own eyes, after all. 

Yani was also at a loss. “We’re investigating the incident. Please calm down…”

But such words were insufficient to placate the crowd. 

Just then, Zu An returned and loudly asked, “What’s going on out at sea? Why is there violent lightning in the deeper parts of the sea? There seems to be some kind of mysterious formation there. Is it from Dreamland?”

Yani was taken aback. “It’s not from Dreamland. The sea has gotten more violent in recent years, and sea monsters have occasionally appeared in the vicinity. However, we have never had a sea monster approach the beach before…”

Those words further stoked the guests’ anger. They angrily said that Dreamland wasn’t taking care of their guests’ safety.

Just then, a gentle voice called out, “The safety of our guests is our utmost priority. Put your mind at ease. There has never been a security incident in the many times we have hosted the Dream Gathering. I’ll offer you a satisfactory explanation once we get to the bottom of the matter.”

The crowd looked over and saw a seductive woman sashaying her way down the stairs. She had naturally curly hair, and her body swayed as she moved. Many guests looked at her with heated gazes. 

“Paying respect to the Ninth Elder!” Yani and the other staff members bowed. 

The crowd was taken aback. This woman is of even higher standing than Yani?

“It’s my pleasure to meet all of you. I am Diodora from the Dreamland Elder Council.” The seductive woman did a simple introduction. “I have been made aware of the incident at the beach, but there is something amiss about it. It should have been impossible for the sea monster to appear in the vicinity of the beach…”

Zu An sneered, “Are you saying that I put my friend’s life at risk to slander you?”

Several guests supported him. They had witnessed the incident, and this female elder was trying to shirk responsibility for it.

“We’ll get to the bottom of this…”

Before she could finish speaking, Zu An interjected, “I don’t need you to investigate this incident. I need you to help me save my friend!”

It would be difficult for him to single-handedly breach that mysterious sea region, and who knew if the sea monster had any helpers?

“I was about to get to that.” Diodora turned to the guard captain and said, “Yani, take your guards on board our combat ship and journey into the sea to rescue that guest.”

Yani was hesitant. “But the security here…”

“Don’t worry. I’ll take charge,” Diodora replied with a smile.

Yani breathed a sigh of relief. “With the Ninth Elder present, I’m sure everything will be fine.”

The guests assessed Diodora as they wondered if this seductive female elder was even stronger than Yani. 

Yani went up to Zu An and said, “Guest, please come with me.”

Zu An was about to leave when Tingxue spoke up. “I’ll go with you.”

Another person in the crowd chimed in. “I’m coming as well.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2735: The Brightest Pearl In Space
chapters.body:
Chapter 2735: The Brightest Pearl In Space

The crowd looked over. 

The voice came from a sprightly young lady with golden hair that glowed brilliantly under the hotel’s light. She had blue eyes that resembled the deep ocean. Her graceful dress suggested that she was from a powerful family.

Zu An was perplexed. Why was the other party offering to tag along when he didn’t know her?

Could she be Firework’s friend? But Firework has never mentioned her, and I don’t think I’ve ever seen the two of them together. 

While he was feeling confused, someone had already recognized her. “Isn’t she the granddaughter of the Acadia House’s patriarch, the first in line to inherit the house?”

“The Acadias, who are ranked top three in the Round Table Council?”

“You’re talking about the Universal Conglomerate’s brightest pearl?”

“Who else other than her?”

“Wow. Isn’t it often said that the Acadias possess wealth that can match a nation?”

… 

Zu An was surprised. 

Employee 9527 had told him that the Universal Conglomerate’s highest leadership was the Round Table Council, which consisted of seven people. Of the seven people, there were three that wielded considerably greater influence and authority. 

Those three were of equal stature. They were the founders of the Universal Conglomerate, and it wouldn’t be far-fetched to say that they were its true owners.

The Acadia House was one of the three founding houses. If that lady was the Acadia House’s first miss, it would be a gross understatement to say that her family wealth matched a nation's. 

A white-haired butler anxiously stepped forward to dissuade the golden-haired miss. “Miss, you mustn’t put yourself in danger for a stranger.”

The golden-haired miss shook her head. She walked straight up to Zu An and curtsied. “Pleased to meet you. I am Isabella Acadia.”

Zu An returned the greeting. After a quick self-introduction, he asked, “Miss Acadia, may I ask why…”

“Please, call me Isabella,” the golden-haired lady said with a sweet smile. “I sense the aura of our Universal Conglomerate’s golden friendship emblem from you. It shows that we’re indebted to you. As a member of the Acadia House, I can’t turn a blind eye when you’re in need of help.”
