Upon hearing those words, the other guests looked at Zu An in a different light. 

They hadn’t expected this humble-looking chap to have the Universal Conglomerate’s legendary golden friendship emblem. As long as he had this emblem in hand, he would be taken care of wherever the Universal Conglomerate’s influence extended. 

And now that he had struck a connection with the Acadia’s first miss, he was practically on a rocketship bound for greatness!

Zu An was grateful. “Thank you, Isabella!”

The old butler stepped back with a sigh, though he continued to look worried. 

With Isabella joining the crew, other guests began enthusiastically offering their help, hoping to forge a connection with the Acadias’ first miss. It helped that Isabella was beautiful and adorable as well.

Seeing that, Diodora said with a smile, “Our big miss will be holding her coming-of-age ceremony two days later. She’ll be hosting the Dream Gathering this time. If you have any wish, you can relay it to our big miss there. If you can pay the price, Dreamland will fulfill your wish.

“Unfortunately, you won’t be able to participate in the Dream Gathering if you miss the timing. There are too many variables to this rescue operation. Captain Yani, you’re more experienced in this field. Do you think two days will be enough to make a return trip?” 

Yani leaned against the pillar as she lazily replied, “Dreamland has always had a hostile relationship with the ocean races. We’re venturing into their turf this time. It’s unlikely we’ll be able to make it back within two days.”

Those words prompted many guests to retract their offer to help.

“Ah, I just remembered I have other matters to attend to. I’m afraid I won’t be able to join you for the rescue operation.”

The others gave similar excuses too. The volunteer count for the rescue operation plummeted. 

Zu An frowned. He had traveled to Dreamland to find a way to save Chu Chuyan and reunite with the Goddess of Wu Mountain and the ancient Mermaid Queen. There was no saying when a second opportunity would arise if he missed this one. 

I’m not as close to Firework, and there’s no guarantee she’s still alive after being captured by the sea monster…

In the end, his gaze hardened in determination. Firework had helped him a lot along the way. How could he turn a blind eye to her when she was in need of help?

I’ll find another way to save Chu Chuyan and the others…

Having made up his mind, he stepped forward and said, “Captain Yani, let’s set off quickly.” 

The longer they waited, the more danger Firework would be in.

Yani was surprised. She hadn’t expected him to give up this opportunity for a woman. A mischievous smile crept onto her face as she said, “I didn’t think you’d be the sentimental type. Let’s go.”

Tingxue calmly said, “I’ll go with you.”

Zu An was surprised. “But you might miss the Dream Gathering.”

“Firework is my friend as well,” Tingxue said flatly. “I already don’t have many friends. I don’t want to lose another one.”

Zu An felt a warm surge in his heart. Tingxue had appeared nonchalant or even impatient to Firework’s incessant chatter. He hadn’t expected Tingxue to already consider Firework a friend. 

Isabella stepped forward and said, “Don’t forget about me.”

The old butler was startled. “Big miss, the Dream Gathering is of utmost importance…”

Isabella interjected, “What dreams do you think I could have that requires Dreamland to fulfill?”

The old butler was rendered speechless. With the Acadias’ immense fortune, Isabella could even get someone to pluck the stars in the sky for her. In the many years he had served her, nothing had been beyond her reach. 

A few other guests joined the rescue operation. To them, Dreamland’s promise felt unrealistic, not to mention they wouldn't necessarily be able to afford their wish. In contrast, the Universal Conglomerate’s Isabella Acadia was very real, and forging close ties with her could bring about great benefits in the future. 

Even better, if one could win Isabella’s heart, they could soar through the skies like a phoenix.

“Feel free to follow us if you aren’t afraid of death.” Yani grabbed a coat, flung it on her shoulders, and made her way to the harbor.

A combat ship was waiting there. It resembled a modern military frigate, equipped with cannons, missile launchers, and radar. There were older-looking weapons inscribed with runes too, possibly weapons from magic civilizations.

This bolstered Zu An’s confidence. With the aid of this powerful ship, Yani, Tingxue, and other experts onboard, his chances of rescuing Firework were much higher now. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2736: Doubt
chapters.body:
Chapter 2736: Doubt

Since they were setting off to rescue someone, there was no excessive bureaucratic paperwork. It didn’t take long for the military frigate to set off.

The vessel traveled at an incredible speed. Some kind of force at the frigate’s bow parted the seawater in half, allowing it to sail with minimal resistance. On top of that, there were waves alongside the frigate that pushed it ahead. This was likely the result of a powerful formation. 

Most of the guests gathered around Isabella, wanting to get close to her. They weren’t interested in rescuing Firework; they just wanted to capitalize on this rare opportunity to socialize with the Acadias’ successor. 

The white-haired butler stepped in front of Isabella to ensure the guests maintained their distance from her.

Isabella maintained a dignified front, showing no impatience whatsoever. She took care of everyone’s emotions, leaving them with a sense of accomplishment, as if she were granting them each special treatment.

Zu An was impressed. As expected of a powerful house’s successor. Her socializing skill is incredible. 

But he wasn’t in the mood to fawn on her. He stood at the frigate’s bow and observed the ocean. It's been a while since Firework was captured. I wonder if she’s still safe. 

Dark clouds gathered in the distance, and the waves grew choppier. 

Looking at the darkened sky, Zu An had an ominous premonition. The book in his mind flipped open, revealing a white skeleton image. He was reminded of the divination Qi Yaoguang had made before he left the Cultivation World. Could the danger she divined be related to this trip?

He had crossed blows with that sea monster. It was powerful, but not impossible to overcome. Additionally, he was accompanied by top experts, including Tingxue and Yani. Isabella and her butler were also no doubt formidable in their own right, or else they wouldn’t dare to join the trip.

The other guests are experts as well, and Dreamland has provided us with a good frigate. We should have the advantage here.

Even so, Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. His unease intensified. 

Just then, Yani emerged from the cabin. She spotted Tingxue standing by the railing and was immediately enchanted. Tingxue’s hair and white dress fluttered in the sea breeze, making her look like a beautiful portrait. 

Yani retracted her gaze and walked up to Zu An. “Are you sure this is the right direction?”

Zu An instinctively took a step back. He wasn't short by any means, but this woman was towering. When she was standing in front of him, he could only see her strained shirt button, and that put some pressure on him. 

“Indeed. The ocean creatures told me this was the way.”

He had communicated with ocean beings through the Crown of the Ocean Deity to obtain a rough direction.

Yani’s eyebrows shot up. “Oh? It seems you have the ability to commune with the ocean.”

It wasn't hard to figure that out when she had seen him part the ocean water when battling the sea monster by the beach.

Zu An didn’t deny it. “What lies in the depths of the ocean?”

Yani shook her head. “We don’t know.”

Zu An was bewildered. “Aren’t you the masters of Dreamland? Surely you should know the situation on this planet, no?”

Yani sighed. “As you said, it’s ‘Dreamland’ and not ‘Dreamocean’. We govern the land. Anything else is beyond our jurisdiction.” 

Zu An was speechless. I don’t even know how to refute that.

Yani looked into the distance and said, “All we know is that the ocean is a forbidden zone. We have sent experts in to explore the area, but none returned alive.”

Zu An’s heart shuddered. If even she's saying that, the Dreamocean must indeed be dangerous.

“If it’s that dangerous, why did you open a hotel next to the beach?” Zu An asked angrily. Firework wouldn’t have met with danger if not for that. 

“Only the forbidden zones in Dreamland are dangerous. The other places are very safe. We have always maintained a non-interference policy with the sea monsters. It’s only in recent days that ferocious sea monsters started appearing in the vicinity. Even so, we never expected one to invade our beach,” Yani said with a frown, as if she couldn’t make sense of the situation either.

Anger surged into Zu An’s mind, but he forced himself to calm down. There was no point assigning blame right now, especially when he was relying on Yani’s help to rescue Firework. 

“Do you have a way to pass through the lightning sea?”

“This frigate can withstand the lightning at the outer perimeter, but the lightning grows stronger the further we venture in. We don’t have the means to reach the inner area. If we can’t find your friend in the outer perimeter, I’m afraid…” Yani didn’t finish her sentence, but her intent was relayed loud and clear.

Zu An’s heart sank. I reckon Firework won’t be able to withstand the terrifying lightning either. 

“Don’t you think there’s something weird about this matter?” Yani suddenly asked. 

“How so?” Zu An replied. 

“We haven’t had any conflict with the sea monsters for thousands of years, but they suddenly appeared on our beach.”

“Hah. You already mentioned that ferocious sea monsters have recently appeared in the vicinity, but you didn’t take any preventive measures. That’s just an accident waiting to happen.”

“Contrary to what you may believe, we did take thorough protective measures. We installed anti-shark nets and protective formations. It shouldn’t have been possible for a sea monster to enter our beach.”

“But it just happened.”

“What you see may not be the whole truth.”

“What do you mean?”

“I understand how you feel, but don’t you find it weird? Sea monsters crave flesh and blood. They don’t hesitate to devour a person if a chance arises. And yet, that sea monster kidnapped your friend instead of eating her.” Yani paused. “Do you think it’s normal for sea monsters to kidnap a person?” 

Zu An frowned. “Sea monsters have sentience. They might be up to something.”

Yani chuckled. “Do you truly understand that friend of yours?”

Zu An stiffened up. He did have an inkling of suspicion, but he hadn't thought deeper into it. Perhaps others would think they were a couple in love, but he had only met Firework not long ago on the Universal Conglomerate’s spaceship. Other than her name and her faith, he knew nothing about her. Even those things could be fake. 

He thought about how friendly Firework had been to him from their first meeting. Could she have other motives?

Judging from his expression, Yani could tell that her words were getting through, so she continued, “We’ll do our best to rescue the guest, but I am responsible for the safety of the other guests onboard, too. I seek your understanding in this.”

Shortly after Yani left, Zu An suddenly felt the temperature plummet. He turned around and saw Tingxue walking to his side. 

“I overheard your conversation.”

“What’s your take?”

“I can’t say much, but I sense Firework bears no malice.”

Her words cleared the fog in Zu An’s mind. That’s true. Firework might have been keeping secrets from me, but it doesn’t mean she’s hostile. There’s no need to overthink. 

Just then, the frigate’s siren blared. A shadow appeared on the distant ocean surface. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2737: A Bizarre Dragon
chapters.body:
Chapter 2737: A Bizarre Dragon

There was no lack of experts on the frigate, and they quickly identified the shadow to be a massive iceberg in the ocean. Unlike other clear and pristine icebergs, this one was grayish and dull.

“Careful! There’s an iceberg ahead,” one of the crew members shouted. 

“Shatter it,” Yani commanded. 

Their frigate was big enough to crash through most icebergs without taking any damage, but she sensed something amiss about the iceberg and thus decided to play it safe. 

The crew members shot beams at the iceberg. 

The white-haired butler pulled Isabella behind him for protection, whereas the other guests quickly gathered around Isabella while uttering cheesy lines like “Don’t worry, I’ll protect you.”

Zu An watched as the destructive beam struck the iceberg.

Boom!

A third of the iceberg was shaved away, and cracks appeared in other areas. It looked as if it was going to shatter very soon.

But the crowd on the frigate looked grim. Normal icebergs would have completely shattered upon being struck by that beam, yet only a portion of it had shattered after so many attacks. Something was off about it. 

The relatively calm ocean suddenly became tumultuous, as if the surrounding water had begun to boil. Huge waves raged, undermining even the frigate’s stability formation. As a result, those standing on the deck began sliding from side to side.

“Be careful, big miss!”

“Guests, please make sure to hold onto something.”

“Prepare for battle!”

… 

The ocean ahead of them slowly parted, and an enormous dragon raised its head. 

The crowd finally understood what that iceberg was. It was a tumor growing on the dragon’s head; it was only encased in thick ice because it was on an ice dragon.

And now, the ice dragon was fuming after having its tumor struck.

Zu An was stunned. It wasn't his first time fighting a dragon, but compared to what stood before him, the ones he'd fought were nothing more than snakes. The pressure he felt was on a completely different level.

He had also never seen a dragon with such a color—it was an unnerving grayish-white. 

Dragons weren’t rare entities in the Myriad Worlds. There were even worlds where they were considered a delicacy. For that reason, the guests didn’t show much fear upon seeing this one.

One even flew over and laughed heartily. “Miss Isabella, please watch as I vanquish the beast blocking your way!”

Two more guests joined him to flaunt their valor to Isabella.

Zu An was flabbergasted. I should have known there are bootlickers in every world… But still, are these fellows out of their minds to challenge that dragon? Or are the experts of the Myriad Worlds that strong?

The one who came forward first was worried others would steal his limelight, so he roared, “I’ll be taking the life of this beast!”

He whipped out a spear and charged at the grayish-white dragon with a revolving motion that quickly brewed a tornado. Due to the wind from his movements, a fifty-meter-wide gap formed in the waters, and any fish or sea monsters that came close to the tornado exploded into a bloody mist. 

Zu An could tell that it wasn't really a tornado, though. Every gust of wind was a spear sweep. That man’s spearmanship is impressive. 

The spearman looked at the grayish-white dragon in front of him and smirked. 

This big oaf isn’t reacting even though I’m already this close to it. Hmph! Does it intend to take my ultimate move head-on? It’s courting death!

If I can kill this dragon in a single blow, I might win Miss Isabella’s heart with my valor and rise to the top of the world… 

All of a sudden, he narrowed his eyes and exclaimed, “No, this is…”

His words were cut short. The grayish-white dragon opened its mouth and devoured him together with the tornado. 

Urp~

As if it was showing respect for the spearman’s all-out attack, the dragon burped.

Zu An was stunned. You were still looking incredible a second ago, so why did you get gobbled up in the next second?

Cold sweat trickled down his back. Had he been up against that spearman, he would have had to resort to his many skills to create a window for victory. 

And yet, that spearman hadn’t lasted a second against the dragon. The incredible tornado of spears he'd brewed with his spearmanship had failed to pierce the dragon’s scales.

Mocking laughter echoed from the sky. “Useless. Someone with mediocre skills shouldn’t be sullying Miss Isabella’s eyes. Watch me instead!”

That person murmured an incantation and morphed into a massive avatar that towered even over the dragon. “Miss Isabella, I’ll scale that lizard for you to make a belt.”

The avatar reached out to grab the dragon by the neck. In his eyes, the dragon was merely a massive snake, so it should share the same weakness as well. His hands moved at a speed at odds with his massive size, grabbing the dragon almost instantaneously. 

He was about to twist the dragon’s head off when he saw disdain flash through the dragon’s eyes. He exclaimed in horror, “H-how is this possible…”

The dragon’s grayish-white aura crept onto the avatar’s hand, causing it to swiftly deflate like a punctured ball. The aura continued to encroach toward the guest until it finally touched him.

The arrogant man froze up. He plummeted onto the ocean surface, shattering into countless pieces. 

The crowd on the frigate was stunned.

The third man in the sky was dressed like a cowboy in a Western movie. He harrumphed. “Only fools would engage an ice dragon in close-quarters combat.”

Zu An frowned. He realized the grayish-white aura wasn't ice, but something he was very familiar with—death energy! 

The cowboy made a move before he could be warned, though. He whipped out a gun from his waist. The gun’s barrel extended then continued rapidly transforming as if it had come out of a Transformers movie, until it was a huge cannon barrel.

“Eat my lightning cannon!” The cowboy roared in laughter as he pulled the trigger. 

A blue light burst out of the barrel and instantly struck the dragon’s forehead. 

Pah!

The dragon’s head exploded, blasting flesh bits into the surroundings. Its body plummeted into the ocean, raising huge waves.

The cowboy descended slightly so that the huge waves could serve as his backdrop. He performed a bow toward the frigate and said, “My dear Miss Isabella, this is my gift to you. Are you satisfied?” 

But what he thought was a cool action didn’t spur any reactions. Confused, he raised his head and saw the crowd staring past him with fearful eyes.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2738: War Goddess
chapters.body:
Chapter 2738: War Goddess

Taken aback, the cowboy hurriedly turned around. 

The dragon who had plummeted into the ocean not long ago had at some point already risen back up. Flesh had fallen off its massive head, revealing the white bones inside. 

“How’s that possible?!” 

It shouldn’t have been possible for anything to survive after sustaining such injuries, so why was this dragon unfazed? 

Unfortunately, the cowboy would never get his answer, as the dragon unleashed a breath that swiftly enveloped him. 

A miserable scream could be heard from within the grayish-white breath before abruptly being cut off. When the breath finally dissipated, a white skeleton wearing a cowboy hat and holding onto a gun fell from the sky. 

A commotion broke out on the frigate. 

Even though those three had been buffoons trying to impress Isabella, they weren’t weak. And yet, they were all killed in a single blow. The dragon was truly dangerous.

Isabella’s eyes reddened. “It’s because of me that they…”

The butler quickly consoled her. “Big miss, this has nothing to do with you.”

The other guests consoled her as well, thinking that her heart was as beautiful as her face. However, after witnessing the trio’s fate, none of them dared to confront the dragon anymore. 

Yani shouted from a higher vantage, “Be careful, everyone. That dragon boasts exceptional defensive prowess.”

The crowd could tell as much without her pointing that out. The trio’s attacks were formidable, but they'd failed to deal any concrete damage to the dragon. 

The dragon’s head had begun regenerating, and it didn’t take long for it to revert to its original appearance. It was as if the earlier attack had never happened.

“Be careful! It has incredible regeneration, too!”

“Holy shit! Is that dragon a Longevity worshiper?”

… 

Upon hearing the discussions on the deck, Zu An frowned.

Isabella walked over and asked, “Mister Zu, are you sure it's death energy?”

“You can’t tell?” Zu An was perplexed. Surely these experts from the Myriad Worlds can recognize death energy, right?

Isabella replied, “Ever since the Deity of Death’s demise, it has been a long time since anyone last saw death energy.”

Zu An was speechless. But the Netherworld is filled with it. As the master of the Netherworld, maybe I have a higher affinity with death energy. 

The frigate suddenly shook violently. Isabella screamed as she fell to the side. Due to her walking toward Zu An earlier, the white-haired butler was some distance away and couldn’t reach her in time. 

Fortunately, Zu An instinctively reached out to grab her hand with one hand and her waist with the other to stop her from falling. “Are you all right?”

Isabella shook her head. Her cheeks were slightly reddened. “Thank you.”

The white-haired butler was taken aback. 

The other guests were anguished. We've been fawning on Miss Isabella, but we never got within a meter of her. But that fellow went ahead to grab her hand and waist! Goddamnit!

Zu An didn’t have time to check on the influx of Rage points in his system, as an enormous tsunami over three hundred meters tall was about to crash into the frigate. 

The dragon, enraged by the earlier attack, had begun its counterattack. 

The frigate fired its cannons at the tsunami. Amazingly, they managed to blast it away. 

The crew members and guests cheered, but that only lasted a second. To their horror, when they spread their divine sense outward, they discovered more tsunamis awaiting them—several hundred, in fact. 

The frigate’s weapons were powerful enough to disperse a couple of tsunamis, but the remaining ones would still crash into them nonetheless. 

Being struck by a tsunami of such a size would be equivalent to having an entire sea crushing them in an instant. No matter how sturdy the frigate was, it wouldn’t be able to take it. 

Yani narrowed her eyes. Her fingers cracked as she clenched her fist. She was about to hurl out a punch when Zu An, standing at the bow of the frigate, suddenly raised his hand.

The raging tsunamis suddenly stopped.

The crowd was stunned.

There were many experts on the frigate, and some wielded the strength to disperse the tsunamis, though they couldn’t ensure that others wouldn’t be hurt by the ocean waves in the wake of their attack. 

And if one thing was for certain, none of them had the power to stop all the tsunamis in place the way Zu An had. 

Zu An was one of the weaker ones on the frigate. Many of those onboard believed that they could defeat him in a fight. However, they didn’t think they could perform the same feat he had just pulled off, even if they were ten times stronger. 

Isabella’s eyes lit up. She couldn’t help but stare at Zu An.

Her suitors clenched their teeth in anger. Damn it! He stole the limelight!

Zu An pressed his palm downward, and the tsunamis returned to the ocean. Such huge movements should have raised gigantic waves and terrifying winds, but that didn’t happen. 

The swimming dragon suddenly realized it couldn’t stir the surrounding water anymore, so it raised its head in bewilderment. When it realized the culprit was Zu An, it opened its mouth and roared. 

A figure flew out of its mouth, while two other figures rose from the ocean. 

It was the trio who had confronted the dragon earlier. Even the cowboy, who'd been reduced to a skeleton earlier, had regrown his flesh, though his skin was eerily pale. 

The dragon uttered a bizarre note, and the deceased trio suddenly opened their eyes. Then, their silhouettes flickered as they flashed toward Zu An. 

One brandished his spear and morphed into a sharp tornado. Another manifested a massive avatar and hurled a gigantic fist down on the frigate. The third transformed his gun into a cannon and fired it.

The crowd on the frigate was horrified. Death was frightening, but something even more frightening than that was being controlled like a puppet after dying. 

Zu An was about to dodge the attacks when Yani suddenly flashed to his side. With a cold harrumph, she first raised her palm and grabbed the incoming cannonball, extinguishing it. Then, she tossed it back at the cowboy with even greater speed.

Kaboom!

The cowboy was blown to bits.

At the same time, Yani used her other hand to grab the incoming speartip between her fingers. With a twist, it broke off. She flicked her fingers, sending the speartip flying into its owner’s forehead. 

The spearman burst apart at the same time as the cowboy.

Following that, the massive avatar’s punch arrived, but Yani casually hurled a punch of her own in response. The thick black clouds in the sky dispersed, revealing the blue sky. As for the unlucky man behind the avatar, he had long disintegrated under the sheer force of Yani’s punch.

Even the dragon’s lower jaw nearly dropped to the ocean floor, let alone those on the frigate.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2739: An Undying Body
chapters.body:
Chapter 2739: An Undying Body

“This woman is insane!”

That was the thought on everyone’s mind. Even Tingxue raised her eyebrows, surprised by Yani’s strength. 

The grayish-white dragon opened its mouth and roared furiously, unleashing an invisible sonic boom that caused the surrounding ocean water to bubble. 

The crew members activated the frigate’s defensive formation, forming a transparent barrier around the vessel. It blocked the sonic boom. 

However, the grayish-white dragon followed with a breath. Those on the frigate shuddered in fear as they remembered how an unlucky person had been killed and enslaved by this breath. Many guests were prepared to leap off the frigate to avoid the attack.

Yani harrumphed as she hurled a punch over. 

It was a simple move, but her punch easily smashed through the dragon breath and charged toward the dragon’s head. Upon contact, its head exploded into a rain of flesh and blood, and its body plummeted into the ocean as a carcass. 

Many on the frigate gulped. Good thing we didn’t cause trouble at the hotel, or else we could have been the recipients of that punch. 

Zu An was dumbstruck, too. Is she One-Punch Woman?

But Yani didn’t look relieved in the least. She eyed the area where the dragon had just plummeted with a frown. The water bubbled, and a massive bone dragon flew out from within. It opened its mouth and unleashed a soundless roar. 

The dragon was truly enraged this time. Despite losing its flesh, it felt more intimidating than before. 

“This appears to be its true form,” the white-haired butler said grimly as he stood in front to shield Isabella. “Big miss, it looks like we have encountered a Deity of Death worshiper, and I suspect that is the legendary envoy, Alati.”

The word ‘envoy’ shocked the nearby guests. Many showed looks of despair. 

In terms of strength, envoys were second only to the universal deity of their respective daos. They wielded power bestowed by their universal deity, and ordinary worshipers wouldn’t stand a chance against them.

Furthermore, Alati was an extremely famous envoy in the Myriad Worlds. Death followed wherever he appeared. Countless famous and legendary experts had fallen to him.

He'd disappeared when the Deity of Death passed away. Some said he'd been protecting the Deity of Death when the Deity of Annihilation personally vanquished him. Some said he'd betrayed the Deity of Death at a critical juncture and gone into hiding. 

No one on the frigate had expected to encounter the legendary Death envoy here. 

The talk about the almighty Death envoy brought a frown to Zu An’s face.

It was then Isabella who voiced the doubt that was lingering in his mind. “Hm? Somehow, it doesn’t feel as powerful as the legendary Alati.”

The white-haired butler replied, “The demise of the Deity of Death was bound to weaken all Death worshipers, especially Alati, who used to be a Death envoy. It’s struggling to maintain its sanity. It’s more like a corpse that is battling by instinct.”

“I see.” Isabella nodded. The other guests breathed a sigh of relief. 

Zu An was awed. As expected of someone tasked to protect the Universal Conglomerate’s princess. That old butler is highly knowledgeable. 

It was then that there was a twist in the battle.

Yani fearlessly charged at the bone dragon and hurled a fist. The dragon fought back ferociously. The terrifying shockwaves rising from their clashes produced a massive whirlpool under them. 

The crew members quickly steered the frigate away from the whirlpool, while the other guests watched the battle. 

Yani clearly wielded the upper hand, with every punch she hurled shattering multiple of her enemy’s bones. 

A few guests whistled. Yani’s beautiful appearance and explosive figure made her quite a spectacle to behold when fighting.

“She’s ferocious. I wonder if she’d be as ferocious in bed.”

“Pft. Her legs would crush that scrawny body of yours.”

… 

Of course, the guests dared not say those words aloud, fearing that Yani would murder them on the spot.

Yani suddenly roared and punched the bone dragon in half. The crowd gasped. 

Forget it. It’s safer to pursue the adorable Isabella.

“Big sister Yani has won!” Isabella cheered.

“Not yet,” Tingxue said.

Confused, Isabella glanced at the old butler for confirmation.

The old butler nodded. “Alati was a Death envoy, after all. It wouldn’t be that easy to kill it, even if it is far weaker than before.”

Shortly after those words were spoken, the bone dragon emerged from the ocean with its shattered bones and torn flesh regenerated.

Yani frowned. She charged at the bone dragon once more and engaged it in an intense fight. 

Some time later, the dragon plummeted into the ocean once more. However, it didn’t take long before emerging yet again. 

This time, Yani learned from her lesson and sought to shatter the bones into powder, thinking there was no way Alati could regenerate from that.

However, the dragon still emerged again shortly after, with the shattered bones reassembled as good as new.

“How is that possible?!” the guests exclaimed in disbelief. Yani had already killed the bone dragon multiple times now, but the latter kept returning to full health. 

Yani’s breathing had become ragged, and her chest puffed up and down, threatening to pop her buttons and break free of her shirt. At this rate, she was going to be at a disadvantage. Despite being the stronger one here, it felt inevitable that she would be defeated.

“Uncle Alfred, are Death envoys immune to death?” Isabella was intrigued. 

The old butler frowned. “That’s impossible. If that was the case, Death worshipers would be invincible. Even the Deity of Death can die, so how can a fallen envoy be immune to death?”

A groan suddenly echoed.

As the battle dragged on, Yani was eventually struck by the dragon’s attack and knocked back. The dragon pursued her relentlessly, not giving her time to rest, determined to exact vengeance for the earlier insult.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2740: Descent
chapters.body:
Chapter 2740: Descent

The guests on deck stirred when they saw Yani in danger, but none of them made a move. Having witnessed the fate that had befallen those three unlucky fools, no one wanted to be afflicted by Alati’s death energy and enslaved after death.

“Uncle Alfred, please save big sister Yani!” the kind-hearted Isabella urged the old butler.

“Big miss, the old master has entrusted me with your safety. I’m afraid I can’t help you with this.” The old butler refused to leave Isabella’s side. An earlier moment of carelessness had resulted in Isabella being held by a young man. That nearly made him break out in cold sweat. 

It’s a relief that the man meant no harm, or else big miss would have been in danger. I can’t let the same mistake happen again.

Zu An was about to make a move when Tingxue said, “I’ll deal with that dragon. You save her.”

She charged forth without unsheathing her sword, unleashing a huge wave of sword ki. 

Boom!

The bone dragon was torn apart by the sharp sword ki and plummeted into the ocean. 

The ensuing powerful shockwave knocked Yani back. She was so weakened from being exposed to death energy that the shockwave made her spurt blood, and she also fell toward the ocean. 

Alarmed, Zu An controlled the water to slow Yani’s rate of descent as he rushed over to grab her. Each time he came into close contact with her, he was awed by the vastness she carried in her bosom.

“Thank you…” Yani said weakly, looking unlike her earlier imposing war goddess image. 

Zu An expelled the irrelevant thoughts from his head and asked, “How are you feeling?”

“Not too well.” Yani raised her hands, revealing the grayish-white energy covering them. The energy tried to creep up her arm, but she suppressed it from spreading further with her own ki. “The death energy assimilates my own energy. The more I try to expel it, the more it fuels its growth.”

Stopping the death energy only fueled its strength further, and not stopping it spelled immediate death. It was hopeless either way. 

Just then, the ocean surface began to swirl. Zu An could feel the bone dragon regenerating, so he said, “Let’s return to the frigate first. Can you fly?”

Yani frowned. “It should be possible.”

Knowing she was putting on a strong front, Zu An said with a smile, “I’ll carry you.” He lifted her in a princess carry and brought her back to the frigate.

Startled, Yani stared at Zu An’s face, and slowly, she started feeling a little uncomfortable. 

The others on deck also stared at them in bewilderment. Even Tingxue glanced over, as if questioning why they were flirting when they were fighting for their lives here. 

However, it all made sense when they noticed the death energy afflicting Yani’s body. Her condition was poor enough to warrant such treatment.

“Big sister Yani, I have some medicine…” Isabella reached into her waistbag and fumbled around. 

“Big miss, that’s indeed the legendary death energy. You mustn’t get close to them. No living being can get rid of death energy after being afflicted with it; it will continuously sap its host’s life energy until the host returns to death’s embrace.” Alfred stopped Isabella from heading to Yani’s side.

The guests who wanted to check the situation also retreated in fear. What the hell! That weird energy can spread?!

Alfred’s words were credible, especially after they'd witnessed the plight of those three fools. 

“Is there no way to save big sister Yani?” Isabella asked anxiously.

“Only Longevity’s life priests or Reproduction’s offspring priests have the power to curb death energy with their tremendous life force, but this is death energy from Death’s envoy. Normal priests wouldn’t be able to deal with it.” Alfred looked around and confirmed there were no such priests onboard the frigate. 

“Should we toss her into the ocean…” someone proposed. The crowd looked at him with weird eyes, so he stiffened his neck and continued, “I bet you had the same thought, too. If we keep her on the ship, the death energy on her will just grow stronger. When it finally blows up, all of us will be infected by it!”

The crowd stirred. As beautiful and powerful as Yani was, that was already a thing of the past. She was now a walking poison that could bring everyone down with her. 

“We can’t do that! Big sister Yani is our comrade. How can we abandon her? What if your comrades abandon you when you’re injured?” Isabella raised her arms to guard Yani. 

The crowd couldn’t do anything to Isabella, so they could only try to convince her. 

“Miss Isabella, we know that you’re kind, but there is no other choice. We’ll die otherwise.”

“The death energy is still suppressed in her arms. She might still survive if we chop off her arms. Due to the death energy’s contamination, it’s unlikely she’ll be able to regrow her arms, but she should still be able to live a normal life with prosthetics.”

… 

Hearing the discussions, Zu An said, “Let me give it a try.” He didn’t think the death energy was as terrifying as they put it. 

“Don’t get infected yourself. You’ll only make things worse.” Many guests were alarmed. Yani was strong enough to temporarily suppress the death energy, but Zu An was far weaker. If he were to get afflicted by the death energy, it could act up right away, and that would put everyone on the deck in danger. 

“Don’t worry. You can toss me overboard if I get infected,” Zu An replied coldly. He pushed aside the others and walked up to Yani. 

The ocean billowed. Alati regenerated once more and let out a miserable howl. 

“You’re too noisy!” Tingxue harrumphed. She unleashed another wave of sword ki at the bone dragon, causing it to scatter yet again. Then, she quickly turned her gaze back to Zu An with a tinge of concern in her eyes. 

The crowd on the deck gulped. How is this loner that strong? She’s as powerful as Yani! No, she’s even stronger than Yani in some aspects, like how she easily dealt with Alati without coming into contact with it. 

Zu An didn’t make a reckless move. He first examined the grayish-white death energy on Yani’s hands before placing his hand on her shoulder and channeling the Primordial Origin Sutra. 

The encroaching death energy was intimidated by the Primordial Origin Sutra’s cleansing power and retreated.

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. It works. 

So, he grabbed Yani’s hands. He had noticed her meridians were different from a normal person's, so he figured it could be better to deal with the affected area directly.

“Careful!” Isabella warned in astonishment. 

Zu An flashed a smile at her. “Don’t worry.”

He channeled his Primordial Origin Sutra. The death energy afflicting Yani’s hands melted like snow in the heat of summer and quickly vanished. 

With the death energy purged, Yani swiftly regained her energy. She stretched her limbs and exclaimed in delight, “Thank you! I owe you my life.”

The crowd couldn’t believe their eyes. That fellow can control the ocean and cleanse death energy? What’s his background?

Alfred asked, “That young master over there, are you a Longevity or Reproduction worshiper?”

“Neither, I am a Deity of Memory worshiper.” 

Zu An’s eyes widened as soon as he answered that question. He felt a compelling force descending upon him. The dark clouds in the sky stirred, forming a peculiar eye. A divine voice boomed in his mind, “You’re my worshiper? Why am I not aware of that?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2741: Deity Of Memory Meng!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2741: Deity Of Memory Meng!

Zu An’s surroundings morphed. There was no frigate, bone dragon, or even ocean. He was transported into a space with data waterfalls similar to those in the Matrix flowing around him. Taking a closer look, the flowing data looked peculiar, resembling a combination of 1s and 0s yet not quite.

But Zu An wasn't in the mood to fuss over such details. Cold sweat trickled down his body. 

His previous experience with Mi Li allowed him to swiftly grasp the situation. He had drawn another universal deity’s gaze, and the other party appeared to be Mi Li’s nemesis—the Deity of Memory, Meng. 

He hurriedly said, “Paying respects to the great Deity of Memory. I may not be your official worshiper, but I have always looked up to you. I once met a Mirror Scribe, and through him, I learned the importance of faithfully recording historical events. I have always aspired to pursue the path of memory.”

“Oh? If that is your aspiration, why do you worship Mi Li?” an impassive voice responded amid the flickering data.

Sweat rained from Zu An. Mi Li was the Deity of Memory’s nemesis. That was readily evident from how Mirror Scribes sought to record facts, whereas Time Scribes sought to alter recorded history. They were antithetical entities to each other.

It also belatedly struck Zu An that universal deities could read minds, so he resigned himself to his fate and replied, “Mortals like us have no choice in a universal deity’s summons.”

“Are you saying that Mi Li forced you to worship her?” There was a peculiar tinge to the voice. 

“I’m afraid that’s not the case. Every universal deity wields compelling power to mortals like us. I willingly accepted the Deity of Chaos and Enigma back then,” Zu An mustered his courage and replied. 

He dared not talk badly about Mi Li. If Meng turned around and bragged about this matter to Mi Li, he would die a dog’s death. 

“So that’s what you’re worried about. Don’t worry, I won’t report you to Mi Li. Come under me instead, and I’ll protect you from Mi Li,” the voice said teasingly. 

Crap, I forgot they could read minds.

Zu An gulped. He probed, “Can I worship you while maintaining my faith in Chaos and Enigma?”

“Hm?” Terrifying pressure suddenly crushed down on Zu An. “A greedy one, aren’t you?”

Every cell in Zu An’s body was quivering, and it felt as if he could disintegrate at any moment. His soul wouldn’t even have the chance to undergo reincarnation. He added, “This will allow me to be of greater value to you. If the Deity of Chaos and Enigma learned about my betrayal, they would be guarded against me. That would prevent me from acquiring important intelligence.”

“You’re interesting. I can see why you piqued Mi Li’s attention.” A chuckle echoed as the pressure lightened. “Speak. What mission did Mi Li assign you? Let me see if you are of sufficient value or not.”

“They tasked me to worship Chaos and Enigma by altering the true history recorded by your worshipers,” Zu An carefully replied.

“That scoundrel!” The Deity of Memory was infuriated. “That bitch Mi Li is going too far!”

Zu An was speechless.

“You have done well. Continue staying on that side and provide me with intelligence.” Meng appeared to be satisfied with the intelligence. 

“Yes.” Zu An bowed. He carefully probed, “But how can I convince others I’m your worshiper?”

“Are you asking for benefits? You’re a cunning one.” Meng harrumphed. “What did Mi Li give you?”

Zu An hesitated before replying, “The Deity of Chaos and Enigma made me their envoy.”

Meng was speechless

There was a long silence before a roar echoed in the distance, “That lunatic! Only that bitch Mi Li would do something like that!”

… 

It was a long time later before Meng said, “Since you’re an envoy on that side, I’ll make you a Memory envoy as well.”

Zu An was overjoyed. “Thank you, Deity of Memory!” 

Hehehe, there’s nothing better than to butter up these…

His heart suddenly quivered, and he hurriedly emptied his mind lest Meng overhear his blasphemous thoughts. 

“You’re too weak. If you’re killed by accident, my reputation will be sullied. Very well, I shall bestow you with the ‘Eye of Truth’ and ‘Annals of History’.” 

Two specks of light descended upon Zu An. One fell into his eyes, and he vaguely sensed a slight change in his vision. The other fell into his hand and morphed into a mirror.

No wonder they're called Mirror Scribes. They do indeed have mirrors. 

“Your mirror is much better than the ones bestowed upon ordinary Mirror Scribes,” Meng coldly said. “This mirror is called the ‘Annals of History’. If you ever find yourself in danger, you can use the River of History as a barrier to block your enemy’s attack. No matter how strong the attack is, it can’t traverse the River of History to hurt you.”

Zu An was delighted to hear that. With this to protect me, I’ll be nigh invincible!

“Aside from that, you also have the power to unleash the River of History and randomly toss your enemy into a segment of it.”

“Is there a restriction to that ability?” Won’t I be invincible if this mirror can be used defensively and offensively?

“Of course there is. You won’t be able to trap top experts in the River of History. The stronger they are, the shorter the duration you can trap them. This item is meant to buy you time so you can escape, not for you to do other stuff.”

Zu An smiled sheepishly. Oops, my thoughts were overheard. 

“The Eye of Truth allows you to see through your enemy’s illusions and disguises, lest you die without knowing how,” Meng said. 

Zu An’s heart felt warm. The Deity of Memory must truly care about me to give me so many defensive artifacts. I’m touched… Even the data waterfalls didn’t look as intimidating as they were before.

“It’s good that you know. You know what you have to do from now on.”

“Thank you for the generous bestowments. I’ll devote myself to you!” Zu An raised his head and tried to use the Eye of Truth. The data waterfalls vanished, and he saw a beautiful figure. 

“Hmph!” A cold harrumph echoed, and Zu An’s eyes, nose, and mouth bled. 

“What are you looking at?” Meng scoffed imposingly and coldly. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2742: Key
chapters.body:
Chapter 2742: Key

The beautiful figure vanished, replaced by blood-red data. 

Zu An withstood the terrifying pressure and hurriedly said “It may have only been a brief glimpse, but I am awe-inspired by Memory’s grandeur and divinity. It’s my utmost blessing and fortune to be a Memory envoy.”

“You have a glib tongue.” The data slowly reverted to green, and the pressure gradually receded. “Never be so brazen as to peer at a universal deity again. It’ll only bring destruction upon yourself.”

“Thank you for the lesson,” Zu An obediently replied. 

“I need you to investigate a matter,” Meng said. 

“It’s my honor to serve you.” Zu An wondered if he was going too far with the subservience, but then again, since he was dealing with a universal deity here, it likely wasn't excessive. 

“I sense the Deity of Death’s aura at your side. There are many unrecorded details about the Deity of Death’s demise, and that violates the way of Memory. Thus, I want you to look into the matter.” Meng was displeased. She thought her Memory worshipers were good-for-nothings for failing to record such an important event.

Zu An took this chance to probe, “I heard the Deity of Death was killed by the Deity of Annihilation. Is that true?”

“It’s not that simple…” Meng suddenly paused and said, “That’s not something you should concern yourself with. Such secrets will only put you in greater danger.”

“I understand.”

“You may return now. Remember your mission.”

The scenery suddenly morphed, and Zu An found himself standing at the frigate’s bow once more. The sea breeze blew, and he belatedly realized he was soaked from head to toe. 

Haa! No wonder everyone warns me against carelessly talking about universal deities. It’s fortunate I reacted fast, or else I could have died there. It was a close shave, but I obtained two special abilities from that encounter. 

I'm now both Chaos and Enigma’s and Memory’s envoy. I wonder if I’ll be ripped apart if Mi Li learns about this… Hold on, Meng referred to Mi Li as a ‘bitch’ earlier. Is Mi Li a woman?

He had glimpsed Meng using the Eye of Truth, and the latter appeared to be a woman. 

Are universal deities really genderless?

He shuddered and hurriedly shook off those wayward thoughts. I misspoke and summoned Meng earlier. I might not be as lucky if my thoughts wander and summon something else instead.

“Is that the Deity of Memory Meng’s gaze?”

“It does feel like Memory.”

“Brother Zu is truly Memory’s worshiper.”

“There are so many Memory worshipers in the world, but how many receive Memory’s gaze? Brother Zu is destined for great things. He might have a chance to become a Memory envoy in the future.”

… 

The crowd’s attitude changed from their earlier nonchalance to amicableness.

I am already a Memory envoy. 

Zu An chose not to reveal it, not because he didn’t want to brag but because he was afraid the news would reach Mi Li’s ears. He raised his head and looked around, and his expression soon tensed up. 

“Big brother Zu, you saved big sister Yani earlier. Do you have a way to deal with Alati? Big sister Tingxue has scattered that bone dragon several times now, but she isn’t able to kill it,” Isabella said worriedly.

When it sensed the Deity of Memory’s divine power, Alati hadn’t dared to cause trouble, but when it realized that the Deity of Memory hadn’t descended, it quickly returned to whatever it was doing. 

Had it retained its sanity, it would have retreated right away. However, it was completely driven by instinct right now, which was why it was obsessed with bringing down the people who hurt it.

Just like Yani, Tingxue could easily subdue Alati but not kill it. Even if she ground the latter’s bones to powder, it still regenerated. Is it due to the death energy, or because it was Death’s envoy?

“Let me give it a try.” Zu An flew over and looked at the ferocious bone dragon. He channeled his Primordial Origin Sutra and murmured an incantation. “Dust to dust, earth to earth. What should not remain should cease to exist…”

The roaring bone dragon suddenly became quiet. As it listened to the incantation, its twisted expression slowly became peaceful. A light breeze blew, and the grayish-white energy cloaking Alati gradually grew faint. 

A middle-aged man’s silhouette manifested on Alati’s head. His fierce appearance suggested that he'd been a formidable figure back then. He cupped his hands toward Zu An and said, “Thank you, young man. I am finally free after so many years.”

“You are Death Envoy Alati?” Zu An asked curiously.

“It has been many years since I heard that name. I didn’t expect there to be someone who recognized me.” The middle-aged man sighed wistfully. 

“How did you end up in… that state?” Zu An took this opportunity to gather more information.

“I was cursed.” As the middle-aged man recalled the past, a sliver of anger and fear flickered through his eyes. “Don’t ask who cursed me. Learning too much will only bring you misfortune.”

He passed a token over and said, “I have been waiting for a fated someone. Countless years have passed, and I even gave up hope. I didn’t expect to find you.”

“What is this?” Zu An examined the token. It was golden with a jade-like texture. A layer of pristine light gleamed on its surface. It was bizarrely shaped, reminiscent of a gear.

“It’s a key.”

“A key to what?”

“I can’t reveal too much. You’ll know in due time.”

Zu An wanted to curse. Argh, another riddler! But he came to terms with it when he thought about what he had just been through. It felt almost as if the universal deities and strong entities had some ‘keyword surveillance’ ability, in which uttering specific words drew their attention. 

He wasn’t confident he could survive a repeat of the earlier situation.

After handing Zu An the token, the middle-aged man closed his eyes and said, “It’s finally over…”

Zu An had more questions to ask, but the silhouette began dissipating under the breeze. At the same time, the bone dragon, whom neither Yani nor Tingxue had been able to kill, disintegrated into ashes, vanishing without a trace. 

The crowd rushed up to Zu An when he returned to the frigate. Yani asked, “Did he give you something?”

“He gave me a key,” Zu An replied honestly.

He figured since he had no idea what this key was for, perhaps revealing it to others could spark further intel about it.

The crowd fell into deep thought. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2743: Devil Eye
chapters.body:
Chapter 2743: Devil Eye

Zu An took in the crowd’s expressions. Hm, it looks like they have other motives for joining this rescue operation out in the ocean. He stowed away the key and asked, “Miss Yani, can we speed up a little?”

After being stalled by the bone dragon, he was getting increasingly worried about Firework. 

Yani wanted to return the favor after being rescued by him, so she immediately took control of the frigate and went full speed ahead. 

After witnessing his special abilities and close association with the Deity of Memory, many people tried to strike up a conversation with Zu An, but he was too worried about Firework, so he dealt with them perfunctorily.

The commotion from the earlier fight appeared to have frightened away the other monsters in the ocean, so they didn’t encounter any danger along the way. Soon, dense storm clouds could be spotted in the distance. Thunderous claps reverberated.

The crowd had heard about it from Zu An, but all this time, they'd thought he was exaggerating. It was only now that they realized the storm clouds were more frightening than they'd expected.

Lightning bolts as thick as pillars flashed here and there. Any normal cultivator would have been burnt to crisp upon contact. 

Yani went up to Zu An and asked, “Are you sure your friend is in there?”

Zu An nodded. “She was brought in there.” That was what the ocean creatures he'd communed with told him. 

“Our frigate’s defensive formation only allows us to search the outer area. I’m afraid we’ll have to turn back if we don’t find your friend there,” Yani reminded him. 

Zu An turned to the crowd and said, “I’m grateful that you've come out here to help me rescue my friend, but things are only going to get more dangerous from this point on. You don’t have to risk your life with me. There are lifeboats on the frigate, so you can wait out here for us.”

“You’re being too polite. Allow me to head into the stormy region with you. It’ll help to have an extra pair of eyes looking around.” 

Zu An had already proven his worth, so it wouldn’t hurt to sell him a favor.

On top of that, those gathered on this frigate were experts of their respective worlds, and they were confident in their strength. They were also curious about what lay within the stormy region, thinking there could be fortuitous encounters there. 

Isabella also spoke up. “Big brother Zu, we came here to rescue your friend. It doesn’t make sense for us to back out now. You don’t have to worry about us. We’ll take care of ourselves and leave if we sense ourselves reaching our limits.”

Zu An bowed to the crowd. “Thank you!”

Yani steered the frigate toward the stormy region. They could already feel furious winds even when they were only at the borders of the stormy region, and the waves were already taller than the frigate. 

Zu An used the Crown of the Ocean Deity’s ability to suppress the ocean water, allowing the frigate to steer stably into the stormy region. 

Despite already knowing about this ability, the other guests were astounded.

The butler Alfred remarked, “Only a small handful of water deities and ocean deities wield such a water control ability, and none of them are… this young.”

Zu An smiled. “Are you saying I look like a kid?”

Alfred laughed. “You’re a generous person. I can see why you were given our Conglomerate’s cherished friendship emblem.”

Isabella also spoke up. “Big brother Zu, you should drop by my home after this.”

Upon hearing those words, the crowd blazed with envy. Isabella’s invitation held significance, and more importantly, she was inviting him not to her office, but her home! Is the Acadia House going to get a new son-in-law?

“I’ll have to make a trip back home first. My wives are worried about my safety,” Zu An replied. When he said those words, he couldn’t help but eye Tingxue, since she resembled Yan Xuehen a lot.

The crowd was rendered speechless. Why are you going all out to save another woman when you already have multiple wives? On top of that, you’re still flirting with Miss Tingxue and Miss Isabella. Don’t you think you’re being too greedy?!

Isabella was taken aback. “Yes, family should come first. May I visit big brother Zu’s world then? I’m sure it’s a beautiful place.”

The guests exchanged looks. They had been fawning on Isabella all this time, and while Isabella had treated them with respect, the sense of distance was unmistakable. They hadn't thought she could be this proactive. 

Even Alfred frowned. Due to our big miss’ background, her words and actions are bound to invite speculation. It’s not a good idea for her to follow another man to his homeland. However, he could tell that Isabella harbored innocent thoughts, so he didn’t stop her.  

Zu An smiled. “My homeland is indeed a beautiful place. It’d be my honor to host you.”

“It’s a deal!” Isabella replied with a sweet smile that could easily win anyone over. She felt like an energetic little sister that brought joy to the room. 

While they were chatting, the frigate had already steered into the stormy region. Thick lightning bolts fell toward the frigate. They were almost purple in color, and it felt as if they contained calamitous power. 

The crowd tensed up. Several people whipped out their weapons, ready to deflect the lightning if needed. 

However, the bolts falling toward the frigate were stopped by a transparent barrier, upon which they dissipated into the surroundings as little lightning specks. 

The crowd were awed by Dreamland’s wealth. To think that even an ordinary frigate would be equipped with such a formidable barrier!

Zu An examined the barrier. It ingeniously combined technology with formations, and it was designed specifically to ward off lightning. Dreamland must have tried venturing into this stormy region before.

Despite being protected by the barrier, the crowd remained tense. The visibility had abruptly plummeted in the stormy region, so much so that the experts onboard could only see several hundred meters away at most. The dense stormy clouds looming over them were suffocating. 

“It resembles an apocalypse I once witnessed. The world was torn apart shortly after.” 

“Was an Annihilation worshiper here?”

… 

Despite its advanced technologies, Dreamland’s frigate shook like a toy in a child’s hand, seeming as if it would capsize at any moment. 

Alfred suddenly spoke up. “This storm reminds me of a planet I once passed by. That planet was ravaged by a massive storm that spanned thousands of kilometers. The storm was visible even from space. For that reason, there were no living beings on that planet.”

“Why didn’t we notice this earlier when arriving at this planet?” Isabella asked.

“Big miss, our vision was obscured by the clouds. We couldn’t have noticed these storm clouds,” Alfred explained. 

Isabella turned to Yani and asked, “Big sister Yani, how big is the stormy region?”

Yani replied, “These storm clouds have existed in the Dreamocean for a very long time. We call the region the Devil Eye. It wasn’t that big initially, spanning only a radius of a thousand square meters, and it was stable until several centuries ago. But in recent years, the Devil Eye began rapidly expanding at a rate that baffled even us.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2744: Road Of No Return
chapters.body:
Chapter 2744: Road Of No Return

The guests present here were all top experts in their respective worlds. They understood that something must have happened to cause such a change in recent centuries, and it could spell a great fortuitous encounter. Although they'd first joined this expedition to forge friendly ties with the Acadias, their interest was now piqued. A fortuitous encounter that stirred a phenomenon of this extent had to be extraordinary. It would be highly beneficial to obtain it.

Their initial intention had been to back out as soon as things looked dangerous, but now, they were determined to see to the end of the expedition no matter how dangerous it was. 

The bigger the waves, the more valuable the fish!

Isabella asked, “Big brother Zu, since you can control it, why don’t we travel in the ocean?”

Before Zu An could say a word, Yani replied, “That might work elsewhere, but this region of the ocean is affected by the terrifying storm. There are countless typhoons joining the ocean to the lightning in the sky, imbuing the water with electricity. Furthermore, many hidden currents have formed underwater. It’d be more dangerous to travel in the ocean.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. “She’s right. This ocean region now contains lightning and wind. I only have sway over the ocean. It wouldn’t be easy for me to control it.”

Isabella was worried. “I didn’t think this ocean region was so violent and dangerous! If the sea monster brought big sister Firework here, she might be… Big brother Zu, you might have to mentally prepare yourself.”

Zu An’s gaze remained unwavering. “Even if she’s dead, I won’t stop until I see her corpse. I can’t give up just like that.”

The other guests agreed. 

“We've already come this far. How can we give up halfway through?”

“If sea monsters can survive here, there could be a hidden safe zone, but we’ll have to explore the area to know.”

… 

Isabella was perplexed. These people weren’t that enthusiastic earlier. What made them change their minds?

Yani sighed. “Young master Zu can control the ocean, while our frigate can just about defend against the lightning. It’s a pity none of us can control the wind, or else it would simplify matters.”

Isabella’s eyes lit up. She reached into her bag and fumbled for a while before pulling out a pearl. The pearl was nowhere as brilliant as other pearls, but it seemed weirdly heavy. 

As soon as she pulled out the pearl, the roaring wind around them suddenly stopped. The vigorously swaying frigate also regained its stability.

“It’s the Wind Quelling Pearl! It’s said that this legendary item can suppress all winds. I didn’t expect it to exist!”

“It stopped even such terrifying typhoons! As expected of a legendary artifact!”

… 

There were plenty of knowledgeable individuals on board the frigate. It didn’t take long for someone to recognize the pearl. 

While gripped with excitement, some guests looked at Isabella in confusion and asked, “Miss Isabella, why didn’t you take out this pearl earlier?”

“I forgot about it.” Isabella’s face reddened. “I received this pearl as a gift when I was nine. It has been in my bag since.”

The crowd was speechless.

Their eyes fell on Isabella’s dainty bag. It’s not a joke when they say the Acadias’ wealth can rival a nation. The treasures inside that bag could be greater than most fortuitous encounters out there.

But no one in their right mind would rob the Acadias’ big miss. Given the Universal Conglomerate’s immense influence, they would only be courting their own deaths.

Zu An didn’t think that much into it. He was merely amazed that Isabella’s palm-sized bag could hold so many items. It must be a spatial artifact. Feels almost like Doraemon’s pocket.

Yani exclaimed in excitement, “With the assistance of this Wind Quelling Pearl, we’ll be able to venture deeper into the storm!”

She instructed Isabella to quell the winds in their immediate surroundings so the ocean water would remain calm. That would allow the frigate to swiftly sail further in.

Despite the devastating winds and lightning wreaking havoc in the surroundings, the narrow space around the frigate remained unharmed. The prowess of the Wind Quelling Pearl was impressive indeed. 

Some time later, the frigate sailed through the terrifying storm and arrived at calm waters. 

The guests on the deck were amazed. They'd never expected the center of that terrifying storm to be this peaceful.

Isabella remarked in delight, “Big brother Zu, the sea monster might have brought your friend here. We might be able to find her soon.”

“Let’s hope so.” Zu An looked at the calm waters with an ominous feeling in his heart. 

It’s far too calm. How could the center of a terrifying storm be this serene?

“Hm? A fog is settling in.”

“Careful. The fog might be poisonous.”

“Don’t worry, I’ve checked. It’s not death energy.”

… 

Upon realizing the fog wasn’t poisonous, the guests breathed a sigh of relief.

As the frigate ventured deeper in, the fog continued to thicken, eventually reaching a point where visibility was limited to a hundred-meter radius.

“Something is weird about the fog. It’s hindering my divine sense,” Alfred said with a frown.

The crowd hurriedly tested it out. They were appalled to realize that they couldn’t sense what was within the fog. 

Experts of their caliber could spread their divine sense a far distance away as easily as eating and drinking. To have their visibility limited to a hundred meters was as good as becoming blind to them. They immediately lost their sense of security.

“The frigate’s radar isn’t working either.” Yani’s heart sank. Nothing was working, whether it be cultivation, magic, or technological tools.

“Things are getting too dangerous. I need to take responsibility for my lady’s safety. I believe we’re in grave danger, and I request for the frigate to turn back right away,” Alfred told Yani.

Even the ambitious ones who coveted the fortuitous encounter lost their courage. This place was simply too eerie. It would be wiser to return first.

Isabella was flustered. “Alfred, I’m going to continue…”

Alfred interjected before she could finish speaking. “Big miss, I can heed your words for other matters, but the old master has given me the discretion to make decisions on your behalf when your safety is at stake.”

Isabella was stunned. She looked at Zu An apologetically and said, “Big brother Zu, I…”

Yani suddenly interjected with a grim expression, “Now is not the time to be arguing about this. I’m afraid it might be impossible for us to turn back.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2745: How Could It Be?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2745: How Could It Be?

“What’s wrong?” The guests were horrified. 

“We have traveled through the fog for some time now. With our divine sense failing and all the frigate’s navigational tools malfunctioning, we can’t find our way back anymore,” Yani said with a frown.

“Why can’t we just turn around and return where we came from? We've been moving in a straight line all this time,” a guest suggested.

“We thought we were traveling in a straight line, but I just realized that isn’t the case at all. There’s something very off about this place.” Yani shook her head. 

“So many experts are gathered on this frigate. Do you think we wouldn’t have noticed it if we steered away from our original direction? I’ll give it a try.” 

Skeptical of Yani’s words, the guest flew from the frigate’s bow to around a hundred meters away before flying back. He didn’t fly further away, as he was worried he would be in danger if he strayed from the crowd’s gaze. He asked, “How is it? I flew in a straight line, right?”

The guests on the frigate stared at him in bewilderment. “Are you certain you flew in a straight line?”

“Of course! Do you think I can’t tell whether I’m flying in a straight line for a distance this short?” the guest snorted in disdain. Seconds later, he asked with a frown, “Hold on. Was I not flying in a straight line earlier?”

The other guests shook their heads. “You first flew left, then right, and at some point, you started flying in circles.”

“That’s impossible!” the guest exclaimed in disbelief. How can I not tell whether I’m flying in a straight line or not?

Someone was worried that the guest was working with Yani to fool everyone else, so he decided to verify it. He flew forth from the frigate and returned shortly after, at which he turned to Isabella and asked, “Esteemed Miss Isabella, was I flying in a straight line earlier?”

Even if he didn’t trust others, there was no way the Acadias’ first miss could be bribed to deceive him. 

Isabella shook her head. “You flew haphazardly too, but there was a difference in your flight trajectory. You circled a few more rounds, and the direction of your flight was different as well.”

By this point, the other guests had already turned pale. This is terrible. Even their flight trajectories were different. That means the distortion in our senses isn't constant, making it impossible to figure out a pattern in the distortion. 

Alfred spoke up. “A formidable power must have warped the fundamental laws of physics in this place, resulting in this peculiar phenomenon.”

“Could it be the Deity of Death? There was an ex-Death envoy outside.”

“That’s impossible. The Deity of Death has been dead for countless years, and no one has been able to find any traces of him. It’s too much of a coincidence for us to bump into him just like that.”

“And there’s no death energy in the vicinity either.”

… 

There was another reason that no one voiced out loud. If the Deity of Death was behind this, how could the Dreamland remain oblivious to this for so many years?

“What should we do? Do we continue wandering around?” someone asked with a quivering voice. 

Not only were they unable to find their way back, but even if they continued venturing forward, there was no guarantee they would find anything. 

“Actually, I have something that may help us with directions.” As Isabella spoke, she reached into her bag and pulled out a glass tank with a bizarre-looking fish swimming amid corals. “This is an East Fish. It’ll always head eastward no matter how complicated the environment is.”

“I've heard legends about this fish! Having it can steeply lower the difficulty of most labyrinth dungeons!”

“This is wonderful! We can return now.”

… 

Zu An was more astounded by how Isabella pulled out a living creature from her bag, considering how most spatial artifacts could only hold dead items. Could her bag contain an entire world in it?

The guests stared intently at the fish as they waited for it to point out a direction.

The East Fish swam in circles as if it couldn’t determine where east was. Perhaps stressed by all the eyes focused on it, it eventually flopped over and revealed its belly, no longer wanting to swim.

The crowd was speechless.

Isabella’s face heated up. The shower of compliments she had received from the crowd earlier turned into humiliation for her. “Why is this happening? The East Fish has never reacted like that before.”

Sensing her fluster, Zu An spoke up on her behalf, “This fog could be more than a simple labyrinth. It could be a distortion of fundamental physical laws that renders the concept of direction meaningless. Naturally, the East Fish wouldn’t be able to determine where east is.”

The others voiced their agreement. Isabella shot a grateful look at Zu An. 

Alfred spoke up as well. “For even the East Fish to fail, the distortion must be severe. Does anyone have other ideas? We need to work together to overcome this crisis. We shouldn’t be holding anything back now.”

A guest scattered beans around, which grew into talisman figures. The figures scattered into the surroundings, and a thorough map of the area appeared in the air. The figures had to be extraordinary treasures.

Before the crowd could celebrate, the map flickered before abruptly vanishing. The guest smacked his chest in bitter regret as he bemoaned, “My talisman figures!”

He had lost control over all the figures.

Another guest sneered. 

What an idiot! Why would you send your precious artifact into the fog when you already know something is off about it? 

He pulled out an eight-trigram compass and placed his hand on it. The compass began revolving quickly. The runes on its surface drifted out into midair to construct words. That guest’s eyes lit up in excitement.

However, the runes suddenly distorted the next moment, and he groaned and spurted blood on his compass. His face turned pale, a sign that he had sustained serious internal injuries. 

“It all falls on me in the end,” an old woman dressed in a mage robe snorted. She swiftly formed a series of hand seals while murmuring an obscure incantation. 

“That appears to be something from a supernatural civilization.” 

“Is it some kind of shamanistic ritual? Hold on, what is she using as a sacrifice?”

The knowledgeable guests were appalled. Is she going to use us as tribute?

The answer was soon revealed. 

The woman’s arm was first twisted off her shoulder, then it swiftly withered, as if some invisible entity had siphoned away its blood. A spatial crack appeared in the sky, and it looked as if a mysterious entity would descend. A blood-red eye emerged from the crack. The pressure it emanated left the crowd with goosebumps. 

But then, the eye suddenly widened as if it had seen something terrifying. It immediately darted back into the crack while murmuring something in a language that no one understood.

Flabbergasted, the old woman shouted, “How can you leave without doing anything after accepting my tribute…”

But the eye paid no heed to her complaint, and the spatial crack closed. The old woman had already weakened from losing her arm and a considerable amount of her blood essence, and the immense anger she felt from being betrayed caused her to pass out.

“What’s going on?” The crowd was confused. Why did the mysterious entity suddenly retreat? Was the tribute insufficient?

Zu An’s face turned grim, as he understood what that entity said before it left.

“What?! How can it be ****-”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2746: Destination
chapters.body:
Chapter 2746: Destination

Zu An wouldn’t have understood those words either if he hadn’t inherited Fleeting Baldie’s knowledge of languages. It was a language from a supernatural world, a powerful one that rarely interacted with the other worlds. Even Fleeting Baldie had only grasped a bit of their language. 

'How can it be ****'? What could that last word be?

He couldn’t understand the last word due to his limited grasp of the language, but he could sense the mysterious entity’s extreme fear of whatever that was. Just the eye of that entity was enough to heap immense pressure on everyone on the frigate, so it was hard to imagine what could have provoked such a huge response from it. 

The old woman’s lips quivered. She wanted to curse but didn’t dare to do so, and that left her feeling unbearably stifled.

The others understood how she felt. She had paid a heavy price, only for the mysterious entity to take her tribute but not do any work. Anyone in her place would have wanted to curse as well.

Isabella hurriedly took out a pill from her bag and fed it to the old woman. The flesh around the old woman’s shoulder quickly regrew, and it didn’t take long for her arm to follow. 

The old woman thanked Isabella profusely. She had the means to regrow her arm as well, but that would take more time, and she would have to consume tonics to ensure a smooth recovery. Yet those problems were all circumvented with Isabella’s pill. 

Isabella did live up to her reputation as the Acadias’ big miss after all.

“I still have no idea why that lord suddenly retreated. He normally gets things done after accepting my tribute.” The old woman was still confused by the earlier situation.

Zu An told the guests what he'd heard from the mysterious entity.

Isabella looked at Zu An with surprised eyes. She was impressed that on top of all the abilities he had already displayed, he also understood that supernatural world’s language.

The guests were appalled. What could have struck fear in an entity of that level? Could this place truly be related to the Deity of Death?

Since they couldn’t even figure out the directions, neither advancing nor retreating was an option for them. This put the crowd at a loss.

Just then, Yani turned to Zu An and asked, “Didn’t Alati hand you a key earlier? Could that be a clue?”

Zu An took out the uniquely-shaped key. He'd found nothing special about it when he examined it earlier, but right now, the key was emanating a white light, as if resonating with something. 

The light converged in the sky and slowly formed a map.

“This…” The previously fearful crowd suddenly trembled in excitement and greed. They realized they might have truly stumbled upon a fortuitous encounter. Under normal circumstances, they wouldn’t have found a fortuitous encounter even if they had devoted their lives to searching for it, but it just so happened that Zu An had the map.

We’re on the same boat now. Surely he can’t lock us out after we find the fortuitous encounter? As per convention in the Myriad Worlds, once we find it, it’ll be up to the individual to obtain whatever they can from it.

Something guarded by the ex-Death envoy Alati and this law-warping labyrinth was bound to be extraordinary. Thus, the guests urged Yani to follow the map.

Yani said, “Since we can’t retreat anymore, we’ll have to advance. We might find another way out of this place.”

Zu An frowned. “I came here to rescue my friend.”

The other guests tried to persuade Zu An. “There’s not a single living being around us. It’s unlikely that you’ll find Miss Firework by wandering aimlessly in this fog labyrinth. It’d be wiser for us to follow the map. Think about it. The sea monster should be familiar with this region for it to bring Miss Firework here, so she could be at the location indicated on the map.”

Zu An thought those words made sense. He'd tried communicating with the sea creatures in the vicinity, only to find there were no living beings around them. Their only clue was the map, so their best bet was to try their luck there. 

Yani began sailing the frigate according to the map's directions. Said map also contained an arrow that always pointed in the right direction despite the distortion.

Often, when the crowd thought they should continue heading forward, the arrow told them to head in the opposite direction. So in the end, Yani ordered her subordinates to close their eyes and just blindly follow her instructions, lest they be fooled by their senses. As a result, the frigate kept changing directions, sometimes even circling non-stop on the spot.

Yet the crowd felt themselves approaching their destination. 

Alfred, who was standing in front of Isabella to guard her, sent her a telepathic message. “Big miss, please make sure to keep close to me. A place guarded by an ex-Death envoy is bound to be fraught with dangers.”

He didn’t want to come here, as it would expose Isabella to too much danger, but unfortunately, he didn’t have a choice. They had an artifact to create a ship, but they wouldn’t be able to navigate their way out of an ocean region where even the East Fish had given up. It would be courting death to leave the frigate now, so they could only follow the others and hope for the best.

Isabella maintained her graceful smile while replying telepathically, “Uncle Alfred, it should be safest for us to stay close to big brother Zu.”

Alfred frowned. “Big miss, you can know a person but not their heart. It hasn’t been long since you met him…”

Isabella shook her head. “Big brother Zu is willing to take such a huge risk to rescue a friend whom he has only just met; he is a trustworthy person. Of everyone on this frigate, he has the purest motive.

“Not to forget, he has obtained Alati’s key and received the Deity of Memory’s favor. This shows he is blessed with luck. Staying with someone who is upright and has luck on his side increases our chances of surviving this crisis.”

“Our big miss has grown up.” Alfred was moved. Our big miss is kind but not foolish. If anything, she is sharper than most people.

The frigate continued advancing. By the time their location pin overlapped with their destination on the map, the bizarre fog had already vanished, revealing a vast ocean region.

“Huh? Why is there nothing here?”

The crowd was baffled. There was nothing but water surrounding them.
